Captures interaction type (call, email, SMS, in-person meeting, video interview, portal message), interaction direction (inbound, outbound), interaction channel, interaction subject/summary, recruiter ID, interaction timestamp, response received flag, response timestamp, sentiment tag (positive, neutral, negative), and linked job order ID if applicable. Supports NPS tracking, candidate experience measurement, recruiter activity KPI reporting, and SLA compliance monitoring in Bullhorn CRM. This is the transactional activity log for candidate communications — distinct from status_history which tracks lifecycle state changes.'
}

Table candidate.assessment {
  assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each candidate assessment or screening evaluation record in the staffing and recruitment process.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the requisition (Req) or job order for which this assessment was administered, if the assessment was req-specific. Null for general talent pool assessments.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate who completed or was subject to this assessment. Links to the candidate master profile in the Bullhorn ATS.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal recruiter, hiring manager, or human evaluator who conducted or reviewed the assessment. Null for automated/system-administered assessments.']
  administered_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the assessment was administered or conducted. Used for validity tracking, expiration calculation, and Time to Fill (TTF) analytics.']
  administered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the assessment was started or administered. Supports duration calculation and audit trail requirements.']
  adverse_action_date date [note: 'Date on which the adverse action decision was communicated to the candidate. Required for FCRA compliance tracking and EEOC reporting.']
  adverse_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an adverse action (e.g., disqualification from placement) was taken based on the result of this assessment. Triggers FCRA-mandated pre-adverse and adverse action notification workflows.']
  allotted_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The maximum time in minutes allotted for the candidate to complete the assessment as defined by the assessment instrument. Used to flag time overruns and ensure standardized administration.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this assessment record, used for cross-system reference and candidate communication (e.g., ASM-2024-00123). Sourced from Bullhorn ATS or the assessment provider platform.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the assessment record. Drives workflow routing in the Applicant Tracking System (ATS) and candidate ranking processes.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assessment administered to the candidate. Drives scoring logic, pass/fail thresholds, and Quality of Submission (QoS) workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: skills_test|behavioral|cognitive|technical_screen|video_interview|drug_screen|reference_check — promote to reference product]']
  collection_site varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the collection facility where a drug screen or physical assessment specimen was collected. Applicable for drug screen and physical assessment types.']
  competency_level varchar [note: 'The proficiency level demonstrated by the candidate on this assessment, mapped to a standardized competency scale. Used for candidate ranking and job matching in the ATS.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the candidate completed or submitted the assessment. Used to calculate assessment duration and monitor candidate engagement.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the candidate provided consent for this assessment. Required for FCRA compliance documentation for background checks and drug screens.']
  consent_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate provided explicit written or electronic consent prior to the administration of this assessment, as required by FCRA for background checks and applicable privacy regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this assessment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'The method or channel through which the assessment was delivered to the candidate. Impacts score interpretation, proctoring requirements, and compliance with standardized testing protocols.']
  drug_screen_panel varchar [note: 'The type of drug screening panel administered when assessment_type is \'drug_screen\'. Determines which substances are tested and aligns with client-specific or DOT-mandated requirements.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time in minutes the candidate spent completing the assessment, calculated from administered_timestamp to completed_timestamp. Used for quality assurance and detecting anomalous completion patterns.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the assessment result is no longer considered valid for placement decisions. Particularly relevant for drug screens, background checks, and certifications with regulatory shelf-life requirements.']
  is_proctored boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment was administered under proctored conditions to ensure integrity and prevent cheating. Relevant for high-stakes technical screens and cognitive assessments.']
  is_req_specific boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assessment was administered specifically for a particular job order/requisition (True) or as a general talent pool qualification assessment (False). Drives reusability logic for candidate redeployment.']
  medical_review_officer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the licensed Medical Review Officer (MRO) who reviewed and certified the drug screen result. Required for DOT-regulated positions and federally mandated drug testing programs.']
  normalized_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'The standardized score after applying normalization logic (e.g., percentile conversion, scale adjustment) to enable cross-assessment and cross-candidate comparison. Drives Quality of Submission (QoS) and candidate ranking workflows.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recruiter or assessor observations, qualitative feedback, or contextual notes about the assessment administration or result. Supports human-in-the-loop review workflows.']
  pass_fail_result varchar [note: 'The binary or categorical outcome of the assessment indicating whether the candidate met the required threshold. Drives ATS workflow routing, submittal eligibility, and Quality of Submission (QoS) scoring.']
  passing_threshold decimal(10,4) [note: 'The minimum score required for a candidate to pass this assessment. Used to derive pass/fail result and enforce client-specific or role-specific screening standards.']
  percentile_rank decimal(5,2) [note: 'The candidate\'s score expressed as a percentile relative to the norming population for this assessment. Enables comparative ranking of candidates for a given requisition.']
  provider_assessment_code varchar [note: 'The external identifier assigned to this assessment by the third-party assessment provider platform. Enables reconciliation between internal records and provider systems (e.g., Sterling, HireVue, Codility).']
  provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party vendor or platform that administered the assessment (e.g., HireVue, Codility, Wonderlic, Sterling Background Check). Used for vendor performance tracking and cost allocation.']
  raw_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'The unprocessed numeric score returned directly by the assessment provider or evaluator before normalization. Retained for audit and re-scoring purposes.']
  reference_source_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who provided a reference check response, applicable when assessment_type is \'reference_check\'. Stored for audit and compliance purposes.']
  reference_source_relationship varchar [note: 'The professional relationship of the reference provider to the candidate (e.g., supervisor, colleague). Applicable when assessment_type is \'reference_check\'. Informs the weight given to the reference in QoS scoring.']
  result_detail varchar [note: 'Narrative or structured detail describing the assessment outcome beyond the pass/fail result (e.g., drug screen substance detected, reference check summary, behavioral competency ratings). Supports recruiter review and compliance documentation.']
  reusable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment result can be reused for other job orders or placements without re-administration, subject to expiration date. Supports candidate redeployment and bench management efficiency.']
  score_scale_max decimal(10,4) [note: 'The maximum possible score on the assessment instrument\'s scoring scale. Required to interpret raw and normalized scores in context and calculate score percentages.']
  score_scale_min decimal(10,4) [note: 'The minimum possible score on the assessment instrument\'s scoring scale. Required to interpret raw and normalized scores in context.']
  skill_category varchar [note: 'The primary skill domain or competency area evaluated by this assessment (e.g., \'Java Development\', \'Microsoft Excel\', \'Forklift Operation\', \'Customer Service\'). Supports skills inventory management and talent pool segmentation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this assessment record originated (e.g., Bullhorn ATS, Sterling Background Check, HireVue, Codility). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bullhorn|sterling|hirevue|codility|wonderlic|successfactors|manual|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  title varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the specific assessment instrument administered (e.g., \'Wonderlic Personnel Test\', \'Codility Java Developer Screen\', \'HireVue Structured Interview\'). Supports candidate ranking and QoS reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this assessment record was last modified. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer and audit compliance.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the assessment instrument or test form administered. Ensures score comparability across candidates assessed with different versions of the same test.']

  Note: 'Stores results of formal candidate assessments and screening evaluations administered during the recruitment process. Captures assessment type (skills test, behavioral assessment, cognitive test, technical screen, video interview score, drug screen result, reference check score), assessment provider (HireVue, Codility, Wonderlic, etc.), assessment date, raw score, normalized score, pass/fail result, assessor ID (if human), assessment version, expiration date, and linked job order ID if assessment was req-specific. Supports QoS and candidate ranking workflows.'
}

Table candidate.education {
  education_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each candidate education record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the education data product within the candidate domain.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate who holds this education record. Links the education history to the candidate master profile in the Bullhorn ATS/CRM candidate entity.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regional or national accreditation body that accredits the institution (e.g., HLC, SACSCOC, ABET, ACEN for nursing programs). Used to validate institutional legitimacy for compliance-sensitive placements in healthcare, government, and defense sectors.']
  concentration varchar [note: 'Specific academic concentration, specialization, or track within the major (e.g., \'Data Analytics\' within a Computer Science major, \'Corporate Finance\' within an MBA). Provides additional granularity for advanced qualification matching on specialized job orders.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this education record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, and CCPA data governance obligations.']
  credential_evaluation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the accredited foreign credential evaluation organization that assessed the international degree (e.g., World Education Services (WES), Educational Credential Evaluators (ECE)). Required for OFCCP and I-9 compliance documentation.']
  credential_evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the foreign credential evaluation process for internationally-earned degrees. Applicable only when is_international_credential is True. Required for OFCCP compliance on federal contractor placements and I-9 employment eligibility workflows.']
  credential_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the education credential. Tracks whether the credential is active and valid, expired, revoked by the institution, or pending issuance. Drives compliance checks in the credentialing management workflow.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the completeness and reliability of this education record, calculated based on field population rates and verification status. Used by Power BI Analytics dashboards to surface low-quality candidate profiles for recruiter review.']
  degree_name varchar [note: 'Full verbatim name of the degree or certificate as it appears on the official credential document (e.g., \'Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering\', \'AWS Certified Solutions Architect\'). Preserves the exact credential title for client-facing submittals and compliance documentation.']
  degree_type varchar [note: 'Type of academic credential or degree earned or pursued by the candidate. Core qualification field used in job order matching for roles requiring minimum education levels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high_school_diploma|associate|bachelor|master|doctorate|certificate|bootcamp_certificate|professional_license|ged|other — promote to reference product]']
  field_of_study varchar [note: 'Broad academic discipline or field in which the degree or certificate was awarded (e.g., Computer Science, Nursing, Business Administration). Used for skills taxonomy mapping and job order qualification matching in the ATS.']
  gpa decimal(4,3) [note: 'Candidate\'s reported Grade Point Average (GPA) on a 4.0 scale, if provided. Optional field sourced from resume parsing or candidate self-submission. Some client job orders specify minimum GPA thresholds for entry-level placements.']
  gpa_scale decimal(4,1) [note: 'The maximum GPA scale used by the institution (e.g., 4.0, 5.0, 10.0). Required to normalize GPA values across institutions with different grading scales for accurate qualification comparison.']
  graduation_date date [note: 'Actual or expected date the candidate graduated or completed the degree or certificate program. Null if the candidate did not complete the program. Used for tenure calculations and qualification date-gating on job orders.']
  graduation_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit calendar year of graduation or program completion. Stored separately from graduation_date to support cases where only the year is known (e.g., resume-parsed data). Used for cohort analytics and workforce planning.']
  honors varchar [note: 'Academic honors designation awarded at graduation (e.g., Cum Laude, Magna Cum Laude, Summa Cum Laude). Used for premium candidate profiling and client-facing talent quality reporting.']
  institution_city varchar [note: 'City where the educational institution is located. Used for geographic context in credential verification and compliance reporting.']
  institution_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the educational institution is located. Supports international credential evaluation and EEOC/OFCCP reporting.']
  institution_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the educational institution attended by the candidate (e.g., University of Michigan, DeVry Institute, Coursera). Used for qualification matching and credential verification workflows.']
  institution_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state abbreviation (or equivalent province/territory code) where the educational institution is located. Used for regional compliance and credential verification routing.']
  institution_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the educational institution. Drives qualification matching logic for job orders requiring specific institution types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: university|community_college|trade_school|bootcamp|online_platform|high_school|vocational|seminary|other — promote to reference product]']
  is_accredited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution holds recognized regional or national accreditation (True). Many client job orders, particularly in healthcare, government, and defense, require degrees from accredited institutions. Drives qualification gate logic in the ATS.']
  is_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate completed and received the degree or certificate (True) or attended without completing (False). Critical for qualification matching — incomplete degrees may not satisfy job order minimum education requirements.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Soft-delete flag indicating whether this education record has been logically deleted (True) from the active candidate profile without physical removal. Supports GDPR right-to-erasure workflows and CCPA deletion request processing while preserving audit history.']
  is_highest_degree boolean [note: 'Flags this education record as the candidate\'s highest level of academic achievement (True). Used by ATS qualification matching logic to quickly identify the most advanced credential without scanning all education records.']
  is_international_credential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the education credential was earned at a non-US institution (True) and therefore may require foreign credential evaluation. Triggers the international credential evaluation workflow for EEOC and OFCCP compliance on federal contracts.']
  is_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the education record has been independently verified by a third-party credential verification service (True) or is based solely on candidate self-reported data (False). Critical for compliance on job orders requiring verified credentials.']
  major varchar [note: 'Primary academic concentration or major declared by the candidate within their degree program (e.g., Software Engineering, Accounting, Registered Nursing). Provides granular qualification detail beyond field of study for precise job order matching.']
  minor varchar [note: 'Secondary academic concentration or minor declared by the candidate within their degree program. Supplementary qualification data used for advanced candidate matching and talent pool analytics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recruiter or system-generated notes related to this education record (e.g., discrepancies found during verification, candidate clarifications, special circumstances). Supports recruiter workflow in Bullhorn ATS.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which this education record was originally captured. Tracks whether data originated from automated resume parsing, candidate self-entry via portal, recruiter manual entry in Bullhorn, or a background check report. Supports data quality scoring and sourcing analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: resume_parse|candidate_portal|recruiter_entry|ats_import|vms_import|background_check|linkedin_import|other — promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this education record was ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Bullhorn ATS, SAP SuccessFactors, and Sterling Background Check. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bullhorn|sap_successfactors|sterling_bgc|candidate_portal|beeline_vms|manual|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system_record_ref varchar [note: 'The native record identifier for this education entry in the originating source system (e.g., Bullhorn education record ID, SAP SuccessFactors education history ID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer and operational systems.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the candidate began attending the educational institution or program. Used to calculate duration of study and to validate enrollment periods during credential verification.']
  thesis_title varchar [note: 'Title of the candidate\'s thesis or dissertation for graduate-level degrees (Master\'s or Doctorate). Used for highly specialized technical placements where research expertise is a qualification criterion.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this education record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and SOC 2 Type II audit compliance.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the education credential was verified by the designated verification source. Used for compliance audit trails and to determine if re-verification is required based on credential age policies.']
  verification_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference or case number assigned by the credential verification provider (e.g., Sterling BGC order ID, National Student Clearinghouse transaction ID). Enables traceability back to the source verification record for audit and dispute resolution.']
  verification_source varchar [note: 'The source or method used to verify the education credential. Identifies whether verification was performed via Sterling Background Check, National Student Clearinghouse, direct institution contact, or is candidate self-reported. Required for FCRA compliance documentation.']

  Note: 'Structured education history for each candidate, capturing institution name, institution type (university, community college, trade school, bootcamp, online platform), degree type (high school diploma, associate, bachelor, master, doctorate, certificate, bootcamp certificate), field of study, major, minor, graduation date, GPA (if provided), is_highest_degree flag, verified flag, and verification source. Sourced from resume parsing and candidate-submitted data. Used for qualification matching on job orders requiring specific educational credentials.'
}

Table candidate.pay_rate_agreement {
  pay_rate_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the pay rate agreement record. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement or assignment record associated with this pay rate agreement. Populated once a placement is confirmed; nullable during negotiation phase.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Pay rates vary by client work location due to prevailing wage laws, cost-of-labor adjustments, and location-specific rate cards. Critical for payroll accuracy, compliance, and margin calculation. No e']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order or requisition (Req) for which this pay rate agreement applies. Scopes the agreement to a specific engagement context.']
  pay_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_rate. Business justification: Links negotiated pay rate agreement (candidate domain) to operationalized payroll rate (payroll domain). Staffing firms negotiate rates with candidates during placement, then activate those rates in p']
  primary_pay_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recruiter who negotiated and captured this pay rate agreement on behalf of the staffing firm. Supports accountability and commission tracking.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate for whom this pay rate agreement is established. Links to the candidate master record in the Candidate domain.']
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Wage/hour determinations validate FLSA classification, overtime eligibility, and minimum wage compliance for specific pay arrangements. Pay rate agreements must reference the compliance determination ']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this pay rate agreement, used in payroll handoff documents, offer letters, and TempWorks Payroll integration. Format: PRA- followed by 8-12 digits.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pay rate agreement. Draft indicates initial capture; pending_approval awaits manager sign-off; approved is authorized but not yet effective; active is currently in force; expired has passed its end date; cancelled was voided before activation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The current approval workflow status for this pay rate agreement. Pending awaits review; approved indicates authorization by the designated approver; rejected requires renegotiation; escalated has been referred to senior management for exception handling.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the pay rate agreement was formally approved by the designated approver. Null if not yet approved. Used for SLA tracking on approval turnaround time.']
  burden_rate_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The employer burden rate percentage applied on top of the base pay rate to account for employer-side costs including FICA, FUTA, SUTA, Workers Compensation (Workers Comp), and benefits. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 18.50 for 18.50%). Used in gross margin and spread calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pay rate agreement record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting SOC 2 Type II compliance and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this pay rate agreement becomes binding and active. Pay calculations in TempWorks Payroll use this date to determine which rate applies for a given pay period.']
  engagement_type varchar [note: 'The type of staffing engagement for which this pay rate agreement applies. Temp (temporary), contract, temp-to-perm (Contract-to-Hire), direct hire (permanent placement), or SOW (Statement of Work) project-based engagement. Drives payroll and billing treatment.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this pay rate agreement ceases to be effective. Null for open-ended agreements. When populated, triggers a rate review or renewal workflow. Aligns with assignment end date or contract term.']
  flsa_exemption_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate is classified as exempt or non-exempt under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA). Non-exempt workers are entitled to overtime pay; exempt workers are not. Critical for OT rate applicability and payroll compliance.']
  holiday_rate_multiplier decimal(6,4) [note: 'The multiplier applied to the base pay rate for hours worked on designated holidays (e.g., 1.5 for time-and-a-half, 2.0 for double time). Null if no special holiday rate applies. Used in TempWorks Payroll holiday pay calculations.']
  is_rate_negotiable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pay rate in this agreement is open to further negotiation by the candidate or recruiter. True if negotiable; False if the rate is fixed (e.g., set by a VMS program or client rate card). Supports recruiter workflow guidance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pay rate agreement record was most recently modified. Audit trail field supporting change tracking, SOC 2 Type II compliance, and Silver layer incremental processing in Databricks.']
  max_weekly_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'The maximum number of hours per week the candidate is authorized to work under this agreement before requiring manager or client approval for additional hours. Supports overtime cost control and ACA compliance monitoring.']
  min_guaranteed_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'The minimum number of hours per week or pay period guaranteed to the candidate under this agreement, regardless of actual hours worked. Common in certain contract arrangements. Null if no minimum guarantee applies.']
  msa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Master Service Agreement (MSA) or contract document that governs the terms under which this pay rate agreement was established. Links to DocuSign CLM contract records.']
  negotiation_date date [note: 'The date on which the pay rate was negotiated and agreed between the recruiter and the candidate. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this agreement. Distinct from the effective date when the rate becomes active.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recruiter or manager notes related to this pay rate agreement, such as special negotiation context, candidate expectations, client constraints, or exception justifications. Supports operational context for payroll and compliance teams.']
  ot_threshold_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'The number of hours worked in a week (or day, depending on state law) after which overtime rates apply. Defaults to 40 hours per week per FLSA but may be lower for certain states (e.g., 8 hours/day in California). Drives OT rate application in TempWorks Payroll.']
  prevailing_wage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay rate agreement is subject to prevailing wage requirements under the Davis-Bacon Act, Service Contract Act, or applicable state prevailing wage laws (common for government and public sector contracts). True if prevailing wage rules apply.']
  prevailing_wage_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The government-mandated prevailing wage rate applicable to this engagement when prevailing_wage_flag is True. The base_pay_rate must be equal to or greater than this value to maintain compliance. Null if prevailing wage does not apply.']
  rate_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded explanation for why this pay rate agreement was created or modified (e.g., initial placement, merit increase, market adjustment, assignment extension, client rate change, cost-of-living adjustment). Supports audit and analytics.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this pay rate agreement originated. Bullhorn for ATS-driven agreements; TempWorks for payroll-initiated rates; Beeline for VMS-driven rate cards; Manual for agreements entered directly into the data platform.']
  source_system_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this pay rate agreement record in the originating source system (e.g., Bullhorn placement pay rate ID, TempWorks pay rate record ID). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and integration traceability.']
  vms_rate_card_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the VMS (Vendor Management System) rate card or rate schedule that governs this pay rate agreement when the engagement is managed through a VMS program (e.g., Beeline). Null for non-VMS engagements.']
  work_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code where the candidate performs work under this agreement. Determines applicable state wage laws, SUTA rates, workers comp rates, and state income tax withholding requirements.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'The NCCI or state-specific workers compensation class code assigned to this pay rate agreement based on the job duties and risk classification. Drives workers comp premium calculation in TempWorks Payroll.']

  Note: 'Records the negotiated or agreed pay rate terms between the staffing firm and the candidate for a specific engagement context. Captures pay rate type (hourly, daily, weekly, salary), base pay rate, OT (overtime) rate, DT (double time) rate, per diem rate, currency, effective date, expiration date, pay rate basis (W-2, 1099, EOR), burden rate applied, negotiated by recruiter ID, and approval status. Distinct from the billing-side bill rate — this is the candidate-facing pay agreement. Supports payroll processing handoff to TempWorks.'
}

Table candidate.right_to_represent {
  right_to_represent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each Right to Represent (RTR) grant record. Primary key for this entity. Role classification: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for which the RTR was granted. Required for client-wide or program-wide RTR scope; also populated for single-req scope to support client-level compliance reporting.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: RTR is often granted by specific client contact (hiring manager, HR representative). Tracking who granted RTR supports dispute resolution, audit requirements, and relationship accountability. Role-pre']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific job order (requisition) for which the RTR was granted. Applicable when RTR scope is limited to a single requisition.']
  primary_right_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal recruiter or staffing consultant who obtained and recorded the RTR grant from the candidate. Used for accountability, audit, and performance tracking.']
  prior_rtr_right_to_represent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous RTR record that this record supersedes or replaces, enabling lineage tracking when an RTR is renewed, extended, or re-scoped. Null for original grants.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate who granted the Right to Represent (RTR) authorization. Links to the candidate master record in the candidate domain.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: In VMS/MSP programs, multiple staffing suppliers compete for the same requisitions. Right-to-represent tracks which supplier has submission rights for a specific candidate to a specific client/req. Th']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the VMS program (e.g., Beeline) under which this RTR was captured, applicable when rtr_scope is \'program_wide\'. Null for non-VMS engagements.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the candidate formally acknowledged the RTR terms. Distinct from grant_timestamp in cases where consent is captured asynchronously (e.g., portal consent with delayed confirmation).']
  candidate_acknowledged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has explicitly acknowledged the RTR terms, including understanding that the staffing firm will represent them to the specified client or program. True = acknowledged; False = not yet acknowledged.']
  candidate_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device used by the candidate when granting RTR consent via a digital portal. Captured for audit and legal evidence purposes. Applicable when grant_method is \'portal_consent\'. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR and CCPA.']
  consent_document_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL or storage path to the RTR consent document (e.g., signed PDF, DocuSign completed envelope). Confidential as it may contain candidate signature and personal details. Null when no document was captured.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this RTR record was first created in the system. Distinct from grant_timestamp, which captures the real-world consent event.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this RTR grant is subject to an active dispute, such as a competing vendor claiming prior right to represent the same candidate to the same client. True = disputed; False = undisputed.']
  dispute_resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the outcome and resolution of an RTR dispute, including the basis for the decision (e.g., timestamp comparison, client confirmation). Populated only when dispute_flag is True.']
  eeoc_disposition_code varchar [note: 'Standardized EEOC disposition code associated with the candidate\'s submittal outcome linked to this RTR, used for adverse action tracking and OFCCP/EEOC reporting. Populated after submittal disposition is determined. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: interviewed|not_selected|hired|withdrawn|no_show|position_cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  email_thread_reference varchar [note: 'Message ID or thread reference from the email system capturing the candidate\'s email-based RTR consent. Applicable when grant_method is \'email\'. Used for audit evidence retrieval.']
  exclusivity_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the exclusivity period of the RTR grant ends, after which the candidate may be submitted by other vendors. Null when exclusivity_flag is False or exclusivity is open-ended.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RTR grant is exclusive to this staffing firm for the specified scope, preventing the candidate from being submitted by competing vendors to the same client or requisition. True = exclusive; False = non-exclusive.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the RTR grant expires and is no longer valid for new submittals. Null indicates no expiration (open-ended grant). Enforced by ATS workflow to prevent unauthorized submittals after expiry.']
  grant_method varchar [note: 'The channel or mechanism by which the candidate provided RTR consent. \'verbal\' — spoken consent captured by recruiter; \'email\' — written consent via email; \'portal_consent\' — self-service ATS/candidate portal click-through; \'signed_form\' — wet or e-signature on RTR document; \'vms_portal\' — consent captured through a Vendor Management System (VMS) portal such as Beeline.']
  grant_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time the candidate granted the Right to Represent authorization. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction, distinct from the record audit timestamp.']
  is_revoked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has revoked the RTR grant prior to its natural expiration. True = revoked; False = not revoked. Used as a fast-path filter in submittal eligibility checks.']
  nda_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client requires a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) to be in place before the candidate\'s profile can be shared. True = NDA required prior to submittal; False = no NDA requirement.']
  nda_signed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required NDA has been executed by the candidate prior to or concurrent with the RTR grant. Relevant when nda_required is True. True = NDA signed; False = NDA not yet signed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General free-text notes entered by the recruiter or compliance team regarding this RTR grant, including any special conditions, client-specific requirements, or contextual information not captured in structured fields.']
  ofccp_audit_ready boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this RTR record has all required documentation and metadata to satisfy an Office of Federal Contract Compliance Programs (OFCCP) audit, including consent evidence, timestamps, and scope definition. True = audit-ready; False = documentation incomplete.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'The worker classification and engagement type for which the RTR is being granted. \'w2_temp\' — W-2 temporary placement; \'1099_contract\' — independent contractor (1099); \'contract_to_hire\' — contract-to-hire (temp-to-perm); \'direct_hire\' — direct placement/permanent; \'eor\' — Employer of Record (EOR) arrangement.']
  revocation_initiated_by varchar [note: 'Identifies who initiated the revocation of the RTR grant. \'candidate\' — candidate self-revoked; \'recruiter\' — recruiter revoked on behalf of candidate; \'system\' — automated revocation triggered by business rule (e.g., placement made); \'client\' — client requested removal.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or categorized explanation provided by the candidate or recruiter for why the RTR was revoked. Examples: candidate accepted another offer, candidate withdrew from process, candidate requested removal from client consideration.']
  revocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time the candidate revoked the RTR grant. Null when is_revoked is False. Required for audit trail and to determine the window during which submittals were authorized.']
  rtr_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable identifier for the RTR grant, used in recruiter communications, audit trails, and submittal documentation. Format: RTR-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  rtr_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the breadth of the RTR authorization. \'single_req\' — limited to one specific requisition/job order; \'client_wide\' — covers all open requisitions at the named client; \'program_wide\' — covers all requisitions within a managed service program (MSP/VMS program).']
  rtr_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the RTR grant. \'active\' — valid and usable for submittal; \'expired\' — past expiration date; \'revoked\' — candidate withdrew consent; \'pending\' — awaiting candidate confirmation; \'superseded\' — replaced by a newer RTR for the same scope.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system where the RTR consent was captured. \'bullhorn\' — Bullhorn ATS/CRM; \'beeline_vms\' — Beeline VMS portal; \'candidate_portal\' — self-service candidate portal; \'email\' — email-based capture; \'manual\' — manually entered by recruiter.']
  source_system_rtr_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this RTR record in the originating source system (e.g., Bullhorn submittal ID, Beeline VMS consent token). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this RTR record, including status changes, revocations, or scope updates.']
  valid_days_duration varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days for which the RTR grant is valid from the grant_timestamp. Used to calculate expiration_date and to standardize RTR validity policies across client programs.']
  verbal_consent_notes varchar [note: 'Recruiter-entered notes documenting the circumstances of a verbal RTR consent, including date, time, and summary of conversation. Required when grant_method is \'verbal\' to satisfy audit requirements.']

  Note: 'Tracks RTR (Right to Represent) grants issued by candidates authorizing the staffing firm to submit their profile to a specific client or job order. Captures candidate ID, job order ID, client ID, RTR grant method (verbal, email, portal consent, signed form), RTR grant timestamp, RTR expiration date, RTR scope (single req, client-wide, program-wide), revocation flag, revocation timestamp, and revocation reason. Critical for compliance with co-employment rules and preventing unauthorized submittals. Supports OFCCP and EEOC audit readiness.'
}

Table candidate.talent_pool_membership {
  talent_pool_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each talent pool membership record. Primary key for the talent_pool_membership entity in the candidate domain Silver layer.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for which this talent pool is specifically curated, if applicable. Null for general or non-client-specific talent pools. Supports client-specific bench management and VMS program alignment.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Talent pools often organized by client location ("pool for Chicago warehouse site") to support targeted sourcing, redeployment, and bench management. Standard MSP/VMS practice. No existing location FK']
  primary_talent_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the recruiter or staffing coordinator who added the candidate to the talent pool. Supports accountability tracking and recruiter performance analytics.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate who is a member of the talent pool. Links to the candidate master record in the Bullhorn ATS/CRM candidate object.']
  redeployment_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the assignment that is ending and triggering the redeployment of this candidate into the talent pool. Null for non-redeployment pool memberships. Links to the placement.assignment product.']
  talent_pool_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the curated talent pool or talent community to which the candidate belongs. Talent pools may represent skill-based communities, redeployment pools, client-specific bench lists, or specialty practice groups.']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Vendor Management System (VMS) program associated with this talent pool membership, if the pool is managed under a Managed Service Provider (MSP) or VMS-driven contingent workforce program via Beeline VMS.']
  added_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the candidate was added to the talent pool. Used for tenure-in-pool analytics and redeployment SLA tracking.']
  availability_date date [note: 'Date from which the candidate is available to begin a new assignment or placement. For redeployment candidates, this is typically the day after the current assignment ends. Critical for matching and dispatch workflows.']
  availability_type varchar [note: 'The type of work engagement the candidate is available for within this talent pool context. Indicates whether the candidate is seeking full-time, part-time, contract, per diem, or flexible arrangements. Supports job order matching logic.']
  bgc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the candidate\'s background check (BGC) as it pertains to eligibility for placements from this talent pool. \'clear\' indicates a passed background check; \'adverse_action\' indicates a disqualifying result; \'expired\' indicates the BGC has exceeded its validity period. Sourced from Sterling Background Check integration.']
  contact_attempt_count varchar [note: 'Total number of recruiter contact attempts made to the candidate since being added to this talent pool. Used to identify unresponsive candidates and trigger escalation or exit workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this talent pool membership record was first created in the system. Represents the audit creation time in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trails, and SLA measurement.']
  drug_screen_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the candidate\'s drug screening result as it pertains to eligibility for placements from this talent pool. \'negative\' indicates a passed drug screen; \'positive\' indicates a failed result; \'expired\' indicates the screen has exceeded its validity period. Sourced from Sterling Background Check.']
  engagement_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (0–100) reflecting the candidate\'s level of engagement with the talent pool, based on recruiter interactions, response rates, and activity signals. Higher scores indicate more responsive and engaged candidates. Used to prioritize outreach and predict placement likelihood.']
  everify_status varchar [note: 'Current E-Verify employment authorization status for the candidate. E-Verify is the federal electronic employment eligibility verification system. Required for federal contractor placements and certain state-mandated programs. Sourced from compliance.everify_case.']
  exit_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the candidate exited or was removed from the talent pool. Null if the membership is still active. Used for pool churn analysis and redeployment outcome tracking.']
  exit_reason varchar [note: 'Reason the candidate exited the talent pool. \'placed\' indicates a successful placement was made; \'declined_offer\' indicates the candidate declined a placement offer; \'unresponsive\' indicates the candidate became unreachable; \'disqualified\' indicates the candidate no longer meets pool criteria; \'converted_to_perm\' indicates a temp-to-perm conversion occurred. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: placed|declined_offer|unresponsive|disqualified|converted_to_perm|pool_dissolved|candidate_request|credential_lapse — promote to reference product]']
  i9_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has a valid, completed I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification on file, confirming work authorization in the United States. Required for all W-2 placements. Sourced from compliance.i9_verification.']
  interview_count varchar [note: 'Number of client interviews the candidate has completed as a result of submittals from this talent pool membership. Used to measure pool conversion effectiveness and recruiter performance.']
  is_redeployment_candidate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pool membership record was created as part of a formal redeployment workflow triggered by an ending assignment. True when the candidate is being actively redeployed; False for general talent pool members. Supports bench and redeployment tracking KPIs.']
  is_willing_to_relocate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate is willing to relocate for a placement sourced from this talent pool. True if the candidate has expressed willingness to relocate; False if the candidate requires local placements only. Expands the geographic matching radius for job order fulfillment.']
  is_willing_to_travel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate is willing to travel for assignments sourced from this talent pool. Relevant for travel nursing, per diem, and project-based staffing programs where travel is required.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent recruiter contact or outreach activity with the candidate regarding this pool membership. Used to identify stale pool members who have not been contacted recently and to enforce pool maintenance SLAs.']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the candidate\'s membership in the talent pool. \'active\' indicates the candidate is actively managed within the pool; \'pending_review\' indicates awaiting recruiter qualification; \'suspended\' indicates temporarily paused; \'exited\' indicates the candidate has been removed from the pool.']
  membership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the candidate\'s pool membership. \'redeployment\' indicates the candidate is being actively redeployed from an ending assignment; \'bench\' indicates an unassigned worker available for dispatch; \'talent_community\' indicates a passive talent community member; \'client_specific\' indicates a pool curated for a specific client program; \'specialty_practice\' indicates a domain-specific skill pool. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: redeployment|bench|talent_community|client_specific|specialty_practice|pipeline|vms_preferred|msp_approved — promote to reference product]']
  pay_rate_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the pay rate values (pay_rate_min, pay_rate_max). Defaults to \'USD\' for domestic U.S. operations. Supports multi-currency operations for international staffing programs.']
  pay_rate_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum hourly or unit pay rate the candidate is targeting for placements sourced from this talent pool. Expressed in USD. Used alongside pay_rate_min to define the candidate\'s acceptable pay range for matching and negotiation.']
  pay_rate_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'The minimum hourly or unit pay rate the candidate is willing to accept for placements sourced from this talent pool. Expressed in USD. Used by recruiters during job order matching to filter viable candidates. Supports spread and gross margin analysis.']
  pool_qualification_date date [note: 'Date on which the candidate was formally qualified and approved for inclusion in this talent pool by a recruiter or pool manager. May differ from added_date if a review/approval workflow is required before activation.']
  preferred_location_city varchar [note: 'The candidate\'s preferred city for work assignments sourced from this talent pool. Used for geographic matching of job orders to pool members. Supports workforce scheduling and dispatch workflows.']
  preferred_location_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state code for the candidate\'s preferred work location. Used for geographic filtering during job order matching and for state-specific compliance checks (SUTA, workers comp, wage-hour laws).']
  preferred_work_type varchar [note: 'The candidate\'s preferred employment classification or engagement type within this pool. Reflects whether the candidate prefers W-2 temporary, 1099 independent contractor, Employer of Record (EOR), or direct hire arrangements. Informs compliance and worker classification checks.']
  primary_skill_category varchar [note: 'The primary skill category or discipline that qualifies the candidate for this talent pool (e.g., \'Information Technology\', \'Healthcare - Nursing\', \'Light Industrial\', \'Finance & Accounting\'). Used for pool segmentation and job order matching. Free-text aligned to the pool\'s defined skill taxonomy.']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'Recruiter-assigned priority tier indicating the urgency and desirability of placing this candidate from the pool. Tier 1 represents highest priority (e.g., actively redeploying workers with imminent assignment end dates); Tier 4 represents passive pipeline members. Drives recruiter workflow prioritization.']
  qualification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the recruiter or pool manager documenting the rationale for the candidate\'s inclusion in the talent pool, any qualification criteria met, or special considerations for placement matching.']
  redeployment_target_date date [note: 'Target date by which the recruiter aims to redeploy the candidate into a new assignment. Typically aligned with or shortly after the ending assignment\'s end date. Used for redeployment SLA and Time to Start (TTS) tracking.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'The sourcing channel or mechanism through which the candidate was identified and added to this talent pool. \'redeployment\' indicates the candidate was added as part of an assignment-end redeployment workflow; \'vms_import\' indicates the candidate was sourced via Beeline VMS. Supports sourcing channel attribution analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ats_sourcing|redeployment|referral|vms_import|job_board|direct_apply|recruiter_outreach|social_media|campus|alumni — promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this talent pool membership record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Silver layer. \'bullhorn\' indicates the record was sourced from Bullhorn ATS/CRM; \'beeline_vms\' indicates a VMS-managed pool imported from Beeline.']
  source_system_record_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this talent pool membership record in the originating source system (e.g., Bullhorn Tearsheet Member ID or Beeline Pool Member ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and deduplication in the Silver layer.']
  submittal_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the candidate has been submitted (submitted to a client for consideration) from this talent pool membership. A submittal is a formal presentation of a candidate\'s profile to a client for a specific job order. Tracks pool productivity and Quality of Submission (QoS) metrics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this talent pool membership record. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'The IRS and DOL worker classification applicable to this candidate within the context of this talent pool. Determines tax treatment, benefits eligibility, and co-employment risk. \'w2_employee\' indicates a W-2 temporary employee; \'1099_ic\' indicates an Independent Contractor (IC); \'eor\' indicates Employer of Record arrangement; \'corp_to_corp\' indicates a business-to-business engagement.']

  Note: 'Association entity tracking which curated talent pools or talent communities a candidate belongs to, including redeployment pool assignments when a candidate is being actively redeployed from an ending assignment'
}

Table candidate.diversity_profile {
  diversity_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the diversity profile record. Primary key for this data product in the candidate domain Silver layer.']
  privacy_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a data subject access, deletion, or correction request that triggered a change to this diversity profile record. Links to the compliance domain privacy_request product for GDPR/CCPA audit trail.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate whose voluntary self-identification diversity data this record captures. Links to the core candidate profile in the candidate domain.']
  affirmative_action_plan_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Affirmative Action Plan (AAP) under which this candidate\'s diversity data is tracked for OFCCP compliance. Links the candidate\'s self-ID data to the applicable AAP establishment for federal contractor reporting.']
  anonymized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the personally identifiable elements of this diversity profile record have been anonymized following data retention expiry or candidate deletion request. Anonymized records may be retained for aggregate statistical reporting.']
  collection_date date [note: 'The date on which the voluntary self-identification data was collected from the candidate. Used for compliance audit trails and to determine data currency for EEOC/OFCCP reporting cycles.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the voluntary self-identification data was collected from the candidate. Supports audit trail and data quality assessment for EEOC/OFCCP compliance reporting.']
  collection_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which the self-identification data was collected or ingested. Supports data lineage and system-of-record traceability for compliance audits.']
  consent_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate provided consent for their diversity data to be used in reporting. Supports GDPR, CCPA, and FCRA consent audit requirements.']
  consent_to_report_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has provided explicit consent for their voluntary self-identification data to be used in EEOC, OFCCP, and internal diversity reporting. Required for lawful use of sensitive demographic data.']
  consent_withdrawn_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate withdrew consent for use of their diversity data in reporting. Nullable when consent has not been withdrawn. Triggers data suppression from active reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this diversity profile record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance record-keeping requirements.']
  data_classification_level varchar [note: 'The data sensitivity classification level assigned to this diversity profile record. Diversity data is classified as \'restricted\' by default per EEOC, OFCCP, FCRA, and internal data governance policy to enforce access control.']
  data_retention_expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which this diversity profile record must be purged or anonymized per applicable data retention policies under GDPR, CCPA, FCRA, and OFCCP record-keeping requirements. Supports automated data lifecycle management.']
  disability_status_code varchar [note: 'Voluntary self-identified disability status per ADA and Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act. Captures whether the candidate identifies as having a disability as defined under Section 503 and ADA.']
  disability_type_description varchar [note: 'Optional free-text description of the disability type if voluntarily provided by the candidate. Captured only when disability_status_code is \'yes_disability\' and the candidate elects to provide additional detail.']
  eeo1_snapshot_date date [note: 'The workforce snapshot date used for EEO-1 Component 1 reporting, typically a pay period between October 1 and December 31. Identifies which reporting cycle this record contributes to.']
  eeoc_job_category_code varchar [note: 'The EEOC EEO-1 job category code associated with the candidate\'s placement or role at time of data collection. Used to stratify diversity data by occupational category for EEO-1 Component 1 reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exec_senior_officials|first_mid_officials|professionals|technicians|sales_workers|admin_support|craft_workers|operatives|laborers|service_workers — promote to reference product]']
  form_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the voluntary self-identification form used to collect the data. Tracks form revisions to ensure compliance with current EEOC/OFCCP category definitions and regulatory updates.']
  gender_identity_code varchar [note: 'Voluntary self-identified gender identity of the candidate. Captured per EEOC and OFCCP reporting requirements. \'self_describe\' allows free-text entry captured in gender_identity_description.']
  gender_identity_description varchar [note: 'Free-text self-description of gender identity provided by the candidate when gender_identity_code is \'self_describe\'. Nullable for all other gender identity codes.']
  included_in_aap_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this candidate\'s diversity data was included in the OFCCP Affirmative Action Plan (AAP) for the applicable reporting establishment and year.']
  included_in_eeo1_report_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this candidate\'s diversity data was included in the annual EEOC EEO-1 Component 1 filing. Supports compliance audit and reconciliation between headcount and filed data.']
  invitation_sent_date date [note: 'The date on which the voluntary self-identification invitation was sent to the candidate. Required for OFCCP compliance to demonstrate that candidates were invited to self-identify at the appropriate stage of the hiring process.']
  lgbtq_identity_flag boolean [note: 'Voluntary self-identification flag indicating whether the candidate identifies as LGBTQ+. Captured for internal diversity program tracking and inclusion reporting. Not used for EEOC/OFCCP mandatory filings.']
  no_response_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why no response was received from the candidate for the self-identification invitation. Nullable when response_received_flag is True. Supports OFCCP audit documentation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by compliance or HR staff regarding this diversity profile record. May include data quality observations, correction history, or compliance review comments. Access restricted per data classification policy.']
  ofccp_covered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate is associated with a federal contractor placement and therefore subject to OFCCP affirmative action and reporting obligations under Executive Order 11246, Section 503, and VEVRAA.']
  post_offer_collection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the self-identification data was collected after a job offer was extended. Post-offer collection is the standard timing for Section 503 disability self-ID per OFCCP regulations.']
  pre_offer_collection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the self-identification data was collected prior to a job offer being extended. OFCCP and EEOC require that self-ID data collected pre-offer be maintained separately and not used in selection decisions.']
  race_ethnicity_code varchar [note: 'Voluntary self-identified race/ethnicity category per EEOC standard classifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hispanic_or_latino|white|black_or_african_american|native_hawaiian_or_pacific_islander|asian|american_indian_or_alaska_native|two_or_more_races|prefer_not_to_answer — promote to reference product]']
  race_ethnicity_label varchar [note: 'Human-readable display label corresponding to the race/ethnicity code as reported by the candidate on the voluntary self-identification form. Stored for reporting clarity and audit trail.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the diversity profile record. \'active\' indicates the current valid record; \'superseded\' indicates a prior version replaced by a newer submission; \'withdrawn\' indicates candidate-requested removal; \'pending_review\' indicates data quality review in progress.']
  reminder_sent_date date [note: 'The date on which a reminder to complete the voluntary self-identification form was sent to the candidate. Supports OFCCP documentation requirements for outreach and invitation efforts.']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year for which this diversity profile record is applicable for EEOC EEO-1 and OFCCP affirmative action plan reporting. Supports annual compliance filing cycles.']
  response_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate responded to the voluntary self-identification invitation, regardless of whether they chose to disclose or selected \'prefer not to answer\'. Supports OFCCP response rate tracking.']
  source_form_reference varchar [note: 'The document or form reference number of the original voluntary self-identification form submitted by the candidate. Supports audit trail and document retrieval for OFCCP compliance reviews.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this diversity profile record was last modified. Tracks data currency and supports change audit trail for EEOC, OFCCP, GDPR, and CCPA compliance.']
  verified_by_recruiter_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recruiter has reviewed and confirmed that the self-identification data was collected through the proper voluntary process and that no coercion or improper influence occurred. Supports EEOC and OFCCP audit readiness.']
  veteran_status_code varchar [note: 'Voluntary self-identified veteran status per VEVRAA (Vietnam Era Veterans\' Readjustment Assistance Act) categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_veteran|protected_veteran|active_duty|recently_separated|armed_forces_service_medal|disabled_veteran|prefer_not_to_answer — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Stores voluntary self-identification data for EEOC, OFCCP, and diversity reporting purposes. Captures race/ethnicity (self-identified, per EEOC categories), gender identity, veteran status (non-veteran, protected veteran, active duty), disability status (per ADA/Section 503), collection method (voluntary self-ID form, portal), collection date, consent to use for reporting flag, and data classification level (restricted). Maintained separately from the core profile to enforce access control and comply with FCRA, EEOC, and OFCCP regulations.'
}

Table candidate.candidate_compliance {
  candidate_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for candidate_compliance']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the candidate profile record whose compliance status is being tracked']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific compliance requirement that must be fulfilled']
  assigned_date date [note: 'Date on which this compliance requirement was assigned to this candidate (typically at application submission or placement offer acceptance). Used to calculate time-to-completion and identify overdue requirements based on grace_period_days.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date on which this candidate successfully completed and had verified this specific compliance requirement. Null if requirement_status is not_started, in_progress, or failed. Used to calculate time-to-compliance metrics and determine re-verification due dates. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this candidate-requirement fulfillment record was first created in the compliance tracking system.']
  due_date date [note: 'Calculated deadline by which this candidate must complete this requirement, derived from assigned_date plus grace_period_days from the compliance_requirement master. Used for escalation workflows and onboarding gate enforcement.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this candidate\'s fulfillment of this requirement expires and requires re-verification. Calculated from completion_date plus re_verification_interval_days from the compliance_requirement master record. Null for one-time requirements that do not expire. Drives automated alerts for upcoming expirations. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to this candidate-requirement fulfillment record. Used for audit trails and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers and recruiters to document special circumstances, waiver justifications, verification issues, candidate-specific exceptions, or audit notes related to this specific candidate-requirement fulfillment. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this candidate\'s fulfillment of this specific requirement. not_started = requirement assigned but no action taken; in_progress = candidate has begun submission/verification; completed = requirement fully satisfied and verified; expired = previously completed but now past expiration date requiring re-verification; waived = requirement exempted for this candidate via approved waiver; failed = candidate did not meet requirement criteria. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  verification_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or document management system reference to the uploaded verification documents for this candidate-requirement fulfillment (e.g., scanned I-9, background check report PDF, certification scan). Supports audit evidence retrieval.']
  verification_source varchar [note: 'The system, vendor, or process through which this requirement was verified for this candidate (e.g., \'E-Verify\', \'Sterling Background Check\', \'Manual HR Review\', \'Client Portal Upload\', \'Certemy License Verification\'). Provides audit trail for compliance verification chain of custody. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the compliance officer or HR staff member who verified and approved this candidate\'s fulfillment of this requirement. Provides accountability for verification decisions.']
  waiver_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the waiver was officially approved. Required when waiver_flag is true.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the authorized individual who approved the waiver for this candidate-requirement combination. Required when waiver_flag is true. Provides accountability for compliance exceptions.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this candidate has been granted an approved waiver or exception for this specific requirement. True = requirement waived for this candidate; False = standard requirement enforcement applies. Waivers must be documented with approval authority and business justification in notes field. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the fulfillment status of compliance requirements by individual candidates in the staffing pipeline. It captures the operational tracking of which requirements each candidate has completed, when they were verified, their current status, and any waivers or exceptions granted. Each record links one candidate profile to one compliance requirement with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific candidate-requirement fulfillment relationship. Essential for onboarding clearance gates, placement eligibility determination, and regulatory audit readiness.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, each candidate must satisfy multiple compliance requirements (I-9 employment eligibility, background checks, drug screens, industry certifications, client-specific training mandates), and each compliance requirement applies to many candidates across the talent pool. The business actively manages candidate compliance fulfillment as an operational process with dedicated compliance officers who track completion status, verification dates, expiration schedules, and waivers for each candidate-requirement pair. This is not a derived analytical correlation but a core operational workflow that gates onboarding clearance and placement eligibility.'
}

Table candidate.audit_sample {
  audit_sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each audit sample record. Primary key for the association.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance audit in which this candidate was sampled']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the candidate profile included in the audit sample']
  auditor_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the auditor during review of this candidate\'s records. May include observations, context, mitigating factors, or follow-up questions. Tagged PII as it may reference candidate-specific details.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this audit sample record was created. Indicates when the candidate was added to the audit sample.']
  deficiency_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of compliance deficiencies, gaps, or findings identified for this candidate during the audit. May include documentation gaps (missing I-9, incomplete background check), process failures (late tax filing), or regulatory breaches (misclassification). Tagged PII as it may reference candidate-specific details.']
  finding_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings identified for this specific candidate within this audit. A candidate may have zero findings (clean record) or multiple findings across different compliance areas. Aggregates to the audit-level total_findings_count.']
  inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this candidate profile was included in the final audit sample (true) or was initially considered but excluded (false). Tracks both sampled and non-sampled candidates for audit trail purposes.']
  remediation_completed_date date [note: 'Actual date on which corrective action for this candidate\'s audit findings was completed and verified. Used to track compliance with remediation timelines and regulatory commitments. Null if remediation not yet complete.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which corrective action for this candidate\'s audit findings must be completed. Set by compliance team based on finding severity and regulatory requirements. Null if no findings or no remediation required.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of corrective action or remediation for findings related to this candidate in this audit. Tracks lifecycle from identification through closure. Not_applicable indicates no findings were identified for this candidate.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk severity rating assigned to findings for this candidate in this audit. Critical findings require immediate escalation and remediation. Aggregates to audit-level overall_risk_rating. Null if no findings identified.']
  sample_selection_reason varchar [note: 'The methodology or rationale for why this candidate was selected for inclusion in the audit sample. Indicates whether selection was random, risk-based, regulatory-mandated, or client-requested. Critical for audit defensibility and statistical validity.']
  sample_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned to this candidate within the audit sample. Used for audit workpaper organization and tracking progress through the sample. Supports audit methodology documentation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to this audit sample record. Tracks changes to findings, remediation status, or auditor notes.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the inclusion of a candidate profile in a compliance audit sample. It captures which candidates were selected for audit review, the selection criteria, findings specific to that candidate in that audit, and remediation status. Each record links one profile to one compliance_audit with attributes that exist only in the context of this audit sampling relationship. Critical for audit response, corrective action tracking, and regulatory compliance documentation.. Existence Justification: In staffing compliance operations, audits (I-9, OFCCP, wage/hour, client audits) routinely sample multiple candidate/worker records for review, and individual candidates may be included in multiple audits over time (e.g., an I-9 audit in Q1, an OFCCP audit in Q3, and a client compliance audit the following year). The business actively manages audit samples as operational records, tracking which candidates were selected, why they were selected, findings specific to each candidate in each audit, and remediation status for each candidate-audit combination.'
}

Table candidate.talent_pool {
  talent_pool_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for talent_pool']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client organization this talent pool is dedicated to, if it is a client-specific pool. Null for general pools.']
  owner_team_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal team or business unit responsible for managing this talent pool.']
  parent_talent_pool_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on talent_pool (parent_talent_pool_id)']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user (recruiter, account manager, or talent pool manager) who owns and manages this talent pool.']
  ats_pool_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this talent pool in the source Applicant Tracking System (e.g., Bullhorn pool ID).']
  available_candidate_count varchar [note: 'The number of candidates in this talent pool who are currently available for immediate placement (not on assignment).']
  bullhorn_pool_code varchar [note: 'Specific identifier for this talent pool in the Bullhorn ATS system, supporting integration and synchronization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this talent pool record was first created in the system.']
  current_candidate_count varchar [note: 'The current number of active candidates assigned to this talent pool.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the talent pool, including its purpose, target candidate profiles, and intended use cases.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this talent pool was deactivated or archived. Null for currently active pools.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this talent pool became active and available for candidate sourcing and placement activities.']
  experience_level varchar [note: 'The target experience or seniority level of candidates in this talent pool (entry, junior, mid-level, senior, executive, expert).']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic reach or coverage area of the talent pool (local, regional, national, international, remote).']
  is_bench_pool boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this talent pool represents bench candidates (employees between assignments awaiting redeployment).']
  is_client_specific boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this talent pool is dedicated to a specific client organization.']
