[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: skills_gap|overqualified|salary_mismatch|culture_fit|communication|availability|client_preference|position_cancelled|other — promote to reference product]']
  reschedule_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this interview has been rescheduled. Tracks scheduling friction and supports QoS scoring. High reschedule counts may indicate candidate or client engagement issues.']
  reschedule_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the most recent reschedule of this interview. Used for root cause analysis of scheduling inefficiencies and SLA compliance reporting.']
  round varchar [note: 'Sequential round number for this interview within the overall hiring process for the submittal (e.g., 1 = first round, 2 = second round). Enables multi-stage interview funnel analytics and time-to-hire stage tracking.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date and time of the interview. Combined with scheduled_start_timestamp to derive interview duration for scheduling analytics and client SLA reporting.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp representing the planned start date and time of the interview. Used for calendar coordination, scheduling efficiency metrics, and Time-to-Fill (TTF) stage duration calculations. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this interview record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer integration quality monitoring.']
  source_system_interview_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this interview record in the originating source system (e.g., Bullhorn interview ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and idempotent data loading in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  technical_skills_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Numeric rating for the candidate\'s technical skills competency as assessed during the interview, typically on a scale of 1.0 to 5.0. Supports competency-based analytics and QoS scoring.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interview record was most recently modified. Supports change data capture (CDC) patterns in the Databricks Silver layer and audit trail requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  video_meeting_link varchar [note: 'URL or dial-in details for virtual interview platforms (e.g., Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Google Meet). Populated for video and hybrid format interviews. Sent to candidate and client as part of confirmation communications.']
  vms_req_number varchar [note: 'The requisition or interview reference number assigned by the client\'s Vendor Management System (VMS) such as Beeline. Required for MSP/VMS program reconciliation and cross-system traceability.']

  Note: 'Represents a client interview event for a submitted candidate, covering the full interview lifecycle from scheduling through feedback collection and hiring recommendation. Captures interview date/time, type (phone screen, video, onsite, panel), format, client interviewer names, candidate confirmation status, coordinator, location or video link, reschedule/cancellation tracking, overall rating, individual competency ratings, hire recommendation (yes/no/maybe), detailed feedback notes, feedback submission date, decision outcome (advance, reject, hold), and rejection reason code. Central to interview coordination workflows, scheduling efficiency metrics, QoS scoring, and submittal-to-hire ratio analytics. Single source of truth for all interview scheduling and feedback data.'
}

Table recruitment.interview_feedback {
  interview_feedback_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each interview feedback record. Primary key for the interview_feedback data product.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the resulting placement record if the candidate was hired following this interview. Nullable; populated only when decision_outcome results in a hire. Enables end-to-end TTF and fall-off rate tracking.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client contact record representing the interviewer on the client side. Supports client relationship management and interviewer-level analytics.']
  interview_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.interview. Business justification: Interview feedback documents a specific interview event. Currently interview_feedback links to submittal, profile, req_id, and client_contact, but lacks a direct FK to the interview event itself. This']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) for which the candidate was interviewed. Enables time-to-fill (TTF) and submittal-to-hire ratio reporting at the requisition level.']
  performance_scorecard_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scorecard template used to structure the interview evaluation. Links feedback to the specific competency framework applied, enabling cross-requisition comparability of ratings.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate who was interviewed. Used to track interview outcomes across a candidate\'s recruitment lifecycle and compute fall-off rates.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal recruiter who managed the submittal and is accountable for this feedback outcome. Enables recruiter-level QoS scoring, submittal-to-hire ratio, and performance analytics.']
  submittal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate submittal record that led to this interview. Links feedback to the specific submission event for Quality of Submission (QoS) scoring and submittal-to-hire ratio analytics.']
  availability_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate\'s start date availability was confirmed as acceptable during the interview. Directly impacts time-to-start (TTS) KPI and assignment scheduling.']
  communication_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Interviewer\'s numeric rating of the candidate\'s verbal and written communication skills. A key competency dimension in staffing QoS scoring models.']
  concerns_noted varchar [note: 'Interviewer\'s free-text description of concerns or gaps identified during the interview. Supports recruiter coaching, sourcing refinement, and risk assessment for placement quality.']
  confirmed_start_date date [note: 'Candidate\'s confirmed available start date as discussed and agreed during the interview. Used to compute time-to-start (TTS) and coordinate onboarding scheduling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interview feedback record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA measurement for feedback turnaround time.']
  cultural_fit_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Interviewer\'s numeric rating of the candidate\'s alignment with the client organization\'s culture and values. Supports retention prediction and placement quality analytics.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Formal disposition decision made following the interview: advance to next stage, reject, hold for future consideration, offer extended, or candidate withdrawn. Drives ATS workflow progression and TTF analytics.']
  experience_relevance_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Interviewer\'s numeric rating of how relevant the candidate\'s prior work experience is to the specific job order requirements. Informs recruiter sourcing quality feedback loops.']
  feedback_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the client submitted the interview feedback (e.g., ATS portal, VMS portal, email, phone). Supports process improvement and digital adoption analytics.']
  feedback_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the client was expected to submit interview feedback per the agreed SLA. Enables SLA compliance tracking and client responsiveness KPI reporting.']
  feedback_notes varchar [note: 'Qualitative free-text feedback provided by the interviewer summarizing the candidate\'s performance, strengths, and areas of concern. Primary narrative input for recruiter coaching and QoS analysis.']
  feedback_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric identifier for this feedback record, used in client communications, ATS workflow references, and audit trails. Distinct from the internal surrogate key.']
  feedback_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the feedback record: pending (awaiting client input), submitted (feedback received), overdue (past due date without submission), or cancelled (interview cancelled). Drives recruiter follow-up workflows.']
  feedback_submission_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the interviewer formally submitted the feedback record. Used to measure client responsiveness SLA (time from interview to feedback receipt) and track SLA compliance.']
  hire_recommendation varchar [note: 'Interviewer\'s explicit hire recommendation for the candidate: \'yes\' (recommend hire), \'no\' (do not recommend), or \'maybe\' (conditional/further evaluation needed). Core input to the hiring decision workflow and submittal-to-hire ratio KPI.']
  interview_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the interview was conducted. Used to compute time-to-fill (TTF), time-to-start (TTS), and interview-to-offer cycle time KPIs.']
  interview_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the interview in minutes as reported or recorded. Provides context for feedback depth and supports interviewer efficiency analytics.']
  interview_round varchar [note: 'Sequential round number of the interview within the hiring process for this job order (e.g., 1 = first round, 2 = second round). Enables stage-level funnel analysis and time-to-fill tracking.']
  interview_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the interview began. Supports scheduling analytics, interviewer availability tracking, and SLA measurement for client responsiveness.']
  interview_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the interview format conducted. Drives analytics on interview stage conversion rates and informs QoS scoring methodology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phone_screen|video|in_person|panel|technical|behavioral|case_study|group — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  interviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the client-side interviewer who conducted the interview and submitted this feedback. Used for interviewer-level QoS analytics and client relationship management. Classified as confidential business data.']
  interviewer_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the client-side interviewer. Provides context for the seniority level of the evaluation and supports analysis of hiring decision authority.']
  is_structured_interview boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interview followed a standardized, structured format with predefined competency-based questions. Supports EEOC compliance documentation and interview quality analytics.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this interview feedback record. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental Silver layer processing.']
  next_step_recommendation varchar [note: 'Interviewer\'s recommended next action for the candidate (e.g., \'schedule second round\', \'extend offer\', \'consider for future role\'). Guides recruiter workflow and accelerates time-to-fill.']
  overall_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Composite numeric rating assigned by the interviewer to the candidate\'s overall performance, typically on a 1.0–5.0 scale. Primary input to QoS (Quality of Submission) scoring and submittal-to-hire ratio analytics.']
  professionalism_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Interviewer\'s numeric rating of the candidate\'s professional demeanor, presentation, and conduct during the interview. Contributes to overall QoS scoring.']
  qos_contribution_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score derived from this feedback record\'s ratings that contributes to the recruiter\'s overall Quality of Submission (QoS) metric. Calculated at the feedback level before aggregation into the recruiter-level QoS KPI. Distinct from the aggregated KPI — this is the raw per-feedback input value.']
  redeployment_candidate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interviewer or recruiter flagged this candidate as suitable for redeployment to another open requisition if not selected for the current role. Supports bench management and redeployment analytics.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for a rejection decision outcome. Populated only when decision_outcome = \'reject\'. Enables root-cause analysis of fall-off rates and recruiter sourcing quality improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: skills_mismatch|salary_expectations|culture_fit|overqualified|underqualified|communication|availability|withdrew|other — promote to reference product]']
  rejection_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text elaboration of the rejection reason, providing additional context beyond the standardized rejection_reason_code. Supports recruiter coaching and sourcing quality improvement.']
  salary_expectation_fit varchar [note: 'Interviewer\'s assessment of whether the candidate\'s salary expectations align with the budgeted bill rate and pay rate range for the position. Informs offer management and rejection reason analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this feedback record originated (e.g., Bullhorn ATS, Beeline VMS, Salesforce, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver layer.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this feedback record in the originating operational system (e.g., Bullhorn interview feedback ID). Enables traceability and deduplication during Silver layer ingestion.']
  strengths_noted varchar [note: 'Interviewer\'s free-text description of the candidate\'s key strengths observed during the interview. Used in recruiter feedback loops and candidate profile enrichment.']
  technical_skills_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Interviewer\'s numeric rating of the candidate\'s technical skills and domain knowledge relevant to the job order requirements. Feeds competency-level QoS analytics.']

  Note: 'Captures client feedback and evaluation results following a candidate interview. Records overall rating, individual competency ratings, hire recommendation (yes/no/maybe), feedback notes, feedback submission date, interviewer name, decision outcome (advance, reject, hold), and rejection reason code. Feeds into QoS scoring and submittal-to-hire ratio analytics.'
}

Table recruitment.hiring_decision {
  hiring_decision_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the hiring decision record. Primary key for the hiring_decision data product in the recruitment domain. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  accrual_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accrual. Business justification: Hiring decisions require immediate accrual of placement fees, recruiter commissions, onboarding costs, and background check expenses before invoicing occurs. Critical for accurate period-end financial']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement record created as a result of a positive hiring decision. Populated only when decision_type is \'hired\'. Links the recruitment lifecycle to placement initiation.']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.bgc_order. Business justification: Hiring decisions depend on background check completion and results. Links decision record to the specific BGC that informed it, supporting audit trails and compliance documentation. Essential for trac']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client contact who rendered the hiring decision. Represents the decision maker on the client side, used for accountability and relationship management reporting.']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Final hiring decisions verify all required credentials are current and valid. Tracks which credential instances cleared the candidate for hire, supporting compliance audits and client requirement veri']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) for which this hiring decision was made. Links the decision to the originating client requirement.']
  placement_compliance_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.placement_compliance_check. Business justification: Hiring decisions depend on compliance clearance status (BGC pass/fail, drug screen results, credential verification). This link tracks which compliance check record influenced the hiring decision outc']
  primary_hiring_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal recruiter responsible for managing this submittal and hiring decision. Used for recruiter performance reporting, TTF attribution, and commission calculations.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate profile for whom the hiring decision was rendered. Supports candidate lifecycle tracking and redeployment analytics.']
  revenue_recognition_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_recognition_event. Business justification: Hiring decisions trigger revenue recognition for placement fees under ASC 606 - performance obligation is satisfied when candidate accepts offer and starts work. Finance must create revenue recognitio']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Hiring decisions consume SOW headcount allocations and budget. Real business process: SOW capacity management, financial tracking, and deliverable fulfillment reporting to clients.']
  submittal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate submittal record that this hiring decision evaluates. Links the hiring decision back to the specific submittal in the recruitment lifecycle.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Hiring decisions attribute placements to suppliers. Core to supplier scorecard calculation (fill rate, time-to-fill), tier promotion/demotion decisions, performance-based allocation adjustments, and s']
  ats_decision_ref varchar [note: 'The native record identifier for this hiring decision in the source Applicant Tracking System (ATS), typically Bullhorn. Used for cross-system data lineage and reconciliation.']
  bgc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a background check (BGC) is required for this placement as a condition of the hiring decision. Drives credentialing workflow initiation and compliance tracking.']
  bgc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the background check process associated with this hiring decision. Used to gate placement initiation and ensure compliance with client credentialing requirements.']
  bill_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The agreed hourly or unit bill rate to be charged to the client for this placement at the time of hiring decision. Confidential commercial rate used for revenue recognition and margin analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hiring decision record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking per SOC 2 and GDPR requirements.']
  decision_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the client rendered the final hiring decision. Principal business event date used for TTF and TTS KPI calculations and SLA compliance measurement.']
  decision_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the recruiter or account manager regarding the hiring decision. Captures context, client feedback, and any special circumstances not covered by structured fields.']
  decision_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this hiring decision record, used for cross-system reference and audit trail. Typically sourced from Bullhorn ATS or VMS system.']
  decision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hiring decision. \'pending\' indicates awaiting client response; \'hired\' confirms placement initiation; \'rejected\' closes the submittal negatively; \'on_hold\' suspends evaluation; \'withdrawn\' indicates candidate or client retraction; \'backfill\' flags a replacement hire scenario.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the hiring decision was recorded in the system. Used for audit trail, SLA measurement, and time-series analytics. Distinct from decision_date which captures the business event date.']
  decision_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the hiring decision outcome. \'hired\' triggers placement initiation; \'rejected\' records a negative outcome; \'on_hold\' defers the decision; \'backfill\' indicates a replacement for a vacated position; \'no_decision\' records client non-response.']
  delivery_model varchar [note: 'Indicates the recruitment delivery model under which this hiring decision was made. \'direct\' is standard staffing; \'rpo\' is Recruitment Process Outsourcing; \'msp\' is Managed Service Provider; \'vms\' is Vendor Management System-driven; \'sow\' is Statement of Work engagement.']
  eeoc_category varchar [note: 'EEOC-defined job category classification for this hiring decision, used for EEO-1 reporting and OFCCP compliance. Restricted as it may be linked to individual candidate records. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: executive_senior_officials|first_mid_level_officials|professionals|technicians|sales_workers|administrative_support|craft_workers|operatives|laborers|service_workers — promote to reference product]']
  fall_off_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hiring decision has been flagged as at risk of a fall-off (placement that does not start or ends prematurely). Used for proactive placement management and fall-off rate KPI tracking.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'FLSA worker classification applicable to this placement at the time of hiring decision. Determines overtime eligibility, pay rate compliance, and worker classification risk. Critical for DOL compliance.']
  interview_round_count varchar [note: 'Total number of interview rounds conducted with the candidate prior to the hiring decision. Used for process efficiency analytics and QoS (Quality of Submission) measurement.']
  is_backfill boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hiring decision is for a backfill position — replacing a worker who has left or been reassigned. Used for workforce planning analytics and headcount management.']
  is_temp_to_perm boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hire resulted from a temp-to-perm (contract-to-hire) conversion, where a temporary worker transitions to a permanent employee of the client. Drives conversion fee billing and fall-off rate tracking.']
  offer_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate accepted the job offer. Used to calculate offer acceptance rate, a key recruitment KPI, and to distinguish hired decisions from declined offers.']
  offer_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal job offer was extended to the candidate. Used for offer-to-acceptance cycle time analytics and recruitment funnel reporting.']
  offer_declined_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided when a candidate declines a job offer. Used for competitive intelligence, compensation benchmarking, and recruiter coaching. Populated only when offer_accepted is False.']
  offer_extended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal job offer was extended to the candidate as part of this hiring decision. Distinguishes between decisions where an offer was made versus decisions made prior to offer stage.']
  on_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why a hiring decision has been placed on hold. Populated when decision_type or decision_status is \'on_hold\'. Used for pipeline management and client communication tracking.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The agreed hourly or unit pay rate for the candidate at the time of hiring decision. Confidential compensation data used for spread and gross margin calculation.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the intended placement type associated with this hiring decision. Drives billing model, compliance requirements, and downstream payroll processing. Aligns with job order placement type.']
  proposed_start_date date [note: 'The candidate\'s proposed or agreed-upon start date at the time of the hiring decision. Used for TTS calculation, workforce scheduling, and onboarding initiation.']
  qos_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the Quality of Submission (QoS) for this candidate submittal as assessed at the time of hiring decision. Measures how well the submitted candidate matched the job requirements. Used for recruiter performance and sourcing quality analytics.']
  rate_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the bill rate and pay rate. Supports multi-currency operations for international staffing engagements.']
  rejection_reason_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorical classification of the reason a candidate was rejected by the client. Used for QoS (Quality of Submission) analytics, recruiter performance measurement, and sourcing strategy improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: skills_mismatch|compensation|culture_fit|overqualified|availability|client_cancelled|background_check|communication|experience_gap|position_filled_internally — promote to reference product if more than 6 values needed]']
  rejection_reason_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing specific detail about why the candidate was rejected by the client. Complements rejection_reason_category with granular context for recruiter coaching and QoS improvement.']
  sourcing_channel varchar [note: 'The recruitment sourcing channel through which the hired or evaluated candidate was originally identified. Used for sourcing effectiveness analytics and channel ROI measurement.']
  submittal_to_decision_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days elapsed from the candidate submittal date to the hiring decision date. Measures client responsiveness and interview cycle efficiency. Used in SLA measurement and client engagement analytics.']
  ttf_days varchar [note: 'Calculated number of calendar days from job order open date to the date a hire decision was made. Core recruitment KPI measuring speed of talent acquisition. Stored as a calculated value at decision time for historical reporting and SLA compliance.']
  tts_days varchar [note: 'Calculated number of calendar days from the hire decision date to the candidate\'s confirmed start date on assignment. Measures onboarding and placement initiation speed. Stored at decision time for KPI reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hiring decision record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  vms_req_number varchar [note: 'The requisition number assigned by the client\'s Vendor Management System (VMS) for this job order. Used for cross-system reconciliation in MSP/VMS-driven delivery models.']

  Note: 'Records the final hiring decision made by the client for a candidate submittal. Captures decision date, decision type (hired, rejected, on-hold, backfill), decision maker (client contact), rejection reason category, rejection reason detail, time-to-fill (TTF) calculated value in days, time-to-start (TTS) calculated value in days, and whether the hire resulted from a temp-to-perm conversion. Links the recruitment lifecycle to placement initiation.'
}

Table recruitment.req_pipeline {
  req_pipeline_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each candidate pipeline record within a requisition. Primary key for the req_pipeline data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete recruitment pipeline event with a full lifecycle from sourcing through hire or rejection.']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.bgc_order. Business justification: Pipeline tracking includes BGC status as a stage gate. Links pipeline record to background check in progress, enabling bottleneck analysis and SLA monitoring. Essential for visibility into credentiali']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hiring manager or client contact associated with this pipeline record. Used for interview scheduling, hiring decision tracking, and client visibility dashboards.']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Pipeline stages track credential readiness as candidates progress. Links candidate\'s pipeline position to their credential status, supporting readiness reporting and identifying credentialing bottlene']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order or requisition (Req) that this pipeline record belongs to. Links the candidate progression to the specific open position being filled.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate whose progression through the recruitment pipeline is being tracked. Core party reference linking pipeline activity to the candidate profile.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal recruiter or sourcing specialist responsible for managing this candidate\'s progression through the pipeline. Used for recruiter workload management and performance KPI attribution.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Pipeline tracking by supplier enables VMS program management: allocation enforcement, supplier capacity monitoring, bottleneck identification, and stage-level performance analysis. Essential for MSP o']
  vms_submittal_id varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by the Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, for this candidate submittal. Used to reconcile pipeline records between the internal ATS and the client\'s VMS for MSP/RPO program reporting.']
  ats_submission_code varchar [note: 'Native submission or candidate-job record identifier from the Bullhorn ATS/CRM system. Used for data lineage, system reconciliation, and cross-referencing pipeline records with the source system.']
  client_feedback varchar [note: 'Structured or free-text feedback provided by the client or hiring manager following a submittal or interview. Used for QoS improvement, recruiter coaching, and submittal-to-hire ratio analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pipeline record was first created in the system, typically triggered by the initial sourcing or submittal event in Bullhorn ATS. Audit trail field for data lineage.']
  delivery_model varchar [note: 'Recruitment delivery model under which this pipeline record is managed. Indicates whether the requisition is fulfilled through direct staffing, Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO), Managed Service Provider (MSP), or Vendor Management System (VMS) channel.']
  expected_start_date date [note: 'Anticipated start date for the candidate if hired, as agreed between the client and the staffing firm. Used for time-to-start (TTS) forecasting and workforce scheduling.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the candidate was formally hired or selected for the position. Terminal milestone used to calculate time-to-fill (TTF) and submittal-to-hire ratio KPIs.']
  interview_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the interview was completed. Distinct from the scheduled timestamp; used to measure interview-to-decision cycle time and pipeline velocity.']
  interview_scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the interview was scheduled with the client or hiring manager. Used for interview scheduling management and pipeline stage duration calculations.']
  interview_type varchar [note: 'Type of interview conducted or scheduled for the candidate at the interviewing stage of the pipeline. Used for interview scheduling management and hiring manager visibility dashboards.']
  is_aging_alert boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pipeline record has exceeded the expected stage duration threshold and triggered an aging alert. Used by recruiters and hiring managers to prioritize action on stalled candidates.']
  is_bottleneck_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pipeline record has been flagged as a bottleneck based on stage conversion rate analysis. Used for pipeline health monitoring and TTF forecasting in RPO and MSP delivery models.']
  is_priority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pipeline record has been marked as a priority by the recruiter or account manager, typically for urgent requisitions or high-value client accounts. Drives recruiter workload prioritization.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this pipeline record, refreshed on each stage transition event to maintain current-state accuracy in the Silver Layer.']
  offer_accepted_date date [note: 'Date the candidate formally accepted the offer. Used to calculate offer acceptance rate and time-to-start (TTS) metrics.']
  offer_bill_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Bill rate (per hour or per annum) associated with the offer for this pipeline record. Used to calculate spread (bill rate minus pay rate) and gross margin at the pipeline stage.']
  offer_extended_date date [note: 'Date a formal offer was extended to the candidate. Marks the entry into the offered pipeline stage and is used to calculate offer-to-acceptance cycle time.']
  offer_pay_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Pay rate (per hour or per annum) offered to the candidate at the offer stage. Used for offer competitiveness analysis and spread/markup calculations. Confidential business data.']
  pipeline_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the recruiter or account manager regarding the candidate\'s progression, client feedback, or special circumstances for this pipeline record. Supports recruiter workload management and hiring manager communication.']
  pipeline_record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this pipeline record, typically sourced from Bullhorn ATS (e.g., SUB-2024-00123). Used in VMS scorecards, recruiter dashboards, and RPO/MSP reporting to uniquely reference a candidate-to-requisition progression record.']
  pipeline_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the candidate within the recruitment pipeline for this requisition. Drives stage conversion rate analytics, bottleneck detection, and time-to-fill (TTF) forecasting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sourced|screened|submitted|interviewing|offered|hired|rejected|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']
  pipeline_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this pipeline record indicating whether the candidate\'s progression is actively being managed, placed on hold, closed due to hire or rejection, or archived for historical reference.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'Type of placement being pursued for this pipeline record. Distinguishes between temporary staffing, contract-to-hire (temp-to-perm), and direct/permanent placement, which affects billing, compliance, and conversion fee calculations.']
  previous_stage varchar [note: 'The pipeline stage immediately preceding the current stage for this record. Enables stage transition analysis and supports pipeline velocity reporting without requiring a full history table join. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sourced|screened|submitted|interviewing|offered|hired|rejected|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  qos_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quality of Submission (QoS) score assigned to this pipeline record, reflecting how well the submitted candidate matches the job order requirements. Used in VMS scorecard reporting and recruiter performance management.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Basis on which the pay rate and bill rate are expressed for this pipeline record (e.g., hourly, annual, daily, per diem). Required for accurate spread and gross margin calculations.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why a candidate was rejected or withdrew from the pipeline. Used for fall-off rate analysis, QoS (Quality of Submission) improvement, and EEOC adverse action tracking. Null if candidate has not been rejected or withdrawn.']
  rejection_stage varchar [note: 'Pipeline stage at which the candidate was rejected or withdrew. Enables stage-level fall-off rate analysis and identification of pipeline bottlenecks. Null if candidate has not been rejected or withdrawn. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sourced|screened|submitted|interviewing|offered|hired|rejected|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rtr_confirmed_date date [note: 'Date the Right to Represent (RTR) authorization was confirmed by the candidate. Used for compliance auditing and to ensure submittals are made only with valid RTR.']
  rtr_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Right to Represent (RTR) authorization obtained from the candidate before submittal to the client. Tracks whether RTR is pending, confirmed, expired, or not required. Critical for compliance with co-employment and exclusive representation rules.']
  sourced_date date [note: 'Date the candidate was first identified and added to the pipeline for this requisition. Marks the start of the recruitment lifecycle for TTF and TTS (Time to Start) calculations.']
  sourcing_channel varchar [note: 'Channel or method through which the candidate was originally sourced for this requisition (e.g., job board, employee referral, direct sourcing, VMS portal). Used for sourcing effectiveness analytics and RPO campaign performance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: job_board|referral|direct_source|social_media|agency|internal_database|vms_portal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stage_duration_business_days varchar [note: 'Number of business days the candidate has spent or spent in the current pipeline stage. Calculated at each stage transition event refresh. Used for bottleneck identification and SLA compliance monitoring.']
  stage_entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the candidate entered the current pipeline stage. Used to calculate stage duration in business days and identify aging alerts for time-to-fill (TTF) forecasting.']
  stage_exit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the candidate exited the current pipeline stage and transitioned to the next stage or a terminal state. Null if the candidate is still in the current stage.']
  stage_transition_count varchar [note: 'Total number of stage transitions this pipeline record has undergone since creation. Used to identify candidates who have been recycled through stages or experienced unusual pipeline paths.']
  worker_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the worker engagement type for this pipeline record. Distinguishes between W-2 employees, 1099 independent contractors (IC), corp-to-corp arrangements, and full-time equivalents (FTE). Critical for IRS worker classification compliance and burden rate calculations.']

  Note: 'Real-time snapshot of candidate progression through recruitment stages for a specific job order or requisition. Tracks pipeline stage (sourced, screened, submitted, interviewing, offered, hired, rejected, withdrawn), stage entry/exit timestamps, stage duration in business days, candidate counts per stage, and pipeline health indicators including stage conversion rates, aging alerts, and bottleneck flags. Primary tool for recruiter workload management, hiring manager visibility dashboards, VMS scorecard reporting, and time-to-fill forecasting in RPO and MSP delivery models. Refreshed on each stage transition event to maintain current-state accuracy.'
}

Table recruitment.candidate_screening {
  candidate_screening_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each candidate screening record. Primary key for the candidate_screening data product. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — entity represents a discrete recruitment screening event with its own lifecycle.']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Initial screenings verify candidate claims required credentials (licenses, certifications). Links screening to the credentials being verified, supporting early disqualification of non-compliant candid']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) for which this candidate was screened. Ties the screening activity to a specific open position or req.']
  prior_screening_candidate_screening_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous screening record if this is a re-screen (is_rescreening = True). Enables longitudinal tracking of candidate screening history and improvement over time.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate who was screened. Links this screening record to the candidate master profile in the candidate domain.']
  screening_template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the standardized screening template or scorecard used to conduct this screening. Ensures consistency across recruiters and supports structured evaluation for EEOC compliance.']
  submittal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate submittal record created as a result of this screening, if the candidate was advanced. Links the screening outcome to the downstream submittal and client interview pipeline.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Screenings performed by or for specific suppliers in supplier-managed screening models. Tracks supplier quality control processes, pre-submittal screening compliance, and enables supplier-level screen']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the screening session concluded. Used to calculate actual screening duration and assess thoroughness of the screening process.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the screening session began. Compared against scheduled_start_time to measure punctuality and recruiter/candidate adherence to schedule.']
  advanced_to_submittal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate was advanced to the submittal stage following this screening. Key metric for submittal-to-hire ratio and QoS (Quality of Submission) reporting. True = candidate was submitted to the client.']
  availability_date date [note: 'The earliest date the candidate confirmed they are available to start an assignment or permanent role, as captured during the screening. Key input for TTS (Time to Start) KPI calculations.']
  concerns_noted varchar [note: 'Structured or free-text summary of concerns or red flags identified during the screening. Used for conditional pass decisions, additional screening requirements, and EEOC-compliant documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this screening record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage, SLA measurement, and TTF KPI calculations from screening initiation.']
  disqualification_category varchar [note: 'High-level category classifying the reason for candidate disqualification. Supports EEOC/OFCCP adverse action reporting and pipeline analytics to identify systemic screening bottlenecks.']
  disqualification_reason varchar [note: 'Structured reason code or description explaining why a candidate did not pass the screening (e.g., insufficient experience, salary mismatch, availability conflict, skills gap). Required for EEOC adverse action documentation and fall-off rate analysis.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the screening session in minutes, as recorded by the screener. Used for recruiter productivity analytics and screening quality benchmarking.']
  eeoc_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the screening was conducted in accordance with EEOC (Equal Employment Opportunity Commission) guidelines, including use of job-related, non-discriminatory questions and standardized evaluation criteria.']
  is_rescreening boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this screening is a re-screen of a candidate who was previously screened for the same or a different position. Supports fall-off rate analysis and redeployment tracking for bench candidates.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this screening record was most recently modified. Supports data lineage, change tracking, and Silver layer incremental load processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'The result of the screening activity indicating whether the candidate passed, failed, received a conditional pass, withdrew, or needs rescheduling. Drives the next step in the recruitment pipeline and submittal decision.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numeric score assigned to the candidate following the screening, typically on a standardized scale (e.g., 0–100). Supports QoS (Quality of Submission) metrics and submittal-to-hire ratio analysis.']
  passing_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum score required for a candidate to pass this screening type. Used to automate pass/fail determination and ensure consistent, objective evaluation standards across recruiters.']
  pay_rate_expectation decimal(10,2) [note: 'The candidate\'s stated pay rate or salary expectation captured during the screening, expressed as an hourly or annual figure per the pay_rate_expectation_type. Critical for assessing fit against the job order\'s bill rate and spread targets.']
  pay_rate_expectation_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate\'s pay rate expectation is expressed as an hourly, annual, daily, or weekly rate. Required to correctly interpret and compare pay_rate_expectation against job order rate cards.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'The type of placement the candidate is being screened for (e.g., temporary, temp-to-perm, contract-to-hire, direct placement, RPO). Influences screening criteria, scoring thresholds, and downstream workflow.']
  relocation_willingness boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate expressed willingness to relocate for the position during the screening. Relevant for permanent placement and direct hire roles where geography is a key fit criterion.']
  rtr_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate\'s Right to Represent (RTR) consent was obtained. Required for audit trails and to establish the timeline of exclusive representation rights.']
  rtr_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recruiter obtained the candidate\'s Right to Represent (RTR) consent during or following this screening. RTR is a critical compliance step in staffing to prevent double-submission conflicts and protect the agency\'s exclusive right to submit the candidate to a specific client.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the screening was scheduled to conclude. Used to calculate planned screening duration and manage recruiter calendar blocks.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the screening was scheduled to begin. Used for calendar management, recruiter scheduling, and candidate communication. Distinct from screening_date which captures the day-level date for reporting.']
  score_scale_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum possible score on the scoring scale used for this screening. Enables normalization of scores across different screening types and tools for consistent QoS benchmarking.']
  score_scale_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum possible score on the scoring scale used for this screening. Required alongside score_scale_max to correctly interpret and normalize the overall_score.']
  screener_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the recruiter, hiring manager, or interviewer who conducted the screening. Used for accountability, workload reporting, and quality-of-submission (QoS) analysis by screener.']
  screener_role varchar [note: 'The organizational role of the person who conducted the screening (e.g., recruiter, account manager, hiring manager, technical lead). Supports QoS analysis and ensures appropriate screening authority.']
  screening_channel varchar [note: 'The medium or channel through which the screening was conducted (e.g., phone, video conference, in-person, email, chat). Distinct from screening_type which captures the nature of the assessment.']
  screening_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the screening activity was conducted or is scheduled to occur. Used for TTF (Time to Fill) and TTS (Time to Start) KPI calculations and recruiter activity reporting.']
  screening_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the screener capturing qualitative observations, candidate responses, behavioral indicators, and overall impressions. Confidential and used by recruiters and account managers for submittal decisions.']
  screening_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this screening event, used in recruiter communications, ATS tracking, and audit trails (e.g., SCR-2024-00123). Sourced from Bullhorn ATS interview/screening record number.']
  screening_source varchar [note: 'Indicates how the candidate entered this screening event — whether from the ATS pipeline, an inbound application, a referral, a sourcing campaign, the talent pool, or a VMS requisition. Supports sourcing effectiveness analytics.']
  screening_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the screening event. Tracks whether the screening has been scheduled, is in progress, has been completed, was cancelled, or the candidate was a no-show. Drives recruiter workflow and ATS pipeline stage updates.']
  screening_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the screening activity performed. Distinguishes between phone screens, skills assessments, behavioral interviews, technical tests, video screens, and panel interviews. Distinct from credentialing activities (BGC, drug screen) which are owned by the credentialing domain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phone_screen|skills_assessment|behavioral_interview|technical_test|video_screen|panel_interview|structured_interview|competency_interview — promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this screening record originated (e.g., Bullhorn ATS, SAP SuccessFactors, Beeline VMS, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and Silver layer reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this screening record in the originating operational system (e.g., Bullhorn interview ID). Enables traceability from the Silver layer back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit.']
  strengths_noted varchar [note: 'Structured or free-text summary of the candidate\'s key strengths identified during the screening. Used to craft compelling submittal write-ups and support QoS metrics.']
  work_authorization_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate\'s work authorization status (e.g., U.S. work eligibility) was verbally confirmed during the screening. Supports I-9 and E-Verify readiness tracking. Distinct from formal I-9 verification owned by the compliance domain.']

  Note: 'Records the results of candidate screening activities performed during the recruitment process. Captures screening type (phone screen, skills assessment, behavioral interview, technical test), screening date, screener name, screening outcome (pass, fail, conditional), scoring details, notes, disqualification reason, and whether the candidate was advanced to submittal. Distinct from credentialing (BGC, drug screen) which is owned by the credentialing domain.'
}

Table recruitment.sourcing_activity {
  sourcing_activity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each individual sourcing activity record. Primary key for the sourcing_activity data product. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client account for whom this sourcing activity was performed. Enables client-level sourcing effort attribution and SLA performance tracking.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the job order (requisition) for which this sourcing activity was performed. Links sourcing effort to a specific open req for TTF and productivity analytics.']
  platform_job_posting_id varchar [note: 'External identifier of the job posting on the sourcing platform (e.g., Indeed job ID, LinkedIn job post ID) associated with this sourcing activity. Enables cross-platform posting performance tracking.']
  sourcing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sourcing campaign this activity is associated with. Enables aggregation of activities under a campaign for channel effectiveness and ROI reporting.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recruiter who performed this sourcing activity. Links to the internal employee/user record in the HR system. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Sourcing activities can be supplier-specific in distributed sourcing models where suppliers conduct targeted searches. Enables tracking supplier sourcing effort, channel effectiveness, and cost-per-hi']
  activity_date timestamp [note: 'The real-world date and time when the sourcing activity was performed by the recruiter (e.g., when the LinkedIn outreach was sent, when the Boolean search was executed). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  activity_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Time in minutes spent by the recruiter performing this sourcing activity. Used for recruiter time allocation analysis, productivity benchmarking, and cost-per-source calculations.']
  activity_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the sourcing activity, used in recruiter dashboards, campaign reports, and ATS references (e.g., SA-2024-00123). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  activity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sourcing activity record. Tracks whether the activity was planned, actively executed, completed, cancelled, or placed on hold. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the type of sourcing action performed by the recruiter. Drives channel effectiveness analysis and recruiter productivity reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linkedin_outreach|job_board_search|referral_contact|ats_search|boolean_search|campus_event|cold_call|email_blast|social_media|networking_event|internal_mobility|agency_referral — promote to reference product]']
  ats_search_saved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the search query used in this sourcing activity was saved in the ATS for future reuse. Supports search strategy management and recruiter efficiency tracking.']
  candidates_contacted_count varchar [note: 'Number of candidates directly contacted or engaged during this sourcing activity (e.g., InMails sent, emails sent, calls made). Measures outreach volume and recruiter engagement effort.']
  candidates_identified_count varchar [note: 'Number of candidate profiles identified or surfaced during this sourcing activity. Used to measure top-of-funnel sourcing volume and recruiter productivity.']
  candidates_responded_count varchar [note: 'Number of candidates who responded positively to outreach during this sourcing activity. Used to compute response rate and assess channel engagement quality.']
  candidates_submitted_count varchar [note: 'Number of candidates from this sourcing activity who were formally submitted to a job order as a submittal. Connects sourcing effort to downstream QoS (Quality of Submission) metrics.']
  channel_category varchar [note: 'Higher-level grouping of the sourcing channel for roll-up reporting (e.g., job_board, social_media, referral, direct_outreach, event, internal). Enables cross-channel comparison at the category level.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sourcing activity record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  delivery_model varchar [note: 'The recruitment delivery model under which this sourcing activity was performed. Distinguishes RPO (Recruitment Process Outsourcing), MSP (Managed Service Provider), internal recruiting, direct placement, or hybrid models for program-level reporting.']
  diversity_sourcing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sourcing activity was specifically targeted at diverse candidate pools as part of a diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) sourcing initiative. Supports EEOC and OFCCP compliance reporting.']
  employment_type_target varchar [note: 'The employment type being sourced for during this activity (e.g., contract W-2, temp-to-perm, direct hire, 1099 independent contractor, per diem). Aligns sourcing effort with placement type requirements.']
  external_activity_ref varchar [note: 'The identifier of this activity record in the originating external system (e.g., Bullhorn activity ID, LinkedIn InMail thread ID). Enables cross-system traceability and deduplication.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (e.g., 2024-Q2, 2024-W23) in which this sourcing activity occurred. Enables period-over-period sourcing volume and productivity reporting aligned to financial reporting cycles.']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the follow-up action associated with this sourcing activity. Used by recruiters to manage outreach cadence and pipeline progression.']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up action is required after this sourcing activity (e.g., re-contact a candidate, revisit a search). Drives recruiter task management and pipeline hygiene.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the recruiter describing observations, outcomes, or context for this sourcing activity (e.g., market conditions, candidate feedback, search refinements). Supports qualitative analysis.']
  outreach_message_template varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the message template used for candidate outreach during this activity (e.g., LinkedIn InMail template, email campaign template). Supports A/B testing and message effectiveness analysis.']
  pay_rate_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum pay rate (in USD) being offered or targeted for candidates sourced in this activity. Defines the upper compensation boundary for candidate qualification and spread analysis.']
  pay_rate_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum pay rate (in USD) being offered or targeted for candidates sourced in this activity. Used to qualify candidates against compensation expectations and compute spread/markup.']
  response_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of candidates who responded to the total candidates contacted during this activity (candidates_responded_count / candidates_contacted_count). Stored as a decimal (e.g., 0.2500 = 25%). Enables channel effectiveness benchmarking.']
  rtr_obtained_count varchar [note: 'Number of candidates from this sourcing activity for whom Right to Represent (RTR) authorization was obtained. RTR is a critical compliance and competitive metric in staffing, preventing double-submission conflicts.']
  search_query_text varchar [note: 'The Boolean search string, keyword query, or search criteria used during ATS or job board search activities. Captures the exact search logic for reproducibility and search strategy auditing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this sourcing activity record originated (e.g., Bullhorn ATS, Beeline VMS, LinkedIn Recruiter, Indeed, manual entry). Supports data lineage and cross-system reconciliation.']
  sourcing_channel varchar [note: 'The specific platform, tool, or medium used to perform the sourcing activity (e.g., LinkedIn Recruiter, Indeed, Dice, internal ATS, referral network). Feeds channel effectiveness and cost-per-source reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linkedin|indeed|dice|careerbuilder|ziprecruiter|internal_ats|monster|glassdoor|referral|campus|social_media|cold_call — promote to reference product]']
  sourcing_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Direct monetary cost incurred for this sourcing activity (e.g., job board posting fee, LinkedIn Recruiter seat cost allocation, event attendance cost). Used for cost-per-hire and cost-per-source analytics.']
  sourcing_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the sourcing team or delivery model responsible for this activity (e.g., RPO team, MSP delivery, internal recruiting, offshore sourcing hub). Supports team-level productivity and delivery model analytics.']
  target_location varchar [note: 'Geographic area or market targeted during this sourcing activity (e.g., city, metro area, state, remote). Used for geographic sourcing coverage and market penetration analysis.']
  target_skill_set varchar [note: 'Primary skill set or competency area being sourced for during this activity (e.g., Java Developer, Registered Nurse, CDL Driver). Enables skill-level sourcing effectiveness analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sourcing activity record was last modified. Supports change tracking and incremental data pipeline processing. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  vms_req_number varchar [note: 'The requisition number assigned by the client\'s Vendor Management System (VMS) such as Beeline, associated with the job order being sourced for. Required for MSP/VMS program compliance and cross-system reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Granular transactional record of individual sourcing actions taken by recruiters to identify and engage candidates. Captures activity type (LinkedIn outreach, job board search, referral contact, ATS search, Boolean search, campus event), activity date, recruiter performing the activity, sourcing channel used, number of candidates identified, number of candidates contacted, response rate, and associated sourcing campaign. Feeds channel effectiveness and recruiter productivity reporting.'
}

Table recruitment.recruiter_assignment {
  recruiter_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the recruiter assignment record. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete business event (assignment of a recruiter to a job order/requisition) with a clear lifecycle.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the resulting placement record when the job order is successfully filled. Links the recruiter assignment to the downstream placement outcome for end-to-end recruitment lifecycle tracking and performance attribution.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for which this job order is being filled. Enables client-level workload analysis, account manager coordination, and client SLA compliance reporting without requiring a join through the job order.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Recruiter assignments must be allocated to cost centers for labor cost tracking, recruiter utilization analysis, and client account profitability reporting. Finance tracks recruiter time costs by cost']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order or requisition (Req) that this recruiter is assigned to fill. Central anchor of the recruiter assignment — every assignment must be tied to an open job order.']
  primary_split_partner_recruiter_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'For split-desk assignments, the identifier of the partner recruiter sharing the fee or credit on this job order. Null for non-split assignments. Enables split-fee reconciliation and dual-recruiter performance attribution.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a candidate from the bench (unassigned workers) being considered for redeployment into this job order. Supports redeployment-first sourcing strategies that reduce time-to-fill and improve bench utilization.']
  readiness_status_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.readiness_status. Business justification: Recruiters need visibility into candidate credentialing readiness for their assigned reqs. Links assignment to readiness evaluation, enabling recruiters to prioritize placement-ready candidates and id']
  recruiter_desk_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the recruiting desk or practice group to which this assignment belongs. A desk typically represents a specialized vertical (e.g., IT, Healthcare, Finance) or geographic territory. Supports workload balancing and desk-level performance reporting.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Recruiters are assigned to fill specific SOW positions with defined scope, timelines, and headcount targets. Real business process: workload allocation, SOW delivery accountability, and client commitm']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Recruiter assignments in hybrid delivery models where internal recruiters manage supplier relationships per requisition. Tracks supplier coordination responsibility, enables supplier-recruiter perform']
  team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the recruiting team (e.g., RPO delivery team, MSP coordinator team) responsible for this assignment. Enables team-level workload aggregation and performance analytics distinct from individual recruiter metrics.']
  tertiary_recruiter_approved_by_manager_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the recruiting manager or team lead who approved this recruiter assignment. Supports approval workflow tracking, management accountability, and audit compliance for assignment governance.']
  actual_fill_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the job order was filled (placement confirmed). Used to calculate realized Time-to-Fill (TTF) against the target and to measure recruiter performance against SLA commitments.']
  actual_submittal_count varchar [note: 'The actual number of candidate submittals delivered by the recruiter against this job order as of the current date. Compared against target_submittal_count to measure QoS performance and recruiter productivity.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the recruiting manager formally approved this recruiter assignment. Supports governance, SLA start-date anchoring, and audit trail for assignment authorization.']
  assigned_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the recruiter was formally assigned to the job order or requisition. Used as the start anchor for Time-to-Fill (TTF) and Time-to-Start (TTS) KPI calculations and workload reporting.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the recruiter\'s active responsibility for the job order ended, either through placement, reassignment, job order closure, or cancellation. Nullable for currently active assignments.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the recruiter assignment. \'active\' = recruiter is actively working the req; \'reassigned\' = ownership transferred to another recruiter; \'closed\' = job order filled or cancelled; \'on_hold\' = req paused by client; \'cancelled\' = assignment voided before any activity.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the recruiter\'s role on this job order. \'primary\' = sole owner; \'backup\' = secondary coverage; \'split_desk\' = shared fee arrangement between two recruiters; \'rpo_team\' = Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO) team member; \'msp_coordinator\' = Managed Service Provider (MSP) program coordinator; \'overflow\' = temporary capacity relief assignment.']
  backfill_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a backfill assignment is required because the original placed candidate left the role prematurely (fall-off). When True, triggers a new recruiter assignment workflow for the same job order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when this recruiter assignment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance tracking. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  delivery_model varchar [note: 'Indicates the staffing delivery model under which this recruiter assignment operates. \'direct\' = standard direct staffing; \'rpo\' = Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO) engagement; \'msp\' = Managed Service Provider (MSP) program; \'hybrid\' = combination of RPO and direct delivery.']
  fall_off_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assignment has been flagged as at risk of a fall-off (placement cancellation after acceptance). Triggered by early warning signals such as candidate counter-offer, client budget freeze, or extended start date delay. Supports proactive retention and backfill planning.']
  interview_to_offer_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'The ratio of client interviews conducted to offers extended for this assignment. Measures the quality of candidates advanced to interview stage and the recruiter\'s ability to match client expectations. Populated at assignment closure.']
  is_exclusive_assignment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recruiter has exclusive ownership of the job order (True) or whether the req is being worked by multiple recruiters simultaneously (False). Exclusive assignments typically carry higher accountability and commission expectations.']
  is_rpo_engagement boolean [note: 'Flags whether this recruiter assignment is part of a formal Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO) engagement where the staffing firm is acting as an embedded talent acquisition function for the client. Drives RPO-specific reporting, billing, and SLA tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recruiter or manager notes related to this assignment, such as special client instructions, candidate pipeline status, sourcing challenges, or escalation context. Supports operational communication and audit trail.']
  performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A composite numeric score (0.00–100.00) evaluating the recruiter\'s performance on this specific assignment, incorporating QoS (Quality of Submission), TTF adherence, submittal-to-hire ratio, and client satisfaction. Populated post-closure for performance reviews and compensation decisions.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority classification for this recruiter assignment, reflecting urgency of the job order fill. \'critical\' = immediate fill required (e.g., client escalation, compliance risk); \'high\' = expedited fill; \'medium\' = standard SLA; \'low\' = flexible timeline. Drives recruiter workload prioritization.']
  reassignment_date date [note: 'The date on which the recruiter assignment was transferred from one recruiter to another. Used to measure the impact of reassignments on TTF and to track assignment continuity for client relationship management.']
  reassignment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded explanation for why this recruiter assignment was transferred to a different recruiter. Common reasons include recruiter departure, workload rebalancing, specialization mismatch, client request, or performance issue. Populated only when assignment_status = \'reassigned\'.']
  sla_ttf_target_days varchar [note: 'The contractually or operationally agreed number of calendar days within which the recruiter must fill the job order from the assignment date. Drives TTF KPI measurement and client SLA compliance reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this recruiter assignment record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration auditing in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this record in the originating operational system (e.g., Bullhorn assignment ID, Beeline req ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and traceability from the Silver Layer back to the system of record.']
  sourcing_strategy varchar [note: 'The primary candidate sourcing approach the recruiter is employing for this assignment. Supports sourcing effectiveness analytics and cost-per-hire analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: job_board|social_media|referral|database_search|headhunting|campus|redeployment|other — promote to reference product]']
  split_fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'For split-desk assignments, the percentage of the placement fee or gross margin allocated to this recruiter. Enables accurate commission calculation and split-fee arrangement tracking. Null for non-split assignments. Range: 0.00–100.00.']
  submittal_to_hire_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'The ratio of candidate submittals to confirmed hires for this assignment (e.g., 5.00 means 5 submittals per hire). A key QoS and recruiter efficiency metric. Populated at assignment closure. Lower ratios indicate higher submittal quality.']
  target_fill_date date [note: 'The target date by which the recruiter is expected to fill the job order with a placed candidate. Drives Time-to-Fill (TTF) SLA tracking and recruiter accountability. Set at assignment creation based on client SLA or internal KPI targets.']
  target_submittal_count varchar [note: 'The planned number of qualified candidate submittals the recruiter is expected to deliver for this job order. Used for workload planning, Quality of Submission (QoS) benchmarking, and SLA compliance tracking against client expectations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when this recruiter assignment record was most recently modified. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  vms_req_number varchar [note: 'The requisition (Req) number assigned by the client\'s Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, for MSP-managed programs. Required for VMS-driven assignments to correlate internal Bullhorn records with external VMS tracking and rate management.']
  workload_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'A numeric weight (e.g., 0.50–3.00) representing the relative complexity or effort burden of this assignment compared to a standard job order. Used in workload balancing algorithms to ensure equitable distribution across the recruiting desk. Higher values indicate more complex or time-intensive requisitions.']

  Note: 'Defines recruiter ownership and accountability for filling a specific job order or requisition. Tracks assigned recruiter, assignment date, type (primary, backup, split-desk, RPO team, MSP coordinator), status (active, reassigned, closed), reassignment reason, target submittal count, and performance against target. Supports workload balancing across desks, recruiter performance tracking, split-fee arrangements, and RPO/MSP delivery team management.'
}

Table recruitment.recruitment_sla_target {
  recruitment_sla_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sla_target']
  approved_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: SLA target approval is a contract governance process requiring employee attribution for client agreement audit trails and accountability. Current approved_by string field should be normalized to staff']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: SLA targets with penalty_clause_flag=true have budget implications (penalties owed to clients, bonus payments for exceeding targets) that must be tracked in formal budget records for financial plannin']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for which this SLA target is defined. Null when the SLA is a program-default rather than client-specific. Links to the client.client_account product.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: SLA targets are defined in MSAs and govern performance measurement frameworks. Real business process: contractual compliance reporting and penalty calculation. Complements existing link to contract.sl']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: SLA targets often include credentialing completion timeframes (e.g., BGC turnaround, license verification). Links SLA definition to the credential package it measures, supporting performance tracking ']
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the holiday calendar used when business_hours_only_flag is True. Determines which days are excluded from SLA time calculations. Supports regional and client-specific holiday schedules.']
  job_category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order category or classification (e.g., IT, Healthcare, Light Industrial) to which this SLA target applies. Enables category-specific SLA differentiation within a client or program.']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MSP or RPO program under which this SLA target applies. Used to associate SLA targets with Beeline VMS program configurations for contingent workforce programs.']
  superseded_by_recruitment_sla_target_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer recruitment_sla_target record that replaced this one when sla_status is \'superseded\'. Enables forward navigation in the SLA version chain for audit and historical analysis.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: SLA targets can be supplier-specific in tiered VMS programs where different suppliers have different performance commitments. Essential for supplier contract management, tier-based SLA enforcement, an']
  vms_sla_id varchar [note: 'The SLA target identifier as recorded in the Beeline VMS system for MSP and contingent workforce programs. Used for cross-system reconciliation between internal SLA tracking and VMS-reported performance metrics.']
  applicable_work_state varchar [note: 'U.S. state code (2-letter USPS abbreviation) where this SLA target applies, when the SLA is geographically scoped. Null for national or program-wide SLAs. Supports state-specific compliance and reporting requirements.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this SLA target was formally approved by the designated approver. Distinct from effective_date, which is when measurement begins. Supports governance audit trails.']
  breach_severity_tier varchar [note: 'Severity classification applied when a breach of this SLA target is detected. Drives escalation routing, client notification protocols, and remediation SLA timelines. Critical breaches may trigger contractual penalties per MSA terms.']
  breach_threshold_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The numeric value at which an SLA breach is formally declared. May differ from target_value to allow a tolerance band (e.g., target TTF = 5 days, breach declared at 7 days). Used to trigger breach notifications and escalation workflows.']
  bullhorn_sla_ref varchar [note: 'The SLA target reference identifier as configured in Bullhorn ATS/CRM. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the Bullhorn operational system and the Silver Layer data product.']
  business_hours_only_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SLA measurement counts only business hours/days (True) or calendar hours/days (False). Critical for TTF and TTS calculations where weekends and holidays may be excluded per contract terms.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the MSA, SOW, or program contract document that establishes this SLA target. Links to the DocuSign CLM contract record. Confidential commercial data used for contract compliance auditing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SLA target record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage, SOC 2 Type II compliance, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
