  delivery_model varchar [note: 'Identifies the recruitment delivery model under which this SLA target applies. Values: direct (standard staffing), msp (Managed Service Provider program), rpo (Recruitment Process Outsourcing), vms (Vendor Management System-driven), hybrid (combination model). Aligns SLA targets with the appropriate operational context.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this SLA target becomes binding and measurement begins. Aligns with the MSA or program contract start date. Used to determine which SLA version applies to a given job order or measurement period.']
  exclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Free-text description of conditions under which a requisition or period is excluded from SLA measurement. Examples: client-caused delays, force majeure events, requisitions placed on hold by the client, or requisitions cancelled within 24 hours of opening.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this SLA target ceases to be binding. Null for open-ended SLA targets that remain active until explicitly superseded or suspended. Used in SLA version management and client contract renewal workflows.']
  job_level varchar [note: 'The seniority or job level tier to which this SLA target applies. Allows differentiated SLA targets for entry-level versus senior or executive placements, reflecting the longer fill cycles for higher-level roles.']
  measurement_basis varchar [note: 'Defines the unit of analysis for SLA measurement. per_requisition: measured individually for each job order/req. per_period: aggregate performance across all reqs in the measurement period. per_program: aggregate across the entire MSP/RPO program. per_category: aggregate within a job order category.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often performance against this SLA target is formally measured and reported. Weekly: high-cadence operational programs. Monthly: standard client business reviews. Quarterly: strategic RPO program reviews. Annually: contract renewal assessments.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target_value. Defines how the SLA target is quantified: business_days (for TTF/TTS), count (for submittal_count), rating (for QoS on a numeric scale), percentage (for fill_rate), ratio (for interview_to_offer_ratio).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, negotiation history, special conditions, or operational guidance related to this SLA target. Used by account managers and program directors for internal documentation.']
  penalty_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a contractual financial penalty applies upon breach of this SLA target. True: breach triggers a financial penalty per the MSA. False: breach results in remediation obligations only, without financial penalty. Sensitive commercial information.']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the contractual penalty terms applicable upon SLA breach, such as credit amounts, service fee reductions, or remediation obligations. Populated only when penalty_clause_flag is True. Confidential commercial contract data.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of placement engagement this SLA target governs. Values: temporary, contract, temp_to_perm (Contract-to-Hire), direct_hire (Permanent Placement), sow (Statement of Work). Different placement types typically carry different SLA expectations.']
  reporting_label varchar [note: 'Client-facing display label for this SLA target as it appears in business review presentations, MSP scorecards, and client portal dashboards. May differ from the internal sla_name to match client-preferred terminology.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next formal review of this SLA target, typically aligned with contract renewal cycles or quarterly business reviews. Triggers account management workflows to renegotiate or reaffirm SLA commitments.']
  scope_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA target is specific to a named client, applies as a default across an MSP/RPO program, is a default for a job order category, or is a global organizational default. Drives SLA resolution hierarchy during measurement.']
  scorecard_category varchar [note: 'The scorecard category or section under which this SLA target is reported in MSP client business reviews and RPO program scorecards. Examples: \'Speed\', \'Quality\', \'Compliance\', \'Diversity\'. Used for Power BI scorecard dashboard grouping.']
  sla_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the SLA target, such as \'Time-to-Fill Target – IT Contracts\' or \'Submittal Count SLA – Healthcare RPO Program\'. Used for display in client business reviews and Power BI dashboards.']
  sla_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number for the SLA target record, used in client communications, MSP scorecards, and RPO program documentation. Sourced from Bullhorn ATS or Beeline VMS.']
  sla_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SLA target record. Draft: under negotiation or configuration. Active: currently in force and being measured. Suspended: temporarily paused by mutual agreement. Expired: past the expiration_date. Superseded: replaced by a newer SLA target version.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the category of SLA target being defined. Values: time_to_fill (TTF target in business days), time_to_start (TTS target in calendar days), submittal_count (minimum number of qualified submittals per requisition), qos_min_rating (minimum Quality of Submission rating), interview_to_offer_ratio (minimum ratio of interviews resulting in offers), fill_rate (minimum percentage of requisitions filled within period).']
  target_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The numeric threshold that defines the SLA commitment. Interpretation depends on sla_type and measurement_unit: e.g., 5.0 (business days for TTF), 3.0 (submittals for submittal_count), 4.5 (rating for qos_min_rating), 0.85 (85% fill rate). This is the contractually agreed performance target.']
  tolerance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allowable percentage variance from the target_value before a warning or breach is triggered. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 10.00 = 10%). Provides a configurable buffer for minor performance fluctuations without triggering formal breach procedures.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SLA target record was most recently modified. Used for change detection in incremental data loads, audit trails, and SLA version management workflows.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for this SLA target, incremented each time the target is renegotiated or updated. Enables historical tracking of SLA evolution over the client relationship lifecycle. Version 1 is the initial agreed target.']
  warning_threshold_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The numeric value at which an at-risk warning is triggered before a formal breach occurs. Enables proactive intervention by recruiters and account managers. For example, a TTF warning at 4 days before a 5-day target and 7-day breach threshold.']

  Note: 'Defines Service Level Agreement (SLA) targets for recruitment delivery performance against specific clients, programs, or job order categories. Captures SLA type (time-to-fill target, time-to-start target, submittal count target, QoS minimum rating, interview-to-offer ratio, fill rate), target value, measurement unit, client-specific or program-default flag, SLA effective and expiration dates, measurement frequency (weekly, monthly, quarterly), and breach threshold with severity tiers. Supports MSP scorecard compliance, RPO program SLA monitoring, and client business review reporting.'
}

Table recruitment.sla_breach {
  sla_breach_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sla_breach']
  accrual_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accrual. Business justification: SLA breaches with penalty_applicable=true trigger immediate accrual of penalty liability owed to client before credit memo is issued. Finance must recognize liability in period of breach for accurate ']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.bgc_order. Business justification: SLA breaches may be caused by delayed background checks. Tracks which BGC order caused the breach, supporting root cause analysis and vendor performance management. Essential for identifying credentia']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for whom the recruitment SLA was in effect. Enables client-level SLA breach reporting, NPS correlation, and MSA penalty tracking.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: SLA breaches are measured against MSA-defined thresholds and trigger contractual penalties or remediation obligations. Real business process: penalty calculation, client dispute resolution, and contra']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) against which this SLA breach occurred. Links the breach to the specific open position being filled, enabling job-order-level SLA performance analysis.']
  primary_sla_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the recruiter primarily responsible for the job order at the time of the SLA breach. Enables recruiter-level SLA performance analysis, coaching, and accountability tracking.']
  recruitment_sla_target_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SLA definition record that was breached. Links to the agreed-upon SLA terms, thresholds, and measurement criteria established in the client\'s MSA or VMS program.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: SLA breaches attributed to specific suppliers for performance management. Drives supplier scorecard penalties, tier demotion decisions, allocation reductions, and contractual penalty calculations. Cor']
  actual_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual measured value of the SLA metric at the time of breach (e.g., actual days taken to fill, actual number of submittals delivered, actual QoS score achieved). Compared against sla_threshold_value to quantify the breach magnitude.']
  breach_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the SLA breach formally occurred — i.e., the date the agreed SLA threshold was crossed. Used for period-based breach reporting and trend analysis.']
  breach_detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the SLA breach event was first detected or triggered by the system or a recruiter. This is the principal real-world event timestamp for this transaction, distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  breach_magnitude decimal(10,2) [note: 'The quantified difference between the actual metric value and the agreed SLA threshold (actual_value minus sla_threshold_value). Represents how far the SLA was missed, used for severity scoring and penalty calculation.']
  breach_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable reference number assigned to this SLA breach event (e.g., SLA-BR-2024-000123). Used in client communications, audit trails, and continuous improvement reporting.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the breach indicating its business impact. \'minor\' = threshold exceeded by a small margin with low client impact; \'major\' = significant threshold exceedance with moderate client impact; \'critical\' = severe breach with high client impact, potential contractual penalty, or risk of client attrition.']
  breach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SLA breach record. \'open\' indicates breach is newly identified; \'under_review\' indicates root cause analysis in progress; \'remediated\' indicates corrective action taken; \'closed\' indicates fully resolved; \'disputed\' indicates client or internal party contests the breach; \'waived\' indicates client formally waived the breach penalty.']
  breach_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the type of SLA breach. \'ttf_exceeded\' = Time to Fill (TTF) deadline missed; \'submittal_count_missed\' = required number of submittals not delivered; \'qos_below_threshold\' = Quality of Submission (QoS) score fell below agreed threshold; \'tts_exceeded\' = Time to Start (TTS) deadline missed; \'interview_sla_missed\' = interview scheduling SLA not met; \'offer_sla_missed\' = offer extension SLA not met. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ttf_exceeded|submittal_count_missed|qos_below_threshold|tts_exceeded|interview_sla_missed|offer_sla_missed — promote to reference product]']
  bullhorn_breach_ref varchar [note: 'The corresponding record identifier or note reference in Bullhorn ATS/CRM for this SLA breach event. Used for cross-system traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  client_dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason provided by the client when disputing the SLA breach classification or penalty. Populated only when breach_status is \'disputed\'. Used for dispute resolution tracking.']
  client_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the client was formally notified of the SLA breach. Populated when client_notified is True. Used to track notification timeliness and MSA compliance.']
  client_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client has been formally notified of this SLA breach. True = client notification sent; False = client not yet notified. Supports compliance with contractual notification obligations in the MSA.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA breach record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  days_to_resolve varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days elapsed between the breach_date and the resolution_date. Stored as a business metric to support SLA breach resolution KPI reporting without requiring runtime date arithmetic.']
  delivery_model varchar [note: 'The recruitment delivery model under which the breached SLA was operating. \'direct\' = standard direct staffing; \'rpo\' = Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO); \'msp\' = Managed Service Provider (MSP); \'vms\' = Vendor Management System (VMS)-driven program. Affects SLA terms and penalty structures.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'The organizational escalation level to which this breach has been raised. \'none\' = no escalation; \'recruiter\' = handled at recruiter level; \'manager\' = escalated to recruiting manager; \'director\' = escalated to director; \'executive\' = escalated to executive leadership. Tracks escalation path for critical breaches.']
  internal_review_notes varchar [note: 'Internal free-text notes captured during the breach review process. May include observations from the recruiting manager, account manager, or quality assurance team. Not shared with the client.']
  is_repeat_breach boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this breach is a repeat occurrence of the same breach type for the same client within the current contract period. True = repeat breach; False = first occurrence. Used for pattern analysis and escalation triggers.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The job title of the position associated with the breached job order. Captured as a denormalized field to support breach analysis by role type without requiring a join to the job order table.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The financial penalty or credit amount assessed against the staffing firm for this SLA breach, as defined in the client\'s MSA. Expressed in USD. Populated only when penalty_applicable is True. Used for financial impact reporting and accounts receivable adjustments.']
  penalty_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a contractual financial penalty or credit is applicable to this SLA breach per the terms of the client\'s Master Service Agreement (MSA). True = penalty applies; False = no penalty (e.g., first offense, waived, or non-penalized SLA tier).']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'The type of placement associated with the breached job order. \'contract\' = temporary/contingent; \'temp_to_perm\' = Temp-to-Perm conversion; \'direct_placement\' = permanent direct hire; \'contract_to_hire\' = Contract-to-Hire. Affects SLA terms and breach severity interpretation.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Categorical description of the corrective action taken in response to the SLA breach. Used to track remediation patterns and measure effectiveness of interventions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sourcing_campaign_launched|recruiter_reassigned|client_escalation|job_order_revised|rate_adjustment|additional_sourcing_channels|process_improvement|no_action_required|other — promote to reference product]']
  remediation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing detailed notes on the remediation steps taken, responsible parties, and expected outcomes. Supports client reporting and internal continuous improvement documentation.']
  repeat_breach_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative count of breaches of the same type for the same client in the current contract period, including this instance. Used to trigger escalation thresholds and penalty multipliers defined in the MSA.']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'The end date of the SLA measurement reporting period in which this breach occurred. Used in conjunction with reporting_period_start to define the evaluation window for client SLA scorecards.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'The start date of the SLA measurement reporting period in which this breach occurred. Used to align breach records with client SLA reporting cycles (weekly, monthly, quarterly).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the SLA breach was formally resolved and closed. Used to calculate breach duration (resolution_date minus breach_date) and measure remediation cycle time.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the primary root cause of the SLA breach. Used for continuous improvement analysis and recruiter coaching. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: candidate_shortage|client_unresponsive|recruiter_capacity|vms_delay|compensation_mismatch|job_order_unclear|approval_delay|other — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing detailed explanation of the root cause of the SLA breach. Supplements the root_cause_category with specific context, circumstances, and contributing factors identified during review.']
  sla_measurement_period varchar [note: 'The measurement period over which the SLA metric is evaluated (e.g., \'monthly\' for monthly submittal count SLAs, \'per_requisition\' for TTF measured per job order). Determines the reporting window for breach assessment.']
  sla_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the SLA threshold value (e.g., \'business_days\' for TTF, \'count\' for submittal quantity, \'percentage\' for QoS score). Enables correct interpretation of the threshold value across different breach types.']
  sla_threshold_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'The agreed contractual threshold value for the SLA metric that was breached (e.g., 5 business days for TTF, 3 submittals required, 85% QoS score). The unit of measure is captured in sla_threshold_unit.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this SLA breach record originated. \'bullhorn\' = Bullhorn ATS/CRM; \'beeline_vms\' = Beeline VMS; \'power_bi\' = Power BI Analytics Platform (system-generated alert); \'manual\' = manually entered by operations staff.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA breach record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  vms_req_number varchar [note: 'The requisition number assigned by the client\'s Vendor Management System (VMS) for the associated job order. Populated when delivery_model is \'vms\' or \'msp\'. Used for cross-system reconciliation with Beeline VMS.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason the SLA breach penalty or classification was formally waived. Populated only when breach_status is \'waived\'. Documents the business justification for the waiver for audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of an SLA breach event in the recruitment process. Captures breach date, breach type (TTF exceeded, submittal count missed, QoS below threshold), associated SLA definition, job order reference, client reference, breach severity (minor, major, critical), root cause category, remediation action taken, and breach resolution date. Supports client reporting and continuous improvement programs.'
}

Table recruitment.referral {
  referral_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the referral record. Primary key.']
  accrual_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accrual. Business justification: Referrals with bonus_eligible=true require accrual of bonus liability when candidate is placed, before actual bonus payment occurs (often 30-90 days post-placement). Finance must accrue bonus expense ']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the placement record if the referred candidate was successfully placed. Null if referral has not resulted in placement.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client account associated with the referral, if applicable. May be null for general referrals not tied to a specific client.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the job order or requisition for which the candidate was referred. May be null if referral is general talent pool submission.']
  original_referral_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original referral record if this is a duplicate. Null if this is the first referral for this candidate.']
  originating_referral_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on referral (originating_referral_id)']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific referral program or campaign under which this referral was submitted. Used to track program-specific rules and incentives.']
  referral_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the candidate who was referred. Links to the candidate master record.']
  referral_recruiter_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recruiter assigned to process and follow up on this referral.']
  referral_referrer_candidate_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the placed worker or candidate who made the referral, if referrer_type is placed_worker or candidate. Null otherwise.']
  referral_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal employee who made the referral, if referrer_type is employee. Null for non-employee referrers.']
  referrer_client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client contact who made the referral, if referrer_type is client_contact. Null otherwise.']
  submittal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the submittal record if the referred candidate was submitted to a client. Null if referral has not progressed to submittal stage.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Referrals can originate from supplier networks (supplier employees referring candidates). Tracks referral source for bonus allocation, supplier relationship credit, and enables supplier referral progr']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date when the referral was accepted and the candidate was moved into active consideration. Null if referral was rejected.']
  bonus_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the referral bonus to be paid to the referrer upon successful placement and completion of eligibility criteria.']
  bonus_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the referral bonus amount.']
  bonus_denial_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a referral bonus was denied or cancelled, if applicable. Null if bonus was approved and paid.']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this referral qualifies for a referral bonus based on program rules, referrer type, and placement outcome.']
  bonus_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the referral bonus was paid to the referrer. Null if bonus has not yet been paid.']
  bonus_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the referral bonus payment process. Tracks approval and payment workflow.']
  bullhorn_referral_code varchar [note: 'External identifier from Bullhorn ATS/CRM system for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this referral record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  days_to_placement varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from referral submission to successful placement. Key metric for referral program effectiveness. Null if not yet placed.']
  is_duplicate_referral boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this referral is a duplicate of an existing candidate record or prior referral. Used to manage bonus eligibility and prevent duplicate processing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments provided by the referrer about the candidate\'s qualifications, background, or fit for the role.']
  placement_date date [note: 'Date when the referred candidate was successfully placed. Used to calculate time-to-placement from referral and determine bonus eligibility timing.']
  referral_date date [note: 'Date when the referral was submitted or recorded in the system. Used to calculate bonus eligibility windows and track referral velocity.']
  referral_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the referral, used for tracking and communication purposes.']
  referral_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the referral. Tracks progression from submission through placement and bonus payment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|under_review|accepted|rejected|placed|bonus_pending|bonus_paid|expired — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  referrer_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the referrer for communication and bonus notification purposes.']
  referrer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person who made the referral. Captured for tracking and bonus payment purposes.']
  referrer_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the referrer.']
  referrer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the person or entity who made the referral. Determines bonus eligibility rules and tracking requirements.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date when the referral was rejected or declined. Null if referral was accepted or is still under review.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the referral was rejected. Used for referrer feedback and program improvement. Null if referral was not rejected.']
  relationship_to_candidate varchar [note: 'Description of the referrer\'s relationship to the referred candidate (e.g., former colleague, friend, family member, professional contact). Used for quality and compliance tracking.']
  reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the referral was reviewed by the recruiting team. Used to track referral processing time and responsiveness.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel or method through which the referral was submitted. Used for program effectiveness analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|portal|mobile_app|phone|in_person|social_media|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this referral record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Tracks candidate referrals from employees, placed workers, or external sources, capturing referrer identity, referred candidate, referral date, and referral bonus eligibility'
}

Table recruitment.job_board_integration {
  job_board_integration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job board integration record. Primary key.']
  api_credential_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the API credentials used to authenticate with the job board platform.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Job board spend must be tracked against marketing/sourcing budgets for variance analysis, spend control, and budget forecasting. Finance monitors job board costs against approved budgets to prevent ov']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for which the job is being posted.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Job board costs (cost_per_posting, billing_model charges) must be allocated to cost centers for ROI analysis, cost-per-application tracking, and departmental budget management. Finance tracks sourcing']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order being posted to the job board.']
  parent_job_board_integration_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on job_board_integration (parent_job_board_integration_id)']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Job board spend is allocated to specific SOW budgets for cost tracking and client billing. Real business process: SOW financial management, vendor spend reconciliation, and pass-through cost recovery.']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the VMS program if this posting is part of a managed service provider engagement.']
  application_conversion_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of clicks that resulted in applications, calculated as (applications / clicks) * 100.']
  applications_count varchar [note: 'Total number of candidate applications received through this job board posting.']
  auto_refresh_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the posting is automatically refreshed or re-posted on the job board.']
  billing_model varchar [note: 'Pricing structure used by the job board for this posting.']
  click_through_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of views that resulted in clicks, calculated as (clicks / views) * 100.']
  clicks_count varchar [note: 'Total number of clicks or interactions with the job posting on the job board.']
  cost_per_application decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average cost per candidate application received, calculated as total cost divided by applications count.']
  cost_per_posting decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost charged by the job board for this specific posting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the job board integration record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the posting cost.']
  delivery_model varchar [note: 'Staffing delivery model context for this job board integration: direct placement, Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO), Managed Service Provider (MSP), or Vendor Management System (VMS).']
  diversity_sourcing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job board is specifically used for diversity and inclusion sourcing initiatives.']
  external_posting_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the job board platform for this posting.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach of the job board platform for candidate sourcing.']
  integration_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the job board integration record.']
  integration_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the job board integration.']
  job_board_name varchar [note: 'Name of the job board or aggregator platform where the job is posted.']
  job_board_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the job board platform by its market focus and audience.']
  last_refresh_date date [note: 'Date when the job posting was last refreshed or re-posted on the job board.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes and comments about the job board integration, posting performance, or configuration details.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Overall performance assessment of this job board integration based on cost, quality, and conversion metrics.']
  placement_count varchar [note: 'Total number of successful placements that originated from this job board posting.']
  posting_duration_days varchar [note: 'Total number of days the job posting is or was active on the job board.']
  posting_end_date date [note: 'Date when the job posting expires or was removed from the job board.']
  posting_method varchar [note: 'Technical method used to submit the job posting to the job board.']
  posting_rules varchar [note: 'Business rules and criteria governing when and how jobs are posted to this board.']
  posting_start_date date [note: 'Date when the job posting went live on the job board.']
  posting_tier varchar [note: 'Service level or visibility tier purchased for the job posting on the board.']
  posting_url varchar [note: 'Direct web link to the job posting on the job board platform.']
  quality_of_submission_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average quality rating of candidates sourced through this job board integration.']
  refresh_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between automatic refresh cycles for the job posting.']
  submittal_count varchar [note: 'Total number of candidate submittals to clients that originated from this job board posting.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the candidate demographic or skill set targeted by this job board.']
  time_to_fill_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from posting start date to first placement for this job board integration.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the job board integration record was last modified.']
  views_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the job posting was viewed on the job board.']

  Note: 'Master record for job board and aggregator integrations (Indeed, LinkedIn, ZipRecruiter, CareerBuilder) tracking posting rules, API credentials, cost per posting, and performance metrics'
}

Table recruitment.submittal_compliance_check {
  submittal_compliance_check_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this submittal-requirement verification record. Primary key.']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific compliance requirement being verified']
  submittal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the candidate submittal being verified for compliance']
  blocking_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether failure to meet this requirement blocks the submittal from progressing to placement. True means the submittal cannot proceed without satisfying this requirement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance check record was first created in the system.']
  documentation_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required documentation for this compliance requirement has been collected and verified for this submittal.']
  documentation_received_date date [note: 'The date on which the required documentation for this compliance requirement was received from the candidate for this submittal.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this compliance verification expires and must be re-verified for this submittal (e.g., annual background check, drug screen validity period).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, issues, or special circumstances related to this requirement verification for this submittal.']
  requirement_met_date date [note: 'The date on which this specific compliance requirement was successfully verified and met for this submittal. Null if requirement is still pending or failed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance check record was last modified.']
  verification_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance verification was performed for this requirement on this submittal.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current state of compliance verification for this submittal-requirement combination. Indicates whether the requirement has been met, is pending documentation, has been waived, or has failed verification.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance staff member who performed the verification of this requirement for this submittal.']
  waiver_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the granted waiver expires and the requirement must be re-verified or the submittal becomes non-compliant. Null if no waiver granted or waiver is permanent.']
  waiver_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a compliance waiver has been granted for this specific requirement on this submittal, allowing progression despite non-compliance.']
  waiver_granted_by varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member (compliance officer or authorized manager) who granted the waiver for this requirement on this submittal.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why a compliance waiver was granted for this requirement on this submittal.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance verification checkpoint between a candidate submittal and a specific compliance requirement. It captures the requirement-by-requirement compliance status for each submittal, including verification dates, waiver grants, blocking status, and documentation completeness. Each record links one submittal to one compliance requirement with attributes that exist only in the context of this verification relationship. Central to pre-placement compliance workflows and submittal clearance processes.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, each candidate submittal must satisfy multiple compliance requirements (I-9 verification, background checks, drug screens, client-specific mandates, industry credentials), and each compliance requirement applies to many submittals across different candidates and job orders. The compliance team actively manages requirement-by-requirement verification status for each submittal, tracking verification dates, waivers, blocking status, and documentation completeness as part of the pre-placement clearance workflow.'
}

Table joborder.order_header {
  order_header_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for order_header']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounting_period. Business justification: Job orders must be associated with accounting periods for revenue recognition timing, period close processes, financial reporting compliance, and revenue cutoff determination—fundamental to accrual ac']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account that originated this requisition. Links the job order to the client master record in the client domain. Supports client-level demand aggregation and SLA tracking.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific client contact (hiring manager or procurement contact) who submitted or owns this requisition. Used for communication routing and accountability tracking.']
  client_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific client work site or office location where the placed worker will report. Drives workers compensation classification, tax jurisdiction, and scheduling.']
  client_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the negotiated rate card governing bill rate and pay rate ranges for this requisition. Ensures pricing compliance with client MSA terms.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Job orders inherit bill/pay rates from contracted rate schedules. Linking enables automated rate validation, prevents off-contract pricing, supports rate variance analysis, and ensures every requisiti']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Job orders must track cost center for client billing allocation, internal P&L reporting by service line, and budget consumption tracking—fundamental to staffing operations financial management and pro']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Job orders generate revenue and must map to GL accounts for revenue recognition, financial statement preparation, and audit trail—core financial control ensuring proper revenue classification by servi']
  msa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Master Service Agreement governing the commercial terms for this requisition. Links to the DocuSign CLM contract record. Ensures billing and compliance terms are applied correctly.']
  preferred_supplier_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.preferred_supplier_list. Business justification: Orders may be restricted to preferred supplier lists by client program. Order routing eligibility, supplier access control, and PSL compliance enforcement require direct PSL reference.']
  rate_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.rate_agreement. Business justification: Job orders are priced under specific rate agreements with suppliers. Rate validation, markup enforcement, contract compliance auditing, and invoice reconciliation require direct link to governing rate']
  sow_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.sow_agreement. Business justification: SOW-based orders reference specific statements of work for scope, deliverables, and pricing. SOW compliance tracking, milestone management, and project-based billing require direct SOW link. Removes d']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Job orders are awarded to/fulfilled by specific suppliers. Supplier assignment drives submittal routing, performance tracking, and invoice reconciliation. Core staffing workflow requires knowing which']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the VMS program through which this requisition was received, when applicable. Links to the Beeline VMS program configuration for rate card and supplier management rules.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'The anticipated or confirmed end date of the temporary assignment or contract engagement. Drives redeployment planning, bench management, and extension workflow triggers.']
  bgc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a background check (BGC) is required for candidates on this requisition. Triggers the Sterling Background Check credentialing workflow. Governed by FCRA adverse action requirements.']
  bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly or unit rate charged to the client for each worker placed on this requisition. Expressed in USD per hour for temporary and contract engagements. Core component of gross margin and spread calculations. MONETARY_TRIPLET component for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  clearance_required varchar [note: 'Security clearance level required for the position. Critical for government and defense staffing engagements. Drives candidate eligibility screening and credentialing requirements.']
  conversion_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'The fee charged to the client upon permanent conversion of a temp-to-perm worker. May be expressed as a flat dollar amount or percentage of first-year salary. Applicable only when is_temp_to_perm is true.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this job order header record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for the TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and SLA baseline calculations.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary rate fields on this requisition (bill_rate, pay_rate, etc.). Supports multi-currency operations for international staffing engagements. MONETARY_TRIPLET currency component.']
  drug_screen_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pre-placement drug screening is required for candidates on this requisition. Triggers the credentialing workflow in Sterling Background Check upon placement.']
  education_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum educational qualification required for candidates on this requisition. Used in candidate screening and EEOC-compliant job requirement documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|high_school|associate|bachelor|master|doctorate|professional_cert — promote to reference product]']
  employment_classification varchar [note: 'Worker tax and employment classification for this requisition. W-2 = employee on staffing firm payroll; 1099 = independent contractor; C2C = corp-to-corp engagement. Drives payroll processing, tax withholding, and co-employment risk assessment.']
  headcount_filled varchar [note: 'Current count of positions on this requisition that have been successfully filled with an active placement. Derived operationally from placement records but stored here for fast reporting and SLA dashboards.']
  headcount_remaining varchar [note: 'Number of unfilled positions remaining on this requisition (headcount_target minus headcount_filled). Drives active recruiting prioritization and capacity planning.']
  headcount_target varchar [note: 'Total number of positions authorized by the client for this requisition. Represents the maximum number of workers that can be placed against this job order. Used for demand planning and fulfillment tracking.']
  hours_per_week decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected number of hours per week for the position. Used to determine full-time equivalent (FTE) classification, ACA benefits eligibility thresholds, and total assignment cost projections.']
  intake_date date [note: 'The date the requisition was formally received and entered into the ATS/CRM. Serves as the start point for Time to Fill (TTF) and Time to Start (TTS) SLA calculations. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  is_backfill boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this requisition is a backfill for a previously vacated position rather than a new headcount addition. Backfill reqs typically have faster fill expectations and known role profiles.']
  is_temp_to_perm boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this requisition has a contractual temp-to-perm (contract-to-hire) conversion option. When true, a conversion fee schedule applies upon permanent hire. Drives conversion tracking and fee billing.']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the position including responsibilities, qualifications, and working conditions as provided by the client. Source content for ATS job postings and candidate screening.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The client-specified job title for the open position. Used in candidate matching, submittal communications, and workforce analytics. May differ from the internal job category classification.']
  markup_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage markup applied over the pay rate to derive the bill rate, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.45 = 45%). Encapsulates burden, overhead, and profit margin. Used for pricing analysis and client negotiations.']
  min_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant work experience required for candidates to be considered for this position. Used in ATS candidate screening and quality of submission (QoS) scoring.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the job order requisition. Drives recruiter workflow, reporting dashboards, and SLA tracking. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Values: open (actively recruiting), filled (all headcount placed), on_hold (client-paused), cancelled (client-withdrawn), closed (administratively closed).']
  originating_source varchar [note: 'The channel or system through which this requisition was received. Tracks demand intake source for channel analytics and process efficiency measurement. VMS = Beeline or similar; CRM = Salesforce/Bullhorn direct; Direct = client phone/email; Portal = self-service client portal.']
  ot_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly bill rate charged to the client for overtime hours worked by placed workers on this requisition. Typically 1.5x the regular bill rate per FLSA requirements. Required for accurate client invoicing.']
  ot_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly pay rate for overtime hours paid to workers placed on this requisition. Must comply with FLSA 1.5x overtime rule for non-exempt workers. Drives payroll cost calculations.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly or unit rate paid to the worker placed on this requisition. Expressed in USD per hour. Used to calculate spread (bill rate minus pay rate) and gross margin. Must comply with FLSA minimum wage and applicable prevailing wage requirements.']
  per_diem_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Daily allowance paid to the worker for travel, lodging, or meals when the assignment requires relocation or travel. Expressed in currency per day. Applicable for travel-intensive or remote assignments.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the engagement model for this requisition. Determines billing structure, conversion fee applicability, and compliance requirements. Temp = temporary staffing; Contract-to-Hire = temp with conversion option; Direct Placement = permanent placement; SOW = Statement of Work project-based engagement.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Recruiter-assigned or client-designated urgency level for this requisition. Drives work queue prioritization and resource allocation. Critical = immediate fill required; High = fill within SLA; Medium = standard; Low = backfill or pipeline.']
  req_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable requisition number assigned at intake. Used by clients, recruiters, and VMS systems to reference the job order. Corresponds to the Bullhorn Job Order external ID or VMS requisition number. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'The work shift schedule required for this position. Drives candidate availability matching, differential pay calculations, and scheduling in Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  target_start_date date [note: 'The client-requested date by which the placed worker should begin the assignment. Used to calculate Time to Start (TTS) SLA performance and to prioritize recruiter activity.']
  ttf_target_days varchar [note: 'The SLA-committed number of calendar days from intake to first qualified submittal or fill, as agreed with the client. Used to measure recruiter and program performance against TTF KPI benchmarks.']
  tts_target_days varchar [note: 'The SLA-committed number of calendar days from requisition intake to worker start date. Distinct from TTF; measures the full cycle from demand signal to worker on-site.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this job order header record was last modified. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for the TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.']
  vms_req_number varchar [note: 'The requisition number assigned by the client\'s VMS platform (e.g., Beeline). Distinct from the internal req_number; used for cross-system reconciliation and supplier portal submissions.']
  work_location_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the position requires the worker to be physically on-site, fully remote, or a hybrid arrangement. Impacts candidate sourcing geography, per diem eligibility, and tax nexus determination.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a client requisition (req) — the authoritative job order header record and central hub of the joborder domain. Captures the full lifecycle of a client demand request from intake through closure. Stores requisition number, job title, position type (temp, contract-to-hire, direct placement, SOW), employment classification (W-2, 1099, C2C), headcount target (total positions authorized, positions filled, positions remaining), backfill indicator, priority level, order status (open, filled, cancelled, on-hold, closed), intake date, target start date, assignment end date, and originating source (VMS, CRM, direct). Central hub connecting client demand to candidate supply in the ATS/CRM. All other joborder products reference this entity via req_id.'
}

Table joborder.position_requirement {
  position_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the position requirement record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a structured demand profile (position specification) attached to a job order requisition.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Position requirements often specify client cost center for budget authorization and departmental charge-back, enabling budget validation before submittal and accurate client billing allocation in ente']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent job order requisition that this position requirement is attached to. Links the position specification back to the demand record in the Job Order domain.']
  bgc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a background check (BGC) is required for candidates submitting to this position. Drives the credentialing workflow in Sterling Background Check and compliance tracking.']
  bgc_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type(s) of background check required (e.g., criminal, employment verification, education verification, credit, motor vehicle record). Drives the BGC package selection in Sterling Background Check. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: criminal|employment_verification|education_verification|credit|motor_vehicle|sex_offender_registry|federal — promote to reference product]']
  bill_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The agreed-upon hourly or periodic rate charged to the client for this position. Used in revenue recognition, spread calculation (Bill Rate minus Pay Rate), and gross margin analysis. Sourced from Bullhorn Job Order or Beeline VMS rate card.']
  certifications_required varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of mandatory professional certifications or licenses required for the position (e.g., PMP, RN, CPA, CDL). Drives the credentialing verification workflow and readiness status checks.']
  client_screening_criteria varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any client-specific screening requirements beyond standard criteria (e.g., proprietary assessments, client interviews, specific onboarding steps, NDA requirements). Sourced from client MSA or VMS program requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this position requirement record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, TTF calculation baseline, and data lineage tracking. Aligns with SOC 2 Type II audit requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary rate fields on this position requirement (pay_rate_min, pay_rate_max, bill_rate). Supports multi-currency international staffing operations.']
  drug_screen_panel varchar [note: 'Specifies the drug screening panel type required for this position (e.g., 5-panel, 10-panel, 12-panel, hair follicle, oral fluid). Drives the specific test order in Sterling Background Check.']
  drug_screen_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pre-placement drug screen is required for this position. Triggers the drug screening workflow in Sterling Background Check and credentialing readiness tracking.']
  education_field_of_study varchar [note: 'Specific academic discipline or field of study required or preferred for the position (e.g., Computer Science, Nursing, Accounting). Complements education_level for precise candidate qualification.']
  education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum educational attainment required for the position. Used as a screening criterion in candidate qualification. Values align with standard U.S. education levels: none, high_school, associate, bachelor, master, doctorate, professional_cert. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|high_school|associate|bachelor|master|doctorate|professional_cert — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the engagement type for this position. Drives billing model, pay structure, and compliance requirements. Values: contract (temporary staffing), temp_to_perm (Temp-to-Perm conversion eligible), direct_hire (Direct Placement/FTE), contract_to_hire (Contract-to-Hire), per_diem (Per Diem assignment), sow (Statement of Work engagement).']
  end_date date [note: 'Expected or confirmed end date for the position assignment. Drives assignment duration tracking, extension planning, redeployment workflows, and bench management. Nullable for permanent/direct hire positions.']
  everify_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether E-Verify employment eligibility verification is required for this position. Mandatory for federal contractors and certain state-regulated engagements.']
  headcount varchar [note: 'Number of open positions (headcount) to be filled for this position requirement. Drives sourcing volume targets, TTF SLA tracking, and workforce planning analytics.']
  hours_per_week decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected number of hours per week for the position. Used to determine full-time equivalent (FTE) status, overtime eligibility under FLSA, and bill/pay rate calculations.']
  job_category varchar [note: 'Broad occupational category classifying the position (e.g., Information Technology, Healthcare, Finance, Engineering, Administrative). Used for workforce analytics, reporting, and candidate pool segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Information Technology|Healthcare|Finance|Engineering|Administrative|Manufacturing|Legal|Creative|Sales|Operations — promote to reference product]']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative job description including responsibilities, duties, and role overview as provided by the client. Used for candidate matching, job postings, and submittal qualification. Sourced from Bullhorn Job Order description field.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The official job title for the open position as specified by the client. Used in candidate matching, submittal qualification, and job postings. Sourced from the Bullhorn Job Order title field.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this position requirement record was last updated. Used for change tracking, SLA monitoring, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Silver Layer.']
  max_submittals varchar [note: 'Maximum number of candidate submittals the client will accept for this position. Enforces quality of submission (QoS) discipline and prevents submittal flooding in VMS-managed programs.']
  max_years_experience varchar [note: 'Maximum number of years of relevant professional experience acceptable for the position. Used to prevent over-qualification mismatches and manage bill rate expectations.']
  min_years_experience varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant professional experience required for the position. Used as a hard filter in candidate screening and submittal qualification.']
  pay_rate_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum pay rate (per hour or per period) approved for this position. Defines the upper bound of the pay rate range. Used in candidate offer negotiations and gross margin planning.']
  pay_rate_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum pay rate (per hour or per period) approved for this position. Defines the lower bound of the pay rate range used in candidate offer negotiations and spread/margin calculations.']
  physical_demands varchar [note: 'DOL physical exertion classification for the position. Used for workers compensation risk rating, OSHA compliance, and candidate physical qualification screening. Aligns with DOL Dictionary of Occupational Titles (DOT) physical demand levels.']
  position_req_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this position requirement, used in client communications, VMS portals, and ATS workflows. May align with the client\'s internal requisition number or Beeline VMS position code.']
  position_requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the position requirement. Drives operational workflows: draft (intake in progress), open (actively sourcing), on_hold (client paused), filled (placement made), cancelled (client withdrew), closed (naturally ended), expired (TTF deadline passed without fill). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|on_hold|filled|cancelled|closed|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  preferred_skills varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited or free-text list of desired but non-mandatory skills that enhance candidate suitability. Used for secondary ranking in candidate matching and quality of submission (QoS) scoring.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this position requirement, used to triage recruiter effort and sourcing resources. Critical positions receive immediate attention; low priority positions are worked as capacity allows.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the billing and pay rate period for this position. Determines how bill_rate and pay_rate_min/max are interpreted in payroll and billing calculations.']
  required_skills varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited or free-text list of mandatory technical and functional skills required for the position. Used as primary filter criteria in ATS candidate matching and submittal qualification scoring (QoS).']
  security_clearance_level varchar [note: 'U.S. government security clearance level required for the position. Critical for federal staffing engagements and OFCCP compliance. Values: none, public_trust, confidential, secret, top_secret, TS/SCI (Top Secret/Sensitive Compartmented Information).']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'The work shift schedule required for the position. Drives scheduling, differential pay calculations, and candidate availability matching in Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  start_date date [note: 'Target or confirmed start date for the position. Used in Time to Start (TTS) SLA measurement, assignment scheduling, and onboarding planning.']
  travel_requirement varchar [note: 'Expected travel frequency for the position. Used in candidate screening and per diem/expense planning. Values represent percentage of time traveling: none, occasional, up to 25%, up to 50%, up to 75%, frequent (75%+).']
  ttf_target_days varchar [note: 'Client-agreed or internal SLA target for the number of calendar days to fill this position from requisition open date. Key KPI for recruiter performance and SLA compliance reporting.']
  tts_target_days varchar [note: 'Client-agreed or internal SLA target for the number of calendar days from candidate selection to worker start date. Distinct from TTF; measures onboarding and credentialing speed.']
  work_city varchar [note: 'City where the position is physically located or where the worker is expected to report. Used for geographic candidate matching, tax jurisdiction determination, and workers compensation classification.']
  work_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the position is located. Required for international staffing operations, GDPR applicability determination, and EOR arrangement identification.']
  work_location_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the position requires the worker to be physically present at the client site (on_site), work fully remotely (remote), or a combination of both (hybrid). Drives candidate geographic filtering and sourcing strategy.']
  work_state varchar [note: 'U.S. state (or equivalent jurisdiction) where the position is located. Critical for state-specific labor law compliance, SUTA tax assignment, workers compensation policy selection, and wage/hour determination.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'Tax and legal classification of the worker for this position. W-2 (employee on payroll), 1099 (Independent Contractor), Corp-to-Corp (IC through their own entity), or EOR (Employer of Record arrangement). Critical for IRS compliance and co-employment risk management.']

  Note: 'Defines the detailed position specifications attached to a requisition. Captures job description, required education level, years of experience, work location type (on-site, remote, hybrid), shift type, travel requirements, physical demands, security clearance level, drug screen requirement, background check requirement, and any client-specific screening criteria. Represents the structured demand profile that drives candidate matching and submittal qualification.'
}

Table joborder.skill_requirement {
  skill_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the skill requirement record. Primary key for the skill requirement entity.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent job order (requisition) that this skill requirement belongs to. Links the skill requirement to the specific client requisition.']
  primary_skill_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (recruiter, account manager, or system administrator) who created this skill requirement record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  tertiary_skill_deactivated_by_user_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who deactivated this skill requirement. Null if the requirement is still active. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this skill requirement is currently active for the job order. True if active and used in candidate matching; False if deprecated or removed from requirements. Used for requirement lifecycle management.']
  alternative_skill_names varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of synonyms or alternative names for this skill. Used to improve candidate search and matching. Examples: Java could include J2EE, Java EE, Jakarta EE.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'The primary method used to evaluate candidate proficiency in this skill. Resume review indicates screening from candidate documents; technical test indicates formal assessment; behavioral interview indicates structured questioning; practical demonstration indicates hands-on evaluation; certification verification indicates credential validation; reference check indicates third-party validation.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'The name of the specific certification or credential required if certification_required_flag is True. Examples include PMP, CPA, RN, AWS Certified Solutions Architect, or Certified Welder. Null if no certification is required.']
  certification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal certification or credential is required for this skill. True if certification is mandatory; False if experience alone is sufficient. Used for credentialing and compliance verification.']
  client_specific_terminology varchar [note: 'Client-specific name or terminology for this skill if different from the standard skill_name. Used to align with client vocabulary in submittals and communications. Null if client uses standard terminology.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this skill requirement record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and requirement lifecycle tracking.']
  deactivated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this skill requirement was deactivated or removed from the job order. Null if the requirement is still active. Used for requirement lifecycle analysis.']
  deactivation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why this skill requirement was deactivated or removed from the job order. Examples include client request, requirement change, skill no longer needed, or job order modification. Null if still active.']
  last_market_rate_survey_date date [note: 'The date when market rate data for this skill was last surveyed or updated. Used to ensure pay rate and bill rate competitiveness. Null if no market survey has been conducted.']
  market_demand_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of current market demand for this skill. Low indicates abundant supply; moderate indicates balanced supply/demand; high indicates competitive market; critical indicates severe shortage. Used for sourcing strategy and rate negotiations.']
  minimum_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum acceptable score (0-100 scale) a candidate must achieve for this skill to be considered qualified. Used in automated candidate screening and QoS evaluation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this skill requirement record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  proficiency_level varchar [note: 'The level of expertise or mastery required for this skill. Beginner indicates basic familiarity; intermediate indicates working knowledge; advanced indicates deep expertise; expert indicates subject matter expertise; master indicates industry-recognized authority.']
  requirement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the skill is mandatory (must-have for candidate consideration), preferred (strongly desired but not required), or nice-to-have (beneficial but not critical). Used for candidate screening and Quality of Submission (QoS) scoring.']
  skill_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the skill type. Technical skills include programming languages and technical tools; soft skills include communication and leadership; industry-specific skills include domain knowledge; certifications include professional credentials; languages include spoken/written languages; tools and platforms include software applications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: technical|soft|industry_specific|certification|language|tool|platform — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  skill_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code or identifier for the skill from an external taxonomy or classification system. Examples include O*NET skill codes, SOC codes, or client-specific skill identifiers.']
  skill_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the skill requirement including specific technologies, methodologies, or competencies expected. Provides context for recruiters and candidates beyond the skill name.']
  skill_name varchar [note: 'The name of the skill, competency, or technology required for the job order. Examples include Java, Project Management, SAP, Nursing, Welding, or Communication Skills.']
  skill_obsolescence_date date [note: 'The date when this skill is expected to become obsolete or significantly less relevant. Used for workforce planning and skill gap analysis. Null if the skill has no known obsolescence timeline.']
  skill_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking indicating the relative importance of this skill within the job order. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for weighted candidate matching and QoS scoring algorithms.']
  skill_source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system or taxonomy from which this skill requirement was derived. Examples include O*NET, client-provided skill list, internal skill taxonomy, or VMS skill catalog.']
  skill_verification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal verification or validation of this skill is required before candidate submission or placement. True if verification is mandatory; False if self-reported skill is acceptable. Used for compliance and quality assurance.']
  skill_weight_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage weight assigned to this skill in the overall candidate evaluation and QoS scoring. All skill weights for a job order should sum to 100. Used in ATS matching algorithms.']
  training_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether training or upskilling programs are available for candidates who do not fully meet this skill requirement. True if training is offered; False if candidates must possess the skill prior to placement.']
  training_duration_hours decimal(7,2) [note: 'The estimated number of hours required to complete training for this skill if training_available_flag is True. Used for onboarding planning and Time to Start (TTS) calculations. Null if no training is available.']
  years_of_experience_required decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum number of years of hands-on experience required for this specific skill. Used in candidate screening and QoS scoring. Supports fractional years (e.g., 2.5 years).']

  Note: 'Captures the individual skill, competency, and technology requirements associated with a requisition. Stores skill name, skill category (technical, soft, industry-specific), proficiency level required (beginner, intermediate, expert), whether the skill is mandatory or preferred, years of experience required for the skill, and certification requirement flag. Enables structured skill-based candidate matching and QoS (Quality of Submission) scoring within the ATS.'
}

Table joborder.rate_override {
  rate_override_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rate override record. Primary key.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Rate overrides deviate from contracted baseline rates. Linking the source schedule enables approval workflows (overrides exceeding contract terms require escalation), variance tracking for client bill']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Rate overrides impact revenue and margin, requiring GL account mapping for proper revenue classification, financial statement accuracy, and audit support—critical when overrides change revenue recogni']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent job order (requisition) for which this rate override applies.']
  vendor_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'External identifier of the VMS rate card from which this override was derived or against which it was validated. Links to Beeline or other VMS platform.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this rate override was formally approved by the authorized approver (internal management or client stakeholder).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved this rate override. May be internal manager, finance director, or client procurement contact.']
  bill_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'The hourly or unit rate charged to the client for this job order. This is the revenue rate that appears on client invoices.']
  burden_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total burden cost per hour including payroll taxes (FICA, FUTA, SUTA), workers compensation insurance, benefits, and other statutory costs. Applied on top of pay rate.']
  client_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the client formally approved this rate override. Nullable if client approval is not required or not yet received.']
  client_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate override requires explicit client approval before activation. True if client sign-off is mandatory per MSA or VMS rules.']
  client_approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the client contact who approved this rate override. Typically a procurement manager, hiring manager, or VMS program manager.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rate override record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this rate override (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP).']
  dt_bill_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'The hourly bill rate charged to the client for double-time hours (typically hours worked on holidays or beyond a certain threshold). Usually 2x the regular bill rate.']
  dt_pay_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'The hourly pay rate paid to the worker for double-time hours. Typically 2x the regular pay rate, may be required by state law or client policy.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this rate override expires or is no longer applicable. Nullable for open-ended rate agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this rate override becomes active and applicable to placements and billing. Part of the rate validity period.']
  gross_margin_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The gross margin as a percentage of the bill rate. Calculated as ((bill_rate - pay_rate) / bill_rate) * 100. Key profitability metric.']
  markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage markup applied to the pay rate to calculate the bill rate. Calculated as ((bill_rate - pay_rate) / pay_rate) * 100.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rate override record was last modified. Updated whenever any field changes. Part of audit trail.']
  msa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the Master Service Agreement that governs this rate override. Links to the contractual framework.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, special conditions, or instructions related to this rate override. Used for internal communication and audit trail.']
  ot_bill_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'The hourly bill rate charged to the client for overtime hours (typically hours worked beyond 40 in a workweek). Usually 1.5x the regular bill rate.']
  ot_pay_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'The hourly pay rate paid to the worker for overtime hours. Must comply with FLSA overtime requirements (typically 1.5x regular pay rate).']
  override_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the rate override, used for tracking and reference in communications with clients and internal stakeholders.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for the rate override. Explains why rates differ from standard MSA or VMS rate card (e.g., specialized skills, market conditions, client negotiation, competitive pressure).']
  override_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate override. Tracks approval workflow and activation state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|rejected|active|expired|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pay_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'The hourly or unit rate paid to the placed worker for this job order. This is the base compensation rate before burden costs.']
  per_diem_allowance decimal(10,2) [note: 'Daily allowance provided to the worker for meals, lodging, or incidental expenses when the assignment requires travel or work away from home location.']
  rate_cap decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum bill rate ceiling negotiated with the client or imposed by VMS program rules. Rates cannot exceed this cap regardless of other factors.']
  rate_floor decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum bill rate floor negotiated with the client or required by internal profitability policies. Rates cannot fall below this floor.']
  rate_source varchar [note: 'Origin or basis for the rate override. Indicates whether rates come from Master Service Agreement (MSA) rate schedule, Vendor Management System (VMS) rate card, or were specially negotiated.']
  rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the rates (hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, per unit, per project). Defines how rates are applied and billed.']
  requested_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who requested this rate override. Typically the account manager, recruiter, or client relationship owner.']
  spread decimal(15,2) [note: 'The difference between bill rate and pay rate (bill_rate - pay_rate). Represents the gross margin before burden costs are applied.']

  Note: 'Stores the approved bill rate, pay rate, markup percentage, spread (bill rate minus pay rate), burden rate, and gross margin targets for a requisition. Captures OT (overtime) bill rate, DT (double time) bill rate, per diem allowance, and any client-negotiated rate caps or floors. Tracks rate effective dates, rate approval status, and the rate source (MSA rate schedule, VMS rate card, negotiated). Serves as the financial backbone of the job order connecting to billing and payroll domains.'
}

Table joborder.sla_commitment {
  sla_commitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sla_commitment']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client VMS or MSP program under which this SLA is governed, if applicable.']
