The business actively manages SLA measurement as an operational process: for each assignment, performance is measured against applicable SLAs during defined measurement periods, actual values are compared to targets, breaches are flagged, and penalties/credits are calculated. This measurement data is used for client QBRs, scorecard reporting, and contract compliance tracking.'
}

Table placement.assignment_credential_compliance {
  assignment_credential_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each assignment-credential compliance record']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the worker assignment requiring credential verification']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the credential type required for this assignment']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user (recruiter, compliance officer, or placement coordinator) who performed the credential verification for this assignment']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding compliance status, verification issues, or special circumstances for this credential on this assignment']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state for this credential on this assignment. Compliant = verified and current; Pending_verification = submitted but not yet verified; Expired = credential lapsed during assignment; Waived = client approved waiver; Not_required = requirement removed. Captured from detection phase relationship data.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the credential instance as it applies to this assignment. Tracked per assignment to trigger compliance alerts. Captured from detection phase relationship data.']
  is_client_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specific credential is mandatory per the client contract for this assignment, which may differ from the general credential definition']
  last_compliance_check_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which compliance status was reviewed or re-verified for this credential on this assignment']
  next_compliance_check_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for next compliance review, calculated based on credential renewal cycle and assignment duration']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which this specific credential was verified as valid for this assignment. Captured from detection phase relationship data.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify this credential for this assignment. Primary_source = verified directly with issuing authority; Candidate_provided = document submitted by candidate; Client_verified = client performed verification; Third_party_service = verified via background check vendor; System_integration = auto-verified via API integration. Captured from detection phase relationship data.']
  waiver_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the client approved a waiver for this credential requirement on this assignment']
  waiver_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the waiver expires and full compliance must be achieved']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a compliance waiver was granted by the client for this credential on this specific assignment. Captured from detection phase relationship data.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for granting the credential waiver for this assignment']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance verification relationship between a worker assignment and a required credential. It captures the verification status, expiration tracking, and waiver management for each credential required for a specific assignment. Each record links one assignment to one credential with attributes that exist only in the context of ensuring placement compliance and regulatory adherence.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, each assignment requires multiple credentials (professional licenses, safety certifications, immunizations, background checks, drug screens) and each credential type applies to many assignments over time. The business actively manages assignment-credential compliance as a distinct operational process, tracking verification dates, expiration monitoring, waiver approvals, and compliance status for each assignment-credential pair. This is not derivable from transaction history—it is an operational compliance management entity.'
}

Table placement.location_compliance_mandate {
  location_compliance_mandate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this location-requirement mandate record. Primary key.']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to compliance.compliance_requirement identifying the requirement enforced at this location']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance requirement that applies to this work location']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance team member responsible for auditing this requirement at this location. Enables workload distribution and accountability tracking.']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the work location where this compliance requirement is enforced']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state for this requirement at this location: compliant (all workers meet requirement), non_compliant (one or more violations detected), pending_review (audit in progress), exempt (location granted exemption), waived (client waived requirement).']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this location-requirement mandate record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this compliance requirement became enforceable at this specific work location. May differ from the requirement\'s global effective_date due to site-specific rollout schedules or client contract start dates.']
  enforcement_level varchar [note: 'Site-specific enforcement rigor for this requirement: strict (blocking - prevents placement if not met), standard (warning - flags non-compliance but allows placement with override), advisory (informational only). Driven by client contract terms and risk tolerance.']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation if this location has been granted an exemption or waiver from this requirement. Includes reference to approval authority and expiration date if applicable.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which compliance with this requirement was audited at this work location. Used to track audit frequency and identify locations overdue for review.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that last modified this mandate record.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this mandate record.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this requirement is mandatory (true) or recommended/optional (false) at this specific work location. Allows for site-specific enforcement variations where a federal requirement may be mandatory at federal contractor sites but optional elsewhere.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next compliance audit of this requirement at this location. Calculated based on compliance_frequency from the requirement master and last_audit_date.']
  site_specific_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing location-specific variations, exceptions, or implementation guidance for this requirement at this site. Examples: \'Client requires notarized I-9 copies\', \'OSHA posting must be in both English and Spanish\', \'Background check must include county-level search\'.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that created this mandate record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enforcement relationship between work locations and compliance requirements. It captures which federal, state, and client-specific compliance requirements are mandatory at each work location, along with site-specific enforcement parameters, audit history, and implementation status. Each record links one work location to one compliance requirement with attributes that exist only in the context of this site-specific mandate.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, work locations are subject to multiple compliance requirements (OSHA postings, I-9 verification, state-specific rules, client contractual mandates, federal contractor obligations), and each compliance requirement applies to many work locations across the client portfolio. The compliance team actively manages which requirements are enforced at which sites, tracks site-specific enforcement levels (strict vs. advisory), conducts location-specific audits, and maintains site-specific implementation notes (e.g., \'Client requires bilingual OSHA postings\'). This is an operational enforcement relationship, not a derivable correlation.'
}

Table placement.location_credential_requirement {
  location_credential_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for this location-credential requirement record. Primary key.']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the credential that is required at this work location']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to placement.work_location identifying the location with this requirement']
  client_mandated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential requirement was mandated by the client (true) versus being a Staffing Hr internal policy requirement (false). Affects waiver authority and compliance reporting.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this location-credential requirement record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this credential requirement becomes effective for this work location. Workers placed after this date must possess this credential.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which this credential requirement is no longer enforced for this work location. Null indicates an ongoing requirement.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after credential expiration during which the worker can continue working at this location while renewal is in progress. Overrides the credential definition grace period when location-specific rules apply.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this requirement record.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this requirement record.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential is absolutely mandatory for placement at this location (true) or preferred but not blocking (false). Mandatory credentials block placement if not held.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining location-specific nuances of this credential requirement, such as client-specific verification procedures or exemption conditions.']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this location-credential requirement. Active requirements are enforced during placement eligibility checks.']
  verification_level varchar [note: 'The level of verification required for this credential at this specific location. Some high-security or healthcare locations may require primary source verification even when the credential definition allows secondary verification.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this requirement record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the credential requirements mandated for a specific work location. It captures which credentials are required for workers to be placed at each location, with location-specific enforcement rules and effective dates. Each record links one work location to one credential with attributes that define the requirement parameters for that specific location-credential combination.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, work locations require multiple credentials (state licenses, facility-specific badges, OSHA certifications, immunizations, security clearances) and each credential type applies to multiple work locations across the client portfolio. The business actively manages location-credential requirement matrices to ensure placement compliance, with location-specific enforcement rules such as mandatory vs. preferred status, verification levels, and grace periods that vary by client site.'
}

Table timesheet.timesheet {
  timesheet_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the timesheet record. Primary key for the timesheet header entity representing a single worker-assignment-pay period combination. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — canonical primary key.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active placement or assignment under which the worker is performing services. Ties the timesheet to a specific job order, client, and pay/bill rate agreement. Essential for billing and payroll rate resolution.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client (end-customer) at whose site or under whose direction the worker performed services during this pay period. Used for client-level billing aggregation and SLA reporting.']
  client_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.rate_card. Business justification: Timesheets bill at rates defined in client rate cards; billing accuracy and rate card compliance audits require this link. Essential for bill rate validation, rate card compliance reporting, and margi']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Timesheets must validate bill/pay rates against contracted rate schedules for billing compliance and rate variance detection. Payroll and billing systems reconcile timesheet rates to contracted schedu']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Timesheets are charged to cost centers for client billing and internal P&L reporting. Staffing firms track profitability by cost center (client/project). Enables cost center variance analysis and clie']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Timesheet approval gates require verification that worker holds valid credentials for the assignment period. Staffing operations mandate credential compliance before hours are released to payroll/bill']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Timesheets drive GL postings for labor expense recognition under GAAP. Every approved timesheet posts to a GL account (typically labor expense) for financial statement preparation. Core to staffing fi']
  i9_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.i9_verification. Business justification: Timesheet approval and payroll processing require valid work authorization. Staffing firms must verify I-9 status before processing pay; expired work authorization blocks timesheet approval. Real busi']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client invoice record in Salesforce Revenue Cloud to which this timesheet\'s billable hours were applied. Enables timesheet-to-invoice traceability for billing reconciliation and revenue recognition. Populated after invoice generation; null prior to billing run.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Timesheets track work performed at specific client sites for location-based billing rates, state tax withholding, workers comp classification, and OSHA reporting. Essential for compliance and cost cen']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: Timesheets within managed programs follow program-specific SLAs and billing rules. Essential for managed program billing consolidation, program-level SLA measurement, and MSP fee calculation in staffi']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) that originated the worker\'s assignment. Enables timesheet-to-requisition traceability for workforce planning and fill-rate analytics.']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payroll processing batch in TempWorks Payroll to which this approved timesheet was assigned. Enables traceability from the timesheet to the payroll run, supporting payroll reconciliation, reprocessing, and audit. Populated after payroll processing; null prior to payroll run.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contingent worker who submitted this timesheet. Links to the worker/candidate master record in the staffing system. Supports payroll processing and worker-level labor analytics.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual (supervisor, client manager, or VMS approver) who approved or rejected the timesheet. Supports approval audit trail, accountability reporting, and SLA measurement for time-to-approve KPI. Required for FLSA §516 recordkeeping and SOC 2 Type II audit compliance.']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.vms_program. Business justification: Timesheets submitted through VMS platforms require program-level routing and compliance tracking. Essential for VMS submission routing, program-specific approval workflows, and supplier performance me']
  worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Worker classification (W2 vs IC) determines tax withholding, OT eligibility, and bill rate markup on timesheets. Misclassification creates liability. Real business process: compliant rate calculation ']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the timesheet through the submission and approval lifecycle. Controls downstream payroll processing and client billing eligibility. \'draft\' = worker has not yet submitted; \'submitted\' = awaiting supervisor/client approval; \'approved\' = authorized for payroll and billing; \'rejected\' = returned to worker for correction; \'pending_review\' = escalated for secondary review; \'voided\' = cancelled and excluded from processing.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the timesheet was formally approved by the authorized approver (supervisor or client manager). Triggers eligibility for payroll processing and client billing. Null if not yet approved. Supports approval SLA measurement and audit compliance.']
  bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Hourly bill rate (in USD) charged to the client for regular straight-time hours during this pay period. Sourced from the assignment/VMS rate card. Used by Salesforce Revenue Cloud to generate client invoices. Classified confidential as client pricing data.']
  billable_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total hours that are billable to the client for this pay period. Typically equals regular + OT + DT + holiday hours (excludes PTO and unpaid absences which are not client-billable). Authoritative input to Salesforce Revenue Cloud for client invoice generation. May differ from total_hours when non-billable paid time is present.']
  break_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total unpaid break time (meal periods) recorded during the pay period. Tracked to support state-mandated meal and rest break compliance (California IWC Wage Orders, Washington WAC 296-126-092, Oregon ORS 653.261). Ensures break deductions are correctly applied to gross hours to derive net payable hours.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the timesheet record was first created in Kronos Workforce Ready. Represents the audit record creation event. Required for FLSA §516 minimum 3-year retention compliance and SOC 2 Type II audit trail.']
  dt_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total double-time hours worked during the pay period. Applicable under state-specific laws (e.g., California Labor Code §510 — hours over 12 in a day or all hours on the 7th consecutive day). Billed at the DT bill rate and paid at 2x the regular pay rate. Null or zero in jurisdictions without DT requirements.']
  expense_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total approved expense reimbursements (in USD) for the pay period beyond per diem (e.g., mileage, tools, uniforms). Payable to the worker and potentially billable to the client per MSA terms. Tracked separately from wages for payroll and billing accuracy.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Worker\'s FLSA classification for this assignment: \'exempt\' (not entitled to OT under FLSA salary/duties tests) or \'non_exempt\' (entitled to OT at 1.5x for hours over 40/week). Drives overtime eligibility logic in payroll processing. Subject to DOL enforcement and IRS worker classification rules.']
  holiday_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Hours worked on designated company or client-observed holidays during the pay period. May be billed at a premium holiday bill rate and paid at a holiday pay rate per the assignment agreement or client MSA. Tracked separately for accurate payroll and billing rate application.']
  is_disputed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the timesheet has been formally disputed by the worker, client, or staffing firm. Triggers a dispute resolution workflow and places a hold on payroll processing and client billing until resolved. Supports dispute rate KPI and compliance audit.']
  is_late_submission boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the worker submitted the timesheet after the client or staffing firm\'s defined submission deadline for the pay period. Used for late submission rate KPI reporting, worker compliance tracking, and payroll processing delay root cause analysis.']
  kronos_timesheet_ref varchar [note: 'Native timesheet identifier from the Kronos Workforce Ready source system. Enables direct traceability from the Silver layer record back to the operational system of record for audit, reconciliation, and support purposes.']
  meal_break_waiver boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the worker signed a valid meal break waiver for this pay period, as permitted under California IWC Wage Orders for shifts of 6 hours or less, or mutual consent waivers for second meal periods. Required for California, Oregon, and Washington meal break compliance documentation. Null for non-applicable jurisdictions.']
  ot_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Hourly bill rate (in USD) charged to the client for overtime hours. Typically 1.5x the regular bill rate per the client MSA or VMS rate card. Used by Salesforce Revenue Cloud for OT billing line generation.']
  ot_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total overtime hours worked during the pay period, typically hours exceeding 40 in a workweek under FLSA federal rules, or daily thresholds under state law (e.g., California 8-hour daily OT rule). Billed at the OT bill rate and paid at 1.5x the regular pay rate. Critical for FLSA §207 compliance and accurate payroll/billing.']
  ot_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Hourly pay rate (in USD) applied to overtime hours. Typically 1.5x the regular pay rate per FLSA §207. May vary by state law or assignment agreement. Used by TempWorks Payroll for OT gross wage calculation.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the pay period covered by this timesheet. Defines the closing boundary for hours worked. Used in conjunction with pay_period_start_date for FLSA compliance, payroll cutoff, and billing period matching.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the pay period covered by this timesheet. Combined with pay_period_end_date defines the authoritative date range for hours worked, used for FLSA overtime calculation, payroll processing, and client invoice period alignment.']
  pay_period_type varchar [note: 'Frequency classification of the pay period covered by this timesheet. Determines payroll processing schedule and FLSA workweek overtime calculation methodology. \'weekly\' = 7-day period; \'biweekly\' = 14-day period; \'semi_monthly\' = twice per month; \'monthly\' = full calendar month.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Hourly pay rate (in USD) at which the worker is compensated for regular straight-time hours during this pay period. Sourced from the assignment agreement. Used by TempWorks Payroll to calculate gross wages. Subject to FLSA minimum wage compliance and state minimum wage laws. Classified confidential as worker compensation data.']
  per_diem_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total per diem reimbursement amount (in USD) for the pay period, covering meals, lodging, and incidental expenses for workers on travel assignments. Non-taxable up to IRS GSA per diem rates. Payable to the worker but may or may not be billable to the client depending on the MSA. Tracked separately from wages for IRS tax treatment.']
  pto_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Hours of paid time off (PTO) used by the worker during the pay period. Includes vacation, sick leave, and other approved paid absences. Payable but not billable to the client. Tracked for PTO balance management and payroll cost allocation. Subject to state-mandated paid sick leave laws (e.g., California SB 3, Washington ESSB 5975).']
  regular_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total straight-time (non-overtime, non-double-time) hours worked by the worker during the pay period. Represents the base billable and payable hours at the standard pay rate and bill rate. Core input to payroll gross pay calculation and client invoice generation. Subject to FLSA §207 threshold monitoring.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason provided by the approver when a timesheet is rejected. Captures the basis for rejection (e.g., hours discrepancy, missing punch, unauthorized OT, incorrect cost center). Supports worker correction guidance, rejection pattern analysis, and audit documentation.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the worker electronically submitted the timesheet for approval. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the timesheet lifecycle. Used for SLA tracking (time-to-approve), late submission reporting, and audit trail under FLSA §516 recordkeeping requirements.']
  timesheet_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally visible timesheet reference number used in worker communications, client invoices, and payroll remittances. Sourced from Kronos Workforce Ready and carried through to billing and payroll systems.']
  total_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Sum of all compensable hours for the pay period: regular + OT + DT + holiday + PTO hours. Represents the total payable hours used as the basis for gross pay calculation. Excludes unpaid break hours and unpaid absences. Authoritative hours figure fed to TempWorks Payroll for payroll processing.']
  unpaid_absence_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Hours of unpaid absence recorded during the pay period (e.g., unexcused absences, unpaid leave). Not billable to the client and not payable to the worker. Tracked for attendance management, assignment compliance monitoring, and potential assignment termination triggers.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the timesheet record. Tracks any changes post-creation including corrections, re-submissions, or approval status changes. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver layer and audit compliance.']
  vms_timesheet_ref varchar [note: 'External timesheet identifier assigned by the client\'s Vendor Management System (e.g., Beeline VMS) when the client manages contingent labor through a VMS program. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the staffing firm\'s Kronos records and the client\'s VMS approval records. Null for non-VMS-managed assignments.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Client-issued work order or purchase order number associated with the worker\'s assignment for this pay period. Required on client invoices for accounts payable matching. Sourced from the client\'s procurement or VMS system. Null if the client does not require PO-based billing.']
  worker_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the worker\'s engagement type for this timesheet period. Determines tax treatment, payroll processing path, and co-employment risk profile. \'w2_temp\' = W-2 temporary employee; \'w2_contract\' = W-2 contract worker; \'1099_ic\' = Independent Contractor (1099); \'corp_to_corp\' = C2C vendor engagement; \'eor\' = Employer of Record arrangement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: w2_temp|w2_contract|1099_ic|corp_to_corp|eor — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Core master record for a worker\'s timesheet submission covering a defined pay period. Represents the authoritative timesheet header sourced from Kronos Workforce Ready, capturing the worker, assignment, client, pay period dates, submission status, total regular hours, OT hours, DT hours, per diem, approval state, and audit timestamps. This is the SSOT for all billable and payable hours for a given worker-assignment-period combination. Subject to FLSA §516 recordkeeping requirements (minimum 3-year retention of hours worked, wages paid, and conditions of employment). Supports state-specific meal/rest break compliance tracking for California, Washington, Oregon, and other jurisdictions with mandatory break laws.'
}

Table timesheet.time_entry {
  time_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a single shift-level or daily time entry record. Primary key for the time_entry product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE — granular child record of a timesheet header representing one worked period.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active placement or assignment under which this time entry is recorded. Ties the worked hours to the specific job order, client, and bill/pay rate agreement governing this engagement.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client (end-customer) at whose worksite the hours were worked. Used for client billing, SLA reporting, and workforce analytics segmented by client account.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Individual time entries inherit rate schedules from assignments; payroll and billing systems validate each entry\'s bill/pay rates against contracted schedules. Real business process: line-item rate va']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Individual time entries must validate worker credentials to determine billable/payable status. Expired credentials make hours non-billable in VMS programs. Critical for per-entry compliance auditing i']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Individual time entries may post to different GL accounts based on labor category, shift differential, or client contract terms. Required for detailed expense classification and multi-GL timesheet sce']
  labor_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.labor_category. Business justification: time_entry currently has labor_category as STRING code. Should FK to labor_category.labor_category_id for authoritative labor category definition including rates, FLSA classification, and billing rule']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contingent worker whose time is being recorded. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE RESOURCE_REFERENCE category. Links to the worker master record for pay rate, classification, and compliance lookups.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisor, manager, or client approver who approved this time entry. Required for the approval workflow audit trail and to satisfy FLSA §516 recordkeeping requirements that time records be verified by an authorized representative.']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent timesheet header record that groups this time entry within a pay period. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE HEADER_REFERENCE category. Required for payroll aggregation and client billing rollup.']
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Each time entry must comply with FLSA/state wage-hour rules. OT calculation, meal break compliance, and minimum wage validation depend on wage-hour determination. Real business process: compliant time']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical client worksite location where the hours were worked. Required for workers\' compensation premium calculation (state and class code), OSHA recordkeeping, and multi-state payroll tax jurisdiction determination.']
  absence_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for any absence or non-worked time within this entry period (e.g., SICK, FMLA, JURY, BEREAVEMENT, NO_SHOW, WEATHER). Null when the worker was present for the full scheduled shift. Required for FMLA tracking and workforce attendance analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sick|fmla|jury_duty|bereavement|no_show|weather|personal|military — promote to reference product]']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'The actual clock-out date-time for the worked period. Paired with actual_start_time and break_duration_minutes to compute net hours worked. Required for FLSA §516 and California Labor Code §226 itemized wage statement backing data.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'The actual clock-in date-time for the worked period as captured by Kronos (badge swipe, biometric, mobile punch, or manual entry). Used for attendance analysis, late-arrival tracking, and FLSA recordkeeping.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this time entry was approved by the authorized approver. Distinct from submission and creation timestamps. Required for SLA measurement (time-to-approve) and FLSA §516 audit trail.']
  bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly bill rate (in USD) charged to the client for this time entry. Captured at entry level to preserve the contractual rate-in-effect. Used by Salesforce Revenue Cloud to generate client invoices. The spread (bill_rate − pay_rate) drives gross margin reporting.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total unpaid break time in minutes deducted from the gross shift duration to arrive at net hours worked. Includes all meal and rest break periods. Required for accurate net hours calculation and California Labor Code §512 meal period compliance.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Client or internal cost center code to which the labor hours are allocated for billing and financial reporting. Sourced from the client\'s organizational hierarchy in Beeline VMS or Salesforce Revenue Cloud. Enables cost allocation reporting in Oracle NetSuite ERP.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this time entry record was first created in the source system (Kronos Workforce Ready). Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Used for data lineage, ETL watermarking, and audit trail.']
  entry_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the worked period occurred (yyyy-MM-dd). Serves as the primary business event date for this time entry. Used for daily labor reporting, schedule variance analysis, and FLSA workweek calculations. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE anchor date.']
  entry_source varchar [note: 'The method or system through which this time entry was captured: Kronos hardware clock (badge/biometric), Kronos mobile app, manual supervisor entry, VMS import from Beeline, or supervisor-entered correction. Supports audit trail requirements and data quality monitoring.']
  entry_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the time entry record. Controls whether the entry is editable, pending approval, approved for payroll/billing processing, rejected for correction, voided, or locked after payroll run. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT lifecycle dimension.']
  flsa_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is classified as FLSA-exempt (True) for this engagement, meaning overtime pay requirements under FLSA §207 do not apply. False for non-exempt workers who are entitled to OT at 1.5x for hours exceeding 40 per workweek. Critical for correct OT calculation in payroll.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hours in this time entry are billable to the client. False for internal training time, orientation hours, or non-billable absence periods. Drives inclusion/exclusion in Salesforce Revenue Cloud invoice generation.']
  is_payable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hours in this time entry are payable to the worker. May differ from is_billable in cases such as orientation (payable but not billable) or disputed hours (billable but payment withheld pending resolution). Drives TempWorks Payroll gross pay inclusion.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Standardized job classification code identifying the role or position being performed during this time entry. Aligns with the job order\'s position code and is used for EEOC reporting, OFCCP compliance, and workforce analytics by job classification.']
  kronos_punch_ref varchar [note: 'Source system identifier from Kronos Workforce Ready linking this time entry back to the originating raw punch event record. Enables drill-through from the Silver layer aggregated entry to the raw punch event for audit and dispute resolution. Null for manually entered records.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of work performed during this entry (e.g., Light Industrial, IT Professional, Healthcare, Administrative, Engineering). Used for workforce analytics, cost allocation, and Beeline VMS rate card alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: light_industrial|it_professional|healthcare|administrative|engineering|skilled_trades|clerical|professional — promote to reference product]']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering number of this time entry within its parent timesheet header. Enables ordered display and processing of multiple daily entries within a single timesheet period. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE category.']
  meal_break_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required meal break was taken in compliance with applicable state law (e.g., California Labor Code §512 requires a 30-minute unpaid meal period for shifts exceeding 5 hours). True = compliant; False = potential violation requiring premium pay or legal review.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to this time entry record. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Tracks corrections, retroactive adjustments, and re-approvals. Essential for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver layer pipeline.']
  net_hours_worked decimal(6,4) [note: 'Actual compensable hours worked during this entry period, computed as (actual_end_time − actual_start_time) − break_duration_minutes, expressed in decimal hours. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_QUANTITY category. This is the authoritative hours figure fed to payroll and client billing. Subject to FLSA §516 recordkeeping.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly pay rate (in USD) applicable to this time entry for worker compensation. Captured at the entry level to preserve the rate-in-effect at time of work, which may differ from the current placement rate due to retroactive adjustments or mid-period rate changes. Feeds TempWorks Payroll gross pay calculation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT monetary dimension.']
  pay_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hours worked for compensation purposes: regular straight-time (REG), overtime at 1.5x (OT), double-time at 2x (DT), holiday premium, per diem allowance, or paid time off (PTO). Drives the pay rate multiplier applied in TempWorks Payroll and the bill rate applied in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  per_diem_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Per diem allowance amount (in USD) associated with this time entry for travel, lodging, or meal reimbursement when the worker is on a remote or travel assignment. Tracked separately from wages for IRS per diem tax treatment. Zero if no per diem applies.']
  pto_hours decimal(6,4) [note: 'Number of paid time off (PTO) hours applied to this entry when the worker is absent but entitled to paid leave. Distinct from net_hours_worked (which reflects physically worked hours). Feeds PTO balance deduction in SAP SuccessFactors and payroll processing in TempWorks.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason provided by the approver when a time entry is rejected (entry_status = rejected). Documents the basis for rejection to guide worker or supervisor correction. Null when entry is not in rejected status.']
  rest_break_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required paid rest breaks were provided in compliance with applicable state law (e.g., California IWC Wage Orders require a 10-minute paid rest period for every 4 hours worked). True = compliant; False = potential premium pay obligation.']
  schedule_variance_hours decimal(6,4) [note: 'Delta between planned hours (derived from scheduled_start_time and scheduled_end_time) and actual net_hours_worked for this entry. Positive value indicates overtime relative to schedule; negative indicates early departure or absence. Key KPI for workforce scheduling compliance and dispatch analytics.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'The planned shift end date-time as defined in the worker\'s schedule. Paired with scheduled_start_time to derive planned shift duration for schedule variance and workforce planning analytics.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'The planned shift start date-time as defined in the worker\'s schedule in Kronos Workforce Ready. Used to compute schedule variance (planned vs. actual hours delta) and to assess punctuality and dispatch compliance.']
  shift_differential_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the applicable shift differential pay rule (e.g., EVE15, NIGHT20, WKD10). Maps to the differential rate table in TempWorks Payroll to apply the correct premium multiplier to base pay rate. Distinct from shift_type which is a broad category.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the shift period for differential pay and workforce scheduling analytics. Day, evening, night, weekend, and holiday shifts may carry different pay and bill rate differentials per the client MSA or worker agreement.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code (e.g., CA, NY, TX) identifying the state where the work was physically performed. Determines applicable state wage and hour laws, state income tax withholding jurisdiction, SUTA rate, and state-specific break and overtime rules. Critical for multi-state payroll compliance.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the worker or supervisor submitted this time entry for approval. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category. Used to measure submission timeliness against payroll cutoff deadlines and SLA compliance.']
  vms_time_entry_ref varchar [note: 'External reference identifier assigned by the client\'s Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, for this time entry when the client manages contingent labor through a VMS program. Enables reconciliation between Staffing Hr\'s internal records and the client\'s VMS for invoice matching and dispute resolution.']
  workers_comp_class_code varchar [note: 'NCCI or state-specific workers\' compensation classification code applicable to the work performed during this entry. Determined by the job type and worksite state. Used by TempWorks Payroll to calculate workers\' comp premium allocation and for state insurance reporting.']

  Note: 'Granular daily or shift-level time record representing a single worked period for a contingent worker. This is the unified SSOT for all shift-level and daily worked-time data — there is no separate shift product. Captures the specific date, planned and actual start/end times, break duration, meal break compliance flag, rest break compliance flag, net hours worked, pay type (regular, OT, DT), shift differential code, shift type (day/evening/night/weekend), labor category, cost center, schedule variance (planned vs actual hours delta), and entry source (Kronos clock, mobile, manual). Child record of the timesheet header. Incorporates shift-level schedule comparison, shift differential tracking, and dispatch-relevant shift metadata. Feeds both payroll and billing calculations. Links raw punch events to the timesheet summary. Subject to FLSA §516 and state-specific recordkeeping requirements including California Labor Code §226 (itemized wage statement backing data).'
}

Table timesheet.timesheet_approval_workflow {
  timesheet_approval_workflow_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each timesheet approval workflow record. Serves as the primary key for this entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — tracks the multi-step approval lifecycle for a single timesheet submission.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker placement or assignment associated with this timesheet approval workflow. Ties the approval to the specific contingent worker assignment and client engagement.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client organization for whom the contingent worker performed services. Used to route approval to the correct client contact and enforce client-specific SLA rules.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Client-side timesheet approvers are contacts; approval routing, SLA tracking, and audit trails require knowing which client contact approved hours. Critical for dispute resolution and compliance audit']
  pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll pay period to which the approved timesheet hours will be applied. Links the approval workflow to downstream payroll processing and ensures hours are captured in the correct pay cycle.']
  primary_timesheet_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual (supervisor, client contact, or back-office auditor) who performed the approval action at this workflow stage. Enables full audit trail of approver identity.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contingent worker (candidate) who submitted the timesheet being reviewed in this approval workflow. Supports worker-level audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  tertiary_timesheet_delegated_approver_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual who acted on behalf of the primary approver when delegation was in effect (e.g., during leave or absence). Populated only when approval_action is \'delegated\'. Supports audit trail integrity.']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent timesheet record for which this approval workflow instance was initiated. Links the approval workflow to the specific worker time submission being reviewed.']
  action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the approver performed the approval action at this workflow stage. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Critical for SLA compliance measurement and audit trail.']
  approval_action varchar [note: 'The specific action taken by the approver at this workflow stage. Distinct from approval_status in that it captures the discrete action event (e.g., delegated) rather than the resulting state. Used for audit trail and workflow analytics.']
  approval_channel varchar [note: 'The interface or channel through which the approver submitted their approval action. Supports channel adoption analytics and helps identify approval bottlenecks by channel type.']
  approval_stage varchar [note: 'The specific step in the multi-stage approval workflow at which this record applies. Stages include worker submission, supervisor review, client approval, back-office audit, and payroll release. Each stage represents a distinct checkpoint in the timesheet lifecycle.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this approval workflow stage. Indicates whether the stage is awaiting action (pending), has been approved, rejected, escalated to a higher authority, auto-approved per system rules, or recalled by the submitter.']
  approver_role varchar [note: 'The functional role of the approver at this workflow stage. Distinguishes between worker self-submission, direct supervisor review, client manager approval, back-office audit, payroll admin release, and MSP coordinator oversight.']
  auto_approval_rule_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system-defined auto-approval rule that triggered automatic approval of this timesheet stage without manual intervention. Populated only when approval_action is \'auto_approved\'. Enables audit of automated decisions.']
  bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly rate at which the client is billed for the worker\'s time as defined in the client contract or VMS program. Used to validate that approved hours will generate the correct client invoice amount.']
  billing_released_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approved timesheet hours have been released to the client billing system (Salesforce Revenue Cloud) for invoice generation. Enables reconciliation between approved hours and billed hours.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this approval workflow record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and SOC 2 compliance purposes.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this timesheet approval workflow record is associated with a formal dispute raised by the worker, client, or staffing agency. When True, the record is flagged for dispute resolution review and may be excluded from standard payroll processing until resolved.']
  dispute_reference_number varchar [note: 'The case or ticket number associated with a formal timesheet dispute. Populated when dispute_flag is True. Used to link the approval workflow record to the dispute management process for resolution tracking.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded explanation for why this approval stage was escalated to a higher authority. Populated when approval_action is \'escalated\'. Supports escalation pattern analysis and workflow optimization.']
  final_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this workflow record represents the final, fully-approved state of the timesheet across all required approval stages. When True, the timesheet is cleared for payroll processing and client billing. Enables efficient filtering for downstream payroll and billing feeds.']
  kronos_timesheet_ref varchar [note: 'The source system reference identifier from Kronos Workforce Ready for the timesheet record associated with this approval workflow. Supports data lineage tracing and reconciliation with the system of record.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly rate at which the contingent worker is compensated for approved hours. Used to validate payroll calculations downstream. The spread (bill_rate minus pay_rate) represents gross margin.']
  payroll_released_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approved timesheet hours have been released to the payroll processing system (TempWorks Payroll) for worker compensation. Distinct from final_approved_flag as it confirms the downstream handoff has occurred.']
  per_diem_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total per diem dollar amount claimed by the worker on this timesheet. Populated when per_diem_claimed is True. Subject to IRS per diem rate limits and client contract terms. Feeds into billing and payroll calculations.']
  per_diem_claimed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker claimed a per diem allowance (daily expense reimbursement for travel, meals, or lodging) on this timesheet. Per diem claims require separate approval validation against the assignment agreement.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason the timesheet was rejected at this approval stage. Used for root-cause analysis of rejection patterns and worker coaching. Populated only when approval_action is \'rejected\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hours_discrepancy|missing_entries|unauthorized_overtime|unapproved_per_diem|policy_violation|duplicate_submission|other — promote to reference product]']
  rejection_reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative provided by the approver explaining the specific reason for rejecting the timesheet at this stage. Supplements the rejection_reason_code with contextual detail for dispute resolution and worker communication.']
  resubmission_count varchar [note: 'The number of times the worker has resubmitted this timesheet after a rejection. A high resubmission count indicates recurring errors or disputes and is used for quality-of-submission (QoS) analytics and worker coaching.']
  sla_breach_minutes varchar [note: 'The number of minutes by which the approval action exceeded the SLA deadline. Populated only when sla_compliant_flag is False. Zero or null when SLA was met. Enables severity-based SLA breach analysis and client reporting.']
  sla_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approval action was completed within the SLA-defined deadline for this stage. True if the action_timestamp is on or before sla_due_timestamp; False if the SLA was breached. Supports SLA compliance reporting and client KPI dashboards.']
  sla_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The contractually or operationally defined deadline by which this approval stage must be completed per the applicable SLA. Derived from the SLA configuration for the client or program. Used to calculate SLA compliance.']
  stage_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering of this approval stage within the overall workflow. Enables reconstruction of the full approval chain in chronological order and supports multi-step workflow analytics.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the worker originally submitted the timesheet that triggered this approval workflow. Used as the baseline for SLA elapsed time calculations and Time-to-Approve (TTA) reporting.']
  total_dt_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of double-time (DT) hours recorded on the timesheet, applicable in jurisdictions or contracts where hours beyond a threshold (e.g., 12 hours/day or 7th consecutive day) are compensated at double the regular rate.']
  total_ot_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of overtime (OT) hours recorded on the timesheet, typically hours worked beyond 40 per week or 8 per day per FLSA or applicable state law. Requires explicit approval and is a common rejection trigger.']
  total_regular_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of straight-time (non-overtime) hours recorded on the timesheet being reviewed in this approval workflow. Used to validate hours against scheduled hours and client purchase order limits.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this approval workflow record was last modified. Tracks the most recent state change for audit trail purposes and supports incremental data pipeline processing.']
  vms_workflow_ref varchar [note: 'The external workflow identifier assigned by the Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, for this timesheet approval transaction. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the internal approval record and the client\'s VMS portal.']
  work_week_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the work week covered by the timesheet being approved. Used to align the approval workflow with payroll processing cycles and client billing periods.']
  workflow_instance_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this approval workflow instance, used in communications, dispute resolution, and audit references. Typically formatted as a sequential or alphanumeric code (e.g., WF-2024-00123).']

  Note: 'Tracks the multi-step approval workflow lifecycle for a timesheet submission. Captures each approval stage (worker submission, supervisor review, client approval, back-office audit), the approver identity, approval action (approved, rejected, escalated, auto-approved), action timestamp, rejection reason, and SLA compliance flag. Enables full audit trail of who approved what and when, supporting dispute resolution and compliance requirements.'
}

Table timesheet.absence_record {
  absence_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each absence record. Primary key for the absence_record data product within the timesheet domain.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the active placement or assignment during which the absence occurred. Used to associate the absence with the specific client engagement and job order.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client organization at whose worksite the worker was assigned when the absence occurred. Used for client SLA compliance tracking and notification workflows.']
  i9_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.i9_verification. Business justification: Extended absences may trigger work authorization re-verification, especially for visa holders with expiring EADs. Staffing firms must track I-9 status during leave. Real business process: work authori']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Absences impact client site staffing levels and trigger replacement workflows; site managers need absence visibility for scheduling. Essential for client site headcount management and replacement requ']
  primary_absence_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contingent worker associated with this absence record. Links to the worker master record in the workforce management system.']
  pto_accrual_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.pto_accrual. Business justification: Absence records consume PTO balances tracked in pto_accrual. This link enables leave balance reconciliation, ensures absence hours deducted match accrual transactions, and supports audit of PTO usage ']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the timesheet record for the pay period in which this absence falls. Links the absence to the authoritative hours record for payroll and billing.']
  absence_duration_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total calendar duration of the absence expressed in days (may be fractional for partial-day absences). Derived from absence_start_date and absence_end_date but stored for reporting efficiency and workforce availability analytics.']
  absence_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the absence period. Null for open-ended absences (e.g., ongoing FMLA leave). Combined with absence_start_date to determine total absence duration.']
  absence_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of scheduled work hours missed during the absence period. Used for payroll deduction calculations, PTO balance consumption, and workforce availability reporting.']
  absence_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the worker, manager, or HR coordinator providing additional context for the absence (e.g., nature of illness, family emergency details). Classified as confidential due to potential sensitivity of leave-related information.']
  absence_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to this absence record (e.g., ABS-2024-00123). Used in worker communications, manager approvals, and leave management correspondence.']
  absence_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the absence period. Used to calculate absence duration, determine pay period impact, and trigger FMLA tracking windows.']
  absence_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the absence by leave category as defined in the leave management policy. Drives payroll treatment (paid vs. unpaid), compliance recordkeeping, and workforce availability reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PTO|Sick Leave|FMLA|Holiday|Jury Duty|Bereavement|Unexcused|Personal|Military|Workers Comp — promote to reference product]']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the absence request through the manager approval process. Drives payroll processing eligibility and client notification triggers. Pending = submitted awaiting review; Approved = manager authorized; Denied = rejected; Cancelled = worker withdrew; Under Review = escalated.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or system identifier of the manager, supervisor, or automated workflow that approved or denied the absence request. Supports audit trail and accountability for leave decisions.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the absence request was approved or denied by the authorized approver. Null if still pending. Used for approval workflow SLA tracking and audit trail.']
  client_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the client was notified of the worker absence. Null if client_notified is False. Used to measure notification SLA compliance against MSA contractual obligations.']
  client_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client organization has been notified of this worker absence (True) or not (False). Critical for client SLA compliance tracking — unnotified absences may breach MSA notification obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this absence record was first created in the system. Establishes the audit trail start point and is used for data lineage tracking in the Silver layer lakehouse.']
  documentation_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the worker must submit required leave documentation. Under FMLA, employers may require documentation within 15 calendar days of the leave request. Drives compliance follow-up workflows.']
  documentation_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required supporting documentation (e.g., physician certification for FMLA, jury summons, death certificate for bereavement) has been received and verified (True) or is outstanding (False).']
  fmla_hours_used decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of hours counted against the worker\'s annual FMLA entitlement (up to 480 hours / 12 weeks per year). Populated only when fmla_qualifying is True. Required for DOL FMLA recordkeeping compliance.']
  fmla_qualifying boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence qualifies under the Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) (True) or not (False). Triggers FMLA tracking, 3-year recordkeeping retention, and DOL compliance workflows.']
  is_paid boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the absence is compensated (True) or unpaid (False). Determines payroll treatment: paid absences draw from PTO/sick balances or holiday pay; unpaid absences result in payroll deductions.']
  is_partial_day boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the absence covers only part of a scheduled workday (True) rather than full days (False). Partial-day absences require hour-level tracking for accurate payroll deduction and FMLA intermittent leave accounting.']
  kronos_leave_ref varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this absence record as assigned by the Kronos Workforce Ready leave management module. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and integration with the Silver layer from the operational system of record.']
  leave_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code from the Kronos leave management module identifying the specific reason for the absence (e.g., FMLA-SELF, FMLA-FAMILY, PSL-CA, JURY, BRVMT-IMM). Supports regulatory reporting and state-specific paid sick leave law compliance.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the payroll pay period in which this absence record falls. Combined with pay_period_start_date to define the payroll processing window for this absence.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the payroll pay period in which this absence record falls. Used to align absence data with payroll processing cycles in TempWorks Payroll.']
  payroll_processed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence record has been included in a completed payroll processing run (True) or is still pending payroll (False). Prevents double-processing and confirms the absence has been reflected in worker compensation.']
  per_diem_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker\'s per diem allowance is affected by this absence (True) or not (False). Per diem payments may be suspended or prorated for absent days depending on the assignment contract terms.']
  pto_balance_before decimal(6,2) [note: 'Worker\'s available PTO balance in hours immediately prior to this absence being applied. Provides a point-in-time snapshot for audit, dispute resolution, and worker self-service reporting.']
  pto_hours_consumed decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of accrued Paid Time Off (PTO) hours deducted from the worker\'s PTO balance to cover this absence. Zero for unpaid absences or absences covered by other leave types (e.g., FMLA, holiday pay).']
  replacement_requested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a replacement worker has been requested to cover this absence (True) or not (False). Triggers workforce scheduling and dispatch workflows to source a backfill worker for the client site.']
  scheduled_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of hours the worker was originally scheduled to work during the absence period. Provides the baseline against which absence_hours is measured for partial-day absence tracking.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence has caused or is at risk of causing a breach of the client SLA (True) — for example, if the absence results in insufficient staffing levels below the contracted headcount threshold. Feeds client SLA compliance reporting.']
  state_leave_law_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the applicable state-specific paid sick leave or family leave law governing this absence (e.g., CA-HWHFA for California Healthy Workplaces Healthy Families Act, NY-PFL for New York Paid Family Leave). Ensures compliance with state-level leave mandates beyond federal FMLA.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the worker or manager submitted the absence request in Kronos Workforce Ready. Establishes the start of the approval workflow and is used for SLA measurement of approval turnaround time.']
  termination_trigger boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence has triggered an assignment termination workflow (True) in Bullhorn ATS/CRM. Applicable for contingent workers with unexcused absences that breach the no-call/no-show policy threshold.']
  unexcused_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence has been classified as unexcused (True) — i.e., the worker did not provide adequate notice or documentation. Unexcused absences for contingent workers may trigger assignment termination workflows per staffing agency policy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this absence record was last modified. Tracks changes to approval status, documentation receipt, payroll processing, and other lifecycle updates for audit and data quality purposes.']

  Note: 'Records worker absences, PTO usage, sick leave, FMLA leave, holiday observances, jury duty, bereavement, and unexcused absences within a pay period. Captures absence type, absence date range, hours absent, PTO balance consumed, leave reason code, approval status, paid/unpaid indicator, client notification flag, and replacement worker request trigger. Sourced from Kronos Workforce Ready leave management module. Feeds payroll deductions, workforce availability reporting, and client SLA compliance tracking. For contingent workers, unexcused absences may trigger assignment termination workflows. Subject to FMLA recordkeeping requirements (3-year retention) and state-specific paid sick leave laws (e.g., California Healthy Workplaces, Healthy Families Act).'
}

Table timesheet.schedule {
  schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the planned work schedule record in the Kronos Workforce Ready scheduling module. Primary key for the schedule data product.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contingent worker placement assignment this schedule belongs to. Links the planned schedule to the active placement record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client organization at whose worksite the scheduled shift is to be performed. Links to the client master record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Shift schedules are built using contracted rate cards; scheduling systems pull bill/pay rates from rate schedules when creating shifts for accurate cost forecasting. Real business process: shift costi']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) that originated the placement and governs the scheduled work. Links to the job order record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  ot_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.ot_rule. Business justification: schedule currently has ot_rule_type as STRING and ot_threshold_hours as DECIMAL. Should FK to ot_rule.ot_rule_id for authoritative OT calculation rules including daily/weekly thresholds, multipliers, ']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contingent worker whose planned work schedule is defined by this record. Links to the candidate/worker master record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  readiness_status_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.readiness_status. Business justification: Schedules can only be published for workers meeting credential requirements for that assignment/client. Scheduling systems validate readiness_status before confirming shifts. Standard gate in VMS/MSP ']
  supervisor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the on-site supervisor or manager responsible for overseeing the contingent worker during this scheduled shift. Used for escalation routing, timesheet approval workflows, and client relationship management.']
  template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reusable schedule template in Kronos Workforce Ready from which this schedule instance was generated. Enables template-based scheduling for recurring shift patterns and simplifies bulk schedule management.']
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Schedule creation must respect wage-hour determinations: OT thresholds, meal break requirements, rest periods, and spread-of-hours rules. Real business process: compliant shift scheduling to avoid vio']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical worksite or client facility where the scheduled shift is to be performed. Used for geographic workforce distribution reporting, per diem eligibility determination, and site-level scheduling analytics.']
  bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly rate charged to the client for the contingent worker\'s time on this scheduled shift. Used for client invoicing in Salesforce Revenue Cloud and for spread/gross margin calculation. Confidential business financial data.']
  confirmed_by_worker boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contingent worker has acknowledged and confirmed the scheduled shift in Kronos Workforce Ready. Used for workforce readiness tracking and to reduce no-show risk.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Client or internal cost center code to which the scheduled labor hours are to be allocated. Used for client billing segmentation, internal labor cost allocation, and financial reporting in Oracle NetSuite ERP.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this schedule record was first created in the source system (Kronos Workforce Ready). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and schedule lead-time analytics.']
  day_of_week varchar [note: 'The day of the week on which this shift is scheduled. Used for recurring schedule pattern analysis, weekend/weekday differential pay identification, and workforce scheduling analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Client department or business unit code associated with the scheduled shift. Used for client-side labor distribution reporting, workforce analytics, and billing breakdown by department.']
  dt_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly rate charged to the client for double-time (DT) hours on this schedule. Used for accurate client invoicing when DT hours are incurred per the applicable rate agreement.']
  dt_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly compensation rate applicable to double-time (DT) hours, typically triggered after a defined threshold of daily or weekly hours (e.g., 12+ hours/day in certain states). Used for payroll processing in TempWorks Payroll.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this schedule definition expires or is superseded. Nullable for open-ended schedules that remain in effect until the assignment end date. Used for schedule versioning and historical comparison.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The calendar date from which this schedule definition becomes active and applicable to the worker\'s assignment. Defines the beginning of the schedule\'s validity window. Used for schedule versioning and historical comparison.']
  expected_weekly_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total planned working hours per week for the worker on this assignment schedule. Used for FTE (Full-Time Equivalent) calculation, OT threshold monitoring, and workforce planning. Typically 40 hours for full-time contingent placements.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hours on this scheduled shift are billable to the client. False for internal training shifts, orientation periods, or non-billable administrative time. Drives client invoicing logic in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  is_holiday boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scheduled shift falls on a recognized holiday, which may trigger holiday premium pay rates per the client MSA (Master Service Agreement) or worker contract.']
  is_per_diem_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contingent worker is eligible to receive per diem allowance for this scheduled shift based on travel distance, assignment location, and IRS tax home rules.']
  kronos_schedule_ref varchar [note: 'The native schedule record identifier as assigned by the Kronos Workforce Ready source system. Used for cross-system reconciliation, data lineage, and re-ingestion deduplication in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this schedule record was most recently updated in the source system. Used for change detection in incremental data loads, audit trail, and schedule modification tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes or special instructions associated with this scheduled shift, such as dress code requirements, access badge instructions, or client-specific directives. Not used for structured data.']
  ot_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly rate charged to the client for overtime (OT) hours worked beyond the standard threshold on this schedule. Used for accurate client invoicing when OT hours are incurred.']
  ot_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly compensation rate applicable to overtime (OT) hours worked beyond the standard threshold (typically 40 hours/week or 8 hours/day) on this schedule. Must comply with FLSA overtime premium requirements (minimum 1.5x regular pay rate).']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly compensation rate to be paid to the contingent worker for hours worked on this scheduled shift. Used for payroll processing in TempWorks Payroll and for spread/gross margin calculation. Confidential business financial data.']
  per_diem_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Daily allowance amount payable to the contingent worker for meals, lodging, or incidental expenses when the assignment requires travel or work away from the worker\'s tax home. Governed by IRS per diem rules. Null if per diem is not applicable to this schedule.']
  position_title varchar [note: 'The job title or role designation of the contingent worker for this scheduled shift, as defined in the job order. Used for workforce reporting, skill-based scheduling analytics, and compliance with EEOC job classification requirements.']
  published_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the schedule was published and made visible to the contingent worker. Used to measure schedule advance notice compliance and worker communication lead time.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the schedule record as assigned by Kronos Workforce Ready (e.g., SCH-2024-00123). Used for cross-system reference and operational communication.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the planned schedule record. Draft indicates the schedule is being built; published indicates it has been released to the worker; confirmed indicates worker acknowledgement; modified indicates a post-publish change; cancelled indicates the shift was removed; completed indicates the shift period has passed.']
  scheduled_break_minutes varchar [note: 'Total planned unpaid break duration in minutes for this shift as defined in the schedule template. Used to calculate net scheduled working hours and to validate compliance with mandatory rest period regulations.']
  scheduled_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of planned working hours for this shift, excluding unpaid break time. Represents the expected billable and payable hours for the shift. Used as the baseline for actual vs. scheduled variance reporting and OT threshold monitoring.']
  shift_date date [note: 'The specific calendar date on which this scheduled shift is to occur. Distinct from shift_start_time in that it provides a day-level grain for schedule reporting, calendar views, and daily workforce planning without requiring timestamp precision.']
  shift_end_time timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time at which the contingent worker\'s shift is scheduled to end, expressed in local worksite time with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used in conjunction with shift_start_time to derive scheduled shift duration for variance analysis.']
  shift_start_time timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time at which the contingent worker\'s shift is scheduled to begin, expressed in local worksite time with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the baseline for actual time-entry variance analysis and OT calculation.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scheduled shift by time-of-day or day-of-week category. Used for differential pay rate application, OT (Overtime) and DT (Double Time) rule triggering, and workforce analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: day|evening|night|weekend|holiday|on_call — promote to reference product if additional shift types are required]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this schedule record was sourced or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across integrated systems in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  worker_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the contingent worker confirmed the scheduled shift. Null if the worker has not yet confirmed. Used for advance notice compliance measurement and scheduling analytics.']

  Note: 'Defines the planned work schedule for a contingent worker on a specific assignment, including scheduled shift start/end times, days of week, shift type (day/evening/night/weekend), expected weekly hours, and effective date range.
