Sourced from Kronos Workforce Ready scheduling module. Serves as the baseline against which actual time entries are compared for variance analysis and OT calculation.'
}

Table timesheet.shift {
  shift_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for a specific scheduled or worked shift instance for a contingent worker. Primary key for the shift data product in the Kronos Workforce Ready time and attendance system.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the active placement or assignment under which this shift is worked. Links the shift to the specific job order and client engagement governing the worker\'s deployment.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client organization at whose worksite this shift is performed. Used for client-level labor reporting, billing, and SLA compliance tracking.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Actual worked shifts must reference the rate schedule used for billing/payroll; variance analysis compares shift rates to contracted rates for margin analysis. Real business process: shift billing rec']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the job order (requisition) associated with this shift. Connects the shift to the client\'s staffing request for labor analytics and billing reconciliation.']
  pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payroll pay period to which this shift belongs. Links the shift to the correct payroll processing cycle in TempWorks Payroll for wage calculation and tax filing.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contingent worker assigned to this shift. Links to the worker/candidate master record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM and Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.schedule. Business justification: shift represents an actual worked shift instance, while schedule represents the planned schedule. A shift should link back to the schedule it was planned under to enable variance analysis (scheduled v']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supervisor, manager, or client contact who approved this shift. Used for approval audit trail, accountability tracking, and compliance with client MSA approval requirements.']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent timesheet record that aggregates this shift along with other shifts in the same submission period. Links shift-level data to the timesheet approval workflow.']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific physical worksite or location where the shift is performed. Supports multi-site client operations, workers\' compensation jurisdiction assignment, and OSHA compliance.']
  absence_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for a missed or partial shift absence (e.g., NCNS for no-call-no-show, SICK, FMLA, PTO, JURY). Used for attendance tracking, FMLA compliance, and workforce reliability analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NCNS|SICK|FMLA|PTO|JURY|BEREAVEMENT|MILITARY — promote to reference product]']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'The actual clock-out date and time recorded when the worker ended the shift. Sourced from Kronos punch data. Used for early departure tracking, schedule variance, and payable hours calculation.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'The actual clock-in date and time recorded when the worker began the shift. Sourced from Kronos punch data. Used for tardiness tracking, schedule variance, and payable hours calculation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the shift approval workflow. \'pending\' = awaiting supervisor review; \'approved\' = authorized for payroll and billing; \'rejected\' = returned for correction; \'disputed\' = worker or client contest; \'auto_approved\' = system-approved per configured rules.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shift was approved by the authorized approver. Used for SLA tracking on approval turnaround time and payroll cutoff compliance.']
  billable_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total hours approved for client invoicing for this shift. May differ from worked_hours due to client-specific billing rules, approved adjustments, or non-billable time. Feeds Salesforce Revenue Cloud for invoice generation.']
  break_minutes varchar [note: 'Total scheduled or actual break time in minutes deducted from the shift duration. Includes meal breaks and rest periods. Used for net hours calculation and compliance with mandatory break laws (e.g., FLSA, state labor codes).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Client or internal cost center code to which this shift\'s labor cost is allocated. Supports client-side cost allocation reporting, internal P&L tracking, and Oracle NetSuite ERP general ledger posting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shift record was first created in the system, either by the scheduling engine or manual entry. Serves as the record audit creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Client department or business unit code associated with this shift. Used for granular labor cost reporting to clients and workforce analytics by department.']
  differential_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the pay differential applicable to this shift based on timing (e.g., evening, night, weekend, holiday). Drives premium pay rate calculations in TempWorks Payroll and corresponding bill rate adjustments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DAY|EVE|NIGHT|WEEKEND|HOLIDAY|HAZARD|ONCALL — promote to reference product]']
  dt_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked beyond the double-time threshold (typically 12 hours/day or 7th consecutive day per California Labor Code and similar state laws) classified as double-time (DT) at 2x pay rate. Used for payroll and client billing at DT bill rate.']
  geofence_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker\'s clock-in/clock-out was verified within the approved geofence boundary of the worksite. Supports fraud prevention, buddy-punching detection, and client worksite compliance requirements.']
  is_holiday boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift falls on a recognized company or federal holiday. Drives holiday pay premium calculations in TempWorks Payroll and holiday bill rate surcharges for client invoicing.']
  is_weekend boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift falls on a Saturday or Sunday. Used to apply weekend shift differential pay codes and corresponding client bill rate adjustments per MSA terms.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Standardized job classification code for the role performed during this shift. Drives pay rate lookup, workers\' compensation classification, and EEOC/OFCCP labor category reporting.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shift record was most recently modified. Tracks the latest change for data lineage, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for supervisor or worker notes related to this shift, such as reasons for schedule deviation, special instructions, incident observations, or client-specific comments. Used for operational context and dispute resolution.']
  ot_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked beyond the standard threshold (typically 40 hours/week or 8 hours/day depending on state law) classified as overtime (OT) at 1.5x pay rate. Critical for FLSA compliance, payroll cost management, and client billing at OT bill rate.']
  payable_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total hours approved for worker compensation for this shift. May differ from worked_hours due to guaranteed minimum hours, rounding rules, or approved adjustments. Feeds TempWorks Payroll for worker pay calculation.']
  per_diem_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of per diem allowance payable to the worker for this shift. Applicable for travel assignments and remote deployments. Sourced from assignment-level per diem rate configuration. Feeds TempWorks Payroll.']
  per_diem_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is eligible to receive a per diem allowance for this shift (e.g., for travel assignments, remote worksite deployments). Drives per diem payment processing in TempWorks Payroll.']
  punch_source varchar [note: 'Method or system through which the shift\'s time punches were captured. \'time_clock\' = physical Kronos terminal; \'mobile_app\' = Kronos mobile; \'web_portal\' = browser entry; \'supervisor_entry\' = manager-entered; \'vms\' = Beeline VMS submission; \'manual_adjustment\' = back-office correction.']
  regular_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Portion of worked hours classified as straight-time regular pay hours, not subject to overtime or double-time premiums. Used for payroll processing and client billing at the standard pay/bill rate.']
  schedule_variance_minutes varchar [note: 'Difference in minutes between actual worked hours and scheduled hours for this shift (actual minus scheduled). Positive values indicate overtime beyond schedule; negative values indicate early departure or missed time. Key metric for schedule adherence and labor analytics.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'The planned end date and time of the shift as defined in the workforce schedule. Used alongside scheduled_start_time to determine planned shift duration and schedule adherence.']
  scheduled_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total planned hours for the shift as defined in the workforce schedule, calculated from scheduled_start_time to scheduled_end_time minus planned break time. Used for headcount planning and FTE calculations.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'The planned start date and time of the shift as defined in the workforce schedule. Used for schedule adherence analysis, variance calculation, and dispatch confirmation.']
  shift_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the shift is scheduled or was worked. Serves as the principal business event date for the shift, used for daily labor reporting, payroll period alignment, and client billing.']
  shift_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this shift instance, typically generated by Kronos Workforce Ready. Used for cross-system reference, timesheet reconciliation, and worker/supervisor communication.']
  shift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shift. \'scheduled\' = future shift confirmed; \'in_progress\' = worker clocked in; \'completed\' = shift ended and hours recorded; \'missed\' = worker did not appear; \'cancelled\' = shift removed before start; \'no_call_no_show\' = worker absent without notification.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift by its operational nature. \'regular\' = standard scheduled shift; \'overtime\' = OT-designated shift; \'holiday\' = worked on a recognized holiday; \'on_call\' = standby/on-call shift; \'split\' = non-contiguous shift segments; \'per_diem\' = per diem eligible shift.']
  start_variance_minutes varchar [note: 'Difference in minutes between actual_start_time and scheduled_start_time (actual minus scheduled). Positive values indicate tardiness; negative values indicate early arrival. Used for punctuality tracking and SLA compliance.']
  worked_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total actual hours worked during the shift, calculated from actual_start_time to actual_end_time minus actual break time. Represents the gross worked duration before OT/DT classification. Feeds payroll and client billing.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'NCCI or state-specific workers\' compensation classification code applicable to the work performed during this shift. Determines workers\' comp insurance premium calculation and is required for accurate burden rate costing.']

  Note: 'Represents a specific scheduled or worked shift instance for a worker, capturing the shift date, planned start/end, actual start/end, shift duration, break time, net hours, shift differential code, and variance from schedule. Distinct from time_entry in that it represents the shift-level aggregation and schedule comparison, while time_entry captures individual punches. Supports scheduling dispatch and labor analytics.'
}

Table timesheet.ot_rule {
  ot_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the overtime and double-time calculation rule record. Primary key for the ot_rule entity. [ROLE: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — this entity defines enumerated OT/DT calculation rules used as a reference by time entry and payroll processing; exempt from per-role minimums per canonical-attrs-enforced rule.]']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: OT rules implement state-specific compliance requirements (CA daily OT, NY spread-of-hours, etc.). Each OT rule must reference the governing state compliance rule. Real business process: jurisdiction-']
  superseded_by_rule_ot_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ot_rule_id of the newer rule that replaces this rule when rule_status is \'superseded\'. Enables rule version chain tracing for historical timesheet recalculation and compliance audits. Null if this rule has not been superseded.']
  applicable_law_reference varchar [note: 'Specific statutory or regulatory citation governing this OT rule (e.g., \'FLSA §207\', \'California Labor Code §510\', \'Nevada Revised Statutes §608.018\', \'Davis-Bacon Act 40 U.S.C. §3141\'). Used for compliance documentation, legal audit trails, and regulatory reporting to DOL and OFCCP.']
  applies_to_billable_hours boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this OT rule governs the classification of billable OT hours for client invoicing purposes (True), in addition to payable hours for worker compensation. When True, OT bill rate multipliers are applied in Salesforce Revenue Cloud for client billing. When False, the rule applies only to payroll (pay side) calculations.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the payroll manager, compliance officer, or HR administrator who approved this OT rule configuration for activation. Required for SOC 2 Type II change management controls and FLSA compliance audit trails. Populated when rule_status transitions to \'active\'.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this OT rule configuration was formally approved for activation by the authorized approver. Distinct from effective_date (when the rule starts being applied) and created_timestamp (when the record was created). Supports change management audit trails per SOC 2 Type II.']
  bill_rate_dt_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the standard bill rate to calculate the DT bill rate charged to the client for double-time hours (e.g., 2.00 means the client is billed at 2x the straight-time bill rate for DT hours). Null if the rule does not include a DT tier or if applies_to_billable_hours is False.']
  bill_rate_ot_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the standard bill rate to calculate the OT bill rate charged to the client for overtime hours (e.g., 1.50 means the client is billed at 1.5x the straight-time bill rate for OT hours). May differ from the pay-side OT multiplier based on client MSA terms. Null if applies_to_billable_hours is False.']
  blended_rate_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the regular rate of pay for OT purposes when a worker is on multiple concurrent assignments with different pay rates. \'weighted_average\' = FLSA §207(g)(1) weighted average of all rates; \'highest_rate\' = FLSA §207(g)(2) highest rate method; \'primary_assignment\' = rate of the primary assignment governs; \'not_applicable\' = worker is on a single assignment only.']
  break_deduction_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unpaid meal/rest break deductions are applied before OT threshold calculation under this rule. True = break time is deducted from hours worked before OT is computed; False = all clocked hours count toward OT threshold without break deduction. Affects OT calculation in Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OT rule record was first created in the system (ISO 8601 format with timezone offset). Provides audit trail for rule configuration history and supports SOC 2 Type II compliance logging requirements.']
  daily_dt_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked in a single workday beyond which double-time (DT) pay is triggered (e.g., 12.00 hours per California Labor Code §510). Hours worked above this threshold are compensated at the DT multiplier rate. Null if the rule does not include a daily DT threshold.']
  daily_ot_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked in a single workday beyond which daily overtime pay is triggered (e.g., 8.00 hours per California Labor Code §510). Hours worked above this threshold and below the DT threshold are compensated at the OT multiplier. Null if the rule does not apply a daily OT threshold.']
  dt_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Pay rate multiplier applied to the worker\'s regular rate of pay for hours classified as double-time (e.g., 2.00 for double-time per California Labor Code §510). Null if the rule does not include a DT tier. Used by TempWorks Payroll for DT wage calculation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this OT rule becomes effective and begins to be applied to time entry calculations (inclusive). Used for date-range-based rule selection to ensure the correct rule version is applied to historical and current timesheets. Aligns with FLSA and state law effective dates.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this OT rule ceases to be effective (inclusive). Null for open-ended rules with no planned expiration. Used in conjunction with effective_date for date-range-based rule version management. When a rule is superseded, this date is set to the day before the new rule\'s effective_date.']
  flsa_exemption_category varchar [note: 'Specific FLSA exemption category when flsa_exemption_status is \'exempt\' or \'partial_exempt\' (e.g., \'executive\', \'administrative\', \'professional\', \'computer_employee\', \'highly_compensated\', \'207k_public_safety\'). Null for non-exempt workers. Required for FLSA compliance documentation and OFCCP audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: executive|administrative|professional|computer_employee|highly_compensated|207k_public_safety|outside_sales|seasonal — promote to reference product]']
  flsa_exemption_status varchar [note: 'FLSA overtime exemption classification applicable under this rule. \'non_exempt\' = worker is entitled to OT pay under FLSA; \'exempt\' = worker meets FLSA white-collar or other exemption criteria and is not entitled to OT; \'partial_exempt\' = specific exemption applies to some but not all OT provisions (e.g., §207(k) for law enforcement/fire). Drives OT eligibility in TempWorks Payroll.']
  holiday_hours_ot_treatment varchar [note: 'Defines how holiday hours are treated for OT threshold calculation. \'count_as_worked\' = holiday pay hours count toward weekly OT threshold; \'exclude\' = holiday hours are excluded from OT threshold calculation; \'premium_separate\' = holiday premium is calculated separately and does not affect OT threshold. Configured per client MSA or state law.']
  job_category_scope varchar [note: 'Job category or occupational classification to which this OT rule is restricted (e.g., \'light_industrial\', \'healthcare_rn\', \'it_professional\', \'clerical\', \'all\'). Null or \'all\' indicates the rule applies across all job categories. Supports differentiated OT rules for prevailing wage or union-covered job categories.']
  jurisdiction_level varchar [note: 'Level of legal jurisdiction governing this OT rule. \'federal\' = applies nationwide under FLSA; \'state\' = applies within a specific U.S. state; \'local\' = applies within a specific municipality or county; \'contract\' = applies per client MSA or SOW terms regardless of geography.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OT rule record was most recently updated (ISO 8601 format with timezone offset). Used to detect configuration changes, trigger downstream recalculation of affected timesheets, and support SOC 2 Type II audit logging.']
  ot_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Pay rate multiplier applied to the worker\'s regular rate of pay for hours classified as overtime (e.g., 1.50 for time-and-a-half per FLSA §207). Used by TempWorks Payroll to calculate OT wages and by Salesforce Revenue Cloud to calculate OT bill amounts.']
  prevailing_wage_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this OT rule incorporates prevailing wage requirements under the Davis-Bacon Act (for federal construction contracts) or the Service Contract Act (SCA) for government service contracts. True = prevailing wage OT rates apply; False = standard commercial OT rates apply. Critical for government staffing contract compliance.']
  prevailing_wage_determination_number varchar [note: 'DOL wage determination number associated with the prevailing wage schedule governing this OT rule (e.g., \'WD 2023-0001\'). Populated only when prevailing_wage_applies is True. Required for Davis-Bacon Act and SCA compliance documentation on government staffing contracts.']
  pto_counts_toward_ot boolean [note: 'Indicates whether paid time off (PTO) hours are included in the weekly hours count for OT threshold purposes. True = PTO hours count toward the OT threshold (e.g., 32 hours worked + 8 PTO = 40 hours, no OT triggered); False = only actual hours worked count toward OT threshold per FLSA standard. Configurable per client MSA.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this OT/DT rule for reference in payroll configurations, VMS integrations, and client billing systems (e.g., \'FLSA_FED_STD\', \'CA_510_DAILY\', \'DAVIS_BACON_GOV\'). Used as the business key in Kronos Workforce Ready and TempWorks Payroll rule tables.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the overtime rule, including the legal basis, applicable thresholds, and any special conditions (e.g., seventh-consecutive-day provisions, blended rate requirements, prevailing wage applicability). Supports compliance documentation and auditor review.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the overtime rule (e.g., \'Federal FLSA Standard 40-Hour Weekly OT\', \'California Daily OT 8/12 Rule\', \'Davis-Bacon Prevailing Wage OT\'). Used in payroll configuration UIs, compliance reports, and worker assignment documentation.']
  rule_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority rank used for conflict resolution when multiple OT rules are applicable to the same time entry (e.g., a worker in California on a government contract may have federal FLSA, California state, and Davis-Bacon rules all applicable). Lower integer = higher priority. Ensures deterministic OT rule selection in Kronos Workforce Ready and TempWorks Payroll.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the OT rule record. \'active\' = currently applied to time entry calculations; \'inactive\' = disabled and not applied; \'draft\' = under review before activation; \'superseded\' = replaced by a newer rule version but retained for historical reference.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the overtime rule by its governing authority or applicability scope. \'federal\' = FLSA-based federal standard; \'state\' = state-specific labor law (e.g., California, Nevada); \'client_specific\' = client-negotiated OT terms in MSA; \'prevailing_wage\' = Davis-Bacon Act or SCA government contract rules; \'blended\' = multi-assignment blended rate OT calculation.']
  seventh_day_dt_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'On the seventh consecutive workday, the number of hours beyond which double-time pay applies (e.g., 8.00 hours per California Labor Code §510 — first 8 hours at OT, beyond 8 at DT). Null if seventh_day_ot_applies is False or if no DT tier applies on the seventh day.']
  seventh_day_ot_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rule includes the seventh-consecutive-day OT provision, where all hours worked on the seventh consecutive day of a workweek are paid at OT rate (True) per California Labor Code §510. False for rules that do not include this provision.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this OT rule configuration was sourced or is mastered. \'kronos\' = Kronos Workforce Ready time and attendance; \'tempworks\' = TempWorks Payroll rule configuration; \'beeline\' = Beeline VMS rate and rule management; \'manual\' = manually entered by payroll or compliance team; \'bullhorn\' = Bullhorn ATS/CRM placement configuration.']
  source_system_rule_ref varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this OT rule in the originating operational system (e.g., Kronos Workforce Ready pay rule ID, TempWorks Payroll OT rule code). Enables reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the source system for audit and troubleshooting purposes.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state abbreviation (ISO 3166-2:US) identifying the state whose labor law governs this OT rule (e.g., \'CA\' for California Labor Code §510, \'NV\' for Nevada daily OT). Null for federal-level rules applicable nationwide. Used for multi-state OT rule resolution in Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  union_agreement_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this OT rule is governed by or must comply with a collective bargaining agreement (CBA) or union contract. True = union OT provisions apply and may override standard FLSA or state rules; False = no union agreement governs this rule. Relevant for unionized light industrial and healthcare staffing placements.']
  union_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the collective bargaining agreement (CBA) that governs this OT rule when union_agreement_applies is True (e.g., \'SEIU Local 1199 CBA 2023-2026\', \'Teamsters Local 25 CBA\'). Null when union_agreement_applies is False. Used for compliance documentation and payroll audit.']
  weekly_dt_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked in a workweek beyond which double-time pay is triggered (e.g., some state or client rules apply DT after 60 hours/week). Null if the rule does not apply a weekly DT threshold. Distinct from the daily DT threshold.']
  weekly_ot_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked in a workweek beyond which overtime pay is triggered (e.g., 40.00 hours per FLSA §207 federal standard). Hours worked above this threshold are compensated at the OT multiplier rate. Null if the rule does not apply a weekly OT threshold.']
  worker_classification_scope varchar [note: 'Worker tax classification to which this OT rule applies. \'w2_employee\' = applies only to W-2 employees on payroll; \'1099_ic\' = applies to independent contractors (IC) per contract terms; \'all\' = applies regardless of worker classification. Aligns with IRS worker classification rules and FLSA co-employment considerations.']
  workweek_start_day varchar [note: 'The designated first day of the workweek for purposes of weekly OT threshold calculation (e.g., \'sunday\' for a Sunday–Saturday workweek per FLSA §207). Workweek definition is fixed and regularly recurring per FLSA. Configured in Kronos Workforce Ready per client or assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sunday|monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Reference entity defining overtime and double-time calculation rules applicable to specific clients, states, job categories, or worker classifications. Captures the weekly OT threshold (e.g., 40 hrs/week federal FLSA §207), daily OT threshold (e.g., 8 hrs/day California Labor Code §510), DT threshold (e.g., 12 hrs/day California), seventh-consecutive-day rules, applicable FLSA exemption status, state-specific OT law references, effective date range, and rule priority for conflict resolution. Supports blended OT rate calculation for workers on multiple assignments. Includes prevailing wage OT rules for Davis-Bacon Act compliance on government staffing contracts. Ensures compliant OT/DT classification across all time entries in multi-state staffing operations.'
}

Table timesheet.pay_period {
  pay_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each pay period record in the staffing operation. Primary key for the timesheet_pay_period reference entity. [_canonical_skip_reason: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — this entity is a calendar/reference table defining pay period cycles; per-role minimums do not apply, but comprehensive coverage is still provided for operational completeness.]']
  applicable_states varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of U.S. state codes (e.g., \'CA,NY,TX\') for which this pay period definition applies. Critical for multi-state staffing operations where state-specific payroll laws (e.g., California daily OT, New York wage payment frequency) require different pay period configurations. Supports SUTA and state tax compliance.']
  approval_deadline timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp by which client supervisors or staffing coordinators must approve submitted timesheets. Drives SLA enforcement for approval workflows and escalation triggers in Kronos Workforce Ready. Supports on-time payroll processing and client billing cycle alignment.']
  billing_cycle_date date [note: 'The date on which client invoices are generated for billable hours worked during this pay period. May differ from check_date in cases where billing and payroll cycles are offset. Feeds Salesforce Revenue Cloud invoice generation and Oracle NetSuite Accounts Receivable.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'The standard calendar year (4-digit integer, e.g., 2024) to which this pay period belongs. Distinct from fiscal_year when the business operates on a non-calendar fiscal year. Used for W-2 and 1099 tax year alignment in TempWorks Payroll.']
  check_date date [note: 'The date on which worker paychecks or direct deposit payments are issued for this pay period. Represents the actual pay date visible on W-2 wage statements and worker pay stubs. Used for tax reporting, FICA, FUTA, and SUTA liability calculations in TempWorks Payroll.']
  client_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment or client grouping for which this pay period definition is applicable (e.g., \'Healthcare\', \'Light Industrial\', \'IT Staffing\', \'All\'). Supports multi-client staffing operations where different client verticals may operate on different pay cycles per MSA (Master Service Agreement) terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pay period record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 Type II compliance. Populated automatically at record insertion and never updated.']
  dt_threshold_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked beyond which double-time (DT) pay rates apply for this pay period. Applicable in states such as California where DT is mandated after 12 hours in a workday or on the 7th consecutive workday. Supports multi-state payroll compliance in TempWorks Payroll.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this pay period definition record became effective in the system. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) tracking for pay period calendar changes (e.g., a client switching from weekly to bi-weekly pay cycles). Enables historical payroll reprocessing and audit compliance.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date on which this pay period definition record ceases to be effective. Null indicates the record is currently active with no planned end date. Supports SCD Type 2 history tracking for pay period calendar changes and enables point-in-time payroll audit queries.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the pay period. All timesheet hours submitted with a work date on or before this date are grouped into this pay period. Together with start_date, defines the authoritative time boundary for worker compensation and client billing.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'The fiscal month (1–12) within the fiscal year to which this pay period belongs. Supports monthly financial close processes in Oracle NetSuite ERP and monthly workforce analytics reporting in Power BI Analytics Platform.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter (1–4) within the fiscal year to which this pay period belongs. Used for quarterly payroll tax filings (941, SUTA), financial reporting, and KPI dashboards in Power BI Analytics Platform.']
  fiscal_week_number varchar [note: 'The sequential fiscal week number (1–53) within the fiscal year. Used for weekly labor analytics, overtime threshold tracking, and workforce scheduling reports in Kronos Workforce Ready and Power BI Analytics Platform. Particularly important for weekly pay period types.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year (4-digit integer, e.g., 2024) to which this pay period belongs. Used for financial reporting, budget allocation, and year-end tax filings in Oracle NetSuite ERP. Supports multi-year analytics in Power BI Analytics Platform.']
  holiday_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay period contains one or more observed holidays that affect standard workday counts, holiday pay eligibility, or payroll processing schedules. When True, payroll coordinators must review holiday pay rules and adjusted cutoff dates. Sourced from Kronos Workforce Ready holiday calendar.']
  holiday_count varchar [note: 'Number of observed holidays falling within this pay period. Used to calculate adjusted workday counts, holiday pay liability, and per diem adjustments. Supports holiday pay eligibility rules for temporary workers under client MSA (Master Service Agreement) terms.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay period record is currently active and available for timesheet assignment and payroll processing. False records represent historical, future-dated, or deactivated periods. Used to filter active periods in Kronos Workforce Ready scheduling and TempWorks Payroll run selection.']
  is_current_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the currently active pay period (i.e., today\'s date falls within start_date and end_date). Only one record per period_type should have this flag set to True at any given time. Used as a quick filter in operational dashboards and payroll processing queues.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for payroll coordinators to document special instructions, exceptions, or contextual information for this pay period (e.g., \'Adjusted cutoff due to Thanksgiving holiday\', \'Early check date for Christmas week\'). Supports operational communication and audit documentation.']
  ot_threshold_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked beyond which overtime (OT) pay rates apply for this pay period. Typically 40.00 hours for weekly FLSA overtime, but may vary by state law (e.g., California daily OT at 8 hours). Critical for payroll compliance and accurate OT cost calculations in TempWorks Payroll.']
  payroll_cutoff_date date [note: 'The deadline by which all timesheets must be submitted and approved for inclusion in this pay period\'s payroll run. Timesheets received after this date are held for the next pay period or processed as off-cycle adjustments. Operationally critical for TempWorks Payroll batch processing.']
  payroll_provider_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the payroll processing provider or entity responsible for executing payroll for this pay period (e.g., \'TEMPWORKS\', \'EOR_PARTNER_01\'). Relevant for EOR (Employer of Record) arrangements where a third-party provider processes payroll on behalf of the staffing firm.']
  payroll_run_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of payroll run associated with this pay period. regular = standard scheduled payroll; off_cycle = unscheduled run for missed payments or terminations; supplemental = bonus or commission payments; correction = retroactive adjustment for prior period errors. Drives payroll processing logic in TempWorks Payroll.']
  per_diem_period_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether per diem allowances are applicable and tracked within this pay period. When True, per diem expense records are expected to be submitted alongside timesheets. Relevant for travel-intensive placements (e.g., healthcare travel nurses, field technicians) where IRS per diem rates apply.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this pay period across systems (e.g., PP-2024-01-001). Used for cross-system reconciliation between Kronos Workforce Ready, TempWorks Payroll, and Salesforce Revenue Cloud billing cycles.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for the pay period (e.g., \'Week 1 Jan 2024\', \'Bi-Weekly Period 3 - Q1 2024\'). Used in payroll reports, worker pay stubs, and client billing statements for clear period identification.']
  period_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordinal number of this pay period within its fiscal year and period type (e.g., 1st bi-weekly period = 1, 2nd = 2). Used for period-over-period comparison analytics, payroll run ordering, and audit trail sequencing.']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pay period. open = timesheets can be submitted; processing = payroll run in progress; locked = no further timesheet changes permitted; closed = payroll finalized and disbursed; cancelled = period voided (rare, e.g., system error). Drives workflow gating in TempWorks Payroll and Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pay period cycle frequency. Weekly = 52 periods/year; bi_weekly = 26 periods/year; semi_monthly = 24 periods/year; monthly = 12 periods/year. Critical for multi-client operations where different clients may operate on different pay cycles. Drives payroll run scheduling in TempWorks Payroll.']
  pto_accrual_period_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether PTO (Paid Time Off) accruals are calculated and posted during this pay period. Some staffing arrangements accrue PTO on a per-pay-period basis per state law (e.g., California AB 1522, New York Paid Sick Leave). Drives PTO accrual batch processing in TempWorks Payroll and SAP SuccessFactors.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this pay period definition originated. Kronos Workforce Ready is the primary source for time and attendance pay period calendars; TempWorks Payroll for payroll-specific period definitions; Beeline VMS for contingent workforce program pay cycles. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_period_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this pay period record in the originating source system (e.g., Kronos pay period ID, TempWorks period key). Used for cross-system reconciliation, data lineage tracing, and integration audit trails between the lakehouse silver layer and operational systems.']
  standard_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Expected number of standard straight-time hours for a full-time worker during this pay period (e.g., 40.00 for a weekly period, 80.00 for bi-weekly). Used as the baseline for overtime (OT) threshold calculations, FTE normalization, and payroll burden rate computations.']
  start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the pay period. All timesheet hours submitted with a work date on or after this date are grouped into this pay period. Authoritative boundary for timesheet aggregation and payroll processing.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The IRS tax year (4-digit integer) to which payroll taxes for this period are reported. Typically matches calendar_year but may differ for periods spanning year-end (e.g., a bi-weekly period starting Dec 28 and ending Jan 10). Critical for W-2, 1099, FICA, FUTA, and SUTA tax filing accuracy.']
  timesheet_submission_deadline timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp by which contingent workers must submit their timesheets for the pay period. Includes time-of-day precision to support automated reminder workflows in Kronos Workforce Ready and Bullhorn ATS/CRM. Distinct from payroll_cutoff_date which governs the payroll processing batch.']
  total_calendar_days varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days in this pay period (end_date minus start_date plus 1). Used for pro-ration calculations for partial-period placements, per diem allowance calculations, and FTE (Full-Time Equivalent) normalization in workforce analytics.']
  total_workdays varchar [note: 'Number of standard business workdays (Monday–Friday, excluding holidays) within this pay period. Used for expected hours calculations, attendance rate reporting, and workforce scheduling in Kronos Workforce Ready. Supports SLA (Service Level Agreement) headcount compliance reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this pay period record in the data platform. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance. Updated automatically on every record modification.']
  vms_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay period is associated with a VMS (Vendor Management System) managed contingent workforce program (e.g., Beeline VMS). VMS programs may have distinct pay period calendars, approval workflows, and billing cycle alignment requirements separate from direct-client placements.']
  year_end_period_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay period spans or falls at the calendar year-end boundary (e.g., a bi-weekly period crossing December 31 into January). When True, special year-end payroll processing rules apply including W-2 cutoff handling, FICA wage base reset, and FUTA/SUTA annual limit resets in TempWorks Payroll.']

  Note: 'Reference entity defining pay period cycles used across the staffing operation. Captures pay period type (weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, monthly), period start date, period end date, payroll processing cutoff date, check date, fiscal week number, holiday adjustment flag, and active status. Serves as the authoritative calendar for timesheet grouping, payroll runs, and billing cycle alignment. Critical for multi-state operations where different clients may operate on different pay cycles.'
}

Table timesheet.approved_hours_summary {
  approved_hours_summary_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for approved_hours_summary']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active placement or assignment under which the worker performed the hours captured in this summary. Ties the hours to a specific job order and client engagement.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client (end-customer) for whom the worker performed services during this pay period. Used to route billable amounts to the correct client invoice.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Approved hours summaries aggregate billing using rate schedules; invoicing systems pull contracted rates for billing calculations and client invoice generation. Real business process: invoice generati']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Approved hours are the basis for cost center charge-backs and client invoicing. Staffing firms use summary-level cost center data for management reporting and client billing reconciliation; core to fi']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Approved hours summaries drive batch GL postings for payroll accrual and revenue recognition. The bridge between operational timesheet data and financial accounting; required for automated period-end ']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order or requisition (Req) that originated the worker\'s assignment. Enables traceability from approved hours back to the originating staffing request.']
  pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pay period calendar record that defines the start and end dates of the period covered by this approved hours summary.']
  pay_stub_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_stub. Business justification: Approved hours summaries feed directly into pay stub generation. This link enables reconciliation between approved billable hours and actual payment, critical for payroll audit, variance analysis, and']
  placement_compliance_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.placement_compliance_check. Business justification: Hours cannot be approved for billing/payroll until placement passes all compliance checks (I-9, BGC, credentials, safety training). Real business process: compliant billing gate preventing payment for']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contingent worker whose approved hours are summarized in this record. Links to the worker master record in the Candidate/Worker domain.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual (typically the client supervisor or staffing coordinator) who formally approved the timesheet hours for this pay period.']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source timesheet record from which this approved hours summary was derived. Provides traceability back to the raw time entry data in Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for any post-approval amendment to this hours summary. Required when is_amended is True. Supports audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the approved hours summary record within the timesheet approval workflow. \'approved\' indicates hours are cleared for billing and payroll; \'disputed\' indicates a client or worker challenge is open; \'amended\' indicates a post-approval correction was applied.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the timesheet hours were formally approved by the authorized approver. Represents the principal business event timestamp that triggers downstream billing and payroll processing.']
  bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly rate charged to the client for the worker\'s regular hours during this pay period, as defined in the client MSA or VMS rate card. Expressed in USD per hour. Used to calculate total billable amount.']
  billing_transmission_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the current state of this approved hours summary\'s transmission to the client billing system (Salesforce Revenue Cloud). \'invoiced\' means a client invoice has been generated from this record.']
  burden_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The total employer burden rate applied to this worker\'s payable wages for the pay period, expressed as a decimal percentage. Encompasses FICA, FUTA, SUTA, Workers Compensation (Workers Comp), and benefits costs. Used to calculate fully-loaded labor cost.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this approved hours summary record was first created in the Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations for international staffing engagements.']
  dt_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly rate charged to the client for double-time (DT) hours during this pay period. Typically 2x the regular bill rate per the client MSA or applicable state labor law.']
  dt_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total approved double-time (DT) hours worked during the pay period, applicable in jurisdictions or client agreements requiring 2x pay for hours beyond a daily or weekly threshold (e.g., California daily OT rules).']
  flsa_exemption_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is classified as exempt or non-exempt under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) for this assignment. Non-exempt workers are entitled to overtime pay at 1.5x for hours exceeding 40 per week.']
  gross_margin_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'The gross margin (Spread) for this pay period, calculated as total_billable_amount minus total_payable_amount minus burden costs. Represents the staffing agency\'s gross profit contribution from this worker-assignment combination before SG&A.']
  holiday_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total approved hours worked on designated company or client holidays during the pay period. Holiday hours may carry a premium bill rate and pay rate per the client MSA or worker agreement.']
  is_amended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this approved hours summary record has been amended after initial approval (e.g., due to a timesheet correction, missed punch adjustment, or retroactive rate change). True triggers a reconciliation process in payroll and billing.']
  is_approved boolean [note: 'Boolean flag confirming that all hours in this summary have been formally approved by an authorized approver. True indicates the record is cleared for payroll and billing handoff. False indicates approval is still pending or was revoked.']
  ot_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly rate charged to the client for overtime (OT) hours during this pay period. Typically 1.5x the regular bill rate, but may be negotiated differently in the client MSA.']
  ot_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total approved overtime (OT) hours worked during the pay period, typically hours exceeding 40 per week for non-exempt workers under FLSA. Billed and paid at the OT multiplier rate (commonly 1.5x).']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the pay period covered by this approved hours summary. Defines the inclusive end boundary for all hours aggregated in this record.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the pay period covered by this approved hours summary. Defines the inclusive start boundary for all hours aggregated in this record.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly rate paid to the worker for regular hours during this pay period, as defined in the worker\'s placement agreement. Expressed in USD per hour. Used to calculate total payable amount and gross margin (Spread).']
  payroll_transmission_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the current state of this approved hours summary\'s transmission to the payroll processing system (TempWorks Payroll). \'confirmed\' means payroll has acknowledged receipt and the record is locked for the pay run.']
  per_diem_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The daily per diem allowance rate paid to the worker per approved per diem unit. Must not exceed IRS-published per diem limits to maintain tax-free treatment. Expressed in USD per day.']
  per_diem_units decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of per diem units (typically days) approved for the worker during the pay period. Per diem is a daily allowance paid to workers for meals, lodging, or incidentals when working away from their tax home. Not subject to payroll taxes when within IRS limits.']
  pto_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total approved paid time off (PTO) hours for the worker during the pay period. PTO hours are payable to the worker but are typically not billable to the client unless specified in the MSA.']
  regular_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total approved regular (straight-time) hours worked by the worker during the pay period, excluding overtime and double-time. Used as the base quantity for both client billing and worker payroll at the standard bill rate and pay rate respectively.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this approved hours summary was sourced or triggered. Kronos Workforce Ready is the primary source; Beeline VMS may be the source for MSP-managed programs; Manual indicates a staffing coordinator override entry.']
  summary_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this approved hours summary record, formatted as AHS-{YYYY}-{WW}-{SEQUENCE}. Used in billing and payroll correspondence to reference a specific approved period.']
  total_billable_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total gross amount to be invoiced to the client for this pay period, calculated as the sum of (regular_hours × bill_rate) + (ot_hours × ot_bill_rate) + (dt_hours × dt_bill_rate) + (holiday_hours × applicable holiday bill rate). Feeds Salesforce Revenue Cloud invoice generation.']
  total_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Sum of all approved hour types (regular + OT + DT + holiday) for the worker during the pay period. Represents the total time-based quantity used for workforce analytics and FTE calculations. Excludes PTO hours.']
  total_payable_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total gross wages payable to the worker for this pay period, calculated as the sum of (regular_hours × pay_rate) + (ot_hours × ot_pay_rate) + (dt_hours × dt_pay_rate) + (holiday_hours × holiday pay rate) + (per_diem_units × per_diem_rate). Feeds TempWorks Payroll processing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this approved hours summary record was last modified. Tracks amendments such as retroactive adjustments or correction reprocessing.']
  vms_requisition_number varchar [note: 'The requisition or purchase order number assigned by the client\'s Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, for this worker\'s engagement. Required on client invoices for MSP-managed programs.']
  work_location_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the worker performed services on-site at the client location, fully remote, or in a hybrid arrangement during this pay period. Affects per diem eligibility and certain state tax obligations.']
  worker_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the worker\'s tax and employment relationship for this assignment. W-2 indicates a staffing agency employee; 1099 indicates an independent contractor (IC); C2C indicates a corp-to-corp engagement. Drives payroll tax treatment and burden rate application.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'The National Council on Compensation Insurance (NCCI) workers compensation class code assigned to the worker for this assignment. Determines the workers comp insurance premium rate applied within the burden rate calculation.']

  Note: 'Unified aggregated summary of approved hours for a worker-assignment-client combination within a pay period, broken down by pay type (regular, OT, DT, per diem, holiday). Serves as the single authoritative handoff record for both client invoice generation (billing domain) and payroll processing (payroll domain). Captures total regular hours, OT hours, DT hours, per diem units, bill rate applied, pay rate applied, total billable amount, total payable amount, burden rate components, approval confirmation flag, and payroll transmission status. Replaces separate billable and payable hour summaries to ensure a single reconciled SSOT. Distinct from raw time_entry records — this is the approved, billing-and-payroll-ready output of the timesheet approval process.'
}

Table timesheet.per_diem_claim {
  per_diem_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each per diem allowance claim record submitted by a contingent worker. Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific placement or assignment for which the per diem claim is being submitted. Ties the claim to a client engagement, job order, and work location for billing and compliance purposes.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client (end customer) associated with the assignment for which the per diem is claimed. Used to determine client-billable per diem charges and to apply client-specific per diem rate agreements.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Per diem costs are allocated to cost centers for client reimbursement and project costing. Travel-intensive staffing engagements require cost center tracking of per diem for accurate client billing an']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Per diem expenses post to specific GL accounts (travel/expense accounts) for financial reporting and tax compliance. Staffing firms must track per diem separately from wages for IRS reporting and clie']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client invoice in Salesforce Revenue Cloud to which this per diem charge has been applied. Populated once the claim is approved and included in a billing cycle. Null if not yet billed or not client-billable.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Per diem rates vary by work location; IRS compliance and client billing require linking claims to specific client sites. Essential for GSA locality-based per diem rate determination and client site-sp']
  pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll pay period in which this per diem reimbursement will be or was processed. Links the claim to the TempWorks Payroll pay cycle for non-taxable reimbursement disbursement.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contingent worker (candidate/associate) who submitted this per diem claim. Links to the worker/candidate master record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM and TempWorks Payroll.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal staff member or manager who approved or rejected the per diem claim. Supports approval audit trail, delegation-of-authority compliance, and workflow accountability.']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the timesheet record for the corresponding work period during which the per diem expense was incurred. Links per diem claims to the authoritative hours record in Kronos Workforce Ready for payroll and billing reconciliation.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the per diem claim was approved or rejected by the designated approver. Used for SLA measurement, audit trail, and payroll cutoff alignment.']
  approved_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The dollar amount approved by the designated approver after review of the per diem claim. May differ from the claimed amount if the claim exceeds the IRS/GSA rate or client-agreed cap. Feeds payroll non-taxable reimbursement and client billing. Denominated in USD.']
  billable_to_client boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approved per diem amount is billable to the client under the terms of the Master Service Agreement (MSA) or Statement of Work (SOW). When true, the approved amount is included in the client invoice generated in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  billing_processed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client-billable per diem amount has been included in a client invoice. Prevents duplicate billing and supports reconciliation between per diem claims and accounts receivable records in Oracle NetSuite ERP.']
  claim_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the per diem expense was incurred by the worker. This is the business event date (not the submission date) and is used for IRS per diem rate lookup, payroll period alignment, and client invoice period matching.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable business identifier for the per diem claim, used in worker communications, approval workflows, payroll references, and client billing. Format: PDC-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the per diem claim within the approval workflow. Drives payroll processing eligibility and client billing inclusion. Valid values: draft, submitted, under_review, approved, rejected, cancelled.']
  claimed_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The gross dollar amount claimed by the worker for the per diem expense. This is the worker-submitted amount before approval review or adjustment. Denominated in USD.']
  client_bill_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The dollar amount to be billed to the client for this per diem claim. May differ from the approved_amount if the client agreement specifies a different reimbursement cap or markup. Denominated in USD. Feeds Salesforce Revenue Cloud invoicing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this per diem claim record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker for data governance, SOC 2 compliance, and record lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this claim record. Defaults to USD for domestic assignments. Required for international assignments to support multi-currency payroll and billing.']
  gsa_locality_code varchar [note: 'The General Services Administration (GSA) locality code corresponding to the expense location. Used to precisely identify the applicable per diem rate tier for the city/county, especially for high-cost localities that exceed the standard continental US (CONUS) rate.']
  irs_per_diem_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The applicable IRS/GSA standard per diem rate for the expense location and claim date at the time of submission. Used as the reference ceiling for non-taxable reimbursement treatment. Amounts at or below this rate are non-taxable; amounts above are treated as taxable wages. Denominated in USD.']
  irs_rate_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year of the IRS/GSA per diem rate table used to determine the applicable rate for this claim. IRS per diem rates are updated annually; this field ensures the correct rate schedule is referenced for audit and compliance purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, worker-provided justification, or operational notes related to the per diem claim. May include travel purpose, special circumstances, or client-specific instructions. Not used for approval decisions.']
  partial_day_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the per diem claim is for a partial day (e.g., first or last day of travel). IRS rules require a 75% rate reduction for the first and last day of travel. When true, the claimed amount should reflect the reduced partial-day rate.']
  payroll_processed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approved per diem reimbursement has been included in a payroll run and disbursed to the worker. Prevents duplicate payroll processing and supports reconciliation between per diem claims and payroll records.']
  per_diem_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the per diem allowance category being claimed. Determines applicable IRS GSA rate, taxability treatment, and whether the expense is billable to the client. Valid values: travel, lodging, meals, incidentals, meals_and_incidentals (M&IE combined rate).']
  receipt_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a receipt or supporting documentation is required for this per diem claim based on the per diem type, claimed amount, or client policy. Lodging per diem typically requires receipts; meals and incidentals under the GSA rate generally do not.']
  receipt_submitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker has submitted a receipt or supporting documentation for this per diem claim. Used in the approval workflow to enforce receipt requirements and for IRS substantiation compliance.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the approver when a per diem claim is rejected. Required when claim_status is \'rejected\'. Supports worker communication, dispute resolution, and process improvement analytics.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this per diem claim originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability across integrated platforms. Valid values: Kronos (Kronos Workforce Ready), TempWorks (TempWorks Payroll), Bullhorn (Bullhorn ATS/CRM), Beeline (Beeline VMS), Manual (direct entry).']
  source_system_claim_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier or reference number assigned to this per diem claim in the originating source system (e.g., Kronos expense record ID, TempWorks reimbursement reference). Supports cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the worker formally submitted the per diem claim for approval. Used for SLA tracking, audit trails, and late-submission compliance monitoring.']
  taxable_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The portion of the approved per diem amount that exceeds the IRS/GSA standard rate and must be treated as taxable wages subject to FICA, FUTA, SUTA, and income tax withholding. Calculated as max(0, approved_amount - irs_per_diem_rate). Feeds TempWorks Payroll tax processing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this per diem claim record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change for audit trail, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  work_week_ending_date date [note: 'The last day of the payroll work week to which this per diem claim belongs. Used to align per diem reimbursements with the correct payroll processing cycle in TempWorks Payroll and to match the corresponding timesheet period.']

  Note: 'Records per diem allowance claims submitted by contingent workers for travel, lodging, and meal reimbursements associated with a specific assignment. Captures claim date, per diem type (travel, lodging, meals, incidentals), claimed amount, approved amount, expense location, receipt indicator, approval status, and IRS per diem rate reference. Feeds both payroll (non-taxable reimbursements) and billing (client-billable per diem charges).'
}

Table timesheet.adjustment {
  adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each timesheet adjustment record. Serves as the primary key for the timesheet_adjustment entity. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — canonical minimum categories enforced.']
  adjusted_pay_stub_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_stub. Business justification: Timesheet adjustments often trigger pay stub corrections or reissues. Tracking which specific pay stub was affected (vs. just the pay_run) is essential for audit trails, worker communication about cor']
  adjustment_approved_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the timesheet adjustment. Required for segregation-of-duties compliance and audit trail. Null if adjustment is pending or rejected.']
  adjustment_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user (worker, staffing coordinator, or manager) who initiated the timesheet adjustment request. Required for accountability, audit trail, and workflow routing.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active placement or assignment under which the original timesheet was submitted. Links the adjustment to the specific client engagement, job order, and pay/bill rate agreement.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client organization associated with the placement. Required for client billing reconciliation and invoice adjustment when billable hours are corrected.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Rate adjustments reference the applicable rate schedule; retroactive rate changes must cite the amended rate schedule for audit trail and billing correction. Real business process: rate correction and']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Adjustments reallocate costs between cost centers when workers are reassigned or time is reclassified. Common in staffing when correcting cost center misallocations; required for accurate client billi']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Adjustments create correcting journal entries that must reference specific GL accounts for audit trail and SOX compliance. Staffing firms require GL-level tracking of all payroll/billing adjustments f']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client invoice or credit memo generated as a result of this billing adjustment. Links the timesheet correction to the corresponding accounts receivable transaction in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll run in which this adjustment was processed. Enables reconciliation between timesheet adjustments and payroll output records. Null until the adjustment is applied to payroll.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contingent worker whose timesheet is being adjusted. Enables worker-level payroll reconciliation, compliance reporting, and dispute resolution. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE canonical category.']
  regulatory_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_violation. Business justification: Timesheet adjustments may be triggered by wage-hour violations discovered in DOL/state audits (unpaid OT, misclassified hours). Real business process: remediation tracking linking adjustments to viola']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original approved timesheet record that this adjustment corrects or amends. Core linkage enabling full audit trail from original submission to corrected values. Maps to the PARTY_REFERENCE canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable business identifier for the adjustment (e.g., ADJ-2024-000123). Used in dispute resolution, payroll reconciliation, and client billing communications. Maps to the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the adjustment record. Tracks progression from initial draft through approval and application to payroll and billing. Maps to the LIFECYCLE_STATUS canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the nature of the adjustment. Drives downstream payroll and billing processing logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hours_correction|rate_correction|pay_type_reclassification|retroactive_change|break_correction|absence_correction — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  affects_billing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this timesheet adjustment impacts client billable hours or bill rates, triggering a credit memo or supplemental invoice. False for adjustments that only affect worker pay (e.g., internal pay type reclassifications with no billing impact).']
  affects_payroll boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this timesheet adjustment impacts worker payable hours or pay rates, requiring a payroll correction or supplemental pay run. False for adjustments that only affect billing (e.g., bill rate corrections with no pay rate change).']
  applied_to_billing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the approved adjustment was applied to client billing in Salesforce Revenue Cloud. Null until billing is updated. Confirms the client invoice or credit memo has been generated for the adjustment.']
  applied_to_payroll_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the approved adjustment was applied to the payroll run in TempWorks Payroll. Null until the adjustment is processed. Confirms the adjustment has been actioned in the payroll system of record.']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the approval tier required for this adjustment based on the adjustment type, dollar impact, or client contract requirements. Single-level approvals are handled internally; dual or client-required approvals involve additional stakeholders.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the timesheet adjustment was approved by the designated approver. Null if not yet approved. Used for approval cycle time analytics and SLA compliance reporting.']
  bill_amount_adjusted decimal(12,2) [note: 'The corrected gross client billing amount (in USD) after the adjustment. Drives credit memo issuance or supplemental invoice generation in Salesforce Revenue Cloud. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category.']
  bill_amount_original decimal(12,2) [note: 'The gross client billing amount (in USD) calculated from the original timesheet hours and bill rate before adjustment. Baseline for client invoice reconciliation and credit memo calculation.']
  bill_rate_adjusted decimal(10,4) [note: 'The corrected client bill rate (per hour) in USD after the adjustment. Used when a rate correction changes the amount invoiced to the client. Drives credit memo or supplemental invoice generation.']
  bill_rate_original decimal(10,4) [note: 'The client bill rate (per hour) recorded on the original timesheet, in USD. Preserved for billing reconciliation when rate corrections affect client invoices. Represents the rate charged to the client before adjustment.']
  client_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client has formally approved the timesheet adjustment, required when the adjustment affects billable hours or bill rates under the client\'s Master Service Agreement (MSA). True when client sign-off is obtained.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this timesheet adjustment record was first created in the system. Maps to the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this adjustment record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international staffing engagements. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category.']
  hours_adjusted decimal(6,2) [note: 'The corrected number of hours after the adjustment is applied. Replaces the original hours value for payroll processing and client billing. The difference between hours_adjusted and hours_original represents the net hours change.']
  hours_original decimal(6,2) [note: 'The number of hours recorded on the original approved timesheet for the work date and pay type being corrected. Preserved as the pre-adjustment baseline for audit, dispute resolution, and reconciliation.']
  is_retroactive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment applies to a prior pay period that has already been processed in payroll and/or billing. Retroactive adjustments require special handling in payroll tax recalculation (FICA, FUTA, SUTA) and may trigger amended invoices.']
  pay_amount_adjusted decimal(12,2) [note: 'The corrected gross worker pay amount (in USD) after the adjustment is applied. Represents the authoritative payable amount for the adjusted period. The difference from pay_amount_original drives payroll debit or credit entries. Maps to MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category.']
  pay_amount_original decimal(12,2) [note: 'The gross worker pay amount (in USD) calculated from the original timesheet hours and pay rate before adjustment. Serves as the financial baseline for payroll reconciliation and adjustment impact analysis.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the pay period to which this adjustment applies. Together with pay_period_start_date, defines the exact payroll cycle impacted by the correction.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the pay period to which this adjustment applies. Determines which payroll run and billing cycle are affected by the correction.']
  pay_rate_adjusted decimal(10,4) [note: 'The corrected worker pay rate (per hour) in USD after the adjustment. Used for rate correction and retroactive change adjustment types. Drives recalculation of worker gross pay for the affected period.']
  pay_rate_original decimal(10,4) [note: 'The worker pay rate (per hour) recorded on the original timesheet, in USD. Preserved for rate correction adjustments and retroactive pay rate changes. Sensitive compensation data.']
  pay_type_adjusted varchar [note: 'The corrected pay type code after adjustment. Used when a pay type reclassification adjustment is applied (e.g., regular hours reclassified as overtime (OT) or double-time (DT)). Drives recalculation of worker pay and client bill amounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular|overtime|double_time|per_diem|pto|holiday|on_call — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pay_type_original varchar [note: 'The pay type code recorded on the original timesheet before adjustment (e.g., regular, overtime (OT), double-time (DT), per diem, paid time off (PTO), holiday, on-call). Preserved for full audit trail and reclassification tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular|overtime|double_time|per_diem|pto|holiday|on_call — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code categorizing why the timesheet adjustment was initiated. Supports root-cause analysis, compliance reporting, and process improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data_entry_error|system_error|missed_punch|rate_change|ot_reclassification|client_dispute|worker_dispute|manager_correction — promote to reference product]']
  reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative explanation provided by the requestor describing the specific circumstances requiring the timesheet adjustment. Supplements the reason code with contextual detail for audit and dispute resolution.']
  requestor_role varchar [note: 'The business role of the individual who submitted the adjustment request. Provides context for approval routing and compliance audit without requiring a join to the user table.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated or triggered the timesheet adjustment (e.g., Kronos Workforce Ready, Bullhorn, Beeline VMS, TempWorks, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation across integrated systems.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the timesheet adjustment request was formally submitted into the workflow system. Maps to the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for SLA tracking and audit trail.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this timesheet adjustment record was last modified. Maps to the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Tracks all changes for compliance and audit purposes.']
  work_date date [note: 'The specific calendar date on which the hours being adjusted were originally worked. Enables day-level audit trail and supports overtime calculation corrections at the daily level.']

  Note: 'Records post-submission corrections and amendments to approved timesheets, capturing the original timesheet reference, adjustment type (hours correction, rate correction, pay type reclassification, retroactive change), original values, adjusted values, adjustment reason, requestor, approver, and effective pay period. Maintains a complete audit trail of all timesheet modifications for compliance, dispute resolution, and payroll/billing reconciliation.'
}

Table timesheet.punch_event {
  punch_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual punch event record in the Kronos Workforce Ready system. Serves as the immutable primary key for this append-only audit-grade event log. Role: EVENT_LOG.']
  amended_from_punch_punch_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original punch event record that this amended record corrects. Null for original (non-amended) punch events. Enables full amendment chain traceability for DOL audit defense and FLSA wage-and-hour dispute resolution. Supports the immutable append-only audit log pattern.']
