  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active placement or assignment under which the worker is clocking time. Associates the punch event to a specific client engagement, job order, and pay/bill rate context for downstream payroll and billing processing.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client cost center or department to which the worker\'s labor hours are allocated for this punch event. Supports labor cost distribution, client billing by department, and workforce analytics. Particularly relevant for transfer punch types where the worker moves between cost centers mid-shift.']
  pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll pay period within which this punch event falls. Links the raw punch event to the TempWorks Payroll processing cycle for wage calculation, tax withholding, and W-2 reporting.']
  primary_punch_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Kronos system user (supervisor, payroll admin, or system process) who created the amendment to this punch event. Null for original punch events. Supports accountability tracking and SOC 2 access control audit requirements.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contingent worker who performed the punch action. Links to the worker master record for identity resolution, payroll processing, and FLSA compliance reporting.']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Kronos scheduled shift that this punch event is associated with. Used to calculate schedule adherence, early/late arrival deviations, and unscheduled overtime. Null if the punch was not associated with a pre-defined schedule.']
  time_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.time_entry. Business justification: punch_event captures raw clock-in/clock-out events from Kronos, while time_entry is the processed daily time record. Multiple punch events (clock in, clock out, break punches) contribute to a single t']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent timesheet record that aggregates this punch event into a daily or weekly time entry. Multiple punch events roll up into a single timesheet for approval and payroll processing.']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical client worksite or facility where the worker is assigned and where the punch event occurred. Used for geofence configuration lookup, workers\' compensation jurisdiction determination, and site-level labor analytics.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason code explaining why this punch event record was created as an amendment to a prior punch. Null for original punch events. Required for DOL audit defense and internal compliance review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: missed_punch|device_failure|system_error|supervisor_correction|worker_request|schedule_adjustment|other — promote to reference product]']
  amendment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp when the amendment to this punch event was recorded in Kronos. Null for original punch events. Provides the audit trail timestamp for change management and DOL recordkeeping compliance.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp when the punch event was approved by a supervisor or auto-approved by the system. Null if not yet approved. Marks the point at which the punch event becomes eligible for inclusion in payroll processing and client billing.']
  billable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hours associated with this punch event are billable to the client. True: hours are billable and will feed Salesforce Revenue Cloud invoicing; False: non-billable (e.g., orientation, training, administrative time). Drives client billing eligibility in the downstream billing pipeline.']
  biometric_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the punch event was authenticated via biometric verification (fingerprint scan, facial recognition) on the Kronos time clock. True: biometric match confirmed worker identity; False: biometric check failed or was bypassed. Null if the device does not support biometric authentication. Critical for buddy-punch prevention and FLSA compliance.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the Kronos time clock hardware, mobile application instance, or web portal session that captured the punch event. Used for device-level audit trails, buddy-punch fraud detection, and equipment management. Sourced directly from the Kronos Workforce Ready device registry.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this punch event triggered one or more Kronos exception conditions (e.g., missed punch, early in, late out, geofence violation, unscheduled punch). True: one or more exceptions exist; False: no exceptions. Drives exception queue routing for supervisor review and payroll hold workflows.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of exception condition triggered by this punch event in Kronos. Examples include: missed_punch, early_in, late_in, early_out, late_out, long_break, short_break, geofence_violation, unscheduled_punch. Null if exception_flag is False. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: missed_punch|early_in|late_in|early_out|late_out|long_break|short_break|geofence_violation|unscheduled_punch — promote to reference product]']
  flsa_workweek_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the FLSA-defined 7-consecutive-day workweek period within which this punch event falls. The FLSA workweek is fixed and recurring, defined per employer or client site. Critical for accurate overtime calculation — OT is calculated per workweek, not per pay period.']
  geofence_validated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the punch event location coordinates fell within the approved geofence boundary for the assigned worksite at the time of the punch. True: worker was within the geofenced zone; False: worker was outside the boundary, triggering an exception alert. Null if geofence validation was not applicable (e.g., physical time clock punch).']
  grace_period_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Kronos grace period rule was applied to this punch event, allowing a small window (e.g., 5 minutes) before or after the scheduled time without triggering an exception or early/late flag. Supports schedule adherence reporting accuracy.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp when this punch event record was first ingested into the Databricks Silver layer from the Kronos source system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED marker for data lineage, SLA monitoring of data pipeline latency, and audit trail completeness.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address of the device or network from which the punch event was submitted (applicable for web portal and mobile app punches). Used for fraud detection, buddy-punch investigation, and network-level audit trails. May be considered PII under GDPR and CCPA in some jurisdictions.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'The Kronos job code or labor category code associated with the work being performed at the time of the punch. Drives pay rate selection, bill rate application, and FLSA overtime classification. May change on a transfer punch when the worker switches tasks or roles mid-shift.']
  kronos_punch_ref varchar [note: 'The native punch event identifier assigned by the Kronos Workforce Ready source system. Preserved for cross-system reconciliation, data lineage tracing, and support ticket resolution. Enables exact record matching between the Databricks Silver layer and the Kronos operational system.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp when this punch event record was last modified in the Databricks Silver layer (e.g., status update from raw to validated, amendment applied). Supports incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail completeness.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate (WGS-84 decimal degrees) captured at the moment of the punch event from a mobile device or GPS-enabled time clock. Used for geofence validation to confirm the worker was physically present at the designated worksite. Null if the device does not support GPS.']
  location_accuracy_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'The GPS accuracy radius in meters reported by the mobile device at the time of the punch event. Lower values indicate higher precision. Used to qualify geofence validation results — punches with accuracy radius exceeding the geofence boundary tolerance may be flagged for manual review.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate (WGS-84 decimal degrees) captured at the moment of the punch event from a mobile device or GPS-enabled time clock. Paired with latitude for geofence boundary validation and worksite presence verification.']
  pay_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pay category applicable to the hours captured by this punch event. regular: standard straight-time hours; overtime (OT): hours exceeding FLSA or state OT thresholds; double_time (DT): hours qualifying for double-time premium; per_diem: expense reimbursement-based pay; holiday: holiday premium pay; pto: Paid Time Off. Drives pay rate multiplier selection in TempWorks Payroll.']
  punch_source varchar [note: 'The channel or interface through which the punch event was submitted. time_clock: physical Kronos hardware terminal; mobile_app: Kronos mobile application; web_portal: browser-based Kronos portal; supervisor_entry: manually entered by a supervisor on behalf of the worker; api: submitted via system integration. Supports fraud detection and channel-level analytics.']
  punch_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the punch event record. raw: received from source device, not yet validated; validated: passed geofence, biometric, and schedule checks; amended: manually corrected by supervisor or payroll admin; rejected: failed validation rules; voided: nullified for audit purposes. Drives downstream time_entry aggregation eligibility.']
  punch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact UTC timestamp when the worker performed the clock action (punch-in, punch-out, break start, break end, or transfer). This is the authoritative real-world event time used for FLSA wage-and-hour calculations, DOL audit defense, and overtime determination. Stored in UTC per ISO 8601.']
  punch_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating the nature of the clock action recorded. clock_in: worker begins a work period; clock_out: worker ends a work period; break_start: worker begins an unpaid or paid break; break_end: worker resumes work after a break; transfer: worker moves between cost centers, departments, or job codes mid-shift. Drives FLSA break compliance and hours calculation logic.']
  raw_payload varchar [note: 'The complete raw JSON or XML payload received from the Kronos Workforce Ready source system for this punch event. Stored as an immutable string to support full audit reconstruction, dispute resolution, and re-processing in the event of transformation errors. Retention: minimum 3 years per FLSA recordkeeping requirements.']
  rounded_punch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The effective punch timestamp after application of the configured Kronos rounding rule. Used as the basis for hours calculation in time_entry aggregation. Null if rounding_rule_applied is False. Stored separately from the raw punch_timestamp to preserve the original immutable record for FLSA audit defense.']
  rounding_rule_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Kronos time rounding rule was applied to the raw punch timestamp to produce the effective time used in hours calculations. True: rounding was applied (e.g., nearest 15-minute increment); False: raw timestamp used as-is. FLSA permits rounding only when it averages out over time and does not systematically undercount hours.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp of the worker\'s scheduled shift end time as defined in Kronos. Compared against the actual clock-out punch_timestamp to calculate early departure or overtime variance for schedule adherence and FLSA overtime analysis.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp of the worker\'s scheduled shift start time as defined in Kronos. Compared against the actual punch_timestamp to calculate early/late arrival variance for schedule adherence reporting and SLA compliance tracking.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'The IANA time zone identifier (e.g., \'America/Chicago\', \'America/New_York\') of the worksite where the punch event occurred. Required to correctly convert UTC punch_timestamp to local work date for FLSA workweek boundary calculations and state wage-and-hour law compliance.']
  work_date date [note: 'The calendar date (in the worker\'s local time zone) on which the punch event occurred. Used for daily timesheet aggregation, FLSA workweek calculations, overtime determination, and client billing period alignment. Derived from punch_timestamp adjusted to the site\'s configured time zone.']

  Note: 'Raw, immutable clock event record capturing individual worker punch-in and punch-out events from Kronos Workforce Ready time clocks, mobile apps, or web portals. Stores the exact UTC timestamp, punch type (in/out/break-start/break-end/transfer), device ID, location coordinates, geofence validation result, biometric verification flag, IP address, and raw source payload. Multiple punch events are processed into a single time_entry record. Serves as the tamper-proof audit-grade source record for FLSA compliance, wage-and-hour dispute resolution, and DOL audit defense. Retention policy: minimum 3 years per FLSA recordkeeping requirements.'
}

Table timesheet.labor_category {
  labor_category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the labor category record. Primary key for the labor_category reference entity. [_canonical_skip_reason: Entity role is REFERENCE_LOOKUP — a reference/code table defining labor classifications; per-role minimums are exempt, but MASTER_RESOURCE categories are applied given the entity carries substantive business attributes beyond a simple enumeration.]']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Labor categories map to rate schedule job categories; rate schedules define bill/pay rates per labor category for contract compliance. Real business process: rate card mapping and labor category prici']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Labor categories may have default cost center mappings for specific service lines or client types. Enables automated cost center allocation rules in staffing operations; common for standardized client']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Labor categories map to GL accounts for standardized expense classification across engagements. Staffing firms use labor category-to-GL mappings for consistent financial reporting and automated timesh']
  ot_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the overtime rule set applicable to this labor category. Determines how overtime hours are calculated (e.g., daily OT after 8 hours, weekly OT after 40 hours, California daily/weekly OT rules). Links to the ot_rule reference entity as specified in the product description.']
  vendor_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_rate_card. Business justification: Labor categories must map to vendor-specific rate cards for rate validation, margin analysis, and vendor bill rate verification. When evaluating vendor submissions or processing timesheets, staffing f']
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Labor categories must have validated wage-hour determinations (FLSA classification, prevailing wage, exempt status). Rate cards cannot be published without determination. Real business process: compli']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business user or manager who approved this labor category definition and its associated rates. Required for governance and audit compliance in rate card management.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this labor category record was formally approved by the designated approver. Used for governance, audit trail, and effective dating validation.']
  billable_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether time entries coded to this labor category are billable to the client. When true, hours are included in client invoicing. When false, hours are internal or non-billable (e.g., orientation, training, bench time).']
  burden_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The total employer burden rate percentage applied to the base pay rate for workers in this labor category. Includes employer-side FICA, FUTA, SUTA, workers comp, and benefits costs expressed as a percentage of base pay. Used for gross margin and spread analysis.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the labor category. Used in timesheets, billing systems, and VMS integrations to reference the category. Sourced from Kronos Workforce Ready and synchronized to Beeline VMS and Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  category_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the labor category, including scope of work, typical duties, and applicable skill requirements. Used for client-facing documentation, MSA exhibits, and SOW labor category definitions.']
  category_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the labor category (e.g., \'Software Engineer – Senior\', \'Light Industrial – Forklift Operator\', \'Administrative – Data Entry\'). Displayed in timesheets, client invoices, and workforce analytics dashboards.']
  category_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification grouping the labor category into a workforce segment (e.g., professional, light industrial, clerical, technical, healthcare, executive, skilled trades). Drives workforce analytics segmentation and client billing tier assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: professional|light_industrial|clerical|technical|healthcare|executive|skilled_trades — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this labor category record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle management in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.']
  default_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default hourly bill rate (in USD) charged to the client for workers assigned to this labor category. Serves as the baseline rate before client-specific or contract-specific overrides are applied. Used by Salesforce Revenue Cloud for invoice generation.']
  default_dt_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default hourly double-time (DT) bill rate charged to the client for double-time hours worked by workers in this labor category. Applicable in states or contracts requiring double-time pay (e.g., California labor law).']
  default_dt_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default hourly double-time (DT) pay rate paid to workers in this labor category for double-time hours. Applicable under state-specific labor laws or union agreements requiring double-time compensation.']
  default_ot_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default hourly overtime (OT) bill rate charged to the client for overtime hours worked by workers in this labor category. Typically 1.5x the standard bill rate unless a client-specific rate card overrides it.']
  default_ot_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default hourly overtime (OT) pay rate paid to workers in this labor category for overtime hours. Must comply with FLSA overtime requirements (minimum 1.5x regular rate for non-exempt workers).']
  default_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default hourly pay rate (in USD) paid to workers assigned to this labor category. Serves as the baseline rate before worker-specific or assignment-specific overrides are applied. Used by TempWorks Payroll for compensation calculation.']
  eeo_job_category varchar [note: 'EEOC EEO-1 job category assigned to this labor category for equal employment opportunity reporting. Required for federal contractors and employers with 100+ employees. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: executives_senior_officials|first_mid_officials|professionals|technicians|sales_workers|administrative_support|craft_workers|operatives|laborers_helpers|service_workers — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this labor category definition and its associated rates become effective. Supports rate versioning and historical rate lookups for timesheet processing and billing reconciliation.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this labor category definition and its associated rates expire. Null for open-ended categories. After this date, the category is no longer available for new timesheet entries unless renewed or superseded.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether workers in this labor category are classified as exempt or non-exempt under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA). Non-exempt workers are entitled to overtime pay; exempt workers are not. Critical for overtime rule application and payroll compliance.']
  ic_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether workers in this labor category may be engaged as independent contractors (1099) rather than W-2 employees. Drives worker classification compliance checks per IRS worker classification rules and DOL economic reality test.']
  job_function_code varchar [note: 'Standardized job function code classifying the primary work function of this labor category (e.g., IT, Finance, Operations, Engineering, Healthcare). Aligns with O*NET or SOC occupational classification codes for workforce analytics and regulatory reporting.']
  kronos_labor_category_ref varchar [note: 'The native labor category identifier in Kronos Workforce Ready, the system of record for time and attendance. Used for system integration and data lineage tracing between the lakehouse silver layer and the Kronos source system.']
  labor_category_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the labor category record. \'active\' indicates the category is available for use in timesheet entries and billing; \'inactive\' indicates it has been retired; \'pending\' indicates it is awaiting approval; \'deprecated\' indicates it has been superseded by a newer category.']
  markup_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Default markup percentage applied over the pay rate to derive the bill rate for this labor category. Represents the spread as a percentage of pay rate. Used in pricing models and profitability analysis. Markup = (Bill Rate - Pay Rate) / Pay Rate × 100.']
  max_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable hourly bill rate for this labor category. Used as a ceiling in VMS rate card negotiations and client contract management. Bill rates above this ceiling require management approval.']
  min_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum allowable hourly pay rate for workers assigned to this labor category. Enforces compliance with applicable federal, state, or local minimum wage laws and prevailing wage requirements. Pay rates below this floor are rejected during payroll processing.']
  per_diem_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether workers assigned to this labor category are eligible for per diem allowances (daily expense reimbursements for travel, lodging, or meals). Affects payroll processing and client billing for travel-based assignments.']
  prevailing_wage_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this labor category is subject to prevailing wage requirements under the Davis-Bacon Act or Service Contract Act (SCA) for government or federally funded contracts. When true, the prevailing_wage_rate field applies.']
  prevailing_wage_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The federally or state-mandated prevailing wage rate (in USD per hour) applicable to this labor category for government or public works contracts. Determined by DOL wage determinations under the Davis-Bacon Act or SCA. Null when prevailing_wage_indicator is false.']
  rate_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the default bill/pay rate tier associated with this labor category. Rate tiers define pricing bands used across client contracts and VMS programs. Links to rate tier configuration in Beeline VMS and Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  sca_labor_category_code varchar [note: 'The official Service Contract Act (SCA) labor category code or title as defined in the applicable DOL wage determination. Used to map internal labor categories to SCA-compliant job classifications for federal service contracts. Required for compliance reporting on government staffing contracts.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'Proficiency or seniority tier associated with the labor category (e.g., entry, junior, mid, senior, lead, principal, executive). Used to differentiate bill/pay rate tiers and to support workforce planning analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entry|junior|mid|senior|lead|principal|executive — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  soc_code varchar [note: 'U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) code mapped to this labor category. Used for EEOC reporting, DOL compliance, workforce analytics, and labor market benchmarking. Format: XX-XXXX (e.g., 15-1252 for Software Developers).']
  temp_to_perm_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether placements in this labor category are eligible for temp-to-perm conversion (contract-to-hire). When true, a conversion fee may apply upon permanent placement. Used in Bullhorn ATS/CRM for placement tracking and conversion fee calculation.']
  union_applicable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this labor category is subject to a collective bargaining agreement (CBA) or union work rules. When true, the union_code field identifies the applicable union. Affects pay rates, overtime rules, and benefit requirements.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the labor union or collective bargaining agreement (CBA) applicable to this labor category. Null when union_applicable is false. Used to apply union-specific pay rules, benefit contributions, and work rules in payroll processing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this labor category record was last modified. Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.']
  vms_category_code varchar [note: 'The labor category code as defined in the client\'s Vendor Management System (VMS), specifically Beeline VMS. Used to map internal labor categories to VMS-defined categories for contingent workforce program management and rate card alignment.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'NCCI (National Council on Compensation Insurance) workers compensation class code associated with this labor category. Determines the workers comp insurance rate applied to payroll for workers in this category. Used by TempWorks Payroll for burden rate calculation.']

  Note: 'Reference entity defining labor categories and work classifications used to categorize time entries for cost allocation, billing rate determination, and workforce analytics. Captures category code, category name, skill level, FLSA classification (exempt/non-exempt), prevailing wage indicator, prevailing wage rate (for Davis-Bacon Act compliance on government staffing contracts), union code applicability, SCA (Service Contract Act) labor category mapping, and default bill/pay rate tier. Used by clients and staffing operations to segment labor costs by function, skill, or contract type. Links to ot_rule for determining applicable overtime calculation rules.'
}

Table timesheet.timesheet_dispute {
  timesheet_dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each timesheet dispute record. Primary key for the timesheet_dispute data product. TRANSACTION_HEADER role entity.']
  adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.timesheet_adjustment. Business justification: timesheet_dispute tracks formal disputes raised against timesheets. When a dispute is resolved, it often results in a timesheet_adjustment. This FK links the dispute to the adjustment that resolved it']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active placement or assignment under which the disputed timesheet was submitted. Ties the dispute to the specific job order and client engagement.']
  billing_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing adjustment or credit memo created in Salesforce Revenue Cloud as a result of the dispute resolution. Null until the dispute is resolved and a billing correction is issued to the client.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client organization associated with the disputed timesheet. Used for client-facing SLA tracking and billing dispute resolution.']
  pay_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll adjustment transaction created in TempWorks Payroll as a result of the dispute resolution. Null until the dispute is resolved and a payroll correction is processed.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contingent worker whose timesheet is under dispute. Used to associate the dispute with the worker\'s placement and payroll records.']
  regulatory_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_violation. Business justification: Timesheet disputes may escalate to regulatory violations (unpaid wages, misclassification claims filed with DOL/state agencies). Real business process: dispute escalation tracking linking worker compl']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific internal staff member (coordinator, payroll analyst, or client manager) currently assigned to work the dispute. Enables individual-level SLA tracking and workload reporting.']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific timesheet record that is the subject of this dispute. Links the dispute to the authoritative hours record in Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly bill rate charged to the client for the disputed timesheet period. Used to calculate the billable adjustment amount for client invoicing. Sourced from Beeline VMS or the client contract.']
  claimed_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The number of hours the dispute initiator asserts should have been recorded. Compared against recorded_hours to determine the hours discrepancy and calculate the disputed pay or bill amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage. May differ from submitted_timestamp if there is a processing lag.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the dispute at creation. Used in worker and client communications, back-office tracking, and SLA reporting. Format: DISP- followed by 8–12 digits.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the dispute. Drives SLA tracking and operational dashboards. Values: open (newly raised), under_review (being investigated), resolved (outcome determined), escalated (elevated to senior staff or client), withdrawn (initiator retracted), closed (finalized with no further action).']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the dispute. Drives routing, resolution workflow, and compliance reporting. Values: hours_discrepancy (worker claims hours differ from recorded), rate_dispute (bill rate or pay rate is incorrect), missing_time (hours not captured), unauthorized_ot (overtime not pre-approved), per_diem_dispute (per diem amount contested), other.']
  disputed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The gross monetary value under dispute, expressed in USD. Calculated as the difference between the claimed and recorded hours multiplied by the applicable pay or bill rate. Used for payroll adjustment and client billing reconciliation.']
  disputed_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The number of hours being contested in the dispute. Represents the delta between what the worker claims and what was recorded, or the total hours in question for missing-time disputes. Used for payroll adjustment calculations.']
  disputed_work_date date [note: 'The specific calendar date on the timesheet that is being disputed. For multi-day disputes, this represents the start date of the disputed period.']
  disputed_work_date_end date [note: 'The end date of the disputed work period for multi-day disputes. Null for single-day disputes. Together with disputed_work_date defines the full disputed pay period window.']
  dt_hours_disputed decimal(6,2) [note: 'The number of double-time hours specifically contested within the dispute. Applicable in states or contracts where double-time rates apply (e.g., California labor law). Null if no double-time hours are in dispute.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute has been escalated beyond the standard resolution workflow (e.g., to senior management, legal, or the client\'s MSP/VMS program). True when dispute_status is escalated or when SLA breach has occurred.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of why the dispute was escalated. Captured when escalation_flag is True. Includes reasons such as SLA breach, high monetary value, repeated dispute by same worker, or client-requested escalation.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the dispute was escalated. Used to measure escalation response time and track SLA compliance from the point of escalation.']
  initiated_by varchar [note: 'Party who raised the dispute. Determines the communication and resolution workflow. Values: worker (contingent worker self-reported), client (client contact raised the issue), back_office (internal staffing coordinator identified discrepancy), system (automated detection via Kronos or payroll reconciliation).']
  initiator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who raised the dispute (worker name, client contact name, or back-office staff name). Captured at time of submission for audit trail purposes.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the dispute record. Tracks status changes, note additions, and resolution updates for audit and SLA compliance.']
  ot_hours_disputed decimal(6,2) [note: 'The number of overtime hours specifically contested within the dispute. Relevant for unauthorized_ot dispute types where the client or back-office challenges OT hours that were not pre-approved. Governed by FLSA overtime provisions.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The worker\'s hourly pay rate applicable to the disputed timesheet period. Used to calculate the payable adjustment amount for the worker. Sourced from TempWorks Payroll and the placement record.']
  per_diem_amount_disputed decimal(10,2) [note: 'The per diem reimbursement amount being contested in the dispute. Applicable when dispute_type is per_diem_dispute. Per diem rates are governed by IRS guidelines and client MSA terms.']
  prior_dispute_count varchar [note: 'The number of previously submitted disputes associated with the same worker and placement at the time this dispute was raised. Used for risk flagging, pattern detection, and compliance reporting. A high count may indicate a systemic timekeeping issue.']
  recorded_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The number of hours as originally recorded on the timesheet at the time the dispute was raised. Provides the baseline for comparison against the worker\'s or client\'s claimed hours.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the dispute was formally resolved and a resolution_outcome was recorded. Used to calculate actual resolution cycle time against SLA targets.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the findings, evidence reviewed, and rationale for the resolution outcome. Captured by the resolving party and retained for compliance and audit purposes.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'The final determination of the dispute. Values: approved_in_full (all disputed hours/amounts accepted), approved_partial (partial adjustment granted), denied (dispute rejected), withdrawn (initiator retracted before resolution), no_action_required (investigation found no discrepancy). Null while dispute is open or under review.']
  resolved_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The final monetary adjustment amount approved as a result of the dispute resolution. Drives payroll correction entries in TempWorks and billing adjustments in Salesforce Revenue Cloud. Null until resolution_outcome is set.']
  resolved_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The final agreed-upon number of hours after dispute resolution. May equal claimed_hours (full approval), a value between recorded_hours and claimed_hours (partial), or recorded_hours (denial). Used to drive payroll and billing adjustments.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'The internal role or team currently responsible for investigating and resolving the dispute. Used for workload assignment, SLA accountability, and escalation routing. Values: staffing_coordinator, payroll_team, client_manager, vms_admin, escalation_team.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute resolution exceeded the defined SLA target hours. True when the dispute was not resolved within sla_target_hours of submitted_timestamp. Feeds SLA compliance reporting and client KPI dashboards.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'The contractually or operationally defined number of business hours within which the dispute must be resolved. Sourced from the client MSA or internal SLA policy. Used to calculate SLA compliance and breach status.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the dispute was submitted. Values: portal (worker or client self-service portal), email (submitted via email to back-office), phone (verbal submission logged by coordinator), vms (raised through Beeline VMS), ats (raised through Bullhorn ATS/CRM), manual (back-office data entry). Used for channel analytics and process improvement.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the dispute was formally submitted into the system. This is the principal business event timestamp used as the SLA clock start for dispute resolution tracking.']
  supporting_document_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting documentation (e.g., badge swipe records, supervisor sign-off, email correspondence, system logs) has been attached to the dispute record. True when at least one document has been submitted as evidence.']
  timesheet_week_ending_date date [note: 'The week-ending date of the pay period associated with the disputed timesheet. Used to align the dispute with the correct payroll cycle and billing period for adjustment processing.']
  vms_dispute_reference varchar [note: 'The dispute or ticket reference number assigned by the client\'s VMS platform (e.g., Beeline) when the dispute was raised through a managed service program. Enables cross-system traceability for MSP/VMS-managed accounts. Null for non-VMS clients.']

  Note: 'Tracks formal disputes raised by workers or clients against submitted or approved timesheets. Captures dispute type (hours discrepancy, rate dispute, missing time, unauthorized OT), dispute initiator (worker, client, back-office), disputed timesheet reference, disputed amount, dispute status (open, under review, resolved, escalated), resolution outcome, resolution date, and responsible party. Supports SLA tracking for dispute resolution and feeds compliance reporting.'
}

Table timesheet.vms_timesheet_sync {
  vms_timesheet_sync_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for each VMS timesheet synchronization record in the Staffing Hr lakehouse Silver layer. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete sync event with its own lifecycle between Kronos Workforce Ready and a client VMS platform.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contingent worker placement record associated with this timesheet sync. Identifies the specific assignment under which the worker submitted hours, linking the sync event to the placement lifecycle in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client organization whose VMS platform is the target or source of this synchronization. Identifies the MSP/VMS-managed program client for billing dispute resolution and program reporting.']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: VMS sync occurs within managed programs (MSP/RPO); program-specific integration rules and SLA tracking require this link. Essential for managed program performance reporting and program-specific VMS r']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contingent worker whose timesheet hours are being synchronized. Enables worker-level reconciliation reporting and audit trails for payroll and billing accuracy.']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal Kronos Workforce Ready timesheet record being synchronized. Links this sync event to the authoritative timesheet in the internal time-and-attendance system.']
  vms_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vms_enrollment. Business justification: VMS timesheet synchronization requires vendor enrollment context to route data correctly to VMS platforms (Fieldglass, Beeline). Integration middleware must identify which vendor enrollment applies to']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the MSP/VMS-managed contingent workforce program within the VMS platform. Used to segment sync records by client program for program-level reporting, SLA tracking, and billing reconciliation under MSP agreements.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval state of the timesheet within the client VMS platform. \'pending\' = awaiting client manager approval; \'approved\' = client approved in VMS; \'rejected\' = client rejected in VMS; \'disputed\' = under active dispute; \'recalled\' = previously approved but recalled for correction. Drives billing eligibility.']
  bill_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The hourly bill rate applied to this timesheet for client invoicing purposes, as agreed in the MSA or VMS rate card. Used to validate that the rate applied in the VMS matches the contracted rate in Kronos/Bullhorn. A mismatch triggers a billing dispute.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The client cost center code associated with this timesheet as recorded in the VMS platform. Used for client-side labor cost allocation and is a common source of field-level mapping discrepancies between Kronos and VMS systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this VMS timesheet sync record was first created in the Staffing Hr lakehouse. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED timestamp for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  dt_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total double-time (DT) hours recorded on the timesheet being synchronized, applicable in states such as California where DT is mandated for hours beyond 12 in a day or 8 on the 7th consecutive day. Ensures accurate billing and payroll at DT rates.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'The error code returned by the VMS platform or integration middleware when a sync attempt fails or is rejected. Used for automated error classification, retry logic routing, and integration support triage. Null when sync_status is \'synced\'.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Human-readable error description returned by the VMS platform or integration layer when a sync fails or is rejected. Provides context for manual investigation and resolution of failed sync events. Null when sync_status is \'synced\'.']
  field_mapping_discrepancies varchar [note: 'A structured description of specific field-level mismatches identified between the Kronos Workforce Ready timesheet and the VMS timesheet record (e.g., cost center code mismatch, job code mismatch, worker ID format difference). Stored as a delimited string of field names with discrepancy details. Used for root-cause analysis and integration mapping corrections.']
  hours_variance decimal(8,2) [note: 'The difference between vms_total_hours and total_hours (VMS hours minus Kronos hours). A non-zero value indicates a discrepancy requiring reconciliation. Positive values mean the VMS recorded more hours; negative values mean Kronos recorded more hours. Used to prioritize reconciliation effort and assess billing risk.']
  integration_batch_ref varchar [note: 'The batch or job run identifier from the integration middleware that processed this sync record. Used for operational troubleshooting, replay of failed batches, and integration audit trails. Distinct from internal ETL metadata — this is a business-visible integration reference used by the integration operations team.']
  kronos_timesheet_ref_code varchar [note: 'The internal timesheet identifier as assigned by Kronos Workforce Ready. Used to cross-reference the internal time-and-attendance record with the VMS record during reconciliation and audit.']
  last_retry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time of the most recent retry attempt for this synchronization record. Used alongside retry_count to calculate retry intervals, enforce exponential backoff policies, and identify stale failed syncs requiring manual intervention.']
  manual_override_by varchar [note: 'The username or employee identifier of the Staffing Hr operator who applied the manual override to this sync record. Required for SOC 2 audit trail and accountability. Populated only when manual_override_flag is True.']
  manual_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a human operator manually overrode the automated sync result for this record (True = manual override applied; False = automated sync result). Manual overrides are required when automated reconciliation cannot resolve discrepancies. Triggers enhanced audit logging per SOC 2 controls.']
  manual_override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the operator when a manual override is applied to this sync record. Required when manual_override_flag is True. Documents the business justification for the override for audit and compliance purposes.']
  ot_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total overtime (OT) hours recorded on the timesheet being synchronized, typically hours worked beyond 40 per week or 8 per day per FLSA or applicable state law. Critical for accurate billing at OT bill rates and worker compensation at OT pay rates.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The hourly pay rate for the contingent worker associated with this timesheet, as recorded in the payroll system. Used alongside bill_rate to calculate spread and gross margin. Confidential compensation data.']
  per_diem_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The per diem (daily expense allowance) amount included in this timesheet record, if applicable. Per diem is a non-taxable reimbursement for meals and incidentals for workers on travel assignments. Must be reconciled between Kronos and VMS to ensure accurate billing and payroll.']
  reconciliation_outcome varchar [note: 'The final outcome of the reconciliation process for this sync record. \'matched\' = hours match exactly with no intervention; \'reconciled\' = discrepancy resolved through manual or automated correction; \'unresolved\' = discrepancy remains open; \'escalated\' = referred to billing dispute team; \'voided\' = timesheet voided and excluded from billing. Critical for billing dispute management and MSP program reporting.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the reconciliation outcome was determined and recorded for this sync record. Used for SLA measurement of reconciliation cycle time and billing cutoff compliance.']
  regular_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total regular (straight-time) hours recorded on the timesheet being synchronized. Used to verify that regular hours match between Kronos Workforce Ready and the VMS platform to prevent billing disputes.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this synchronization has been retried after an initial failure. Used to enforce maximum retry thresholds, trigger escalation workflows, and measure integration reliability. A high retry count may indicate a systemic VMS connectivity or data mapping issue.']
  sync_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates the direction of the timesheet data flow: \'push\' means Kronos Workforce Ready is sending hours to the VMS, \'pull\' means the VMS-approved timesheet is being retrieved into Kronos. Determines the authoritative source for reconciliation.']
  sync_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this timesheet synchronization attempt. \'pending\' = awaiting processing; \'synced\' = successfully matched in both systems; \'failed\' = technical error prevented sync; \'rejected\' = VMS rejected the timesheet data; \'partial\' = some fields synced but discrepancies remain. This is the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for the sync transaction.']
  sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this synchronization attempt was executed. Represents the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP — the principal real-world event time of the sync operation. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  timesheet_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the authoritative source system from which the timesheet data originated for this sync event. \'Kronos\' = Kronos Workforce Ready is the source of record; \'VMS\' = the client VMS platform is the source of record (pull scenario); \'Manual\' = hours were entered manually outside of either system. Used for data lineage and audit.']
  total_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total billable hours on the timesheet (sum of regular, OT, and DT hours) as recorded in the source system. Used as the primary reconciliation figure between Kronos Workforce Ready and the VMS platform. Discrepancies in this field trigger billing dispute workflows.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this VMS timesheet sync record was last modified, such as after a retry, manual override, or reconciliation outcome update. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED timestamp.']
  vms_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the client manager approved the timesheet within the VMS platform. Marks the point at which hours become billable and payable. Used for Time-to-Approve (TTA) SLA reporting and billing cycle cutoff determination.']
  vms_approver_name varchar [note: 'The name of the client manager or supervisor who approved or rejected the timesheet within the VMS platform. Required for audit trail, dispute resolution, and SLA compliance reporting under MSP/VMS program agreements.']
  vms_job_ref_code varchar [note: 'The job or requisition identifier as assigned by the client VMS platform. Used to associate the synced timesheet with the correct VMS job order or requisition for cost center allocation and client billing.']
  vms_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the client Vendor Management System (VMS) platform involved in this synchronization event. Identifies whether the sync is with Beeline, SAP Fieldglass, VNDLY, Coupa, Workday, or another VMS. Critical for platform-specific error handling and integration routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Beeline|Fieldglass|VNDLY|Coupa|Workday|Other — promote to reference product if additional platforms are onboarded]']
  vms_timesheet_ref_code varchar [note: 'The external timesheet identifier assigned by the client VMS platform (e.g., Beeline timesheet number, Fieldglass time sheet ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and billing dispute resolution. This is the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for the sync transaction as known externally.']
  vms_total_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total hours as recorded or approved within the client VMS platform for this timesheet period. Compared against total_hours from Kronos to identify discrepancies. A mismatch between this field and total_hours is the primary trigger for reconciliation workflows and billing dispute escalation.']
  vms_worker_ref_code varchar [note: 'The worker identifier as assigned by the client VMS platform (e.g., Beeline worker ID, Fieldglass contingent worker number). Used to map the internal worker record to the VMS worker profile during sync and reconciliation. Distinct from the internal worker_id.']
  work_week_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the work week covered by the timesheet being synchronized. Together with work_week_start_date, defines the exact pay period for payroll and billing reconciliation.']
  work_week_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the work week covered by the timesheet being synchronized. Used to align the sync record with the correct pay period and billing cycle for payroll processing and client invoicing.']

  Note: 'Tracks the bidirectional synchronization and reconciliation of timesheet records between the internal Kronos Workforce Ready system and client Vendor Management System (VMS) platforms such as Beeline, Fieldglass, VNDLY, or Coupa. Captures the VMS platform identifier, VMS timesheet reference ID, sync direction (push/pull), sync status (pending, synced, failed, rejected, partial), sync timestamp, error codes, field-level mapping discrepancies, retry count, manual override flags, and reconciliation outcome. Critical for MSP/VMS-managed programs where client approval occurs within the VMS portal and hours must match exactly between systems to avoid billing disputes.'
}

Table payroll.pay_run {
  pay_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each payroll processing cycle (pay run) executed through TempWorks Payroll. Serves as the primary key and authoritative anchor for all payroll transactions within a given cycle.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounting_period. Business justification: Each pay run posts to a specific accounting period for expense recognition and period close. Required for accurate financial reporting, especially for pay runs spanning period boundaries or off-cycle ']
  approved_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Payroll runs require approval by internal staff (payroll manager, controller, CFO). Role-prefixed to distinguish from other approvals. Critical for payroll controls, SOX compliance, and audit trail.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: Payroll runs are audited for wage/hour compliance, worker classification accuracy, and tax withholding correctness. DOL and state audits sample pay runs for FLSA compliance validation - audit sample s']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the GL journal entry batch in Oracle NetSuite ERP to which this payroll run\'s accounting entries were posted. Used for GL reconciliation and audit trail.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Pay runs are executed by specific legal entities for tax filing, compliance, and financial statement consolidation. Required for multi-entity staffing operations to track entity-specific payroll expen']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payroll run was formally approved by the authorized approver. Captured for audit trail, SLA compliance, and payroll processing timeline analytics.']
  branch_code varchar [note: 'Staffing branch or cost center code associated with this payroll run. Used for branch-level payroll cost allocation, P&L reporting, and operational analytics in Power BI.']
  check_date date [note: 'Date printed on physical payroll checks or used as the effective date for direct deposit transactions. May differ from pay_date when banking processing windows shift the settlement date.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the legal employing entity or EOR entity under which this payroll run is processed. Supports multi-entity payroll operations and co-employment models where Staffing Hr acts as the employer of record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pay run record was first created in TempWorks Payroll. Serves as the audit record creation timestamp for data lineage and SOC 2 Type II compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this payroll run. Typically USD for domestic operations; supports multi-currency for international EOR engagements.']
  disbursement_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to disburse net pay to workers in this run. Direct deposit (ACH) is standard; check and pay card are alternatives for workers without bank accounts; wire for special high-value payments.']
  gl_post_date date [note: 'Date on which this payroll run\'s journal entries were posted to the Oracle NetSuite ERP General Ledger. Marks the accounting period in which payroll costs are recognized.']
  ic_worker_count varchar [note: 'Number of 1099 independent contractors (ICs) included in this payroll run. Used for IRS 1099-NEC filing thresholds and worker classification compliance monitoring.']
  is_multi_state boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payroll run spans workers in more than one US state, triggering multi-state tax withholding, SUTA apportionment, and additional compliance review requirements.']
  pay_date date [note: 'Scheduled date on which worker net pay is disbursed via direct deposit, check, or pay card. Determines the tax deposit due date per IRS deposit schedule rules.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which workers are paid within this run. Drives pay period date calculations and tax withholding schedules per IRS Publication 15 annualization tables.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the pay period covered by this payroll run. Used for timesheet cutoff, overtime calculation, and tax period alignment.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the pay period covered by this payroll run. Together with pay_period_end_date, defines the time window for which hours worked and earnings are being compensated.']
  payroll_state_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of two-letter US state codes (e.g., \'CA,TX,NY\') representing all states in which workers were paid in this run. Drives multi-state tax filing obligations and SUTA rate application.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll run was finalized and posted to the General Ledger in Oracle NetSuite ERP, making it immutable for the accounting period.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when TempWorks Payroll completed the payroll calculation and tax computation processing for this run. Used for payroll operations SLA monitoring and processing time analytics.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the pay run, used in reconciliation, audit trails, and payroll operations communications. Format: PR-YYYY-MM-NNNN.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payroll run within TempWorks Payroll workflow. Progresses from open (data entry) through processing, approved, and posted (finalized to GL). Voided indicates the run was reversed.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payroll run indicating its operational purpose. Regular runs cover standard pay periods; off-cycle handles out-of-band payments; adjustment corrects prior-period errors; void-and-reissue replaces a voided run; supplemental covers bonuses or special payments.']
  tax_quarter varchar [note: 'Calendar quarter (1–4) to which this payroll run belongs, used for IRS Form 941 quarterly payroll tax filing and FUTA/SUTA quarterly reporting.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year to which this payroll run\'s earnings and withholdings are attributed for IRS W-2 and 1099 tax reporting purposes. Critical for year-end tax form generation.']
  total_deductions decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all voluntary and involuntary employee deductions (benefits, garnishments, 401k, health insurance premiums, etc.) withheld from worker gross pay in this run. Reconciles gross-to-net pay calculations.']
  total_dt_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of double time (DT) earnings for all workers in this run, applicable in states such as California where DT is mandated for hours exceeding 12 in a workday or 8 on the seventh consecutive day.']
  total_employer_burden decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all employer-side payroll costs beyond gross wages in this run, including employer FICA, FUTA, SUTA, workers comp premiums, and employer-paid benefits. Used to calculate the burden rate and total cost of workforce deployment.']
  total_fica_employee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of employee-side Federal Insurance Contributions Act (FICA) withholdings (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%) across all workers in this run. Reported on IRS Form 941.']
  total_fica_employer decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of employer-side Federal Insurance Contributions Act (FICA) matching contributions (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%) for all W-2 workers in this run. Included in total employer burden calculation.']
  total_futa_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of Federal Unemployment Tax Act (FUTA) employer tax accrued in this payroll run, calculated at 6.0% on the first $7,000 of each employee\'s wages (net of SUTA credit). Filed annually on IRS Form 940.']
  total_gross_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all gross earnings for all workers in this payroll run before any deductions or tax withholdings. Includes regular pay, overtime (OT), double time (DT), per diem, and other earnings components.']
  total_net_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of net pay disbursed to all workers in this run after all tax withholdings and deductions. Represents the total cash outflow for worker compensation and is the primary reconciliation figure against bank ACH/check totals.']
  total_ot_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of overtime (OT) premium earnings for all workers in this run, calculated at 1.5x the regular rate for hours exceeding 40 per workweek per FLSA. Supports OT cost monitoring and client billing reconciliation.']
  total_per_diem_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of per diem allowances paid to workers in this run for travel, lodging, and meal reimbursements. Per diem amounts within IRS GSA rates are non-taxable; amounts above are taxable wages.']
  total_pto_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of paid time off (PTO) earnings disbursed in this payroll run, including vacation, sick, and holiday pay. Supports PTO accrual reconciliation and state-mandated paid leave compliance.']
  total_regular_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of straight-time (regular rate) earnings for all workers in this run, excluding overtime, double time, and premium pay components. Used for pay rate analytics and FLSA regular rate of pay calculations.']
  total_suta_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) employer tax accrued across all applicable states in this payroll run. Rates and wage bases vary by state; critical for multi-state payroll compliance.']
  total_tax_withholding decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all employee-side tax withholdings across all workers in this run, including federal income tax, state income tax, local taxes, and employee FICA (Social Security and Medicare). Used for IRS Form 941 and state tax deposit reconciliation.']
  total_workers_comp_premium decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of workers compensation insurance premiums accrued for all workers in this payroll run, calculated based on payroll amounts and applicable workers comp class codes and rates. Supports insurance carrier reporting and burden rate analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pay run record was last modified in TempWorks Payroll or the Silver Layer. Supports change data capture, data lineage, and audit trail requirements.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or reason code for voiding this payroll run. Required when run_status = \'voided\' to support audit compliance and payroll error tracking.']
  voided_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll run was voided, if applicable. Populated only for runs with run_status = \'voided\'. Supports payroll reversal audit trail and GL correction tracking.']
  w2_worker_count varchar [note: 'Number of W-2 (Wage and Tax Statement) employees included in this payroll run. Distinguishes co-employed temporary workers from independent contractors for tax and compliance reporting.']
  worker_count varchar [note: 'Total number of workers (W-2 employees and 1099 independent contractors) included in this payroll run. Used for headcount reporting, payroll audit reconciliation, and workforce analytics.']

  Note: 'Master record for each payroll processing cycle executed through TempWorks Payroll. Captures the payroll run header including pay period start and end dates, pay date, pay frequency, run type (regular, off-cycle, adjustment, void-and-reissue), run status (open, processing, approved, posted, voided), total gross pay, total net pay, total tax withholdings, total deductions, total employer burden, worker count, processing timestamps, and approver. Serves as the authoritative anchor for all payroll transactions within a given cycle and the primary reconciliation point for payroll operations.'
}

Table payroll.pay_stub {
  pay_stub_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each individual pay stub record. Primary key for the pay_stub data product. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — serves as the anchor for all gross-to-net pay disbursement details for a single worker in a single pay run.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounting_period. Business justification: Pay stubs are recognized in specific accounting periods for expense matching and period close. Required for accurate period-end accruals and financial statement preparation in staffing operations with']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement assignment for which this pay stub was generated. Ties earnings to the specific client engagement, job order, and work location for labor cost allocation and billing reconciliation.']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.bgc_order. Business justification: Background check completion is payment eligibility gate; pay held until BGC clears for new placements. Staffing firms track which BGC order cleared a worker for specific pay period.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Pay stubs must reference governing MSA for audit trail, co-employment determination, and compliance verification. Required for client audits, dispute resolution, and demonstrating adherence to contrac']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Payroll costs are allocated to cost centers for departmental P&L, budget variance analysis, and client billing markup calculations. Essential for staffing agency profitability tracking by branch/divis']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Payroll processing validates credential status before issuing payment; compliance holds block pay_stub generation when mandatory credentials expire. Staffing firms routinely hold paychecks until BGC/l']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire employees receive pay stubs when staffing firm handles payroll during transition or under PEO arrangements. Links pay records to permanent placement for payroll continuity and compliance.']
  everify_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.everify_case. Business justification: E-Verify case status must be resolved before continuing payroll for new hires at federal contractor clients. TNC (Tentative Non-Confirmation) cases require payroll suspension until resolution - federa']
  i9_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.i9_verification. Business justification: Every pay stub requires valid I-9 work authorization verification before payment issuance. Staffing firms cannot pay workers without documented work eligibility - core compliance gate for payroll proc']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Each pay stub is issued by a specific legal entity for W-2 reporting and tax compliance. Required for multi-entity staffing operations to track which entity is the employer for each worker assignment.']
  onboarding_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to onboarding.onboarding_engagement. Business justification: Payroll validation requires verifying worker completed onboarding before issuing first paycheck. Links pay stub to the onboarding engagement that authorized payment. Critical for compliance audits and']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.order_header. Business justification: Pay stubs must link to job orders for client billing reconciliation, revenue recognition by order, and job-level margin analysis. Essential for staffing P&L reporting and invoice validation against co']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll run batch that produced this pay stub. Enables grouping of all stubs processed in the same payroll cycle and supports reconciliation against the payroll run header.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker (candidate/employee) for whom this pay stub was generated. Links to the candidate or worker master record in the staffing system. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: W-2 workers on SOW engagements receive pay stubs. Links payroll record to SOW engagement for project cost tracking, client billing validation, and worker payment documentation.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Internal employees (recruiters, account managers, operations staff) are paid through the staffing firm\'s own payroll. Pay stubs for internal staff must link to staff_profile for payroll processing, ta']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: When workers are vendor-supplied (subcontractor model), pay stubs must track the originating vendor for cost allocation, vendor invoice validation, and dispute resolution. Accounts payable reconciliat']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.timesheet. Business justification: Pay stubs are generated directly from approved timesheets in staffing operations. This link enables traceability from time worked to payment issued, critical for payroll audit, dispute resolution, and']
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Pay stub generation requires validated wage/hour determinations to ensure compliant overtime calculations, salary basis tests, and prevailing wage compliance. Staffing payroll systems validate each pa']
  worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Pay stub processing depends on correct worker classification (W-2 vs 1099) to determine tax withholding obligations, FICA applicability, and reporting requirements. Classification drives payroll tax t']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Type of bank account designated for direct deposit disbursement. \'checking\' or \'savings\' as required by NACHA ACH transaction codes (PPD). Determines the ACH SEC code used in the payment file. Null for check and pay card disbursements.']
  ach_trace_number varchar [note: 'Unique 15-digit NACHA ACH trace number assigned to the direct deposit transaction for this pay stub. Null for check and pay card disbursements. Used for ACH payment tracing, return item resolution, and bank reconciliation.']
  bank_account_number_masked varchar [note: 'Worker\'s bank account number stored in masked format (e.g., \'****6789\') for display and audit purposes. Full account number is stored in a separate encrypted vault. Classified restricted/PII-financial. Null for check and pay card disbursements.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit ABA routing transit number identifying the worker\'s receiving financial institution for direct deposit. Classified restricted/PII-financial as it is a component of banking credentials. Null for check and pay card disbursements.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Sequential check number printed on the physical paycheck when pay_delivery_method is \'check\'. Null for direct deposit and pay card disbursements. Used for check reconciliation, stop-payment requests, and positive pay bank file matching.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pay stub record was first created in the payroll system. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for audit trails and payroll processing SLA measurement.']
  deposit_allocation_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount or percentage value of net pay allocated to this account in a split-deposit arrangement. Populated when deposit_allocation_type is \'fixed_amount\' or \'percentage\'. Null when deposit_allocation_type is \'full_net\'.']
  deposit_allocation_type varchar [note: 'Specifies how net pay is allocated to this bank account in a split-deposit configuration. \'full_net\' = entire net pay to one account; \'fixed_amount\' = a specific dollar amount; \'percentage\' = a defined percentage of net pay. Supports multi-account direct deposit configurations.']
  deposit_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence (1 = highest priority) determining the order in which net pay is allocated across multiple bank accounts in a split-deposit configuration. Ensures correct disbursement sequencing when a worker has designated multiple accounts.']
  dt_earnings decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of earnings for double-time hours (dt_hours × pay_rate × 2.0). Applicable in states with double-time mandates. Stored as a discrete earnings component for gross pay audit and state compliance reporting.']
  dt_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total double-time hours worked during the pay period, subject to the 2x pay multiplier. Applicable in states such as California with daily double-time thresholds (e.g., hours beyond 12 in a workday). Required for accurate gross pay calculation in multi-state payroll.']
  federal_income_tax_withheld decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of federal income tax withheld from the worker\'s wages for this pay period based on W-4 elections and IRS withholding tables. Required for W-2 reporting (Box 2) and federal tax deposit obligations.']
  futa_taxable_wages decimal(10,2) [note: 'Portion of the worker\'s gross wages subject to Federal Unemployment Tax Act (FUTA) employer tax for this pay period, up to the $7,000 annual wage base. Required for IRS Form 940 filing and employer burden rate reporting.']
  gross_pay decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total pre-deduction, pre-tax earnings for the pay period. Sum of regular_earnings, ot_earnings, dt_earnings, per_diem_amount, and any other pay components. Represents the gross-base amount in the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this transaction. Used as the basis for all tax withholding calculations.']
  medicare_tax_withheld decimal(10,2) [note: 'Employee share of Medicare (HI) tax withheld under FICA at the applicable rate (1.45%, plus 0.9% Additional Medicare Tax for high earners). Required for W-2 reporting (Box 6) and FICA deposit compliance.']
  net_pay decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final take-home amount disbursed to the worker after all deductions and tax withholdings (gross_pay − total_deductions − total_tax_withheld). Represents the net total in the MONETARY_TRIPLET. The amount actually paid via direct deposit, check, or pay card.']
  ot_earnings decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of earnings for overtime hours (ot_hours × pay_rate × 1.5). Stored separately from regular earnings to support FLSA compliance reporting, overtime cost analytics, and client billing reconciliation.']
  ot_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total overtime hours worked during the pay period, subject to the 1.5x pay multiplier under FLSA. Includes hours exceeding the standard weekly threshold or daily OT thresholds per applicable state law. Critical for gross pay calculation and FLSA compliance reporting.']
  pay_date date [note: 'The actual date on which payment was or is scheduled to be disbursed to the worker. Represents the principal business event date (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category). Used for cash flow reporting, tax period assignment, and worker payment scheduling.']
  pay_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which net pay is disbursed to the worker. \'direct_deposit\' = ACH electronic transfer to bank account; \'check\' = physical paper check; \'pay_card\' = prepaid debit card for unbanked workers. Determines downstream disbursement routing logic.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the worker is paid, determining the pay period cadence for this stub. Drives payroll scheduling, tax withholding annualization, and benefit deduction timing. Most staffing workers are paid weekly or biweekly.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the pay period covered by this stub. Defines the closing boundary of the earnings window. Used for accrual cutoffs, overtime determination, and tax period assignment.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the pay period covered by this stub. Combined with pay_period_end_date defines the exact earnings window. Required for FLSA overtime calculations and multi-state payroll compliance.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The worker\'s base hourly or unit pay rate applied during this pay period, expressed in USD. Represents the agreed compensation rate from the placement rate card. Used to calculate regular earnings and validate gross pay. Classified confidential as worker compensation data.']
  pay_rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pay rate basis for this stub. \'hourly\' = standard time-based; \'salary\' = fixed periodic amount; \'per_diem\' = daily allowance-based; \'piece_rate\' = unit-of-output based. Determines gross pay calculation method and FLSA overtime applicability.']
  per_diem_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Non-taxable per diem allowance paid to the worker for travel, meals, or lodging during the pay period. Sourced from approved per diem claims. Tracked separately from taxable wages for IRS accountable plan compliance and gross pay composition.']
  prenote_status varchar [note: 'Status of the NACHA prenote (zero-dollar test transaction) process for this bank account. \'pending\' = prenote initiated; \'sent\' = prenote transmitted to bank; \'verified\' = bank confirmed account valid; \'failed\' = account validation failed. Governs whether live ACH disbursement is permitted.']
  pto_accrual_balance decimal(8,2) [note: 'Worker\'s remaining PTO accrual balance as of this pay stub\'s pay period end date, expressed in hours. Snapshot value captured at time of pay run for worker statement display and leave liability reporting.']
  pto_hours_used decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of PTO hours applied to this pay stub for the pay period. Represents paid leave hours that contribute to gross pay but are not worked hours. Required for PTO accrual balance management and FLSA leave tracking.']
