  regular_earnings decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of earnings for regular straight-time hours (regular_hours × pay_rate). Core component of gross pay. Stored as a raw earnings component to support gross-to-net audit trail and payroll reconciliation.']
  regular_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total straight-time hours worked by the worker during the pay period, up to the overtime threshold (typically 40 hours per week under FLSA). Sourced from approved timesheet records in Kronos Workforce Ready. Used to calculate regular earnings.']
  social_security_tax_withheld decimal(10,2) [note: 'Employee share of Social Security (OASDI) tax withheld under FICA at the applicable rate (6.2% up to the annual wage base). Required for W-2 reporting (Box 4) and FICA tax deposit compliance.']
  state_income_tax_withheld decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of state income tax withheld for the primary work state during this pay period. Supports multi-state payroll compliance and state W-2 / equivalent reporting. May be zero for states with no income tax.']
  stub_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable identifier for this pay stub as printed on the worker\'s pay statement (e.g., \'PS-2024-00045231\'). Used for worker inquiries, audit trails, and payroll reconciliation. Satisfies BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  stub_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pay stub within the payroll processing workflow. \'draft\' = in calculation; \'calculated\' = gross-to-net complete pending approval; \'approved\' = authorized for disbursement; \'issued\' = payment released to worker; \'voided\' = cancelled before disbursement; \'reversed\' = post-issuance correction applied. Satisfies LIFECYCLE_STATUS requirement.']
  suta_taxable_wages decimal(10,2) [note: 'Portion of the worker\'s gross wages subject to State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) employer tax for this pay period, up to the applicable state wage base. Required for state unemployment tax filing and workforce cost burden rate calculation.']
  tax_filing_status varchar [note: 'Worker\'s federal income tax filing status as declared on IRS Form W-4, used to determine the correct withholding amount for this pay stub. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single|married_filing_jointly|married_filing_separately|head_of_household|exempt|qualifying_surviving_spouse — promote to reference product]']
  total_deductions decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate dollar amount of all pre-tax and post-tax deductions applied to this pay stub, including benefits premiums, garnishments, voluntary deductions, and advances. Represents the adjustment component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Supports gross-to-net reconciliation.']
  total_tax_withheld decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate dollar amount of all federal, state, and local taxes withheld from the worker\'s gross pay for this pay period. Includes FICA (Social Security and Medicare), federal income tax, and applicable state/local income taxes. Key component of gross-to-net calculation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this pay stub record. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Tracks corrections, reversals, and status transitions for audit compliance.']
  w2_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay stub is associated with a W-2 employee (True) versus a 1099 independent contractor (False). Drives tax withholding applicability, FICA obligations, and year-end tax form generation routing in TempWorks Payroll.']
  work_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation for the state in which the worker performed services during this pay period. Determines applicable state income tax withholding, SUTA rate, workers compensation jurisdiction, and state-specific wage and hour laws.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'NCCI or state-specific workers compensation classification code assigned to the worker for this pay period based on job duties and work location. Drives workers comp premium calculation and is required for policy compliance and audit.']
  workers_comp_premium decimal(10,2) [note: 'Employer-paid workers compensation insurance premium allocated to this pay stub for the pay period, calculated as (workers_comp_rate × payroll_basis). Represents a key component of the employer burden rate and total labor cost.']
  ytd_gross_pay decimal(14,2) [note: 'Cumulative gross pay earned by the worker from the start of the calendar year through this pay stub\'s pay period end date. Displayed on the worker\'s pay statement and used for W-2 wage box population, FICA wage base tracking, and annual compensation reporting.']
  ytd_net_pay decimal(14,2) [note: 'Cumulative net pay disbursed to the worker from the start of the calendar year through this pay stub\'s pay period end date. Displayed on the worker\'s pay statement for annual earnings visibility and financial planning.']
  ytd_tax_withheld decimal(14,2) [note: 'Cumulative total of all taxes withheld from the worker\'s pay from the start of the calendar year through this pay stub\'s pay period end date. Displayed on the pay statement and used for W-2 Box 2/4/6 population and tax deposit reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record representing the individual worker pay statement generated for each pay run, including full pay disbursement details. Captures worker identifier, pay run reference, gross pay, regular hours, overtime hours, double-time hours, per diem amounts, gross-to-net calculation details, total deductions, total tax withholdings, net pay, and stub status. Disbursement attributes: pay delivery method (direct deposit, check, pay card), check number or ACH trace number, bank routing number, account number (masked), account type (checking, savings), deposit allocation type (full net, fixed amount, percentage split), allocation amounts for split-deposit configurations, deposit priority for multiple accounts, prenote status, account verification status, and pay card routing for unbanked workers. SSOT for individual worker earnings per pay period, associated payment disbursement instructions, and banking configuration — consolidates all direct deposit and ACH details formerly tracked separately.'
}

Table payroll.pay_rate {
  pay_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the pay rate record. Primary key for the pay_rate data product in the payroll domain.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker assignment this pay rate applies to. Supports multi-rate scenarios where a worker may have different rates across concurrent assignments.']
  burden_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.burden_rate. Business justification: Pay rate structures should reference the master burden rate definition for accurate cost modeling and margin calculation. pay_rate currently has burden_rate_pct: DECIMAL(7,4) which is a denormalized v']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for which the worker is placed. Used to validate rate alignment with client rate cards and MSA terms.']
  client_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.rate_card. Business justification: Pay rates must align with client-approved rate cards for margin calculation, compliance verification, and invoice reconciliation. Core relationship in staffing operations ensuring pay rates stay withi']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Pay rates are governed by MSA-negotiated rate schedules; critical for billing compliance, margin calculation, and audit trail. Staffing operations require tracing worker pay rates to governing client ']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Pay rates must align with contracted rate cards to ensure margin integrity and prevent billing disputes. Staffing operations require direct linkage for rate validation, markup verification, and client']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pay rates and bill rates drive cost center profitability analysis (bill rate vs. pay rate + burden). Required for branch/division margin reporting and pricing decisions in staffing operations.']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: Rate cards are tied to credential packages; different pay rates for workers with advanced certifications or security clearances. Healthcare staffing commonly pays premium rates for specialty certifica']
  credential_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_requirement. Business justification: Rate approval workflows validate that credential requirements are satisfied before activating pay rates; prevents paying workers who lack mandatory client-required credentials. Common in healthcare/IT']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire placements have negotiated salary rates that drive payroll processing. Links placement deal terms to payroll rate structure for accurate salary administration and cost tracking.']
  labor_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.labor_category. Business justification: Staffing firms structure pay rates by labor category/skill level for rate card management and client billing. This link supports rate card configuration, prevailing wage compliance, and ensures pay ra']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Pay rates frequently vary by work location due to metro differentials, cost-of-living adjustments, and site-specific client agreements. Essential for accurate rate management and pricing by location i']
  onboarding_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to onboarding.onboarding_engagement. Business justification: Pay rates are established during onboarding process. Tracking which onboarding engagement set the rate supports audit trails, rate dispute resolution, and compliance verification that rates were prope']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) associated with this pay rate. Enables rate traceability back to the originating client requisition.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker/candidate whose compensation this pay rate governs. Links pay rate to the individual worker record.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOWs define engagement-specific terms including pay rate structures and worker classifications. Staffing firms must link pay rates to SOWs for scope compliance, change order management, and SOW-level ']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Staffing agencies use subcontractor vendors to fulfill placements when internal candidate pools are insufficient. Pay rates must track which vendor supplied the worker for vendor invoice reconciliatio']
  vms_rate_id varchar [note: 'External rate identifier assigned by the Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, when the rate is VMS-driven. Used for reconciliation between TempWorks Payroll and Beeline VMS rate management. Populated only when rate_source = \'vms_driven\'.']
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Pay rates require validated wage/hour determinations for FLSA exemption status, prevailing wage compliance, and minimum wage validation. Staffing firms must document compliant rate approval before ass']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the pay rate record. Pending = awaiting manager or compliance approval; approved = active and payroll-eligible; rejected = denied; expired = past effective end date. Controls whether the rate can be used in payroll processing.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager, recruiter, or compliance officer who approved this pay rate. Provides audit trail for rate authorization in compliance with internal controls and OFCCP requirements.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pay rate was formally approved. Part of the rate lifecycle audit trail. Required for SOC 2 Type II compliance and internal payroll controls.']
  bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The rate charged to the client for each unit of the worker\'s time (typically per hour). Stored alongside the pay rate to enable spread and gross margin calculation. The spread (bill_rate minus regular_pay_rate) is the primary revenue metric in staffing. Sourced from the client rate card or VMS.']
  conversion_fee_waiver_date date [note: 'Date after which the client\'s conversion fee obligation is waived if the worker is converted to a permanent employee. Typically tied to a minimum number of hours worked or weeks on assignment per the MSA terms. Null if no conversion fee waiver applies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pay rate record was first created in TempWorks Payroll. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for SOC 2 Type II compliance and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary rate fields are denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency payroll for international staffing operations.']
  dt_rate_multiplier decimal(5,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the regular pay rate to calculate the double-time (DT) pay rate. Typically 2.0x, applicable in states such as California for hours over 12 in a day or on the 7th consecutive day. Applied by TempWorks Payroll for DT hours.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this pay rate is no longer active. Nullable for open-ended rates. When populated, payroll processing after this date must use a successor rate record. Supports rate versioning and assignment extensions.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this pay rate becomes active and applicable to payroll processing. Defines the beginning of the rate\'s binding period. Payroll transactions on or after this date use this rate.']
  fica_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is exempt from FICA (Social Security and Medicare) withholding for this assignment. True for certain visa holders (e.g., F-1, J-1 students) or specific worker classifications. Drives TempWorks Payroll tax withholding logic.']
  flsa_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is classified as exempt from FLSA overtime requirements (True = exempt salaried; False = non-exempt hourly). Determines whether OT and DT multipliers apply. Critical for wage and hour compliance and DOL audit defense.']
  is_blended_rate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay rate is a blended rate covering multiple job duties or skill levels within a single assignment. True = blended rate applies; False = single-role rate. Common in SOW engagements and multi-skill temporary placements.']
  markup_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage markup applied over the regular pay rate to arrive at the bill rate. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.45 = 45% markup). Used for pricing analysis, margin reporting, and VMS rate negotiations. Markup = (bill_rate - regular_pay_rate) / regular_pay_rate.']
  max_straight_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum number of hours per week or pay period at the regular pay rate before overtime (OT) rates apply. Typically 40 hours per week under FLSA, but may be lower per client contract or state law (e.g., California daily OT threshold). Drives OT calculation trigger in TempWorks Payroll.']
  min_guaranteed_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum number of hours per pay period the worker is guaranteed to be paid, regardless of actual hours worked. Common in certain staffing contracts and SOW engagements. Impacts gross pay floor calculation in TempWorks Payroll.']
  ot_rate_multiplier decimal(5,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the regular pay rate to calculate the overtime (OT) pay rate. Standard FLSA minimum is 1.5x for hours over 40 per week. May be higher per client contract or state law. Applied by TempWorks Payroll for OT hours on timesheets.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the worker is paid. Determines payroll cycle alignment in TempWorks Payroll. Weekly is most common for temporary staffing; bi-weekly and semi-monthly for salaried placements.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Internal compensation band or grade classification assigned to this pay rate (e.g., G1, G2, Band A). Used for workforce analytics, compensation equity analysis, and EEOC/OFCCP reporting on pay equity across worker populations.']
  per_diem_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Daily non-taxable allowance paid to the worker for meals, lodging, and incidentals when working away from their tax home. Expressed in currency per day. Must comply with IRS per diem guidelines to maintain non-taxable status. Tracked separately from regular pay for tax reporting.']
  pto_accrual_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker accrues Paid Time Off (PTO) under this pay rate configuration. Eligibility may depend on assignment duration, worker classification, or client program rules. Drives PTO accrual processing in TempWorks Payroll.']
  pto_accrual_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Rate at which PTO hours accrue per hour worked or per pay period, expressed in hours. For example, 0.0385 hours of PTO per hour worked equates to approximately 1 week of PTO per year. Populated only when pto_accrual_eligible = True.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this pay rate configuration within TempWorks Payroll and Beeline VMS. Used for rate card lookups and VMS-driven rate matching.']
  rate_negotiation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the rationale, context, or special conditions behind the negotiated pay rate. May include references to candidate counter-offers, market data justifications, or client-specific rate exceptions. Supports recruiter and account manager context for future rate reviews.']
  rate_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the pay rate determination. Negotiated = individually agreed with worker; vms_driven = rate set by Vendor Management System (VMS) such as Beeline; standard = internal standard rate table; client_rate_card = derived from client-specific rate card agreement.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the worker is compensated. Hourly = per hour worked; salary = fixed periodic amount; piece_rate = per unit produced; daily = per day worked. Drives gross pay calculation logic in TempWorks Payroll.']
  regular_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The base pay rate for the worker expressed in the applicable rate_type unit (e.g., dollars per hour for hourly workers, dollars per day for daily workers). This is the principal quantitative compensation fact. Used as the basis for gross pay calculation in TempWorks Payroll.']
  shift_differential_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Additional pay rate amount added to the regular pay rate for workers assigned to non-standard shifts (e.g., evening, overnight, weekend). Expressed as a flat dollar amount per hour or as a percentage add-on depending on the shift_differential_type field.']
  shift_differential_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the shift_differential_rate is a flat dollar amount added per hour or a percentage of the regular pay rate. Determines how TempWorks Payroll calculates the effective shift pay.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this pay rate record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and conflict resolution in the Databricks Silver Layer when rates are ingested from multiple upstream systems.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code where the worker performs services under this pay rate. Determines applicable SUTA rate, state income tax withholding rules, state-specific overtime laws, and workers comp jurisdiction. Critical for multi-state payroll compliance.']
  suta_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code designating the state to which SUTA (State Unemployment Tax Act) taxes are remitted for this worker. May differ from work state_code in multi-state scenarios or when the worker\'s base state differs from the work location state.']
  temp_to_perm_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker under this pay rate is eligible for conversion from temporary to permanent (temp-to-perm or contract-to-hire) placement. Affects conversion fee applicability and rate renegotiation triggers upon conversion.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pay rate record was last modified. Tracks rate amendments, approval status changes, and effective date adjustments. Required for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'Tax and employment classification of the worker for this pay rate. W-2 = employee on company payroll; 1099 = independent contractor (IC); corp_to_corp = contractor billing through their own entity; eor = Employer of Record arrangement. Drives tax withholding, FICA applicability, and payroll processing rules in TempWorks Payroll.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'NCCI or state-specific workers compensation classification code associated with the job duties for this pay rate. Determines the workers comp insurance premium rate applied to the worker\'s wages. Critical for accurate burden rate calculation and insurance compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the pay rate structure for a worker on a specific assignment or job order. Captures regular pay rate, overtime (OT) rate multiplier, double-time (DT) rate multiplier, per diem rate, shift differential rate, effective start and end dates, rate type (hourly, salary, piece-rate, daily), pay frequency (weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly), currency, rate source (negotiated, VMS-driven, standard), and approval status. Supports multi-rate scenarios for workers on multiple assignments.'
}

Table payroll.earnings_line {
  earnings_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual earnings line item within a worker pay stub. Primary key for the earnings_line product in the payroll domain. TRANSACTION_LINE role — each record represents one earnings component (e.g., regular pay, OT, per diem) within a parent pay stub header.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement assignment under which this earnings line was generated. Links the pay line to the specific client engagement, job order, and work location context in Bullhorn ATS/CRM. Enables earnings analysis by assignment, client, and job order.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Individual earnings must trace to contracted rate schedules for audit compliance and dispute resolution. Staffing operations require line-level validation that billed rates match contracted terms, esp']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Earnings lines are the atomic unit of cost allocation to cost centers for GL posting and profitability analysis. cost_center_code exists as denormalized attribute; FK enforces referential integrity fo']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Time-to-pay processing validates credential status at earning line level; blocks payment for specific shifts worked without valid credentials (e.g., expired nursing license). Granular compliance enfor']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire placements generate earnings (salary, bonuses, adjustments). Links payroll earnings to permanent placement for fee reconciliation, guarantee period tracking, and revenue recognition valida']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Earnings lines post to specific GL accounts (wages expense, contract labor, per diem) for financial statement preparation. gl_account_code exists as denormalized; FK enforces chart of accounts integri']
  onboarding_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to onboarding.onboarding_engagement. Business justification: Individual earnings require traceability to the onboarding engagement that authorized the work arrangement. Essential for compliance audits verifying workers were properly onboarded before earning wag']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.order_header. Business justification: Individual earnings lines require job order context for client billing detail, markup calculation per order terms, and granular job profitability analysis. Core to staffing agency revenue and cost tra']
  original_line_earnings_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original earnings_line_id that this adjustment or correction line is reversing or modifying. Populated only when is_adjustment is True. Enables full audit trail of payroll corrections and supports prior-period adjustment reporting. Null for original (non-adjustment) earnings lines.']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payroll processing run that generated this earnings line. Links to the payroll batch/run record in TempWorks Payroll. Used for payroll audit trails, reprocessing identification, and reconciliation of payroll runs against approved timesheets and pay stub totals.']
  pay_stub_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent pay stub (TRANSACTION_HEADER) that this earnings line belongs to. Satisfies HEADER_REFERENCE minimum category for TRANSACTION_LINE role. Links the line item to the worker\'s gross-to-net pay stub record in TempWorks Payroll.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the worker (temporary associate, contractor, or permanent employee) whose earnings this line represents. Satisfies PARTY_REFERENCE minimum category. Links to the candidate/worker master record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM and SAP SuccessFactors.']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: SOW engagements generate earnings when worker is W-2 employee. Milestone or hourly SOW work creates payroll earnings that must link to engagement for billing reconciliation and cost tracking.']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved timesheet record from which this earnings line was derived. Links payroll earnings back to the source time and attendance data captured in Kronos Workforce Ready. Null for non-hour-based earnings such as bonuses or expense reimbursements.']
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Each earnings line must comply with overtime rules, prevailing wage requirements, and FLSA classifications. Payroll systems validate earnings against wage/hour determinations to ensure compliant OT ca']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific client work location where the worker performed services during this pay period. Used for workers compensation premium allocation by location, multi-state tax nexus determination, and client billing reconciliation. Links to the placement work location record.']
  check_date date [note: 'The date on which the worker\'s paycheck or direct deposit is issued. Represents the principal business event date for this earnings line. Used for tax period determination (W-2 year allocation), cash flow reporting, and worker payment confirmation. Satisfies BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP minimum category.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this earnings line record was first created in the payroll system. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED minimum category. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and SOC 2 Type II compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary amounts on this earnings line. Defaults to USD for domestic US staffing operations. Relevant for international EOR (Employer of Record) arrangements and cross-border staffing engagements. Supports multi-currency payroll processing for global staffing operations.']
  earning_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code identifying the type of earning component (e.g., REG for regular pay, OT for overtime, DT for double time, HOL for holiday, PTO for paid time off, BONUS, COMM for commission, PERDIEM, EXPREIM for expense reimbursement, SHFTDIFF for shift differential). Satisfies RESOURCE_REFERENCE minimum category as the classification key for the earning type resource. Drives tax treatment, GL account mapping, and compliance reporting.']
  earning_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the earning component as it appears on the worker\'s pay stub. Examples: \'Regular Hours\', \'Overtime Pay\', \'Holiday Pay - Labor Day\', \'PTO Payout\', \'Weekly Per Diem - Chicago Assignment\'. Provides transparency to the worker and supports payroll audit trails.']
  earning_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the earning component. Drives gross pay decomposition, tax withholding logic, and compliance reporting. Regular = straight-time wages; OT (Overtime) = FLSA-mandated 1.5x pay; DT (Double Time) = 2x pay per CBA or client agreement; PTO (Paid Time Off) = accrued leave payout; Per Diem = non-taxable daily allowance; Expense Reimbursement = accountable plan reimbursement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular|overtime|double_time|holiday|pto|bonus|commission|per_diem|expense_reimbursement|shift_differential|other — promote to reference product]']
  flsa_exemption_status varchar [note: 'FLSA overtime exemption classification for the worker on this earnings line. Non-exempt workers are entitled to OT pay at 1.5x for hours over 40/week. Exempt classifications (executive, administrative, professional, highly compensated) are not entitled to FLSA overtime. Drives overtime eligibility and compliance with DOL wage and hour regulations.']
  gross_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Computed gross dollar amount for this earnings line, calculated as hours_quantity multiplied by pay_rate, or as a flat amount for non-hourly earnings. Represents the worker\'s pre-tax, pre-deduction compensation for this specific earning component. Satisfies LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT minimum category for TRANSACTION_LINE role. Feeds into gross pay totals on the pay stub.']
  hours_quantity decimal(10,4) [note: 'Number of hours or units associated with this earnings line. For hour-based earnings (regular, OT, DT, holiday, PTO), this is the number of hours worked or paid. For unit-based earnings (piece rate, per diem days), this represents the unit count. Null for flat-amount earnings such as bonuses or expense reimbursements. Satisfies LINE_QUANTITY minimum category for TRANSACTION_LINE role.']
  is_adjustment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this earnings line is a payroll adjustment or correction entry rather than an original earnings line. True for retroactive pay corrections, prior-period adjustments, and manual override entries. Adjustment lines may carry negative gross_amount values to reverse prior earnings. Used for payroll audit and SOX compliance.']
  is_fica_subject boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this earning component is subject to FICA (Federal Insurance Contributions Act) taxes, comprising Social Security (6.2%) and Medicare (1.45%) withholding. False for per diem reimbursements, expense reimbursements under accountable plans, and certain non-cash fringe benefits. Critical for accurate payroll tax calculation and IRS Form 941 filing.']
  is_overtime boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this earnings line represents overtime pay as defined under FLSA (Fair Labor Standards Act) — hours worked beyond 40 in a workweek compensated at a minimum of 1.5x the regular rate. Used for FLSA compliance reporting, overtime cost analytics, and client billing reconciliation where OT bill rates differ from straight-time rates.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this earning component is subject to federal and state income tax withholding. True for wages, OT, DT, holiday pay, bonuses, and commissions. False for qualifying per diem allowances within IRS GSA limits, accountable plan expense reimbursements, and certain fringe benefits. Drives FICA, FUTA, SUTA, and income tax withholding calculations.']
  labor_category_code varchar [note: 'Classification code identifying the labor category or job classification for this earnings line (e.g., LIGHT_IND for light industrial, IT_PROF for IT professional, CLERICAL, HEALTHCARE). Used for workforce cost analytics, bill rate reconciliation, and compliance with prevailing wage determinations under DOL regulations. Aligns with timesheet.labor_category.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential line number ordering this earnings component within the parent pay stub. Satisfies LINE_SEQUENCE minimum category for TRANSACTION_LINE role. Used for display ordering on pay stubs and for deterministic processing order during gross pay calculation.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of this earnings line within the payroll workflow. Pending = awaiting payroll approval; Approved = authorized for payment; Processed = included in a completed payroll run; Voided = cancelled/reversed; Adjusted = corrected via an adjustment entry. Satisfies LIFECYCLE_STATUS minimum category for TRANSACTION_LINE role.']
  ot_multiplier decimal(5,4) [note: 'The pay rate multiplier applied to the base pay rate for premium pay earnings. Standard values: 1.0000 for regular pay, 1.5000 for OT (overtime), 2.0000 for DT (double time). May reflect CBA (collective bargaining agreement) or client-specific premium rates. Used to validate gross_amount calculation and support FLSA compliance audits.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the worker is paid, determining the pay period structure for this earnings line. Weekly is most common for temporary staffing workers. Biweekly and semimonthly are common for permanent placements. Drives payroll processing schedule in TempWorks Payroll and tax withholding table selection.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the pay period to which this earnings line applies. Defines the closing boundary of the work period being compensated. Used in conjunction with pay_period_start_date for FLSA overtime workweek calculations and state wage statement compliance.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the pay period to which this earnings line applies. Combined with pay_period_end_date, defines the work period being compensated. Critical for multi-state payroll compliance, FLSA overtime calculation, and tax period allocation.']
  pay_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'The hourly, daily, or unit rate applied to this earnings line to compute the gross amount. Represents the worker\'s agreed compensation rate for this earning type (e.g., straight-time rate, OT rate at 1.5x, DT rate at 2x). Sourced from the placement rate agreement in Bullhorn ATS/CRM. Confidential as it contains worker compensation data.']
  per_diem_rate_daily decimal(10,2) [note: 'The daily per diem rate applied for this earnings line when earning_type is per_diem. Compared against GSA (General Services Administration) published rates to determine taxability — amounts within GSA limits are non-taxable; excess amounts are taxable wages. Null for non-per-diem earning types.']
  per_diem_type varchar [note: 'Classification of per diem allowance type when earning_type is per_diem. Lodging = accommodation allowance; Meals and Incidentals (M&IE) = daily meal and incidental expense allowance; Combined = single daily rate covering both; None = not a per diem line. GSA per diem rates determine taxability thresholds. Null or \'none\' for non-per-diem earning types.']
  prevailing_wage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this earnings line is subject to prevailing wage requirements under the Davis-Bacon Act, Service Contract Act, or state prevailing wage laws. True for government contract assignments requiring certified payroll reporting. Triggers additional compliance reporting obligations to DOL and OFCCP.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the work shift associated with this earnings line (e.g., DAY, EVE for evening, NIGHT, WKND for weekend). Used to apply shift differential pay rates and to reconcile earnings with Kronos Workforce Ready scheduling data. Relevant for shift_differential earning types.']
  source_system_ref varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating payroll system (TempWorks Payroll) for this earnings line. Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record. Used for audit trails, data lineage, and dispute resolution.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The calendar tax year (YYYY) to which this earnings line is attributed for W-2 or 1099 reporting purposes. Determined by the check_date. Critical for year-end tax form preparation, IRS reporting, and multi-year payroll analytics. Enables accurate W-2 box 1 (wages) and 1099-NEC box 1 (nonemployee compensation) aggregation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this earnings line record was most recently modified. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED minimum category. Tracks payroll corrections, status changes, and retroactive adjustments. Used for incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  work_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code where the work was performed for this earnings line. Drives state income tax withholding, SUTA (State Unemployment Tax Act) employer contributions, state-specific overtime rules, and workers compensation premium allocation. Critical for multi-state payroll compliance in staffing operations where workers may be deployed across multiple states.']
  worker_type varchar [note: 'Tax and employment classification of the worker for this earnings line. W-2 Temp = temporary employee on agency payroll; W-2 Perm = permanent placed employee; 1099 IC (Independent Contractor) = self-employed contractor; EOR (Employer of Record) = worker employed through EOR arrangement. Determines tax withholding obligations, FICA applicability, FUTA/SUTA liability, and W-2 vs 1099 year-end tax form issuance.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'NCCI (National Council on Compensation Insurance) workers compensation class code applicable to this earnings line. Determines the workers comp insurance premium rate applied to the gross wages. Critical for accurate workers comp cost allocation, experience modification factor calculations, and compliance with state workers compensation insurance requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional line-item record capturing each individual earnings component within a worker pay stub. Covers regular pay, OT pay, DT pay, holiday pay, PTO payout, bonus, commission, per diem, expense reimbursement, shift differential, and other earning types. Stores hours or units, rate applied, earning code, earning description, taxability flag, and computed gross amount. Enables granular gross pay decomposition for reporting and compliance.'
}

Table payroll.tax_line {
  tax_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for tax_line']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Tax withholdings and employer taxes post to specific GL liability and expense accounts (FICA payable, FUTA expense, SUTA expense). Required for accurate balance sheet, P&L, and tax liability reconcili']
  original_tax_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original tax line record that this adjustment line corrects or reverses. Null for original (non-adjustment) lines. Enables audit trail linking between original and corrected tax line records.']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll processing run (batch) in which this tax line was computed. Supports reconciliation of all tax lines within a single payroll cycle.']
  pay_stub_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent pay stub (paycheck) that this tax line belongs to. Establishes the header/detail relationship between the pay stub and its individual tax line items. TRANSACTION_LINE HEADER_REFERENCE category.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker (W-2 employee or 1099 independent contractor) for whom this tax line is computed. TRANSACTION_LINE PARTY_REFERENCE category.']
  tax_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.tax_filing. Business justification: Tax line records (individual tax obligations per pay stub) feed into aggregate tax filings (941, state quarterly filings). Adding FK tax_filing_id → tax_filing.payroll_tax_filing_id links each tax_lin']
  tax_withholding_election_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.tax_withholding_election. Business justification: Tax withholding execution (tax_line) should reference the worker\'s tax withholding election (W-4 form data) that drove the calculation. tax_line currently has denormalized attributes filing_status, al']
  add_medicare_surtax_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tax line represents the Additional Medicare Tax (0.9% surtax) applicable to high-earning employees whose wages exceed the IRS threshold ($200,000 for single filers). Employer does not match this surtax.']
  adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tax line is an adjustment or correction to a previously processed tax line (e.g., retroactive rate change, prior-period correction, amended return adjustment). When True, adjustment_reason should be populated.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for a tax line adjustment when adjustment_flag is True (e.g., \'Retroactive SUTA rate change Q1 2024\', \'W-4 correction applied\', \'Prior period underpayment correction\').']
  check_date date [note: 'The date on which the paycheck or direct deposit was issued to the worker. Determines the tax deposit liability date and the calendar year to which the tax obligation is attributed for W-2 reporting purposes.']
  computed_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The calculated tax dollar amount for this line in the current pay period (taxable_wage_base × tax_rate, or flat amount for flat-rate taxes). Core value used in gross-to-net calculation and payroll tax remittance. TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT category.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax line record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 Type II compliance requirements. TRANSACTION_LINE RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  deposit_schedule varchar [note: 'The employer\'s IRS-assigned federal tax deposit schedule for this tax type (monthly, semi-weekly, or next-day for large deposits). Determines the tax_deposit_liability_date. State deposit schedules may differ.']
  futa_credit_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The FUTA state tax credit rate (up to 5.4%) applied to reduce the gross FUTA rate of 6.0% when the employer has paid SUTA taxes in a credit-reduction state. Applies only to FUTA tax type lines.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering number of this tax line within the parent pay stub. Enables deterministic ordering and display of all tax line items on a pay stub. TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE category.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the pay period to which this tax line applies. Together with pay_period_start_date, defines the exact payroll period for tax obligation accrual and filing.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the pay period to which this tax line applies. Used for period-over-period tax reporting, quarterly filing (Form 941/940), and annual W-2 reconciliation.']
  remittance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the tax remittance for this line to the applicable tax authority. Tracks whether the tax has been deposited on time, is pending, was remitted late (triggering penalty exposure), has been adjusted, or voided. TRANSACTION_LINE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from TempWorks Payroll (the system of record) for this tax line. Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational payroll system.']
  suta_experience_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The employer\'s SUTA experience-rated tax rate for the applicable state, as assigned by the state unemployment agency based on the company\'s claims history. Applies only to SUTA tax type lines. Drives burden rate calculations.']
  suta_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code identifying the state to which SUTA taxes are remitted for this worker. May differ from the worker\'s work state in multi-state assignments. Applies only to SUTA tax type lines.']
  tax_authority_level varchar [note: 'Level of government authority imposing this tax obligation. Supports multi-state payroll compliance and jurisdiction-level tax filing and reporting.']
  tax_deposit_liability_date date [note: 'The date by which the employer must remit this tax to the applicable tax authority (IRS, state, or local). Determined by the employer\'s deposit schedule (monthly or semi-weekly for federal; varies by state). Critical for avoiding late deposit penalties.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is exempt from this specific tax type (e.g., FICA exemption for certain visa holders, FUTA exemption for specific worker categories). When True, computed_amount is zero and the exemption basis should be documented.']
  tax_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or basis for the tax exemption when tax_exempt_flag is True (e.g., \'F-1 Visa FICA Exemption\', \'W-4 Exempt Election\', \'Agricultural Worker FUTA Exemption\'). Required for compliance audit trail.']
  tax_jurisdiction_fips varchar [note: 'Five-digit FIPS code uniquely identifying the county or county-equivalent jurisdiction for this tax line. Enables precise multi-state and local tax jurisdiction mapping and filing.']
  tax_jurisdiction_locality varchar [note: 'Name of the local taxing jurisdiction (city, county, municipality, school district) for local/city tax lines. Null for federal and state-level taxes. Supports local tax filing compliance.']
  tax_jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state code (USPS abbreviation) for the state in which this tax obligation applies. Null for federal-only taxes. Critical for multi-state payroll tax filing and SUTA rate management.']
  tax_quarter varchar [note: 'Calendar quarter (1–4) in which this tax line falls, derived from the check date. Used for quarterly payroll tax filing (IRS Form 941, FUTA Form 940, and state equivalents) and quarterly reconciliation.']
  tax_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The effective tax rate (as a decimal fraction, e.g., 0.062000 for 6.2%) applied to the taxable wage base to compute the tax amount for this line. For flat-dollar taxes, this may be zero with the flat amount captured in computed_amount.']
  tax_side varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether this tax line represents an employee-side withholding (deducted from worker gross pay) or an employer-side tax obligation (borne by the staffing company). Critical for gross-to-net calculation and burden rate validation. TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT category (discriminator).']
  tax_type_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific type of tax obligation on this line (e.g., FIT, SIT, FICA_SS_EE, FICA_MED_EE, FICA_SS_ER, FICA_MED_ER, FUTA, SUTA, LOCAL, ADD_MED, SUPP). Used for tax filing, reporting, and reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FIT|SIT|LOCAL|FICA_SS_EE|FICA_MED_EE|FICA_SS_ER|FICA_MED_ER|FUTA|SUTA|ADD_MED|SUPP — promote to reference product]']
  tax_type_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the tax type (e.g., \'Federal Income Tax\', \'California State Income Tax\', \'FICA Social Security Employee\', \'Federal Unemployment Tax\'). Displayed on pay stubs and tax reports.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year (e.g., 2024) to which this tax line is attributed for annual tax reporting, W-2/1099 issuance, and year-end reconciliation.']
  taxable_wage_base decimal(18,4) [note: 'The portion of the worker\'s gross wages subject to this specific tax in the current pay period, after applying any applicable wage base caps (e.g., Social Security wage base, FUTA/SUTA wage base limits). TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_QUANTITY category.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax line record was last modified. Tracks adjustments, corrections, and status changes for audit trail and data lineage purposes. TRANSACTION_LINE RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  wage_base_annual_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'The annual taxable wage base ceiling for this tax type (e.g., $168,600 for Social Security in 2024; $7,000 for FUTA). Once the worker\'s YTD wages exceed this limit, no further tax is assessed for this tax type in the calendar year.']
  wage_base_cap_reached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker has reached the annual taxable wage base ceiling for this tax type in the current calendar year. When True, no further tax is assessed for this tax type until the next calendar year reset.']
  withholding_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the tax withholding for this line: percentage of taxable wages, flat dollar amount, IRS tax table lookup, supplemental flat rate, or exempt (no withholding). Drives the calculation logic applied in TempWorks Payroll.']
  worker_tax_form_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is classified as a W-2 employee (subject to withholding) or a 1099 independent contractor (IC) for this tax line. Drives which tax obligations apply and which annual tax form is issued. Aligns with IRS worker classification rules.']
  ytd_tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative tax amount withheld or assessed for this worker under this tax type from the start of the calendar year through and including this pay period. Critical for quarterly (941/940) and annual (W-2/1099) tax reconciliation and filing.']
  ytd_taxable_wages decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative taxable wages for this worker under this tax type from the start of the calendar year through and including this pay period. Used to enforce annual wage base limits and for W-2/1099 annual reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing all payroll tax obligations — both employee withholdings and employer-side taxes — per worker per pay stub within a pay run. Employee side: federal income tax, state income tax, local/city tax, FICA Social Security and Medicare employee shares, additional Medicare surtax, and supplemental withholdings. Employer side: FICA Social Security and Medicare employer match, FUTA, SUTA by state, and local employer taxes. Stores tax side discriminator (employee/employer), tax authority jurisdiction, tax type code, taxable wage base, tax rate applied, withholding method (percentage, flat, table-based), computed amount, YTD taxable wages, YTD amount, and multi-state jurisdiction details. SSOT for ALL payroll tax line items — both employee withholdings and employer taxes are unified in this single product with a tax_side discriminator. Critical for gross-to-net calculation, burden rate validation, multi-state payroll tax filing, and quarterly/annual tax reconciliation.'
}

Table payroll.deduction_line {
  deduction_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each deduction line item on a worker pay stub. Primary key for the deduction_line product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE — represents one deduction item within a payroll check/pay stub header.']
  benefit_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the benefit enrollment record that drives this deduction for benefit-type deductions (medical, dental, vision, 401k, FSA, HSA, life, supplemental). Null for non-benefit deductions such as garnishments or voluntary deductions not tied to a benefit plan.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Employer contributions (benefits, 401k match) are cost center expenses impacting departmental burden rates and profitability. Required for accurate cost center P&L in staffing operations with multi-lo']
  garnishment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing court or agency garnishment order for garnishment-type deductions (child support, tax levy, creditor garnishment). Null for non-garnishment deductions.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Deductions post to liability accounts (401k payable, health insurance payable) and expense accounts (employer match). gl_account_code exists as denormalized; FK required for accurate balance sheet and']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll processing run batch in which this deduction line was calculated and applied. Enables traceability of deductions back to a specific payroll cycle execution.']
  pay_stub_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent payroll check (pay stub) to which this deduction line belongs. TRANSACTION_LINE role: HEADER_REFERENCE — links this line to its parent payroll transaction header.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker (employee, temporary associate, or contractor) whose pay is being reduced by this deduction. Supports multi-worker payroll processing across W-2 and 1099 classifications.']
  annual_limit_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount that may be withheld for this deduction type in a calendar year, as set by IRS regulations or plan rules (e.g., IRS 401k elective deferral limit, FSA annual contribution cap, HSA annual contribution limit). Used to automatically stop withholding once the limit is reached. Null for deductions with no annual cap.']
  arrears_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Dollar amount of deduction that was missed in a prior pay period and is being carried forward for collection in a subsequent period. Common for garnishments when disposable earnings were insufficient to cover the full order amount, or for benefit premiums missed during a leave of absence. Zero if no arrears exist.']
  benefit_carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance carrier or financial institution administering the benefit plan associated with this deduction (e.g., \'Aetna\', \'MetLife\', \'Fidelity Investments\', \'WageWorks\'). Used for carrier remittance reconciliation and benefits vendor management. Null for non-benefit deductions.']
  benefit_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current enrollment status of the worker in the benefit plan driving this deduction. Determines whether premium withholding should be active. COBRA status indicates the worker is continuing coverage under COBRA after employment separation. Null for non-benefit deductions.']
  benefit_plan_name varchar [note: 'Name of the employer-sponsored benefit plan associated with this deduction (e.g., \'Aetna PPO Gold\', \'Delta Dental Premier\', \'VSP Vision Basic\', \'Fidelity 401k Plan\'). Populated only for benefit-type deductions. Sourced from the benefit enrollment record and used for benefits administration reporting and carrier remittance.']
  benefit_plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the benefit plan type driving this deduction. Used to categorize benefit deductions for ACA reporting, W-2 Box 12 coding, and benefits analytics. Null for non-benefit deductions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical|dental|vision|401k|roth_401k|fsa|hsa|life|supplemental — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  check_date date [note: 'The date on which the paycheck containing this deduction was issued to the worker. Represents the actual payment date used for tax deposit timing, W-2 year assignment, and garnishment remittance deadlines. TRANSACTION_LINE role: BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision).']
  court_order_number varchar [note: 'Official court case number or agency order reference for garnishment-type deductions (child support, tax levy, creditor garnishment). Required for legal compliance and remittance to the issuing court or agency. Null for non-garnishment deductions.']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'The coverage tier elected by the worker for this benefit plan, which determines the premium amount withheld. Applicable to medical, dental, and vision benefit deductions. Values: employee_only (single coverage), employee_spouse (worker plus spouse/domestic partner), employee_children (worker plus dependents), family (worker plus all dependents). Null for non-benefit deductions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this deduction line record was first created in the source payroll system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the deduction amount (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency payroll processing for international staffing operations and EOR (Employer of Record) arrangements.']
  deduction_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Dollar amount withheld from the worker\'s gross pay for this deduction line in the current pay period. For benefit deductions, represents the employee premium contribution. For garnishments, represents the court-ordered withholding amount. TRANSACTION_LINE role: LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT — the principal monetary fact for this line.']
  deduction_category varchar [note: 'Granular business category of the deduction used for benefits administration reporting, compliance, and analytics. Distinguishes benefit plan types (medical, dental, vision, 401k, FSA, HSA, life, supplemental) from garnishment subtypes (child_support, tax_levy, creditor_garnishment, student_loan) and other voluntary deductions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical|dental|vision|401k|roth_401k|fsa|hsa|life|supplemental|child_support|tax_levy|creditor_garnishment|student_loan|uniform|other — promote to reference product]']
  deduction_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code identifying the specific deduction type as configured in the payroll system (e.g., MED01, DENT01, 401K, GARNCS, UNIFEE). Maps to the deduction setup table in TempWorks Payroll and drives calculation rules, GL account mapping, and tax treatment.']
  deduction_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this deduction is applied to the worker\'s paycheck. Drives whether the deduction is taken every pay period or on a specific schedule (e.g., some benefit premiums are taken only on first and third paycheck of the month for semimonthly payroll). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: per_pay_period|weekly|biweekly|semimonthly|monthly|quarterly|annual|one_time — promote to reference product]']
  deduction_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the deduction as it appears on the worker\'s pay stub (e.g., \'Medical Insurance - Employee\', \'Child Support Garnishment\', \'401(k) Contribution\'). Sourced from the deduction code setup in TempWorks Payroll.']
  deduction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this deduction line: active (currently being withheld), suspended (temporarily halted, e.g., worker on leave), terminated (permanently stopped, e.g., benefit cancelled or garnishment satisfied), pending (set up but not yet effective), waived (worker or employer elected to waive for this period). TRANSACTION_LINE role: maps to LIFECYCLE_STATUS of the deduction arrangement.']
  deduction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deduction by tax treatment and origin: pre_tax (reduces taxable gross, e.g., 401k, FSA, HSA, Section 125 benefits), post_tax (applied after tax calculation, e.g., Roth 401k, after-tax life insurance), benefit (employer-sponsored benefit premium withholding), garnishment (court-ordered or agency-mandated wage withholding), voluntary (worker-elected non-benefit deduction). Drives gross-to-net calculation sequencing.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this deduction arrangement became active for the worker. For benefit deductions, aligns with the benefit enrollment effective date. For garnishments, aligns with the court order service date. Determines when withholding begins.']
  employer_contribution_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Dollar amount contributed by the employer toward this benefit deduction in the current pay period (e.g., employer share of medical premium, employer 401k match). Applicable only to benefit-type deductions. Zero or null for garnishments and purely employee-funded deductions. Used in total compensation reporting and burden rate calculations.']
  enrollment_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the worker\'s benefit enrollment became effective, triggering the start of premium withholding for this deduction. May differ from the deduction effective_date if there is a retroactive enrollment adjustment. Null for non-benefit deductions.']
  enrollment_termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the worker\'s benefit enrollment ended, triggering cessation of premium withholding. Populated when a worker loses eligibility, voluntarily cancels coverage, or separates from employment. Null if enrollment is ongoing. Null for non-benefit deductions.']
  garnishment_issuing_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the court, government agency, or child support enforcement agency that issued the garnishment order (e.g., \'Cook County Circuit Court\', \'IRS\', \'Illinois DCSS\'). Used for remittance routing and compliance documentation. Null for non-garnishment deductions.']
  garnishment_remittance_date date [note: 'Date by which the withheld garnishment amount must be remitted to the issuing court or agency. Derived from the court order terms and state-specific remittance deadlines. Null for non-garnishment deductions.']
  is_arrears_recovery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deduction line represents a recovery of a previously missed (arrears) deduction amount rather than a standard current-period deduction. True when the deduction_amount includes or consists entirely of arrears recovery. Used to distinguish regular deductions from catch-up withholdings in payroll analytics.']
  is_fica_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deduction amount is exempt from FICA (Social Security and Medicare) taxes. True for qualified Section 125 benefit deductions and HSA employer contributions. False for most other deductions. Drives FICA taxable wage base calculation in payroll processing.']
  is_pretax boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deduction reduces the worker\'s taxable gross wages before FICA, federal income tax, and applicable state income tax are calculated. True for Section 125 cafeteria plan deductions (medical, dental, vision, FSA), traditional 401k contributions, and HSA contributions. False for post-tax deductions such as Roth 401k and after-tax life insurance. Critical for accurate gross-to-net payroll calculation.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering number of this deduction line within the parent paycheck. Enables ordered display of deductions on pay stubs and supports reconciliation of multiple deductions per pay period. TRANSACTION_LINE role: LINE_SEQUENCE.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the pay period in which this deduction line was applied. Defines the close of the earnings window for this deduction calculation. Used in conjunction with pay_period_start_date for period boundary validation and compliance reporting.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the pay period in which this deduction line was applied. Combined with pay_period_end_date, defines the earnings period for which this deduction is calculated. Supports multi-state payroll compliance and period-over-period deduction analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this deduction line record originated. Primarily TempWorks Payroll for payroll-processed deductions, SAP SuccessFactors for benefit enrollment-driven deductions, or manual for corrections entered directly. Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which this deduction arrangement was or is scheduled to be permanently stopped. For benefit deductions, aligns with benefit enrollment termination or coverage end date. For garnishments, the date the order is satisfied or released. Null if the deduction is ongoing with no defined end.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this deduction line record was last modified in the source payroll system (e.g., amount correction, status change, suspension). Supports change data capture (CDC) processing in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  w2_box_code varchar [note: 'IRS W-2 form box code under which this deduction amount must be reported at year-end (e.g., \'D\' for 401k elective deferrals, \'W\' for HSA employer contributions, \'DD\' for employer-sponsored health coverage cost). Drives W-2 generation in TempWorks Payroll. Null for deductions not reportable on W-2.']
  work_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state code for the state in which the worker performed services during this pay period. Determines applicable state income tax withholding rules, state-specific garnishment limits, SUTA taxability, and state benefit deduction regulations. Critical for multi-state payroll compliance.']
  ytd_deduction_amount decimal(14,4) [note: 'Cumulative total of this deduction withheld from the worker\'s pay from the start of the calendar year through and including the current pay period. Used for W-2 reporting, IRS annual contribution limit enforcement (e.g., 401k IRS limit, FSA/HSA annual caps), and year-end tax reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Transactional line-item record for each voluntary or involuntary deduction applied to a worker pay stub, serving as the SSOT for all payroll deductions including benefit enrollment-driven withholdings. For benefit-type deductions: stores benefit plan name, plan type (medical, dental, vision, 401k, FSA, HSA, life, supplemental), coverage tier (employee only, employee+spouse, family), employee premium contribution, employer contribution, benefit carrier, enrollment effective and termination dates, and enrollment status. For garnishment-type deductions: references the governing garnishment order. For all deductions: stores deduction code, deduction type (pre-tax, post-tax, benefit, garnishment), deduction amount, deduction frequency, YTD deduction total, court order reference for garnishments, and deduction status (active, suspended, terminated). Consolidates benefit enrollment tracking with deduction processing — enrollment attributes drive premium withholding calculations each pay period.'
}

Table payroll.burden_rate {
  burden_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the burden rate record. Primary key for the burden_rate data product in the payroll domain.']
  approved_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Burden rate approvals by finance/operations staff. Role-prefixed for clarity. Reuses existing approved_by column pattern. Required for pricing accuracy, margin management, and financial controls.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific placement assignment for which this burden rate is configured, when applicability_scope is \'assignment_specific\'. Null for broader-scope rates. Links to the placement assignment record.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for which this burden rate is specifically configured, when applicability_scope is \'client_specific\'. Null for enterprise-wide or assignment-specific rates. Links to the client account master in the CRM.']
  coe_risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.coe_risk_assessment. Business justification: Burden rates for co-employment arrangements must reflect risk assessment outcomes. High co-employment risk drives higher insurance loading, indemnification costs, and legal reserves - risk assessment ']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Burden rates (FICA, SUTA, workers\' comp) are negotiated in MSAs and determine employer cost structure. Staffing firms must link burden to MSA for accurate gross margin calculation, client billing, and']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Burden rates (FICA, FUTA, SUTA, WC, benefits) are often cost-center-specific for overhead allocation and pricing. Required for accurate job costing and margin analysis by branch/division in staffing a']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Burden rate components (FICA, FUTA, SUTA, WC, benefits) map to specific GL expense accounts for costing and financial reporting. Required for accurate job costing and margin analysis in staffing opera']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Workers\' comp rates, SUTA rates, and employer burden vary significantly by work location and state jurisdiction. Required for accurate cost modeling, pricing, and margin analysis by client location in']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.order_header. Business justification: Burden rates can be order-specific when negotiated client terms, special insurance requirements, or custom rate cards apply. Supports accurate order-level cost modeling, pricing validation, and margin']
  primary_ot_burden_rate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the burden rate record applicable to overtime (OT) hours for this worker classification and state. Overtime pay triggers different FICA and WC cost calculations. Null if the same burden rate applies to both regular and OT hours.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Vendor-supplied workers often have different burden rates (FICA, SUTA, workers\' comp) due to co-employment, PEO models, or vendor-specific insurance policies. Accurate cost modeling and bill rate calc']
  tertiary_superseded_by_burden_rate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the burden rate record that replaced this record when status is \'superseded\'. Enables forward navigation in the rate version chain for audit and historical analysis.']
  wc_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.wc_policy. Business justification: Burden rate calculations include workers compensation premium components that are defined by specific WC policies. burden_rate currently has wc_policy_number: STRING which is a denormalized reference.']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.work_location. Business justification: Workers comp rates, SUTA rates, and burden components vary by work location due to state jurisdiction and site hazard classification. Links burden structure to location for accurate cost modeling.']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope at which this burden rate is applied: enterprise_wide (default for all placements), client_specific (negotiated rate for a specific client account), assignment_specific (applied to a single placement or assignment), or branch_specific (applicable to a specific branch or division).']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this burden rate structure was formally approved by the authorized approver. Required for audit trail and SOC 2 change management compliance.']
  benefits_load_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Employer-paid benefits cost expressed as a percentage of gross wages included in the burden rate. Covers health insurance, dental, vision, 401(k) match, and other statutory or voluntary benefits provided to workers. Varies by worker classification and benefit plan enrollment.']
  branch_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the staffing branch or division to which this burden rate applies when applicability_scope is \'branch_specific\'. Supports multi-branch operations where burden structures differ by geographic market or business unit.']
  burden_rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the burden rate record. \'active\' indicates the rate is in use for payroll cost calculations; \'superseded\' indicates a newer version has replaced this record; \'draft\' indicates pending approval before activation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this burden rate record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 Type II compliance requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this burden rate structure becomes effective and is applied to payroll cost calculations, spread analysis, and bill rate margin validation. Aligns with payroll period start dates.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Type of staffing engagement to which this burden rate applies. Temporary and contract placements typically carry higher WC and benefits burden; direct hire placements may carry only a placement fee structure. Temp-to-perm engagements may have conversion fee implications.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this burden rate structure ceases to be effective. Null indicates an open-ended rate with no scheduled expiration. Used to enforce rate versioning and ensure correct rate application during payroll processing.']
  fica_employer_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Employer\'s share of FICA tax expressed as a percentage of gross wages (e.g., 7.6500 for 7.65%). Comprises Social Security (6.2%) and Medicare (1.45%) employer contributions. Applied to gross pay up to applicable wage bases per IRS regulations.']
  futa_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Federal Unemployment Tax Act employer tax rate expressed as a percentage of gross wages (e.g., 0.6000 for 0.60% net after state credit). Applied to the first $7,000 of each employee\'s wages per calendar year per IRS regulations.']
  general_liability_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'General liability insurance cost expressed as a percentage of gross payroll included in the employer burden structure. Covers third-party bodily injury and property damage claims arising from staffing operations.']
  is_eor_model boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this burden rate applies to an Employer of Record (EOR) engagement model where the staffing firm acts as the legal employer of record for co-employment or client-directed workforce arrangements. EOR models carry distinct burden and compliance obligations.']
  is_peo_model boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this burden rate applies to a Professional Employer Organization (PEO) co-employment model where the PEO assumes employer tax and benefits obligations. PEO burden structures differ from standard staffing burden rates.']
  markup_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Target markup percentage applied over the pay rate to derive the bill rate, incorporating the total burden and desired gross margin. Used in spread analysis and client billing validation. Expressed as a percentage of pay rate (e.g., 45.00 = 45% markup).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for payroll analysts or finance staff to document special conditions, exceptions, carrier negotiation details, or regulatory basis for this burden rate configuration. Supports audit documentation and knowledge transfer.']
  overhead_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Internal overhead allocation expressed as a percentage of gross wages included in the burden rate. Covers branch operating costs, recruiter commissions, back-office administration, and other indirect costs attributable to workforce deployment.']
  pay_rate_max decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum hourly pay rate threshold (in USD) at which this burden rate structure is applicable. Defines the upper bound of the pay rate band for this burden rate. Null indicates no upper limit.']
  pay_rate_min decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum hourly pay rate threshold (in USD) at which this burden rate structure is applicable. Used to define pay rate banding for tiered burden rate structures where different pay levels carry different overhead or benefits loads.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this burden rate structure (e.g., \'BR-CA-WC-5403-2024\'). Used in billing systems, VMS integrations, and rate card references.']
  rate_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the burden rate structure (e.g., \'California Light Industrial WC Class 5403 2024\'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and analyst-facing tools.']
  rate_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the burden rate values indicating how the rate was established: manual (entered by payroll/finance staff), system_calculated (derived by TempWorks Payroll engine), carrier_provided (supplied by WC or GL insurance carrier), or state_mandated (set by state regulatory authority).']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the burden rate structure indicating its scope of applicability: standard (default enterprise-wide), client_specific (negotiated for a client), assignment_specific (tied to a single placement), state_override (state-level regulatory override), or wc_class_specific (Workers Compensation class-driven rate).']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state code (ISO 3166-2:US) identifying the state jurisdiction to which this burden rate applies. Critical for multi-state payroll compliance, SUTA rate assignment, and state-specific Workers Compensation rate overrides.']
  suta_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'State Unemployment Tax Act employer contribution rate expressed as a percentage of gross wages for the applicable state jurisdiction. Varies by state experience rating, industry classification, and annual state-assigned rate notices. Applied to state-specific taxable wage bases.']
  suta_wage_base decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum annual gross wages per employee subject to SUTA tax in the applicable state jurisdiction (e.g., $7,000 in some states, $56,500 in others). Required for accurate SUTA cost modeling and burden rate calculation.']
  target_gross_margin_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Target gross margin percentage for placements using this burden rate structure, representing the desired spread (bill rate minus total employer cost) as a percentage of bill rate. Used in profitability reporting and bill rate validation.']
  total_burden_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Aggregate employer burden rate expressed as a percentage of gross wages, representing the sum of all burden components (FICA employer share, FUTA, SUTA, WC, general liability, benefits load, and overhead). Used to compute total employer cost per worker hour and validate bill rate margins. Note: this is a stored configuration value, not a real-time computed aggregate.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this burden rate record was last modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and SOC 2 audit trail requirements.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for this burden rate record, incremented each time the rate structure is updated. Supports rate versioning and historical rate lookups for payroll audits and retroactive adjustments.']
