  wc_base_rate_per_100 decimal(8,4) [note: 'Workers Compensation insurance premium base rate expressed as dollars per $100 of gross payroll for the applicable NCCI class code and state jurisdiction (e.g., 2.3500 = $2.35 per $100). This is the primary WC cost driver in the burden rate structure.']
  wc_class_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the NCCI Workers Compensation class code (e.g., \'Carpentry — Construction\', \'Clerical Office Employees\'). Used in reporting, audits, and WC policy documentation.']
  wc_experience_mod_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'Experience modification factor (e-mod) applied to the WC base rate to adjust premiums based on the employer\'s actual loss history relative to industry average. Values below 1.0 indicate better-than-average loss history; above 1.0 indicates worse. Sourced from WC carrier or NCCI.']
  wc_hazard_category varchar [note: 'Risk hazard category assigned to the WC NCCI class code indicating the relative injury risk level of the job duties. Used for internal risk segmentation, pricing decisions, and WC carrier negotiations.']
  wc_ncci_class_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit NCCI Workers Compensation classification code identifying the job duty category for WC premium calculation (e.g., \'5403\' for Carpentry, \'8810\' for Clerical Office). Drives WC base rate per $100 of payroll and is the primary driver of WC cost in staffing operations.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'Worker tax and employment classification to which this burden rate applies. W-2 employees carry full employer tax burden; 1099 independent contractors carry no employer FICA/FUTA/SUTA; EOR (Employer of Record) and PEO models have distinct burden structures. Aligns with IRS worker classification rules.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the employer burden rate structure applied to worker pay for cost modeling, spread calculation, and client billing markup in staffing operations. Captures burden components including FICA employer share percentage, FUTA rate, SUTA rate by state, workers compensation (WC) rate by NCCI class code (with state-specific overrides, class description, hazard category, and base rate per $100 of payroll), general liability rate, benefits load percentage, overhead percentage, and total burden percentage. Stores effective dates, state jurisdiction, WC policy reference, client or assignment applicability, and rate source (manual, system-calculated, carrier-provided). Used to compute total employer cost per worker hour, validate bill rate margins, and support spread analysis for staffing profitability reporting.'
}

Table payroll.wc_policy {
  wc_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for wc_policy']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_account. Business justification: In co-employment and EOR arrangements, workers\' comp policies may be client-specific or shared across clients. Essential for risk management, premium allocation, and determining coverage responsibilit']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Workers\' comp policies are MSA-level insurance obligations; MSAs specify minimum coverage requirements. Staffing firms must link WC policies to MSAs for compliance verification, certificate of insuran']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Workers comp premiums are allocated to cost centers for burden calculation and client billing. Required for accurate cost center profitability and pricing in staffing operations with varying risk prof']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Workers comp premiums post to insurance expense GL accounts for P&L reporting and cost allocation. Required for accurate expense classification and burden rate calculation in staffing operations with ']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Workers\' comp policies are jurisdiction-specific and tied to specific work locations. Required for accurate premium calculation, state compliance, and tracking which client locations are covered under']
  osha_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.osha_incident. Business justification: Workers\' comp policies are triggered by OSHA recordable incidents. Claim filing, reserve setting, and premium adjustments depend on incident data - operational link between safety incident tracking an']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_run. Business justification: Workers compensation premium calculations are tied to specific payroll runs. wc_policy currently has pay_run_reference: STRING which is a denormalized reference. Adding structured FK pay_run_id → pay_']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Workers\' comp policies for vendor-supplied workers are often held by the vendor, not the staffing agency. Certificate of insurance (COI) tracking, premium audit, and liability determination require li']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.work_location. Business justification: Workers compensation policies are issued per work location and jurisdiction. Each site has specific WC class codes and rates. Links policy coverage to physical work location.']
  audit_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of premium adjustment resulting from the most recent carrier audit, representing the difference between estimated premiums paid and actual earned premium based on audited payroll. Positive values indicate additional premium owed; negative values indicate a return premium credit.']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the insurance carrier conducts a premium audit to reconcile estimated premiums against actual payroll. Staffing firms with high payroll volume often negotiate more frequent audits to reduce year-end true-up exposure.']
  base_rate_per_100 decimal(10,4) [note: 'The manual (base) WC premium rate expressed as a dollar amount per $100 of covered payroll for the associated class code and state jurisdiction, before application of the experience modification factor (EMR). Sourced from the applicable state rating bureau or NCCI.']
  burden_rate_wc_component decimal(8,6) [note: 'The WC premium cost expressed as a decimal rate applied to gross payroll, representing the WC component of the total employer burden rate. Used in bill rate calculations, spread analysis, and gross margin reporting. Derived from the modified rate and historical payroll mix for this class code.']
  carrier_naic_code varchar [note: 'The five-digit NAIC company code assigned to the insurance carrier by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners. Used for regulatory identification and carrier verification in multi-state WC compliance.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the insurance carrier or underwriter providing the workers compensation coverage under this policy. Used for carrier reporting, premium remittance, and audit correspondence.']
  client_cost_allocation_code varchar [note: 'The internal cost allocation code used to attribute WC premium costs to specific client accounts or job orders. Enables client-level WC cost reporting, profitability analysis, and accurate bill rate construction inclusive of WC burden.']
  co_employment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this WC policy record involves a co-employment arrangement where both the staffing firm and the client share employer responsibilities. Affects WC coverage determination and premium allocation between parties.']
  computed_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated WC premium dollar amount for the premium period, computed as (payroll_subject_to_wc / 100) multiplied by the modified_rate_per_100. Represents the accrued WC cost for the period and is used in burden rate application and client cost allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this workers compensation policy record was first created in the payroll system. Provides audit trail for record provenance and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  deductible_amount_per_claim decimal(18,2) [note: 'The per-claim deductible amount the employer is responsible for funding before the carrier pays WC benefits, applicable to large deductible programs. Zero for guaranteed cost policies. Impacts cash flow planning and loss reserve requirements.']
  deposit_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The initial deposit premium paid to the carrier at policy inception, typically a percentage of the estimated annual premium. Represents the upfront cash outlay and is reconciled against actual earned premium at audit.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the workers compensation insurance policy becomes active and coverage begins. Used to determine coverage applicability for worker assignments and premium period calculations.']
  employer_liability_limit_disease_policy decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate policy limit of employers liability coverage for bodily injury by disease. Distinct from the per-accident limit and the per-employee disease limit. Used for risk management reporting and MSA compliance verification.']
  employer_liability_limit_each_accident decimal(18,2) [note: 'The per-occurrence limit of employers liability coverage (Part B of the WC policy) for bodily injury by accident. Standard limits are $100,000, $500,000, or $1,000,000. Used for risk management and client contract compliance.']
  eor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this WC policy record applies to workers placed under an Employer of Record (EOR) arrangement, where the staffing firm acts as the legal employer for WC purposes. Drives co-employment risk management and premium allocation logic.']
  estimated_annual_premium decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated total annual WC premium for this policy as projected at policy inception, based on estimated payroll and applicable rates. Used for budgeting, cash flow planning, and comparison against actual accrued premiums during the audit cycle.']
  experience_mod_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'The experience modification rate (EMR) assigned by NCCI or the applicable state rating bureau, reflecting the employer\'s actual loss history relative to expected losses for the class. A factor below 1.0 indicates better-than-average loss experience; above 1.0 indicates worse. Applied to the base rate to compute the modified premium rate.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the workers compensation insurance policy expires and coverage ends. Triggers renewal workflow and is used to validate coverage during assignment management and payroll processing.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'The date of the most recently completed premium audit conducted by the insurance carrier. Used to track audit cycle compliance, identify overdue audits, and reconcile estimated versus actual premium balances.']
  loss_sensitive_program_type varchar [note: 'For loss-sensitive policies, identifies the specific program structure. Retrospective rated programs adjust final premium based on actual losses; large deductible programs require the employer to fund losses up to a deductible threshold. Null or \'none\' for guaranteed cost policies.']
  minimum_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum premium amount guaranteed to the carrier regardless of actual payroll volume, as stipulated in the policy terms. Relevant for low-volume periods and ensures the carrier recovers minimum administrative costs.']
  modified_rate_per_100 decimal(10,4) [note: 'The effective WC premium rate per $100 of payroll after applying the experience modification factor (EMR) to the base rate. This is the rate used in actual premium calculations for payroll runs. Computed as base_rate_per_100 multiplied by experience_mod_factor.']
  ncci_class_description varchar [note: 'The standard NCCI descriptive label for the workers compensation class code, as defined in the NCCI Scopes Manual. Provides human-readable context for the class code used in carrier reporting, premium audits, and cost allocation to client assignments.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next premium audit by the insurance carrier. Used for audit preparation planning, payroll record retention compliance, and proactive reconciliation of estimated versus actual premiums.']
  payroll_subject_to_wc decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross payroll dollar amount for the premium period that is subject to workers compensation premium calculation for this class code and state jurisdiction. Excludes non-covered remuneration per NCCI rules (e.g., tips, certain overtime premiums). Used as the basis for premium computation.']
  policy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, underwriting conditions, endorsements, or special program terms associated with this WC policy record. Used by payroll and risk management teams to document carrier-specific requirements or exceptions.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'The externally-assigned policy number issued by the insurance carrier that uniquely identifies this workers compensation insurance policy. Used for carrier reporting, premium audit reconciliation, and regulatory filings.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the workers compensation policy. Active policies are in force and covering workers; expired policies have passed their expiration date; cancelled policies were terminated before expiration; pending policies are bound but not yet effective.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the WC insurance policy structure. Guaranteed cost policies have fixed premiums; loss-sensitive policies (e.g., retrospective rated, large deductible) have premiums that vary based on actual loss experience. Drives premium calculation methodology and financial risk exposure.']
  policy_year varchar [note: 'The four-digit calendar or fiscal year of the workers compensation policy period. Used to group premium transactions for annual audit reconciliation, loss run reporting, and year-over-year cost trend analysis.']
  premium_basis varchar [note: 'The basis on which WC premiums are calculated. In staffing, the standard basis is payroll (gross remuneration subject to WC), but some specialty programs may use flat or per-capita bases. Determines how payroll_subject_to_wc is computed.']
  premium_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the payroll period for which this WC premium transaction was calculated. Together with premium_period_start_date, defines the coverage window for the premium accrual.']
  premium_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the payroll period for which this WC premium transaction was calculated. Defines the coverage window for the premium accrual and aligns with the pay run cycle.']
  schedule_credit_debit_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The schedule rating credit (negative value) or debit (positive value) percentage applied to the manual premium, reflecting underwriter judgment on risk characteristics not captured by the experience modification factor. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., -0.10 for a 10% credit).']
  state_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The two-letter US state code representing the state jurisdiction under which this workers compensation policy is filed and governed. Critical for multi-state WC compliance, SUTA alignment, and state-specific rate application for temporary and contract workers.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this workers compensation policy record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  wc_class_code varchar [note: 'The four-digit NCCI (National Council on Compensation Insurance) classification code that categorizes the type of work performed by covered workers. Determines the base WC rate applied per $100 of payroll. Each class code reflects the risk level of the job duties.']
  workers_comp_code_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the rating bureau or authority that issued the WC class code and rates for this policy record. Most states use NCCI; California uses WCIRB; a few states have independent bureaus. Critical for multi-state compliance and rate validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NCCI|WCIRB|NYBCOMP|DELAWARE|INDIANA|MICHIGAN|MINNESOTA|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  ytd_premium_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative workers compensation premium accrued from the start of the policy year through the current premium period for this class code and state jurisdiction. Used for premium audit reconciliation, carrier reporting, and financial forecasting of WC costs.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional record for workers compensation insurance policies and premium calculations administered through TempWorks Payroll — unified SSOT for both WC policy administration and premium accrual. Policy attributes: policy number, insurance carrier, policy effective and expiration dates, state jurisdiction, experience modification factor (EMR), policy type (guaranteed cost, loss-sensitive), premium basis (payroll-based), audit frequency, and policy status. Premium transaction attributes: WC class code, NCCI class description, state jurisdiction, payroll amount subject to WC, WC rate applied per $100 of payroll, computed premium amount, experience modification factor applied, YTD premium total, premium period, and pay run reference. Supports multi-state WC compliance for temporary and contract workers, carrier reporting, premium audit reconciliation, accurate burden rate application, and cost allocation to client assignments.'
}

Table payroll.tax_filing {
  tax_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for tax_filing']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounting_period. Business justification: Tax filings (941, 940, state quarterly) must align with accounting periods for accrual, liability tracking, and period close. Required for financial statement accuracy and tax compliance reconciliatio']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: Tax filings are subject to compliance audits by IRS, state tax authorities, and DOL. Audit scope includes payroll tax returns, deposit schedules, and withholding accuracy - audit trail links filings t']
  original_filing_tax_filing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original payroll_tax_filing_id that this amended filing corrects. Null for original (non-amended) filings. Enables amendment chain tracking for IRS audit readiness.']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll run or payroll batch that generated the tax liabilities captured in this filing. Links the tax filing back to the source payroll processing event in TempWorks Payroll. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE (to a MASTER_AGREEMENT/processing entity) for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  amended_filing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents an amended or corrected tax filing (True) versus an original filing (False). Amended filings (e.g., Form 941-X) correct errors in previously submitted returns and are tracked separately for audit purposes.']
  balance_due_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining tax balance owed to the tax authority after applying all deposits and credits (tax_liability_amount minus deposit_amount minus prior_deposit_amount). A positive value indicates underpayment; zero or negative indicates overpayment or credit.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation or acknowledgment number returned by the tax authority (e.g., EFTPS trace number, state portal confirmation) upon successful receipt of the tax deposit or filed return. Used for audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax filing record was first created in TempWorks Payroll and ingested into the Silver Layer. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this filing record (e.g., \'USD\'). Supports international operations and EOR arrangements. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual dollar amount deposited to the tax authority for this filing period. May differ from tax_liability_amount due to prior deposits, adjustments, or credits. Net amount remitted. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  deposit_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the tax deposit was transmitted to the tax authority via EFTPS or state equivalent. Used to determine timeliness and calculate any late deposit penalties. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision) for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  deposit_method varchar [note: 'Method used to transmit the tax deposit to the tax authority. \'EFTPS\' = IRS Electronic Federal Tax Payment System (mandatory for most employers); \'ACH\' = Automated Clearing House bank transfer for state deposits; \'check\' = paper check (rare); \'state_portal\' = state-specific online payment portal.']
  due_date date [note: 'Statutory deadline by which this tax deposit or return must be submitted to the tax authority to avoid penalties. Determined by IRS deposit schedule rules (semi-weekly or monthly) or statutory filing deadlines for annual/quarterly returns.']
  ein varchar [note: 'IRS-assigned Employer Identification Number (EIN) of the legal entity filing this return. Required on all federal and state payroll tax filings. In EOR (Employer of Record) and co-employment models, this may reflect the EOR entity\'s EIN rather than the client\'s EIN.']
  eor_arrangement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tax filing is processed under an Employer of Record (EOR) or co-employment arrangement (True), where the staffing firm acts as the legal employer for tax purposes on behalf of a client. Affects EIN used and liability allocation.']
  federal_income_tax_withheld decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total federal income tax withheld from worker wages and remitted to the IRS for this filing period. Reported on IRS Form 941 Line 3. Applies to W-2 employees; not applicable for 1099 independent contractors.']
  fica_employee_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee share of FICA taxes (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%) withheld from worker paychecks and remitted as part of this filing. Applicable for W-2 employees; not applicable for 1099 independent contractors.']
  fica_employer_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employer matching share of FICA taxes (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%) owed by the staffing firm or EOR entity as the employer of record. Equal to the employee FICA amount under standard rules.']
  filed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the tax return was electronically submitted to the tax authority. Provides precise audit trail for compliance and IRS/DOL audit readiness. Null if return has not yet been filed.']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this tax filing or deposit transaction, used for reconciliation with the tax authority and TempWorks Payroll records. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  filing_period_type varchar [note: 'Frequency classification of the tax filing period. Determines deposit schedule (e.g., semi-weekly vs. monthly depositor rules under IRS Publication 15) and aligns with the form type (e.g., quarterly for 941, annual for 940).']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the payroll tax filing or deposit obligation. \'pending\' = liability identified but not yet deposited; \'deposited\' = funds transferred to tax authority; \'filed\' = return submitted; \'amended\' = corrected filing submitted; \'voided\' = filing cancelled; \'rejected\' = filing rejected by authority. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'The IRS or state tax form type associated with this filing obligation. Common values include: 941 (Employer\'s Quarterly Federal Tax Return), 940 (Federal Unemployment Tax Act annual return), 944 (annual federal tax return for small employers), W-2 (Wage and Tax Statement), 1099-NEC (Non-Employee Compensation), state SUTA return, state income tax withholding return. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 941|940|944|W-2|1099-NEC|SUTA|state_income_tax|local_income_tax — promote to reference product]']
  futa_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Federal Unemployment Tax Act (FUTA) tax liability for this filing period. Calculated at 6.0% on the first $7,000 of each employee\'s wages, net of SUTA credit (typically reducing effective rate to 0.6%). Reported on IRS Form 940.']
  interest_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of interest charged by the tax authority on late or underpaid tax deposits. Accrues from the deposit due date to the actual payment date. Zero if no interest assessed.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the employer entity as registered with the IRS and state tax authorities for this filing. Supports multi-entity payroll structures including EOR, PEO, and co-employment arrangements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by payroll staff regarding this tax filing, such as reasons for amendments, penalty dispute details, or special handling instructions. Supports operational context for compliance reviews.']
  overpayment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount overpaid to the tax authority for this filing period. May be applied as a credit to the next period or requested as a refund. Null or zero if no overpayment exists.']
  payroll_period_end_date date [note: 'Last date of the payroll period covered by this tax filing. Defines the end of the wage and tax liability window for this return.']
  payroll_period_start_date date [note: 'First date of the payroll period covered by this tax filing. Combined with payroll_period_end_date, defines the wage and tax liability window for this return.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of IRS or state-assessed penalties for late deposit, late filing, or underpayment of payroll taxes. Zero if no penalties assessed. Critical for compliance monitoring and SLA tracking.']
  prior_deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax deposits made in prior periods within the same filing period that are applied against the current tax liability. Used for reconciliation between cumulative deposits and the final return balance due.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state or territory code (ISO 3166-2:US) for the jurisdiction of this filing. Null for federal-level filings. Critical for multi-state payroll tax compliance across all worker assignment states.']
  state_income_tax_withheld decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total state income tax withheld from worker wages and remitted to the state tax authority for this filing period. Applicable for states with income tax; null for states without state income tax (e.g., TX, FL, WA).']
  suta_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) tax liability for this filing period. Rate and wage base vary by state and employer experience rating. Critical for multi-state staffing operations where workers are assigned across multiple states.']
  tax_authority_level varchar [note: 'Jurisdictional level of the tax authority receiving this filing: \'federal\' (IRS/DOL), \'state\' (state revenue or unemployment agency), or \'local\' (city/county tax authority). Supports multi-state payroll compliance reporting and audit readiness.']
  tax_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the federal, state, or local tax authority to which this filing or deposit is submitted (e.g., \'IRS\', \'California EDD\', \'New York State DTF\', \'City of Philadelphia Revenue Department\'). Used for multi-jurisdiction payroll compliance tracking.']
  tax_liability_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax liability computed for this filing period based on taxable wages and applicable tax rates. Represents the gross amount owed to the tax authority before any prior deposits or credits are applied. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  tax_quarter varchar [note: 'Calendar quarter (1–4) to which this filing applies. Applicable for quarterly filings such as IRS Form 941 and quarterly SUTA returns. Null for non-quarterly filing types.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit calendar year to which this tax filing applies (e.g., 2024). Used for annual reconciliation, W-2/1099 processing, and IRS/DOL audit readiness.']
  total_taxable_wages decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate gross taxable wages subject to the specific tax type covered by this filing for the filing period. Basis for computing the total tax liability. Excludes non-taxable fringe benefits and pre-tax deductions where applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this tax filing record, including status changes, amendments, or deposit updates. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of workers covered by this filing: \'W-2\' for employees subject to withholding and employer taxes, \'1099\' for independent contractors (IC) subject only to self-employment tax reporting, or \'both\' for filings covering mixed worker populations. Critical for IRS worker classification compliance.']
  worker_count varchar [note: 'Number of workers (W-2 employees or 1099 contractors) included in this tax filing period. Reported on IRS Form 941 Line 1 as the number of employees who received wages. Supports headcount analytics and workforce planning.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking payroll tax deposit and filing obligations submitted to federal, state, and local tax authorities. Captures filing type (941, 940, SUTA, state income tax), tax authority, filing period, total taxable wages, total tax liability, deposit amount, deposit date, filing due date, filing status (pending, deposited, filed, amended), confirmation number, and penalty/interest amounts if applicable. Supports multi-state tax compliance and IRS/DOL audit readiness.'
}

Table payroll.tax_form {
  tax_form_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for each annual tax form record (W-2, W-2c, 1099-NEC, 1099-MISC) generated in TempWorks Payroll. Globally unique across all form types and tax years.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: W-2/1099 forms must reference MSA for co-employment determination and tax responsibility allocation. Critical for EOR/PEO arrangements where legal employer status affects tax filing obligations and de']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employer or payer entity (staffing company legal entity) responsible for issuing this tax form. Determines the EIN used on the form.']
  original_form_tax_form_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original tax_form record that this corrected form (W-2c or amended 1099) supersedes. Null for original (non-corrected) forms. Enables correction history chain tracking for audit and IRS reconciliation.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker or independent contractor (IC) for whom this tax form was generated. Links to the candidate/worker master record in Bullhorn ATS or TempWorks Payroll.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tax form record was first created in TempWorks Payroll during the year-end tax form generation process. Supports audit trail and SOC 2 compliance.']
  distribution_date date [note: 'Date the worker\'s copy of the tax form was distributed (mailed, electronically delivered, or handed in person). Must be on or before January 31 of the year following the tax year per IRS requirements.']
  distribution_method varchar [note: 'Method used to distribute the worker\'s copy of the tax form. Mail: physical paper mailed to worker address. Electronic: delivered via secure online portal (requires worker consent). In-person: handed directly to worker.']
  efile_accepted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the IRS FIRE system or SSA ACCUWAGE system accepted the e-file submission for this tax form. Null if not yet filed or if paper-filed. Critical for IRS filing deadline compliance.']
  efile_submission_reference varchar [note: 'IRS or SSA-assigned submission identifier returned upon successful e-file transmission. For W-2s: SSA ACCUWAGE submission ID. For 1099s: IRS FIRE system submission ID. Used to track filing status and retrieve acknowledgements.']
  employer_ein varchar [note: 'Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) of the staffing company or EOR entity issuing the tax form. Appears in Box c of W-2 and Payer TIN field of 1099 forms. Required for IRS e-filing and state tax agency submissions.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the tax form. Draft: form generated but not yet submitted to IRS/SSA. Filed: successfully submitted. Corrected: a W-2c or amended 1099 has been issued. Voided: form cancelled. Accepted: IRS/SSA e-file acknowledgement received.']
  form_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator identifying the IRS tax form type. W-2 for W-2 employees and temporary workers; W-2c for corrected W-2s; 1099-NEC for independent contractor (IC) nonemployee compensation; 1099-MISC for miscellaneous income. Drives form-specific field population and filing rules.']
  is_corrected boolean [note: 'True if this record represents a corrected tax form (W-2c or amended 1099). The corrected checkbox is checked on the physical form. Links to the original form via original_form_id for correction history tracking.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tax form record was most recently modified (e.g., correction applied, filing status updated, distribution recorded). Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  local_income_tax_withheld_box19 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 19: Total local income tax withheld from the worker\'s wages. Reported to the applicable local tax authority. Null if no local tax applies.']
  local_wages_box18 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 18: Total wages subject to local income tax withholding (e.g., city or county taxes). Applicable in jurisdictions such as New York City, Philadelphia, or Ohio municipalities. Null if no local tax applies.']
  misc_other_income_box3 decimal(12,2) [note: '1099-MISC Box 3: Other income payments not reportable in other boxes, such as prizes, awards, or taxable per diem amounts exceeding IRS limits. Null for W-2 and 1099-NEC form types.']
  misc_rents_box1 decimal(12,2) [note: '1099-MISC Box 1: Rent payments of $600 or more paid to a landlord or property owner. Applicable when staffing company pays office or facility rent to a non-corporate payee. Null for W-2 and 1099-NEC form types.']
  nec_compensation_box1 decimal(12,2) [note: '1099-NEC Box 1: Total nonemployee compensation paid to an independent contractor (IC) during the tax year. Includes fees, commissions, prizes, and awards for services rendered. Null for W-2 form types. Threshold: $600 or more triggers filing requirement.']
  nec_federal_tax_withheld_box4 decimal(12,2) [note: '1099-NEC Box 4: Federal income tax withheld under backup withholding rules (24% rate) when the contractor failed to provide a valid TIN or IRS notified backup withholding is required. Null for W-2 form types.']
  paper_filed boolean [note: 'True if this tax form was submitted to the IRS/SSA via paper rather than electronic filing. Staffing companies filing 10 or more forms are required to e-file per IRS regulations. Tracks compliance with mandatory e-file thresholds.']
  payer_tin varchar [note: 'Payer\'s Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) as reported on 1099 forms. Typically the same as the employer EIN but may differ for EOR or co-employment arrangements where a third-party PEO is the payer of record.']
  recipient_tin_type varchar [note: 'Type of TIN used for the recipient/worker on this tax form. SSN for W-2 employees and individual contractors; EIN for contractors operating as a business entity; ITIN for foreign workers without an SSN. Drives IRS validation rules.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state abbreviation for the primary state of employment reported on this tax form (W-2 Box 15 state code). Drives state tax agency filing and multi-state payroll compliance. For workers with wages in multiple states, the primary state is recorded here.']
  state_employer_number varchar [note: 'State-assigned employer identification number used for state income tax withholding reporting (W-2 Box 15). Required for state e-filing submissions. Format varies by state.']
  state_filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the state tax agency filing for this form. Not_required: state has no income tax or filing not required. Pending: prepared but not yet submitted. Filed: submitted to state agency. Accepted: state agency confirmed receipt. Supports multi-state payroll compliance tracking.']
  state_income_tax_withheld_box17 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 17: Total state income tax withheld from the worker\'s wages. Reported to the applicable state tax agency. Null for 1099 form types or states with no income tax.']
  state_wages_box16 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 16: Total wages subject to state income tax withholding. May differ from Box 1 federal wages due to state-specific pre-tax exclusions. Supports multi-state payroll compliance. Null for 1099 form types.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The calendar tax year (e.g., 2024) for which this form reports wages, compensation, and withholdings. Drives IRS filing deadlines and year-end reconciliation cycles.']
  w2_box12_code_a_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 12 Code A: Uncollected Social Security tax on tips. One of up to four Box 12 entries capturing pre-tax benefit and special compensation amounts. Null if not applicable.']
  w2_box12_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of IRS Box 12 codes reported on this W-2 (e.g., \'D|DD|W\'). Code D = 401(k) elective deferrals; Code DD = employer-sponsored health coverage cost; Code W = HSA employer contributions; Code C = taxable cost of group-term life insurance. Supports multi-code scenarios common in staffing. Null for 1099 form types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A|B|C|D|DD|E|F|G|H|J|K|L|M|N|P|Q|R|S|T|V|W|Y|Z|AA|BB|DD|EE|FF|GG|HH — promote to reference product]']
  w2_box12_total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate dollar amount across all Box 12 codes reported on this W-2. Used for year-end reconciliation of pre-tax benefit deductions (401k, HSA, health coverage). Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_federal_tax_withheld_box2 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 2: Total federal income tax withheld from the worker\'s wages during the tax year. Reported to IRS and used by worker for federal tax return filing. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_medicare_tax_withheld_box6 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 6: Employee\'s share of Medicare (FICA) tax withheld. Equals 1.45% of Box 5 wages, plus 0.9% Additional Medicare Tax for high earners. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_medicare_wages_box5 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 5: Total wages and tips subject to Medicare (FICA) tax. No wage base cap applies. Includes wages above the Social Security wage base. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_other_compensation_box14 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 14: Employer-reported informational amounts such as state disability insurance (SDI) withheld, union dues, educational assistance, or other items not captured in other boxes. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_retirement_plan_box13 boolean [note: 'W-2 Box 13 checkbox: True if the worker was an active participant in an employer-sponsored retirement plan (401k, SIMPLE IRA, etc.) during the tax year. Affects IRA deductibility on the worker\'s personal return. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_ss_tax_withheld_box4 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 4: Employee\'s share of Social Security (FICA) tax withheld. Equals 6.2% of Box 3 wages up to the wage base. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_ss_wages_box3 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 3: Total wages subject to Social Security (FICA) tax, capped at the annual Social Security wage base limit. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_statutory_employee_box13 boolean [note: 'W-2 Box 13 checkbox: True if the worker is classified as a statutory employee (e.g., certain drivers, traveling salespeople). Affects self-employment tax treatment. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_third_party_sick_pay_box13 boolean [note: 'W-2 Box 13 checkbox: True if the worker received third-party sick pay (e.g., from an insurance carrier) that is included in wages. Common in staffing for workers on short-term disability. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_wages_box1 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 1: Total taxable wages, tips, and other compensation subject to federal income tax withholding. Excludes pre-tax benefit deductions (401k, health insurance). Null for 1099 form types.']
  worker_address_city varchar [note: 'City of the worker\'s mailing address as printed on the tax form. Used for form distribution and state tax filing jurisdiction determination.']
  worker_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the worker\'s mailing address as printed on the tax form for distribution. Required for paper form mailing and IRS/SSA filing. Appears in Box f of W-2 and Recipient Address of 1099.']
  worker_address_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code of the worker\'s mailing address. Drives state tax filing jurisdiction and multi-state payroll compliance rules.']
  worker_address_zip varchar [note: 'ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code of the worker\'s mailing address as printed on the tax form.']
  worker_copy_distributed boolean [note: 'True if the worker\'s copy of the tax form (Copy B for W-2, Copy B for 1099) has been distributed to the worker by the IRS deadline (January 31). Tracks compliance with worker distribution requirements.']
  worker_legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the worker or independent contractor as it appears on the tax form (W-2 Box e or 1099 Recipient Name). Must match SSA records for W-2 employees to avoid name/SSN mismatch errors.']
  worker_ssn varchar [note: 'Worker\'s Social Security Number (SSN) as reported on the tax form. Appears in Box a of W-2 and Recipient TIN field of 1099 forms. Masked in most operational views; full value required only for IRS e-filing and SSA submission. Governed by FCRA and IRS regulations.']
  workers_comp_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code for the state under which workers compensation (Workers Comp) insurance was carried for this worker during the tax year. Relevant for W-2 employees on temporary assignments. Used in year-end workers comp audit reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Master record representing annual tax forms generated for both W-2 employees/temporary workers and 1099 independent contractors. For W-2 forms: captures Box 1 wages, Box 2 federal income tax withheld, Boxes 3-6 Social Security and Medicare wages and withholdings, state and local wages and withholdings, pre-tax benefit amounts (Box 12 codes), and W-2 filing status. For 1099 forms: captures 1099-NEC nonemployee compensation (Box 1), 1099-MISC miscellaneous income, backup withholding, and state income. Stores tax year, worker/contractor identifier, employer/payer EIN, form type discriminator (W-2, W-2c, 1099-NEC, 1099-MISC), filing status (draft, filed, corrected), e-file confirmation, and correction history. SSOT for ALL annual tax form reporting — unified W-2 and 1099 populations common in staffing operations where both employee and IC worker types coexist. Supports IRS e-filing, state filing, worker distribution, and year-end reconciliation.'
}

Table payroll.pto_accrual {
  pto_accrual_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each PTO accrual policy or transaction record. Serves as the primary key for the pto_accrual data product in the payroll domain.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounting_period. Business justification: PTO accruals are period-based liabilities for financial statement reporting (balance sheet liability, P&L expense). Required for accurate period close and GAAP compliance in staffing operations with a']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client assignment or placement under which PTO is being accrued. Supports staffing-specific scenarios where temporary workers accrue PTO across multiple client assignments.']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire employees accrue PTO when staffing firm is employer of record. Links benefit accruals to permanent placement for PTO policy administration and liability tracking.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: PTO liability posts to specific GL liability accounts for balance sheet reporting and expense recognition. Required for accurate GAAP-compliant financial statements in staffing operations offering PTO']
  onboarding_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to onboarding.onboarding_engagement. Business justification: PTO accrual eligibility and start dates are determined during onboarding based on policy assignment and hire date. Tracking the onboarding engagement supports accurate accrual calculations and resolve']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.order_header. Business justification: PTO accrual policies can vary by client contract and requisition in MSP/VMS programs. Some clients require specific PTO rules per engagement. Tracks which requisition\'s contract terms govern PTO accru']
  pay_stub_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll pay stub that triggered this accrual transaction. Serves as the accrual trigger linking PTO earned to a specific payroll processing event.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PTO policy governing this accrual record. Links to the policy definition that specifies accrual method, rate, cap, rollover rules, and eligibility criteria.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the worker (W-2 temporary, FTE, or contract employee) for whom this PTO accrual record applies. Links to the worker master record in TempWorks Payroll and SAP SuccessFactors.']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: W-2 workers on SOW engagements accrue PTO if engagement terms allow. Links accrual to SOW for benefit tracking during project-based work and liability management.']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: PTO accrual policies must comply with state-specific paid sick leave mandates and vacation payout laws. Staffing firms configure accrual rules per state compliance requirements - state rule validation']
  accrual_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is currently eligible to accrue PTO under the applicable policy. May be false if the worker is in the waiting period, has exceeded the accrual cap, or is classified as ineligible (e.g., 1099 contractor).']
  accrual_eligible_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the worker first became eligible to accrue PTO under the applicable policy, after satisfying any waiting period. Derived from worker_hire_date plus waiting_period_days but stored explicitly for audit and compliance purposes.']
  accrual_method varchar [note: 'Method by which PTO is earned under the applicable policy. \'hours_based\' accrues PTO per hour worked; \'tenure_based\' adjusts rate by length of service; \'front_loaded\' grants the full balance at the start of a period; \'per_period\' grants a fixed amount each pay period; \'anniversary\' grants on the worker\'s anniversary date.']
  accrual_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the accrual period for which this PTO accrual transaction applies. Together with accrual_period_start_date, defines the exact window of time covered by this accrual record.']
  accrual_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the accrual period (pay period, week, month, or year) for which this PTO accrual transaction applies. Used to align PTO accrual with payroll processing cycles.']
  accrual_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Rate at which PTO is earned, expressed as hours of PTO per hour worked (for hours-based) or hours per pay period (for per-period methods). For example, 0.033333 hours PTO per hour worked equals approximately 1 hour PTO per 30 hours worked.']
  accrual_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this PTO accrual record. \'active\' indicates ongoing accrual; \'suspended\' indicates accrual paused (e.g., unpaid leave); \'closed\' indicates the record is finalized (termination or policy end); \'pending\' indicates awaiting payroll confirmation; \'forfeited\' indicates balance was forfeited at year-end.']
  accrual_year varchar [note: 'The calendar or fiscal year to which this accrual record belongs. Used for YTD calculations, annual cap enforcement, rollover processing, and year-end W-2 reporting.']
  accrual_year_type varchar [note: 'Defines the basis for the accrual year used in this policy. \'calendar\' uses Jan 1–Dec 31; \'fiscal\' uses the company fiscal year; \'anniversary\' resets on the worker\'s hire anniversary; \'hire_date\' uses the worker\'s individual hire date as the year start.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for any manual PTO hours adjustment recorded in hours_adjusted. Required when hours_adjusted is non-zero. Examples: \'Retroactive policy correction\', \'System migration balance transfer\', \'Manager-approved exception\'.']
  balance_as_of_date date [note: 'The effective date through which the current_balance_hours has been calculated. Ensures point-in-time accuracy for balance reporting, worker inquiries, and termination payout calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this PTO accrual record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 compliance. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  current_balance_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The worker\'s available PTO balance in hours as of the balance_as_of_date. Represents the running total of accrued minus used minus forfeited hours. This is the authoritative balance used for payroll processing and worker self-service.']
  hours_accrued decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of PTO hours earned by the worker during this specific accrual period. Calculated as hours_worked multiplied by accrual_rate for hours-based policies, or the fixed grant amount for front-loaded/per-period policies.']
  hours_adjusted decimal(10,2) [note: 'Manual adjustment to PTO hours applied during this period (positive for additions, negative for corrections). Used for retroactive corrections, policy changes, or administrative overrides. Requires an adjustment reason.']
  hours_forfeited decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of PTO hours forfeited by the worker during this period due to use-it-or-lose-it policy rules, exceeding the maximum accrual cap, or rollover cap enforcement at year-end. Critical for compliance reporting in states with mandatory PTO payout laws.']
  hours_used decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of PTO hours consumed (taken) by the worker during this accrual period. Reduces the available PTO balance. Sourced from approved time-off requests processed through Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  hours_worked decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total hours worked by the worker during the accrual period that are eligible to generate PTO accrual. Used as the basis for hours-based accrual calculations.']
  max_accrual_cap_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum number of PTO hours a worker may accumulate under the applicable policy at any point in time. Once reached, no additional hours accrue until the balance is reduced. Enforced per policy and state jurisdiction.']
  payout_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of unused PTO hours paid out to the worker upon termination or separation. Populated only when payout_on_termination_flag is true and the worker has separated. Used to generate the termination PTO payout payroll transaction.']
  payout_on_termination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unused PTO hours must be paid out to the worker upon separation from employment. Mandatory in states such as California, Colorado, and Illinois. Drives termination payroll processing in TempWorks Payroll.']
  payout_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly pay rate used to calculate the monetary value of PTO payout upon termination. Typically the worker\'s regular pay rate at time of separation. Confidential financial data used in final paycheck processing.']
  policy_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the PTO policy became effective and applicable to eligible workers. Used for policy versioning and to determine which policy rules apply to a given accrual period.']
  policy_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the PTO policy expires or is superseded by a new policy version. Null for currently active policies with no defined end date. Supports policy versioning and historical accrual audits.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether this record represents a policy definition, a periodic accrual transaction, a manual adjustment, a termination payout, or a year-end rollover event. Supports the unified SSOT design of this data product.']
  rollover_cap_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum number of PTO hours that may be carried over from one accrual year to the next under a rollover-with-cap policy. Null for use-it-or-lose-it or unlimited rollover policies.']
  rollover_rule varchar [note: 'Rule governing what happens to unused PTO hours at the end of the accrual year. \'use_it_or_lose_it\' forfeits all unused hours; \'rollover_with_cap\' carries over up to rollover_cap_hours; \'unlimited_rollover\' carries over all unused hours. Must comply with state law (e.g., California prohibits use-it-or-lose-it for vacation).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this PTO accrual record. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability across TempWorks Payroll, Kronos Workforce Ready, and SAP SuccessFactors.']
  state_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code identifying the jurisdiction whose paid leave laws govern this PTO policy and accrual record. Critical for multi-state payroll compliance (e.g., CA, NY, OR, CO, IL). Determines mandatory accrual minimums, rollover rules, and payout-on-termination requirements.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date the worker\'s employment or assignment ended. Used to trigger final PTO balance calculation, determine payout-on-termination eligibility, and close the accrual record. Null for active workers.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this PTO accrual record was last modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads in the Databricks Silver Layer, and audit compliance.']
  waiting_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from the worker\'s hire or assignment start date before PTO accrual begins. Common in staffing for temporary workers (e.g., 90-day waiting period). Must comply with state-mandated sick leave laws that may restrict waiting periods.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'Employment classification of the worker for PTO eligibility purposes. Determines which PTO policy applies. W-2 temporary workers, FTEs, and contract workers may have different accrual entitlements. 1099 independent contractors are generally ineligible. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: w2_temp|fte|contract|1099|eor — promote to reference product]']
  worker_hire_date date [note: 'The date the worker was originally hired or began their first qualifying assignment. Used to calculate tenure for tenure-based accrual policies, waiting period eligibility, and anniversary-based accrual year resets.']
  ytd_hours_accrued decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative PTO hours accrued by the worker from the start of the current accrual year through the current accrual period. Used for W-2 reporting, annual cap enforcement, and compliance with state-mandated accrual limits.']
  ytd_hours_used decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative PTO hours used by the worker from the start of the current accrual year through the current accrual period. Used for annual usage reporting, compliance audits, and workforce analytics.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional record managing PTO policy definitions and worker-level accrual balances — unified SSOT for both policy rules and individual accrual tracking. Policy attributes: policy name, accrual method (hours-based, tenure-based, front-loaded), accrual rate per hour worked or per period, maximum accrual cap, rollover rules (use-it-or-lose-it, rollover with cap, unlimited), waiting period before accrual begins, payout-on-termination flag, state jurisdiction applicability, worker classification eligibility (W-2 temp, FTE, contract), and policy effective dates. Accrual transaction attributes: worker identifier, accrual period, hours accrued, hours used, hours forfeited, current PTO balance, YTD accrued and used hours, accrual trigger (pay stub reference), and balance as-of date. Supports compliance with state-mandated paid sick leave laws (California, New York, Oregon, etc.), PTO payout on separation, and staffing-specific scenarios where temporary workers accrue PTO across multiple client assignments.'
}

Table payroll.garnishment_order {
  garnishment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a single court-ordered or agency-mandated wage garnishment record in the TempWorks Payroll system. TRANSACTION_HEADER primary key.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.assignment. Business justification: Garnishment orders follow worker across assignments. Links order to current assignment for payroll withholding processing, remittance tracking, and compliance with court orders during active placement']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire employees may have garnishment orders. When staffing firm processes payroll for direct hires, garnishments must be withheld and remitted. Links order to permanent placement.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Garnishments post to liability accounts (garnishment payable) for balance sheet reporting and remittance tracking. Required for accurate liability reporting and compliance with court-ordered withholdi']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll company or legal entity within TempWorks Payroll responsible for processing this garnishment. Relevant for multi-entity staffing organizations where workers may be employed under different legal entities (e.g., EOR entities, co-employment entities).']
  onboarding_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to onboarding.onboarding_engagement. Business justification: Garnishment orders received during pre-employment screening or at hire must be linked to onboarding engagement for compliance documentation. Supports audit trail showing when garnishment was identifie']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker (employee or contractor) whose wages are subject to this garnishment order. Links to the worker master record in TempWorks Payroll / SAP SuccessFactors.']
  regulatory_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_violation. Business justification: Garnishment orders may result from regulatory violations (child support enforcement failures, tax liens, court-ordered restitution). Compliance tracking links enforcement actions to payroll deduction ']
  administrative_fee_amount decimal(8,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the per-pay-period administrative fee the employer is permitted to charge the employee for processing the garnishment, as allowed by applicable state law. Typically $1–$5 per pay period. Zero if the state does not permit an administrative fee.']
  arrears_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of past-due arrears included in the garnishment order, separate from the current ongoing obligation. Particularly relevant for child support orders where arrears withholding may be calculated at a different rate than current support.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this garnishment order record was first created in the TempWorks Payroll system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker per SOC 2 Type II and internal data governance requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which withholding under this garnishment order must begin, as specified by the issuing authority. Payroll must begin deducting no later than the first pay period that includes this date.']
  employer_response_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the employer submitted its response to the issuing authority. Used to verify timely compliance with mandatory response deadlines and to support audit documentation.']
  employer_response_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the employer must respond to the issuing authority confirming receipt and intent to comply with the garnishment order, or to contest the order if applicable. Typically within 10–15 business days of receipt for IWOs.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the garnishment order expires or is scheduled to end. Null for open-ended orders (e.g., ongoing child support). Payroll must cease withholding after this date unless a renewal order is received.']
  garnishment_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the garnishment order. Active: withholding is in progress. Suspended: temporarily halted per court or agency instruction. Satisfied: total obligation has been fully paid. Terminated: order cancelled or revoked. Pending: received but not yet activated in payroll.']
  garnishment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the garnishment by legal category. Determines applicable withholding limits and priority rules under federal and state law. Values: child_support (Title IV-D orders), creditor_garnishment (civil court judgment), student_loan (Department of Education administrative garnishment), tax_levy (IRS or state tax levy), bankruptcy (Chapter 13 trustee payment).']
  is_arrears_order boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this garnishment order includes or is exclusively for past-due arrears (True) versus a current ongoing obligation only (False). Relevant for child support orders where arrears withholding may trigger a higher CCPA cap (up to 65% vs. 50%).']
  is_income_withholding_order boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this garnishment is an Income Withholding Order (IWO) issued under Title IV-D of the Social Security Act (True). IWOs have specific federal processing requirements including mandatory employer response timelines and standardized form usage (OMB 0970-0154).']
  issuing_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the court, government agency, or administrative body that issued the garnishment order (e.g., \'Cook County Circuit Court\', \'IRS — Automated Collection System\', \'Illinois Department of Healthcare and Family Services — Child Support Division\').']
  issuing_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state or territory code of the jurisdiction that issued the garnishment order. Used to apply the correct state-specific withholding limits and priority rules, which may be more protective than federal CCPA limits.']
  iwo_form_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the standardized federal Income Withholding Order form used (e.g., \'OMB 0970-0154 Rev. 06/2021\'). Required for audit and compliance tracking of IWO processing. Null when is_income_withholding_order is False.']
  max_withholding_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'The maximum percentage of disposable earnings that may be withheld under this order, as governed by the Consumer Credit Protection Act (CCPA) and FLSA. Stored as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.50 = 50%). Payroll must not exceed this cap even if the ordered amount would otherwise require it. Defaults vary by garnishment type (e.g., 50–65% for child support, 25% for creditor garnishments).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for payroll staff to record additional context, special instructions from the issuing authority, or internal processing notes related to this garnishment order (e.g., \'Worker contested order — awaiting court ruling\', \'SDU requires paper check until EFT enrollment complete\').']
  order_number varchar [note: 'The externally-issued case or order number assigned by the issuing court, agency, or government authority (e.g., court docket number, IRS levy notice number, state child support case number). This is the primary business identifier used to cross-reference with the issuing authority.']
  priority_order varchar [note: 'Integer ranking that determines the sequence in which this garnishment is applied when a worker has multiple simultaneous garnishment orders. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Child support orders are federally mandated to take priority over other garnishments. Used by payroll to allocate available disposable earnings across competing orders.']
  received_date date [note: 'The date on which the employer (Staffing Hr) received the garnishment order from the issuing authority. Triggers the employer\'s legal obligation to begin withholding within the required timeframe. Distinct from the effective_date specified in the order.']
  remittance_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number for EFT/ACH remittance to the garnishment payee. Stored in masked or tokenized form per PCI/security policy. Null when remittance_method is \'check\'.']
  remittance_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for mailing garnishment remittance payments to the payee. Used when electronic remittance (EFT/EFT) is not available.']
  remittance_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (suite, PO Box, unit number) for the garnishment remittance payee mailing address.']
  remittance_city varchar [note: 'City of the garnishment remittance payee mailing address.']
  remittance_frequency varchar [note: 'How frequently withheld garnishment funds must be remitted to the payee. Most child support SDUs require remittance within 7 business days of the pay date. IRS levies may require per-payroll remittance.']
  remittance_method varchar [note: 'Method by which withheld garnishment funds are transmitted to the remittance payee. EFT/ACH is required for most State Disbursement Units (SDUs) under Title IV-D. Check is used for payees that do not accept electronic payment.']
  remittance_payee_name varchar [note: 'Name of the entity to whom withheld funds must be remitted (e.g., \'State Disbursement Unit — Illinois\', \'IRS — ACS\', \'Chapter 13 Trustee — Northern District of Illinois\'). Used to generate the payable in Oracle NetSuite ERP accounts payable.']
  remittance_reference_code varchar [note: 'Payee-assigned reference code or FIPS code that must appear on each remittance payment to allow the receiving agency to correctly apply the payment to the correct case (e.g., SDU case number, IRS notice number, FIPS employer code).']
  remittance_routing_number varchar [note: 'ABA routing transit number for EFT/ACH remittance to the garnishment payee. Nine-digit number identifying the receiving financial institution. Null when remittance_method is \'check\'.']
  remittance_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state or territory code of the garnishment remittance payee mailing address.']
  remittance_zip varchar [note: 'ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code of the garnishment remittance payee mailing address.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier assigned by TempWorks Payroll to this garnishment order in the operational system of record. Used for lineage tracing, reconciliation, and re-ingestion deduplication in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason the garnishment order was suspended (e.g., \'Court-ordered stay pending appeal\', \'Worker on unpaid leave — no disposable earnings\', \'Agency-issued suspension notice received\'). Populated only when status is \'suspended\'.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which the garnishment order was terminated, satisfied, or revoked, resulting in cessation of withholding. Populated when status transitions to \'satisfied\' or \'terminated\'. Null while the order is active or pending.']
  total_obligation_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'The total dollar amount owed under this garnishment order as specified by the issuing authority (e.g., total judgment debt, total child support arrears). Null for ongoing orders with no defined total (e.g., current child support with no arrears). Used to determine when the order is satisfied.']
  total_withheld_to_date decimal(14,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar amount withheld from the worker\'s pay and remitted under this garnishment order since the effective date. Updated each payroll cycle. Used to track progress toward satisfying the total obligation and to determine when the order is fully paid.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this garnishment order record was most recently modified in the TempWorks Payroll system. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and for audit trail compliance.']
  withholding_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount to be withheld from the worker\'s pay per pay period when withholding_method is \'fixed_amount\'. Null when withholding_method is \'percentage\'. Expressed in USD.']
  withholding_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the garnishment is calculated as a fixed dollar amount per pay period or as a percentage of the worker\'s disposable earnings. Determines which of the two withholding fields (withholding_amount or withholding_percentage) is operative.']
  withholding_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of the worker\'s disposable earnings to be withheld per pay period when withholding_method is \'percentage\'. Stored as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.25 = 25%). Null when withholding_method is \'fixed_amount\'.']

  Note: 'Master record for court-ordered or agency-mandated wage garnishments applied to worker pay. Captures garnishment type (child support, creditor garnishment, student loan, tax levy, bankruptcy), issuing authority, case or order number, ordered amount or percentage, maximum withholding limit (per CCPA/FLSA disposable earnings rules), priority order when multiple garnishments exist, remittance payee, remittance address, effective date, expiration date, and garnishment status (active, suspended, satisfied, terminated).'
}

Table payroll.pay_adjustment {
  pay_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pay_adjustment']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounting_period. Business justification: Payroll adjustments must be tracked by accounting period for audit trail, period close, and prior period adjustment reporting. Required for accurate financial statement preparation and SOX compliance ']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staffing assignment or placement associated with the pay adjustment. Ties the correction to the specific client engagement and job order context.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Pay adjustments (corrections, retro pay) must reference governing MSA for client billing adjustments and dispute resolution. Required to determine if adjustment triggers client invoice revision under ']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire placements may require payroll adjustments for signing bonuses, salary corrections, or guarantee period true-ups. Links adjustments to permanent placement for financial reconciliation.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the GL journal entry batch in Oracle NetSuite ERP to which this adjustment was posted. Supports financial reconciliation and audit trail between payroll and accounting systems.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.order_header. Business justification: Pay adjustments must link to job orders for client billing corrections, invoice adjustments, and accurate margin impact analysis when rates or hours are retroactively corrected. Essential for financia']
  original_pay_stub_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original pay stub or paycheck record that this adjustment corrects, voids, or reissues. Maintains the audit trail linking the correction back to the source pay event.']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pay run in which this adjustment was originally processed or to which it is being applied. Links the adjustment to the parent payroll cycle in TempWorks Payroll.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker (W-2 employee or 1099 independent contractor) whose pay is being adjusted. PARTY_REFERENCE category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role requirements.']
  regulatory_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_violation. Business justification: Pay adjustments are often triggered by regulatory violations (wage/hour violations, misclassification back-pay, overtime corrections). Staffing firms track remediation payments linked to specific viol']
  adjusted_deduction_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Corrected total deduction amount applied in this adjustment. Covers benefit deductions, garnishments, and other pre/post-tax deductions that were incorrectly processed in the original pay run.']
  adjusted_dt_pay decimal(18,4) [note: 'Corrected double time (DT) earnings component of the adjustment. Applicable for assignments with contractual or state-mandated double time provisions.']
  adjusted_fica_employee decimal(18,4) [note: 'Corrected employee-side FICA (Social Security and Medicare) withholding amount. Required for tax correction adjustments and amended W-2 (W-2c) filings with the IRS.']
  adjusted_fica_employer decimal(18,4) [note: 'Corrected employer-side FICA matching contribution amount. Required for employer tax liability recalculation and amended payroll tax filings (Form 941-X).']
  adjusted_gross_pay decimal(18,4) [note: 'The corrected gross pay amount after the adjustment is applied. Represents the new gross earnings figure that replaces or supplements the original pay stub amount.']
  adjusted_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'The corrected number of hours being applied in this adjustment. Used for missed hours corrections and retroactive rate change recalculations. Supports Kronos Workforce Ready time and attendance reconciliation.']
  adjusted_net_pay decimal(18,4) [note: 'The corrected net pay amount after all adjustments to gross pay, taxes, and deductions. Represents the actual dollar amount owed to or recovered from the worker. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET — net total.']
  adjusted_ot_pay decimal(18,4) [note: 'Corrected overtime (OT) earnings component of the adjustment. Tracks overtime pay corrections separately to support FLSA overtime compliance auditing and reporting.']
  adjusted_pay_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The corrected pay rate to be applied in this adjustment. Used for retroactive rate change adjustments to document the rate that should have been applied.']
  adjusted_regular_pay decimal(18,4) [note: 'Corrected regular (straight-time) earnings component of the adjustment. Captures the regular hours pay correction separately from overtime and other pay components.']
  adjusted_tax_withholding decimal(18,4) [note: 'The corrected total employee tax withholding amount applied in this adjustment. Covers federal, state, and local income tax corrections. Supports IRS tax correction and amended W-2 processing.']
  adjustment_date date [note: 'The business effective date on which the payroll adjustment is applied. Represents the real-world event date of the correction, distinct from the record creation timestamp. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable identifier for the payroll adjustment (e.g., ADJ-2024-000123). Used in payroll operations, audit correspondence, and SOX control documentation. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the payroll adjustment record. Tracks the adjustment through its lifecycle from initial entry through approval, processing, and final disposition. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payroll adjustment by its business purpose. Values: pay_correction (general earnings correction), void_reissue (check voided and reissued), retro_rate_change (retroactive pay rate change applied), missed_hours (hours omitted from original pay run), tax_correction (withholding or tax filing correction), deduction_correction (benefit or garnishment deduction correction). CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the payroll manager or authorized approver who approved this adjustment. Required for SOX-adjacent dual-control and segregation-of-duties compliance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payroll adjustment was formally approved by the authorized approver. Supports SOX audit trail and segregation-of-duties documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payroll adjustment record was first created in the system. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role requirements. Supports SOX audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this adjustment record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency payroll for international EOR engagements.']
  disbursement_method varchar [note: 'The payment disbursement method used to deliver the adjusted net pay to the worker. Determines the payment processing channel for off-cycle adjustments and reissued checks.']
  gl_post_date date [note: 'The date on which the payroll adjustment entries were posted to the General Ledger in Oracle NetSuite ERP. Used for financial period close reconciliation and accrual management.']
  gross_pay_delta decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net difference between adjusted_gross_pay and original_gross_pay. Positive values indicate additional pay owed to the worker; negative values indicate overpayment recovery. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET — adjustment component.']
  is_off_cycle boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment is processed as an off-cycle (out-of-band) payment rather than being included in the next scheduled pay run. Off-cycle adjustments incur additional processing costs and require separate disbursement.']
  is_reissue boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment record represents the reissued replacement check following a void. Paired with is_void to track the complete void-and-reissue cycle.']
  is_void boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment represents a full void of the original paycheck. When true, the original pay stub is cancelled and a reissue record is expected. Supports void/reissue audit trail and SOX controls.']
