  original_gross_pay decimal(18,4) [note: 'The gross pay amount from the original pay stub prior to any adjustment. Serves as the baseline for calculating the adjustment delta and supports audit trail requirements. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET category.']
  original_pay_date date [note: 'The check date or pay date of the original pay stub being corrected. Critical for retroactive adjustments and void/reissue scenarios to identify the source payroll event.']
  original_pay_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The pay rate (bill rate minus spread) in effect on the original pay stub. Required for retroactive rate change adjustments to document the rate that was incorrectly applied.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the pay period to which this adjustment applies. Used to correctly attribute the correction to the appropriate payroll period for tax and financial reporting.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the pay period to which this adjustment applies. May differ from the current pay period when processing retroactive corrections or missed hours from a prior period.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payroll adjustment was fully processed and posted in TempWorks Payroll. Marks the completion of the adjustment lifecycle.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific reason for the payroll adjustment (e.g., RATE_ERR, HOURS_MISS, TAX_RECALC, VOID_LOST_CHECK, RETRO_PROMO). Supports audit trail, SOX controls, and payroll error trend analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RATE_ERR|HOURS_MISS|TAX_RECALC|VOID_LOST_CHECK|RETRO_PROMO|DEDUCT_ERR|GARNISH_ADJ|BONUS_CORR|OT_RECALC|PTO_ADJ — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative explanation of why the payroll adjustment was initiated. Provides context beyond the reason code for audit review, SOX documentation, and payroll operations investigation.']
  requires_w2c boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment requires the issuance of an amended W-2 (Form W-2c) to the worker and the IRS. Set to true when the adjustment affects a prior tax year\'s reported wages or withholdings.']
  tax_quarter varchar [note: 'The tax quarter (1–4) to which this adjustment applies. Required for quarterly payroll tax filing corrections (IRS Form 941-X, FUTA Form 940, SUTA state filings).']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The tax year to which this payroll adjustment applies. Critical for W-2c (amended W-2) and 1099 correction filings when the adjustment crosses calendar year boundaries.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payroll adjustment record was last modified. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role requirements. Tracks changes through the approval and processing workflow.']
  work_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code where the worker performed the work subject to this adjustment. Determines applicable state income tax, SUTA, and workers compensation rules for multi-state payroll compliance.']
  worker_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the worker for tax and payroll processing purposes. W-2 (Wage and Tax Statement employee), 1099 (Independent Contractor), or EOR (Employer of Record). Determines applicable tax withholding rules and adjustment processing logic.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing manual corrections, retroactive pay adjustments, voids, and reissues applied outside of a standard pay run. Covers adjustment types including pay correction, void and reissue, retroactive rate change, missed hours correction, tax correction, and deduction correction. Stores original pay stub reference, adjustment reason code, adjusted earnings or deduction amounts, approver, approval date, and adjustment processing status. Maintains audit trail for payroll corrections and supports SOX-adjacent controls.'
}

Table payroll.tax_withholding_election {
  tax_withholding_election_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each tax withholding election record. Primary key for the tax_withholding_election data product in the payroll domain.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active placement or assignment associated with this withholding election, enabling assignment-level tax configuration for multi-assignment workers.']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire employees submit W-4 elections when staffing firm processes payroll under PEO or transition arrangements. Links tax elections to permanent placement for withholding compliance.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Withholding elections are entity-specific for multi-entity employers with different EINs. Required for accurate tax withholding and W-2 reporting in staffing operations with multiple legal entities em']
  onboarding_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to onboarding.onboarding_engagement. Business justification: Tax withholding elections are collected during onboarding. Linking to onboarding engagement supports IRS compliance verification that W-4/state tax forms were obtained before first paycheck, required ']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker (W-2 employee or 1099 independent contractor) whose tax withholding elections are captured in this record. Links to the candidate or worker master record.']
  superseded_by_election_tax_withholding_election_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer tax_withholding_election record that replaced this one. Populated when election_status = \'superseded\'. Enables forward-chaining of the election history for audit trails.']
  work_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.work_authorization. Business justification: Tax withholding setup requires work authorization verification to determine tax treaty eligibility, withholding method for visa holders, and FICA exemption status. Critical for international worker pa']
  worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Tax withholding elections (W-4) are only applicable to W-2 employees, not independent contractors. Worker classification determines withholding eligibility and form type - misclassified workers trigge']
  additional_withholding_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional flat dollar amount the worker requests to be withheld from each paycheck beyond the calculated withholding. Entered in Step 4c of the 2020+ W-4 or the equivalent line on pre-2020 forms. Applied per pay period.']
  allowances_claimed varchar [note: 'Number of withholding allowances claimed by the worker on pre-2020 W-4 forms. Each allowance reduces the amount of federal income tax withheld. Applicable only for W-4 forms prior to the 2020 redesign. Null for 2020+ W-4 forms which use the step-based approach.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee ID of the payroll supervisor or manager who reviewed and approved this withholding election entry. Required for IRS lock-in letter overrides and exempt status elections as part of internal controls.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this withholding election record was first created in the payroll system. Supports audit trail, SOC 2 Type II compliance, and IRS record retention requirements for withholding forms.']
  deductions_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of itemized or other deductions entered in Step 4b of the 2020+ W-4. Reduces withholding by accounting for deductions that exceed the standard deduction. Null for pre-2020 W-4 forms.']
  dependent_credit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of qualifying dependent credits entered in Step 3 of the 2020+ redesigned W-4. Reduces the amount of income subject to withholding. Includes child tax credit ($2,000 per qualifying child under 17) and other dependent credits ($500 per other dependent). Null for pre-2020 W-4 forms.']
  document_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the physical or electronic withholding form document stored in the document management system (e.g., DocuSign CLM or SAP SuccessFactors document repository). Enables retrieval of the original signed form for audit and compliance verification.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this withholding election becomes effective and begins driving tax withholding calculations in pay runs. Aligns with IRS requirement that new W-4 elections take effect no later than the start of the first payroll period ending on or after the 30th day after the form is received.']
  election_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by payroll staff regarding this withholding election. May include reasons for IRS lock-in overrides, documentation of verbal instructions, or notes on unusual withholding configurations.']
  election_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this withholding election record, used for audit trails, payroll processing references, and worker communications (e.g., TWE-2024-00123).']
  election_source varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which the withholding election was submitted. Paper form = physical W-4 or state form submitted; Electronic self-service = worker submitted via online portal (e.g., SAP SuccessFactors ESS); Employer default = system default applied when no form received; IRS lock-in = IRS-mandated withholding rate overriding worker election; Verbal instruction = documented verbal request.']
  election_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this withholding election. Active = currently driving withholding calculations; Superseded = replaced by a newer election; Pending = submitted but not yet effective; Cancelled = voided before taking effect; Expired = past the expiration date.']
  election_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for withholding elections for the same worker and jurisdiction. Increments each time the worker submits a new withholding form. Enables tracking of election history and supersession chain for audit and compliance purposes.']
  entered_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee ID of the payroll staff member or system process that entered this withholding election into TempWorks Payroll. Supports audit trail requirements for payroll compliance and SOC 2 Type II controls.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this withholding election expires and is no longer valid. Applicable for exempt-from-withholding elections which expire annually (February 15 of the following year per IRS rules), or for state-specific elections with defined validity periods.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'The worker\'s federal or state tax filing status as declared on the withholding form. Drives the applicable standard deduction and tax bracket used in withholding calculations. Values align with IRS filing status categories.']
  flat_withholding_rate_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'Fixed percentage rate applied to gross wages for income tax withholding when the withholding method is \'percentage\' or \'flat_amount\'. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2200 = 22%). Used for supplemental wage withholding at the IRS flat supplemental rate or for state flat-rate withholding.']
  form_type varchar [note: 'The specific tax withholding form type submitted by the worker. Examples include W-4 (federal), W-4P (pension), state equivalents (e.g., CA DE-4, NY IT-2104, IL W-4, TX no state income tax). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: W-4|W-4P|W-4S|W-4V|DE-4|IT-2104|IL-W-4|VA-4|NC-4|GA-G-4 — promote to reference product]']
  irs_lock_in_allowances varchar [note: 'The maximum number of withholding allowances permitted by the IRS lock-in letter, overriding the worker\'s own W-4 election. Employer must not allow the worker to claim more allowances than this value.']
  irs_lock_in_filing_status varchar [note: 'The filing status mandated by the IRS lock-in letter, overriding the worker\'s own W-4 election. Populated only when an IRS lock-in letter is in effect. Employer must use this status for withholding calculations.']
  irs_lock_in_letter_date date [note: 'Date of the IRS lock-in letter received by the employer mandating a specific withholding rate for this worker. When present, the employer must withhold at the IRS-specified rate regardless of the worker\'s W-4 elections. Null if no lock-in letter applies.']
  is_exempt_from_withholding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker has claimed exemption from federal or state income tax withholding. True = worker claims exempt status and no income tax will be withheld. Exempt status expires February 15 each year and must be renewed. Employer must retain the exempt W-4 and notify IRS if required.']
  is_state_exempt_from_withholding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker has claimed exemption from state income tax withholding on the state withholding form. Exempt criteria and renewal requirements vary by state.']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this election applies to federal, state, or local tax withholding. Federal elections are governed by IRS W-4; state elections by equivalent state withholding forms; local elections by municipality-specific forms.']
  locality_name varchar [note: 'Name of the local taxing jurisdiction (city, county, or municipality) for local income tax withholding elections. Applicable in states with local income taxes such as Ohio, Pennsylvania, Kentucky, and New York City.']
  multiple_jobs_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker checked the Step 2 multiple jobs checkbox on the 2020+ W-4, signaling they hold multiple concurrent jobs or their spouse also works. When true, withholding is calculated using the higher withholding rate tables to prevent under-withholding.']
  nonresident_alien_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is a nonresident alien (NRA) for U.S. tax purposes. NRA workers are subject to special withholding rules under IRS Publication 15 and must follow specific W-4 instructions (cannot claim exempt, must add an additional amount to wages for withholding calculation). Relevant for staffing firms placing international workers.']
  other_income_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of other non-wage income (interest, dividends, retirement income) entered in Step 4a of the 2020+ W-4. Increases withholding to cover tax on non-wage income. Null for pre-2020 W-4 forms.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'The pay frequency associated with this withholding election, used to annualize wages for withholding table lookups. Must match the worker\'s actual pay frequency in the pay run. Drives the correct withholding amount per period.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the withholding election form was received by the employer or payroll department. Used to determine the required effective date per IRS rules (must take effect no later than the first payroll period ending on or after the 30th day after receipt).']
  state_additional_withholding_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional flat dollar amount the worker requests to be withheld for state income tax beyond the calculated state withholding. Applied per pay period in addition to the standard state withholding calculation.']
  state_allowances_claimed varchar [note: 'Number of withholding allowances claimed on the state withholding form. Many states still use an allowance-based system independent of the federal W-4 redesign (e.g., California DE-4, New York IT-2104). Null for states with no income tax or states that have adopted the federal W-4.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation identifying the state jurisdiction for state or local withholding elections. Null for federal elections. Supports multi-state payroll compliance across all states where Staffing Hr places workers.']
  state_filing_status varchar [note: 'The worker\'s filing status as declared on the state withholding form, which may differ from the federal filing status. State-specific values vary by jurisdiction (e.g., California uses single, married, head of household with different withholding tables). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single|married|married_higher_rate|head_of_household|exempt — promote to reference product]']
  supplemental_wage_method varchar [note: 'Method used to withhold income tax on supplemental wages (bonuses, commissions, overtime, per diem above IRS threshold). Flat rate = mandatory 22% federal flat rate for supplemental wages under $1M; Aggregate = combined with regular wages using withholding tables; Optional flat = employer elects flat rate when not mandatory.']
  treaty_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country with which the worker claims a U.S. tax treaty benefit, potentially reducing or eliminating withholding on certain income types. Applicable for nonresident alien workers claiming treaty exemptions via Form 8233 or W-8BEN.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this withholding election record was last modified. Tracks changes to election data for audit trail and data lineage purposes in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  w4_form_version_year varchar [note: 'The tax year version of the W-4 or equivalent withholding form submitted by the worker. Critical because the 2020 redesigned W-4 eliminated allowances and introduced a new step-based structure. Pre-2020 forms use allowances; 2020+ forms use Step 3 dependent credits and Step 4 adjustments.']
  withholding_method varchar [note: 'The calculation method used to compute income tax withholding for this worker. Percentage method uses IRS Publication 15-T tables; Wage bracket method uses bracket tables; Flat amount applies a fixed dollar amount; Annualized projects annual income for withholding; Cumulative adjusts for year-to-date withholding.']
  worker_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the worker for tax withholding purposes. W-2 = employee subject to income tax withholding and FICA; 1099 = independent contractor (IC) not subject to employer withholding; EOR = Employer of Record arrangement where the EOR entity handles withholding. Drives whether withholding elections are applicable.']

  Note: 'Master record capturing a worker\'s federal and state tax withholding elections as submitted via W-4 (federal) and equivalent state withholding forms. Stores filing status (single, married, head of household), number of allowances or Step 3 dependent credits (post-2020 W-4), additional withholding amount, exempt from withholding flag, state withholding form type, state-specific elections, effective date, and election source (paper form, electronic self-service). Drives tax withholding calculation in each pay run.'
}

Table payroll.eor_arrangement {
  eor_arrangement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for eor_arrangement']
  approved_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: EOR arrangements require approval by legal/operations staff. Role-prefixed for clarity. Reuses existing approved_by column pattern. Required for contract management, risk management, and compliance ov']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.assignment. Business justification: EOR arrangements define legal employer for assignments. Links assignment to EOR arrangement for payroll processing, tax filing, benefits administration, and co-employment compliance under specific cli']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for whom workers are placed under this EOR or co-employment arrangement. Links to the client account master in Bullhorn CRM.']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: EOR/PEO arrangements specify which credential packages apply to covered workers; defines compliance responsibility split between co-employers. MSA terms dictate who manages BGC, I-9, licenses under co']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire placements under PEO/EOR models require link to arrangement. Defines legal employer entity, payroll responsibility, tax filing, and benefits provision for permanent placements.']
  i9_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.i9_verification. Business justification: EOR arrangements define which legal entity is responsible for I-9 verification and retention. Co-employment models require clear I-9 responsibility assignment - critical for federal contractor complia']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: EOR arrangements define which legal entity is the employer of record for tax, compliance, and financial reporting. Required for multi-entity staffing operations with co-employment or PEO models to tra']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: EOR arrangements often cover specific client locations or sites. Essential for determining which locations operate under co-employment vs. standard staffing models, affecting payroll processing, tax o']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: EOR arrangements are frequently structured at the managed program level (MSP/RPO programs). Critical for program governance, determining employment model, and ensuring compliance with program-specific']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: SOW-based workers may be under EOR arrangement when co-employment risk is high or client requires legal employer buffer. Links SOW engagement to EOR for compliance and payroll.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: EOR scope and worker coverage are defined at SOW level; critical for determining which workers fall under co-employment, tax responsibility allocation, and benefits administration. SOW defines operati']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: EOR (Employer of Record) arrangements are commonly outsourced to third-party vendors who become the legal employer. Payroll processing, tax filing responsibility, and workers\' comp liability tracking ']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the VMS program (e.g., Beeline) under which this EOR arrangement operates, if the client manages contingent workforce through a VMS platform. Links to the client.vms_program product.']
  wc_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the workers compensation insurance policy assigned to cover workers under this EOR arrangement. Links to the payroll.wc_policy product for premium and coverage details.']
  worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: EOR arrangements are structured based on worker classification determinations. Employee classifications trigger EOR/PEO models with full co-employment, while IC classifications use different legal str']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this EOR or co-employment arrangement was formally approved by the designated internal authority. Distinct from the effective_date which marks when the arrangement becomes operationally active.']
  arrangement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing special terms, client-specific requirements, legal caveats, or operational notes associated with this EOR or co-employment arrangement. Reviewed during arrangement setup and audits.']
  arrangement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the EOR or co-employment arrangement, used in client communications, contracts, and payroll system references. Sourced from TempWorks Payroll arrangement setup. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  arrangement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the EOR or co-employment arrangement. Active = currently in force; pending = awaiting execution or approval; suspended = temporarily paused; terminated = permanently ended; draft = not yet executed. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  arrangement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the legal employer arrangement. EOR = Staffing Hr is the sole legal employer; co_employment = shared employer responsibilities with client; PEO = Professional Employer Organization model; joint_employment = joint liability structure; IC_engagement = Independent Contractor engagement model. CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.']
  benefit_plan_sponsor varchar [note: 'Identifies which party is responsible for sponsoring and administering employee benefit plans (health, dental, vision, 401k) for workers under this arrangement. Staffing_hr = Staffing Hr sponsors; client = client sponsors; shared = joint sponsorship; none = no benefits provided.']
  benefits_eligibility_waiting_days varchar [note: 'Number of days a worker must be employed under this EOR arrangement before becoming eligible for employer-sponsored benefits. Drives benefits enrollment timing in SAP SuccessFactors.']
  burden_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Total employer burden rate expressed as a percentage of gross wages, encompassing FICA employer share, FUTA, SUTA, workers compensation premium, and benefits costs allocated under this arrangement. Used for pricing, billing, and margin analysis.']
  co_employer_client_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the client entity sharing employer responsibilities in a co-employment or joint employment arrangement. Null for pure EOR arrangements where Staffing Hr is the sole employer.']
  covered_worker_count varchar [note: 'Number of workers currently covered under this EOR or co-employment arrangement. Used for capacity planning, premium calculations, and arrangement scope reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EOR arrangement record was first created in the payroll system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 Type II compliance requirements. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  eeoc_reporting_responsibility varchar [note: 'Designates which party is responsible for EEOC workforce composition reporting (EEO-1) for workers under this arrangement. Determines whether workers are counted in Staffing Hr or client EEOC filings.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the EOR or co-employment arrangement becomes legally binding and payroll processing responsibilities take effect. EFFECTIVE_FROM category per MASTER_AGREEMENT role.']
  everify_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether E-Verify electronic employment eligibility verification is required for workers onboarded under this EOR arrangement, based on client contract terms, federal contractor status, or state law mandates.']
  fica_responsibility varchar [note: 'Designates which party is responsible for remitting FICA (Social Security and Medicare) employer-side tax contributions for workers under this arrangement. Critical for multi-entity and co-employment tax compliance.']
  futa_responsibility varchar [note: 'Designates which party is responsible for filing and remitting FUTA federal unemployment taxes for workers under this arrangement.']
  i9_verification_responsibility varchar [note: 'Designates which party is responsible for completing and retaining I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification forms for workers under this arrangement. Critical for Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) compliance.']
  indemnification_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope of liability indemnification terms under this arrangement. Full = Staffing Hr indemnifies client for all employment-related liabilities; partial = shared indemnification with defined limits; none = no indemnification provided.']
  is_multi_state boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this EOR arrangement spans workers in multiple U.S. states, triggering multi-state payroll tax compliance requirements, nexus analysis, and state-specific wage and hour law obligations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EOR arrangement record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data governance requirements.']
  legal_employer_ein varchar [note: 'Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) of the legal employer entity responsible for payroll tax filings, W-2 and 1099 issuance under this arrangement. Required for IRS tax reporting compliance.']
  legal_employer_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the entity designated as the Employer of Record responsible for payroll tax filing, W-2 issuance, workers compensation, and employment law compliance for workers under this arrangement. May be Staffing Hr or a subsidiary entity.']
  markup_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Markup percentage applied over pay rate to derive the bill rate for workers under this EOR arrangement. Reflects the spread between pay rate and bill rate as a percentage, covering burden, overhead, and margin.']
  msa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Master Service Agreement (MSA) governing the overall client relationship under which this EOR arrangement operates. Links to the executed MSA in DocuSign CLM.']
  n1099_filing_responsibility varchar [note: 'Designates which party is responsible for issuing 1099 tax forms to independent contractors under this arrangement. Applicable only when worker_classification includes 1099_IC workers.']
  ofccp_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this EOR arrangement is subject to OFCCP affirmative action and equal employment opportunity requirements due to federal contractor or subcontractor status of the client.']
  ot_policy varchar [note: 'Overtime calculation policy applied to workers under this arrangement. FLSA_standard = federal 40-hour weekly OT threshold; state_specific = state law OT rules (e.g., California daily OT); client_defined = client-negotiated OT terms; none = exempt workers only.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which workers under this EOR arrangement are paid. Drives payroll run scheduling in TempWorks Payroll and determines pay period calendar configuration.']
  payroll_processing_entity_name varchar [note: 'Name of the entity responsible for executing payroll processing, tax withholding, and disbursement under this arrangement. May differ from the legal employer entity in multi-entity or outsourced payroll structures.']
  payroll_state_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of U.S. state codes (e.g., CA,TX,NY) where payroll is processed under this arrangement. Drives multi-state tax compliance, SUTA rate application, and state-specific wage and hour law adherence.']
  per_diem_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether workers under this EOR arrangement are eligible to receive per diem allowances for travel, lodging, or meals. Drives per diem processing in payroll runs.']
  pto_accrual_policy varchar [note: 'PTO accrual policy applied to W-2 workers under this EOR arrangement. Accrual_based = hours-based accrual; front_loaded = lump-sum grant at period start; none = no PTO benefit; client_defined = client-specific PTO terms.']
  sow_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Statement of Work (SOW) associated with this EOR arrangement, if applicable. Used for project-based or managed service engagements where the EOR arrangement is scoped to a specific SOW.']
  suta_responsibility varchar [note: 'Designates which party is responsible for filing and remitting SUTA state unemployment taxes for workers under this arrangement. Multi-state arrangements may have varying SUTA responsibilities by jurisdiction.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the EOR or co-employment arrangement ends and payroll processing responsibilities cease. Nullable for open-ended arrangements. EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category per MASTER_AGREEMENT role.']
  w2_filing_responsibility varchar [note: 'Designates which party is responsible for issuing W-2 Wage and Tax Statements to workers at year-end under this arrangement. Determines which EIN appears on worker W-2 forms.']
  wc_policy_responsibility varchar [note: 'Designates which party is responsible for maintaining and funding workers compensation insurance coverage for workers under this arrangement. Staffing_hr = Staffing Hr carries the policy; client = client carries the policy; shared = cost-sharing arrangement.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'Tax and employment classification of workers covered under this arrangement. W2_employee = standard W-2 employee; 1099_IC = Independent Contractor; W2_temp = temporary W-2 worker; W2_perm = permanent W-2 employee. Drives tax withholding, benefit eligibility, and compliance obligations.']

  Note: 'Master record defining Employer of Record (EOR) and co-employment payroll arrangements where Staffing Hr acts as the legal employer of record for workers placed at client sites. Captures arrangement type (EOR, co-employment, PEO), client reference, worker population covered, legal employer entity, payroll processing entity, benefit plan sponsorship responsibility, workers comp policy assignment, tax filing responsibility allocation, liability indemnification terms, effective dates, and arrangement status. Supports complex multi-entity payroll structures.'
}

Table payroll.arrangement_compliance_requirement {
  arrangement_compliance_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for this arrangement-requirement compliance obligation record']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the compliance requirement']
  payroll_eor_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the EOR or co-employment arrangement subject to this compliance requirement']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance requirement that must be satisfied for this EOR arrangement']
  assigned_compliance_officer varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the compliance officer responsible for monitoring and ensuring compliance with this requirement for this arrangement']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this compliance requirement must be audited or re-verified for this specific EOR arrangement. May differ from the requirement\'s general compliance_frequency based on arrangement risk profile or contractual terms']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state for this requirement within this arrangement. Values: compliant (requirement satisfied), non_compliant (violation detected), pending_verification (under review), grace_period (within allowed grace period), waived (contractually waived), not_applicable (requirement determined not to apply)']
  documentation_location varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location (file path, document management system ID, or URL) where compliance documentation for this requirement-arrangement combination is maintained']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this compliance requirement becomes applicable and enforceable for this specific EOR arrangement. May differ from the requirement\'s general effective_date based on arrangement start date or contractual terms']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this compliance requirement ceases to apply to this EOR arrangement, typically aligned with arrangement termination or requirement sunset']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit or verification check for this requirement within this arrangement']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance audit or re-verification of this requirement for this arrangement, driven by audit_frequency']
  non_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting any non-compliance issues, remediation plans, or special circumstances related to this requirement for this arrangement']
  responsibility_party varchar [note: 'Designates which party is responsible for satisfying this compliance requirement within this EOR arrangement context. Values: staffing_hr_eor (Staffing Hr as legal employer), client_co_employer (client in co-employment model), shared (joint responsibility), third_party_vendor (outsourced to compliance vendor)']
  waiver_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which any granted waiver expires and the compliance requirement becomes enforceable again for this arrangement']
  waiver_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal waiver has been granted exempting this arrangement from this compliance requirement, typically based on contractual negotiation or regulatory exception']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance obligation assignment between EOR arrangements and regulatory/contractual compliance requirements. It captures which party (Staffing Hr as EOR vs. client as co-employer) is responsible for satisfying each compliance requirement within the context of a specific EOR arrangement, along with compliance status tracking, audit schedules, and effective dates. Each record links one EOR arrangement to one compliance requirement with responsibility allocation and compliance monitoring attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship.. Existence Justification: In staffing EOR operations, each EOR arrangement must satisfy multiple compliance requirements (I-9, E-Verify, EEOC reporting, OFCCP requirements, state-specific mandates, client-specific contractual requirements), and each compliance requirement applies to many EOR arrangements across different clients and worker populations. The business actively manages compliance obligation assignments, tracking which party (Staffing Hr as EOR vs. client as co-employer) is responsible for each requirement within each arrangement context, along with compliance status, audit schedules, and documentation. This is an operational compliance management process, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table payroll.pay_stub_compliance_validation {
  pay_stub_compliance_validation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each compliance validation record. Primary key.']
  pay_stub_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the pay stub being validated for compliance']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance requirement being validated against']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this validation record was first created in the compliance system.']
  exception_approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the compliance officer who approved the exception for this validation. Null if no exception granted.']
  exception_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the granted exception expires and the requirement must be re-validated. Null if no exception or exception is permanent.']
  exception_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a compliance exception was granted for this specific pay stub-requirement combination. True if exception approved, False otherwise.']
  exception_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why a compliance exception was granted for this validation. Null if no exception granted.']
  requirement_met_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this specific compliance requirement was satisfied for this pay stub. True = compliant, False = non-compliant or pending.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this validation record, supporting audit trail requirements.']
  validation_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the compliance requirement was validated for this pay stub. Automated system check, manual compliance team review, third-party verification service, or client attestation.']
  validation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from the compliance team regarding this specific validation, including findings, remediation steps, or audit references.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance validation check was performed for this pay stub against this requirement. Satisfies audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the validation event between pay_stub and compliance_requirement. It captures the compliance verification status for each pay stub against each applicable federal and client-specific requirement. Each record links one pay stub to one compliance requirement with validation outcome, timestamp, method, and exception handling attributes that exist only in the context of this compliance check.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, each pay stub must be validated against multiple compliance requirements (I-9 verification, wage/hour rules, tax withholding regulations, client-specific mandates), and each compliance requirement applies to many pay stubs across workers and pay periods. The compliance team actively manages validation records for audit defense, tracking which requirements were met, validation methods used, and exceptions granted for specific pay stub-requirement combinations.'
}

Table payroll.pto_policy {
  pto_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pto_policy']
  superseded_pto_policy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pto_policy (superseded_pto_policy_id)']
  accrual_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which PTO hours are accrued under this policy. Not applicable for lump sum or unlimited policies.']
  accrual_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate at which PTO hours are earned per accrual period (e.g., 3.33 hours per pay period, 0.0385 hours per hour worked). Null for non-accrual policies.']
  accrual_start_event varchar [note: 'Event that triggers the start of PTO accrual for an employee under this policy.']
  accrual_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for PTO accrual and balance tracking (hours or days).']
  advance_notice_required_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days advance notice required for employees to request PTO usage. Zero if no advance notice required.']
  annual_allocation_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total PTO amount granted per year for lump sum policies, or maximum accrual cap for accrual policies. Expressed in accrual units.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this PTO policy was officially approved for implementation.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the HR executive or authority who approved this PTO policy.']
  blackout_periods_defined boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy includes defined blackout periods during which PTO usage is restricted or prohibited.']
  carryover_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unused PTO can be carried over to the next accrual period or calendar year.']
  carryover_maximum decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of PTO that can be carried over to the next period if carryover is allowed. Null if no carryover or unlimited carryover.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PTO policy record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the PTO policy ceases to be active. Null for open-ended policies.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the PTO policy becomes active and begins applying to eligible employees.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Text description of employee eligibility requirements for this PTO policy (e.g., \'Full-time employees working 30+ hours per week\', \'All W-2 employees after 90-day probation\').']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Employment classification eligible for this PTO policy.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this PTO policy was last reviewed for compliance and accuracy.']
  maximum_balance decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum PTO balance an employee can accumulate under this policy. Null for unlimited policies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PTO policy record was last modified in the system.']
  negative_balance_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees can use PTO before it is fully accrued, resulting in a negative balance.']
  negative_balance_limit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum negative PTO balance allowed if negative balances are permitted. Expressed as a positive number representing the maximum deficit.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next policy review to ensure ongoing compliance with labor laws and business needs.']
  payout_maximum decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum PTO balance that will be paid out on termination if payout is allowed. Null if full balance is paid or no payout.']
  payout_on_termination boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unused PTO balance is paid out to the employee upon termination of employment.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the PTO policy used in payroll processing and employee communications.']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed text description of the PTO policy terms, conditions, and special provisions.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the PTO policy (e.g., \'Standard Full-Time PTO\', \'Part-Time Accrual Plan\').']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the PTO policy structure: accrual-based (earned over time), lump sum (granted upfront), unlimited (no cap), or hybrid (combination).']
  policy_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the policy document for change tracking and compliance audit purposes (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.3\').']
  proration_method varchar [note: 'Method used to prorate PTO allocation for employees hired mid-period: none (no proration), hire_date (prorated from hire date), calendar_year (prorated within calendar year), anniversary_year (prorated within employment anniversary year).']
  state_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for which this PTO policy is designed to comply with state-specific labor laws. Null for policies that apply across all states.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PTO policy. Active policies are in use; inactive policies are suspended; draft policies are under review; archived policies are historical.']
  usage_increment decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum increment in which PTO can be used (e.g., 0.25 for quarter-hour increments, 1.0 for full-hour increments, 8.0 for full-day increments).']
  waiting_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days an employee must wait after hire date before PTO accrual begins or before they can use accrued PTO. Zero if no waiting period.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'Worker tax classification eligible for this PTO policy. W-2 employees typically receive PTO; 1099 contractors typically do not.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pto_policy. Referenced by policy_id.'
}

Table billing.invoice {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the invoice record in the Staffing Hr billing system. Primary key for the billing.invoice data product.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounting_period. Business justification: Invoices must be assigned to accounting periods for revenue recognition, period close, and financial statement preparation.']
  ar_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.ar_account.ar_account_id — Critical for order-to-cash: billing invoices must trace to AR sub-ledger accounts for financial close, aging reports, and revenue recognition. Without this FK, finance cannot reconcile invoiced amount']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staffing placement or assignment that generated this invoice. Applicable for time-and-materials and per-diem billing models. Null for SOW-based or direct placement fee invoices.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account being billed. Links the invoice to the client master record in the Client domain.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Invoices must reference governing MSA for payment terms, liability caps, dispute resolution, and indemnification scope. Critical for AR collections, legal disputes, and payment processing. MSA defines']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.gl_account']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Invoices issued by specific legal entities for tax reporting, regulatory compliance, and consolidated financial statements.']
  original_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice that this invoice replaces in a void-and-rebill scenario. Populated on the replacement invoice to maintain the audit chain linking corrected invoices back to their originals. Null for original invoices not created as part of a rebill.']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll pay run associated with this invoice, enabling reconciliation between billing and payroll for spread and gross margin analysis.']
  sourcing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.sourcing_campaign. Business justification: In RPO/MSP models, sourcing campaign costs are billed back to clients. Invoice references campaign for cost recovery, ROI analysis, and campaign budget reconciliation. Real business need in managed pr']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Invoices for vendor-supplied contingent workers must track supplier for vendor performance analysis, dispute resolution, supplier payment reconciliation, and margin reporting by supplier—core MSP/VMS ']
  amount_paid decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total amount received from the client against this invoice to date. May be less than net_amount for partially paid invoices. Used to calculate the outstanding balance and drive collections workflows.']
  bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly or daily rate charged to the client for the staffing services on this invoice. For time-and-materials billing, this is the agreed bill rate per hour. For per-diem billing, this is the daily rate. Core input for spread and gross margin calculations. Sourced from the client rate card or VMS rate schedule.']
  billing_model varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing methodology used for this invoice. Determines how line items are calculated and how revenue is recognized. \'time_and_materials\' = hourly bill rate x hours worked; \'per_diem\' = daily flat rate; \'project_based\' = milestone or deliverable-based; \'sow_based\' = Statement of Work (SOW) fixed-fee; \'direct_placement_fee\' = one-time permanent placement fee.']
  billing_period_end date [note: 'The last date of the service period covered by this invoice. Together with billing_period_start, defines the billing window for which staffing services are being charged.']
  billing_period_start date [note: 'The first date of the service period covered by this invoice. For time-and-materials invoices, this is the start of the timesheet week or pay period. Used for revenue recognition period alignment and client billing reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time this invoice record was first created in the billing system. Audit trail field capturing when the invoice was generated, whether in draft or final form. Sourced from Salesforce Revenue Cloud system timestamp.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the credit memo issued against this invoice, if applicable. Populated when a credit adjustment is applied to resolve a dispute, correct a billing error, or process a void-and-rebill. Links to the corresponding credit memo record in Oracle NetSuite ERP.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this invoice (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency billing for international staffing engagements. Defaults to USD for domestic US operations.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The channel through which this invoice was or will be delivered to the client. Determines the transmission workflow in Salesforce Revenue Cloud. VMS portal delivery is required for clients using Beeline or similar VMS platforms.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total discount or credit applied to the invoice, such as volume discounts, contractual rate adjustments, or promotional credits negotiated in the Master Service Agreement (MSA). Reduces the net amount payable.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason provided by the client for disputing this invoice. Populated when status is \'disputed\'. Common reasons include rate discrepancies, unauthorized hours, missing PO number, or timesheet approval issues. Used for dispute resolution tracking and root cause analysis.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which full payment is expected from the client, calculated from invoice_date plus the agreed payment terms (e.g., Net 30, Net 45). Drives accounts receivable aging buckets and collections escalation workflows.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total pre-tax, pre-discount invoice amount representing the sum of all billable charges before adjustments. Calculated as the sum of all line item bill rate charges for the billing period. Core component of the monetary triplet for revenue reporting.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was formally issued to the client. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the invoice. Used as the basis for payment due date calculation and revenue recognition period assignment.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable invoice identifier issued to the client. Unique across all billing periods and client accounts. Used on client-facing documents and referenced in payment remittances. Sourced from Salesforce Revenue Cloud invoice numbering sequence.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the invoice. Drives accounts receivable workflows, collections actions, and revenue recognition. \'draft\' = not yet issued; \'sent\' = delivered to client; \'paid\' = fully settled; \'partially_paid\' = partial payment received; \'disputed\' = client has raised a dispute; \'void\' = cancelled, typically triggering a rebill.']
  is_void boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice has been voided. True when the invoice has been cancelled and is no longer collectible. Voided invoices are retained for audit trail purposes and typically trigger a replacement invoice in void-and-rebill workflows.']
  markup_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage markup applied over the pay rate to arrive at the bill rate, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.45 = 45%). Calculated as (bill_rate - pay_rate) / pay_rate. A standard staffing industry profitability metric used in client pricing negotiations and margin analysis.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final amount due from the client after applying taxes and discounts. Calculated as gross_amount + tax_amount - discount_amount. This is the amount the client is expected to remit. Core component of the monetary triplet.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special billing instructions associated with this invoice. May include client-specific billing requirements, dispute resolution comments, or internal processing notes. Not transmitted to the client unless explicitly included in the invoice document.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,4) [note: 'Remaining unpaid amount on the invoice, calculated as net_amount minus amount_paid. Drives accounts receivable aging reports and collections prioritization. Zero when invoice is fully paid.']
  overtime_hours_billed decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of overtime (OT) hours included in the total hours billed on this invoice. Overtime hours are billed at a premium rate per FLSA regulations (typically 1.5x the regular bill rate). Tracked separately for compliance reporting and client billing transparency.']
  paid_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time full payment was received and confirmed for this invoice. Populated when status transitions to \'paid\'. Used for Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation, cash flow reporting, and accounts receivable closure.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly or daily rate paid to the temporary worker for the services billed on this invoice. Used in conjunction with bill_rate to calculate the spread (bill rate minus pay rate) and gross margin. Sourced from TempWorks Payroll and the candidate pay rate agreement.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment terms agreed with the client, defining the number of days from invoice date within which payment is due. Sourced from the client\'s Master Service Agreement (MSA). Drives due_date calculation and accounts receivable aging classification.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Client-issued Purchase Order (PO) number that authorizes the staffing services billed on this invoice. Required by many enterprise clients and Vendor Management System (VMS) programs for invoice approval and payment processing. Must match the PO number in the client\'s procurement system.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'The date on which revenue from this invoice is recognized in the general ledger per GAAP ASC 606 revenue recognition standards. May differ from invoice_date for milestone-based or deferred revenue scenarios. Critical for accurate financial period reporting.']
  sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the invoice was transmitted to the client via email, VMS portal, or EDI. Marks the transition from \'draft\' to \'sent\' status. Used for SLA measurement of billing cycle time and for dispute resolution (proof of delivery).']
  sow_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Statement of Work (SOW) document governing project-based or SOW-based billing engagements. Populated only when billing_model is \'sow_based\' or \'project_based\'. Links to the specific SOW deliverables being invoiced.']
  spread_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'The difference between the bill rate and the pay rate (bill_rate minus pay_rate), representing the gross margin per unit before burden costs. A key profitability metric in staffing known as the \'Spread\'. Stored at the invoice level for aggregate reporting; detailed spread is tracked at the line item level.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax charges applied to the invoice, including applicable state and local taxes on staffing services. Varies by jurisdiction and service type. Some staffing services may be tax-exempt depending on client tax status and state regulations.']
  total_hours_billed decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total number of hours billed to the client on this invoice across all workers and assignments in the billing period. Applicable for time-and-materials billing models. Sourced from approved timesheets in Kronos Workforce Ready. Used for billing reconciliation and workforce utilization reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time this invoice record was last modified in the billing system. Tracks any changes to invoice amounts, status, payment terms, or other fields. Essential for data lineage, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  vms_invoice_number varchar [note: 'External invoice identifier assigned by the client\'s Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline. Required for VMS-managed programs where the VMS controls invoice approval workflows. Used for cross-system reconciliation between Staffing Hr\'s billing system and the client\'s VMS.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why this invoice was voided. Required when is_void is True to support audit trail and financial controls. Drives void-and-rebill workflow routing and financial restatement processes.']
  worker_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct temporary workers or contractors included in this invoice. Provides headcount context for the billing period. Used for workforce analytics, client reporting, and Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) calculations.']

  Note: 'Core billing document issued to a client for staffing services rendered during a billing period. Captures invoice number, date, due date, billing period, total amount, tax amount, net amount, payment terms, status (draft, sent, paid, partially paid, disputed, void), billing model (time-and-materials, per-diem, project-based, SOW-based, direct placement fee), currency, PO number, and client billing account reference. SSOT for all client-facing billing documents across all staffing engagement types. Supports void-and-rebill workflows for retroactive billing corrections.'
}

Table billing.invoice_line {
  invoice_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each invoice line item record in the billing system. Primary key for the invoice_line data product. Role: TRANSACTION_LINE.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounting_period. Business justification: Invoice lines posted to periods for detailed revenue tracking and revenue recognition by service line.']
  assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to candidate.assessment. Business justification: Assessment costs (background checks, drug screens, skills tests) are frequently billed as separate line items to clients in staffing. Direct link enables assessment-specific billing, cost recovery tra']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker placement or assignment that generated this billable line item. Links the billing record to the specific temporary or permanent placement in Bullhorn ATS/CRM. Satisfies RESOURCE_REFERENCE minimum category for TRANSACTION_LINE role.']
  bill_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.bill_rate. Business justification: Each invoice line is billed at a specific bill rate. The invoice_line currently has bill_rate and pay_rate as scalar values, but these should reference the authoritative bill_rate master record. The b']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account being billed for this line item. Links to the client account record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM and Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  client_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific client work site or location where the worker performed services billed on this line. Used for multi-site billing, tax jurisdiction determination, and workforce analytics.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Line items inherit MSA commercial terms when billed directly under MSA without SOW (common for temp staffing). Enables rate validation, markup cap enforcement, and compliance reporting by MSA.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Line items validate against contract rate schedules for VMS compliance and rate variance reporting. Required for invoice approval workflows, rate audit trails, and ensuring billed rates match contract']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Staffing firms bill clients for credential procurement services (background checks, drug screens, license verifications) as invoice line items. Finance teams need to track which specific credential in']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Invoice lines bill direct hire placement fees (one-time or installment). Staffing firms invoice direct hire fees separately from temp billing; finance teams reconcile fee revenue to placements. Essent']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.gl_account']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent invoice header (TRANSACTION_HEADER). Satisfies HEADER_REFERENCE minimum category for TRANSACTION_LINE role. Links this line item to its parent client invoice document in Salesforce Revenue Cloud and Oracle NetSuite ERP.']
  job_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.job_category. Business justification: Revenue reporting by job category is a core P&L metric in staffing. Enables skill vertical profitability analysis, supports market demand forecasting, and drives strategic decisions on which job categ']
  onboarding_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to onboarding.onboarding_engagement. Business justification: Line-level tracking of which onboarding enabled specific billing. Supports onboarding ROI analysis (time-to-bill by onboarding type), cost allocation (onboarding expenses vs. revenue), and client repo']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order or requisition (Req) associated with this billing line. Links the invoice line to the originating client staffing request managed in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  pay_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_rate. Business justification: Margin compliance and rate reconciliation reports require comparing billed rates against approved pay rates per assignment. Staffing operations must validate invoice lines don\'t bill below cost. Criti']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate or worker whose labor or services are being billed on this line item. Links to the candidate profile in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  req_pipeline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.req_pipeline. Business justification: Pipeline records track candidate progression through stages; invoice lines reference pipeline for sourcing attribution, recruiter commission allocation, and time-to-fill analysis tied to billing event']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: Invoice lines bill SOW engagements (hourly, milestone, or deliverable-based). SOW billing differs from standard temp assignments; requires linking invoice lines to specific SOW placements for revenue ']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Statement of Work (SOW) contract under which this line item is billed, applicable for project-based or SOW-based billing models. Links to the SOW document managed in DocuSign CLM.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Line-level supplier tracking enables blended-rate invoicing where multiple suppliers provide workers on same engagement; critical for supplier scorecarding, margin analysis by supplier, and VMS progra']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved timesheet record from Kronos Workforce Ready that substantiates the hours billed on this line item. Applicable for time-and-materials and per-diem billing types.']
  worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Each invoice line\'s billing model, rate structure, and markup depend on worker classification (W2 vs IC affects billing structure, burden inclusion, markup applicability). Core billing logic requires ']
  billing_model varchar [note: 'The contractual billing model under which this line item is invoiced. Time and Materials = hourly billing; Per Diem = daily rate; Project Based = milestone deliverable; SOW (Statement of Work) Based = fixed-scope contract; Direct Placement = permanent hire fee; Temp-to-Perm = conversion fee billing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: time_and_materials|per_diem|project_based|sow_based|direct_placement|temp_to_perm|contract_to_hire|rpo — promote to reference product]']
  billing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billable service type on this line item. Regular = standard hours; OT (Overtime) = hours exceeding FLSA thresholds billed at OT rate; DT (Double Time) = hours billed at double rate; Per Diem = daily allowance billing; Expense = reimbursable expense; Placement Fee = direct hire or temp-to-perm conversion fee.']
  burden_rate_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The employer burden cost allocated to this line item, representing the employer-side payroll taxes and benefits costs (FICA, FUTA, SUTA, Workers Comp, benefits) associated with the worker hours billed. Sourced from TempWorks Payroll burden rate configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this invoice line item record was first created in the billing system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance requirements.']
  credit_memo_reference varchar [note: 'The credit memo document number issued against this invoice line item when a billing dispute is resolved or an adjustment is required. Populated when line_status is \'credited\'. Enables accounts receivable reconciliation in Oracle NetSuite ERP.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this line item (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency billing for international staffing operations.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Any contractual discount or credit applied to this invoice line item, reducing the extended amount. May reflect volume discounts, promotional credits, or negotiated rate adjustments per the client MSA.']
  extended_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'The total billable amount for this line item, calculated as quantity multiplied by bill rate. Represents the gross revenue for this line before any adjustments, credits, or taxes. Satisfies LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT minimum category for TRANSACTION_LINE role.']
  gross_margin_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The net gross margin for this line item after subtracting the burden rate from the spread amount. Gross Margin = Spread Amount - Burden Rate Amount. Core financial performance metric for staffing profitability reporting.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is billable to the client (True) or is a non-billable internal cost line (False). Non-billable lines may represent internal adjustments, write-offs, or courtesy credits that are tracked but not invoiced.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice line item is subject to sales tax or use tax (True) or is tax-exempt (False). Drives tax calculation logic in Oracle NetSuite ERP based on client tax exemption certificates and service type.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the parent invoice, used for ordering and referencing individual line items on the invoice document. Satisfies LINE_SEQUENCE minimum category for TRANSACTION_LINE role.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of this invoice line item. Draft = pending finalization; Approved = validated and ready for client billing; Disputed = client has raised a dispute; Credited = a credit memo has been issued; Voided = line cancelled; Paid = payment received and applied.']
  markup_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The markup percentage applied to the pay rate to derive the bill rate for this line item, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.35 = 35%). Markup Percentage = (Bill Rate - Pay Rate) / Pay Rate. Key profitability metric tracked in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  net_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'The final net billable amount for this line item after applying discounts and adding taxes. Net Amount = Extended Amount - Discount Amount + Tax Amount. Represents the actual amount due from the client for this line.']
  ot_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'The rate multiplier applied to the base bill rate for overtime (OT) or double time (DT) billing lines. Standard OT multiplier is 1.5x; DT multiplier is 2.0x. Applicable only when billing_type is overtime or double_time.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the pay period or work period covered by this invoice line item. Defines the end of the billable service window. Together with pay_period_start_date, establishes the billing period for compliance and audit purposes.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the pay period or work period covered by this invoice line item. Defines the beginning of the billable service window for time-and-materials and per-diem billing.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'The client\'s purchase order number associated with this invoice line item. Required by many enterprise clients for accounts payable processing and invoice matching. Sourced from the client\'s ERP or VMS.']
  quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'The billable quantity for this line item. Represents hours worked for time-and-materials billing, number of days for per-diem billing, or a count of 1 for placement fee billing. Satisfies LINE_QUANTITY minimum category for TRANSACTION_LINE role.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity field. Hours for time-and-materials, days for per-diem, units for deliverable-based SOW billing, flat for fixed-fee placement fees.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'The date on which revenue for this invoice line item is recognized in the General Ledger per ASC 606 revenue recognition standards. May differ from the invoice date or pay period end date for deferred or accrued revenue scenarios.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the billable service or work performed on this line item, as it appears on the client invoice. Examples: \'Temporary Staffing Services - IT\', \'Direct Placement Fee - Software Engineer\', \'Per Diem - Travel Assignment\'.']
  spread_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The gross margin contribution for this line item, calculated as (bill_rate minus pay_rate) multiplied by quantity. Spread is a core staffing industry profitability metric representing the difference between what the client is billed and what the worker is paid.']
  tax_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The calculated tax amount applied to this invoice line item based on the applicable tax code and jurisdiction. Represents sales tax, use tax, or VAT charged to the client.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax jurisdiction or tax treatment code applied to this invoice line item in Oracle NetSuite ERP. Determines applicable sales tax, use tax, or VAT rates based on the client\'s billing location and service type.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this invoice line item record was last modified. Tracks changes to billing amounts, status updates, dispute resolutions, and credit memo applications for audit and compliance purposes.']
  vms_req_number varchar [note: 'The requisition or purchase order number assigned by the client\'s Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, for this billing line. Required for VMS-managed programs to match invoices to approved requisitions and enable straight-through processing.']
  work_date date [note: 'The specific date on which the work was performed, applicable for daily billing lines or per-diem entries. Used for detailed labor analytics and compliance reporting.']
  worker_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the worker\'s employment arrangement for this billing line. W-2 Temp = temporary employee on agency payroll; 1099 IC (Independent Contractor) = self-employed contractor; C2C = corp-to-corp contractor; EOR (Employer of Record) = worker employed by EOR entity; Direct Hire = permanent placement.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail within a client invoice representing a single billable unit of work. Each line ties to a specific worker assignment, timesheet period, or deliverable. Captures line number, service description, billing type (regular, OT, DT, per diem, expense, placement fee), quantity, bill rate, extended amount, pay period dates, worker and job order references, cost center, GL account, tax code, and markup/spread values.'
}

Table billing.bill_rate {
  bill_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a bill rate record. Primary key for the bill_rate data product in the billing domain.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement record this bill rate governs. Ties the negotiated rate to the specific worker assignment for timesheet-to-invoice processing.']
  billing_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.rate_schedule. Business justification: Bill rates are often derived from or reference rate schedules (rate cards). A bill_rate record represents a specific negotiated rate, which may be based on a rate_schedule template. This FK allows tra']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for which this bill rate applies. Establishes the client-rate relationship for invoice generation and profitability reporting.']
  client_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client rate card from which this bill rate is derived. Links to the negotiated rate card established under the client\'s Master Service Agreement (MSA).']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Bill rates must comply with MSA markup caps, rate escalation terms, and pricing frameworks. Essential for rate approval workflows, margin analysis, and contract compliance audits in staffing operation']
  contract_sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOW-specific rates for project-based work with fixed pricing or milestone billing. Required for SOW rate validation, project margin analysis, and ensuring rates match SOW pricing model.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Bill rates often vary by cost center for internal cost allocation and departmental P&L tracking in staffing operations.']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire placements have negotiated fee rates (percentage of salary or flat fee). Rate cards track direct hire fee structures by client/job category. Essential for fee calculation, client rate audi']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Bill rates may specify GL accounts for revenue classification by service type or billing model.']
  job_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.job_category. Business justification: Bill rates in staffing are structured by job category/skill tier within rate cards. Enables rate card analysis by job family, supports pricing strategy by vertical, and facilitates market rate benchma']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific client work site or location for which this bill rate applies. Supports location-based rate differentiation (e.g., higher rates for high cost-of-living markets or specialized facilities).']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: Managed programs (MSP/RPO) have distinct rate governance separate from VMS and general client rates. Program managers need to track program-specific rate approvals, enforce program markup caps, and ge']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific job order (requisition) to which this bill rate is associated. Enables rate traceability at the requisition level for billing and margin analysis.']
  pay_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_rate. Business justification: Bill rate cards must reference corresponding pay rates to calculate spread and markup percentages. Pricing teams set bill rates based on pay rates plus target margin. Core relationship in staffing pri']
  previous_rate_bill_rate_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the prior version of this bill rate record. Enables rate lineage tracking and audit trail for rate changes over the engagement lifecycle.']
  recruitment_sla_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.recruitment_sla_target. Business justification: SLA targets define rate structures (premium rates for expedited fills, volume discounts). Bill rates reference SLA targets for pricing compliance, rate card enforcement, and contractual rate validatio']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: SOW engagements require specific rate cards (project-based, milestone, or deliverable rates).
