  check_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the compliance gate-check. \'initial\' applies to new placements, \'extension\' to assignment extensions, \'reactivation\' to returning workers, \'annual_renewal\' for ongoing long-term assignments, \'client_change\' when a worker transfers to a new client.']
  clearance_date date [note: 'The date on which all required compliance checks were satisfied and the placement was granted compliance clearance to go active. Null if clearance has not yet been granted. Used to calculate time-to-clearance metrics.']
  client_specific_req_status varchar [note: 'Status of any additional compliance requirements mandated by the client\'s MSA or VMS program beyond standard federal and state requirements. Examples include client-specific background check panels, additional drug test panels, site-specific safety certifications, or vendor credentialing requirements in Beeline VMS.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance team annotations, exception documentation, escalation notes, or additional context related to this placement compliance check. Supports audit trail and case management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this placement compliance check record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record origination and supports SOC 2 Type II audit logging requirements.']
  credential_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of required professional credential and license verification for this placement. Applies to placements requiring specific certifications (e.g., nursing licenses, CDL, OSHA certifications, IT certifications). Verified through Sterling Background Check and internal credentialing processes.']
  drug_screen_date date [note: 'The date on which the drug screen specimen was collected and/or the final result was reported. Used to validate that drug screening was completed within the required pre-placement window.']
  drug_screen_status varchar [note: 'Status of the pre-placement drug screening for this worker. \'negative\' clears the worker for placement. \'positive\' blocks placement and may trigger adverse action. \'not_required\' applies when client or placement type does not mandate drug screening. Managed through Sterling Background Check.']
  eeoc_self_id_collected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the EEOC voluntary self-identification form (race, ethnicity, gender, disability, veteran status) has been collected from the worker for this placement. Required for EEOC and OFCCP reporting compliance for federal contractor clients.']
  noncompete_nda_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the review for existing non-compete agreements and NDAs that could restrict the worker\'s placement with the target client. \'conflict_identified\' requires legal review before placement proceeds. Tracked via DocuSign CLM.']
  ofccp_applicability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this placement is subject to OFCCP affirmative action and equal employment opportunity requirements because the client is a federal contractor or subcontractor. Drives additional compliance obligations including AAP tracking and adverse impact analysis.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Status of the worker onboarding process in SAP SuccessFactors, including completion of required forms, policy acknowledgments, direct deposit setup, and benefits enrollment. \'complete\' is required before a placement can generate billable hours.']
  overall_compliance_result varchar [note: 'The final aggregated compliance determination for this placement. \'pass\' authorizes the placement to go active and generate billable hours. \'fail\' blocks activation. \'conditional_pass\' indicates clearance granted with documented exceptions or waivers. \'pending_review\' indicates escalation to compliance team.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'The type of placement being compliance-checked. Determines which compliance requirements are applicable (e.g., I-9 requirements differ for SOW vs. direct placement; workers comp requirements differ for temporary vs. direct hire). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: temporary|contract_to_hire|direct_placement|per_diem|sow|rpo — promote to reference product]']
  safety_training_date date [note: 'The date on which all required safety training modules were completed by the worker in the LMS. Used to validate training currency and identify workers requiring refresher training.']
  safety_training_status varchar [note: 'Status of required OSHA and client-specific safety training completion for this placement. \'complete\' indicates all mandatory safety modules have been passed in the LMS (SAP SuccessFactors). Required for placements in industrial, construction, healthcare, and other regulated environments.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this compliance check record originated or was primarily managed. Supports data lineage tracking in the Silver layer lakehouse and cross-system reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this placement compliance check record was most recently modified. Tracks the latest state change for audit trail purposes and supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  waiver_authorization varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authorized approver who granted a compliance waiver for this placement (if applicable). Required when check_status = \'waived\' or overall_compliance_result = \'conditional_pass\'. Provides audit trail for waiver accountability.']
  waiver_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which a granted compliance waiver expires. After this date, the outstanding compliance item must be completed or the placement may be suspended. Drives automated compliance expiry alerts.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Documented business justification for any compliance waiver granted for this placement. Required for audit trail integrity. Examples include client-approved exceptions, emergency placements with post-start completion commitments, or regulatory exemptions.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'The NCCI workers compensation classification code assigned to this placement based on the job duties and work environment. Drives workers comp premium calculation in TempWorks Payroll and ensures correct coverage is in place before assignment start.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the compliance gate-check results for each worker placement prior to assignment start or extension. Validates that all applicable federal, state, and client-specific compliance requirements are satisfied: I-9 complete, E-Verify authorized, BGC cleared, drug screen passed, required credentials verified, safety training complete, worker classification determined, co-employment risk assessed, wage and hour determination completed, and client-specific requirements met. Each check item has a pass/fail/waived/pending status, check date, reviewer, and waiver authorization (if applicable). Acts as the compliance clearance record before a placement goes active. Supports real-time compliance dashboards and prevents non-compliant placements from generating billable hours.'
}

Table compliance.workers_comp_policy {
  workers_comp_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the workers compensation policy record. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a long-running insurance policy binding the staffing firm to coverage obligations across states and worker populations.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: MSAs specify workers\' comp coverage requirements, carrier ratings, and certificate of insurance obligations. Real business need: MSA insurance compliance for client certificate delivery and audit.']
  osha_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the linked OSHA incident record when a workers comp claim is associated with a workplace injury event. Supports OSHA incident-to-claim correlation for regulatory reporting and safety analytics.']
  premium_allocation_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Workers comp premiums are allocated to cost centers based on headcount, payroll, or claims experience for departmental cost tracking and budget management. Staffing firms allocate insurance costs to o']
  premium_expense_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Workers compensation insurance premiums are expensed to specific GL accounts (typically \'Insurance Expense - Workers Comp\') for financial reporting. Monthly premium accruals and annual true-ups requir']
  regulatory_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_violation. Business justification: When a workers compensation policy issue (e.g., coverage lapse, misclassification, premium fraud) results in a regulatory violation, this FK links to the regulatory_violation record. Clear 1:N relatio']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Workers\' comp policies are issued by insurance carriers (suppliers in vendor management context). Track supplier for claims management, premium reconciliation, carrier performance assessment, and vend']
  adjuster_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the insurance carrier\'s claims adjuster assigned to manage this workers compensation claim. Primary point of contact for claim status updates, reserve changes, and settlement negotiations.']
  adjuster_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number for the claims adjuster assigned to this workers compensation claim. Used for claim status inquiries and coordination of return-to-work activities.']
  aggregate_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum total dollar amount the carrier will pay for all claims under this policy during the policy period. Null indicates unlimited aggregate coverage. Critical for risk exposure assessment and reinsurance planning.']
  annual_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total annual premium amount in USD payable to the insurance carrier for this policy period. Calculated as the sum of (payroll per $100 × class code rate × EMR) across all covered class codes and states. Used for financial planning, burden rate calculation, and cost allocation.']
  audited_payroll_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total payroll in USD for all covered workers as determined by the annual policy audit. Differences between estimated and audited payroll result in premium adjustments (additional premium or return premium). Populated post-audit.']
  body_part_code varchar [note: 'Standardized NCCI body part code identifying the anatomical location of the workplace injury (e.g., 10=Head, 30=Back, 50=Lower Extremities). Used for claim classification, trend analysis, and safety program targeting.']
  carrier_naic_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit NAIC company code uniquely identifying the insurance carrier. Used for regulatory filings, state compliance verification, and carrier lookup in state workers compensation databases.']
  cause_of_injury_code varchar [note: 'Standardized NCCI cause of injury code describing how the injury occurred (e.g., 01=Burn, 27=Fall, 83=Strain). Used for claim classification, loss control program development, and OSHA incident analysis.']
  claim_filing_date date [note: 'Date on which the workers compensation claim was formally filed with the carrier. Used to measure filing timeliness against state-mandated deadlines and carrier SLA requirements.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned claim number for an individual workers compensation claim filed under this policy. Serves as the primary external identifier for claim tracking, adjuster communication, and state reporting. Null for policy header records with no associated claim.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the workers compensation claim. Open: active claim under investigation or treatment. Reserved: financial reserve established. Closed: claim resolved with no further activity expected. Reopened: previously closed claim reactivated due to new medical evidence or litigation.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workers compensation claim by severity and benefit type. Medical only: no lost work time. Lost time: worker missed work beyond the waiting period. Permanent partial/total disability: lasting impairment. Fatality: death benefit claim. Drives reserve levels and EMR impact. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical_only|lost_time|permanent_partial_disability|permanent_total_disability|fatality — promote to reference product]']
  covered_states varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of two-letter US state codes (e.g., CA,TX,NY) for which this policy provides workers compensation coverage. Determines placement eligibility by state. Each state may have distinct rate schedules and class codes under this policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this workers compensation policy or claim record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per SOC 2 Type II controls.']
  deductible_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Per-claim deductible amount in USD that the staffing firm is responsible for paying before the carrier assumes liability. Applicable for large-deductible policy types. Impacts cash flow planning and claim reserve management.']
  emr decimal(5,3) [note: 'Experience Modification Rate (EMR) assigned by NCCI or applicable state rating bureau. A multiplier applied to the manual premium to reflect the employer\'s actual loss history relative to industry average. EMR below 1.0 indicates better-than-average safety performance; above 1.0 indicates worse. Critical for premium calculation and client contract eligibility (many clients require EMR ≤ 1.0).']
  emr_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the current EMR value is applicable. EMR is recalculated annually by NCCI or the state rating bureau. Tracking the effective date ensures the correct EMR is applied to premium calculations for each policy period.']
  estimated_annual_payroll decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total payroll in USD for all covered workers during the policy period, used as the basis for initial premium calculation. Reconciled against actual payroll at policy audit. Sourced from TempWorks Payroll payroll projections.']
  incident_date date [note: 'Date on which the workplace injury or occupational illness occurred. The principal real-world event date for the claim. Used for OSHA recordkeeping, state first report of injury filing deadlines, and claim aging analysis.']
  injury_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the workplace injury or occupational illness, including body part affected, nature of injury, and how the incident occurred. Sourced from the first report of injury and used for OSHA recordkeeping, claim adjudication, and safety trend analysis.']
  litigation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the workers compensation claim has entered litigation (True) or remains in standard adjudication (False). Litigated claims typically have higher total incurred costs and longer closure timelines. Triggers escalation to legal counsel.']
  lost_work_days varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days the injured worker was unable to work due to the compensable injury. Used for OSHA 300 log recordkeeping, DART rate calculation, and claim severity benchmarking.']
  ncci_class_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of NCCI workers compensation class codes applicable under this policy, mapped by job type or occupational category (e.g., 8810 for clerical, 5537 for HVAC). Class codes determine the base rate per $100 of payroll for premium calculation. Managed in TempWorks Payroll workers comp configuration.']
  paid_indemnity_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total indemnity (wage replacement) benefits paid to date on this claim in USD. Includes temporary total disability (TTD), temporary partial disability (TPD), and permanent disability payments. Tracked separately from medical for benefit analysis.']
  paid_medical_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total medical expenses paid to date on this claim in USD, covering physician visits, hospitalization, physical therapy, and prescription costs. Tracked separately from indemnity payments for loss analysis and medical cost management.']
  policy_audit_date date [note: 'Date on which the annual workers compensation policy audit was completed by the carrier. The audit reconciles estimated payroll against actual payroll to determine final premium. Triggers premium adjustment processing.']
  policy_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the workers compensation policy coverage begins. Defines the start of the binding coverage period. Workers placed before this date are not covered under this policy.']
  policy_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the workers compensation policy coverage ends. Triggers renewal workflows and placement eligibility checks. Null indicates an open-ended or continuously renewed policy.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Externally-assigned policy number issued by the insurance carrier. Used as the primary business identifier for the policy in all carrier communications, audits, and state filings. Maps to the policy number field in TempWorks Payroll workers comp configuration.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the workers compensation policy. Drives eligibility checks for new placements and triggers renewal workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|pending|expired|cancelled|non-renewed|reinstated — promote to reference product]']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workers compensation policy structure. Determines how premiums are calculated and how losses affect future costs. Guaranteed cost policies have fixed premiums; loss-sensitive and retrospective rating plans adjust based on actual claims experience. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: guaranteed_cost|loss_sensitive|large_deductible|retrospective_rating|captive — promote to reference product]']
  premium_adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of premium adjustment resulting from the annual payroll audit. Positive value indicates additional premium owed to the carrier; negative value indicates a return premium credit. Feeds into Oracle NetSuite ERP accounts payable or receivable.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for policy renewal negotiation and execution with the carrier. Used to trigger advance renewal workflows, EMR review, and premium renegotiation processes.']
  reserve_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current outstanding reserve amount in USD established by the claims adjuster to cover estimated future claim costs (medical, indemnity, legal). Reserve adequacy directly impacts financial statements and EMR calculations.']
  return_to_work_date date [note: 'Date on which the injured worker returned to work following a lost-time workers compensation claim. Used to calculate total lost work days, measure return-to-work program effectiveness, and determine indemnity benefit end date.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the workers compensation claim was formally settled and closed. Null for open or reserved claims. Used for claim lifecycle duration analysis and financial close-out processing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this workers compensation policy or claim record was ingested. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation. Primary source is TempWorks Payroll workers compensation module.']
  state_claim_number varchar [note: 'Claim number assigned by the state workers compensation board or industrial commission for jurisdictional tracking. Distinct from the carrier claim number. Required for state regulatory filings and hearings.']
  subrogation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier has identified subrogation potential (True), meaning a third party may be liable for the injury and the carrier may seek recovery of paid losses. Subrogation recovery reduces net claim cost and improves EMR.']
  total_incurred_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total incurred cost of the workers compensation claim in USD, representing the sum of all paid losses plus outstanding reserves. The primary financial measure of claim severity. Used for EMR calculation, loss ratio analysis, and financial reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this workers compensation policy or claim record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record for workers\' compensation insurance policies and associated claims covering contingent workers across states. Policy header captures policy number, insurance carrier, policy period, covered states, experience modification rate (EMR), NCCI class codes by job type, premium amounts, and policy renewal dates. Claim detail records capture claim number, incident date, filing date, injury description, claim type (medical only, lost time, permanent partial disability), claim status (open, reserved, closed, reopened), total incurred cost, reserve amount, adjuster, litigation flag, subrogation flag, and settlement date. Links to osha_incident for workplace injury correlation. SSOT for all workers\' compensation policy and claim data. Managed through TempWorks Payroll integration. Supports OSHA incident linkage, state-mandated workers\' comp compliance, experience mod management, and insurance cost optimization.'
}

Table compliance.state_compliance_rule {
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the state-specific compliance rule record. Primary key.']
  superseded_by_rule_state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the state_compliance_rule_id of the newer rule that supersedes this rule. Null if this rule is current and not superseded.']
  superseded_state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on state_compliance_rule (superseded_state_compliance_rule_id)']
  applicability_criteria varchar [note: 'Detailed business logic and conditions that determine when this compliance rule must be applied, including employer size, industry, worker type, and other qualifying factors.']
  applicable_industry_sector varchar [note: 'Industry sector or North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code range to which this rule applies. Null if applies to all industries.']
  applicable_worker_type varchar [note: 'Type of worker classification to which this compliance rule applies (e.g., all workers, W-2 only, 1099 independent contractors, temporary placements). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|W-2|1099|temporary|permanent|contract|direct hire|temp-to-perm — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal compliance team notes, implementation guidance, or special considerations for this state rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this state compliance rule record was first created in the system.']
  documentation_required varchar [note: 'Description of documentation, records, or forms that must be maintained to demonstrate compliance with this state rule.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the state compliance rule becomes legally enforceable and must be applied to staffing operations.']
  employer_size_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum number of employees or Full-Time Equivalents (FTE) that triggers applicability of this compliance rule. Null if applies to all employer sizes.']
  enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the state or local government agency responsible for enforcing this compliance rule (e.g., California Labor Commissioner, New York Department of Labor).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the state compliance rule ceases to be enforceable. Null for rules with no sunset provision.']
  ic_test_type varchar [note: 'Type of legal test used by this jurisdiction to determine independent contractor classification (e.g., ABC test in California, common law test in other states). Only applicable for independent contractor classification rules.']
  jurisdiction_level varchar [note: 'Geographic scope level of the compliance rule indicating whether it applies at state, county, city, or municipal level.']
  jurisdiction_locality varchar [note: 'City, county, or local jurisdiction name where this rule applies if more granular than state-level (e.g., San Francisco, Cook County). Null for state-wide rules.']
  jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state abbreviation where this compliance rule applies (e.g., CA, NY, TX).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this compliance rule was last reviewed by the compliance team to verify accuracy and currency.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this compliance rule to ensure it remains current with legislative changes.']
  penalty_amount_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary penalty amount in USD that may be assessed for violation of this compliance rule. Null if no maximum specified.']
  penalty_amount_min decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary penalty amount in USD that may be assessed for violation of this compliance rule. Null if no minimum specified.']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Description of penalties, fines, or sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance with this state rule.']
  penalty_per_violation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether penalties are assessed per individual violation (True) or as a single penalty regardless of violation count (False).']
  posting_requirement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance rule requires physical posting of notices at worksites or inclusion in job postings (True) or not (False).']
  preempts_local_law boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this state-level rule preempts or supersedes more restrictive local ordinances (True) or allows local jurisdictions to impose stricter requirements (False).']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Official legal citation to the statute, regulation, or ordinance that establishes this compliance requirement (e.g., California Labor Code Section 432.3).']
  requirement_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive description of what the compliance rule requires staffing operations to do or prohibits them from doing.']
  requirement_type varchar [note: 'Category of state-specific compliance requirement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ban-the-box|pay transparency|salary history ban|state e-verify mandate|drug testing restriction|portable background check|paid sick leave mandate|predictive scheduling|wage theft prevention notice|state mini-WARN|independent contractor test variation|workers comp mandate|unemployment insurance requirement|disability insurance requirement|family leave mandate — promote to reference product]']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years that documentation related to this compliance rule must be retained. Null if no specific retention period mandated.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Internal risk rating assigned to this compliance rule based on penalty severity, enforcement frequency, and business impact of non-compliance.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the state compliance rule, typically formatted as state abbreviation followed by rule category code (e.g., CA-BTB001 for California Ban-the-Box).']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the state compliance rule (e.g., California Ban-the-Box Law, New York Salary Transparency Act).']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the state compliance rule indicating whether it is currently enforceable.']
  source_url varchar [note: 'Web URL link to the authoritative source document, legislative text, or regulatory guidance for this compliance rule.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance rule mandates specific training for recruiters, managers, or workers (True) or not (False).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this state compliance rule record was last modified in the system.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this compliance rule, incremented when amendments or updates are made to the underlying statute or regulation.']

  Note: 'Reference master for state-specific and local jurisdiction compliance requirements that vary by geography and impact multi-state staffing operations. Captures state/locality, effective date, expiration date, requirement type (ban-the-box, pay transparency, salary history ban, state E-Verify mandate, state-specific drug testing restrictions, portable background check laws, paid sick leave mandate, predictive scheduling, wage theft prevention notice, state mini-WARN Act, independent contractor classification test variations such as ABC test vs. common law test), applicability criteria (employer size threshold, industry sector, worker type), penalty for non-compliance, enforcement agency, and source citation with URL. Drives jurisdiction-aware compliance enforcement for multi-state staffing operations. Distinct from compliance_requirement which owns federal regulations and client-specific contractual mandates.'
}

Table compliance.placement_requirement_check {
  placement_requirement_check_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each individual requirement check within a placement compliance gate-check']
  placement_compliance_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the placement compliance gate-check record for which this individual requirement check was performed']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific compliance requirement being checked in this placement context']
  blocking_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a failed or pending status for this specific requirement blocks the placement from being cleared and generating billable hours']
  check_date date [note: 'The business date on which this specific requirement check was performed or completed for this placement']
  check_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance team annotations specific to this requirement check, including exception documentation, escalation notes, or special handling instructions']
  check_result varchar [note: 'The outcome of this specific requirement check for this placement: pass (requirement satisfied), fail (requirement not met), waived (requirement bypassed with authorization), pending (check in progress), not_applicable (requirement does not apply to this placement type or worker classification)']
  check_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this individual requirement check record: active (currently valid), expired (waiver or check validity has lapsed), superseded (replaced by a newer check), archived (historical record)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this requirement check record was created']
  evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document link to the evidence that supports this requirement check result (e.g., document ID in content management system, external verification system reference number)']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this requirement check record was last modified']
  review_date date [note: 'The date on which this requirement check result was reviewed and approved by the compliance team']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the compliance team member who reviewed and approved this requirement check result']
  waiver_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a compliance waiver was granted for this specific requirement in the context of this placement']
  waiver_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the granted waiver for this requirement expires, after which the requirement must be satisfied or a new waiver obtained']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Documented business justification for granting a waiver for this specific requirement check (e.g., client-approved exception, emergency placement, requirement superseded by equivalent credential)']

  Note: 'This association product represents the individual compliance requirement check results within a placement compliance gate-check process. Each record links one placement_compliance_check to one compliance_requirement and captures the specific check result, evidence, waiver status, and blocking determination for that requirement in the context of that placement. This is the operational record that compliance teams create, review, and manage to clear workers for assignment.. Existence Justification: In staffing compliance operations, each placement compliance gate-check evaluates the worker and placement against multiple compliance requirements (I-9, E-Verify, BGC, drug screen, credentials, safety training, client-specific mandates), and each compliance requirement is checked across thousands of placements. The business actively manages individual requirement check results with pass/fail/waived status, evidence references, waiver authorizations, and blocking determinations. This is not a derived correlation—it is the operational compliance checklist that humans create, review, approve, and use to block non-compliant placements from going active.'
}

Table compliance.audit_finding {
  audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each audit finding record. Primary key.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance audit during which this finding was identified']
  compliance_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to compliance_audit']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to compliance_requirement']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific compliance requirement that was violated or not met']
  affected_population_size varchar [note: 'Total number of workers, placements, or records affected by this specific finding']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date on which this finding was formally closed after successful remediation and verification']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the recommended or required corrective action to remediate this finding']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the corrective action must be completed and verified']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding mandates a formal corrective action plan']
  deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of deficient instances found within the tested sample (e.g., 15 out of 100 I-9 forms incomplete)']
  evidence_provided varchar [note: 'Description or reference to the audit evidence supporting this finding (document references, interview notes, system screenshots)']
  finding_date date [note: 'The date on which this finding was identified during audit fieldwork']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specific deficiency, gap, or non-compliance instance discovered']
  finding_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for this finding within the audit (e.g., F-001, F-002)']
  finding_severity varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to this specific finding based on potential impact and likelihood of harm']
  finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the finding: open (newly identified), in_remediation (corrective action underway), pending_verification (awaiting closure approval), closed (remediated and verified), accepted_risk (acknowledged but not remediated)']
  remediation_evidence varchar [note: 'Description or reference to evidence demonstrating that the finding has been remediated']
  responsible_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or team responsible for remediating this finding']
  sample_size_tested varchar [note: 'Number of records, transactions, or workers tested in the audit sample that led to this finding']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Finding relationship between compliance_audit and compliance_requirement. It captures each specific deficiency, gap, or non-compliance instance discovered during an audit that relates to a specific regulatory or contractual requirement. Each record links one compliance_audit to one compliance_requirement with attributes that describe the finding severity, remediation status, corrective actions, and evidence. Findings are actively managed business entities with their own lifecycle from identification through remediation to closure.. Existence Justification: In staffing compliance operations, audits systematically examine multiple regulatory and contractual requirements, and each requirement can be evaluated across many audits over time. Audit findings are actively managed business entities with their own lifecycle—compliance teams create finding records, assign owners, track remediation progress, manage corrective action plans, and report on finding trends. The business explicitly manages findings as distinct operational records, not as embedded attributes of audits.'
}

Table compliance.violation_breach {
  violation_breach_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each violation-requirement breach record. Primary key.']
  regulatory_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory violation incident record. Each breach record is associated with exactly one violation incident.']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific compliance requirement that was breached in this violation incident.']
  affected_population_per_requirement varchar [note: 'Number of workers or clients affected by this specific requirement breach. A violation may affect different populations for different requirements (e.g., 50 workers for I-9 violations, 5 workers for OSHA violations in the same incident).']
  breach_date date [note: 'The specific date on which this compliance requirement was breached within the context of this violation incident. May differ from the overall violation discovery date if the breach occurred earlier or if multiple requirements were breached at different times within the same incident.']
  breach_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing how this specific compliance requirement was violated within the incident. Provides requirement-specific context beyond the overall violation description.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this violation-requirement breach record was created in the compliance system.']
  penalty_amount_per_requirement decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty amount allocated to this specific requirement breach. When a violation involves multiple requirements, the total penalty is allocated across the breached requirements. Enables penalty trending and risk assessment by requirement type.']
  remediation_completed_date_per_requirement date [note: 'Actual date on which remediation for this specific requirement breach was completed and verified. Used to calculate remediation cycle time by requirement type.']
  remediation_due_date_per_requirement date [note: 'Target completion date for remediation of this specific requirement breach. Different requirements may have different regulatory deadlines or internal SLAs for remediation.']
  remediation_owner varchar [note: 'Individual or team responsible for remediating this specific requirement breach. Different requirements may have different remediation owners based on functional responsibility (e.g., HR for I-9, Safety for OSHA).']
  remediation_status_per_requirement varchar [note: 'Current remediation status for this specific requirement breach. Enables tracking remediation progress per requirement when a violation involves multiple requirements with different remediation timelines and owners.']
  repeat_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specific requirement has been violated in previous incidents. Critical for regulatory reporting and risk assessment, as repeat violations typically carry higher penalties and regulatory scrutiny.']
  root_cause_per_requirement varchar [note: 'Root cause analysis specific to this requirement breach. A violation may have different root causes for different requirements (e.g., system failure for one requirement, training gap for another).']
  severity_per_requirement varchar [note: 'Severity classification specific to this requirement breach within the violation incident. A single violation may have different severity levels for different requirements breached (e.g., critical for I-9 non-compliance, moderate for recordkeeping delay).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this breach record. Reflects updates to remediation status, penalty allocation, or other breach-specific attributes.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the breach relationship between a regulatory violation incident and the specific compliance requirements that were violated. Each record links one regulatory violation to one compliance requirement that was breached, capturing breach-specific attributes including severity assessment per requirement, financial penalty allocation per requirement, remediation status and timeline per requirement, affected population count per requirement, and breach narrative. Supports multi-requirement violation tracking, penalty allocation by requirement, remediation workflow per requirement, and regulatory reporting on repeat violations by requirement type.. Existence Justification: In staffing compliance operations, a single regulatory violation incident routinely breaches multiple compliance requirements simultaneously (e.g., an I-9 audit finding may violate federal I-9 completion requirements, state-specific verification requirements, and client-specific documentation mandates). Conversely, a specific compliance requirement (e.g., I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification) is violated across many separate incidents over time. The business actively manages this many-to-many relationship by tracking severity, penalties, remediation status, and affected populations per requirement within each violation, enabling requirement-level remediation workflows, penalty allocation, and regulatory reporting on repeat violations by requirement type.'
}

Table compliance.onboarding_compliance_checkpoint {
  onboarding_compliance_checkpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for onboarding_compliance_checkpoint']
  onboarding_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the onboarding engagement for which this compliance requirement is being tracked']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance requirement being fulfilled in this onboarding engagement']
  verified_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal staff member who verified completion of this compliance requirement for this onboarding engagement.']
  bgc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the background check screening process for this worker. [Moved from onboarding_engagement: Background check is a specific compliance requirement. This status belongs to the compliance_requirement_id for background check within the checkpoint association.] [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_required|pending|in_progress|clear|conditional_clear|adverse_action|failed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  checkpoint_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this compliance checkpoint record. Primary key for the association.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when this compliance requirement was fully satisfied for this onboarding engagement. Null if not yet completed.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this compliance requirement fulfillment within this onboarding engagement. Tracks progression from initiation through completion or waiver.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this compliance checkpoint record was created.']
  drug_screen_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the drug screening test for this worker. [Moved from onboarding_engagement: Drug screening is a specific compliance requirement. This status belongs to the compliance_requirement_id for drug screen within the checkpoint association.] [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_required|pending|scheduled|completed_negative|completed_positive|cancelled|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  due_date date [note: 'Calculated or assigned deadline by which this compliance requirement must be completed for this onboarding engagement, based on grace_period_days and onboarding timeline.']
  everify_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the E-Verify electronic employment eligibility verification process. [Moved from onboarding_engagement: E-Verify is a specific compliance requirement. This status belongs to the compliance_requirement_id for E-Verify within the checkpoint association.] [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_required|pending|submitted|employment_authorized|tentative_nonconfirmation|final_nonconfirmation|case_closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  i9_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the I-9 form was fully completed and verified. [Moved from onboarding_engagement: I-9 completion date is relationship data between the I-9 compliance requirement and this onboarding engagement. Should be tracked as completion_date in the checkpoint association.]']
  i9_completion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification form completion for this worker. [Moved from onboarding_engagement: I-9 is a specific compliance requirement. This status belongs to the compliance_requirement_id for I-9 within the checkpoint association, not as a hardcoded attribute on onboarding_engagement.]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, issues, or special circumstances related to this compliance requirement fulfillment for this onboarding engagement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this compliance checkpoint record was last updated.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the completion of this compliance requirement was verified by staff for this onboarding engagement.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify completion of this compliance requirement for this onboarding engagement.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance requirement was waived for this specific onboarding engagement due to business exception or inapplicability.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why this compliance requirement was waived for this onboarding engagement. Required when waiver_flag is true.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the fulfillment tracking between compliance requirements and onboarding engagements. It captures the completion status, verification details, and waiver decisions for each compliance requirement within the context of a specific worker onboarding engagement. Each record links one compliance requirement to one onboarding engagement with attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, each onboarding engagement must satisfy multiple compliance requirements (I-9, E-Verify, background checks, drug screens, client-specific mandates, federal contractor requirements), and each compliance requirement applies to many onboarding engagements across different workers and placements. The business actively manages compliance checkpoint completion as an operational process, tracking status, verification, waivers, and due dates for each requirement-engagement pair.'
}

Table compliance.state_credential_requirement {
  state_credential_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this state credential requirement record. Primary key.']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the credential that is required or recognized by this state compliance rule']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the state compliance rule that mandates or recognizes this credential']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this state credential requirement record was created.']
  documentation_required varchar [note: 'Description of specific documentation, records, or forms that must be maintained to demonstrate compliance with this credential requirement under this state rule.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this credential requirement becomes enforceable under this specific state compliance rule. Moved from state_compliance_rule because effective dates vary by credential within the same rule.']
  enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the state or local government agency responsible for enforcing this specific credential requirement. Denormalized from state_compliance_rule for operational convenience.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this credential requirement ceases to be enforceable under this state compliance rule. Null for ongoing requirements. Moved from state_compliance_rule because expiration dates vary by credential within the same rule.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether possession of this credential is mandatory (true) or optional/recommended (false) under this state compliance rule. Drives placement eligibility logic.']
  jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state abbreviation where this credential requirement applies. Denormalized from state_compliance_rule for query performance in multi-state compliance checks.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty amount in USD for non-compliance with this specific credential requirement under this state rule. May differ from the general rule penalty when credential-specific fines apply.']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this credential requirement indicating whether it is actively enforced, pending implementation, superseded by newer rules, or repealed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this state credential requirement record was last updated.']
  verification_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this credential must be verified under this state compliance rule (e.g., one-time at hire, annually, per placement). Varies by state and credential combination.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the regulatory requirement relationship between state compliance rules and credentials. It captures which credentials are mandated or recognized by specific state-level compliance regulations, including jurisdiction-specific enforcement details, effective periods, and penalty structures. Each record links one state compliance rule to one credential with attributes that exist only in the context of this state-specific regulatory requirement.. Existence Justification: In multi-state staffing operations, a single state compliance rule (e.g., California AB5 independent contractor classification) mandates multiple credentials (IC agreement, worker classification documentation, training certificates), and a single credential (e.g., OSHA 10 safety certification) satisfies requirements across multiple state rules (state-specific safety mandates, prevailing wage requirements, government contract rules). Compliance teams actively manage this matrix to ensure jurisdiction-aware placement eligibility and audit readiness.'
}

Table compliance.checklist_compliance_rule {
  checklist_compliance_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this specific mapping between a state compliance rule and a task checklist template. Primary key.']
  last_modified_by_user_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who most recently modified this mapping']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this compliance rule mapping']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the state-specific compliance rule that must be incorporated into the checklist']
  task_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the task checklist template that must incorporate this compliance rule']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this mapping indicating whether this compliance rule is currently enforced for this checklist. Values: active (enforced), inactive (not enforced), suspended (temporarily disabled).']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance rule was first mapped to this checklist template']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this specific compliance rule becomes required for this specific checklist template. May differ from the rule\'s own effective_date if phased rollout across checklists is needed.']
  enforcement_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for enforcing this compliance rule within this specific checklist context. Indicates urgency and resource allocation for compliance verification. Values: critical, high, medium, low.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this mapping configuration']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance rule is mandatory (True) or optional/recommended (False) for this specific checklist. A rule may be mandatory for some checklists but optional for others based on placement type or client requirements.']
  sla_hours_override varchar [note: 'Checklist-specific override for the time allowed to complete tasks related to this compliance rule. Overrides the checklist\'s default sla_hours when this specific rule has stricter or more lenient timing requirements.']
  task_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position indicating where compliance-related tasks for this rule should appear within the checklist workflow. Different checklists may sequence the same rule differently based on their overall task flow.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational mapping between state compliance rules and task checklist templates. It captures which state-specific compliance requirements must be incorporated into which onboarding/offboarding checklists, with enforcement metadata that exists only in the context of this relationship. Each record links one state compliance rule to one task checklist template with attributes governing how that rule is enforced within that specific checklist context.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, each state compliance rule (e.g., California Ban-the-Box, New York Salary Transparency) must be incorporated into multiple task checklist templates across different placement types, divisions, and client-specific scenarios. Conversely, each task checklist template must incorporate multiple state compliance rules based on the jurisdictions where workers will be placed. The business actively manages these mappings with enforcement metadata including effective dates, mandatory vs. optional status, task sequencing, SLA overrides, and enforcement priority that vary by checklist context.'
}

Table compliance.staff_compliance_certification {
  staff_compliance_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each staff compliance certification record. Primary key for this association.']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific compliance requirement that must be fulfilled by the staff member']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the internal employee who must complete this compliance requirement']
  verified_by_staff_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the staff_profile_id of the compliance officer or manager who verified and approved this certification completion.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date when this compliance requirement was assigned to the staff member. Marks the start of the compliance obligation period.']
  certification_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this certification expires and must be renewed. Null for one-time requirements that do not expire.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this certification: assigned (requirement assigned but not started), in_progress (training or documentation underway), completed (requirement fulfilled), expired (certification lapsed and requires renewal), waived (exemption granted), overdue (past due date without completion).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this certification record was created.']
  documentation_submitted_date date [note: 'Date when the staff member submitted required documentation for this compliance requirement (e.g., certificates, attestations, proof of training).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next recertification, re-verification, or compliance review for this staff member on this requirement. Calculated based on compliance_frequency and re_verification_interval_days from the requirement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from compliance team or staff member regarding this certification (e.g., special circumstances, follow-up actions, audit findings).']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the staff member completed the required training or certification activity. Null if not yet completed or if requirement does not involve training.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this certification record was last updated.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance team verified and approved the completion of this requirement.']
  waiver_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a waiver or exemption was granted for this staff member on this requirement (e.g., due to prior certification, role exemption, or business exception).']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of why a waiver was granted for this certification requirement. Null if no waiver granted.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the certification relationship between compliance_requirement and staff_profile. It captures the assignment, completion, and ongoing tracking of mandatory compliance requirements for internal Staffing Hr employees. Each record links one compliance_requirement to one staff_profile with attributes that track certification status, completion dates, and recertification schedules for regulatory audit readiness.. Existence Justification: In staffing and HR operations, compliance requirements (I-9 training, OFCCP certification, privacy law training, background check training) are assigned to multiple internal staff members based on role, jurisdiction, and client contracts. Each staff member must complete multiple compliance requirements throughout their employment lifecycle. The business actively manages these certifications as operational records with completion tracking, recertification schedules, and audit trail requirements.'
}

Table compliance.establishment {
  establishment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for establishment']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent organization or corporate entity that owns this establishment for consolidated reporting.']
  parent_establishment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on establishment (parent_establishment_id)']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the establishment for regulatory filings and compliance correspondence.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite, floor, or building number.']
  affirmative_action_plan_due_date date [note: 'Next due date for submission or update of the establishment-level Affirmative Action Plan.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the establishment is physically located.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date the establishment ceased operations or was closed, triggering final compliance reporting obligations.']
  compliance_officer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual responsible for regulatory compliance at this establishment.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the establishment location.']
  county_name varchar [note: 'County or parish name for local jurisdiction compliance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the establishment record was first created in the compliance system.']
  doing_business_as_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or DBA name under which the establishment operates, if different from legal name.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet identifier for the establishment used in federal contracting and OFCCP reporting.']
  e_verify_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the establishment enrolled in the E-Verify program for employment eligibility verification.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the establishment became operational and subject to compliance tracking.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for establishment compliance communications.']
  establishment_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the establishment used in regulatory filings and compliance reporting.']
  establishment_name varchar [note: 'Legal or operating name of the establishment as registered with regulatory authorities.']
  establishment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the establishment in the compliance tracking system.']
  establishment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the establishment based on its operational role within the organization.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for regulatory correspondence and compliance documentation.']
  federal_contract_value_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total value of federal contracts held by this establishment in U.S. dollars, determining OFCCP reporting thresholds.']
  federal_employer_identification_number varchar [note: 'IRS-issued employer identification number for tax reporting and compliance purposes.']
  i9_designated_representative_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual authorized to complete and sign Form I-9 on behalf of the establishment.']
  is_e_verify_participant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the establishment is enrolled in the federal E-Verify program.']
  is_eeoc_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the establishment meets the threshold for mandatory EEO-1 Component 1 reporting.']
  is_federal_contractor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the establishment is subject to OFCCP federal contractor compliance requirements.']
  is_osha_recordkeeping_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the establishment is required to maintain OSHA 300 logs based on industry and size.']
  joint_employment_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of joint employment or co-employment risk for this establishment based on client relationship structure.']
  last_dol_wage_hour_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent DOL Wage and Hour Division audit or investigation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the establishment record was last updated in the compliance system.']
  last_ofccp_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent OFCCP compliance audit or desk audit for this establishment.']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit NAICS code identifying the primary industry classification of the establishment for regulatory reporting.']
  osha_establishment_number varchar [note: 'OSHA-assigned establishment identifier for injury and illness recordkeeping and reporting.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the establishment.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code for the establishment address.']
  sic_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit SIC code for legacy regulatory reporting and compliance tracking.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the establishment location.']
  total_employee_count varchar [note: 'Total number of employees working at this establishment for EEOC and OFCCP headcount reporting.']
  workers_compensation_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number for workers compensation insurance coverage at this establishment.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for establishment. Referenced by establishment_id.'
}

Table credentialing.credential {
  credential_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each credential definition record. This is the primary key for the credential master catalog. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — this product defines the taxonomy of credential types recognized by Staffing Hr, not a candidate-held instance.']
  advance_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before expiration that the system should trigger a renewal notification to the candidate and recruiter (e.g., 30, 60, 90 days). Enables proactive credential management to prevent placement disruptions due to lapsed credentials.']
  assessment_passing_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum passing score required for skills assessments or competency tests associated with this credential type (e.g., 70.00 for 70%). Null for non-assessment credential types. Used to evaluate candidate assessment results against the required threshold.']
  assessment_score_type varchar [note: 'Defines how the assessment result is measured and reported for this credential type. Percentage (0-100%), raw score (numeric count), pass/fail (binary outcome), or grade (letter grade). Determines how passing_score is interpreted.']
  bgc_check_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of background check (BGC) associated with this credential when it involves a Sterling Background Check screening. Drives the BGC order type submitted to Sterling. None indicates the credential does not involve a background check component. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: criminal|drug_screen|employment_verification|education_verification|license_verification|credit|none|reference_check|sex_offender|mvr — promote to reference product]']
  ceu_hours_required decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of Continuing Education Unit (CEU) or professional development hours required per renewal cycle for this credential. Null if CEU is not required. Used to validate renewal completeness before credential re-issuance.']
  ceu_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential requires completion of Continuing Education Units (CEU) or Continuing Professional Development (CPD) hours as part of the renewal process. When True, the renewal workflow must capture CEU completion evidence.']
  clearance_level varchar [note: 'For security clearance credential types, specifies the clearance level (e.g., Confidential, Secret, Top Secret, Top Secret/SCI). Null or \'none\' for non-clearance credentials. Critical for government staffing vertical placement eligibility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credential definition record was first created in the Staffing Hr data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 Type II compliance requirements.']
  credential_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the credential type. Drives workflow routing, compliance rules, and renewal logic. Values: license (professional state/federal license), certification (industry-recognized cert), clearance (security clearance), assessment (skills/competency test), immunization (health/vaccination record), safety_certification (OSHA, HAZMAT, etc.). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.']
  credential_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code identifying this credential type across all systems (e.g., RN_LICENSE, OSHA_30, CDL_CLASS_A, CPA_CERT). Used as the business key in ATS, VMS, and compliance systems. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  credential_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the credential, including its purpose, scope, and any special requirements or conditions. Provides context for recruiters, compliance officers, and candidates about what the credential entails and why it is required.']
  credential_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the credential, certification, license, or qualification (e.g., \'Registered Nurse License\', \'OSHA 30-Hour Construction Safety\', \'Commercial Driver License Class A\', \'Certified Public Accountant\'). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.']
  credential_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this credential definition in the Staffing Hr catalog. Active credentials are available for assignment to candidates and placement eligibility checks. Deprecated credentials are retained for historical reference only. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  drug_screen_panel varchar [note: 'Specifies the drug screening panel type when this credential involves a drug screen (e.g., \'5-Panel\', \'10-Panel\', \'12-Panel\', \'DOT 5-Panel\'). Null if the credential does not involve drug screening. Aligns with DOT and employer-specific drug testing requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this credential definition became active and applicable in the Staffing Hr catalog. Used to determine which credential definitions were in effect at any given point in time for historical compliance audits.']
  everify_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential type triggers an E-Verify case submission requirement. Relevant for federal contractor placements and states with mandatory E-Verify laws. Integrates with the compliance.everify_case product.']
  external_credential_code varchar [note: 'The identifier for this credential type as recorded in the originating source system (e.g., Bullhorn credential type ID, Sterling check type code, SAP SuccessFactors qualification ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and deduplication.']
  flsa_relevance varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this credential is relevant to FLSA worker classification determinations (e.g., professional exemption credentials like CPA, PE, RN may support exempt classification). Supports wage and hour compliance analysis.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'The regulatory or professional body that oversees and mandates this credential (e.g., \'EEOC\', \'DOL\', \'OSHA\', \'OFCCP\', \'ASA\', \'SHRM\', \'State Nursing Board\'). May differ from the issuing authority. Used for regulatory reporting and compliance mapping.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the credential expiration date during which the credential is still considered valid for placement purposes (e.g., 30-day grace period while renewal is in process). Zero or null means no grace period is allowed.']
  i9_supporting_doc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential type can serve as a supporting document for I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification (e.g., U.S. Passport, Permanent Resident Card, Employment Authorization Document). Drives I-9 document collection workflows during onboarding.']
  immunization_type varchar [note: 'For immunization credential types, specifies the vaccine or immunization name (e.g., \'Hepatitis B\', \'MMR\', \'TB Test\', \'COVID-19\', \'Influenza\', \'Varicella\'). Null for non-immunization credentials. Required for healthcare staffing vertical compliance.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'The staffing industry vertical for which this credential is applicable. Drives placement eligibility rules and compliance checks by vertical. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthcare|information_technology|skilled_trades|finance|government|general|education|energy|legal|manufacturing — promote to reference product]']
  is_expirable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential type has an expiration date. When True, candidate credential instances must track expiration and renewal. When False, the credential is considered permanent once earned (e.g., some degree-based certifications). Drives renewal workflow triggers.']
  is_multi_state boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential is recognized across multiple states or jurisdictions (e.g., Nurse Licensure Compact states, CDL federal reciprocity). When True, a single credential instance may satisfy requirements in multiple states. Relevant for travel staffing and multi-state workforce deployments.']
  is_placement_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether possession of this credential is a mandatory prerequisite for placement eligibility in the applicable industry vertical or job type. When True, candidates without a valid instance of this credential cannot be submitted or placed. Drives automated compliance gates in the ATS and VMS.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the organization, agency, or body that issues this credential (e.g., \'State Board of Nursing\', \'OSHA\', \'PMI\', \'CompTIA\', \'IRS\', \'Department of Transportation\'). Critical for verification routing and compliance validation.']
  issuing_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country in which this credential is issued and recognized (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Supports international staffing operations and GDPR/CCPA compliance scoping.']
  issuing_state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code where this credential is issued and valid (e.g., CA, NY, TX). Applicable for state-specific professional licenses (e.g., state nursing licenses, state CPA licenses, state CDL). Null for nationally or internationally recognized credentials.']
  license_number_format varchar [note: 'Regular expression or format pattern that defines the valid format for license or certification numbers issued under this credential type (e.g., \'^RN[0-9]{6}$\' for a state nursing license). Used for data validation when candidates enter their credential instance details.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or special instructions related to this credential type (e.g., \'Requires notarized copy\', \'Must be verified within 30 days of placement start\', \'Accepted only from accredited institutions\'). Visible to credentialing coordinators and compliance staff.']
  osha_standard_ref varchar [note: 'For safety certifications, the specific OSHA standard or regulation that mandates or governs this credential (e.g., \'29 CFR 1926.503\' for fall protection, \'29 CFR 1910.120\' for HAZWOPER). Null for non-safety credentials. Supports OSHA compliance reporting.']
  redeployment_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a valid instance of this credential contributes to a candidate\'s eligibility for redeployment to a new assignment without re-credentialing. Supports the redeployment workflow in the placement domain.']
  renewal_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Standard renewal period for this credential expressed in months (e.g., 12 for annual, 24 for biennial, 36 for triennial). Null if the credential does not expire. Used to calculate expected renewal dates and trigger advance notification workflows. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'The date on which this credential definition was retired or deprecated from the Staffing Hr catalog. Null if the credential is still active. Retained for historical reference and audit trail purposes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this credential definition originated (e.g., Bullhorn ATS, Sterling Background Check, SAP SuccessFactors, Beeline VMS). Supports data lineage and master data management reconciliation.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the credential category (e.g., within \'license\': nursing, engineering, financial, transportation; within \'certification\': project management, IT, healthcare). Enables fine-grained filtering and reporting across staffing verticals.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credential definition record was last modified in the Staffing Hr data platform. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data quality monitoring.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The required method for verifying this credential type. Primary source verification contacts the issuing authority directly (required for healthcare licenses). Document review accepts copies of certificates. Third-party uses services like Sterling Background Check. Self-attestation accepts candidate declaration. Automated uses API-based verification.']
  verification_source varchar [note: 'The specific system, database, or service used to verify this credential (e.g., \'Sterling Background Check\', \'NURSYS National Nurse License Verification\', \'SAM.gov Exclusions\', \'State DOT CDL Database\', \'FINRA BrokerCheck\'). Informs the verification workflow routing.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incrementing version number for this credential definition record, supporting optimistic concurrency control and change history tracking. Incremented each time the credential definition is materially updated (e.g., renewal cycle change, new governing body).']

  Note: 'Master record for every credential, certification, license, qualification, immunization, or safety certification type recognized by Staffing Hr across all staffing verticals (healthcare, IT, skilled trades, finance, government). Defines the credential taxonomy including credential name, issuing authority, governing body, credential category (license, certification, clearance, assessment, immunization, safety_certification), applicable industry vertical, required renewal cycle, expiration rules, and whether the credential is mandatory for placement eligibility. Covers professional licenses (RN, CPA, PE, CDL), safety certifications (OSHA 10/30/510), immunizations (Hep B, MMR, TB, COVID-19), security clearances, and skills assessments. This is the SSOT for credential definitions — distinct from a candidate\'s actual held credential instance.'
}

Table credentialing.credential_instance {
  credential_instance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a specific credential instance held by a candidate. This is the primary key for the credential_instance product and serves as the operational SSOT identifier for placement eligibility checks, expiry alerting, and renewal workflows.']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'The Sterling Background Check order identifier associated with this credential instance when the credential category is \'background_check\' or \'drug_screen\'. Enables direct lookup and reconciliation with Sterling BGC system records.']
  credential_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_document. Business justification: A credential instance (e.g., RN license held by candidate) should reference the primary supporting document (e.g., scanned license PDF). This creates a direct link from the credential instance to its ']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the credential type definition (e.g., OSHA 10, RN License, PMP, Background Check, Drug Screen) that classifies this instance. Drives renewal rules, expiration logic, and compliance requirements.']
  drug_screen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.drug_screen. Business justification: A credential instance representing drug screen clearance should link to the actual drug screen transaction. This allows tracking which drug screen event established the credential instance. Removing d']
  education_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.education_verification. Business justification: A credential instance representing an educational credential (e.g., BSN degree) should link to the verification transaction that validated it. This allows tracking which verification event established']
  employment_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.employment_verification. Business justification: A credential instance representing employment history verification should link to the verification transaction that validated it.
