This allows tracking which verification event established the credenti']
  license_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.license_verification. Business justification: A credential instance representing a professional license should link to the verification transaction that validated it. This allows tracking which verification event established the credential instan']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate who holds this credential instance. Links the credential to the worker profile in the Applicant Tracking System (ATS) for placement eligibility evaluation.']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance requirement record that mandates this credential for a specific placement, client, or regulatory context. Links this credential instance to the compliance.compliance_requirement product for eligibility gate enforcement.']
  skills_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.skills_assessment. Business justification: A credential instance representing a skills certification should link to the assessment transaction that validated it. This allows tracking which assessment event established the credential instance. ']
  training_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.training_record. Business justification: A credential instance representing training completion should link to the training record that established it. This allows tracking which training event established the credential instance. No columns']
  alert_threshold_days varchar [note: 'The number of days before expiration at which automated renewal alerts should be triggered for this credential instance. Allows per-credential override of the default system alert threshold to accommodate varying renewal lead times by credential type.']
  bgc_result varchar [note: 'The adjudicated result of the background check from Sterling BGC. \'Clear\' = no disqualifying findings; \'Consider\' = findings requiring individualized assessment per FCRA adverse action rules; \'Adverse\' = disqualifying finding. Applicable only when credential_category = \'background_check\'.']
  ceu_completed decimal(5,2) [note: 'The number of Continuing Education Units (CEUs) or contact hours the candidate has completed toward the current renewal cycle requirement. Compared against ceu_required to determine renewal readiness.']
  ceu_required decimal(5,2) [note: 'The total number of Continuing Education Units (CEUs) or contact hours required to renew this credential for the current renewal cycle. Used to track renewal readiness and drive learning management system (LMS) enrollment recommendations.']
  clearance_granting_agency varchar [note: 'The U.S. government agency that granted the security clearance (e.g., \'DoD\', \'DHS\', \'CIA\', \'NSA\'). Applicable only when credential_category = \'security_clearance\'. Required for government contract staffing compliance.']
  clearance_level varchar [note: 'The U.S. government security clearance level granted to the candidate. Applicable only when credential_category = \'security_clearance\'. Drives eligibility for government and defense staffing placements requiring cleared personnel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credential instance record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOC 2 Type II, FCRA, and GDPR data processing records.']
  credential_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the credential type for reporting and eligibility filtering. Drives specialized attribute applicability (e.g., titer results for immunizations, clearance level for security clearances). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: license|certification|background_check|drug_screen|immunization|security_clearance|training|assessment|education — promote to reference product]']
  days_until_expiration varchar [note: 'Calculated number of calendar days remaining until this credential expires as of the last refresh. Stored for alerting threshold comparisons and dashboard KPI reporting without requiring real-time date arithmetic. Negative values indicate already-expired credentials.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL or storage path to the scanned or digital copy of the credential document (certificate, license card, BGC report, immunization record) stored in the document management system. Access is role-restricted per SOC 2 Type II controls.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this credential instance expires and is no longer valid for placement purposes. Null indicates a non-expiring credential. Drives automated expiry alerting and renewal workflow initiation.']
  investigation_type varchar [note: 'The type of background investigation conducted for the security clearance (e.g., NACLC, SSBI, Tier 1-5). Applicable only when credential_category = \'security_clearance\'. Determines depth of vetting and clearance eligibility.']
  is_placement_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential instance currently satisfies placement eligibility requirements. Computed and stored as an operational flag to enable fast eligibility gate checks in the Bullhorn ATS without real-time recalculation. True = eligible; False = ineligible.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary active instance of this credential type for the candidate when multiple instances exist (e.g., multi-state licenses, renewed credentials with overlapping validity). True = primary instance used for eligibility checks.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the issuing authority originally issued or granted this credential to the candidate. Used to calculate credential age, validity windows, and continuing education unit (CEU) cycle start dates.']
  issuing_authority_name varchar [note: 'The name of the organization, government body, or professional association that issued this credential (e.g., \'State of California Board of Registered Nursing\', \'OSHA Training Institute\', \'PMI\', \'DOT\'). Used for primary source verification routing.']
  issuing_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country in which this credential was issued. Supports international staffing operations and GDPR/CCPA compliance for cross-border credential data.']
  issuing_state varchar [note: 'The U.S. state (two-letter USPS abbreviation) or country subdivision in which this credential was issued. Critical for multi-state license portability analysis and compact license determinations (e.g., Nurse Licensure Compact).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturer lot number of the vaccine administered for immunization credentials. Applicable only when credential_category = \'immunization\'. Required for healthcare facility compliance documentation and adverse event tracking.']
  new_expiration_date date [note: 'The updated expiration date assigned to this credential instance upon successful renewal approval. Populated when renewal_status = \'approved\'. Replaces the prior expiration_date for ongoing validity tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for credentialing coordinators to capture supplemental context about this credential instance — e.g., adjudication rationale for BGC \'consider\' results, special conditions attached to a license, or renewal exception approvals.']
  polygraph_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a polygraph examination was completed as part of the security clearance investigation. Applicable only when credential_category = \'security_clearance\'. Required for TS/SCI and certain agency-specific clearances.']
  renewal_application_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate or staffing coordinator initiated the renewal application with the issuing authority. Marks the start of the renewal lifecycle and is used to calculate renewal processing lead time.']
  renewal_authority_name varchar [note: 'The name of the organization or body responsible for processing the credential renewal (may differ from the original issuing authority in cases of delegated renewal processing).']
  renewal_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The monetary fee charged by the issuing authority for processing the credential renewal. Used for cost tracking, candidate reimbursement workflows, and workforce program budgeting.']
  renewal_fee_paid_by varchar [note: 'Indicates which party is responsible for paying the credential renewal fee. Drives accounts payable routing and candidate reimbursement processing in Oracle NetSuite ERP.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the credential renewal lifecycle for this instance. Drives renewal workflow routing and compliance alerting. \'Pending_documentation\' indicates the candidate must supply additional materials before the renewal authority can process the application.']
  renewal_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the completed renewal application and supporting documentation were formally submitted to the issuing authority. Distinct from renewal_application_date (initiation) — this is the official submission date.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system from which this credential record was ingested or created. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and reconciliation between Sterling BGC, manual uploads, and third-party registries.']
  titer_result varchar [note: 'The quantitative titer test result indicating immunity level for immunization credentials (e.g., \'1:8 positive\' for Hepatitis B). Applicable only when credential_category = \'immunization\'. Required for healthcare staffing placements in clinical settings.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credential instance record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and for audit trail compliance.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'The name or system identifier of the person or automated process that performed the most recent verification of this credential. Supports audit trail and accountability requirements for credentialing compliance reviews.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a specific credential held by a candidate — the instance of a credential type owned by a worker. Tracks credential number, issuing authority, issue date, expiration date, current verification status (pending, verified, expired, revoked), verification method, verification date, source system (Sterling BGC, manual upload, third-party registry), and extended attributes for specialized credential types (e.g., lot number and titer results for immunizations, card number for OSHA certifications, clearance level/granting agency/investigation type/polygraph details for security clearances). Also owns the full renewal lifecycle for the credential instance: renewal application date, submission date, renewal authority, renewal fee, renewal status (initiated, submitted, approved, denied, pending_documentation), new expiration date upon approval, and CEU requirements fulfilled. This is the operational SSOT for \'does this candidate currently hold this credential?\' and \'what is the renewal status?\' — drives placement eligibility checks, expiry alerting, and renewal workflows.'
}

Table credentialing.bgc_order {
  bgc_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the background check order record in the Staffing Hr data lakehouse. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  ap_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_account. Business justification: Background check vendors are suppliers with AP accounts. Staffing firms track vendor invoices, payment terms, 1099 reporting, and vendor performance. Links BGC orders to vendor payment processing and ']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement or assignment record associated with this background check, if the BGC was triggered at the placement stage rather than the job order stage.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: MSAs define BGC package standards (Sterling package codes, turnaround SLAs, adjudication criteria) that govern BGC order configuration. Contract compliance audits require tracing BGC orders to governi']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Background screening costs allocated to cost centers for departmental P&L tracking, budget variance analysis, and vertical-specific profitability reporting. Essential for financial planning and cost m']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: BGC expenses posted to specific GL accounts for financial statement preparation, expense recognition, and GAAP compliance. Required for month-end close, GL reconciliation, and external financial repor']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Background checks have location-specific requirements (county criminal searches, local ordinances, facility security clearances, site-specific access requirements). Real business process: location-bas']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) that triggered this background check order. Establishes the business context for why the BGC was initiated.']
  ordered_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Background check orders are initiated by internal recruiters or compliance specialists. Tracking which staff member ordered each BGC is essential for audit trails, compliance reporting, and operationa']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate for whom the background check order was placed. Links to the candidate master record in the Bullhorn ATS/CRM system.']
  actual_turnaround_hours varchar [note: 'The actual number of hours elapsed from order placement to completion of the background check. Compared against sla_turnaround_hours to assess vendor SLA compliance and Time to Fill (TTF) impact.']
  adjudication_status varchar [note: 'The hiring decision outcome based on the background check results. \'clear\' = no disqualifying findings; \'consider\' = findings require review; \'adverse_action_initiated\' = pre-adverse action notice sent to candidate; \'adverse_action_final\' = final adverse action taken; \'withdrawn\' = adjudication process stopped.']
  adverse_action_notice_date date [note: 'The date the pre-adverse action notice was sent to the candidate, as required by FCRA when a hiring decision is being made based on background check findings. Null if no adverse action was initiated.']
  client_package_code varchar [note: 'Client-specific package code or identifier that maps to the client\'s required screening package as defined in their VMS program or MSA. Used when client mandates a specific vendor package configuration.']
  client_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this background check was mandated by the client as a condition of placement, as specified in the client\'s Master Service Agreement (MSA) or program requirements.']
  completed_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time the background check order was fully completed and results were received from the screening vendor.']
  consent_date date [note: 'The date the candidate provided written authorization for the background check. Required for FCRA compliance documentation.']
  consent_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has provided written authorization for the background check to be conducted, as required by FCRA before a consumer report may be procured.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time this background check order record was first created in the Staffing Hr data lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  credit_check_result varchar [note: 'Result of the credit history check component, typically ordered for finance, accounting, or fiduciary roles. Governed by FCRA permissible purpose requirements. Classified confidential due to sensitive financial history.']
  criminal_check_result varchar [note: 'Result of the criminal history check component, covering county, state, and federal criminal records searches. \'consider\' indicates reportable criminal records were found per FCRA guidelines.']
  dispute_initiated_date date [note: 'The date the candidate formally initiated a dispute of the background check results with the screening vendor or consumer reporting agency, as permitted under FCRA. Null if no dispute was filed.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'The date the candidate\'s dispute was resolved by the screening vendor. Null if no dispute was filed or if dispute is still pending.']
  drug_screen_result varchar [note: 'Result of the drug screening component. \'negative\' = no substances detected; \'positive\' = prohibited substance detected; \'dilute\' = specimen did not meet concentration thresholds; \'cancelled\' = specimen could not be tested.']
  education_verification_result varchar [note: 'Result of the education credential verification component, confirming degrees, diplomas, and certifications as reported by the candidate.']
  employment_verification_result varchar [note: 'Result of the employment history verification component, confirming prior employer details, dates of employment, and job titles as reported by the candidate.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the background check results expire and a new check may be required per client, regulatory, or company policy. Supports credential expiration tracking and renewal workflows.']
  final_adverse_action_date date [note: 'The date the final adverse action decision was communicated to the candidate, following the mandatory waiting period after the pre-adverse action notice. Null if adverse action was not finalized.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'The staffing industry vertical for which this background check was ordered. Different verticals have distinct credentialing and screening mandates (e.g., healthcare requires OIG exclusion checks; government requires security clearance checks).']
  mvr_result varchar [note: 'Result of the motor vehicle record check component, ordered for roles requiring driving. Evaluates license validity, violations, and suspensions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recruiter, compliance officer, or adjudicator notes related to this background check order, such as special instructions, exception approvals, or adjudication rationale.']
  oig_exclusion_check_result varchar [note: 'Result of the OIG exclusion list check, required for healthcare staffing placements to ensure the candidate is not excluded from participation in federal healthcare programs (Medicare, Medicaid).']
  order_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time the background check order was placed with the screening vendor. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for this background check order, used in communications with the candidate, client, and screening vendor.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the background check order. \'ordered\' = submitted to vendor; \'in_progress\' = vendor is processing; \'completed\' = results received; \'disputed\' = candidate has filed a dispute; \'cancelled\' = order voided before completion.']
  ordered_by_user varchar [note: 'The internal Staffing Hr user (recruiter, coordinator, or compliance officer) who initiated and submitted the background check order. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  overall_result varchar [note: 'The consolidated result returned by the screening vendor summarizing all checks in the package. \'clear\' = all checks passed; \'consider\' = one or more checks returned findings requiring review; \'suspended\' = check could not be completed.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'The screening package ordered, defining the scope of checks included. Common packages include criminal, employment verification, education verification, drug screen, credit check, and motor vehicle record (MVR). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: criminal_only|employment_verification|education_verification|drug_screen|credit_check|mvr|criminal_plus_employment|comprehensive — promote to reference product]']
  position_type varchar [note: 'The employment classification of the position for which the background check was ordered. Determines applicable screening requirements and regulatory obligations (e.g., W-2 vs 1099, co-employment considerations).']
  report_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL or document reference to the full background check report stored in the Sterling portal or Staffing Hr document management system. Access is restricted to authorized compliance and HR personnel. Classified confidential due to sensitive screening results.']
  screening_type varchar [note: 'Classification of when and why the background check is being conducted in the employment lifecycle. Pre-employment and post-offer are most common; annual, random, and reasonable suspicion apply to ongoing workforce compliance programs.']
  sex_offender_registry_result varchar [note: 'Result of the national sex offender registry check component. Particularly required for placements in healthcare, education, childcare, and other vulnerable population settings.']
  sla_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the background check order was completed within the contracted SLA turnaround time. Derived operationally from comparing actual_turnaround_hours to sla_turnaround_hours; stored for reporting and vendor scorecard purposes.']
  sla_turnaround_hours varchar [note: 'The contracted or expected turnaround time in hours for the background check order to be completed, as defined in the SLA with the screening vendor. Used to measure vendor performance against KPIs.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this background check order record was sourced. Supports data lineage and reconciliation across Sterling Background Check, Bullhorn ATS/CRM, and manual entry workflows.']
  sterling_order_number varchar [note: 'External order identifier assigned by Sterling Background Check system upon order submission. Used for cross-referencing and status lookups in the Sterling vendor portal.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time this background check order record was last modified in the Staffing Hr data lakehouse. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing.']

  Note: 'Transactional record representing a background check (BGC) order placed with Sterling Background Check or another screening vendor for a specific candidate. Captures order date, package type (criminal, employment verification, education verification, drug screen, credit check, MVR), order status (ordered, in-progress, completed, disputed, cancelled), turnaround SLA, adjudication status, and the triggering job order or placement. Sourced from Sterling Background Check system.'
}

Table credentialing.bgc_result {
  bgc_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual background check component result record. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE — each bgc_result is one component line within a parent bgc_order screening event.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement or job order that triggered this background check order. Links screening results to the specific staffing engagement requiring credentialing.']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent background check order that produced this component result. One order produces multiple result records (one per check component). Maps to the bgc_order product.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate (subject of the background check) in the Candidate domain. Required for FCRA adverse action workflows and candidate credentialing status updates.']
  adjudication_decision varchar [note: 'The internal hiring team or compliance officer adjudication decision applied to this result component after reviewing findings. Distinct from the vendor-returned result_status — this is the employer\'s individualized assessment per EEOC guidance.']
  adverse_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this result component triggered or contributed to an adverse action (pre-adverse or final adverse) against the candidate. Required for FCRA adverse action workflow tracking and compliance reporting.']
  check_component_type varchar [note: 'The type of screening component this result record represents. Drives which component-specific extended attributes are populated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: criminal_search|employment_verification|education_verification|reference_check|drug_screen|credit_check|mvr|sex_offender_registry|global_watchlist — promote to reference product]']
  collection_date date [note: 'The date the drug screen specimen was collected from the candidate. Populated only when check_component_type = \'drug_screen\'. Used for chain-of-custody and turnaround time tracking.']
  collection_site varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the collection facility where the drug screen specimen was collected. Populated only when check_component_type = \'drug_screen\'. Used for chain-of-custody tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bgc_result record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  degree_type varchar [note: 'The type of academic degree or credential being verified for the education verification component. Populated only when check_component_type = \'education_verification\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high_school|associate|bachelor|master|doctorate|certificate|professional|other — promote to reference product]']
  discrepancy_found_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a discrepancy was identified between the candidate\'s stated information and the verified information for employment or education verification components. Triggers adjudication review when true.']
  dot_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this check component is subject to U.S. Department of Transportation (DOT) regulations. Applicable primarily to drug screens and MVR checks for safety-sensitive transportation positions.']
  drug_panel_type varchar [note: 'The drug testing panel configuration used for this drug screen component. Specifies the number and types of substances tested. Populated only when check_component_type = \'drug_screen\'.']
  education_verification_source varchar [note: 'The method or source used to verify the education credential. NSC = National Student Clearinghouse; direct_institution = contacted school registrar directly; third_party = alternate verification service. Populated only when check_component_type = \'education_verification\'.']
  employer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the employer whose employment record is being verified. Populated only when check_component_type = \'employment_verification\'.']
  employment_end_date date [note: 'The verified end date of the candidate\'s employment with the employer being checked. Null if currently employed. Populated only when check_component_type = \'employment_verification\'.']
  employment_start_date date [note: 'The verified start date of the candidate\'s employment with the employer being checked. Populated only when check_component_type = \'employment_verification\'.']
  employment_verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify employment history. \'direct\' = employer contacted directly; \'the_work_number\' = Equifax The Work Number automated service; \'equifax\' = Equifax employment data; \'third_party\' = other verification service. Populated only when check_component_type = \'employment_verification\'.']
  fcra_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all FCRA procedural requirements were satisfied for this result component, including permissible purpose, disclosure, authorization, and adverse action notice obligations.']
  field_of_study varchar [note: 'The academic major, concentration, or field of study associated with the degree being verified. Populated only when check_component_type = \'education_verification\'.']
  final_adverse_action_date date [note: 'Date the final adverse action notice was issued to the candidate after the mandatory FCRA waiting period (minimum 5 business days after pre-adverse notice). Null if no final adverse action was taken.']
  finding_date date [note: 'The date associated with the derogatory finding (e.g., date of criminal offense, date of driving violation). Used for look-back period analysis and individualized assessment under EEOC guidance.']
  finding_detail varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific finding or derogatory information returned by the screening vendor for this component. Populated only when result_status is \'consider\' or \'adverse\'. Confidential — used by adjudicators and compliance officers only.']
  graduation_date date [note: 'The date the candidate graduated or completed the degree program being verified. Populated only when check_component_type = \'education_verification\'.']
  institution_name varchar [note: 'Name of the educational institution whose degree or credential is being verified. Populated only when check_component_type = \'education_verification\'.']
  jurisdiction_searched varchar [note: 'The county, state, or geographic jurisdiction in which this check component was conducted. Relevant for criminal searches, sex offender registry, and MVR checks. Format: \'County, State\' or state abbreviation for statewide searches.']
  lab_processing_date date [note: 'The date the laboratory completed initial analysis of the drug screen specimen. Populated only when check_component_type = \'drug_screen\'. Used for TAT measurement between collection and lab result.']
  mro_decision varchar [note: 'The final verified result determination issued by the Medical Review Officer (MRO) after reviewing the laboratory result and any candidate explanation. This is the authoritative drug screen outcome. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: negative|positive|dilute_negative|dilute_positive|substituted|adulterated|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  mro_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Medical Review Officer (MRO) review for this drug screen result. MRO review is required for all DOT-regulated tests and non-negative results. Populated only when check_component_type = \'drug_screen\'.']
  ordered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time this specific check component was ordered from the screening vendor. Used to calculate component-level turnaround time.']
  overall_recommendation varchar [note: 'The reference contact\'s overall recommendation rating for the candidate as captured in the structured reference check interview. Populated only when check_component_type = \'reference_check\'.']
  pre_adverse_action_date date [note: 'Date the pre-adverse action notice was sent to the candidate as required by FCRA before a final adverse employment decision is made. Mandatory waiting period tracking starts from this date.']
  reference_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the professional reference contact interviewed for this reference check component. PII — restricted access. Populated only when check_component_type = \'reference_check\'.']
  reference_relationship varchar [note: 'The professional relationship of the reference contact to the candidate. Used to assess the credibility and relevance of the reference feedback. Populated only when check_component_type = \'reference_check\'.']
  rehire_eligibility varchar [note: 'The reference contact\'s stated eligibility recommendation for rehiring the candidate. A key structured rating from the reference check interview. Populated only when check_component_type = \'reference_check\'.']
  result_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time this component result was received from the screening vendor (Sterling or alternate). Used for turnaround time (TAT) measurement and SLA compliance tracking.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'The overall outcome status of this check component as returned by the screening vendor. \'clear\' = no derogatory findings; \'consider\' = findings present requiring adjudication review; \'adverse\' = disqualifying finding; \'pending\' = result not yet received; \'cancelled\' = component cancelled before completion.']
  screening_vendor varchar [note: 'The consumer reporting agency (CRA) or screening vendor that conducted and returned this check component result. Sterling is the primary system of record; other vendors may be used for specific programs.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'The biological specimen type collected for drug screen testing. Populated only when check_component_type = \'drug_screen\'. Urine is the standard DOT-mandated specimen type.']
  sterling_order_number varchar [note: 'External order identifier assigned by Sterling Background Check (or alternate screening vendor) for the parent screening order. Used for vendor reconciliation and status polling.']
  sterling_result_number varchar [note: 'External component-level result identifier assigned by Sterling or the screening vendor. Used for vendor-side traceability and dispute resolution.']
  substances_detected varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of substance names or drug classes detected in the drug screen specimen as reported by the MRO. Populated only when mro_decision is \'positive\'. Confidential — restricted to compliance and HR personnel.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bgc_result record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks result updates such as status changes, adjudication decisions, or MRO review completions.']

  Note: 'Consolidated SSOT for all screening and verification outcome data returned from Sterling or another screening vendor. Each record represents a single component result within a background check order or standalone screening event. Captures the check component type (criminal_search, employment_verification, education_verification, reference_check, sex_offender_registry, global_watchlist, drug_screen, credit_check, MVR), result status (clear, consider, adverse, pending, negative, positive, dilute, adulterated, substituted), finding details, county/jurisdiction searched, adjudication decision, adverse action flag, FCRA compliance flags, result received timestamp, and component-specific extended attributes. Drug screen attributes: panel type (5/10/12-panel), specimen type (urine, hair, oral fluid), collection site, collection date, lab processing date, MRO review status and decision, DOT vs. non-DOT flag, substances detected. Education verification attributes: institution name, degree type, field of study, graduation date, verification source (NSC, direct institution, third-party), verification outcome. Employment verification attributes: employer name, employment dates, job title, verification method (direct, The Work Number/Equifax), discrepancies found. Reference check attributes: reference contact name, relationship to candidate, structured ratings (work quality, reliability, communication, rehire eligibility), verbatim comments, overall recommendation. One bgc_order produces multiple bgc_result records. This is the single authoritative source for all screening outcomes — no separate sub-type products exist.'
}

Table credentialing.drug_screen {
  drug_screen_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for a drug screening event record. Primary key for the drug_screen data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete drug screening business event with a clear lifecycle from order through result.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement or assignment for which this drug screen was ordered. Supports pre-placement, post-offer, and random screening workflows.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account on whose behalf the drug screen was ordered. Supports client-specific screening policy enforcement and billing.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: MSAs specify drug screening requirements (DOT/non-DOT, panel types, MRO review standards). Contract compliance verification requires linking drug screens to MSA-mandated standards to demonstrate adher']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Drug screening costs allocated to cost centers for departmental expense tracking, budget management, and vertical-specific cost analysis. Enables cost center managers to track credentialing spend agai']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential. Business justification: Link the drug screen to the master credential definition (e.g., DOT 5-Panel Drug Screen, 10-Panel Drug Screen). This allows standardizing drug screen types across all transactions. Removing panel_type']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the background check package or bundle under which this drug screen was ordered. Drug screens are frequently ordered as part of a broader BGC package that may include criminal checks, employment verification, and credential verification.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Drug screen expenses posted to GL accounts for expense recognition, financial reporting, and compliance with accounting standards. Required for accurate P&L and balance sheet preparation.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order or requisition that triggered the drug screen requirement. Enables traceability from job requirement to screening outcome.']
  ordered_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Drug screens are ordered by internal staff (recruiters, onboarding coordinators). Tracking who initiated each screen is required for compliance audits, vendor reconciliation, and operational oversight']
  parent_screen_drug_screen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original drug screen record when this record represents a recollection or follow-up screen. Enables lineage tracking between an initial screen and any subsequent recollections ordered due to dilute, adulterated, or cancelled results.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate who is the subject of this drug screening event. Links to the candidate master record in the Bullhorn ATS/CRM system.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the testing laboratory as assigned by SAMHSA or Sterling Background Check. Enables lab-level quality and turnaround time reporting.']
  candidate_consent_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate provided written authorization and consent for the drug screen prior to specimen collection. Required under FCRA for background screening and state-specific drug testing consent laws.']
  chain_of_custody_number varchar [note: 'Unique chain-of-custody form number assigned at the time of specimen collection. The COC number tracks the specimen from collection through laboratory analysis and is required for DOT-regulated and legally defensible drug testing programs.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Date on which the specimen was collected from the candidate at the collection site. Critical for chain-of-custody documentation and DOT compliance timelines.']
  collection_site_city varchar [note: 'City where the specimen collection site is located. Supports geographic coverage analysis and candidate convenience reporting.']
  collection_site_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the collection site as assigned by Sterling Background Check or the collection network provider. Enables site-level performance and compliance reporting.']
  collection_site_name varchar [note: 'Name of the collection facility or clinic where the specimen was collected from the candidate. Used for vendor management, geographic coverage analysis, and chain-of-custody documentation.']
  collection_site_state varchar [note: 'U.S. state (two-letter abbreviation) where the specimen collection site is located. Used for state-specific compliance reporting and geographic coverage analysis.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the specimen was collected from the candidate. Required for DOT-regulated screenings and chain-of-custody documentation.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the candidate signed the drug screen authorization and consent form. Required for FCRA compliance documentation and legal defensibility of the screening program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this drug screen record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  dilute_result_action varchar [note: 'Action taken or required when a specimen is reported as dilute. Under DOT regulations, employers may require a second collection under direct observation for dilute results. Non-DOT programs follow client-specific policies. Populated only when result_status is dilute.']
  dot_agency varchar [note: 'The specific DOT modal agency whose regulations govern this drug screen when is_dot_regulated is true. Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) applies to CDL drivers; Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to aviation; Federal Railroad Administration (FRA) to rail; Federal Transit Administration (FTA) to transit; Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration (PHMSA) to pipeline workers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FMCSA|FAA|FRA|FTA|PHMSA|USCG|not_applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the drug screen result expires and a new screen may be required per client policy or regulatory mandate. Relevant for ongoing assignments, contract renewals, and random testing program management.']
  is_dot_regulated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this drug screen is subject to U.S. Department of Transportation (DOT) regulations under 49 CFR Part 40. DOT-regulated screens apply to safety-sensitive transportation positions (CDL drivers, aviation, rail, pipeline, transit). Non-DOT screens follow SAMHSA guidelines but without federal mandate.']
  is_observed_collection boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specimen collection was conducted under direct observation by a trained collector. Observed collections are required under DOT regulations for return-to-duty, follow-up, and certain dilute recollection scenarios.']
  lab_received_date date [note: 'Date on which the specimen was received by the testing laboratory for processing. Used to measure lab turnaround time and identify chain-of-custody gaps.']
  lab_reported_date date [note: 'Date on which the laboratory reported the initial test result to the Medical Review Officer (MRO) or directly to the ordering party. Supports turnaround time SLA measurement.']
  laboratory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the SAMHSA-certified laboratory that processed and analyzed the specimen. Required for chain-of-custody documentation and regulatory audit trails.']
  mro_decision varchar [note: 'Final verified result determination made by the Medical Review Officer (MRO) after reviewing laboratory results and any donor interview. The MRO decision is the authoritative result used for employment decisions. Negative-dilute may require a recollection under direct observation per DOT rules.']
  mro_decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the MRO issued the final verified result decision. Used to measure MRO review turnaround time and for compliance audit documentation.']
  mro_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the licensed physician serving as the Medical Review Officer for this drug screen. Required for DOT compliance documentation and chain-of-custody records.']
  mro_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Medical Review Officer (MRO) review process. MRO review is required for all DOT-regulated screens and recommended for non-DOT positive results. The MRO evaluates laboratory results, interviews the donor, and makes the final verified result determination.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, MRO comments, collection site observations, or compliance remarks associated with this drug screen event. Not used for structured data.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date on which the drug screen was ordered by the staffing firm or client. Represents the principal business event initiation timestamp for this transaction.']
  pass_fail_determination varchar [note: 'Business-level pass or fail determination applied by the staffing firm based on the MRO decision and client-specific drug testing policy. A negative MRO decision typically results in pass; positive results in fail. Inconclusive applies when the result requires further review or recollection.']
  recollection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a second specimen collection is required due to a dilute, adulterated, substituted, or cancelled result. Triggers a follow-up screening workflow in the credentialing process.']
  result_date date [note: 'Date on which the final verified result was reported and made available to the staffing firm. Represents the completion of the screening lifecycle.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Final result status of the drug screening event as reported by the laboratory and confirmed by the MRO. Negative indicates no substances detected above cutoff thresholds. Positive indicates one or more substances detected. Dilute, adulterated, and substituted indicate specimen validity issues. Cancelled indicates the test was voided.']
  screen_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known order number assigned by Sterling Background Check for this drug screening event. Used for vendor tracking, status inquiries, and reconciliation with Sterling invoices.']
  screen_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the drug screening event from order placement through final result delivery. Drives workflow routing and compliance tracking.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'The biological specimen type collected for drug testing. Urine is the most common and DOT-mandated specimen type. Hair testing provides a longer detection window. Oral fluid and breath are used for post-accident and reasonable suspicion testing.']
  sterling_order_number varchar [note: 'Unique order identifier assigned by the Sterling Background Check platform for this drug screen event. Used for API integration, status polling, result retrieval, and invoice reconciliation with Sterling.']
  substances_detected varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of substance categories detected above cutoff thresholds in a positive result (e.g., THC, cocaine, opiates, amphetamines, benzodiazepines). Populated only when result_status is positive. Protected health information subject to FCRA and HIPAA-adjacent privacy requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: THC|cocaine|opiates|amphetamines|PCP|benzodiazepines|barbiturates|methadone|propoxyphene|methaqualone|oxycodone|buprenorphine — promote to reference product]']
  turnaround_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days elapsed from the specimen collection date to the final result date. Used to measure screening program efficiency, vendor SLA compliance, and impact on Time to Start (TTS) for placements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this drug screen record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change detection, incremental processing, and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for a drug screening event ordered for a candidate. Tracks panel type (5-panel, 10-panel, 12-panel), specimen type (urine, hair, oral fluid, breath), collection site, collection date, lab processing date, result status (negative, positive, dilute, adulterated, substituted, cancelled, pending), substances detected, MRO (Medical Review Officer) review status, MRO decision, and DOT vs. non-DOT compliance flag. Sourced from Sterling Background Check drug screen module.'
}

Table credentialing.license_verification {
  license_verification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a single license verification transaction record in the credentialing domain.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Optional reference to the active placement or assignment that triggered this license verification requirement. Null if verification is performed outside of a specific placement context.']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'The order or case identifier from the Sterling Background Check system associated with this license verification, when the verification was performed as part of a broader BGC package. Enables cross-reference to the Sterling BGC record.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account whose credentialing requirements mandated this verification. Populated when client_required_flag is True. Links to the client master record in Bullhorn CRM.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: License verification costs allocated to cost centers for credentialing expense tracking by department/vertical. Supports budget variance analysis and cost center profitability reporting.']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential. Business justification: Link the verification transaction to the master credential definition being verified (e.g., RN License, CPA License). This allows standardizing credential types across all verification transactions. R']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Verification expenses posted to GL accounts for financial statement preparation and expense categorization. Required for accurate credentialing cost recognition and financial reporting.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order or requisition that triggered this license verification requirement. Supports traceability from job requirement to credential verification outcome.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate whose professional license is being verified. Links to the candidate master record in the Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: License verifications are often outsourced to third-party verification vendors. Enables vendor assignment, cost tracking, SLA monitoring, and quality management for verification services. New attribut']
  adverse_action_date date [note: 'Date on which the FCRA-required adverse action notification was sent to the candidate following an adverse verification outcome. Null if no adverse action was required. Required for FCRA compliance documentation.']
  adverse_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the verification result constitutes an adverse finding (e.g., suspended, revoked, not found) that requires adverse action notification procedures under FCRA. When True, triggers the FCRA adverse action workflow.']
  billable_to_client boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost of this license verification is billable to the client as part of the staffing engagement or MSA terms. Supports accounts receivable and client billing workflows in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  client_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this license verification was mandated by a specific client\'s credentialing requirements (e.g., hospital system, government agency) as opposed to being a standard internal credentialing policy check.']
  compact_states varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of two-letter state codes in which this compact license authorizes practice (e.g., \'CA,TX,FL,NY\'). Populated only when is_compact_license is True. Supports multi-state placement eligibility checks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this license verification record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker and supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  document_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier or file path referencing the stored verification evidence document (e.g., screenshot of registry result, PDF certificate copy, DocuSign document ID). Supports audit documentation and compliance record retention.']
  expiration_alert_date date [note: 'Date on which the license expiration alert notification was sent to the candidate and/or credentialing team. Populated when expiration_alert_sent is True.']
  expiration_alert_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an automated expiration alert notification has been sent to the candidate and/or credentialing team ahead of the license expiration date. Prevents duplicate alert sends and tracks notification compliance.']
  is_compact_license boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the license is issued under a multi-state compact agreement (e.g., Nurse Licensure Compact (NLC), Physical Therapy Compact). Compact licenses allow practice in multiple member states without separate licensure, which affects placement eligibility across state lines.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the official body that issued the license (e.g., \'California Board of Registered Nursing\', \'NASBA\', \'NCEES\', \'Texas DMV\'). Used to identify the primary source for verification.']
  issuing_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country that issued the license (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Supports international staffing operations and GDPR/CCPA compliance for cross-border credential verification.']
  issuing_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory code of the jurisdiction that issued the license (e.g., CA, TX, NY). For national licenses, use \'US\'. Supports multi-state compliance tracking for compact license states (e.g., Nurse Licensure Compact).']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the license expires or must be renewed as recorded on the license or registry. Null for licenses with no expiration. Critical for expiration tracking and automated renewal alerts.']
  license_holder_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual as it appears on the license at the time of verification. Used to confirm name match between the candidate record and the registry entry. May differ from current candidate name due to legal name changes.']
  license_issue_date date [note: 'Date the license was originally issued by the licensing authority. Used to calculate license tenure and assess experience level for credentialing compliance.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'The official license number as issued by the licensing authority (e.g., state nursing board, NASBA CPA registry, NCEES PE registry, DMV CDL number). This is the primary identifier submitted to the registry for verification lookup.']
  license_restrictions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any restrictions, conditions, or limitations noted on the license at the time of verification (e.g., \'Probationary - supervised practice only\', \'Restricted to outpatient settings\'). Null if no restrictions apply.']
  license_status_at_verification varchar [note: 'The status of the license as reported by the issuing authority or registry at the exact time of verification. Captures the point-in-time state independent of subsequent changes. Critical for compliance audit trails.']
  next_verification_date date [note: 'The date on which this license must be re-verified per credentialing policy or client SLA requirements. Drives automated renewal alerts and compliance scheduling. Typically set based on license expiration date or periodic re-verification intervals.']
  recheck_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why a recheck is required for this verification. Populated when recheck_required is True. Supports credentialing workflow prioritization and root cause analysis.']
  recheck_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this verification record requires a follow-up recheck due to an inconclusive result, registry unavailability, or pending license renewal. Drives credentialing workflow queues.']
  staffing_vertical varchar [note: 'The staffing industry vertical for which this license verification is being performed. Determines applicable credentialing standards and regulatory requirements (e.g., Joint Commission for healthcare, OFCCP for government). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthcare|information_technology|finance|government|skilled_trades|engineering|other — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this license verification record was most recently modified. Tracks the last state change for audit and data freshness purposes in the Silver layer pipeline.']
  verification_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'The fee charged by the verification source or third-party provider (e.g., Sterling, Nursys) for performing this license verification. Used for credentialing cost tracking and client billing reconciliation.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the license verification was performed against the registry or source. This is the principal business event date for the verification transaction, distinct from the record creation timestamp.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify the license. \'primary_source\' means direct contact with the issuing authority; \'third_party_registry\' means a recognized aggregator (e.g., Nursys, NASBA); \'manual\' means staff-performed lookup; \'automated_api\' means system-to-system integration (e.g., Sterling BGC API).']
  verification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for credentialing staff to record observations, discrepancies, follow-up actions, or contextual notes related to this verification (e.g., \'Name mismatch - candidate confirmed maiden name change\', \'Registry temporarily unavailable - manual call to board completed\').']
  verification_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this verification transaction, used in correspondence with candidates, clients, and licensing boards. Format: LV-{YYYY}-{sequence}.']
  verification_outcome varchar [note: 'The result of the license verification process as determined by the verification source. \'verified\' means the license was confirmed active and valid; \'not_found\' means no matching record was located; other values reflect adverse findings. This is the primary lifecycle status of the verification transaction.']
  verification_source_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific registry, database, or organization used as the verification source (e.g., \'Nursys\', \'NASBA CPA Verify\', \'NCEES MyNCEES\', \'California DMV\', \'Sterling Background Check\'). Provides audit traceability to the exact source consulted.']
  verification_source_url varchar [note: 'Web address of the registry or database used for verification (e.g., https://www.nursys.com). Supports audit documentation and re-verification by compliance staff.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone) when the verification query was executed against the source registry or system. Provides sub-day audit precision required for automated API verifications and SLA measurement.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or system identifier of the staff member, automated process, or third-party service that performed the verification (e.g., \'Sterling BGC API\', \'Jane Smith - Credentialing Specialist\'). Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the verification of a professional license against an official state or national registry (e.g., nursing license via state board, CPA via NASBA, PE via NCEES, CDL via DMV). Tracks license number, issuing state/jurisdiction, license type, verification source (primary source, third-party registry, manual), verification date, verification outcome (verified, not found, expired, suspended, revoked), license status at time of verification, and next scheduled re-verification date. Critical for healthcare, finance, and government staffing verticals.'
}

Table credentialing.skills_assessment {
  skills_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each skills assessment record administered to a candidate. Primary key for the skills_assessment data product in the credentialing domain.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_account. Business justification: Skills assessments are often client-specific (client-branded competency tests, client-required certifications, proprietary skill frameworks). Real business need: client-specific assessment libraries, ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assessment costs allocated to cost centers for recruitment expense tracking by business unit. Enables cost center managers to monitor pre-hire screening spend against departmental budgets.']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential. Business justification: Link the skills assessment to the master credential definition being assessed (e.g., Java Programming Certification, Forklift Operator Certification). This allows standardizing skill credential types ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Assessment expenses posted to GL accounts for recruitment cost recognition and financial reporting. Required for accurate P&L preparation and expense categorization.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order or requisition (Req) that triggered this skills assessment, if applicable. Supports QoS (Quality of Submission) tracking for specific client requirements.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate who completed or was administered this skills assessment. Links to the candidate profile in the Bullhorn ATS/CRM system.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Skills assessments delivered by third-party providers (Criteria Corp, ProveIt, etc.). Essential for vendor management, assessment catalog maintenance, cost per assessment tracking, and provider perfor']
  accommodation_notes varchar [note: 'Description of any ADA or other accommodations applied during the assessment. Classified as confidential due to sensitivity of disability-related information. Only populated when ada_accommodation_applied is true.']
  ada_accommodation_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) reasonable accommodation was applied during this assessment administration (e.g., extended time, alternative format). Required for EEOC and OFCCP compliance documentation.']
  allotted_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The maximum time in minutes permitted for the candidate to complete the assessment as defined by the assessment instrument. Supports ADA accommodation tracking and time-limit compliance.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the skills assessment was administered to the candidate. Principal business event date for this transaction.']
  assessment_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the candidate completed or exited the skills assessment. Used alongside start timestamp to compute actual duration.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this skills assessment record, used in candidate communications, recruiter workflows, and ATS tracking. Follows the format ASA-XXXXXXXXXX.']
  assessment_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the candidate began the skills assessment. Used for duration calculation and proctoring audit trails.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the skills assessment record. Tracks progression from scheduling through completion or cancellation.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assessment format administered to the candidate. Determines the evaluation methodology and scoring approach. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: written_test|practical_evaluation|simulation|coding_challenge|trade_skills_test|behavioral_assessment|psychometric|situational_judgment — promote to reference product]']
  assessment_version varchar [note: 'The version or form identifier of the assessment instrument administered. Ensures score comparability over time as assessment content is updated by the provider.']
  attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential attempt count for this candidate on this specific assessment. Tracks retake history and enforces maximum attempt policies per assessment provider or client requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this skills assessment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'The method or channel through which the assessment was delivered to the candidate. Impacts proctoring requirements, result validity, and client acceptance criteria.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual time in minutes the candidate spent completing the assessment, derived from start and end timestamps. Used for proctoring compliance and assessment integrity monitoring.']
  is_current_result boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assessment record represents the most recent and active result for the candidate on this skill. Set to false for superseded attempts. Supports efficient querying of current credential status without full history scans.']
  is_proctored boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment was administered under supervised proctoring conditions (in-person or remote). Affects result credibility weighting and client acceptance of assessment outcomes.']
  language varchar [note: 'The language in which the assessment was administered, using ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language codes (e.g., EN, ES, FR). Supports multilingual workforce programs and EEOC non-discrimination compliance.']
  max_attempts_allowed varchar [note: 'The maximum number of times a candidate is permitted to attempt this assessment before a waiting period or permanent disqualification applies. Enforced per assessment provider policy or client mandate.']
  max_possible_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'The highest achievable score on this assessment instrument. Required to compute score percentage and normalize results across different assessment providers and versions.']
  pass_threshold decimal(10,4) [note: 'The minimum score required to achieve a passing result on this assessment. May be set by the staffing firm, client, or assessment provider. Used to determine pass/fail result status.']
  pass_threshold_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the pass_threshold value is expressed as a raw score, a percentage, or a percentile rank. Required to correctly interpret and apply the threshold in result determination logic.']
  percentile_rank decimal(5,2) [note: 'The candidate\'s performance relative to the normative population, expressed as a percentile (0-100). Indicates what percentage of the reference population scored at or below this candidate. Supports QoS (Quality of Submission) candidate ranking.']
  proficiency_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative proficiency tier assigned to the candidate based on their assessment score. Used for candidate ranking, job matching, and QoS reporting in recruiter workflows.']
  provider_assessment_code varchar [note: 'The external assessment product code or catalog identifier assigned by the assessment provider (e.g., Criteria Corp test ID, Wonderlic WPT-Q). Enables cross-referencing with provider platforms and normative databases.']
  provider_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assessment provider as internal (staffing firm-administered), third-party vendor, client-mandated, or industry body. Affects scoring normalization and result validity rules.']
  raw_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'The candidate\'s actual numeric score as returned by the assessment instrument before normalization or scaling. Represents the principal quantitative result of the assessment event.']
  result_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the recruiter or assessor regarding the assessment outcome, candidate performance observations, or any anomalies noted during administration. Supports qualitative context for QoS reporting.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'The official outcome of the skills assessment for the candidate. Determines candidate eligibility for submittal to job orders requiring this skill qualification. Core field for QoS (Quality of Submission) tracking.']
  review_date date [note: 'The date on which the assessment result was reviewed and approved by the designated reviewer. Supports credentialing workflow audit trails and compliance documentation.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal recruiter, credentialing specialist, or assessor who reviewed and validated the assessment result. Applicable for practical evaluations and trade skills tests requiring human judgment.']
  score_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The candidate\'s score expressed as a percentage of the maximum possible score (raw_score / max_possible_score * 100). Enables standardized comparison across assessments with different scales.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this skills assessment record originated (e.g., Bullhorn ATS/CRM, SAP SuccessFactors, Sterling, internal platform). Supports data lineage and Silver layer reconciliation.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this assessment record in the originating operational system (e.g., Bullhorn credential ID, SAP SuccessFactors assessment ID). Enables traceability from the Silver layer back to the system of record.']
  staffing_vertical varchar [note: 'The industry vertical or staffing practice area for which this assessment is relevant. Supports segmented reporting and compliance with vertical-specific certification mandates (e.g., healthcare credentialing, IT skills validation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthcare|information_technology|skilled_trades|finance|government|light_industrial|administrative|engineering|legal|education — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this skills assessment record was last modified. Supports change tracking and data lineage in the Silver layer.']
  validity_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the assessment result expires and the candidate must be reassessed to maintain qualification. Critical for credential expiration tracking and compliance with client or regulatory requirements.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'The date from which the assessment result is considered valid and can be used to qualify the candidate for job orders. Typically the assessment_date or the date of official result issuance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for a formal skills assessment administered to a candidate to validate proficiency in a specific skill, technology, or trade. Captures assessment type (written test, practical evaluation, simulation, coding challenge, trade skills test), assessment provider (internal, third-party like Criteria Corp or Wonderlic), skill or competency assessed, assessment date, raw score, percentile rank, pass/fail threshold, result status, and validity period. Supports QoS (Quality of Submission) tracking and candidate ranking.'
}

Table credentialing.training_record {
  training_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each training record. Primary key for the training_record data product in the credentialing domain.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active placement or assignment for which this training is required or associated. Nullable when training is not placement-specific.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account that mandated or is associated with this training requirement. Nullable for general non-client-specific training.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Training costs allocated to cost centers for L&D expense tracking and departmental budget management. Supports training budget variance analysis and cost center profitability reporting.']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential. Business justification: Link the training record to the master credential definition (e.g., HIPAA Training, OSHA 10-Hour Safety). This allows standardizing training credential types across all training records. Removing trai']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Training expenses posted to GL accounts for L&D cost recognition and financial statement preparation. Required for accurate expense categorization and GAAP compliance.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate or placed worker who completed or is enrolled in the training course.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Training courses delivered by external training vendors/LMS providers. Required for vendor selection, course catalog management, compliance tracking, and training cost allocation. Replaces denormalize']
  assigned_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the recruiter, compliance officer, or system process that assigned this training to the worker. Supports accountability and audit trail.']
  attempts_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the worker has attempted the training assessment or course. Used to enforce maximum attempt policies and identify workers needing additional support.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate or credential number issued by the training provider upon successful completion. Used for verification, audit, and regulatory reporting.']
  certificate_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the digital copy of the training completion certificate stored in the document management system. Supports audit and verification workflows.']
  ceu_credits_earned decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of Continuing Education Unit (CEU) credits awarded upon successful completion of the training. Relevant for professional license maintenance and credentialing requirements.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date the worker successfully completed the training course. Null if not yet completed. Used as the primary business event timestamp for compliance certification.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training record indicating whether the worker has completed, is in progress, has not started, failed, had the requirement waived, or the training has expired. Core status field for compliance tracking.']
  course_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the training course as defined in the LMS course catalog (e.g., OSHA-10, HIPAA-101, HARASS-PREV-01). Used as the business identifier for the course.']
  course_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the training course as it appears in the LMS catalog (e.g., \'OSHA 10-Hour General Industry Safety\', \'HIPAA Privacy and Security Compliance\', \'Preventing Workplace Harassment\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage and compliance review.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Mode by which the training was delivered to the worker. Supports analytics on training effectiveness and cost by delivery channel.']
  due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the training must be completed to remain compliant. Used for SLA tracking, automated reminders, and compliance reporting.']
  duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of hours of instruction or seat time for the training course. Used for compliance reporting (e.g., OSHA 10-hour, OSHA 30-hour) and workforce analytics.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the candidate or worker was enrolled or assigned to the training course in the LMS. Represents the business event start of the training lifecycle.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the training certification or compliance credit expires and renewal is required. Critical for credential expiration tracking and placement eligibility validation.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'Staffing industry vertical for which this training is applicable or required (e.g., healthcare, IT, skilled trades). Supports vertical-specific compliance reporting and credentialing mandates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthcare|information_technology|skilled_trades|finance|government|light_industrial|professional|other — promote to reference product]']
  instructor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the instructor or facilitator who delivered the training. Applicable for in-person and virtual instructor-led delivery methods. Null for self-paced e-learning.']
  is_client_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specific training was mandated by the client as a condition of placement. Distinct from regulatory mandates; used for client SLA and compliance reporting.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training is mandatory for the worker\'s current placement, client engagement, or regulatory compliance. Mandatory trainings block placement activation if not completed.']
  is_placement_prerequisite boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of this training is a prerequisite before the worker can begin or continue a placement assignment. Drives onboarding workflow gating.']
  lms_record_code varchar [note: 'External identifier from SAP SuccessFactors LMS for this training completion record. Used for reconciliation and audit trail with the source system of record.']
  max_attempts_allowed varchar [note: 'Maximum number of attempts permitted for this training course before escalation or remediation is required. Enforces assessment integrity policies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, exceptions, or observations related to this training record (e.g., special accommodations, partial credit notes, remediation instructions).']
  pass_fail_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the training assessment or evaluation. \'not_applicable\' is used for informational or attendance-only courses with no graded assessment.']
  passing_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to pass the training assessment, expressed as a percentage. Enables automated pass/fail determination and compliance validation.']
  provider_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the training was delivered by the internal LMS (SAP SuccessFactors), an external training vendor, the client directly, a regulatory body, or via self-study. Determines sourcing and cost attribution.']
  regulatory_requirement_code varchar [note: 'Code or citation of the specific regulatory requirement that mandates this training (e.g., \'OSHA-29CFR1910.132\', \'HIPAA-164.530\', \'FLSA-CHILD-LABOR\'). Links training records to compliance obligations for regulatory reporting.']
  renewal_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required renewals of this training certification (e.g., 365 for annual, 730 for biennial). Used to calculate the next due date upon completion.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training certification requires periodic renewal. Drives automated expiration alerts and renewal workflow initiation.']
