  score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved by the worker on the training assessment or final exam, expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Nullable for courses without a graded assessment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system from which this training record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail in the Silver layer lakehouse.']
  training_location varchar [note: 'Physical location, virtual platform URL, or LMS module path where the training was conducted (e.g., \'Chicago Training Center\', \'Zoom Webinar\', \'SuccessFactors LMS Module 3\'). Applicable for in-person and virtual instructor-led sessions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Silver layer.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager or compliance officer who approved the training waiver. Required for audit trail when a mandatory training is waived.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a mandatory training requirement was waived for this worker (e.g., prior equivalent certification, grandfathered status, client exception). Populated only when completion_status is \'waived\'.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking completion of a required or elective training course by a candidate or placed worker. Captures training course name, training provider (internal LMS via SAP SuccessFactors, external vendor), training category (OSHA safety, HIPAA compliance, harassment prevention, skills development, onboarding orientation), delivery method (in-person, e-learning, virtual), completion date, expiration date, pass/fail status, CEU credits earned, and whether the training is mandatory for a specific placement or client. Sourced from SAP SuccessFactors LMS.'
}

Table credentialing.credential_requirement {
  credential_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for credential_requirement']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account that mandates this credential requirement. Supports client-specific override rules where a client\'s Master Service Agreement (MSA) or Vendor Management System (VMS) program imposes credential standards beyond the default.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: MSAs establish baseline credentialing standards (BGC packages, drug screen requirements, license types) that flow into job-level credential requirements. Contract negotiation defines credential polici']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: A credential requirement may be part of a named credential package (e.g., Healthcare RN Package). This allows grouping requirements into reusable bundles. No columns to remove as credential_requiremen']
  credential_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the credential type definition (e.g., RN License, OSHA 10, Background Check package, Drug Screen panel, CPR Certification). Drives the specific verification workflow and expiration tracking logic.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Credential requirements vary by work location (site-specific safety credentials, facility access badges, local licensing). Healthcare facilities, manufacturing sites, and secure locations require loca']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific job order (requisition) for which this credential requirement applies. Enables automated credentialing gap analysis when a candidate is submitted for a role. Populated when the requirement is job-order-specific rather than placement-type-level.']
  primary_credential_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user (recruiter, compliance officer, or system account) who created this credential requirement record. Supports audit trails and accountability for credentialing rule configuration.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOWs specify project-specific credential requirements beyond MSA baseline (e.g., clearance levels, specialized certifications). SOW scoping process defines which credentials are mandatory for specific']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Vendor Management System (VMS) program under which this credential requirement is enforced. Applicable for Managed Service Provider (MSP) and contingent workforce programs managed through Beeline VMS where program-level credentialing rules apply.']
  acceptable_expiry_window_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days before a credential\'s expiration date that it is still considered acceptable for placement. For example, a value of 30 means a credential expiring within 30 days of the assignment start date is not acceptable. Drives automated expiration alerts and gap analysis.']
  applies_to_assignment_start boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential must be verified and on file before the candidate\'s assignment start date. When True, the credential is a pre-placement gate. When False, the credential may be obtained during or after onboarding within a grace period.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this credential requirement was formally approved by the designated approver. Null for requirements that do not require explicit approval. Supports compliance audit trails.']
  assessment_passing_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum passing score (as a percentage) required on a skills assessment to satisfy this credential requirement. Applicable when credential_category is ASSESSMENT. Null when no minimum score threshold applies or when the credential is pass/fail without a numeric score.']
  bgc_package_code varchar [note: 'The specific Background Check (BGC) package code from Sterling Background Check that must be ordered for this requirement. Applicable when credential_category is BGC. Maps to the client_package_code used in the bgc_order product to ensure the correct screening scope is ordered.']
  client_contract_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific clause, section, or exhibit in the client\'s Master Service Agreement (MSA) or Statement of Work (SOW) that mandates this credential requirement. Supports compliance audit trails and contract-driven credentialing enforcement. Confidential as it references contract terms.']
  client_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this requirement record represents a client-specific override of the default credential requirement for this placement type or job order. When True, the client_account_id is the source of the override rule, superseding the standard requirement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credential requirement record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trails, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  credential_category varchar [note: 'High-level category classifying the type of credential required. Distinguishes professional licenses, certifications, Background Check (BGC) packages, drug screen panels, training completions, skills assessments, and security clearances. Enables category-level reporting and gap analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LICENSE|CERTIFICATION|BGC|DRUG_SCREEN|TRAINING|ASSESSMENT|CLEARANCE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  drug_screen_panel varchar [note: 'Specifies the drug screening panel required (e.g., 5-Panel, 10-Panel, DOT, Hair Follicle). Applicable when credential_category is DRUG_SCREEN. Drives the correct test order through the screening vendor.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this credential requirement becomes enforceable. Requirements with a future effective date are staged for activation. Supports versioning of credentialing rules when client contracts or regulatory mandates change.']
  everify_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether E-Verify employment eligibility verification is required for placements under this requirement. Applicable for federal contractor positions and states with mandatory E-Verify laws.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this credential requirement record ceases to be enforceable. Null indicates the requirement is open-ended with no planned end date. Used to manage time-bound client contract requirements or regulatory mandates with sunset provisions.']
  gap_analysis_blocking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a gap in this credential (missing or expired) will block a candidate submittal in the Applicant Tracking System (ATS). When True, the ATS prevents submission until the credential is satisfied. When False, a warning is raised but submission is permitted.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after assignment start within which a non-pre-placement credential must be obtained and verified. Applicable only when applies_to_assignment_start is False. Enables phased onboarding for credentials that can be completed post-start.']
  i9_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a completed I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification form is required before placement under this requirement. Standard for all US-based placements but may be explicitly flagged for audit and compliance tracking purposes.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'The staffing industry vertical for which this credential requirement applies. Healthcare may require Joint Commission credentials; Government may require security clearances; Skilled Trades may require OSHA certifications. Enables vertical-specific credentialing rule enforcement.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential is a hard requirement (mandatory) or a preferred qualification. When True, a candidate cannot be submitted or placed without satisfying this credential. When False, the credential is preferred but not blocking.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or regulatory body that issues or governs this credential (e.g., State Board of Nursing, OSHA, CompTIA, PMI, DOT). Used for primary source verification routing and compliance documentation.']
  license_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code indicating the jurisdiction in which the professional license must be valid. Applicable for state-issued licenses such as RN, LPN, CPA, or contractor licenses. Null when the credential is federally issued or not state-specific.']
  max_credential_age_days varchar [note: 'Maximum age in days that a credential result remains valid from its completion or issue date. For example, a BGC result may only be valid for 365 days. After this period, a new credential must be obtained. Null indicates no age restriction.']
  oig_exclusion_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an Office of Inspector General (OIG) exclusion list check is required as part of this credential requirement. Mandatory for healthcare staffing placements to ensure candidates are not excluded from federally funded programs.']
  placement_type_code varchar [note: 'Classification of the placement type to which this credential requirement applies when not tied to a specific job order. Supports requirement templates across placement categories such as Contract, Temp-to-Perm (Contract-to-Hire), Direct Hire, Statement of Work (SOW), or Per Diem engagements.']
  renewal_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before credential expiration that a renewal notification should be triggered. Enables proactive credentialing management to prevent assignment disruptions due to lapsed credentials. Applicable only when renewal_required is True.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential requires periodic renewal. When True, the system tracks expiration and triggers renewal workflows. When False, the credential is a one-time requirement (e.g., a completed training module with no expiry).']
  requirement_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the credential requirement including scope, acceptable documentation, and any special instructions for recruiters or candidates. Supports recruiter guidance and candidate communication during the onboarding process.']
  requirement_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for this credential requirement as displayed in the ATS, VMS, or credentialing portal. Examples: \'RN License - CA\', \'OSHA 10 Certification\', \'7-Year Criminal Background Check\', \'10-Panel Drug Screen\'. Used for recruiter-facing display and reporting.']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this credential requirement record. ACTIVE requirements are enforced during candidate submission and onboarding. SUPERSEDED records have been replaced by a newer version. DRAFT records are pending approval before enforcement.']
  security_clearance_level varchar [note: 'Required federal security clearance level for government staffing placements. Applicable when industry_vertical is GOVERNMENT. Values align with US federal clearance tiers: Public Trust, Secret, Top Secret, and Top Secret/Sensitive Compartmented Information (TS/SCI).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this credential requirement originated. Bullhorn ATS/CRM for job-order-level requirements, Beeline VMS for program-level requirements, SAP SuccessFactors for onboarding-driven requirements, Sterling for BGC package mandates, or MANUAL for requirements entered directly.']
  source_system_req_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this credential requirement record in the originating operational system (e.g., Bullhorn requirement ID, Beeline compliance rule ID). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and bi-directional sync with source systems.']
  training_course_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific training course or Learning Management System (LMS) module required when credential_category is TRAINING. Maps to the course catalog in SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System. Examples include OSHA 10, OSHA 30, HIPAA Awareness, or client-specific safety training.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this credential requirement record. Used for change detection, incremental data loads, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  verification_level varchar [note: 'Required level of verification rigor for this credential. PRIMARY_SOURCE requires direct confirmation from the issuing authority (e.g., state licensing board). SELF_REPORTED accepts candidate attestation. THIRD_PARTY requires an independent verification vendor such as Sterling. EMPLOYER_VERIFIED requires confirmation from a prior employer.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this credential requirement record. Incremented each time the requirement is modified. Supports audit trails and historical analysis of how credentialing rules have evolved over time for a given job order or placement type.']
  waiver_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a client or compliance officer can grant a waiver for this credential requirement on a case-by-case basis. When True, a waiver workflow is available in the ATS. When False, no exceptions are permitted regardless of circumstances.']
  waiver_approval_level varchar [note: 'The organizational role level required to approve a waiver for this credential requirement. Applicable only when waiver_allowed is True. Drives the waiver approval workflow routing in the ATS and compliance management system.']

  Note: 'Association entity defining which credentials, licenses, certifications, drug screens, and BGC packages are required for a specific job order or placement type. Captures the job order or placement type reference, required credential type, whether the requirement is mandatory or preferred, the required verification level (primary source vs. self-reported), acceptable expiration window, client-specific override rules, and effective date of the requirement. Enables automated credentialing gap analysis when a candidate is submitted for a role. Named to avoid collision with the placement domain\'s core entities.'
}

Table credentialing.adverse_action {
  adverse_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the adverse action record. Primary key for the adverse_action data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete FCRA-mandated compliance workflow event with a clear lifecycle.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement record if the adverse action was triggered post-placement (e.g., during a periodic re-screen). Nullable when adverse action occurs pre-placement during the hiring process.']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Background Check (BGC) order from Sterling that triggered this adverse action workflow. Links the adverse action to the specific screening result that was the basis for the adverse decision.']
  bgc_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.bgc_result. Business justification: adverse_action is triggered by a specific finding in a bgc_result record. Currently adverse_action → bgc_order, but bgc_order can have MULTIPLE bgc_result records (one per check component: criminal, e']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for whose position the candidate was being considered. Supports client-level adverse action reporting, SLA compliance, and MSA audit requirements.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Adverse action compliance costs allocated to cost centers for legal/compliance expense tracking by department. Supports risk management cost analysis and departmental budget monitoring.']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: An adverse action may be triggered by a specific credential instance failure (e.g., expired license, failed drug screen). This allows tracking which credential instance caused the adverse action. No c']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Legal and compliance costs from adverse action processes posted to GL accounts for compliance expense tracking and financial reporting. Required for accurate legal expense recognition.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) for which the candidate was being considered when the adverse action was triggered. Provides context for the position type and client requirements that informed the adverse decision.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate subject to the adverse action process. PARTY_REFERENCE category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role minimum requirements. Links to the candidate master record in Bullhorn ATS.']
  action_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the adverse action case, used in candidate correspondence, compliance audits, and regulatory reporting. Format typically follows AA-YYYY-NNNNNN. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role minimum requirements.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the adverse action workflow. Tracks progression from pre-adverse notice through dispute period to final disposition. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role minimum requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_adverse_notice_sent|dispute_period_open|dispute_received|under_individualized_assessment|final_adverse_issued|withdrawn|overturned — promote to reference product]']
  applicable_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'State or local jurisdiction whose fair chance or adverse action laws govern this case, in addition to federal FCRA requirements. Stored as two-letter US state code or city/state combination (e.g., CA, NYC-NY). Determines additional procedural requirements.']
  candidate_notified_of_cra_rights boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate was informed of their right to dispute the accuracy or completeness of the consumer report directly with the CRA, as required by FCRA § 615(a)(1)(C).']
  client_required_action boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse action was initiated at the client\'s request based on their screening standards, as opposed to the staffing firm\'s own adjudication policy. Relevant for co-employment risk management and MSA compliance.']
  consumer_reporting_agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Consumer Reporting Agency (CRA) that provided the BGC report used as the basis for the adverse action (e.g., Sterling Talent Solutions). FCRA requires the CRA name, address, and phone be included in the adverse action notice.']
  copy_of_report_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a copy of the consumer report (BGC report) was provided to the candidate with the pre-adverse action notice, as required by FCRA § 615(a)(1)(A).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adverse action record was first created in the system, marking the initiation of the FCRA adverse action workflow. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role minimum requirements.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date the final disposition was recorded, marking the closure of the adverse action workflow. Used for compliance reporting timelines and SLA measurement.']
  disposition_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for the final disposition, particularly when the adverse action was withdrawn or overturned. Documents the business rationale for compliance audit purposes.']
  dispute_period_end_date date [note: 'Date the candidate dispute period expires. FCRA requires a reasonable waiting period (commonly 5 business days) before a final adverse action notice may be issued. Final adverse action cannot be issued before this date.']
  dispute_period_start_date date [note: 'Date the candidate dispute period begins, typically the same as or the day after the pre-adverse action notice date. Marks the start of the minimum waiting period during which the candidate may dispute the BGC findings.']
  dispute_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate submitted a formal dispute of the BGC findings during the dispute period. Drives the individualized assessment and review workflow when True.']
  dispute_received_date date [note: 'Date the candidate\'s formal dispute response was received by the staffing organization. Nullable when no dispute was submitted. Triggers the individualized assessment review process.']
  dispute_response_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the candidate\'s dispute response content, including any mitigating information, documentation, or explanation provided by the candidate regarding the BGC findings. Confidential business record supporting the individualized assessment.']
  document_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the adverse action notice documents stored in the document management system (e.g., DocuSign CLM). Links to the pre-adverse and final adverse action notice documents for audit retrieval.']
  eeoc_review_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an EEOC compliance review was performed as part of the adverse action process, confirming the decision does not constitute unlawful discrimination based on protected class characteristics.']
  final_adverse_notice_date date [note: 'Date the final adverse action notice was issued to the candidate after the dispute period expired and any dispute review was completed. Must be on or after the dispute_period_end_date. Nullable until final adverse action is issued.']
  final_adverse_notice_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the final adverse action notice to the candidate. Required for FCRA compliance documentation.']
  final_disposition varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the adverse action process. Confirmed = adverse action upheld and candidate not hired/placed. Withdrawn = staffing firm withdrew the adverse action (e.g., position filled, business decision). Overturned = adverse action reversed after dispute review, candidate cleared for placement.']
  individualized_assessment_date date [note: 'Date the individualized assessment was completed. Nullable when no individualized assessment was required (e.g., non-criminal BGC findings). Documents the timeline of the EEOC-compliant review process.']
  individualized_assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Documented rationale and findings from the individualized assessment, including evaluation of offense nature, time elapsed since offense, and nexus to the job duties. Required for EEOC and OFCCP audit defense.']
  individualized_assessment_performed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an individualized assessment was conducted before issuing the final adverse action, as required by EEOC guidance for criminal record-based adverse actions. Evaluates the nature of the offense, time elapsed, and job relevance.']
  legal_review_date date [note: 'Date legal review was completed for this adverse action case. Nullable when legal_review_required is False. Documents the legal clearance timeline for compliance audit purposes.']
  legal_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adverse action case was escalated for legal review prior to issuing the final adverse action notice, typically triggered by complex criminal history, dispute responses, or high-risk jurisdictions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes field for compliance officers to document additional context, escalation details, or case-specific observations not captured in structured fields.']
  ofccp_review_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an OFCCP compliance review was performed for this adverse action, required when the staffing firm is placing workers on federal contracts. Ensures adverse action decisions comply with affirmative action obligations.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Type of staffing engagement for which the candidate was being considered when the adverse action was triggered. Relevant for determining applicable screening standards and fair chance law requirements by position type.']
  pre_adverse_notice_date date [note: 'Date the pre-adverse action notice was sent to the candidate, initiating the mandatory FCRA waiting period before a final adverse action decision can be issued. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role minimum requirements.']
  pre_adverse_notice_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the pre-adverse action notice to the candidate. Required for FCRA compliance documentation to demonstrate the candidate received proper notification.']
  reviewed_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the compliance officer or recruiter who reviewed and processed the adverse action decision. Supports audit trail and accountability requirements.']
  staffing_vertical varchar [note: 'Industry vertical for which the candidate was being screened. Determines the applicable credentialing standards and permissible BGC criteria (e.g., healthcare requires OIG exclusion checks; government may require security clearance checks).']
  state_fair_chance_law_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a state or local fair chance (ban-the-box) law applied to this adverse action, requiring additional procedural steps beyond federal FCRA minimums. Relevant for California (AB 1008), New York City, and other jurisdictions.']
  summary_of_rights_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FCRA Summary of Rights (A Summary of Your Rights Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act) was provided to the candidate along with the pre-adverse action notice, as mandated by FCRA § 615(a)(1)(A).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adverse action record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role minimum requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record managing the FCRA-mandated adverse action process triggered when a BGC result leads to a decision not to hire or place a candidate. Captures the pre-adverse action notice date, candidate notification method, dispute period start and end dates, candidate dispute response, final adverse action notice date, basis for adverse action (specific BGC finding reference), individualized assessment performed flag, EEOC compliance review flag, and final disposition (withdrawn, confirmed, overturned). Critical for FCRA, EEOC, and OFCCP compliance in the staffing industry. Supports the full pre-adverse → dispute → final adverse action workflow required by federal and state fair chance hiring laws. Note: This product captures the credentialing-triggered adverse action workflow; the compliance domain may own broader FCRA reporting and regulatory audit aggregation. Cross-domain SSOT boundary requires global architect resolution.'
}

Table credentialing.credential_package {
  credential_package_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for credential_package']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for which this credential package was defined. Null if the package is a generic vertical or program-level package not tied to a specific client.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: MSAs establish standard credentialing packages (BGC components, drug screens, licenses, training) for all placements under that client agreement. Contract setup creates reusable packages tied to MSA t']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Credentialing package costs (bundled screening/verification) allocated to cost centers for onboarding expense tracking by vertical/program. Enables cost analysis of client-specific credentialing requi']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Package costs posted to GL accounts for onboarding expense recognition and financial reporting. Required for accurate cost of hire tracking and P&L preparation.']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Vendor Management System (VMS) program or Managed Service Provider (MSP) program scope under which this credential package is required. Null if the package is not tied to a specific VMS program.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOWs reference specific credential packages for project delivery (e.g., healthcare SOW requires RN license + BLS + facility-specific training package). SOW fulfillment requires workers meeting package']
  superseded_by_package_credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer credential package that replaces this one when package_status is \'superseded\'. Enables version chain navigation for compliance audits and candidate re-credentialing workflows.']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.vms_program. Business justification: VMS programs define standardized credential packages for supplier compliance. Real business process: VMS-driven credentialing workflows where client VMS platform dictates supplier credential requireme']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the compliance officer or manager who formally approved this credential package version for use. Supports audit trail requirements under SOC 2 and internal governance policies.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this credential package version was formally approved by the compliance officer or designated authority. Required for audit trail and change management documentation.']
  bgc_components_required varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of required BGC components for this package (e.g., criminal_check|employment_verification|education_verification|mvr|oig_exclusion|sex_offender_registry|credit_check). Drives the BGC order configuration in Sterling.']
  bgc_package_code varchar [note: 'The Sterling Background Check package code that defines the specific BGC components required (e.g., criminal check, employment verification, education verification, MVR, OIG exclusion). Maps directly to a Sterling-configured screening package.']
  client_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential package was mandated by a specific client as a condition of placement. When true, the client_id field identifies the requiring client. Client-mandated packages may have stricter requirements than standard vertical packages.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance team annotations regarding regulatory basis, client-specific exceptions, state-specific requirements, or audit findings related to this credential package. Supports SOC 2 and FCRA compliance documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credential package record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 compliance requirements.']
  credential_validity_days varchar [note: 'The number of days for which a completed credential package remains valid before re-credentialing is required. Drives expiration tracking and renewal alerts for active placements (e.g., 365 days for annual re-credentialing).']
  drug_screen_panel_type varchar [note: 'The specific drug screen panel required by this credential package (e.g., 5-panel, 10-panel, DOT 5-panel, hair follicle). Null if drug_screen_required is false.']
  drug_screen_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a drug screen is a mandatory component of this credential package. When true, the drug_screen_panel_type field specifies the required panel.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this version of the credential package becomes effective and can be applied to new candidate credentialing workflows. Candidates onboarded on or after this date must satisfy this package version.']
  everify_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether E-Verify electronic employment eligibility confirmation is required for this credential package. Mandatory for federal contractors and certain state-regulated placements.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this credential package version expires and is no longer valid for new placements. Null for open-ended packages. Supports version lifecycle management and ensures compliance with updated client or regulatory requirements.']
  i9_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification is required as part of this credential package. Typically true for all packages but may be explicitly flagged for compliance audit purposes.']
  immunization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether proof of immunization records is required as part of this credential package. Primarily applicable to healthcare staffing verticals (e.g., flu shot, hepatitis B, MMR, TB test).']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'The staffing industry vertical this credential package is designed to serve (e.g., healthcare, information_technology, light_industrial). Drives vertical-specific credentialing workflows and compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthcare|information_technology|light_industrial|finance|government|skilled_trades|education|other — promote to reference product]']
  is_dot_regulated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drug screen component of this package must comply with U.S. Department of Transportation (DOT) regulations (49 CFR Part 40). Applicable for transportation, logistics, and safety-sensitive positions.']
  job_category varchar [note: 'The job category or occupational classification for which this package applies (e.g., Registered Nurse, Software Engineer, Forklift Operator, Security Analyst). Enables package assignment logic in the ATS based on requisition job category.']
  license_verification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether professional license verification is a mandatory component of this credential package. When true, the required_license_types field specifies which license types must be verified.']
  oig_exclusion_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an Office of Inspector General (OIG) exclusion list check is required. Mandatory for healthcare placements to ensure candidates are not excluded from federally funded programs (Medicare/Medicaid).']
  onboarding_checklist_template varchar [note: 'Code or identifier referencing the onboarding checklist template in SAP SuccessFactors or the ATS that is automatically assigned to candidates when this credential package is applied. Ensures consistent onboarding task generation.']
  package_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this credential package across systems (e.g., HC-TRV-NURSE-V2, FED-IT-CLR-V1, LI-WH-STD-V3). Used for cross-system reference in Bullhorn, Sterling, and Beeline VMS.']
  package_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the credential package, including its intended use case, applicable roles, and any special compliance notes. Used by recruiters and compliance teams to understand the full scope of requirements.']
  package_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the credential package (e.g., Healthcare Travel Nurse Package, Federal Government IT Clearance Package, Light Industrial Warehouse Package). Used in candidate-facing and recruiter-facing workflows.']
  package_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the credential package. Active packages are available for assignment to candidates. Superseded packages have been replaced by a newer version. Archived packages are retained for historical compliance reference.']
  package_version varchar [note: 'Integer version number of this credential package definition, incremented each time the package requirements are materially changed. Enables change tracking and ensures candidates are evaluated against the correct version at time of placement.']
  physical_exam_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pre-placement physical examination is required as part of this credential package. Common for healthcare, light industrial, and safety-sensitive positions.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'The type of placement engagement this credential package applies to: temporary, contract, temp-to-perm (Contract-to-Hire), direct hire, or Statement of Work (SOW). Different placement types may require different credentialing depth.']
  renewal_lead_days varchar [note: 'The number of days before credential expiration that the renewal process should be initiated. Used to trigger automated renewal alerts and recruiter tasks in the ATS (e.g., 30 days before expiry).']
  required_assessment_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of skills assessment or competency test codes required by this package (e.g., MS_OFFICE_BASIC|TYPING_40WPM|FORKLIFT_WRITTEN|CLINICAL_SKILLS_RN). Drives assessment assignment in the ATS onboarding workflow.']
  required_immunizations varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of required immunizations or health screenings for this credential package (e.g., FLU|HEP_B|MMR|VARICELLA|TB_TEST|COVID_VAX). Null if immunization_required is false.']
  required_license_types varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of professional license types that must be verified as part of this credential package (e.g., RN|LPN|CNA|CDL_A|PMP|CISSP). Drives license verification workflows in Sterling and the credentialing team.']
  required_training_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of training course codes or LMS module identifiers that must be completed as part of this credential package (e.g., OSHA_10|HIPAA_PRIVACY|BLOODBORNE_PATHOGENS|FORKLIFT_CERT). Maps to SAP SuccessFactors LMS course catalog.']
  security_clearance_level varchar [note: 'The minimum government security clearance level required by this credential package (e.g., Public Trust, Secret, Top Secret, TS/SCI). Null if security_clearance_required is false.']
  security_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a government security clearance is required as part of this credential package. Applicable to federal government IT and defense staffing placements.']
  skills_assessment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal skills assessment or competency test is required as part of this credential package. When true, the required_assessment_codes field specifies which assessments must be passed.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of specific training courses or modules is a mandatory component of this credential package. When true, required_training_codes specifies the training requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credential package record was last modified. Tracks changes to package requirements over time and supports version control and compliance audit trails.']

  Note: 'Master record defining a named bundle of credentialing requirements (BGC checks, drug screens, license verifications, training completions, assessments) that must be satisfied for a specific client, industry vertical, or job category. Examples: Healthcare Travel Nurse Package, Federal Government IT Clearance Package, Light Industrial Warehouse Package. Captures package name, applicable vertical, client or program scope, list of required credential types, required BGC components, drug screen panel, effective and expiry dates, and package version for change tracking. Enables standardized credentialing workflows across similar placements and drives the candidate_readiness evaluation.'
}

Table credentialing.readiness_status {
  readiness_status_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the credentialing readiness status record. Primary key for this operational go/no-go summary record used by recruiters and compliance teams.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement or assignment record associated with this readiness evaluation. Nullable when the assessment precedes placement creation.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for whom this credentialing readiness is being evaluated. Drives client-specific credentialing requirements and compliance rules.']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the credentialing package definition that specifies the set of required credentials, licenses, and screenings for this evaluation context. Used when readiness is assessed against a package rather than a specific job order.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific job order (Req) for which this credentialing readiness is being assessed. Nullable when the readiness record is tied to a credentialing package rather than a specific job order.']
  primary_readiness_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user (recruiter, compliance officer, or system account) who performed or triggered the most recent credentialing readiness evaluation. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate whose credentialing readiness is being evaluated. Links to the candidate master record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Worker readiness is evaluated against SOW-specific credential requirements to determine placement eligibility. SOW staffing decisions depend on readiness status showing all SOW-mandated credentials ar']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the placement eligibility determination was formally approved by the compliance officer or manager. Populated when approved_by_user_id is set. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  bgc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the background check (BGC) component of the credentialing evaluation. Sourced from Sterling Background Check. Values: not_ordered = BGC has not yet been initiated; ordered = BGC order placed but not started; in_progress = BGC is being processed; clear = BGC returned no disqualifying findings; consider = BGC returned findings requiring individualized assessment; adverse_action = adverse action process initiated; cancelled = BGC order was cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_ordered|ordered|in_progress|clear|consider|adverse_action|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  blocking_items_summary varchar [note: 'Free-text summary of specific items that are actively blocking placement eligibility (as opposed to items that are outstanding but not yet blocking). Distinguishes hard-stop blockers (e.g., adverse BGC result, positive drug screen, OIG exclusion) from soft items that are in progress. Used for escalation and compliance review.']
  client_specific_requirements_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all client-specific credentialing requirements (beyond standard package requirements) have been satisfied. Clients may impose additional requirements via their MSA, VMS program rules, or SOW specifications that go beyond the standard credentialing package.']
  completed_credential_count varchar [note: 'Number of required credentials, licenses, certifications, and screenings that have been successfully completed and verified as of the last evaluation date. Used alongside required_credential_count to determine partial vs. full credentialing status.']
  compliance_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has been placed on a formal compliance hold that prevents placement regardless of other credentialing status. A compliance hold may be triggered by adverse action proceedings, regulatory violations, OIG exclusion findings, or other compliance-related issues requiring formal review before placement can proceed.']
  compliance_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for the compliance hold when compliance_hold is True. Documents the specific regulatory, legal, or policy basis for the hold to support audit trails and adverse action documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credentialing readiness status record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker for the Silver Layer record. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  drug_screen_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the drug screening component of the credentialing evaluation. Sourced from Sterling Background Check / drug screening lab. Values: not_ordered = screen not yet initiated; ordered = order placed; pending_collection = awaiting specimen collection; in_lab = specimen received and being analyzed; negative = passed (no substances detected); positive = failed (substances detected); cancelled = screen was cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_ordered|ordered|pending_collection|in_lab|negative|positive|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  earliest_credential_expiration_date date [note: 'The earliest expiration date among all active credentials, licenses, and certifications held by the candidate for this evaluation context. Used to proactively alert recruiters and compliance teams of impending credential lapses that could affect placement eligibility.']
  evaluation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this readiness evaluation record, used by recruiters and compliance teams to reference and communicate about a specific assessment (e.g., RDNS-2024-00123). Generated by the credentialing system.']
  evaluation_triggered_by varchar [note: 'The business event or action that triggered this credentialing readiness evaluation. Used for audit trail and process analytics. Values: submittal = triggered by candidate submittal to a job order; placement_creation = triggered by placement record creation; assignment_extension = triggered by an assignment extension request; periodic_review = triggered by scheduled periodic re-evaluation; credential_expiration = triggered by an upcoming or past credential expiration; manual_request = triggered by a recruiter or compliance team member; system_automated = triggered by an automated system rule. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submittal|placement_creation|assignment_extension|periodic_review|credential_expiration|manual_request|system_automated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  everify_status varchar [note: 'Current E-Verify case status for the candidate. E-Verify is an internet-based system that compares I-9 information to DHS and SSA records to confirm employment eligibility. Required for federal contractors and certain state-mandated employers. Values: not_initiated = E-Verify case not yet created; pending = case submitted and awaiting result; employment_authorized = work authorization confirmed; tentative_nonconfirmation = initial mismatch requiring candidate response; final_nonconfirmation = employment eligibility could not be confirmed; not_required = E-Verify not required for this placement.']
  expired_credential_count varchar [note: 'Number of credentials, licenses, or certifications that were previously valid but have since expired. A non-zero value may trigger an overall_status of \'expired\' or \'partially_credentialed\' and blocks placement eligibility until renewals are completed.']
  i9_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification for the candidate. Required by the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) for all U.S. employees. Values: not_started = I-9 process not yet initiated; section1_complete = candidate has completed Section 1; section2_complete = employer has completed Section 2 (fully compliant); reverification_required = work authorization document is expiring and reverification is needed; compliant = I-9 is fully complete and compliant; non_compliant = I-9 has deficiencies requiring correction.']
  last_evaluated_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the overall credentialing readiness status was evaluated or recalculated. Used to determine whether the readiness assessment is current and whether a re-evaluation is needed before placement.']
  license_verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of professional license verification for the candidate in the context of this readiness evaluation. Applicable primarily to healthcare, skilled trades, finance, and government staffing verticals. Values: not_required = no license verification required for this role; pending = verification initiated but not yet complete; verified = license confirmed valid and current; failed = license could not be verified or is invalid; expired = license has lapsed; waived = verification requirement formally waived.']
  next_evaluation_date date [note: 'The date on which the credentialing readiness status is scheduled to be re-evaluated. Driven by upcoming credential expiration dates, periodic re-verification requirements, or compliance mandates. Supports proactive credentialing management and prevents placement disruptions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by recruiters or compliance team members regarding the credentialing readiness evaluation. May include context about exceptions, client communications, candidate explanations, or special circumstances affecting the readiness determination.']
  oig_exclusion_status varchar [note: 'Status of the OIG (Office of Inspector General) exclusion list check for the candidate. Required for healthcare staffing placements to ensure the candidate is not excluded from participation in federal healthcare programs (Medicare, Medicaid). Values: not_checked = check not yet performed; clear = candidate not found on OIG exclusion list; excluded = candidate is on the OIG exclusion list and cannot be placed in healthcare roles; not_required = OIG check not required for this staffing vertical.']
  outstanding_credential_count varchar [note: 'Number of required credentials, licenses, certifications, and screenings that remain incomplete, pending, or expired as of the last evaluation date. Equals required_credential_count minus completed_credential_count. Drives urgency and follow-up actions.']
  outstanding_items_summary varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured summary listing the specific credentials, verifications, screenings, or training items that remain outstanding and are blocking full credentialing status. Used by recruiters and compliance teams to communicate action items to candidates and coordinate completion. Example: \'BGC in progress; Drug screen pending collection; OSHA 10 training not started\'.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Current overall credentialing readiness status of the candidate for the associated job order, placement, or credentialing package. Drives the go/no-go placement eligibility decision. Values: fully_credentialed = all required credentials are valid and current; partially_credentialed = some credentials are complete but others are outstanding; not_credentialed = no required credentials have been completed; expired = one or more previously valid credentials have lapsed; pending_review = evaluation is in progress or awaiting compliance team review.']
  placement_eligible boolean [note: 'Operational go/no-go flag indicating whether the candidate is currently eligible to be placed or submitted for the associated job order or assignment based on their credentialing readiness. True = eligible to place; False = not eligible. Used by recruiters as the primary clearance indicator before submittal.']
  placement_eligible_from_date date [note: 'The date from which the candidate is eligible to be placed based on their credentialing readiness. May be a future date if credentials are in progress and expected to be completed by a specific date. Supports advance planning for assignment start dates.']
  placement_eligible_until_date date [note: 'The date through which the candidate\'s credentialing readiness is valid for placement, typically driven by the earliest credential expiration date. After this date, re-credentialing or renewal is required before the candidate can continue in or be placed in a new assignment.']
  required_credential_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct credentials, licenses, certifications, and screenings required for the candidate to be fully credentialed for the associated job order, placement, or credentialing package. Used to calculate completion percentage and track outstanding items.']
  skills_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required skills assessments or competency tests for the candidate in the context of this readiness evaluation. Skills assessments may include technical tests, clinical competency evaluations, or trade-specific proficiency tests depending on the staffing vertical and client requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this readiness status record originated or was primarily populated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Values: bullhorn = Bullhorn ATS/CRM; sterling = Sterling Background Check; sap_successfactors = SAP SuccessFactors; beeline = Beeline VMS; manual = manually entered by a user; system_generated = generated by automated credentialing rules engine.']
  staffing_vertical varchar [note: 'Industry vertical or staffing specialty for which this credentialing readiness is being assessed. Determines the applicable credentialing requirements and compliance mandates (e.g., healthcare requires clinical license verification; government may require security clearance checks). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthcare|information_technology|skilled_trades|finance|government|light_industrial|professional|education|legal|other — promote to reference product]']
  training_completion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of mandatory training requirements (e.g., OSHA safety training, HIPAA compliance training, client-specific onboarding training) that must be completed before the candidate can be placed. Tracked via SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System (LMS).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credentialing readiness status record was most recently modified. Updated whenever the overall_status, component statuses, credential counts, or eligibility flags change. Supports change tracking and data freshness monitoring in the Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  vms_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Clearance status within the client\'s Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, for candidates being placed through a VMS-managed contingent workforce program. Some clients require VMS-level credentialing clearance before a candidate can be activated on an assignment. Values: not_required = VMS clearance not applicable; pending = clearance submitted and awaiting client approval; cleared = VMS clearance granted; rejected = VMS clearance denied; not_submitted = clearance not yet submitted to VMS.']
  waived_credential_count varchar [note: 'Number of normally required credentials that have been formally waived by the client or compliance team for this specific candidate and job order context. Waivers must be documented and approved. Affects the effective required count for placement eligibility determination.']

  Note: 'Operational summary record representing the current overall credentialing readiness status of a candidate for a specific job order, placement, or credentialing package. Captures the candidate reference, job order or package reference, overall status (fully credentialed, partially credentialed, not credentialed, expired, pending review), count of required vs. completed credentials, outstanding items list, last status evaluation date, and placement eligibility flag. This is the operational \'go/no-go\' record used by recruiters and compliance teams before submitting or placing a candidate.'
}

Table credentialing.work_authorization {
  work_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for the work authorization record. Serves as the authoritative identifier for a candidate\'s legal right-to-work record within the Staffing Hr credentialing domain.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_account. Business justification: Work authorization requirements vary by client (federal contractors require E-Verify, some clients sponsor visas, client-specific I-9 policies). Real business process: client-specific work authorizati']
  credential_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the scanned or digitally stored work authorization document (e.g., visa copy, EAD card, passport) in the document management system (DocuSign CLM or equivalent). Enables retrieval of the underlying document for audit and compliance review without storing the document directly in the data lakehouse.']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential. Business justification: Link work authorization to the master credential definition (e.g., H1B Visa, Green Card, US Citizen). This allows standardizing work authorization types across all work authorization records. Removing']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate whose work authorization is being recorded. Links this authorization record to the candidate master profile in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  authorization_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers or recruiters to capture additional context about the candidate\'s work authorization status, including pending renewals, special conditions, attorney notes, or immigration counsel observations. Not used for structured data — structured fields take precedence.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work authorization record. \'active\' indicates the candidate is currently authorized to work; \'expired\' indicates the authorization period has lapsed; \'pending\' indicates verification or renewal is in progress; \'revoked\' indicates authorization was withdrawn; \'under_review\' indicates a compliance review is ongoing. Critical for placement eligibility gating.']
  authorized_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate\'s work authorization expires. Null for permanent authorizations (e.g., US Citizens, Permanent Residents with no expiry). Drives credential expiration alerts, renewal workflows, and assignment end-date compliance checks. Critical for avoiding unauthorized employment liability.']
  authorized_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate\'s work authorization becomes effective. Used to determine earliest eligible placement start date and to enforce compliance with immigration law. Corresponds to the \'authorized to work from\' date on the underlying visa or EAD document.']
  authorized_to_work_unrestricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has unrestricted authorization to work for any employer without limitation (e.g., US Citizens, Permanent Residents). True = no employer or occupation restrictions; False = authorization is tied to a specific employer, occupation, or time period. Used for rapid placement eligibility screening.']
  bullhorn_candidate_ref varchar [note: 'The external candidate identifier from Bullhorn ATS/CRM corresponding to this work authorization record. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the Staffing Hr data lakehouse and the Bullhorn operational system without relying solely on the internal surrogate key.']
  cap_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate\'s H-1B petition is exempt from the annual USCIS H-1B numerical cap (e.g., employed by a cap-exempt institution such as a university, nonprofit research organization, or government research entity). True = cap-exempt; False = subject to annual cap lottery. Relevant only for H-1B authorization type.']
  country_of_authorization varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the jurisdiction in which the candidate is authorized to work (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Supports multi-jurisdiction staffing operations and international placement compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this work authorization record was first created in the Staffing Hr data platform. Supports audit trail requirements, data lineage tracking, and compliance with SOC 2 Type II record-keeping obligations. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  ead_card_number varchar [note: 'The USCIS-issued Employment Authorization Document (EAD) card number (Form I-766) for candidates whose work authorization is evidenced by an EAD card. Distinct from visa_number. Applicable for OPT, CPT, H-4 EAD, TPS, and other EAD-based authorization types. Sensitive PII.']
  everify_case_number varchar [note: 'The case number assigned by the DHS E-Verify system when the candidate\'s work authorization was electronically verified. Links this authorization record to the corresponding E-Verify transaction. Null if E-Verify was not used. Supports compliance with federal contractor E-Verify mandates.']
  expiration_alert_date date [note: 'The date on which the expiration alert notification was sent. Null if expiration_alert_sent is False. Used to audit the timeliness of expiration notifications and to ensure compliance with internal SLA requirements for credential renewal management.']
  expiration_alert_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an automated expiration alert notification has been sent to the responsible recruiter or compliance team regarding the upcoming expiration of this work authorization. True = alert sent; False = alert not yet sent. Supports proactive credential management and prevents unauthorized employment.']
  green_card_priority_date date [note: 'The priority date established when a PERM labor certification or I-140 immigrant petition was filed on behalf of the candidate. Determines the candidate\'s place in the immigrant visa queue. Null if no green card process is underway. Used for long-term workforce planning and retention risk assessment.']
  i140_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether USCIS has approved an I-140 Immigrant Petition for Alien Workers on behalf of the candidate. True = I-140 approved; False = not approved or not filed. An approved I-140 enables H-1B extensions beyond the standard 6-year cap and supports AC21 portability analysis.']
  i94_admission_number varchar [note: 'The 11-digit admission number from the candidate\'s USCIS Form I-94 (Arrival/Departure Record), which establishes the authorized period of admission and status. Used to verify current lawful status and authorized stay duration. Sensitive immigration PII.']
  lca_number varchar [note: 'The Department of Labor (DOL)-assigned Labor Condition Application (LCA) case number associated with an H-1B or H-1B1 visa. The LCA certifies that the employer will pay the prevailing wage and maintain required working conditions. Required for H-1B compliance tracking and DOL audit readiness.']
  opt_stem_extension boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate is on a 24-month STEM OPT extension (applicable to F-1 students with STEM degrees). True = STEM extension applies, extending OPT authorization by 24 months beyond standard 12-month OPT. Relevant only when authorization_type = OPT. Drives extended placement eligibility windows.']
  opt_stem_extension_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the 24-month STEM OPT extension period. Null if opt_stem_extension is False or not applicable. Used to track the outer boundary of OPT-based work authorization for STEM candidates and to trigger timely H-1B sponsorship or transition planning.']
  passport_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country that issued the candidate\'s passport (e.g., IND, CHN, MEX, CAN). Used for immigration compliance, co-employment risk assessment, and OFCCP reporting. Distinct from country_of_authorization.']
  passport_expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the candidate\'s passport as presented during work authorization verification. Used to ensure the underlying identity document remains valid throughout the placement period. Triggers renewal alerts when approaching expiry.']
  passport_number varchar [note: 'The candidate\'s passport number as presented during work authorization verification. Used as a supporting identity document for I-9 compliance and immigration record-keeping. Sensitive government-issued PII — access restricted to authorized compliance personnel.']
  placement_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate\'s work authorization imposes restrictions on placement (e.g., H-1B candidates restricted to the petitioning employer\'s worksite, TN candidates restricted to specific occupations). True = placement restrictions apply; False = no restrictions. Triggers compliance review before assignment to a new client or location.']
  placement_restriction_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any specific placement restrictions associated with the work authorization (e.g., \'H-1B restricted to client XYZ worksite in Chicago, IL per LCA\', \'TN restricted to Software Engineer occupation only\'). Null if placement_restriction_flag is False. Used by recruiters and compliance teams during placement eligibility review.']
  portability_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate is eligible for H-1B portability under AC21 (American Competitiveness in the 21st Century Act), allowing them to change employers while an I-140 petition is pending. True = portability applies; False = not eligible. Critical for assessing whether a candidate can be placed with a new client without a new H-1B petition.']
  reverification_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the candidate\'s work authorization must be re-verified to maintain placement eligibility. Typically set to the authorized_end_date or a configurable number of days prior. Null if reverification_required is False. Drives proactive expiration alerts and compliance workflows.']
  reverification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate\'s work authorization requires re-verification before or upon expiration (e.g., EAD renewals, OPT extensions, TN renewals). True = re-verification is required; False = no re-verification needed (e.g., US Citizens). Drives automated renewal workflow triggers in the credentialing system.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this work authorization record was sourced or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across Bullhorn ATS/CRM, SAP SuccessFactors, and manual compliance entry workflows.']
  sponsoring_employer varchar [note: 'The legal name of the employer currently sponsoring the candidate\'s work visa or authorization (e.g., the H-1B petitioner or PERM sponsor). May be Staffing Hr itself (as Employer of Record) or a client company. Null if no sponsorship is required. Critical for co-employment risk management and portability analysis.']
  sponsoring_employer_ein varchar [note: 'The IRS-assigned Employer Identification Number (EIN) of the sponsoring employer. Used to verify the legal entity responsible for the sponsorship obligation and to support DOL Labor Condition Application (LCA) compliance. Format: XX-XXXXXXX. Confidential business financial identifier.']
  sponsorship_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate requires employer sponsorship (e.g., H-1B petition, PERM labor certification) to maintain or obtain work authorization. True = sponsorship required; False = no sponsorship needed. Critical for client placement eligibility screening — many clients explicitly exclude candidates requiring sponsorship.']
  staffing_vertical varchar [note: 'The industry vertical for which this work authorization record is primarily relevant. Different verticals have distinct credentialing and authorization requirements (e.g., healthcare requires additional license verification; government may require security clearance alignment). Supports vertical-specific compliance reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this work authorization record was most recently modified. Used to detect stale records, support incremental data loads in the Databricks Silver layer, and maintain audit trail integrity for immigration compliance reviews. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  uscis_case_number varchar [note: 'The official case number assigned by the United States Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) for the candidate\'s immigration petition or application (e.g., I-129 H-1B petition receipt number). Format: three-letter service center code followed by 10 digits (e.g., EAC2312345678). Used to track petition status and employer sponsorship obligations.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the work authorization documents were verified by the staffing firm. Used to establish the verification timeline for I-9 compliance audits and to calculate re-verification due dates for time-limited authorizations.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify the candidate\'s work authorization documents. \'document_review\' = physical or digital document inspection; \'e_verify\' = electronic verification via DHS E-Verify system; \'manual\' = manual internal review; \'third_party_vendor\' = verification performed by a credentialing vendor such as Sterling. Supports audit trail and FCRA compliance.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'The name or user identifier of the Staffing Hr compliance officer, recruiter, or authorized representative who performed the work authorization verification. Supports audit trail requirements and accountability for I-9 and immigration compliance.']
  visa_category varchar [note: 'The specific visa category or classification code as issued by the relevant immigration authority (e.g., H-1B, L-1A, L-1B, O-1A, O-1B, TN-1, TN-2, F-1 OPT, J-1). More granular than authorization_type and maps directly to the visa stamp classification. Used for precise immigration compliance reporting.']
  visa_number varchar [note: 'The official visa number or foil number as printed on the candidate\'s visa stamp or entry document. Uniquely identifies the specific visa issuance. Required for immigration compliance tracking and USCIS case management. Sensitive PII — access restricted to authorized compliance personnel.']

  Note: 'Master record capturing a candidate\'s work authorization status and documentation in the United States or other jurisdictions. Tracks authorization type (US Citizen, Permanent Resident, H-1B, OPT, TN, EAD, L-1, O-1, etc.), sponsorship required flag, visa number, visa category, authorized start date, authorized end date, country of authorization, sponsoring employer (if applicable), and USCIS case number. Distinct from I-9 verification (which is the compliance process) — this is the authoritative record of the worker\'s legal right to work. Critical for placement eligibility, co-employment risk management, and ensuring compliance with immigration law in staffing engagements.'
}

Table credentialing.credential_document {
  credential_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for credential_document']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement or assignment record this document supports. Links the credential document to the specific temporary or permanent placement for audit and compliance purposes.']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Background Check (BGC) order in Sterling Background Check that this document supports or was collected as part of. Nullable when the document is not associated with a BGC order.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account that mandated this document as part of their credentialing requirements. Nullable when the document is a general credentialing requirement not tied to a specific client.']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential. Business justification: Link the document to the master credential definition it supports (e.g., RN License document supports RN License credential). This allows standardizing credential types across all documents. Removing ']
  docusign_envelope_id varchar [note: 'Unique envelope identifier assigned by DocuSign CLM when the document was executed or stored electronically. Enables direct retrieval and audit trail linkage within the DocuSign Contract Lifecycle Management system.']
  license_verification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the license verification record this document supports. Links the physical or digital license copy to the corresponding verification record in the credentialing domain.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (Req/Requisition) for which this document was collected as part of the candidate credentialing process. Nullable when the document is collected independently of a specific job order.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate who submitted this credential document. Links the document to the candidate profile in Bullhorn ATS/CRM for credentialing and compliance tracking.']
  superseded_by_document_credential_document_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the newer credential document record that replaced this one. Enables document version lineage tracking and audit trail for FCRA and client audit compliance.']
  candidate_consent_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has provided explicit written consent for the collection, storage, and use of this document for credentialing and background screening purposes. Required under FCRA, GDPR, and CCPA.']
  client_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document was required by a specific client as part of their onboarding or credentialing program. When true, the document must be verified before the candidate can be placed with that client.']
  consent_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate provided consent for the collection and use of this document. Required for FCRA, GDPR, and CCPA audit trails.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credential document record was first created in the Staffing Hr credentialing system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for the master resource lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier or reference number assigned to the document by the issuing authority or institution (e.g., license number on a license copy, diploma serial number, passport number). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this master resource.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the document itself, independent of its verification status. Tracks whether the document is currently valid, expired, revoked by the issuing authority, or superseded by a newer version.']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title or name of the document as it appears on the physical or digital artifact (e.g., \'Registered Nurse License\', \'Bachelor of Science in Computer Science\', \'CPR Certification Card\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this master resource.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the credential document type submitted by the candidate. Drives downstream routing, verification workflows, and compliance checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: license|certification|diploma|transcript|government_id|immunization_record|drug_screen_result|reference_letter|background_check_consent|other — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the document is considered active and valid for credentialing purposes. May differ from issue_date for documents with a delayed activation period (e.g., renewed licenses effective on a future date).']
  expiration_alert_date date [note: 'The date on which the expiration alert notification was sent for this document. Used to track notification compliance and follow-up cadence for credential renewal.']
  expiration_alert_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an automated expiration alert notification has been sent to the candidate and/or credentialing team for this document. Supports proactive renewal management and assignment continuity.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the document expires and is no longer valid for credentialing or placement purposes. Drives automated expiration alerts, renewal workflows, and assignment eligibility checks. Null for documents with no expiration.']
  fcra_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the collection, storage, and use of this document complies with Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) requirements, including candidate consent and permissible purpose. Critical for background check document types.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored digital document (e.g., PDF, JPG, PNG, TIFF). Used for document rendering, archival compliance, and integration with document management systems.']
  file_size_kb varchar [note: 'Size of the stored document file in kilobytes. Used for storage management, upload validation, and document vault capacity planning.']
  hipaa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document contains Protected Health Information (PHI) subject to HIPAA regulations (e.g., immunization records, drug screen results, medical clearance letters). Drives PHI handling, access controls, and retention policies.']
  is_primary_document boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document is the primary or most current version of a given document type for the candidate. When false, the document may be a historical or superseded version retained for audit purposes.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the issuing authority originally issued or granted the document. Used to determine document age, validity windows, and renewal eligibility.']
