  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the institution, government body, or organization that issued the document (e.g., \'California Board of Registered Nursing\', \'CompTIA\', \'U.S. Department of Homeland Security\'). Critical for verification routing and source validation.']
  issuing_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the nation that issued the document. Required for I-9 employment eligibility verification and international credential validation.']
  issuing_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state code of the authority that issued the document. Applicable for state-issued licenses, certifications, and government IDs. Null for federally or internationally issued documents.']
  next_renewal_date date [note: 'The calculated or manually set date by which the candidate must renew this document to maintain credentialing compliance. Drives scheduling of renewal outreach and placement hold triggers.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for credentialing specialists to record observations, exceptions, follow-up actions, or contextual information about the document that is not captured in structured fields.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the document was rejected during the verification review process (e.g., expired, illegible, mismatched name, suspected fraud). Populated only when verification_status is \'rejected\'.']
  renewal_frequency_days varchar [note: 'The number of days between required renewals for this document type. Used to calculate the next renewal due date and schedule proactive outreach to the candidate.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document requires periodic renewal to maintain its validity for credentialing and placement eligibility. Drives renewal workflow triggers and expiration tracking.']
  staffing_vertical varchar [note: 'The staffing industry vertical for which this document is required or relevant. Drives vertical-specific credentialing rules (e.g., healthcare requires immunization records; government requires security clearance documents). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthcare|information_technology|skilled_trades|finance|government|general_staffing|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  storage_uri varchar [note: 'Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) pointing to the physical or digital document stored in DocuSign CLM or the enterprise document vault. Enables retrieval for audits, client requests, and FCRA/HIPAA compliance reviews. Access is governed by role-based security controls.']
  submitted_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate submitted this document to the staffing organization for credentialing review. Serves as the principal business event date (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP at day granularity) for the document intake lifecycle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credential document record was last modified in the Staffing Hr credentialing system. Supports audit trail, change detection, and incremental data pipeline processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify the authenticity and validity of the document (e.g., primary source verification with issuing authority, third-party vendor such as Sterling, manual review, E-Verify for employment eligibility).']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the document\'s verification process. Drives credentialing workflow actions, placement eligibility, and compliance holds. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this master resource.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or system identifier of the credentialing specialist or automated system that performed the document verification. Supports audit trail requirements under FCRA and client audit programs.']
  verified_date date [note: 'The date on which the document was confirmed as authentic and valid by the credentialing team or automated verification system. Null until verification is completed.']

  Note: 'Master record for physical or digital documents submitted by a candidate to support credentialing verification — diplomas, transcripts, license copies, certification cards, immunization records, professional references, and government-issued IDs. Captures document type, document title, issuing institution or authority, issue date, expiration date, document storage reference (DocuSign CLM or document vault URI), verification status, and the credential or verification record it supports. Provides the document audit trail required for FCRA, HIPAA, and client audit purposes.'
}

Table credentialing.education_verification {
  education_verification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated primary key for each education verification record. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — canonical entity with full lifecycle.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement or assignment record associated with this education verification, if the verification was triggered post-offer or at onboarding.']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Background Check (BGC) order in Sterling Background Check that triggered this education verification. Links to the bgc_order record in the credentialing domain.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account that mandated this education verification as part of their screening package or QoS requirements.']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential. Business justification: Link the education verification to the master credential definition (e.g., BSN Degree, MBA). This allows standardizing educational credential types across all verification transactions. Removing degre']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) that required this education verification, linking the credential check to the specific client requirement.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate whose educational credential is being verified. Links to the candidate master record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Education verifications performed by third-party vendors (National Student Clearinghouse, etc.). Required for vendor selection, turnaround tracking, billing reconciliation, and service quality monitor']
  actual_turnaround_hours varchar [note: 'The actual number of hours elapsed from order_date to verification_date. Used to measure SLA compliance and vendor performance for education verification.']
  adverse_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this education verification result triggered an adverse action process under FCRA. True when the outcome negatively impacts the candidate\'s employment eligibility.']
  billable_to_client boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost of this education verification is billable to the client as part of the screening fee pass-through arrangement in the MSA.']
  client_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this education verification was mandated by a specific client as part of their Quality of Submission (QoS) standards or Master Service Agreement (MSA) requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this education verification record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and FCRA compliance documentation.']
  degree_type_verified varchar [note: 'The degree type as confirmed by the institution or National Student Clearinghouse. May differ from the candidate-reported degree_type when a discrepancy is found.']
  discrepancy_detail varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific discrepancy found between candidate-reported and institutionally verified education data (e.g., degree type mismatch, graduation date off by one year, degree not conferred).']
  discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a discrepancy was found between the candidate-reported educational information and the verified institutional records. True triggers adverse action review under FCRA.']
  document_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the supporting document (diploma scan, transcript, NSC report) stored in the document management system. Used for audit and re-verification.']
  enrollment_status_at_graduation varchar [note: 'The enrollment status returned by the institution or NSC at the time of the reported graduation date. Confirms whether the candidate was actually enrolled and completed the program.']
  field_of_study_verified varchar [note: 'The field of study as confirmed by the institution or National Student Clearinghouse. May differ from the candidate-reported field_of_study when a discrepancy is found.']
  graduation_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate graduated or completed the educational program, as reported by the candidate. Compared against institutional records during verification.']
  graduation_date_verified date [note: 'The graduation date as confirmed by the institution or National Student Clearinghouse during verification. May differ from the candidate-reported graduation_date when a discrepancy is found.']
  institution_city varchar [note: 'City where the educational institution is located. Used for geographic analysis and to disambiguate institutions with similar names.']
  institution_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the educational institution is located. Supports international credential verification workflows.']
  institution_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the educational institution (university, college, trade school, high school) as reported by the candidate and cross-referenced during verification.']
  institution_state varchar [note: 'U.S. state (or equivalent) where the educational institution is located. Two-letter USPS state code. Used for jurisdictional compliance and verification routing.']
  nsc_student_number varchar [note: 'Student identifier returned by the National Student Clearinghouse (NSC) during automated verification. Used for re-verification and audit trail purposes.']
  order_date date [note: 'The date on which the education verification was ordered, typically coinciding with the BGC order date. Used for SLA turnaround calculation and compliance audit.']
  recheck_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a re-check of the education verification is required (e.g., institution did not respond, candidate disputed result, NSC record not found).']
  recheck_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this education verification requires a re-check, typically due to an inconclusive result, institution non-participation, or a candidate dispute.']
  report_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL to the full education verification report in Sterling Background Check or the document management system. Access is role-restricted per FCRA and data privacy requirements.']
  sla_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the education verification was completed within the contracted SLA turnaround time. Used for vendor performance reporting and QoS analytics.']
  sla_turnaround_hours varchar [note: 'The contracted or standard Service Level Agreement (SLA) turnaround time in hours for completing this education verification, as defined by the client or BGC package.']
  staffing_vertical varchar [note: 'The staffing industry vertical for which this education verification is being conducted. Different verticals have distinct credentialing mandates (e.g., healthcare requires degree verification for clinical roles). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthcare|information_technology|finance|government|skilled_trades|engineering|education|legal|general — promote to reference product]']
  sterling_order_number varchar [note: 'External order identifier assigned by Sterling Background Check for this education verification component. Used for reconciliation and status tracking with the vendor.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this education verification record was last modified. Tracks status changes, outcome updates, and analyst notes for audit and data lineage purposes.']
  verification_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'The cost charged by the verification vendor (e.g., Sterling, NSC) for conducting this education verification. Used for billing reconciliation and cost allocation.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the education verification was completed and the outcome was determined. Used for turnaround time measurement and QoS reporting.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify the educational credential. NSC (National Student Clearinghouse) is the primary automated method; direct institution contact is used when NSC is unavailable; third-party refers to other accredited verification vendors.']
  verification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the credentialing analyst regarding special circumstances, institution contact details, candidate communications, or other relevant context for this verification.']
  verification_number varchar [note: 'Unique business-facing reference number for this education verification record, used in client communications, QoS reporting, and audit trails.']
  verification_outcome varchar [note: 'Final result of the education verification. \'Verified\' confirms the credential matches institutional records; \'discrepancy_found\' indicates a mismatch; \'institution_not_participating\' means the school does not respond to verification requests (common with National Student Clearinghouse non-members).']
  verification_source_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific source used to verify the credential (e.g., \'National Student Clearinghouse\', \'University Registrar Office\', \'Credentials Inc.\'). Provides audit traceability.']
  verification_source_url varchar [note: 'URL or web reference for the verification source or portal used to confirm the credential. Supports audit documentation and re-verification workflows.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle status of the education verification request. Drives operational queues and SLA tracking.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the verification outcome was recorded in the system. Supports audit trail and FCRA compliance documentation.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the credentialing analyst, system, or vendor representative who completed and recorded the verification outcome. Supports audit trail and quality assurance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the verification of a candidate\'s educational credentials against issuing institutions or the National Student Clearinghouse. Tracks institution name, degree or diploma type, field of study, graduation date, verification method (NSC, direct institution contact, third-party), verification date, verification outcome (verified, not verified, discrepancy found, institution not participating), and the BGC order that triggered the verification. Supports quality of submission (QoS) standards and client-mandated degree requirements.'
}

Table credentialing.employment_verification {
  employment_verification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for the employment verification record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — each record represents a discrete verification event for one prior employer entry in a candidate\'s work history.']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent Background Check (BGC) order under which this employment verification component was initiated. Links to the bgc_order product in the credentialing domain.']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential. Business justification: Link the employment verification to the master credential definition (e.g., 2 Years RN Experience, 5 Years Management Experience). This allows standardizing employment credential types across all veri']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate whose prior employment is being verified. Links this verification record to the candidate\'s profile in the Bullhorn ATS/CRM system.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Employment verifications outsourced to screening vendors. Supports vendor assignment, cost allocation, quality monitoring, and SLA compliance tracking. New attribute needed to capture vendor relations']
  work_history_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific candidate work history entry (prior employer record) that is the subject of this verification. Links to the candidate.work_history product.']
  adverse_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this employment verification result contributed to or triggered an adverse action decision against the candidate. Drives FCRA-mandated pre-adverse and final adverse action notification workflows.']
  candidate_consent_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate provided written authorization for the employment verification prior to initiation, as required by FCRA. Must be True before verification can proceed.']
  candidate_stated_end_date date [note: 'Employment end date as reported by the candidate. Null if the candidate indicated current employment. Retained for discrepancy comparison against the verified end date.']
  candidate_stated_start_date date [note: 'Employment start date as reported by the candidate on their application or resume. Retained as the candidate-stated value for discrepancy comparison against the verified start date.']
  candidate_stated_title varchar [note: 'Job title at the prior employer as stated by the candidate on their application or resume. Retained for discrepancy comparison against the verified job title.']
  client_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this employment verification was specifically required by the client as part of their BGC package or credentialing requirements for the associated job order or placement.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the candidate provided written consent for the employment verification. Required for FCRA compliance documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this employment verification record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 compliance requirements.']
  discrepancy_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the specific nature of the discrepancy found between candidate-stated and verified employment information. Used by adjudicators and compliance teams for adverse action decisions.']
  discrepancy_found_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any discrepancy was identified between the candidate-stated employment information and the verified information. True when verification_outcome is \'discrepancy_found\'. Used as a key filter in adverse action workflows.']
  discrepancy_type varchar [note: 'Category of discrepancy identified between candidate-stated and verified employment information. Populated only when discrepancy_found_flag is True. Drives adjudication and adverse action workflows.']
  eligibility_for_rehire varchar [note: 'Prior employer\'s stated eligibility of the candidate for rehire. A key credentialing data point for staffing placements. \'Not_disclosed\' when the employer declines to provide this information per their HR policy.']
  employer_city varchar [note: 'City of the prior employer\'s work location as stated by the candidate. Used for geographic context and verification routing.']
  employer_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the prior employer\'s location. Supports international employment verification workflows and GDPR/CCPA compliance scoping.']
  employer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the prior employer as stated by the candidate on their application or resume. This is the candidate-stated value prior to verification, retained for discrepancy comparison.']
  employer_state varchar [note: 'U.S. state (two-letter abbreviation) or province of the prior employer\'s work location. Used for jurisdiction-specific verification rules and compliance.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the prior employment relationship as stated by the candidate. Full-Time Equivalent (FTE), contract, temporary (temp), part-time, intern, or other. Relevant for workforce analytics and client-specific credentialing requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fte|contract|temp|part_time|intern|per_diem|seasonal|other — promote to reference product]']
  fcra_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this employment verification was conducted in full compliance with Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) requirements, including proper candidate consent, permissible purpose, and disclosure obligations.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date on which the employment verification was ordered/initiated as part of the BGC process. Used to calculate turnaround time and SLA compliance.']
  reason_for_leaving varchar [note: 'Reason the candidate left the prior employer as confirmed through verification. May differ from candidate-stated reason. Sensitive business data used in placement suitability assessments.']
  recheck_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a recheck of this employment verification is required. Populated only when recheck_required_flag is True. Examples: candidate dispute, employer non-response, data quality issue.']
  recheck_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this employment verification requires a recheck due to inconclusive results, employer non-response, or a dispute initiated by the candidate.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employment verification was completed within the contracted Service Level Agreement (SLA) turnaround time. Used for vendor performance management and client reporting.']
  staffing_vertical varchar [note: 'Industry vertical for which this employment verification is being conducted. Different verticals (e.g., healthcare, IT, government) have distinct credentialing depth requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthcare|information_technology|finance|government|skilled_trades|legal|education|general_staffing — promote to reference product]']
  sterling_verification_number varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by Sterling Background Check to this specific employment verification component within the BGC order. Used for reconciliation with the Sterling source system.']
  turnaround_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual number of hours elapsed from order initiation to verification completion. Used to measure performance against SLA turnaround commitments and to benchmark verification method efficiency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this employment verification record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the employment verification was completed and the outcome was determined. Used for compliance tracking and credential expiration calculations.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the prior employment. Includes direct employer contact (phone/email), The Work Number/Equifax automated service, third-party verification vendor, written request, or employer online portal. Impacts turnaround time and reliability scoring.']
  verification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the Sterling analyst or credentialing team regarding the employment verification process, employer contact attempts, or special circumstances affecting the outcome.']
  verification_outcome varchar [note: 'Final result of the employment verification indicating whether the candidate-stated employment was confirmed, could not be confirmed, or revealed a discrepancy. Core adjudication field used in BGC decision-making.']
  verification_source_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific source used to verify employment (e.g., \'The Work Number by Equifax\', \'HR Direct Contact\', name of third-party verifier). Provides audit trail for the verification chain of custody.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the employment verification process. Tracks the lifecycle from initiation through completion or cancellation.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone) when the employment verification outcome was recorded in the system. Supports audit trail and SLA measurement.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the Sterling Background Check analyst or automated system component that completed and recorded the employment verification outcome.']
  verified_employer_name varchar [note: 'Employer name as confirmed through the verification process. May differ from candidate-stated employer_name if a discrepancy is found (e.g., subsidiary vs. parent company name).']
  verified_end_date date [note: 'Employment end date as confirmed through the verification process. Null if employment is confirmed as ongoing. Compared against candidate_stated_end_date to identify date discrepancies.']
  verified_start_date date [note: 'Employment start date as confirmed through the verification process. Compared against candidate_stated_start_date to identify date discrepancies.']
  verified_title varchar [note: 'Job title at the prior employer as confirmed through the verification process. Compared against candidate_stated_title to identify title discrepancies.']
  verifier_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual at the prior employer (e.g., HR representative) who provided verification confirmation during direct employer contact. Retained for audit and dispute resolution purposes.']
  verifier_contact_title varchar [note: 'Job title of the individual at the prior employer who provided verification. Supports credibility assessment of the verification source.']
  years_of_experience_verified decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total years of experience at this prior employer as calculated from the verified start and end dates. Used in candidate qualification assessments and skills gap analysis for placement suitability.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the verification of a candidate\'s prior employment history as part of the BGC or credentialing process. Tracks employer name, employment dates (start and end), job title, employment type (FTE, contract, temp), verification method (direct employer contact, The Work Number/Equifax, third-party), verification date, outcome (verified, unverified, discrepancy found, employer unresponsive), and any discrepancies between candidate-stated and verified information. Sourced from Sterling BGC employment verification component.'
}

Table credentialing.reference_check {
  reference_check_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the reference check record. Primary key for the credentialing.reference_check data product.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the placement record associated with this reference check, if the check was conducted in support of an active or pending placement decision.']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated background check (BGC) order from Sterling, if this reference check was conducted as part of a broader screening package.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client account for which this reference check was conducted, when the check is tied to a specific client requirement or placement.']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential. Business justification: Link reference check to the master credential definition (e.g., Professional Reference Check, Character Reference). This allows standardizing reference check types across all reference check transacti']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the job order (requisition) for which this reference check was initiated. Ties the reference check to a specific open position in Bullhorn.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the candidate for whom this professional reference check was conducted. Links to the candidate profile in the Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Reference checks outsourced to third-party vendors. Supports vendor assignment, turnaround monitoring, cost tracking, and service quality management. Replaces denormalized third_party_vendor_name with']
  areas_for_improvement varchar [note: 'Summarized areas for development or improvement identified by the reference contact. Used in candidate coaching, onboarding planning, and placement suitability assessments.']
  attempt_count varchar [note: 'Total number of contact attempts made to reach the reference contact before a successful interview or a final unable-to-reach determination. Supports recruiter workflow tracking.']
  candidate_consent_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate provided explicit written consent for the reference check to be conducted. Required for FCRA compliance and GDPR lawful basis for processing.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this reference check, used in recruiter workflows, ATS tracking, and client-facing documentation. Sourced from Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  check_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reference check workflow. Tracks progression from initiation through completion or cancellation.']
  client_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reference check was mandated by the client as part of their onboarding or credentialing requirements under the Master Service Agreement (MSA).']
  communication_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'Structured numeric rating score for the candidate\'s communication skills as assessed by the reference contact, typically on a scale of 1.0 to 5.0.']
  completed_date date [note: 'Date on which the reference check was fully completed and the result was recorded. Used for time-to-fill (TTF) analytics and credentialing workflow SLA measurement.']
  conducted_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the recruiter, HR professional, or third-party vendor who conducted the reference check interview on behalf of Staffing Hr.']
  conducted_by_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the reference check was conducted by an internal recruiter, hiring manager, third-party vendor, or automated reference platform. Supports quality and compliance auditing.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the candidate provided consent for the reference check. Required for FCRA and GDPR compliance documentation and audit trail.']
  contact_date date [note: 'The date on which the reference contact was successfully reached and the reference check interview was conducted. Principal real-world event date for this transaction.']
  contact_method varchar [note: 'Method used to conduct the reference check interview (e.g., phone, email, video call, written questionnaire, or third-party vendor platform).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reference check record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this reference check result is considered expired and a new check may be required. Relevant for clients with recurring credentialing requirements or time-sensitive placements.']
  first_attempt_date date [note: 'Date of the first attempt to contact the reference. Used to measure time-to-complete for reference checks and support SLA (Service Level Agreement) tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General operational notes recorded by the recruiter or coordinator regarding the reference check process, logistics, or any anomalies encountered during the check.']
  overall_recommendation varchar [note: 'Summarized overall recommendation outcome from the reference check interview. Drives placement decision workflows and candidate credentialing status in Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  placement_decision_notes varchar [note: 'Recruiter or hiring manager notes documenting how this reference check result influenced the placement decision. Confidential business record supporting EEOC and OFCCP compliance documentation.']
  reference_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the professional reference contact. PII subject to GDPR, CCPA, and FCRA data handling and retention requirements.']
  reference_employer varchar [note: 'Name of the organization where the reference contact was employed at the time of the reference check. Establishes the professional context of the reference relationship.']
  reference_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the professional reference contact who provided the reference. Personally identifiable information subject to GDPR, CCPA, and FCRA data handling requirements.']
  reference_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number used to contact the professional reference. PII subject to GDPR, CCPA, and FCRA data handling and retention requirements.']
  reference_title varchar [note: 'Professional job title of the reference contact at the time the reference was provided (e.g., Senior Manager, Director of Engineering). Provides context for the weight of the reference.']
  rehire_eligible varchar [note: 'The reference contact\'s stated eligibility determination for rehiring the candidate at their organization. A key indicator used in placement decisions and candidate credentialing.']
  relationship_duration_months varchar [note: 'Number of months the reference contact worked with or managed the candidate. Indicates the depth of the reference\'s knowledge of the candidate\'s performance.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the professional relationship between the reference contact and the candidate (e.g., supervisor, peer, subordinate, client). Determines the credibility and perspective of the reference.']
  reliability_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'Structured numeric rating score for the candidate\'s reliability, punctuality, and dependability as assessed by the reference contact, typically on a scale of 1.0 to 5.0.']
  staffing_vertical varchar [note: 'Industry vertical for which this reference check was conducted. Different verticals (e.g., healthcare, IT, skilled trades) may have specific credentialing and reference check requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthcare|information_technology|finance|skilled_trades|government|general_staffing|legal|engineering|education|manufacturing — promote to reference product]']
  strengths_noted varchar [note: 'Summarized key strengths of the candidate as identified by the reference contact during the reference check interview. Supports candidate profile enrichment and placement matching.']
  teamwork_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'Structured numeric rating score for the candidate\'s ability to collaborate and work effectively in a team environment, as assessed by the reference contact, typically on a scale of 1.0 to 5.0.']
  third_party_reference_code varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by the third-party reference check vendor for this check. Enables reconciliation with vendor records and audit trail.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reference check record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  used_in_placement_decision boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reference check result was formally used as part of the placement decision for the candidate. Supports compliance auditing and EEOC/OFCCP reporting.']
  verbatim_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text verbatim comments and qualitative feedback provided by the reference contact during the interview. Confidential business data used by recruiters and hiring managers in placement decisions.']
  work_quality_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'Structured numeric rating score provided by the reference contact for the candidate\'s quality of work output, typically on a scale of 1.0 to 5.0. Used in placement decision analytics.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a professional reference check conducted for a candidate as part of the screening and credentialing process. Tracks reference contact name, relationship to candidate (supervisor, peer, subordinate, client), employer at time of reference, contact method, contact date, recruiter or third-party conducting the check, structured rating scores (work quality, reliability, communication, rehire eligibility), verbatim comments, overall recommendation (positive, neutral, negative), and whether the reference was used in a placement decision.'
}

Table credentialing.package_requirement {
  package_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for this package requirement record. Primary key.']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the credential that is required by this package']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the credential package that contains this requirement']
  last_modified_by_user_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this package requirement configuration.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this package requirement configuration.']
  acceptable_expiry_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before credential expiration that the credential is still considered acceptable for placement under this package. Allows package-specific tolerance (e.g., client may accept credential expiring within 30 days for short-term assignments).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this package requirement record was created.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sort order for displaying this credential requirement within the package checklist presented to candidates and recruiters.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this credential requirement becomes effective for this package. Supports phased rollout of new requirements.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this credential requirement is no longer applicable for this package. Supports time-bound requirements (e.g., COVID-19 vaccination required only during pandemic period).']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after credential expiration during which the credential is still considered acceptable for this package. Package-specific override of the credential\'s default grace_period_days.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential is mandatory for the package (true) or optional/preferred (false). Drives candidate readiness evaluation logic.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this package requirement record was last modified.']
  renewal_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before credential expiration that the renewal process must be initiated for this package. Drives proactive renewal notifications.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate must renew this credential if it expires during the assignment period covered by this package.']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this requirement within the package. Allows individual requirements to be deprecated without removing the entire package.']
  verification_level varchar [note: 'The level of verification rigor required for this credential within this package context. May differ from the credential\'s default verification_method based on client or program requirements.']
  waiver_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential requirement can be waived for this package under exceptional circumstances (e.g., pending license transfer, temporary assignment).']
  waiver_approval_level varchar [note: 'The organizational approval level required to grant a waiver for this credential requirement within this package context.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the requirement relationship between credential_package and credential. It captures the specific rules and constraints that govern how each credential must be satisfied within the context of a specific credentialing package. Each record links one credential_package to one credential with attributes that define whether the credential is mandatory, verification requirements, expiry windows, renewal policies, and waiver rules for that specific package-credential combination.. Existence Justification: In staffing and recruitment operations, credential packages are reusable bundles that combine multiple credentials with package-specific rules. A single package (e.g., Healthcare Travel Nurse Package) requires multiple credentials (RN license, BLS, immunizations, background check), and a single credential (e.g., RN license) is required by multiple packages across different clients and verticals. Compliance teams actively configure and manage these package-credential relationships, setting mandatory/optional flags, verification levels, expiry windows, and waiver policies for each combination.'
}

Table credentialing.checklist_credential_requirement {
  checklist_credential_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for this checklist credential requirement record. Primary key.']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the credential definition that is required by this checklist template']
  task_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the task checklist template that requires this credential']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this credential requirement. Values: active (currently enforced), inactive (not enforced, may be reactivated), pending (scheduled to become active), superseded (replaced by a newer version of the requirement).']
  blocking_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether failure to meet this credential requirement blocks progression or completion of the checklist (True) or is tracked as non-compliant but does not prevent checklist completion (False). When True and is_mandatory is True, the worker cannot be placed or onboarded until this credential is verified. Drives workflow gating logic.']
  due_date_offset_days varchar [note: 'Number of days relative to the checklist start date (typically assignment start date or onboarding initiation date) by which this credential must be verified and compliant. Negative values indicate credential must be verified before checklist start (e.g., -7 means 7 days before assignment start). Positive values allow verification after start (e.g., 30 means within 30 days of assignment start). Zero means must be verified by start date.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this credential requirement becomes active for this checklist template. Supports versioning and phased rollout of new credential requirements (e.g., new client contract effective date, new regulatory requirement effective date).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this credential requirement is no longer active for this checklist template. Nullable for ongoing requirements. Supports sunsetting of requirements when contracts end or regulations change.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential is mandatory (True) or optional/recommended (False) for completion of this specific checklist. Drives enforcement logic and blocking behavior.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or exceptions related to this credential requirement for this checklist. May include client-specific instructions, alternative verification methods, or conditional logic notes.']
  requirement_source varchar [note: 'The origin or authority that mandates this credential requirement for this checklist. Drives auditability and explains why the requirement exists. Values: regulatory (mandated by law or regulation), client_contract (required by specific client agreement), company_policy (internal Staffing Hr policy), industry_standard (best practice or industry norm), risk_mitigation (added to reduce operational or liability risk).']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Display sequence for this credential requirement within the checklist template. Used to present credentials in a logical order to recruiters and candidates (e.g., licenses first, then certifications, then immunizations).']
  verification_level varchar [note: 'The required verification rigor for this credential within this checklist context. May differ from the credential\'s default verification_method based on client requirements, placement type, or risk profile. Values: primary_source (verify directly with issuer), secondary_source (verify via third-party database), self_attestation (candidate provides documentation), visual_inspection (recruiter reviews copy), not_required (credential tracked but not verified for this checklist).']
  waiver_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential requirement can be waived by an authorized approver (True) or is non-waivable due to regulatory or client contractual mandate (False). When True, a waiver workflow can be initiated; when False, the credential must be satisfied without exception.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the requirement relationship between a task checklist template and a credential type. It captures the specific mandate, verification, and timing rules that govern when and how a credential must be verified as part of completing an onboarding or offboarding checklist. Each record links one credential to one task checklist with attributes that define the enforcement policy, verification level, timing offset, blocking behavior, and waiver eligibility for that specific checklist-credential combination.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, task checklist templates require multiple credentials (licenses, certifications, immunizations, background checks, drug screens) based on placement type, client requirements, and regulatory mandates. Each credential type applies to multiple checklist templates across different roles, geographies, and client accounts. The business actively manages these requirements as configuration rules, specifying for each checklist-credential pair whether the credential is mandatory, what verification level is required, timing offsets, blocking behavior, and waiver eligibility. This is a true operational M:N relationship recognized by the business as \'Checklist Credential Requirements\' or \'Credential Requirement Rules.\''
}

Table credentialing.service {
  service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each credentialing service agreement record']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the credential type being verified or assessed by this supplier']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier providing verification or assessment services for this credential type']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'The internal team or role that approved this supplier for this credential type (e.g., Compliance Team, Credentialing Manager, VP Operations)']
  cost_per_verification decimal(10,2) [note: 'The negotiated price (in USD) that this supplier charges for each verification or assessment of this credential type. Pricing varies by supplier-credential combination. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this credentialing service agreement record was created']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this supplier became approved to provide verification services for this credential type. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this suppliers approval to verify this credential type expires or requires renewal. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which this suppliers service quality for this credential type was audited by internal compliance team']
  performance_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Rolling performance score (0.00 to 5.00) for this supplier on this credential type based on turnaround time, accuracy, and customer satisfaction']
  preferred_supplier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supplier is the preferred/primary vendor for this credential type based on cost, performance, or strategic relationship']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this credentialing service agreement (active, suspended, expired, pending approval, terminated)']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The type of credentialing service this supplier provides for this credential (e.g., primary source verification, background check, drug screening, skills assessment). Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  sla_hours varchar [note: 'Contracted turnaround time in hours for this supplier to complete verification/assessment of this credential type. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this credentialing service agreement record was last modified']
  volume_discount_threshold varchar [note: 'Number of verifications per month at which volume discount pricing takes effect for this supplier-credential combination']

  Note: 'This association product represents the service agreement between credential types and suppliers who provide verification, assessment, or compliance services for those credentials. It captures the operational catalog of which suppliers are approved to verify/assess which credential types, along with service-level agreements, pricing, turnaround times, and performance metrics. Each record links one credential type to one approved supplier with attributes that exist only in the context of this service relationship.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, credential verification is outsourced to specialized suppliers who provide services like background checks, drug screening, license verification, and skills assessments. A single credential type (e.g., RN license, OSHA 10, TB immunization) can be verified by multiple approved suppliers, each with different pricing, turnaround times, and service quality. Conversely, a single supplier typically provides verification services for multiple credential types across their service portfolio. The business actively manages this service catalog, tracking SLAs, costs, performance ratings, and approval status for each supplier-credential combination.'
}

Table credentialing.credential_type {
  credential_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for credential_type']
  parent_credential_type_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on credential_type (parent_credential_type_id)']
  allowed_document_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of acceptable document file types or formats for this credential type (e.g., PDF, JPG, PNG). Null if document upload is not required or any type is accepted.']
  background_check_scope varchar [note: 'For background check credential types, specifies the scope or type of background check (e.g., criminal, employment verification, education verification). Null for non-BGC credential types.']
  category varchar [note: 'High-level category classifying the credential type into major groupings for reporting and filtering.']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the credential type (e.g., RN_LIC, CPR_CERT, BGC_BASIC). Used as a business key for system integration and reporting.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory or compliance framework that mandates or governs this credential type (e.g., Joint Commission, OSHA, state licensing board). Null if not tied to a specific framework.']
  cost_estimate_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated average cost in USD for obtaining or renewing this credential type, including application fees, exam fees, and verification costs. Null if cost is not tracked or highly variable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credential type record was first created in the system.']
  default_validity_period_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days this credential type remains valid from issuance or renewal date. Null if credential does not expire or validity varies by issuer.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the credential type, including its purpose, scope, and any special requirements or notes.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value used to control the sort order of credential types in user interfaces, reports, and dropdown lists. Lower values appear first.']
  drug_screen_panel_type varchar [note: 'For drug screening credential types, specifies the panel or test type (e.g., 5-panel, 10-panel, DOT-compliant). Null for non-drug-screen credential types.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this credential type became or will become active and available for use in the credentialing system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when this credential type was or will be retired or deprecated. Null if the credential type is still active or has no planned end date.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'Primary industry vertical or sector where this credential type is most commonly required (e.g., healthcare, IT, skilled trades).']
  is_client_visible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential type should be visible to client users in client-facing portals and reports (True) or is for internal use only (False).']
  is_mandatory_for_placement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential type is commonly required as a mandatory prerequisite for candidate placement in roles within its industry vertical (True) or is optional/preferred (False).']
  issuing_authority_type varchar [note: 'Type of organization or entity that issues or validates this credential (e.g., government agency, professional association, third-party vendor).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credential type record was last modified or updated.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the credential type (e.g., Registered Nurse License, CPR Certification, Basic Background Check).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or context about this credential type that does not fit in other structured fields.']
  processing_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of days required to complete verification or obtain results for this credential type. Null if processing time is not tracked or highly variable.']
  renewal_reminder_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before expiration when renewal reminders should be triggered for candidates holding this credential type. Null if no reminder is configured.']
  requires_document_upload boolean [note: 'Indicates whether candidates or staff must upload supporting documentation (e.g., certificate, license scan) when recording this credential type (True) or if documentation is optional or not required (False).']
  requires_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential type has an expiration date and requires periodic renewal (True) or is valid indefinitely once obtained (False).']
  skills_assessment_domain varchar [note: 'For skills assessment credential types, specifies the skill domain or competency area being assessed (e.g., Java Programming, Welding, Clinical Nursing Skills). Null for non-assessment credential types.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credential type in the system. Active types are available for assignment; inactive or deprecated types are retained for historical reference only.']
  training_provider_type varchar [note: 'For training credential types, specifies the type of organization that provides the training (e.g., internal company training, external vendor, accredited institution). Null for non-training credential types.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary third-party vendor or service provider used for verification or processing of this credential type (e.g., Sterling for background checks). Null if processed internally or not vendor-dependent.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to verify or validate this credential type (e.g., automated API check, manual document review, third-party verification service).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for credential_type. Referenced by credential_type_id.'
}

Table vendor.supplier {
  supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the supplier (staffing agency, IC supplier, SOW provider, or subcontractor) within the MSP/VMS-managed contingent workforce program. Primary key for all vendor identity across all programs.']
  relationship_manager_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Tracks internal employee (vendor manager/account executive) responsible for managing supplier relationship. Essential for account ownership, performance reviews, escalations, and vendor communication ']
  business_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the supplier\'s primary business model within the contingent workforce program. Drives workflow routing, compliance requirements, and rate card applicability. Values: staffing_agency (W-2 or 1099 temp placement), ic_supplier (Independent Contractor supplier), sow_provider (Statement of Work delivery), subcontractor (tier-2 supplier), peo (Professional Employer Organization).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the supplier record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 Type II compliance requirements.']
  diversity_cert_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the supplier\'s most current diversity certification. Triggers renewal workflow and flags suppliers with lapsed certifications for OFCCP compliance reporting.']
  diversity_certifications varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of active diversity certifications held by the supplier (e.g., MBE=Minority Business Enterprise, WBE=Women Business Enterprise, SDVOB=Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Business, HUBZone, 8(a)). Required for OFCCP supplier diversity reporting and client diversity spend commitments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MBE|WBE|SDVOB|HUBZone|8a|VOSB|LGBTBE|AABE — promote to reference product]']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) 9-digit identifier for the supplier. Used for supplier credentialing, federal contractor verification, and OFCCP compliance reporting.']
  ein varchar [note: 'IRS-issued Employer Identification Number (EIN) for the supplier legal entity. Required for tax reporting (W-9, 1099-MISC), MSA execution, and AP vendor setup in Oracle NetSuite ERP.']
  fall_off_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of placements made by this supplier that ended prematurely (fall-offs) within the defined measurement period. A key quality KPI used in supplier scorecarding and tier classification decisions.']
  hq_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the supplier\'s headquarters location. Used for legal correspondence, MSA execution, and AP remittance.']
  hq_city varchar [note: 'City of the supplier\'s headquarters location.']
  hq_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the supplier\'s headquarters. Supports international supplier programs and GDPR/CCPA jurisdiction determination.']
  hq_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP/postal code of the supplier\'s headquarters. Used for geographic analytics, tax jurisdiction mapping, and remittance address validation.']
  hq_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state code (or equivalent) for the supplier\'s headquarters. Used for SUTA jurisdiction determination, workers compensation rate assignment, and state-level compliance.']
  ic_business_structure varchar [note: 'Legal business structure of the IC supplier entity. Determines tax treatment, liability exposure, and co-employment risk profile. Applicable only when business_type = ic_supplier.']
  ic_compliance_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IC supplier subtype is enrolled in a formal Independent Contractor (IC) compliance program (e.g., MBO Partners, Shiftpixy, WorkMarket). Applicable only when business_type = ic_supplier. Required for IRS worker classification risk mitigation.']
  ic_compliance_vendor varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party IC compliance management vendor (e.g., MBO Partners, Shiftpixy, WorkMarket, Openforce) engaged by the IC supplier to manage worker classification compliance, IC agreements, and tax administration.']
  ic_risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score (typically 1–100) assigned to the IC supplier based on IRS worker classification criteria, co-employment exposure, indemnification coverage, and compliance program enrollment. Higher scores indicate greater misclassification risk. Used for IC supplier risk tiering and audit prioritization.']
  ic_worker_classification varchar [note: 'IRS worker classification determination status for the IC supplier subtype. Reflects the outcome of the classification assessment against IRS common law rules and DOL economic reality test. Applicable only when business_type = ic_supplier.']
  indemnification_agreement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier has executed an indemnification agreement protecting the MSP/client from worker misclassification claims, co-employment liability, and related legal exposure. Critical for IC supplier risk management.']
  insurance_gl_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum required general liability insurance coverage amount (in USD) that the supplier must maintain as a condition of program participation. Validated during onboarding and annual compliance review.']
  insurance_verified_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which the supplier\'s insurance certificates (general liability, workers compensation, E&O) were verified and confirmed to meet program minimums.']
  insurance_wc_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum required Workers Compensation (WC) insurance coverage amount (in USD) that the supplier must maintain. Applicable to staffing agencies placing W-2 workers. Validated during onboarding.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal registered name of the supplier entity as it appears on government filings, MSA, and tax documents. Used for contract execution, invoicing, and compliance reporting.']
  msa_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the Master Service Agreement (MSA) with the supplier became legally binding and operative.']
  msa_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the Master Service Agreement (MSA) with the supplier expires. Triggers contract renewal workflow in DocuSign CLM. Null indicates an evergreen/open-ended agreement.']
  msa_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Master Service Agreement (MSA) between the MSP/staffing firm and the supplier. Governs the legal framework for all placements and SOW engagements. Managed via DocuSign CLM.']
  naics_code varchar [note: '6-digit North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code identifying the supplier\'s primary industry sector. Used for spend analytics, diversity reporting, and regulatory classification.']
  nda_executed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) has been fully executed between the MSP/staffing firm and the supplier. Managed via DocuSign CLM.']
  non_compete_executed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a non-compete or non-solicitation agreement has been executed with the supplier, restricting direct placement of workers with clients outside the MSP program.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the supplier\'s onboarding and program participation within the MSP/VMS-managed contingent workforce program. Drives eligibility to submit candidates and receive job orders.']
  operational_regions varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of geographic regions (U.S. states, metro areas, or country codes) where the supplier actively places workers or delivers services. Used for job order routing, geographic coverage analytics, and program expansion planning.']
  performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite performance score (0.00–100.00) from the supplier\'s most recent scorecard evaluation. Derived from KPIs including Quality of Submission (QoS), Time to Fill (TTF), fill rate, fall-off rate, and SLA compliance. Used for tier reclassification and program allocation decisions.']
  performance_score_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent supplier performance scorecard evaluation. Used to determine score currency and trigger re-evaluation workflows.']
  preferred_supplier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds preferred supplier status within the MSP program, granting priority job order routing, higher volume allocation, and preferred rate card terms.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Business email address of the supplier\'s primary program contact. Used for job order notifications, compliance communications, and VMS system alerts.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the supplier organization responsible for program management, escalations, and relationship management with the MSP.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Business phone number of the supplier\'s primary program contact for operational communications and escalation.']
  right_to_audit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier\'s MSA or IC compliance agreement includes a right-to-audit clause allowing the MSP or client to audit the supplier\'s worker classification practices, payroll records, and compliance documentation.']
  tax_classification varchar [note: 'IRS tax classification governing how workers supplied by this vendor are compensated and taxed. Determines payroll tax obligations, worker classification risk, and billing structure. W-2 = employer-employee; 1099 = independent contractor; Corp-to-Corp (C2C) = business-to-business engagement.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Doing-business-as (DBA) or trade name used in day-to-day operations, marketing, and client-facing communications when different from the legal entity name.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the supplier record. Used for change data capture (CDC), data freshness monitoring, and audit trail compliance.']
  vms_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Enrollment status of the supplier within the Beeline Vendor Management System (VMS). Indicates whether the supplier is actively enrolled and eligible to participate in VMS-managed requisitions and rate card agreements.']
  vms_supplier_code varchar [note: 'Supplier identifier assigned by the Beeline Vendor Management System (VMS) at enrollment. Used for cross-system reconciliation between Beeline VMS, Bullhorn ATS/CRM, and Oracle NetSuite ERP.']

  Note: 'Master record for all third-party staffing suppliers, subcontractors, independent contractor (IC) suppliers, and service providers participating in MSP/VMS-managed contingent workforce programs. Captures vendor identity, legal entity details, business type (staffing agency, IC supplier, SOW provider, subcontractor), tax classification (W-2, 1099, Corp-to-Corp), EIN, DUNS number, NAICS code, diversity certifications (MBE, WBE, SDVOB), primary contact, headquarters address, operational regions, onboarding status, and Beeline VMS enrollment status. For IC supplier subtypes, additionally captures IRS worker classification determination, IC compliance program enrollment status, IC compliance vendor (e.g., MBO Partners, Shiftpixy), indemnification agreement status, right-to-audit clause, insurance minimums, IC risk score, and business structure (sole prop, LLC, S-Corp, C-Corp). SSOT for ALL vendor identity across all programs — all vendor types including staffing agencies, IC suppliers, SOW providers, and subcontractors are unified in this single master record. The ic_supplier subtype is distinguished by business_type and carries additional IC-specific compliance attributes within this same entity to avoid data divergence across staffing agency and IC supplier workflows.'
}

Table vendor.tier_classification {
  tier_classification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each tier classification definition or tier review history record within the vendor MSP/VMS program. Serves as the primary key for this table.']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MSP/VMS-managed contingent workforce program under which this tier classification applies. A vendor may hold different tier levels across different client programs.']
  reviewed_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Identifies internal vendor management analyst who conducted tier review and scorecard evaluation. Required for audit trail, governance accountability, and tier change approval workflows in supplier pe']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor (staffing supplier or subcontractor) whose tier classification is being defined or reviewed. Links to the vendor master record in the vendor domain.']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Tier status is contractually defined in vendor agreements and impacts rate schedules, allocation percentages, and submission priority. Business needs to link tier to governing agreement for tier chang']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status of the tier review decision. \'pending\' = awaiting approver action; \'approved\' = decision ratified; \'rejected\' = decision overturned; \'escalated\' = referred to senior governance. Drives the Beeline VMS tier activation workflow.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the tier review decision was formally approved by the designated authority. Used for SLA compliance on review turnaround time and audit trail.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has an active compliance issue (e.g., expired insurance, failed background check, I-9 deficiency, E-Verify non-compliance) that is a factor in this tier classification or review. True = compliance issue present.']
  compliance_issue_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the type of compliance deficiency flagged for this vendor at the time of tier review (e.g., insurance_lapse, background_check_failure, i9_deficiency, everify_non_compliance, license_expiry). Populated when compliance_flag is True. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: insurance_lapse|background_check_failure|i9_deficiency|everify_non_compliance|license_expiry|drug_screen_failure|osha_violation — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this tier classification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'The final decision made by the review committee regarding the vendor\'s tier classification. \'upgraded\' = moved to a higher-preference tier; \'downgraded\' = moved to a lower-preference tier; \'maintained\' = no change; \'suspended\' = tier privileges temporarily halted; \'terminated\' = removed from program.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this tier classification definition or tier assignment becomes effective for the vendor within the program. For tier review records, this is the date the reclassification decision takes effect.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this tier classification definition or vendor tier assignment expires and requires renewal or review. Null indicates an open-ended tier with no scheduled expiration.']
  is_exclusive_supplier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has been designated as the exclusive supplier for a specific skill category, geography, or client within this program tier. True = exclusive supplier designation applies.']
  is_preferred_vendor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor holds preferred status within this tier, granting first-right-of-refusal on requisitions before other vendors at the same tier level. True = preferred vendor within tier.']
  max_fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum percentage of total program requisition fill volume that vendors at this tier level are permitted to fulfill. Used to enforce program diversity and prevent over-concentration with a single tier. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 60.00 = 60%).']
  min_scorecard_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum vendor performance scorecard score required to qualify for or maintain this tier level. Scores below this threshold trigger a tier review or automatic downgrade. Expressed as a numeric score (e.g., 75.00 out of 100).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the next scheduled tier review is due for this vendor within the program. Calculated based on the review_cycle_frequency and the last review date. Used to drive MSP governance calendar and automated review notifications.']
  previous_tier_code varchar [note: 'The tier code held by the vendor immediately prior to a reclassification event. Populated only for tier_review records. Enables full audit trail of tier movement history and trend analysis.']
  program_eligibility_rules varchar [note: 'Structured text or rule expression defining the criteria a vendor must meet to qualify for this tier level. May include minimum scorecard thresholds, compliance certification requirements, insurance minimums, and contract status conditions.']
  proposed_tier_code varchar [note: 'The tier code proposed for the vendor as a result of a review event. May differ from the final decision outcome if the review committee overrides the initial proposal. Populated only for tier_review records.']
  rationale_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the business rationale for the tier review decision, including key performance factors, compliance issues, or client feedback that influenced the outcome. Required for audit trail completeness.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether this record represents a tier definition (the tier level itself), a tier assignment (a vendor assigned to a tier), or a tier review event (a reclassification decision). Enables the table to serve as both reference and history.']
  review_committee_members varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of names or identifiers of individuals who participated in the tier review committee. Provides governance accountability and audit trail for the review decision.']
  review_cycle_frequency varchar [note: 'The scheduled frequency at which vendors assigned to this tier are reviewed for potential reclassification. Drives the automated review scheduling in the MSP governance calendar.']
  review_date date [note: 'The date on which the tier review committee convened and evaluated the vendor\'s tier classification. Populated only for tier_review records. Used for audit trail and review cycle compliance tracking.']
  review_trigger varchar [note: 'The business reason that initiated this tier review event. \'scheduled\' = routine review cycle; \'performance_based\' = scorecard threshold breach; \'compliance_failure\' = credentialing or regulatory non-compliance; \'client_request\' = client-initiated review; \'contract_renewal\' = triggered at MSA renewal.']
  scorecard_period_end date [note: 'The end date of the vendor performance scorecard period referenced in this tier review. Together with scorecard_period_start, defines the evaluation window for the tier decision.']
  scorecard_period_start date [note: 'The start date of the vendor performance scorecard period referenced in this tier review. Defines the evaluation window used to assess vendor performance metrics that informed the tier decision.']
  scorecard_score_at_review decimal(5,2) [note: 'The vendor\'s actual performance scorecard score at the time of the tier review, as referenced from the scorecard period. Provides the quantitative basis for the tier decision and is preserved for audit purposes.']
  sla_fill_rate_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target fill rate percentage that vendors at this tier are contractually expected to achieve. Expressed as a percentage of requisitions successfully filled (e.g., 85.00 = 85%). Used in SLA compliance monitoring and tier review scoring.']
  sla_quality_of_submission_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum Quality of Submission (QoS) score that vendors at this tier must maintain. QoS measures the percentage of submitted candidates who meet the job requirements and advance to interview or placement. Expressed as a percentage.']
  sla_time_to_submit_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The maximum number of hours within which a vendor at this tier must submit qualified candidates after receiving a requisition. This is the SLA requirement specific to this tier level, used to enforce vendor responsiveness in the VMS.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this tier classification record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-source reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  submission_priority_order varchar [note: 'The sequential order in which vendors at this tier level receive job requisition submissions from the MSP. Determines the cascade sequence when a higher-tier vendor does not fill a requisition within the SLA window.']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the tier level within the program (e.g., T1, T2, T3, T4). Used as the primary routing key in Beeline VMS vendor dispatch logic and rate card lookups.']
  tier_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the tier level, including eligibility criteria, expected performance standards, and program privileges associated with this tier. Used for program documentation and vendor communication.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the tier level as displayed in the VMS and MSP program documentation (e.g., Tier 1 Preferred, Tier 2 Approved, Tier 3 Conditional, Tier 4 Probationary). Drives vendor routing labels in Beeline VMS.']
  tier_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank indicating the priority order of this tier relative to other tiers within the same program. Lower values indicate higher preference (e.g., 1 = most preferred). Drives submission priority sequencing in Beeline VMS requisition routing.']
  tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this tier classification record. \'active\' indicates the tier definition is in use; \'inactive\' means retired; \'suspended\' means temporarily halted; \'pending_review\' means under evaluation for change.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this tier classification record was last modified. Used for change detection, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  vms_tier_code varchar [note: 'The native tier identifier assigned by the Beeline VMS system for this tier classification record. Used for system-of-record reconciliation and integration with Beeline VMS vendor routing and rate management modules.']

  Note: 'Reference and history table defining vendor tier levels and tracking tier reclassification events within MSP/VMS programs. Tier definitions capture tier code, tier name (e.g., Tier 1 Preferred, Tier 2 Approved, Tier 3 Conditional, Tier 4 Probationary), tier description, program eligibility rules, submission priority order, maximum fill-rate allocation percentage, SLA requirements per tier, and review cycle frequency.
