Tier review history captures review date, vendor ID, previous tier, proposed tier, review trigger (scheduled, performance-based, compliance failure, client request), scorecard period referenced, review committee members, decision outcome (upgraded, downgraded, maintained, suspended), effective date of tier change, rationale notes, and approval status. Drives vendor routing logic in Beeline VMS and provides full audit trail for tier movement decisions.'
}

Table vendor.vms_enrollment {
  vms_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each vendor VMS program enrollment record. One record per vendor per VMS program. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a long-running supplier-organization-level enrollment and participation binding within a VMS-managed contingent workforce program.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account that owns or sponsors the VMS program in which the vendor is enrolled. Supports multi-client MSP program management.']
  client_program_id varchar [note: 'The client-assigned or VMS-assigned identifier for the specific contingent workforce program, as referenced in client-facing documentation, SOW agreements, and MSP reporting.']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: VMS enrollment requires specific credentialing packages for program participation. Critical for vendor onboarding requirements, compliance verification, program eligibility determination, and enrollme']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor (staffing supplier, subcontractor, or service provider) being enrolled in the VMS program. Links to the vendor master record.']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: VMS enrollment is formalized through a vendor agreement. Business needs to link enrollment record to the executed agreement for validating enrollment terms, tracking agreement-driven onboarding tasks,']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the VMS contingent workforce program in which the vendor is being enrolled. Links to the client VMS program record.']
  approved_job_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or free-text description of the job categories or labor categories for which the vendor is approved to submit candidates within this VMS program (e.g., \'IT, Engineering, Administrative\'). Governs which requisitions the vendor may respond to.']
  approved_work_locations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or free-text description of geographic locations or client site identifiers where the vendor is approved to place workers under this VMS program enrollment. Restricts vendor activity to authorized geographies.']
  assigned_reviewer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the MSP program staff member or VMS administrator assigned to review and manage this vendor\'s enrollment and onboarding workflow tasks.']
  blocking_task_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any onboarding task is currently flagged as blocking, meaning the vendor cannot achieve full program activation until the blocking task is resolved. True = one or more blocking tasks outstanding; False = no blocking tasks.']
  compliance_attestation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the vendor\'s completion of required compliance attestations within the VMS onboarding workflow, including acknowledgment of program rules, EEOC/OFCCP policies, co-employment guidelines, and data security requirements.']
  contract_execution_status varchar [note: 'Status of the vendor\'s enrollment contract execution within the DocuSign CLM workflow. Executed = fully signed by all parties; Pending_Signature = awaiting one or more signatures; Under_Review = legal or compliance review in progress; Expired = contract term has ended.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this VMS enrollment record was first created in the system. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for MASTER_AGREEMENT role. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  diversity_certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiry date of the vendor\'s diversity or small business certification. Used to trigger renewal reminders and ensure diversity spend reporting accuracy. Null if no diversity certification is held.']
  diversity_certification_type varchar [note: 'Type of diversity or small business certification held by the vendor (e.g., MBE, WBE, SDVOSB, HUBZone, 8(a)). Relevant for client diversity spend reporting and OFCCP compliance. Null if vendor holds no diversity certification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MBE|WBE|SDVOSB|HUBZone|8a|DVBE|SBE|none — promote to reference product]']
  document_submission_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the vendor\'s required document submission task within the VMS onboarding workflow. Documents typically include W-9, certificates of insurance, business licenses, and diversity certifications.']
  electronic_onboarding_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has completed all required electronic onboarding steps within the VMS portal, including document submission, system training, and compliance attestation. True = fully completed; False = incomplete or in progress.']
  enrollment_agreement_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the executed enrollment agreement between the vendor and the MSP/client program. Links to the DocuSign CLM contract record for audit and compliance purposes.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor was formally enrolled or first submitted for enrollment in the VMS program. Represents the start of the supplier-organization-level onboarding lifecycle. Satisfies EFFECTIVE_FROM category for MASTER_AGREEMENT role.']
  enrollment_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor\'s enrollment in the VMS program expires or is scheduled for renewal review. Null for open-ended enrollments. Satisfies EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category for MASTER_AGREEMENT role.']
  enrollment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the MSP program administrator or VMS coordinator regarding special conditions, exceptions, escalations, or context relevant to this vendor\'s enrollment record.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this enrollment record, assigned by the VMS system or MSP program administrator. Used in correspondence, audit trails, and supplier communications. Satisfies BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for MASTER_AGREEMENT role.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the vendor\'s participation in the VMS program. Active = fully enrolled and eligible to submit workers; Pending = enrollment initiated but not yet complete; Under_Review = compliance or performance issue under investigation; Suspended = temporarily barred from submitting; Terminated = permanently removed from program. Satisfies LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for MASTER_AGREEMENT role.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor\'s primary insurance coverage (e.g., general liability, workers compensation) expires. Used to trigger renewal reminders and compliance holds within the VMS program.']
  insurance_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the vendor\'s required insurance documentation verification within the VMS onboarding workflow. Verified = all required certificates of insurance confirmed; Pending = documents submitted but not yet reviewed; Expired = previously verified but coverage has lapsed; Waived = requirement waived by program administrator.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this VMS enrollment record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with Beeline VMS, and audit compliance.']
  msa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the Master Service Agreement (MSA) governing the vendor\'s participation in the program. The MSA establishes the overarching legal and commercial terms under which the vendor operates.']
  msp_sponsor varchar [note: 'Name of the Managed Service Provider (MSP) organization that sponsors and manages this vendor\'s enrollment in the VMS program. Identifies the MSP entity responsible for vendor oversight and program compliance.']
  nda_executed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) has been fully executed between the vendor and the client or MSP as part of the program enrollment requirements. True = NDA executed; False = not yet executed.']
  non_compete_executed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a non-compete or non-solicitation agreement has been fully executed between the vendor and the client or MSP as part of the program enrollment. True = executed; False = not executed.']
  onboarding_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the vendor must complete all onboarding tasks to achieve full program activation. Used for SLA tracking and escalation management within the MSP program.']
  onboarding_task_count varchar [note: 'Total number of onboarding workflow tasks defined for this vendor\'s enrollment in the VMS program. Used to calculate completion percentage and track overall onboarding progress.']
  onboarding_tasks_completed varchar [note: 'Number of onboarding workflow tasks that have been completed for this vendor\'s enrollment. Compared against onboarding_task_count to determine overall completion progress.']
  portal_credentials_status varchar [note: 'Status of the vendor\'s VMS portal access credentials. Provisioned = active login credentials issued; Pending = credentials requested but not yet issued; Revoked = access removed due to termination or suspension; Expired = credentials have lapsed and require renewal.']
  program_activation_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor achieved full program activation status, meaning all onboarding tasks were completed and the vendor was cleared to submit workers or SOW resources into the program.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor\'s enrollment was suspended. Null if the vendor has never been suspended. Used for compliance tracking and reinstatement workflows.']
  system_access_status varchar [note: 'Status of the vendor\'s VMS system access provisioning task, distinct from portal credentials status. Tracks whether IT/system access provisioning workflow has been completed for the vendor organization\'s designated users.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor\'s enrollment was permanently terminated from the VMS program. Null if the vendor is still active or suspended. Required for offboarding and compliance audit trails.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the termination of the vendor\'s enrollment. Performance = removed due to poor performance metrics; Compliance_Failure = removed due to compliance violations; Voluntary_Exit = vendor chose to exit the program; Program_Restructure = program eliminated or restructured; Contract_Expiry = enrollment agreement expired without renewal.']
  training_completion_status varchar [note: 'Status of the vendor\'s completion of required VMS portal and program training modules. Training typically covers system usage, submission workflows, timesheet approval processes, and compliance requirements.']
  vendor_tier varchar [note: 'Tier classification of the vendor within the VMS program, reflecting performance standing and preferred supplier status. Preferred = top-tier, first-look suppliers; Approved = standard program participants; Conditional = approved with conditions or restrictions; Probationary = under performance improvement plan.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor\'s business model within the program. Staffing_Agency = traditional temp/perm staffing; IC_Supplier = Independent Contractor (IC) supplier; SOW_Vendor = Statement of Work (SOW) project vendor; RPO_Provider = Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO) partner; MSP_Subvendor = subvendor operating under the MSP umbrella.']
  w9_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a valid IRS Form W-9 (Request for Taxpayer Identification Number and Certification) has been received and is on file for this vendor. Required for tax reporting and 1099 issuance. True = W-9 on file; False = not on file.']

  Note: 'Tracks each vendor\'s enrollment, participation record, and onboarding task completion within the Beeline VMS contingent workforce program. Captures enrollment date, program name, client program ID, enrollment status (active, suspended, terminated, pending), approved job categories, approved work locations, VMS portal credentials status, electronic onboarding completion flag, MSP sponsor, enrollment agreement reference, and vendor onboarding workflow tasks (document submission, insurance verification, system access provisioning, training completion, contract execution, compliance attestation) with task status, due dates, completion dates, assigned reviewers, and blocking flags. One record per vendor per VMS program. Supports multi-client MSP program management and tracks the full supplier-organization-level onboarding lifecycle from initial enrollment through full program activation. Distinct from worker onboarding (owned by the onboarding domain) — this is supplier-organization-level enrollment and onboarding.'
}

Table vendor.vendor_rate_card {
  vendor_rate_card_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a vendor rate card record within the VMS/MSP program. Primary key for this entity.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Vendor rate cards map to contract rate schedules. Business needs to reconcile vendor-submitted rates against contractually approved schedules for rate exception approvals, markup validation, and ensur']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staffing supplier or subcontractor for whom this rate card is defined. Links to the vendor master record.']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Vendor Management System (VMS) or Managed Service Provider (MSP) program under which this rate card is negotiated and enforced.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this rate card was formally approved by the authorized approver. Establishes the governance timestamp for rate card authorization and supports compliance audit trails.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the MSP program manager, procurement officer, or client representative who formally approved this rate card. Supports audit trail and governance requirements for rate card authorization.']
  burden_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'The total employer cost rate applied on top of the base pay rate to cover mandatory employer taxes and insurance costs including FICA, FUTA, SUTA, Workers Compensation (Workers Comp), and benefits. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.18 = 18%). Used in gross margin and profitability analysis.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which this rate card applies (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Supports international MSP programs and multi-country vendor rate management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rate card record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 compliance requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all rates on this rate card are denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency vendor programs for international MSP engagements.']
  dt_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The negotiated hourly rate charged to the client for double time (DT) hours, typically applicable for holidays, Sundays, or hours beyond a defined daily/weekly threshold per the client MSA or state labor law. Expressed in the currency defined by the currency_code field.']
  dt_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The negotiated hourly rate paid to the worker or vendor for double time (DT) hours. Expressed in the currency defined by the currency_code field. Applicable in states or contracts requiring double-time compensation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this rate card becomes binding and applicable for worker placements and billing. Rates may not be applied to assignments prior to this date.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this rate card ceases to be valid. After this date, a new rate agreement or rate card version must be executed. Nullable for open-ended rate cards.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'The Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) exemption status for workers placed under this rate card. \'exempt\' workers are not entitled to overtime pay; \'non_exempt\' workers must receive overtime at 1.5x the regular rate for hours over 40 per week. Critical for OT rate applicability.']
  geographic_market varchar [note: 'The geographic market or region for which this rate card applies (e.g., \'Northeast US\', \'California\', \'Chicago Metro\', \'National\'). Rates vary by market due to cost-of-living, prevailing wage laws, and local labor market conditions. Supports market rate comparison and geographic spread analysis.']
  is_prevailing_wage boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate card is subject to prevailing wage requirements under the Davis-Bacon Act, Service Contract Act (SCA), or state prevailing wage laws. When True, the pay rate must meet or exceed the applicable prevailing wage determination.']
  is_union_rate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate card applies to unionized workers governed by a Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA). When True, the rates must comply with the applicable CBA wage scales and benefit requirements.']
  job_category varchar [note: 'The broad occupational category for which this rate card applies (e.g., Information Technology, Light Industrial, Clerical, Professional, Healthcare, Engineering, Finance). Aligns with VMS job category taxonomy used in Beeline for rate benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: information_technology|light_industrial|clerical|professional|healthcare|engineering|finance|legal|creative|scientific — promote to reference product]']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The specific job title or role designation associated with this rate card entry (e.g., \'Java Developer\', \'Warehouse Associate\', \'Data Analyst\'). Provides granular role-level rate definition within a broader job category and skill level combination.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'The labor engagement classification that determines how the worker is engaged and billed. \'staff_augmentation\' covers standard contingent placements; \'sow_resource\' covers Statement of Work (SOW) deliverable-based engagements; \'independent_contractor\' covers IC/1099 supplier resources; \'temp_to_perm\' covers contract-to-hire arrangements; \'direct_hire\' covers permanent placement; \'managed_service\' covers MSP-managed resources.']
  markup_percentage decimal(6,4) [note: 'The negotiated markup percentage applied over the pay rate to derive the bill rate, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.35 = 35%). Represents the vendor\'s margin and covers burden costs including FICA, FUTA, SUTA, Workers Compensation, and administrative overhead. Used in spread analysis and vendor benchmarking.']
  max_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The ceiling bill rate above which the vendor may not submit candidates for this job category and skill level within the VMS program. Enforces rate card compliance and controls program spend in the Beeline VMS.']
  min_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The floor bill rate below which the vendor may not submit candidates for this job category and skill level within the VMS program. Enforces rate card compliance and prevents below-floor rate submissions in the Beeline VMS.']
  negotiation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing key points, concessions, or conditions from the rate card negotiation process. Confidential business information used by MSP program managers and procurement teams for future rate renegotiations.']
  ot_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The negotiated hourly rate charged to the client for overtime (OT) hours worked beyond the standard threshold (typically 40 hours/week per FLSA). Expressed in the currency defined by the currency_code field.']
  ot_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The negotiated hourly rate paid to the worker or vendor for overtime (OT) hours. Must comply with FLSA overtime requirements (minimum 1.5x regular pay rate). Expressed in the currency defined by the currency_code field.']
  per_diem_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The daily allowance paid to the worker for meals, lodging, and incidental expenses when working away from their tax home. Expressed in the currency defined by the currency_code field. Must comply with IRS per diem guidelines to maintain tax-free status.']
  rate_card_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about the scope, applicability, or special conditions of this rate card (e.g., specific client programs, project types, or negotiation notes).']
  rate_card_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for this rate card (e.g., \'FY2025 IT Staff Aug - Northeast - Senior\'). Used in VMS program management, vendor communications, and reporting dashboards for easy identification.']
  rate_card_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for this rate card, used in vendor communications, contract references, and VMS system cross-referencing. Typically prefixed with \'RC-\' followed by a program-specific code.']
  rate_card_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rate card. \'draft\' indicates pending negotiation; \'active\' indicates currently in effect; \'expired\' indicates past the expiration date; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a newer version; \'suspended\' indicates temporarily inactive.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'The unit basis on which the bill rate and pay rate are expressed. \'hourly\' is standard for contingent staffing; \'daily\' and \'weekly\' apply to certain professional and light industrial engagements; \'monthly\' applies to long-term retained resources; \'fixed_fee\' applies to SOW deliverable-based engagements.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'The experience and seniority tier of the worker for which this rate applies. Used to differentiate bill rates and pay rates within the same job category. Aligns with VMS skill level taxonomy for rate benchmarking and spread analysis.']
  soc_code varchar [note: 'The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) code associated with the job title on this rate card. Enables labor market benchmarking, prevailing wage compliance, and regulatory reporting alignment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this rate card record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across the technology stack. \'beeline\' is the primary source for VMS-managed rate cards.']
  source_system_rate_card_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this rate card record in the originating source system (e.g., Beeline VMS rate card ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  standard_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The negotiated hourly rate charged to the client for a worker at this job category and skill level during standard (straight-time) hours. Expressed in the currency defined by the currency_code field. This is the primary rate used for client invoicing.']
  standard_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The negotiated hourly rate paid to the worker or vendor for standard (straight-time) hours. Expressed in the currency defined by the currency_code field. Used in spread analysis and gross margin calculations.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code (ISO 3166-2:US) for the primary state jurisdiction of this rate card. Drives state-specific compliance requirements including SUTA rates, Workers Compensation classifications, and state minimum wage compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rate card record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks the most recent change for audit trail and data freshness monitoring.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this rate card (e.g., v1.0, v2.1), enabling tracking of rate card revisions over time and supporting historical rate benchmarking and audit trails.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'The National Council on Compensation Insurance (NCCI) or state-specific Workers Compensation classification code associated with the job category on this rate card. Determines the Workers Comp insurance premium rate applied to the pay rate.']

  Note: 'Defines the standard bill rate and pay rate schedule negotiated with a vendor for specific job categories, skill levels, and labor classifications within a VMS program or MSP engagement. Captures vendor ID, job category, labor category (e.g., IT, light industrial, clerical, professional), skill level (e.g., junior, mid, senior, lead), standard bill rate, standard pay rate, OT bill rate, OT pay rate, DT bill rate, DT pay rate, per diem rate, currency, effective date, expiration date, geographic market, and rate card version. Serves as the rate template from which binding rate_agreement records are executed. Supports rate benchmarking, spread analysis, and market rate comparison across the vendor supply chain.'
}

Table vendor.rate_agreement {
  rate_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying this executed rate agreement record in the vendor domain.']
  client_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate card template from which this executed agreement was derived. Distinct from the agreement itself — the rate card is the template; this record is the binding executed instrument.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Rate agreements in staffing are executed under MSAs. Business needs to trace rate terms back to governing master agreement for compliance audits, dispute resolution, and validating rate agreement term']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Rate agreements reference specific contract rate schedules. Business needs to link to the detailed schedule for billing validation, pay rate verification, and ensuring rate agreement terms match the a']
  docusign_envelope_id varchar [note: 'The DocuSign CLM envelope identifier for the executed rate agreement document, enabling direct retrieval of the signed contract from the DocuSign document repository.']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Vendor Management System (VMS) client program under which this rate agreement is executed.']
  msp_signatory_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Identifies internal employee (procurement director/VP) who signed rate agreement on behalf of MSP. Required for contract authority validation, legal compliance, and signature audit trails in vendor co']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor supplier party that is a signatory to this rate agreement.']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the rate agreement, typically including vendor name, client program, and engagement period (e.g., \'Acme Staffing — TechCorp IT Program FY2025\').']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this executed rate agreement, used for cross-system reconciliation between Beeline VMS, DocuSign CLM, and Oracle NetSuite ERP.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rate agreement. Controls whether rates under this agreement may be applied to active placements and job orders.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate agreement by engagement model. Determines applicable rate structures and compliance obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: msp_vendor|sow_vendor|ic_supplier|direct_placement|temp_to_perm|rpo_vendor — promote to reference product]']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate agreement automatically renews upon expiration unless either party provides written notice of termination within the notice period.']
  burden_rate_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employer burden rate (FICA, FUTA, SUTA, Workers Compensation, benefits) is embedded within the agreed markup percentage or billed separately as a pass-through cost.']
  clm_document_reference varchar [note: 'The internal DocuSign CLM document reference number or URL for the executed rate agreement, used for contract audit trail and legal record retention.']
  conversion_fee_percentage decimal(6,4) [note: 'The agreed conversion fee percentage charged when a contingent worker placed under this agreement is converted to a permanent (FTE) hire by the client (Temp-to-Perm or Contract-to-Hire conversion).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rate agreement record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts in this rate agreement (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency MSP programs.']
  dt_markup_percentage decimal(6,4) [note: 'Negotiated markup percentage applied to double time (DT) hours billed under this agreement, applicable in states or contracts requiring double-time pay.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the negotiated rates and terms of this agreement become binding and applicable to placements and job orders.']
  escalation_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate agreement contains a rate escalation clause permitting periodic rate adjustments (e.g., annual CPI-linked increases) after the rate lock period expires.']
  escalation_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which rate escalations may be applied under the escalation clause. Null if escalation_clause is False.']
  escalation_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The maximum allowable rate escalation percentage per escalation period as defined in the escalation clause. Null if escalation_clause is False. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0300 = 3%).']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which all required signatories completed execution of the rate agreement, making it legally binding. Captured from DocuSign CLM completion event.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this rate agreement expires and rates are no longer binding. Null indicates an open-ended agreement. Triggers renewal workflow when approaching.']
  governing_law_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code indicating the jurisdiction whose laws govern interpretation and enforcement of this rate agreement.']
  markup_percentage decimal(6,4) [note: 'The agreed markup percentage applied over the pay rate to derive the bill rate for contingent workers placed under this agreement. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.4500 = 45%). Core financial term in staffing: Markup % = (Bill Rate - Pay Rate) / Pay Rate.']
  max_exception_markup_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The maximum markup percentage permitted for approved rate exceptions under this agreement. Null if rate_exception_allowed is False.']
  msp_fee_percentage decimal(6,4) [note: 'The MSP program management fee percentage charged to the vendor supplier under this agreement, deducted from the bill rate or invoiced separately depending on program structure.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing special terms, negotiation context, approved exceptions, or operational notes associated with this rate agreement that are not captured in structured fields.']
  ot_markup_percentage decimal(6,4) [note: 'Negotiated markup percentage applied to overtime (OT) hours billed under this agreement. May differ from the standard markup percentage per FLSA overtime billing conventions.']
  per_diem_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether per diem allowances for travel, lodging, and meals are included within the rate structure of this agreement or handled as separate expense reimbursements.']
  program_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational scope of this rate agreement — whether rates apply nationally, regionally, to a specific site, or globally across the client\'s enterprise.']
  rate_exception_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether approved rate exceptions above the negotiated rate card are permitted under this agreement for specialized skill sets or market-driven rate adjustments.']
  rate_lock_end_date date [note: 'End date of the rate lock period. After this date, rates may be renegotiated per the escalation clause terms defined in this agreement.']
  rate_lock_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the rate lock period during which negotiated bill rates and pay rates cannot be unilaterally modified by either party.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to expiration that written notice must be provided to prevent auto-renewal or to initiate renegotiation of terms.']
  spread_ceiling_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum dollar spread (Bill Rate minus Pay Rate) permitted under this agreement, capping the MSP/staffing firm\'s margin to protect vendor competitiveness. Expressed in USD.']
  spread_floor_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The minimum dollar spread (Bill Rate minus Pay Rate) guaranteed under this agreement, ensuring the MSP/staffing firm retains a minimum gross margin regardless of pay rate fluctuations. Expressed in USD.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days of advance written notice required by either party to terminate this rate agreement prior to its expiration date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rate agreement record was most recently modified, supporting change tracking and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  vendor_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized signatory representing the vendor supplier who executed this rate agreement.']
  vendor_tier varchar [note: 'The vendor tier classification at the time this agreement was executed, which may influence markup rates, submission priority, and program access rights.']
  vms_fee_percentage decimal(6,4) [note: 'The VMS technology platform fee percentage (e.g., Beeline transaction fee) applicable to placements under this agreement, distinct from the MSP management fee.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'The worker classification type covered by this rate agreement. Determines tax treatment, co-employment risk, and applicable compliance obligations (W-2, 1099 IC, Corp-to-Corp, or Employer of Record).']

  Note: 'Captures the formally executed rate agreement between the MSP/staffing firm and a vendor supplier for a specific client program or engagement period. Tracks agreement reference number, vendor ID, client program, negotiated markup percentage, spread floor, rate lock period, rate escalation clauses, approved rate exceptions, signatory names, execution date, effective start date, expiration date, and DocuSign CLM document reference. Distinct from the rate card template — this is the binding executed agreement.'
}

Table vendor.sow_agreement {
  sow_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a Statement of Work (SOW) agreement record within the vendor program. Primary key for this entity.']
  beeline_sow_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to this SOW agreement within the Beeline Vendor Management System (VMS). Enables reconciliation between the vendor domain data product and the operational VMS platform.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for whom the SOW project or deliverable is being executed. Links to the client account master record.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: SOWs in staffing are governed by MSAs. Business needs to validate SOW terms (liability, payment, termination) against parent MSA provisions and ensure SOW doesn\'t contradict MSA terms during legal rev']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: SOW agreements for project-based work specify required credentialing packages for worker eligibility. Essential for SOW compliance requirements, worker eligibility determination, and project onboardin']
  docusign_envelope_id varchar [note: 'Unique envelope identifier assigned by DocuSign CLM when the SOW agreement was routed for electronic signature. Enables direct cross-reference to the executed contract document in the CLM system.']
  program_manager_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Links SOW to internal program manager responsible for project oversight, milestone tracking, and vendor deliverable acceptance. Core to project-based staffing operations and SOW lifecycle management.']
  sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: SOWs may have attached SLAs (delivery timelines, quality metrics). Business needs to link SOW to its governing SLA for performance measurement, breach determination, and validating SOW deliverables ag']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor (staffing supplier or subcontractor) who is party to this SOW agreement. Links to the vendor master record in the vendor domain.']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Beeline VMS contingent workforce program under which this SOW agreement is managed and tracked.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Running count of formal amendments executed against this SOW agreement. Tracks the number of change orders or modifications to scope, value, or timeline since original execution.']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOW agreement contains an automatic renewal clause that extends the agreement beyond the original end date without requiring a new execution. Triggers renewal notification workflows.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the vendor submits invoices for work performed under this SOW. Determines the invoicing cadence in Salesforce Revenue Cloud and Oracle NetSuite ERP.']
  client_approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the client-side authorized approver or sponsor who approved and signed this SOW agreement. Used for audit trail and contract governance.']
  co_employment_risk_classification varchar [note: 'Assessment of co-employment risk associated with this SOW engagement. High-risk SOWs may involve workers who could be deemed joint employees of the client, triggering FLSA, EEOC, or IRS worker classification concerns. Supports compliance monitoring.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SOW agreement record was first created in the system. Represents the audit trail creation event for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this SOW agreement (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency vendor programs.']
  deliverables_summary varchar [note: 'Summarized list or description of the specific outputs, work products, or outcomes the vendor is contractually obligated to deliver under this SOW. Supports deliverable-based program management.']
  docusign_clm_reference varchar [note: 'Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) reference number or document ID assigned by DocuSign CLM for the executed SOW agreement. Used for legal document retrieval and audit trail.']
  end_date date [note: 'The contractually agreed date on which the SOW engagement concludes and all deliverables must be completed. Represents the effective-until date of the agreement. Nullable for open-ended engagements.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which the SOW agreement was formally signed and executed by all parties via DocuSign CLM. Marks the transition from draft to executed status.']
  ic_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Independent Contractor (IC) compliance review has been completed for this SOW engagement, confirming the vendor\'s workers meet IRS and state-level IC classification criteria.']
  insurance_certificate_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is required to provide a certificate of insurance (COI) as a condition of this SOW. Typically required for general liability, workers compensation, and professional liability coverage.']
  insurance_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor\'s certificate of insurance was verified and confirmed as meeting the requirements specified in this SOW. Null if insurance verification is not required or not yet completed.']
  latest_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal amendment executed against this SOW agreement. Null if no amendments have been executed. Supports amendment history tracking.']
  milestone_count varchar [note: 'Total number of payment or delivery milestones defined in the SOW milestone schedule. Used for milestone-based pricing structures and progress tracking.']
  nda_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) is required as a condition of this SOW engagement. When true, the NDA must be executed before work commences.']
  non_compete_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a non-compete clause or agreement is required for workers engaged under this SOW. Relevant for sensitive client engagements involving proprietary processes or competitive intelligence.']
  not_to_exceed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum authorized spend ceiling for T&M or NTE-structured SOW agreements. Vendor billings may not exceed this threshold without a formal amendment. Null for fixed-price SOWs.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment terms governing when the client must remit payment to the vendor following invoice submission. Drives accounts payable scheduling in Oracle NetSuite ERP.']
  pricing_structure varchar [note: 'Defines the commercial pricing model for the SOW engagement. Fixed Price (FP) means a set total cost regardless of effort; Time and Materials (T&M) means billing based on hours and expenses; Milestone-Based ties payments to deliverable completion; Not-to-Exceed (NTE) caps total spend.']
  project_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title or name of the project or engagement described in this Statement of Work. Used in reporting, dashboards, and client communications.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to the SOW end date by which either party must provide written notice of intent not to renew, if auto-renewal is enabled. Drives contract expiry alerting.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the project scope, objectives, and work to be performed under this SOW. Captures the high-level summary of deliverables and responsibilities as defined in the executed agreement.']
  sow_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the SOW agreement as referenced in DocuSign CLM, Beeline VMS, and client-facing communications. Used for cross-system reconciliation.']
  sow_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SOW agreement. Tracks progression from initial drafting through execution, active delivery, and final closure or early termination.']
  sow_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SOW engagement type. Project-Based SOWs have defined scope and timeline; Deliverable-Based SOWs are tied to specific outputs; Managed Service SOWs involve ongoing operational management; Consulting SOWs cover advisory services; Staffing SOWs are distinct from staff augmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: project_based|deliverable_based|managed_service|consulting|staffing_sow — promote to reference product]']
  start_date date [note: 'The contractually agreed date on which the SOW engagement commences and the vendor begins work or deliverable execution. Represents the effective-from date of the agreement.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the SOW agreement was early-terminated by either party prior to the contractual end date. Null if the SOW was not terminated early. Triggers final billing and closeout procedures.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the SOW agreement was terminated prior to its scheduled end date. Used for vendor performance analysis and program management reporting.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the SOW agreement as executed, representing the maximum authorized spend or fixed price for the full scope of work. Expressed in USD.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this SOW agreement record. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit compliance requirements.']
  vendor_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the authorized representative from the vendor organization who executed this SOW agreement. Required for contract validity and legal enforceability.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'Tax and employment classification of workers engaged under this SOW. W-2 indicates employees on payroll; 1099 IC indicates Independent Contractors; Corp-to-Corp indicates vendor entity engagement; EOR indicates Employer of Record arrangement. Critical for IRS compliance and co-employment risk management.']

  Note: 'Manages Statement of Work (SOW) agreements executed with vendors for project-based or deliverable-based engagements within the vendor program. Captures SOW number, vendor ID, client ID, project title, scope of work description, deliverables list, fixed price or T&M structure, total contract value, milestone schedule, start date, end date, amendment history, SOW status (draft, executed, active, closed, terminated), DocuSign CLM reference, and co-employment risk classification. Supports SOW vendor program management distinct from staff augmentation. Note: This product owns the vendor-side SOW relationship and program enrollment aspects; the contract domain owns the commercial contract lifecycle and legal terms of the SOW.'
}

Table vendor.performance_scorecard {
  performance_scorecard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for performance_scorecard']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for which this vendor performance is being evaluated. Enables client-specific vendor scorecarding within multi-client MSP programs.']
  evaluator_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Identifies internal vendor management analyst who compiled and finalized scorecard. Essential for scorecard credibility, dispute resolution, tier change recommendations, and performance management acc']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MSP/VMS contingent workforce program under which this vendor is being evaluated. Supports multi-program vendor performance tracking.']
  sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: Scorecards measure SLA compliance (time-to-fill, submittal quality, fill rate). Business needs to link scorecard metrics to the SLA definitions they measure against for breach determination, penalty c']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staffing supplier or subcontractor being evaluated in this scorecard. Links to the vendor master record in the Beeline VMS vendor registry.']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Scorecards measure vendor performance against SLAs and terms defined in vendor agreements. Business needs this link for breach determination, penalty calculation, tier classification changes, and agre']
  active_assignment_count varchar [note: 'Number of active worker assignments managed by this vendor at the end of the evaluation period. Reflects the vendor\'s current headcount contribution to the program.']
  assignment_completion_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of assignments placed by this vendor that were completed through the scheduled end date during the evaluation period. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9200 = 92%). Complement of the fall-off rate.']
  client_satisfaction_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Average client satisfaction rating for this vendor during the evaluation period, collected via structured client feedback surveys. Expressed on a 1.00–5.00 scale. Distinct from NPS — captures direct satisfaction rather than likelihood to recommend.']
  compliance_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of vendor-placed workers who met all required compliance and credentialing requirements (I-9, background checks, drug screens, certifications) on time during the evaluation period. Expressed as a decimal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this scorecard record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field supporting data lineage and SOC 2 Type II compliance requirements.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the vendor\'s stated reason for disputing the scorecard findings. Populated when scorecard_status is \'disputed\'. Supports the formal dispute resolution process.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the scorecard dispute was formally resolved and the scorecard status was updated to finalized or re-published. Supports dispute lifecycle tracking.']
  dispute_submitted_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the vendor formally submitted a dispute against this scorecard. Populated when scorecard_status is \'disputed\'. Used to track dispute resolution SLA compliance.']
  evaluation_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the performance measurement window covered by this scorecard. Defines the inclusive end boundary for all KPI calculations.']
  evaluation_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the performance measurement window covered by this scorecard. Defines the inclusive start boundary for all KPI calculations.']
  evaluation_period_type varchar [note: 'Frequency cadence of the scorecard evaluation cycle. Determines how often vendor KPIs are formally assessed and published within the MSP/VMS program.']
  evaluator_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text qualitative commentary entered by the MSP program manager or evaluator summarizing vendor performance highlights, concerns, and context for the evaluation period. Supports narrative reporting alongside quantitative KPIs.']
  fall_off_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of placements made by this vendor that ended prematurely (before the scheduled assignment end date) during the evaluation period. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 = 5%). A key indicator of placement quality and candidate fit.']
  finalized_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the scorecard was finalized and locked, either after the dispute window expired without challenge or after dispute resolution was completed. Marks the record as immutable for historical reporting.']
  improvement_plan_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the vendor must submit their Performance Improvement Plan in response to a below-threshold scorecard. Populated when improvement_plan_required is True.']
  improvement_plan_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scorecard evaluation has triggered a requirement for the vendor to submit a formal Performance Improvement Plan (PIP). True when overall score falls below the program-defined minimum threshold.']
  interview_to_offer_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of candidate interviews that resulted in a job offer during the evaluation period. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.5000 = 50%). Indicates vendor effectiveness in presenting placeable candidates.']
  job_order_fill_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of job orders assigned to this vendor that were successfully filled during the evaluation period. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8500 = 85%). Measures vendor responsiveness and capacity to fulfill requisitions.']
  nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Net Promoter Score reflecting client satisfaction with this vendor\'s performance during the evaluation period. Ranges from -100 to +100. Derived from client survey responses collected through the MSP program.']
  offer_to_start_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of job offers extended that resulted in a candidate starting the assignment during the evaluation period. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9000 = 90%). Measures vendor reliability in converting offers to active placements.']
  on_time_submittal_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of candidate submittals delivered by this vendor within the program-defined SLA submittal window during the evaluation period. Expressed as a decimal. Measures adherence to program SLA timelines.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite weighted performance score for the vendor across all KPI dimensions for the evaluation period. Expressed as a numeric value on a 0–100 scale. Drives tier reclassification and program allocation decisions.']
  overall_score_grade varchar [note: 'Letter grade derived from the overall performance score, providing a simplified qualitative summary for executive reporting and vendor communications. A=Excellent, B=Good, C=Satisfactory, D=Below Standard, F=Failing.']
  program_allocation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total program job order volume allocated to this vendor during the evaluation period. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 25.00 = 25%). Used to assess vendor share-of-wallet and inform future allocation decisions.']
  published_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the scorecard was formally published and made available to the vendor. Marks the transition from draft to published status and starts the vendor review/dispute window.']
  qos_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'KPI score measuring the quality of candidate submittals from this vendor — assessing how well submitted candidates match job order requirements. Scored on a 0–100 scale. A core MSP/VMS program metric.']
  recommended_tier varchar [note: 'The vendor tier classification recommended as a result of this scorecard evaluation. Populated only when tier_change_recommended is True. Subject to program manager approval before taking effect. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier_1|tier_2|tier_3|preferred|approved|probation — promote to reference product]']
  redeployment_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of workers placed by this vendor who were successfully redeployed to a new assignment upon completion of their prior assignment during the evaluation period. Expressed as a decimal. Measures vendor\'s ability to retain and redeploy talent.']
  scorecard_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the scorecard, used in vendor communications, dispute resolution, and program reporting. Format: SC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  scorecard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the vendor performance scorecard. Draft: under preparation; Published: shared with vendor; Disputed: vendor has raised a formal challenge; Finalized: accepted and locked; Archived: historical record.']
  sla_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score measuring the vendor\'s overall adherence to all program-defined SLA requirements during the evaluation period. Scored on a 0–100 scale. Encompasses submittal timeliness, onboarding compliance, and reporting obligations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this scorecard data originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Beeline VMS is the primary source for MSP/VMS program scorecards.']
  submittal_count varchar [note: 'Total number of candidate submittals made by this vendor during the evaluation period. Denominator for submittal-to-interview and QoS ratio calculations.']
  submittal_to_interview_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of candidate submittals that resulted in a client interview during the evaluation period. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.4000 = 40%). Measures the relevance and quality of vendor submissions.']
  tier_change_recommended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scorecard evaluation has triggered a recommendation to reclassify the vendor\'s tier designation. True if the overall score warrants a tier upgrade or downgrade per program thresholds.']
  ttf_avg_days decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average number of calendar days from job order receipt to first qualified submittal by this vendor during the evaluation period. Raw metric underlying the TTF score.']
  ttf_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'KPI score measuring the vendor\'s performance on Time to Fill — the elapsed time from job order receipt to candidate submittal or placement confirmation. Scored on a 0–100 scale relative to program SLA benchmarks.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this scorecard record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  vendor_tier varchar [note: 'Vendor tier designation at the time of this scorecard evaluation. Captures the tier in effect during the evaluation period and may trigger reclassification upon finalization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier_1|tier_2|tier_3|preferred|approved|probation — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Periodic vendor performance scorecard capturing quantitative and qualitative KPIs for each supplier within an MSP/VMS program. Tracks evaluation period, overall score, TTF (Time to Fill) score, QoS (Quality of Submission) score, submittal-to-interview ratio, interview-to-offer ratio, offer-to-start ratio, fall-off rate, assignment completion rate, compliance rate, NPS score, client satisfaction rating, redeployment rate, and scorecard status (draft, published, disputed, finalized). Drives tier reclassification and program allocation decisions.'
}

Table vendor.vendor_contact {
  vendor_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the vendor contact record. Primary key for the vendor_contact data product.']
  internal_relationship_owner_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Tracks internal employee (account manager/recruiter) who owns relationship with this vendor contact. Critical for communication routing, escalation paths, and account coverage planning in vendor relat']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor/supplier organization this contact represents. Links the contact to the parent vendor record in the vendor domain.']
  communication_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor contact has opted out of non-essential communications such as marketing, newsletters, or program announcements. Mandatory for GDPR/CCPA compliance.']
  contact_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or method through which this vendor contact record was created. Used for data lineage tracking and source system reconciliation.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor contact record. Drives whether the contact is included in active communication routing and program operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor contact record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record age analytics.']
  department varchar [note: 'The department or business unit within the vendor organization where this contact works (e.g., Recruiting, Operations, Finance, Compliance). Supports organizational mapping and routing.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address of the vendor contact. Used for MSP program communications, submittal notifications, compliance alerts, and billing correspondence.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which this vendor contact\'s relationship with the MSP/VMS program was terminated or deactivated. Null for currently active contacts. Used for offboarding tracking and access revocation.']
  external_contact_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for this vendor contact as recorded in the originating source system (e.g., Beeline VMS contact ID, Bullhorn contact ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data integration.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first (given) name of the vendor contact individual. Used for personalized communication and formal correspondence.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Full display name of the vendor contact (first + last name). Used in UI displays, reports, and communication templates within MSP program operations.']
  is_billing_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated to receive billing-related communications including invoices, payment remittances, and billing dispute notifications.']
  is_compliance_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated to receive compliance-related communications including I-9 verification notices, credentialing alerts, and regulatory audit requests.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this individual is the designated primary contact for the vendor organization within the MSP/VMS program. Only one contact per vendor should be flagged as primary at any given time.']
  is_vms_portal_user boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor contact has an active user account in the Beeline VMS portal for submitting candidates, managing timesheets, and accessing program data.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title of the vendor contact at their organization (e.g., Account Manager, Director of Staffing, VP of Operations). Used for relationship management and escalation routing.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which the MSP program team had a documented interaction with this vendor contact. Used for relationship health monitoring and engagement cadence management.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last (family/surname) name of the vendor contact individual. Used for formal identification and correspondence.']
  linkedin_url varchar [note: 'LinkedIn professional profile URL for the vendor contact. Used for professional verification, relationship management, and talent acquisition partnership activities.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile/cell phone number for the vendor contact. Used for urgent communications, SMS notifications, and after-hours contact in time-sensitive staffing situations.']
  nda_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a signed Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) is on file for this vendor contact. Required for contacts who have access to client confidential information or candidate data.']
  nda_signed_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor contact executed the Non-Disclosure Agreement. Used for compliance tracking and NDA renewal management.']
  next_scheduled_contact_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled outreach or business review with this vendor contact. Supports proactive relationship management and QBR (Quarterly Business Review) scheduling.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for MSP program team notes about the vendor contact, including relationship context, communication preferences, escalation history, or special handling instructions.']
  office_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the vendor contact\'s office location. Used for formal correspondence, site visit coordination, and geographic program mapping.']
  office_city varchar [note: 'City of the vendor contact\'s office location. Supports geographic segmentation and regional program management.']
  office_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the vendor contact\'s office location (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Supports international MSP program operations and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  office_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP/postal code of the vendor contact\'s office location. Used for geographic routing and regional program management.']
  office_state varchar [note: 'U.S. state or territory code (2-letter USPS abbreviation) of the vendor contact\'s office location. Used for regional program assignment and compliance jurisdiction determination.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary office/desk phone number for the vendor contact. Used for direct business communication within MSP program operations.']
  phone_extension varchar [note: 'Office phone extension number for the vendor contact. Required when the vendor organization uses a PBX system and direct dial is not available.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'The vendor contact\'s preferred channel for receiving communications from the MSP program team. Drives automated notification routing and outreach strategy.']
  program_access_level varchar [note: 'Level of access granted to this vendor contact within the Beeline VMS program portal. Controls what program data, rate cards, and candidate submissions the contact can view or manage.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Functional role classification of the vendor contact within the MSP/VMS program relationship. Determines communication routing and program responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: account_manager|recruiter|compliance_officer|billing_contact|executive_sponsor|operations_manager|technical_contact|onboarding_coordinator — promote to reference product]']
  secondary_email varchar [note: 'Alternate or backup email address for the vendor contact. Used when primary email is unresponsive or for specific communication streams such as billing or compliance notices.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which this vendor contact became active in the MSP/VMS program relationship. Used for relationship tenure tracking and program onboarding analytics.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the vendor contact\'s primary work location (e.g., America/New_York, America/Chicago). Used to schedule communications and meetings at appropriate local times.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor contact record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  vms_portal_username varchar [note: 'Username assigned to the vendor contact for accessing the Beeline VMS supplier portal. Used for portal access tracking and account management.']

  Note: 'Master record for individual contacts associated with a vendor/supplier organization. Captures contact full name, job title, role type (account manager, recruiter, compliance officer, billing contact, executive sponsor), email, phone, mobile, preferred communication method, contact status (active, inactive), primary contact flag, and the vendor organization they represent. Supports relationship management and communication routing for MSP program operations.'
}

Table vendor.program_allocation {
  program_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each vendor program allocation record within the Beeline VMS-managed contingent workforce program. Primary key for this entity.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account that owns the VMS program for which this allocation is configured. Supports multi-client MSP program management.']
  client_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate card governing the bill and pay rate limits for this vendor\'s placements under this program allocation. Links to the client rate card record in the VMS or CRM system.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Allocations specify max bill/pay rates. Business needs to link to the rate schedule to enforce rate caps during requisition distribution, validate rate exceptions, and ensure allocated rates comply wi']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: Program allocations restricted to vendors meeting specific credentialing packages. Required for vendor eligibility rules, allocation criteria enforcement, compliance gates, and automated vendor qualif']
  msa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Master Service Agreement (MSA) under which this vendor is engaged within the program. The MSA governs the overarching contractual terms including liability, indemnification, and compliance obligations.']
  performance_scorecard_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the most recent vendor performance scorecard that informed this allocation assignment or tier classification. Links to the vendor scorecard record used in the last reclassification decision cycle.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staffing supplier or subcontractor assigned this allocation within the VMS program. Identifies the vendor entity in the vendor master.']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Allocations (submission caps, priority rank, max rates) are contractually defined in vendor agreements. Business needs to enforce allocation rules per agreement terms and validate allocation changes a']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client VMS program under which this allocation is defined. Links to the Beeline VMS program record governing the contingent workforce engagement.']
  allocation_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this allocation record within the Beeline VMS program. Used in MSP reporting and vendor communications to reference a specific fill-rate assignment.']
  allocation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The requisition fill-rate allocation percentage assigned to this vendor for the specified job category within the VMS program. Represents the share of job orders this vendor is expected to fill relative to total program volume. Sum of all active vendor allocations per program and job category should equal 100%.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this vendor program allocation. Active: vendor is receiving job order distributions. Suspended: temporarily halted due to compliance or performance issues. Exhausted: submission quota has been fully consumed. Pending: awaiting activation. Expired: past expiration date. Terminated: permanently removed from program.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the MSP program manager or client stakeholder who approved this allocation assignment. Provides an audit trail for allocation governance and change management.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this allocation was formally approved by the MSP program manager or client stakeholder. Establishes the governance record for allocation activation.']
  auto_distribute boolean [note: 'Indicates whether job orders matching this allocation\'s job category are automatically distributed to this vendor by Beeline VMS without manual MSP intervention. When True, the VMS auto-routes requisitions based on tier and allocation percentage. When False, manual distribution is required.']
  compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has completed all required compliance verifications (insurance certificates, E-Verify enrollment, background check program agreement, OFCCP compliance attestation) to be active under this program allocation.']
  compliance_verified_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor\'s compliance credentials were last verified and confirmed as meeting program requirements. Used to track compliance currency and trigger re-verification workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this program allocation record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and SOC 2 compliance.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this vendor program allocation becomes active and the vendor is eligible to receive job order distributions under the specified terms. Aligns with MSP program cycle start or tier reclassification decision date.']
  exclusive_category boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor holds exclusive rights to fill requisitions in the specified job category within this VMS program. When True, no other vendor may receive job order distributions for this category. Typically reserved for niche or specialized labor categories.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this vendor program allocation expires and the vendor is no longer eligible to receive job order distributions under the specified terms. Nullable for open-ended allocations. Typically aligned with program review cycles or scorecard-driven reclassification events.']
  first_look_days varchar [note: 'The number of business days this vendor has exclusive or priority access to a new requisition before it is released to lower-tier vendors. Enforces preferred supplier first-look access per MSP program rules. Applicable only to Tier 1 and Preferred vendors.']
  ic_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is authorized to supply Independent Contractors (ICs) under this program allocation. When True, the vendor may submit 1099 or Corp-to-Corp workers. Requires additional IC compliance validation per IRS worker classification rules.']
  job_category varchar [note: 'The labor category or job family for which this allocation applies within the VMS program (e.g., IT, Engineering, Administrative, Light Industrial, Healthcare). Governs which requisition types the vendor is authorized to fill under this allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IT|Engineering|Administrative|Light Industrial|Healthcare|Finance|Legal|Creative|Operations|Scientific — promote to reference product]']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'The date on which this allocation was last reviewed by the MSP program manager, typically as part of a periodic scorecard-driven tier reclassification cycle. Supports governance audit trails and ensures allocations are not stale.']
  max_bill_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum hourly bill rate the vendor is authorized to charge the client for placements made under this allocation and job category. Enforced by Beeline VMS rate card controls. Expressed in the program\'s operating currency (USD).']
  max_concurrent_submissions varchar [note: 'The maximum number of candidate submittals this vendor is permitted to have active simultaneously for a single requisition within this program and job category. Controls submission quota per Beeline VMS program configuration to enforce Quality of Submission (QoS) standards.']
  max_markup_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum markup percentage (bill rate minus pay rate, divided by pay rate) the vendor is permitted to apply for placements under this allocation. Enforces MSP program margin controls and prevents rate gouging. Expressed as a percentage.']
  max_pay_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum hourly pay rate the vendor is authorized to pay workers placed under this allocation and job category. Used to enforce spread and markup controls within the MSP program. Expressed in USD.']
  min_fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum requisition fill rate percentage the vendor is contractually required to achieve for this job category within the program period. Used as a performance threshold in scorecard evaluations and tier reclassification decisions. Expressed as a percentage of assigned requisitions successfully filled.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The date on which this allocation is next scheduled for review and potential reclassification based on vendor scorecard results. Drives MSP program management workflows and ensures timely tier adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for MSP program managers to capture contextual notes about this allocation, such as rationale for tier assignment, special client instructions, or conditions attached to the allocation. Not used for structured data.']
  ot_bill_rate_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'The multiplier applied to the standard bill rate for overtime hours billed by the vendor under this allocation. Typically 1.5x for OT and 2.0x for double time (DT). Governs how overtime billing is calculated for workers placed under this program.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank of this vendor within its assigned tier for the job category. Lower values indicate higher priority for job order distribution when multiple vendors share the same tier. Used by Beeline VMS to sequence first-look access among same-tier suppliers.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workforce program under which this allocation operates. MSP: Managed Service Provider program. VMS: Vendor Management System-only program. RPO: Recruitment Process Outsourcing. SOW: Statement of Work-based engagement. IC: Independent Contractor supplier program. Direct: direct client-managed program.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'The frequency at which this vendor program allocation is reviewed and potentially updated based on scorecard performance and tier reclassification decisions. Governs the cadence of MSP program management activities.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for why this allocation was suspended, if applicable. Populated when allocation_status is \'suspended\'. Supports MSP program governance and vendor remediation tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: performance|compliance|rate_violation|contract_breach|client_request|audit_hold|other — promote to reference product if additional values needed]']
  target_time_to_fill_days varchar [note: 'The target number of business days within which the vendor is expected to submit qualified candidates for a requisition under this allocation. Represents the Time to Fill (TTF) SLA commitment for this vendor-program-job category combination.']
  target_time_to_start_days varchar [note: 'The target number of calendar days from requisition approval to worker start date for placements made under this allocation. Represents the Time to Start (TTS) SLA commitment for this vendor-program-job category combination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this program allocation record was most recently modified. Tracks the last change event for audit trail purposes and supports incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  vendor_tier varchar [note: 'Tier classification assigned to the vendor within this VMS program and job category. Determines the vendor\'s priority for receiving job order distribution and first-look access per MSP program rules. Tier 1 vendors receive highest priority; Backup vendors are used only when preferred tiers are exhausted.']
  vms_allocation_ref varchar [note: 'The native allocation or supplier program enrollment reference number assigned by Beeline VMS for this vendor-program-job category combination. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the Beeline VMS operational system.']
  worker_type varchar [note: 'The worker classification type this allocation governs. W-2: temporary employee on agency payroll. 1099: independent contractor. Corp-to-Corp: vendor-supplied contractor via their own entity. EOR: Employer of Record arrangement. SOW: Statement of Work-based resource. Impacts tax treatment, co-employment risk, and IRS worker classification compliance.']

  Note: 'Defines the requisition fill-rate allocation and submission quota assigned to each vendor within a specific client VMS program and job category.
