Captures program ID, vendor ID, job category, tier classification reference, allocation percentage, maximum concurrent submissions allowed, priority rank within tier, allocation effective date, expiration date, and allocation status (active, suspended, exhausted). Governs how job orders are distributed across the vendor supply chain in Beeline VMS to ensure preferred suppliers receive first-look access per MSP program rules. Updated periodically based on scorecard results and tier reclassification decisions.'
}

Table vendor.vendor_compliance {
  vendor_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each vendor compliance record in the MSP/VMS program. Primary key for the vendor_compliance data product.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: Vendor compliance records should link to formal compliance audits that reviewed vendor documentation (insurance, certifications, policies). Enables audit trail, finding remediation tracking, and vendo']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Compliance requirements (insurance limits, certifications, background checks) are defined in MSAs. Business needs to link compliance records to the MSA that mandates them for audit trails, breach dete']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential. Business justification: Vendor compliance items reference specific credential types (insurance certificates, business licenses, etc.). Enables document classification, compliance mapping, audit trails, and standardized compl']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Managed Service Provider (MSP) or Vendor Management System (VMS) program under which this compliance obligation is tracked. A vendor may participate in multiple programs with distinct compliance requirements.']
  officer_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Identifies internal compliance analyst responsible for vendor compliance monitoring, document verification, and remediation tracking. Essential for compliance accountability, audit trails, and risk ma']
  sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: Compliance items (e.g., time-to-submit, on-time document delivery) may be SLA-driven. Business needs to link compliance records to the SLA they satisfy for SLA breach tracking, penalty enforcement, an']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor (staffing supplier, subcontractor, or service provider) to which this compliance record belongs. Links to the vendor master profile in the vendor domain.']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Vendor agreements specify compliance obligations (insurance, E-Verify, diversity certifications). Business needs to link compliance records to the agreement that mandates them for SLA enforcement, tie']
  vendor_document_id varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or DocuSign CLM document ID for the compliance document or certificate submitted by the vendor. Enables direct retrieval of the source document during audits.']
  additional_insured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MSP or end client is listed as an additional insured on the vendor\'s insurance policy. Additional insured status provides direct coverage protection to the certificate holder. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  alert_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before document expiration at which a compliance alert is triggered and sent to the assigned compliance officer and vendor. Enables proactive renewal management to prevent lapses in coverage or certification.']
  alert_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a compliance expiration alert has been triggered for this record based on the configured alert threshold. True when the current date is within alert_threshold_days of document_expiration_date.']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the insurance policy is set to auto-renew upon expiration. When True, the compliance team may rely on automatic renewal; when False, proactive renewal tracking is required to prevent coverage lapses. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the insurance carrier or underwriter that issued the policy documented in this compliance record. Used to verify carrier financial strength ratings and validate certificate authenticity. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  certificate_holder varchar [note: 'Name of the entity listed as the certificate holder on the COI — typically the MSP, the end client, or both. The certificate holder is notified by the carrier in the event of policy cancellation or material change. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate number printed on the Certificate of Insurance (COI) issued by the vendor\'s insurance carrier. Used to uniquely identify and track the specific COI document. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  coi_document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or DocuSign CLM document ID for the actual Certificate of Insurance (COI) PDF submitted by the vendor. Distinct from the general document_reference field; specifically tracks the COI artifact. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the compliance obligation being tracked. Covers insurance certificates, E-Verify enrollment, EEOC/OFCCP obligations, OSHA safety programs, FCRA background check policies, I-9 audit readiness, workers compensation registration, FUTA/SUTA tax registration, non-compete policy, and data security (SOC 2). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: insurance|e_verify|eeoc|ofccp|osha|fcra|i9_audit|workers_comp|futa_suta|non_compete|data_security|other — promote to reference product]']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance disposition for this obligation. \'compliant\' indicates all requirements are met; \'non_compliant\' indicates a deficiency exists; \'pending_review\' indicates documents have been submitted and are under evaluation; \'waived\' indicates the MSP or client has formally waived this requirement for the vendor.']
  coverage_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount of coverage provided under the insurance policy per occurrence or in aggregate, as stated on the Certificate of Insurance (COI). Expressed in USD. Used to verify that vendor coverage meets MSP/client minimum requirements. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  coverage_limit_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the coverage_limit_amount represents a per-occurrence limit, an aggregate annual limit, or a per-claim limit. Critical for accurately assessing whether vendor coverage meets program requirements. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor compliance record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  document_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the submitted compliance document or certificate becomes effective and valid. For insurance certificates, this is the policy inception date. For regulatory filings, this is the filing effective date.']
  document_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance document or certificate expires and must be renewed. Triggers automated compliance alerts when the expiration date is within the configured alert threshold. Critical for insurance COIs, SOC 2 reports, and E-Verify MOUs.']
  everify_employer_number varchar [note: 'The E-Verify employer identification number assigned by USCIS to the vendor upon enrollment in the E-Verify program. Required for vendors operating under federal contracts subject to FAR E-Verify clause. Applicable for compliance_category = \'e_verify\'.']
  everify_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor enrolled in the E-Verify program with USCIS. Used to verify continuous enrollment and compliance with federal contractor E-Verify requirements. Applicable for compliance_category = \'e_verify\'.']
  insurance_type varchar [note: 'Type of insurance coverage documented in this compliance record. Covers the five standard insurance types required in MSP/VMS programs: general liability, professional liability (Errors & Omissions/E&O), workers compensation, cyber liability, and umbrella/excess liability. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  is_insurance_record boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance record is an insurance-category record requiring additional insurance-specific fields (certificate number, carrier, policy number, coverage limit, etc.). When True, all insurance sub-fields must be populated.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the compliance obligation, regulatory basis, and what the vendor must demonstrate to satisfy the requirement. Provides context for compliance officers and auditors reviewing the record.']
  item_name varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable name of the specific compliance item or obligation (e.g., \'General Liability Insurance Certificate\', \'E-Verify Employer Enrollment\', \'OSHA 300 Log Submission\', \'SOC 2 Type II Report\'). Used as the display label in compliance dashboards and audit reports.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent compliance audit or review was conducted for this compliance item. Used to track audit cadence and identify overdue reviews.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance review or audit of this item. Drives compliance calendar and workload planning for compliance officers. May be set automatically based on document expiration or audit frequency rules.']
  non_compete_policy_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has a documented non-compete or non-solicitation policy on file with the MSP program, as required by the Master Service Agreement (MSA). Prevents vendors from directly soliciting placed workers or end clients.']
  policy_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the insurance policy becomes effective, as stated on the Certificate of Insurance. May differ from document_effective_date if the COI was issued after the policy inception date. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  policy_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the insurance policy expires, as stated on the Certificate of Insurance. Used as the primary expiration trigger for insurance-category compliance alerts. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Unique policy number assigned by the insurance carrier for the coverage documented in this compliance record. Used to verify coverage with the carrier and cross-reference against the COI. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  remediation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the remediation plan, corrective actions taken, or outstanding steps required to bring a non-compliant vendor into compliance. Captured by the assigned compliance officer and visible in audit trails.']
  required_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of document the vendor must submit to satisfy this compliance item (e.g., \'Certificate of Insurance (COI)\', \'E-Verify MOU\', \'OSHA 300 Log\', \'SOC 2 Report\', \'W-9\', \'I-9 Audit Report\'). Drives document collection workflows in the VMS program.']
  soc2_report_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the vendor\'s data security compliance is evidenced by a SOC 2 Type I (point-in-time design assessment) or SOC 2 Type II (operational effectiveness over a period) report. Type II is the standard requirement for MSP/VMS data security compliance. Applicable for compliance_category = \'data_security\'.']
  source_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Beeline VMS compliance record ID, DocuSign envelope ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and deduplication in the Silver Layer.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this compliance record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration in the Silver Layer. Primary source is Beeline VMS; manual entries and DocuSign CLM are secondary sources.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor compliance record was most recently modified. Used to detect stale records and support incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authorized individual (compliance director, MSP program manager, or client representative) who approved the waiver of this compliance requirement.']
  waiver_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which an approved compliance waiver expires and the vendor must re-demonstrate compliance or obtain a new waiver. Prevents indefinite waivers from masking persistent compliance gaps.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Documented business justification for waiving a compliance requirement for this vendor. Required when compliance_status is \'waived\'. Must be approved by an authorized compliance officer or program director.']

  Note: 'Tracks vendor-level compliance status, regulatory obligation fulfillment, and insurance certificate records for each supplier in the MSP/VMS program. Captures compliance category (insurance, E-Verify, EEOC, OFCCP, OSHA, FCRA, I-9 audit, workers comp, FUTA/SUTA registration, non-compete policy, data security SOC 2), compliance item description, required document, document expiration date, compliance status (compliant, non-compliant, pending review, waived), last audit date, next review date, remediation notes, and compliance officer assigned. For insurance-category records, additionally captures certificate number, insurance type (general liability, professional liability/E&O, workers compensation, cyber liability, umbrella), carrier name, policy number, coverage limit, certificate holder (MSP/client), additional insured status, auto-renewal flag, and COI document reference. Triggers compliance alerts when documents or certificates approach expiration. Distinct from the credentialing domain which tracks worker-level credentials.'
}

Table vendor.preferred_supplier_list {
  preferred_supplier_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for a Preferred Supplier List (PSL) entry. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: JUNCTION — associates a vendor with a client program and job category within a governed PSL framework, carrying its own lifecycle attributes (status, dates, approval authority, version).']
  approval_authority_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Tracks internal procurement/vendor management director who approved PSL inclusion. Critical for strategic sourcing governance, PSL audit compliance, and supplier selection authority validation in pref']
  client_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client-specific rate card that governs the bill rates and pay rates applicable to this vendor for the job category within the client program. Ensures rate compliance when the vendor submits candidates.']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client VMS program for which this PSL entry governs vendor eligibility. Links the PSL entry to the specific managed service program under which requisitions are issued.']
  msa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Master Service Agreement (MSA) governing the vendor\'s participation in this client program. The MSA must be executed before a vendor can be placed on the PSL. Links to the contract lifecycle management record.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staffing supplier or subcontractor record authorized (or excluded) under this PSL entry. Identifies which vendor the PSL status applies to within the client program.']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: PSL inclusion is contractually governed by vendor agreements. Business needs to link PSL entry to the vendor agreement that grants preferred status for validating PSL eligibility, enforcing PSL-specif']
  vms_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s enrollment or registration identifier within the Beeline VMS for this specific client program. Used to cross-reference the PSL entry with the vendor\'s VMS program participation record and to confirm active enrollment status.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the approval authority formally approved the vendor\'s PSL status for this entry. Distinct from inclusion_date, which reflects when the vendor became operationally active on the PSL.']
  avg_time_to_fill_days decimal(6,2) [note: 'The vendor\'s average Time to Fill (TTF) in calendar days for requisitions fulfilled under this client program and job category. Measures responsiveness and is a core KPI for PSL tier classification and SLA compliance.']
  client_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for client-specific instructions, preferences, or annotations related to this vendor\'s PSL entry. May include preferred contact protocols, special billing instructions, or program-specific requirements communicated by the client.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the vendor for this PSL entry, reflecting whether all required credentialing, insurance, and regulatory requirements have been met. \'compliant\' = all requirements satisfied; \'non_compliant\' = one or more requirements unmet; \'pending_review\' = under evaluation; \'waived\' = client-approved exception granted.']
  conditional_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing client-specific conditions, restrictions, or instructions applicable to this PSL entry when the status is \'conditional\'. Examples include geographic restrictions, role-level caps, or specific compliance prerequisites the vendor must meet.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PSL entry record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  diversity_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor holds a current, verified diversity certification applicable to this PSL entry. Supports client diversity spend reporting and OFCCP compliance without requiring the full classification detail.']
  diversity_classification varchar [note: 'Diversity certification category of the vendor as recognized under this PSL entry (e.g., MBE, WBE, SDVOSB, HUBZone, LGBTBE, SBE). Used for client diversity spend reporting and OFCCP compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MBE|WBE|SDVOSB|HUBZone|LGBTBE|SBE|DBE|VOSB — promote to reference product]']
  exclusion_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor was removed or excluded from the Preferred Supplier List for this client program and job category. Null if the vendor remains active on the PSL. Marks the end of the vendor\'s eligibility window.']
  exclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason for the vendor\'s exclusion or suspension from this PSL entry. Required when psl_status is \'excluded\' or \'suspended\'. Supports audit trails, vendor dispute resolution, and governance reporting.']
  exclusive_supplier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor holds exclusive rights to supply workers for the specified job category within the client program. When true, no other vendor on the PSL may submit candidates for that category.']
  fall_off_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The vendor\'s fall-off rate percentage for placements made under this client program and job category — calculated as placements that ended prematurely (fall-offs) divided by total placements. A key quality metric used in PSL performance evaluation.']
  fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The vendor\'s historical fill rate percentage for requisitions received under this client program and job category — calculated as filled requisitions divided by total requisitions assigned. A key performance indicator (KPI) used in PSL tier evaluation and renewal decisions.']
  ic_supplier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is classified as an Independent Contractor (IC) supplier, providing 1099 workers rather than W-2 employees. Affects worker classification compliance checks and tax treatment for placements under this PSL entry.']
  inclusion_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor was added to the Preferred Supplier List for this client program and job category. Marks the start of the vendor\'s eligibility window under this PSL entry.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor\'s insurance coverage expires. Used to trigger renewal alerts and compliance reviews. If insurance lapses, the vendor\'s PSL status may be automatically suspended pending re-verification.']
  insurance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor\'s required insurance certificates (general liability, workers compensation, professional liability) have been verified and are current for this PSL entry. A prerequisite for PSL active status in most client programs.']
  job_category varchar [note: 'The labor category or skill classification for which this PSL entry governs vendor eligibility (e.g., IT, Engineering, Administrative, Clinical, Light Industrial). Determines which requisition types the vendor is authorized to fulfill within the client program.']
  job_category_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code representing the job category within the VMS or client program. Used for system-level routing of requisitions to eligible vendors and for cross-program reporting.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this PSL entry was most recently reviewed by the approval authority or program governance team. Used to track review cadence compliance and identify stale PSL entries.']
  nda_executed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) has been executed between the vendor and the client for this program. Required for vendors accessing sensitive client information as part of the staffing engagement.']
  non_compete_executed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a non-compete or non-solicitation agreement has been executed with the vendor for this client program. Protects the client\'s workforce and candidate pipeline from direct solicitation by the vendor.']
  performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite performance score assigned to the vendor for this client program and job category, typically on a 0–100 scale. Derived from scorecard metrics including fill rate, time-to-fill (TTF), quality of submission (QoS), and retention. Used to inform PSL tier assignments and renewal decisions.']
  psl_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this PSL entry, used in VMS communications, audit trails, and client-facing documentation. Follows the format PSL-{alphanumeric code}.']
  psl_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor on this PSL entry. \'preferred\' = top-tier vendor with priority routing; \'approved\' = eligible to receive requisitions; \'conditional\' = eligible with restrictions or conditions; \'excluded\' = blocked from receiving requisitions; \'suspended\' = temporarily ineligible pending review.']
  psl_tier varchar [note: 'Tier classification assigned to the vendor within the PSL for this client program and job category. Tier 1 vendors receive first-right-of-refusal on requisitions; lower tiers receive requisitions only when higher tiers cannot fulfill. Governs requisition routing priority in the VMS.']
  psl_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the PSL under which this entry was created or last revised (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Supports PSL versioning and audit trails when the client program updates its approved vendor roster.']
  requisition_visibility varchar [note: 'Controls whether the vendor can see and respond to open requisitions in the VMS for this client program and job category. \'visible\' = vendor sees all eligible reqs; \'hidden\' = vendor is on PSL but not shown reqs; \'restricted\' = vendor sees only specific reqs per conditional rules.']
  review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this vendor\'s PSL status for the client program and job category. Supports governance cadence for PSL maintenance and vendor performance evaluation cycles.']
  right_to_represent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor must obtain a Right to Represent (RTR) confirmation from the candidate before submitting to this client program. When true, the VMS enforces RTR capture prior to submittal acceptance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this PSL entry originated or was last synchronized. Supports data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse and reconciliation between VMS and ATS records.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Beeline VMS PSL record ID). Enables traceability from the Silver Layer lakehouse record back to the operational system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  sow_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is authorized to deliver Statement of Work (SOW) engagements under this PSL entry, in addition to or instead of contingent staffing. Governs SOW requisition routing in the VMS.']
  submission_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of candidate submittals the vendor is permitted to make per requisition for this client program and job category. Enforced by the VMS to manage submittal volume and ensure quality of submission (QoS).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PSL entry record was most recently modified. Tracks the last change event for audit trail purposes and supports incremental data loading in the Silver Layer lakehouse pipeline.']

  Note: 'Manages the Preferred Supplier List (PSL) — the curated, approved roster of vendors authorized to supply contingent workers or SOW services for a specific client program or job category. Captures client program ID, vendor ID, job category, PSL status (preferred, approved, conditional, excluded), inclusion date, exclusion date, PSL version, approval authority, and client-specific notes. Governs which vendors are visible and eligible to receive requisitions in the VMS for each client engagement.'
}

Table vendor.diversity_certification {
  diversity_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each vendor diversity certification record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  previous_certification_diversity_certification_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the prior certification record that this record supersedes or renews. Enables certification history chain tracking for audit and compliance reporting.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor (staffing supplier, subcontractor, or service provider) that holds this diversity certification. Links to the vendor master record in the vendor domain.']
  vendor_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the supporting certification document stored in the document management system (e.g., DocuSign CLM or document repository). Links to the actual certificate, letter of certification, or renewal confirmation.']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Beeline VMS contingent workforce program under which this diversity certification is tracked and reported. Enables diversity spend analytics at the program level for MSP-managed client programs.']
  annual_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'The vendor\'s annual gross revenue in US dollars as reported at the time of certification, used to verify small business size standard eligibility for applicable certification types (HUBZone, 8(a), SDVOB).']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification type supports automatic renewal processing through the certifying body\'s online portal or VMS integration. True = auto-renewal available; False = manual renewal required.']
  beeline_vendor_cert_code varchar [note: 'The external certification record identifier assigned by the Beeline VMS system for this diversity certification. Used for system-of-record reconciliation and VMS data synchronization.']
  certification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers or supplier diversity program managers to record observations, exceptions, conditions, or special circumstances related to this diversity certification (e.g., conditional approval, pending documentation, audit findings).']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'The official certificate number or registration identifier issued by the certifying body (e.g., NMSDC, WBENC, SBA) for this diversity designation. Used for verification and OFCCP supplier diversity reporting.']
  certification_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic scope of this diversity certification. National = recognized across the US; Regional = recognized within a multi-state region; State = state-level certification only; Local = city or county level certification.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the diversity certification. Active = valid and in good standing; Expired = past expiration date without renewal; Pending Renewal = renewal application submitted and under review; Revoked = certification withdrawn by certifying body; Suspended = temporarily inactive pending investigation or documentation.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'The category of diversity designation held by the vendor. MBE = Minority Business Enterprise; WBE = Women Business Enterprise; SDVOB = Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Business; HUBZone = Historically Underutilized Business Zone; 8(a) = SBA 8(a) Business Development Program; LGBTBE = LGBT Business Enterprise; VOSB = Veteran-Owned Small Business. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MBE|WBE|SDVOB|HUBZone|8(a)|LGBTBE|VOSB|AABE|DBE|SBE — promote to reference product]']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'The organization that issued and validates this diversity certification. NMSDC = National Minority Supplier Development Council; WBENC = Women\'s Business Enterprise National Council; SBA = Small Business Administration; USVBA = U.S. Veteran Business Alliance; NGLCC = National LGBT Chamber of Commerce; NVBDC = National Veteran Business Development Council; DOT = Department of Transportation; STATE = State-level certifying authority. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NMSDC|WBENC|SBA|USVBA|NGLCC|NVBDC|DOT|STATE|CITY|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  certifying_body_region varchar [note: 'The regional chapter or affiliate of the certifying body that issued this certification (e.g., NMSDC regional council name, WBENC regional partner organization). Relevant for multi-regional supplier diversity programs.']
  client_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is eligible to be reported as qualifying diverse spend to the client under their supplier diversity program requirements. True = reportable; False = not reportable (e.g., expired, unverified, or excluded by client program rules).']
  control_and_operations boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the diverse owner(s) exercise day-to-day control and management of the business operations, as required by certifying body standards. True = confirmed control; False = control not confirmed or under review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this diversity certification record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Audit trail field for data governance and SOC 2 Type II compliance.']
  diversity_spend_category varchar [note: 'The tier classification of diversity spend associated with this certification. Tier 1 = direct spend with certified diverse vendor; Tier 2 = spend through prime vendor with certified diverse subcontractor; Tier 3 = indirect diversity spend. Used for client diversity spend reporting and OFCCP compliance.']
  document_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supporting certification document has been reviewed and verified by the MSP compliance team or supplier diversity program manager. True = document verified; False = pending verification.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this certification record is considered effective for diversity spend reporting and supplier diversity program participation. May differ from issue date if backdated or prospectively applied.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'The number of employees at the vendor organization as reported at the time of certification. Used for SBA size standard verification and diversity program eligibility assessment.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this diversity certification expires and must be renewed to remain valid. Critical for compliance tracking, renewal alerts, and OFCCP supplier diversity spend reporting eligibility.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the certifying body officially issued or granted this diversity certification to the vendor. Used to establish certification tenure and eligibility windows.']
  minority_group varchar [note: 'For MBE (Minority Business Enterprise) certifications, the specific minority group classification as defined by NMSDC and EEOC guidelines. Applicable only when certification_type = MBE. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Asian|Black|Hispanic|Native_American|Pacific_Islander|Multi|Other — promote to reference product]']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'The six-digit NAICS code representing the primary industry classification of the vendor at the time of diversity certification. Used for SBA size standard determination and diversity spend categorization by industry.']
  ofccp_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification qualifies for inclusion in OFCCP federal contractor supplier diversity compliance reporting. True = qualifies for OFCCP reporting; False = does not qualify.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the vendor business owned by the qualifying diverse individual(s) as documented in the certification application. Must meet minimum thresholds (typically 51%) set by certifying bodies for certification eligibility.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact at the vendor organization responsible for managing this diversity certification. Used for renewal notifications and compliance communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact at the vendor organization responsible for managing and renewing this diversity certification.']
  renewal_alert_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the certification expiration date at which automated renewal alerts should be triggered to the vendor and compliance team. Configurable per certification type or program requirements (e.g., 90 days for WBENC, 60 days for NMSDC).']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor submitted or completed the renewal application for this diversity certification. Distinct from expiration date; tracks the actual renewal action taken.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'The stated reason for revocation or suspension of this diversity certification, as communicated by the certifying body. Populated only when certification_status = revoked or suspended. Required for OFCCP audit trail.']
  small_business_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor also qualifies as a small business under SBA size standards at the time of certification. Relevant for HUBZone, 8(a), and SDVOB certifications that require small business status.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system from which this diversity certification record was ingested or created. Beeline = Beeline VMS integration; Manual = manually entered by compliance team; NMSDC_Portal/WBENC_Portal/SBA_Portal = direct portal integration; Other = other source.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for state-level or regional diversity certifications (e.g., DOT Disadvantaged Business Enterprise state programs, state MWBE certifications). Null for national certifications.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this diversity certification record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit trail compliance.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the compliance officer or supplier diversity program manager who verified the certification document and confirmed its authenticity.']
  verified_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification document was verified by the compliance team. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'Tracks vendor diversity certifications required for OFCCP compliance, client diversity spend reporting, and supplier diversity program participation. Captures certification type (MBE, WBE, SDVOB, HUBZone, 8(a), LGBTBE, VOSB), certifying body (NMSDC, WBENC, SBA, USVBA), certification number, issue date, expiration date, certification status (active, expired, pending renewal, revoked), and supporting document reference. Enables diversity spend analytics and OFCCP supplier diversity reporting.'
}

Table vendor.exclusion {
  exclusion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for exclusion']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account associated with a client-level exclusion. Null when the exclusion scope is program-level or global.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Exclusions may result from MSA breaches (indemnification failures, insurance lapses) or be contractually defined. Business needs to reference the MSA that triggered or governs the exclusion for legal ']
  reviewed_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Identifies internal compliance/legal staff who reviewed exclusion and enforced VMS block. Essential for exclusion governance, legal risk management, and audit accountability in vendor exclusion/debarm']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor (staffing supplier, subcontractor, or service provider) subject to this exclusion or debarment action.']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Exclusions often stem from vendor agreement violations (SLA breaches, compliance failures). Business needs to link exclusion records to the breached agreement for audit trails, dispute resolution, and']
  vendor_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL pointing to supporting documentation for the exclusion (e.g., DocuSign CLM document ID, SharePoint folder path, legal case file reference, audit report ID). Links to evidence such as performance scorecards, compliance audit findings, or legal correspondence.']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Managed Service Provider (MSP) or Vendor Management System (VMS) contingent workforce program from which the vendor is excluded. Null for global exclusions.']
  appeal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the grounds for the appeal, key arguments presented by the vendor, and the rationale for the appeal resolution outcome. Contains sensitive legal and compliance information.']
  appeal_resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the appeal was formally resolved (upheld or denied). Null if the appeal is still pending or no appeal was filed.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any formal appeal filed by the vendor contesting the exclusion decision. Not_appealed means no appeal has been filed; appeal_submitted means the vendor has filed but review has not started; under_review means actively being evaluated; appeal_upheld means the exclusion was overturned; appeal_denied means the exclusion was confirmed.']
  appeal_submitted_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor formally submitted an appeal against the exclusion decision. Null if no appeal has been filed.']
  authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific individual, organization, or regulatory body that issued the exclusion (e.g., \'Jane Smith - MSP Program Director\', \'OFAC\', \'OIG\', \'Client ABC Procurement\'). Provides accountability and audit traceability.']
  authority_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the type of authority that issued or mandated the exclusion: MSP program manager (internal operational decision), client (client-directed blacklist), regulatory_body (SAM.gov, OFAC, OIG debarment), legal_counsel (legal dispute resolution), or executive_leadership (senior management directive).']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Master Service Agreement (MSA), Statement of Work (SOW), or vendor contract that was breached or is relevant to the exclusion action. Typically a DocuSign CLM contract identifier.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this exclusion record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail entry point for the exclusion lifecycle.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the exclusion takes effect and the vendor becomes prohibited from receiving requisitions or job orders within the defined scope.']
  exclusion_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to this exclusion action (e.g., EXC-2024-000123). Used in correspondence, appeals, and audit trails.']
  exclusion_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the breadth of the exclusion: global (entire MSP operation), program_level (specific VMS/MSP program), client_level (specific client account), or job_category_level (specific job categories or skill sets only).']
  exclusion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the exclusion record. Active means the vendor is currently prohibited; suspended means the exclusion is temporarily paused pending review; lifted means the exclusion has been formally removed; pending_review means under evaluation; appealed means a formal appeal is in progress.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the exclusion is scheduled to expire, if time-limited. Null for indefinite or permanent exclusions. After this date, the vendor may be eligible for reinstatement review.']
  job_category_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of job categories, skill sets, or requisition types from which the vendor is excluded when exclusion_scope is job_category_level. Null for broader scope exclusions. Aligns with VMS job category taxonomy.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'The date on which this exclusion record was most recently reviewed by the compliance or MSP program management team. Supports audit trail and governance requirements.']
  narrative varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text description of the circumstances, findings, and rationale that led to the exclusion decision. Contains sensitive compliance and legal information.']
  notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor was formally notified of the exclusion decision via written communication. Null if notification has not yet been sent.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'The channel or method used to formally notify the vendor of the exclusion decision (e.g., email, certified mail, VMS portal message, phone, in-person meeting).']
  notified_vendor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has been formally notified of the exclusion decision. True means notification has been sent; False means notification is pending. Required for FCRA adverse action compliance when applicable.']
  ofac_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has been verified against the OFAC (Office of Foreign Assets Control) Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) and Blocked Persons list. True means an OFAC check was performed and a match was found; False means no match or check not yet performed.']
  oig_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has been verified against the OIG (Office of Inspector General) List of Excluded Individuals/Entities (LEIE). Particularly relevant for healthcare staffing programs. True means an OIG check confirmed exclusion.']
  preferred_supplier_impact varchar [note: 'Indicates the impact of this exclusion on the vendor\'s preferred supplier list (PSL) standing: removed (vendor removed from PSL), suspended (PSL status temporarily suspended), under_review (PSL status under evaluation), no_impact (exclusion is scoped narrowly and does not affect PSL standing).']
  program_allocation_impact varchar [note: 'Describes the effect of this exclusion on the vendor\'s program allocation (requisition routing share): blocked (no allocations permitted), reduced (allocation percentage reduced), suspended (allocations paused), no_impact (scoped exclusion does not affect overall allocation).']
  reason_category varchar [note: 'Standardized category classifying the primary reason for the exclusion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: compliance_failure|performance_failure|legal_dispute|client_blacklist|regulatory_debarment|fraud|contract_breach|worker_misclassification — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_case_number varchar [note: 'The official case, docket, or reference number assigned by the regulatory body (SAM.gov, OFAC, OIG, DOL, EEOC) for the debarment or enforcement action. Used for cross-referencing with government databases.']
  regulatory_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the specific regulatory database or government body that is the source of a regulatory debarment. Null or \'none\' for non-regulatory exclusions. Critical for SAM.gov, OFAC SDN, and OIG exclusion compliance checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAM.gov|OFAC|OIG|EEOC|DOL|OSHA|OFCCP|state_agency|none — promote to reference product]']
  reinstatement_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or authority who formally approved the vendor\'s reinstatement into the program. Provides accountability for reinstatement decisions.']
  reinstatement_conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific conditions, corrective actions, or requirements the vendor must satisfy before reinstatement can be considered (e.g., \'Provide updated compliance certifications, resolve outstanding legal dispute, complete corrective action plan\').']
  reinstatement_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the vendor was formally reinstated and the exclusion was lifted. Null if the vendor has not yet been reinstated.']
  reinstatement_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is eligible to apply for reinstatement into the program after meeting specified conditions. True means reinstatement is possible; False means the exclusion is permanent with no reinstatement pathway.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next periodic review of this exclusion record to assess whether the exclusion should be maintained, modified, or lifted. Supports ongoing compliance monitoring and governance.']
  sam_gov_check_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the vendor\'s status was checked against the SAM.gov federal debarment and exclusion database. Supports periodic re-verification compliance requirements.']
  sam_gov_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor\'s exclusion status has been verified against the SAM.gov (System for Award Management) federal debarment database. True means a SAM.gov check was performed and confirmed debarment; False means not yet verified or no SAM.gov record found.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Risk-based severity classification of the exclusion: critical (regulatory debarment, fraud, or legal judgment), high (significant compliance failure or client blacklist), medium (performance failure with corrective action pathway), low (minor policy violation with limited scope).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this exclusion record was most recently modified. Tracks changes to exclusion status, appeal updates, reinstatement decisions, and other lifecycle events.']
  vms_block_date date [note: 'The date on which the VMS system block was technically activated to prevent this vendor from receiving job orders or requisitions within the Beeline VMS platform.']
  vms_block_enforced boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exclusion has been technically enforced within the Beeline VMS system to prevent requisition routing to this vendor. True means the VMS block is active; False means the block has not yet been applied or has been lifted in the system.']

  Note: 'Maintains the vendor exclusion and debarment registry — suppliers that are prohibited from participating in specific client programs, job categories, or the entire MSP program due to compliance failures, performance issues, legal disputes, client blacklisting, or regulatory debarment (SAM.gov, OFAC, OIG). Captures vendor ID, exclusion scope (program-level, client-level, global), exclusion reason category, exclusion narrative, exclusion effective date, exclusion authority (MSP program manager, client, regulatory body), reinstatement eligibility flag, reinstatement conditions, reinstatement date if applicable, appeal status, and supporting documentation reference. Critical for risk management — the VMS must check this registry before routing any requisition to prevent excluded vendors from receiving job orders. Feeds into preferred_supplier_list eligibility and program_allocation enforcement.'
}

Table vendor.vendor_document {
  vendor_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor document record. Primary key for the vendor document entity.']
  docusign_envelope_id varchar [note: 'DocuSign envelope identifier for electronically signed documents. Links to the DocuSign CLM system for signature tracking and audit trail.']
  primary_previous_version_vendor_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prior version of this document if this is a revision or update. Enables version history tracking and audit trail.']
  source_system_document_vendor_document_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the document in the source system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration with operational systems of record.']
  superseded_vendor_document_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor_document (superseded_vendor_document_id)']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor supplier who submitted or is associated with this document. Links to the vendor supplier master record.']
  uploaded_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Tracks internal employee who uploaded/verified vendor document (insurance cert, W9, MSA). Critical for document management audit trails, compliance verification accountability, and onboarding workflow']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Vendor documents (COIs, W-9s, diversity certifications) are submitted to satisfy vendor agreement requirements. Business needs to associate documents with the agreement they fulfill for compliance ver']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the VMS program under which this document was submitted or is managed. Supports multi-program vendor document tracking.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Security classification level for document access control. Determines who can view, download, or modify the document based on role-based access control.']
  alert_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an expiration alert has been triggered for this document. Used to track alert notification status.']
  beeline_document_code varchar [note: 'Document identifier from the Beeline VMS system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration with Beeline vendor management platform.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash or checksum of the document file. Used for file integrity verification and detecting unauthorized modifications.']
  clm_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the document in the Contract Lifecycle Management system. Links to DocuSign CLM or other CLM platform for contract management.']
  compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document is required for compliance purposes. True if the document is mandatory for regulatory compliance, vendor enrollment, or program participation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the document record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  document_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the document for organizational and retrieval purposes. Groups documents by business function: legal, compliance, financial, or operational.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or tracking number assigned to the document. May be system-generated or vendor-provided reference number.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document. Indicates whether the document is current and active, has been superseded by a newer version, has expired, is archived, is pending review, or has been rejected.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document type. Includes W-9 (Wage and Tax Statement), COI (Certificate of Insurance), NDA (Non-Disclosure Agreement), MSA (Master Service Agreement), rate agreement, diversity certificate, insurance policy, compliance attestation, executed SOW (Statement of Work), training certificate, subcontractor agreement, and other vendor-related document types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: W-9|COI|NDA|MSA|Rate Agreement|Diversity Certificate|Insurance Policy|Compliance Attestation|Executed SOW|Training Certificate|Subcontractor Agreement|Background Check|Drug Screen|I-9|E-Verify|Workers Comp Certificate|Tax Document|Financial Statement — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the document becomes effective or valid. Represents the start of the documents applicability period.']
  expiration_alert_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before expiration when an alert should be triggered to notify stakeholders. Supports proactive document renewal management.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the document expires or is no longer valid. Used for compliance tracking and expiration alerting. Nullable for documents without expiration.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'File format or MIME type of the document. Common formats include PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PNG, JPG, TIF, MSG, EML. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PDF|DOCX|XLSX|PNG|JPG|TIF|MSG|EML — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Original file name of the uploaded document including file extension. Preserves the vendor-submitted file name for reference.']
  file_reference varchar [note: 'Storage location reference, file path, or URL pointing to the physical document file in the document repository or cloud storage system.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the document file in bytes. Used for storage management and download time estimation.']
  is_public boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document is publicly accessible or restricted to authorized users. Controls document visibility and access permissions.']
  last_alert_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent expiration alert was sent for this document. Supports alert frequency management and audit trail.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the document. Provides additional context, instructions, or observations for document management teams.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code for why the document was rejected during verification. Provides feedback to vendors for resubmission.']
  retention_end_date date [note: 'Date when the document retention period ends and the document becomes eligible for purging or archival. Calculated based on retention policy and document lifecycle events.']
  retention_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code or identifier for the retention policy classification that governs how long this document must be retained and when it can be purged. Aligns with legal and regulatory retention requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system from which the document record originated. Supports data lineage and integration tracking across Beeline VMS, DocuSign CLM, and other systems.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title or name of the document as displayed to users. Provides context about document content.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the document record was last updated in the data platform. Supports change tracking and audit trail.']
  upload_date date [note: 'Date when the document was uploaded to the VMS or document management system. Represents the business event date of document submission.']
  uploaded_by_role varchar [note: 'Role or user type of the person who uploaded the document. Distinguishes between vendor-submitted documents and internally uploaded documents.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the document was verified or reviewed for compliance purposes. Supports compliance audit and tracking.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the document verification process. Indicates whether the document has been verified, is pending verification, was rejected, or does not require verification.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the compliance officer or staff member who verified or reviewed the document. Used for compliance audit trail.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number or identifier for the document. Supports version control and tracking of document revisions over time.']

  Note: 'Master record for all vendor-submitted and vendor-related documents stored and versioned within the MSP/VMS program. Captures document type (W-9, COI, NDA, MSA, rate agreement, diversity certificate, insurance policy, compliance attestation, executed SOW, training certificate, subcontractor agreement), document title, file reference/URL, upload date, uploaded by, document version, effective date, expiration date, document status (current, superseded, expired, archived), associated vendor ID, retention policy classification, and document category (legal, compliance, financial, operational). Provides a centralized document repository for vendor management teams who manage hundreds of documents per supplier. Supports document expiration alerting and version control. Distinct from compliance-specific status tracking (owned by vendor_compliance) — this product owns the physical document inventory, version history, and storage references, while vendor_compliance owns the compliance status determination and audit workflow that references these documents.'
}

Table vendor.desk_supplier_allocation {
  desk_supplier_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this desk-supplier allocation record. Primary key.']
  created_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who created this allocation record. Links to employee domain staff profile.']
  recruiter_desk_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the recruiter desk configuration. Identifies which internal recruiting desk has access to this supplier.']
  vms_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor VMS enrollment record. Identifies which enrolled supplier this allocation applies to.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of this desk\'s requisition volume allocated to this supplier. Used for workload distribution and supplier utilization tracking. Sum across all suppliers for a desk may equal 100% or may exceed if overlapping coverage exists.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this desk-supplier allocation. Active = currently operational, Suspended = temporarily inactive, Expired = past expiration date, Pending = not yet effective.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this desk-supplier allocation became active and operational. Used for historical tracking and allocation lifecycle management.']
  exclusive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supplier has exclusive rights to fill certain job categories or requisitions for this desk. True = exclusive arrangement exists, False = non-exclusive.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this desk-supplier allocation expires or is scheduled to end. Null indicates an ongoing allocation with no defined end date.']
  job_category_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or free-text description of the specific job categories or skill families this supplier is authorized to fill for this desk. May be a subset of the supplier\'s overall approved categories in the VMS enrollment.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this allocation record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this desk-supplier allocation, including special arrangements, restrictions, or operational context.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking of this supplier for this desk\'s requisitions. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used in automated req distribution logic and supplier selection workflows.']
  submission_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of candidate submissions this supplier is allowed to make to this desk within a defined period (typically monthly or quarterly). Used for supplier workload management and quality control.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational allocation and access control between VMS-enrolled suppliers and internal recruiter desks. It captures how VMS supplier access is distributed across recruiting desks by job category, geography, or client segment. Each record links one enrolled supplier to one recruiter desk with allocation percentages, submission priorities, job category scope, and exclusive arrangement flags that exist only in the context of this desk-level supplier access relationship. Enables desk-level supplier management, req routing logic, and supplier utilization tracking.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations with VMS programs, suppliers are distributed across multiple internal recruiting desks based on job categories, geographies, and client segments, and each desk works with multiple enrolled suppliers simultaneously. The business actively manages desk-level supplier allocations with specific percentages, priority rankings, job category scopes, submission caps, and exclusive arrangements. This is an operational relationship that humans create, update, and monitor as part of supplier access control and req distribution workflows.'
}

Table vendor.supplier_compliance_obligation {
  supplier_compliance_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each supplier compliance obligation record']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance requirement that applies to this supplier']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier who must meet this compliance requirement']
  assigned_compliance_officer varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the compliance team member responsible for verifying and maintaining this specific supplier-requirement obligation. Used for workload assignment and accountability.']
  audit_result varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent compliance audit for this supplier-requirement pair: pass (audit confirmed compliance), fail (audit identified non-compliance), conditional_pass (compliance achieved with corrective action plan), not_audited (no audit conducted yet).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this supplier for this specific requirement: compliant (supplier has met the requirement and verification is current), non_compliant (supplier has failed to meet or maintain compliance), pending_verification (documentation submitted awaiting compliance team review), waived (requirement waived per waiver_reason), expired (compliance was achieved but has lapsed), not_applicable (requirement determined not to apply to this supplier)']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supplier compliance obligation record was created in the system.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the supporting documentation that evidences this suppliers compliance with this requirement (e.g., certificate number, document management system ID, file storage path). Used for audit trail and compliance verification.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this suppliers compliance with this specific requirement expires and re-verification or renewal is required. Calculated based on verification_date plus the requirements re_verification_interval_days. Triggers compliance alerts when approaching.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit conducted for this supplier-requirement pair. Distinct from verification_date which tracks ongoing verification; audit_date tracks formal periodic audits.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this supplier compliance obligation record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the compliance team to review this supplier-requirement obligation. Calculated based on expiration_date minus a lead-time buffer to allow proactive re-verification before expiration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance team to capture additional context, exceptions, corrective actions, or special handling instructions specific to this supplier-requirement obligation.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance team last verified that this supplier met this specific compliance requirement. Used to calculate re-verification due dates and track audit trail.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance requirement has been waived for this specific supplier. When true, compliance_status should be waived and waiver_reason must be populated.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of why this compliance requirement was waived for this supplier. Required when waiver_flag is true. Captures business justification, approver, and waiver conditions (e.g., client-approved exception, alternative compliance method accepted, requirement not applicable to suppliers business type).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance obligation relationship between compliance requirements and suppliers in staffing operations. It captures which regulatory and contractual compliance requirements apply to which suppliers, tracking the compliance status, verification dates, expiration dates, waivers, and supporting documentation for each supplier-requirement pair. Each record links one compliance_requirement to one supplier with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific compliance obligation.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, compliance requirements apply to multiple suppliers (e.g., I-9 verification applies to all staffing agencies, insurance requirements apply to all IC suppliers), and each supplier must meet multiple compliance requirements (federal regulations, client-specific mandates, industry standards). The compliance team actively manages these supplier-requirement obligations as operational records, tracking compliance status, verification dates, waivers, and documentation for each unique supplier-requirement pair.'
}

Table vendor.supplier_package_qualification {
  supplier_package_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for this supplier package qualification record. Primary key.']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the credential package that defines the requirements this supplier is qualified to fulfill']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this supplier for this credential package. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier who has been qualified to provide workers meeting this credential package']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the supplier was formally approved to provide workers meeting this credential package. Null if approval_status is pending or rejected.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the supplier for this specific credential package. Tracks lifecycle from initial application through approval, conditional approval, suspension, or revocation.']
  compliance_verification_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which the suppliers compliance with this credential package requirements was verified. Used to trigger re-verification workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this supplier package qualification record was created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this supplier qualification becomes effective and the supplier is eligible to submit candidates for placements requiring this credential package.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this supplier qualification expires and must be renewed. Triggers re-qualification workflows and prevents new candidate submissions after expiry.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit of this supplier for this specific credential package. Used to schedule periodic re-audits.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next compliance audit of this supplier-package qualification is due. Triggers audit scheduling workflows.']
  package_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the suppliers capability to fulfill all components of this credential package has been verified through audit, sample credential review, or compliance assessment.']
  qualification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing conditional approval terms, specific limitations, audit findings, or special instructions related to this supplier-package qualification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this supplier package qualification record was last updated.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal approval and qualification of a supplier to provide workers meeting a specific credential package. It captures the vendor approval process, compliance verification status, and package-specific vendor qualifications. Each record links one credential package to one supplier with attributes that exist only in the context of this qualification relationship, including approval status, verification dates, and expiration tracking.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, credential packages define standardized bundles of requirements (BGC, drug screens, licenses, training) for specific client programs or job categories, while suppliers are third-party staffing agencies and IC suppliers who provide workers. The business actively manages a Supplier Qualification Matrix where each supplier must be formally approved to provide workers meeting specific credential packages. One credential package (e.g., Healthcare Travel Nurse Package) can be fulfilled by multiple approved suppliers, and one supplier can be qualified to provide workers for multiple credential packages across different verticals or client programs. This is an operational relationship that humans create, audit, and revoke as part of vendor management and compliance workflows.'
}

Table vendor.supplier_checklist_assignment {
  supplier_checklist_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this supplier-checklist assignment record. Primary key.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific client account that drives this supplier-checklist assignment. Nullable if the assignment applies across all client programs for this supplier. Used when client-specific requirements mandate certain checklists for approved suppliers.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this supplier-checklist assignment. Supports audit trail and accountability for supplier program configuration decisions.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to vendor.supplier identifying which supplier this checklist assignment applies to']
  task_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to onboarding.task_checklist identifying which checklist template is assigned to this supplier']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this assignment. Values: active (in use), inactive (no longer applicable), suspended (temporarily disabled). Allows soft-delete and temporary suspension of supplier-checklist assignments without losing historical data.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of this supplier-checklist assignment. Values: pending (awaiting review), approved (assignment active), rejected (assignment denied), under_review (being evaluated). Tracks governance workflow for supplier program configuration.']
  assigned_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supplier-checklist assignment record was created in the system. Supports audit trail and historical analysis of supplier program configuration changes.']
  customization_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing any supplier-specific customizations or exceptions to the standard checklist template (e.g., waived tasks, additional requirements, client-specific modifications). Documents deviations from the template for this particular supplier.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this checklist assignment becomes active for the supplier. Supports versioning of supplier requirements over time as client programs or compliance rules change.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this checklist assignment is no longer active for the supplier. Nullable for open-ended assignments. Used when supplier program requirements change or supplier is offboarded from a specific client program.']
  mandatory_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the mandatory requirement of the checklist template has been overridden for this specific supplier. True means the checklist is made mandatory (or non-mandatory) for this supplier regardless of the template default. Used for client-specific or supplier-specific compliance exceptions.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Type of staffing program this assignment applies to (union, non-union, healthcare, light_industrial, IT, professional). Supports program-specific checklist requirements where the same supplier may have different onboarding requirements across different workforce programs.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Assignment between supplier and task_checklist. It captures the configuration of which onboarding/offboarding checklists are required for which suppliers based on client program requirements, union status, healthcare credentialing needs, and client-mandated training. Each record links one supplier to one task_checklist with attributes that define when the assignment is effective, whether it has been approved, and any customization notes specific to that supplier-checklist combination.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, suppliers participate in multiple client programs with different compliance requirements, and each program may require multiple checklists (union vs non-union, healthcare credentialing, client-mandated training). One supplier uses multiple checklists across programs; one checklist template applies to multiple approved suppliers. The business actively manages these assignments as supplier program configurations change, client requirements evolve, and new compliance rules are introduced.'
}

Table vendor.supplier_orientation_attendance {
  supplier_orientation_attendance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for this supplier-orientation attendance record. Primary key.']
  orientation_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific orientation session being attended']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier whose workers are attending the orientation session']
  attendance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the supplier\'s attendance at this orientation session. Values: registered, attended, completed, no_show, cancelled. Used for completion tracking and supplier scorecarding.']
  billable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost_allocation for this attendance is billable to the supplier. Used for invoicing and financial reconciliation.']
  cost_allocation decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost amount allocated to this supplier for this orientation session (e.g., per-seat fee, materials cost, facilitator time). Used for billing and cost recovery.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this attendance record was created.']
  no_show_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when attendance_status is no_show. Used for root cause analysis and supplier performance evaluation.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier registered workers for this orientation session. Used for planning and capacity management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this attendance record was last updated.']
  worker_count varchar [note: 'Number of workers from this supplier attending this specific orientation session. Used for capacity planning and cost allocation calculation.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the attendance relationship between suppliers and orientation sessions. It captures supplier participation in scheduled orientation events, tracking registration, attendance outcomes, cost allocation, and no-show patterns used for supplier performance scorecarding. Each record links one supplier to one orientation session with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific attendance event.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, suppliers send workers to multiple orientation sessions over time (different cohorts, different clients, different topics), and each orientation session hosts workers from multiple suppliers (multi-supplier MSP/VMS programs). The business actively manages supplier attendance records for cost allocation, billing, and supplier performance scorecarding based on no-show patterns.'
}

Table contract.msa {
  msa_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for msa']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account that this MSA governs. Links to the client account hierarchy.']
  contract_owner_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: MSAs are managed by internal account managers/contract administrators. Staffing firms track MSA ownership for renewal alerts, compliance monitoring, and client relationship management. contract_owner_']
  docusign_envelope_id varchar [note: 'Unique envelope identifier from DocuSign CLM system for the executed MSA document package.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: MSAs are executed by specific legal entities. Required for financial consolidation, intercompany elimination, tax jurisdiction determination, and regulatory reporting. Staffing firms operate multiple ']
