  task_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed procedural instructions for completing this close task. Provides step-by-step guidance for the assigned owner.']
  task_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the close task (e.g., \'Accrue Payroll Taxes\', \'Reconcile Intercompany Accounts\', \'Prepare Revenue Recognition Journal Entry\').']
  task_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the close task, typically a sequential or hierarchical code used in the close checklist (e.g., \'CL-2024-Q1-010\').']
  task_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering of this task within the close checklist. Used to display tasks in logical execution order.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the close task. Tracks progress through the close checklist and identifies tasks requiring attention.']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the close task by accounting activity type. Determines the nature of work to be performed during the period close process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: accrual|reconciliation|journal_entry|sub_ledger_close|consolidation|reporting|review|approval — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  waiver_date date [note: 'Date when this close task was formally waived. Used for audit trail and control compliance documentation.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for waiving or skipping this close task. Required when task status is set to \'waived\'. Subject to audit review.']

  Note: 'Financial period-end close task record tracking each step in the monthly, quarterly, and annual close process for the staffing enterprise. Captures task name, task type (accrual, reconciliation, journal entry, sub-ledger close, consolidation, reporting), assigned owner, due date, completion date, status (not started, in progress, completed, overdue, waived), dependency on prior tasks, close period reference, and sign-off notes. Manages the structured close checklist ensuring all accounting activities are completed before the period is locked.'
}

Table finance.accrual {
  accrual_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accrual record. Primary key.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounting_period. Business justification: Accrual records should be linked to the accounting period master for proper period management. Currently uses gl_period (STRING) and fiscal_year/quarter (INT) which should be normalized to FK. Removes']
  approver_user_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved the accrual entry, enforcing segregation of duties and approval controls.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific worker assignment related to this accrual, commonly used for payroll and unbilled revenue accruals in staffing.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for which this accrual is recorded, enabling client-level financial analysis.']
  conversion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.conversion. Business justification: Conversion fees are accrued when conversion occurs but invoiced/collected later. Finance tracks accruals by conversion for period-end close, revenue matching, and fall-off risk reserve estimation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire fees are accrued when placement occurs but invoiced/collected later. Finance tracks accruals by specific direct hire placement for period-end close, revenue matching, and guarantee period ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.gl_account']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Accruals reversed when invoices are issued. Core accrual accounting process for unbilled revenue and expense matching.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Accruals for location-specific costs (utilities, on-site coordinator payroll, facilities) require location linkage for accurate site P&L and period-end financial reporting. Essential for location-leve']
  original_accrual_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original accrual record if this is an adjustment or correction entry, maintaining audit lineage.']
  placement_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement record associated with this accrual, used for tracking accruals related to specific candidate placements.']
  reversal_accrual_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on accrual (reversal_accrual_id)']
  reversed_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the journal entry that reversed this accrual, providing audit trail for the reversal transaction.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Payroll and expense accruals for SOW-based engagements must reference the SOW for project cost tracking, margin analysis, and SOW-level P&L reporting. Critical for accurate project accounting and marg']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who prepared and recorded the accrual entry, supporting accountability and audit requirements.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor for vendor accruals, tracking amounts owed to third-party suppliers or subcontractors.']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Vendor cost accruals must reference the vendor agreement for rate validation, contract spend tracking, and vendor liability management. Enables accurate vendor cost accrual and spend reconciliation ag']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.vms_program. Business justification: Accruals for VMS program costs (pending supplier invoices, unbilled MSP fees) need program-level tracking for accurate program P&L and period-end close. Standard practice in VMS/MSP financial manageme']
  accrual_date date [note: 'The date on which the accrual is recorded in the general ledger, representing the business event date for the estimated transaction.']
  accrual_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique accrual reference number used for tracking and reconciliation purposes.']
  accrual_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the accrual record, indicating its state in the accounting workflow.']
  accrual_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the accrual indicating the nature of the estimated expense or revenue. Critical for staffing firms where payroll and billing cycles span period boundaries.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any adjustments made to the original accrual amount, supporting audit trail and variance analysis.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated monetary amount of the accrual recorded in the general ledger before the actual invoice or cash transaction is received.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the accrual was approved, supporting audit trail and compliance requirements.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'The name of the individual who approved the accrual entry for human-readable audit trail purposes.']
  basis_of_estimate varchar [note: 'The methodology used to calculate the accrual amount (e.g., hours × rate for payroll accruals, percentage of revenue, fixed contractual amount).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the accrual record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the accrual amount is denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP).']
  estimate_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The number of hours used in the accrual calculation when basis of estimate is hours × rate, common for payroll accruals in staffing.']
  estimate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage applied to a base amount when the accrual is calculated as a percentage (e.g., bonus accrual as % of revenue).']
  estimate_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The rate per hour or unit used in the accrual calculation when basis of estimate is hours × rate or unit-based.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the accrual amount to the functional currency, if applicable.']
  functional_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The accrual amount converted to the organization\'s functional currency for consolidated financial reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the accrual record was last modified, tracking changes for audit and compliance purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, assumptions, or explanations for the accrual calculation and recording.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the accrual was posted to the general ledger, marking the point of financial commitment.']
  preparer_name varchar [note: 'The name of the individual who prepared the accrual entry for human-readable audit trail purposes.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date on which the accrual is scheduled to be reversed, typically in the subsequent accounting period when the actual transaction is recorded.']
  reversal_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the accrual reversal process, indicating whether the accrual is awaiting reversal, has been reversed, or has been adjusted.']
  source_reference_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the source document or transaction that triggered the accrual, enabling traceability back to the originating event.']
  source_reference_type varchar [note: 'The type of source document or transaction that triggered the accrual (e.g., assignment, pay period, contract, SOW).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system from which the accrual data originated (e.g., Oracle NetSuite ERP, TempWorks Payroll, Salesforce Revenue Cloud).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the accrual record in the source system, enabling cross-system reconciliation and traceability.']

  Note: 'Accrual record capturing estimated expense or revenue amounts recorded in the general ledger before the actual invoice or cash transaction is received. Captures accrual type (payroll accrual, unbilled revenue, vendor accrual, bonus accrual, workers comp accrual), accrual date, GL period, GL account, cost center, accrual amount, currency, basis of estimate (hours × rate, percentage, fixed), source reference (assignment, pay period, contract), reversal date, reversal status, and preparer. Critical for staffing firms where payroll and billing cycles span period boundaries.'
}

Table finance.tax_liability {
  tax_liability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the corporate tax liability record. Primary key for the tax liability entity.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the accounting period in which this tax liability is recognized for general ledger purposes.']
  amended_tax_liability_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on tax_liability (amended_tax_liability_id)']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.business_unit']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Tax liability should link to cost center master for organizational tracking. Currently uses cost_center_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. Removes redundant code column.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Tax liability should link to GL account master for proper accounting classification. Currently uses gl_account_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. Removes redundant code column.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity that holds this tax liability. Links to the corporate legal entity structure.']
  primary_tax_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that created this tax liability record.']
  calculated_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross tax amount calculated by applying the tax rate to the taxable base amount, before any credits or adjustments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax liability record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this tax liability record (typically USD for U.S. operations).']
  extended_due_date date [note: 'New due date if an extension was granted. Null if no extension was requested or granted.']
  extension_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a filing or payment extension was granted by the tax authority for this liability.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the tax return was filed with the tax authority. Null if filing has not yet occurred.']
  filing_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the tax return or filing must be submitted to the tax authority. May differ from payment due date in some jurisdictions.']
  filing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to submit the tax filing to the authority (electronic filing, paper filing, or through a third-party tax service provider).']
  filing_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or confirmation code from the tax authority acknowledging receipt of the tax filing (e.g., IRS confirmation number, state filing ID).']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the tax return filing with the tax authority.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter within the fiscal year (1-4) for quarterly tax reporting and analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which this tax liability belongs, used for financial reporting and compliance tracking.']
  interest_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Interest charges accrued on unpaid or late-paid tax liabilities, calculated according to jurisdiction-specific interest rate rules.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific tax jurisdiction (e.g., state abbreviation, county code, city code). For federal, typically \'US\'. For state, two-letter state code. For local, municipality or county identifier.']
  jurisdiction_level varchar [note: 'Level of government jurisdiction imposing the tax liability (federal, state, local, municipal, or county).']
  jurisdiction_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the tax jurisdiction (e.g., \'United States\', \'California\', \'Los Angeles County\', \'City of San Francisco\').']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax liability record was last modified.']
  net_tax_due decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final net tax amount due after applying all credits and adjustments. This is the amount the enterprise must pay to the tax authority.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes providing additional context, explanations, or special circumstances related to this tax liability.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the tax payment was remitted to the tax authority. Null if payment has not yet been made.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the tax payment must be remitted to the tax authority to avoid penalties and interest.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or confirmation code from the payment transaction, used for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the tax payment obligation indicating whether the liability has been settled.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total penalties assessed for late payment, late filing, underpayment, or other compliance violations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this tax liability record originated (e.g., \'Oracle NetSuite ERP\', \'Tax Compliance Software\').']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this tax liability record in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  tax_adjustments decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional adjustments to the tax liability (positive or negative) due to amendments, corrections, penalties, or interest charges.']
  tax_authority_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or account number assigned by the tax authority for tracking this taxpayer\'s obligations.']
  tax_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the government agency or tax authority to which this liability is owed (e.g., \'Internal Revenue Service\', \'California Franchise Tax Board\', \'City of Los Angeles Tax Collector\').']
  tax_credits_applied decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of tax credits applied to reduce the calculated tax liability (e.g., research and development credits, investment tax credits, foreign tax credits).']
  tax_liability_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the tax liability record, typically generated by the ERP system for tracking and reference purposes.']
  tax_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the tax period for which this liability is calculated.']
  tax_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the tax period for which this liability is calculated. Typically aligns with fiscal or calendar periods.']
  tax_rate decimal(7,5) [note: 'The tax rate applied to the taxable base amount, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.21000 for 21% federal corporate income tax rate).']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the corporate tax obligation. Covers the firm\'s own corporate tax obligations distinct from employer payroll tax (which is owned by the payroll domain). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate_income_tax|sales_tax|use_tax|franchise_tax|gross_receipts_tax|business_license_tax|property_tax|excise_tax — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  taxable_base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The base amount upon which the tax is calculated (e.g., taxable income for corporate income tax, gross receipts for gross receipts tax, sales amount for sales tax).']

  Note: 'Corporate tax liability record tracking the staffing enterprise\'s tax obligations across federal, state, and local jurisdictions — separate from worker-level payroll tax (owned by payroll domain). Captures tax type (corporate income tax, sales tax, use tax, franchise tax, gross receipts tax), jurisdiction (federal, state, local), tax period, taxable base amount, tax rate, calculated tax amount, credits applied, net tax due, payment due date, filing due date, payment status, and filing reference. Covers the firm\'s own corporate tax obligations distinct from employer payroll tax.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fixed asset record. Primary key for the fixed asset master data.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  department_id varchar [note: 'FK to employee.department']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.gl_account']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Fixed asset should be owned by a legal entity for proper asset tracking and financial reporting. No redundant columns to remove as fixed_asset does not currently have legal_entity_code.']
  msa_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the lease agreement if the asset is leased. Links to lease contract master data for payment schedules and terms. Nullable for owned assets.']
  parent_fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fixed_asset (parent_fixed_asset_id)']
  primary_fixed_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee currently responsible for the asset. Used for accountability and asset tracking. Nullable for assets not assigned to individuals.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or supplier from whom the asset was purchased. Links to vendor master data for procurement history.']
  tertiary_fixed_modified_by_user_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the fixed asset record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recognized to date since acquisition. Contra-asset account that reduces the gross book value to net book value.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capitalized cost of the asset at acquisition, including purchase price, delivery, installation, and any costs necessary to bring the asset to its intended use.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the asset was purchased, leased, or placed into service. Marks the start of the depreciation schedule.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the fixed asset by type for accounting and depreciation purposes. Determines applicable depreciation method and useful life.']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the fixed asset (e.g., \'Dell Latitude 5520 Laptop\', \'Herman Miller Aeron Chair\', \'Toyota Camry 2022\').']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fixed asset. Active assets are in service and depreciating; disposed assets have been sold or scrapped; fully depreciated assets remain in service but have zero net book value.']
  asset_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed sub-classification within the asset category (e.g., \'Laptop\', \'Desk\', \'Sedan\', \'Server\', \'HVAC System\').']
  asset_tag_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique identifier affixed to the physical asset for tracking and inventory purposes. Typically a barcode or RFID tag number.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fixed asset record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to allocate the cost of the asset over its useful life. Straight-line is most common for financial reporting; MACRS (Modified Accelerated Cost Recovery System) is used for tax purposes.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date the asset was sold, scrapped, donated, or otherwise removed from service. Nullable for assets still in service.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash or fair value of consideration received from the sale or disposal of the asset. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal.']
  gain_loss_on_disposal decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated difference between disposal proceeds and net book value at disposal date. Positive values represent gains; negative values represent losses. Recognized in the income statement.']
  impairment_date date [note: 'Date the impairment loss was recognized. Nullable for assets not impaired.']
  impairment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset has been tested for impairment and found to have a carrying value exceeding its recoverable amount. Triggers impairment loss recognition.']
  impairment_loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of impairment loss recognized, calculated as the excess of carrying value over fair value. Reduces net book value and is recognized in the income statement.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the insurance policy covering the asset. Used for claims processing and risk management.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance or service performed on the asset. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling.']
  lease_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset is leased (True) or owned (False). Leased assets follow different accounting treatment under ASC 842.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the asset (e.g., \'Dell\', \'Herman Miller\', \'Toyota\', \'Cisco\').']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model or part number for the asset. Used for maintenance, replacement parts, and warranty claims.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fixed asset record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current carrying value of the asset on the balance sheet, calculated as acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation. Also known as carrying amount.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance or service. Used for maintenance planning and budgeting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional information, special handling instructions, or historical context about the asset.']
  physical_location varchar [note: 'Current physical location of the asset (e.g., office address, warehouse, employee assignment, branch location). Used for asset tracking and inventory management.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the purchase order used to acquire the asset. Links to procurement records for audit trail.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life. The amount expected to be recovered through sale or trade-in. Used in depreciation calculations.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number for the asset. Used for warranty tracking, maintenance records, and theft recovery.']
  tax_depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Depreciation method used for tax reporting purposes. Often differs from book depreciation method. MACRS (Modified Accelerated Cost Recovery System) is most common for US tax purposes.']
  useful_life_months varchar [note: 'Expected useful life of the asset in months for depreciation calculation purposes. Determined by IRS guidelines and company policy.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the manufacturer or vendor warranty expires. Used to plan maintenance budgets and replacement decisions.']

  Note: 'Fixed asset master record for capital assets owned or leased by the staffing enterprise — including office equipment, leasehold improvements, furniture, vehicles, and technology infrastructure. Captures asset tag number, asset name, asset category, acquisition date, acquisition cost, useful life (months), depreciation method (straight-line, declining balance, MACRS), accumulated depreciation, net book value, salvage value, disposal date, disposal proceeds, gain/loss on disposal, physical location, assigned cost center, and asset status (active, disposed, fully depreciated). Supports capital expenditure tracking and depreciation scheduling.'
}

Table finance.depreciation_schedule {
  depreciation_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the depreciation schedule record. Primary key for the depreciation schedule entity.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the accounting period for which this depreciation is calculated. Links to the fiscal calendar period.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.business_unit']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  department_id varchar [note: 'FK to employee.department']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fixed asset being depreciated. Links this depreciation schedule entry to the specific asset in the fixed asset register.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the journal entry that posted this depreciation expense to the general ledger. Links the depreciation schedule to the actual GL transaction.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.legal_entity']
  primary_depreciation_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.gl_account']
  primary_depreciation_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this depreciation schedule entry for posting. Supports financial controls and audit requirements.']
  prior_depreciation_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on depreciation_schedule (prior_depreciation_schedule_id)']
  tertiary_depreciation_modified_by_user_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this depreciation schedule record. Supports audit trail and accountability requirements.']
  accumulated_depreciation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total accumulated depreciation for the asset through the end of this period. Represents the cumulative depreciation expense since the asset was placed in service.']
  adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is an adjustment entry to correct a prior period depreciation calculation. True for adjustments, false for regular scheduled depreciation.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any adjustment made to the depreciation calculation. Required when adjustment_flag is true.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this depreciation schedule entry was approved for posting. Null if not yet approved.']
  beginning_net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net book value of the asset at the start of the depreciation period. Calculated as original cost minus accumulated depreciation from prior periods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this depreciation schedule record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this depreciation schedule entry. Ensures proper currency context for financial reporting.']
  depreciation_expense_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The depreciation expense calculated and recognized for this specific period. This amount is posted to the general ledger as a period expense.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'The depreciation calculation method applied for this period. Determines how the asset\'s cost is allocated over its useful life.']
  depreciation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The depreciation rate percentage applied for this period. Used in declining balance and other rate-based depreciation methods.']
  ending_net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net book value of the asset at the end of the depreciation period. Calculated as beginning net book value minus current period depreciation expense.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter (1-4) in which this depreciation period falls. Supports quarterly financial reporting requirements.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this depreciation period falls. Used for annual financial reporting and year-over-year analysis.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this depreciation schedule record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about this depreciation schedule entry. Used for documenting special circumstances, adjustments, or other relevant information.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the depreciation period covered by this schedule entry. Defines the end of the calculation window.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the depreciation period covered by this schedule entry. Defines the beginning of the calculation window.']
  posted_date date [note: 'The date when this depreciation entry was posted to the general ledger. Null if not yet posted.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'The current status of this depreciation schedule entry in the financial posting workflow. Indicates whether the entry has been posted to the general ledger.']
  remaining_useful_life_months varchar [note: 'The remaining useful life of the asset in months at the end of this depreciation period. Used for future depreciation projections.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date when this depreciation entry was reversed. Null if not reversed.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this depreciation entry has been reversed. True if reversed, false otherwise.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why this depreciation entry was reversed. Supports audit trail and financial control requirements.']
  salvage_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life. This amount is not depreciated and represents the expected recovery value.']
  schedule_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the order of this depreciation entry within the asset\'s depreciation lifecycle. Used for chronological ordering and tracking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system that generated or calculated this depreciation schedule entry. Typically the fixed asset or ERP system.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this depreciation schedule entry in the source system. Enables traceability back to the originating system.']
  useful_life_months varchar [note: 'The total useful life of the asset in months as determined at the time of this depreciation calculation. Used in depreciation method calculations.']

  Note: 'Depreciation schedule record capturing the period-by-period depreciation calculation for each fixed asset. Captures fixed asset reference, depreciation period, depreciation method, beginning net book value, depreciation expense for period, accumulated depreciation to date, ending net book value, GL account, cost center, and posting status. Drives the automated depreciation journal entries posted each accounting period and supports fixed asset sub-ledger reconciliation to the GL.'
}

Table finance.legal_entity {
  legal_entity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the legal entity record. Primary key for the legal entity master table.']
  controller_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employee.staff_profile. Business justification: Legal entities require designated financial controller for regulatory filings, audit coordination, financial statement certification, and SOX compliance - critical for multi-entity staffing firms with']
  modified_by_user_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the user who last modified this legal entity record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  parent_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent legal entity if this entity is a subsidiary or branch. Null if this is the top-level parent entity.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the user who created this legal entity record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  accounting_standard varchar [note: 'The primary accounting framework used by the entity for financial reporting. US GAAP is standard for US entities; IFRS may apply for international subsidiaries.']
  consolidation_group varchar [note: 'Identifier for the group of entities that are consolidated together for financial reporting purposes. Used to aggregate financials across related entities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this legal entity record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  dissolution_date date [note: 'The date the entity was legally dissolved, terminated, or ceased operations. Null if the entity is still active.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit unique identifier assigned by Dun & Bradstreet to identify the business entity. Used for credit reporting, vendor registration, and government contracting.']
  ein varchar [note: 'Federal tax identification number issued by the IRS. Also known as Federal Tax ID. Used for all federal tax filings, payroll tax reporting, and IRS correspondence. Format: XX-XXXXXXX.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'The legal structure of the entity as defined by state and federal law. Determines tax treatment, liability, and regulatory obligations.']
  fiscal_year_end_day varchar [note: 'The day of the month on which the fiscal year ends, represented as an integer 1-31. Combined with fiscal_year_end_month to determine the exact fiscal year end date.']
  fiscal_year_end_month varchar [note: 'The month in which the entitys fiscal year ends, represented as an integer 1-12 (1=January, 12=December). Used to determine accounting period alignment and annual close schedules.']
  functional_currency_code varchar [note: 'The primary currency in which the entity conducts business and maintains its books and records. Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP).']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'The date the entity was legally formed or incorporated as recorded in the state filing. This is the official formation date for the legal entity.']
  intercompany_elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether intercompany transactions involving this entity should be eliminated during consolidation. True if elimination is required; False otherwise.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the legal entity is currently active and operational. True if active; False if dissolved, inactive, or dormant.']
  is_disregarded_entity boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity is treated as a disregarded entity for federal tax purposes (e.g., single-member LLC). True if disregarded; False if separate tax entity.']
  is_public_company boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity is a publicly traded company subject to SEC reporting requirements. True if public; False if private.']
  legal_entity_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used internally to identify the legal entity in financial systems, general ledger, and reporting. Typically 2-10 characters.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the incorporated business entity as registered with the state or jurisdiction of incorporation. This is the official name used in contracts, tax filings, and regulatory submissions.']
  lei_code varchar [note: 'Twenty-character alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the legal entity in global financial markets. Required for entities engaging in financial transactions subject to regulatory reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this legal entity record was last modified. Updated whenever any field in the record changes.']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit code that classifies the entitys primary business activity. Used for statistical analysis, regulatory reporting, and industry benchmarking. Staffing agencies typically use 561320 (Temporary Help Services).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the legal entity. Used for documenting exceptions, historical context, or operational guidance.']
  primary_business_description varchar [note: 'A brief narrative description of the entitys primary business activities, products, and services. Used for internal documentation and external disclosures.']
  registered_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the official registered address for the legal entity as filed with the state. This is the address where legal notices and service of process are delivered.']
  registered_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the registered address (suite, floor, building, etc.). Optional field.']
  registered_agent_name varchar [note: 'The name of the individual or company designated as the registered agent for service of process in the state of incorporation.']
  registered_city varchar [note: 'City of the registered address.']
  registered_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the registered address. Typically USA for domestic entities.']
  registered_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code of the registered address. Format: XXXXX or XXXXX-XXXX.']
  registered_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code of the registered address.']
  sec_cik_number varchar [note: 'Ten-digit unique identifier assigned by the SEC to entities that file disclosure documents. Required for public companies and certain private filers.']
  sic_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit code that classifies the entitys industry. Legacy classification system still used by some regulatory agencies. Staffing agencies typically use 7363 (Help Supply Services).']
  state_of_incorporation varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code where the entity was legally incorporated or formed. Determines which state corporate law governs the entity.']
  stock_exchange varchar [note: 'The name of the stock exchange where the entitys shares are traded, if applicable. Examples: NYSE, NASDAQ, LSE. Null if not publicly traded.']
  stock_ticker_symbol varchar [note: 'The ticker symbol used to identify the entitys publicly traded stock. Null if not publicly traded.']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The primary tax jurisdiction to which the entity reports and pays income taxes. Typically the state of incorporation or principal place of business.']

  Note: 'Legal entity master record for each incorporated business entity within the staffing enterprise group. Captures legal entity name, entity type (corporation, LLC, partnership, branch), EIN/tax ID, state of incorporation, registered address, functional currency, fiscal year end, consolidation group, parent entity, intercompany elimination flag, GAAP/IFRS reporting standard, active status, and formation/dissolution dates. SSOT for the corporate structure used in intercompany accounting, consolidated financial reporting, and regulatory filings.'
}

Table finance.budget_allocation {
  budget_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this budget allocation record. Primary key for the budget_allocation association.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the budget being allocated. References the master budget record that defines the total budgeted amount, fiscal year, budget type, and approval status.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the department receiving the budget allocation. References the organizational department that will consume and be accountable for the allocated budget amount.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount allocated from the budget to this specific department. Represents the portion of the total budget assigned to this department for the allocation period.']
  allocated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the finance user who created or approved this budget allocation. Provides accountability for allocation decisions.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'The date when this budget allocation was formally created or approved. Used for audit trail and allocation history tracking.']
  allocation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or justifications for this specific allocation. Captures business rationale, special conditions, or allocation assumptions for this department.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the total budget amount allocated to this department. Used for proportional budget distribution and variance analysis. Should sum to 100% across all departments for a given budget.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this budget allocation. Draft indicates pending approval, Approved indicates ready for use, Active indicates in-use, Frozen indicates temporarily locked, Closed indicates period ended, Revised indicates superseded by a new allocation.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'The financial category of this budget allocation. Classifies the allocation by its purpose (e.g., Headcount, SG&A, Technology, Facilities). Enables category-level tracking and reporting by department.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this budget allocation record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for allocation creation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this budget allocation expires or is superseded. Defines the end of the period during which the department can utilize this allocation. Null indicates an open-ended allocation.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this budget allocation becomes effective. Defines when the department can begin utilizing the allocated budget amount.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this budget allocation is currently active and in effect. True means the allocation is operational, false means it has been superseded or closed.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this budget allocation record was last updated or modified. Tracks changes to allocation amounts, dates, or status.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who last modified this budget allocation record. Provides accountability for allocation changes.']
  variance_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable variance percentage threshold for this specific allocation. Overrides the budget-level threshold when department-specific variance tolerances are needed.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the allocation of budget amounts across organizational departments within Staffing Hr. It captures the distribution of financial budgets (operating, capital, headcount) to specific departments for a defined time period. Each record links one budget to one department with allocation-specific attributes including allocated amount, allocation percentage, effective dates, and budget category. This enables multi-department budget distribution, departmental cost tracking, and variance analysis by department.. Existence Justification: In staffing firms, budgets are allocated across multiple departments to distribute financial resources for different purposes (headcount, operations, technology, facilities). A single enterprise or division-level budget is split among multiple departments (recruiting, sales, operations, support), and each department receives allocations from multiple budget categories and fiscal periods. The business actively manages these allocations with specific amounts, percentages, effective dates, and approval workflows.'
}

Table finance.fiscal_calendar {
  fiscal_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fiscal_calendar']
  prior_fiscal_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fiscal_calendar (prior_fiscal_calendar_id)']
  business_days_in_period varchar [note: 'The total number of business days in the fiscal period. Used for productivity calculations and service level agreement tracking.']
  calendar_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date in Gregorian calendar format. Bridge between fiscal periods and calendar dates for transaction mapping.']
  calendar_month varchar [note: 'The Gregorian calendar month number (1-12). Used for calendar-based reporting and reconciliation with fiscal periods.']
  calendar_month_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the Gregorian calendar month. Used for user-friendly reporting and dashboards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: January|February|March|April|May|June|July|August|September|October|November|December — promote to reference product]']
  calendar_quarter varchar [note: 'The Gregorian calendar quarter (1-4). Used for calendar-based quarterly reporting and reconciliation with fiscal quarters.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'The Gregorian calendar year (YYYY). Used for calendar-based reporting and reconciliation with fiscal periods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fiscal calendar record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  day_of_month varchar [note: 'The day number within the calendar month (1-31). Used for monthly reporting and date-based business rules.']
  day_of_week varchar [note: 'The name of the day of the week for the calendar date. Used for business day calculations and operational scheduling.']
  day_of_week_number varchar [note: 'The numeric representation of the day of week (1=Monday through 7=Sunday per ISO 8601). Used for business day logic and reporting.']
  day_of_year varchar [note: 'The day number within the calendar year (1-365 or 1-366 for leap years). Used for year-to-date calculations and trend analysis.']
  days_in_period varchar [note: 'The total number of days in the fiscal period. Used for pro-rata calculations and period normalization in financial analysis.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period or month within the fiscal year (1-12 or 1-13 for 13-period calendars). Primary unit for monthly financial close and reporting.']
  fiscal_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the fiscal period or month. Defines the closing boundary for monthly financial close and reporting.']
  fiscal_period_name varchar [note: 'The display name or label for the fiscal period (e.g., P01, Period 1, Jan 2024). Used for user-friendly reporting and financial close communication.']
  fiscal_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the fiscal period or month. Defines the opening boundary for monthly financial transactions and accruals.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter within the fiscal year (1-4). Used for quarterly financial reporting and performance analysis.']
  fiscal_quarter_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the fiscal quarter. Used for quarterly financial close and reporting cutoff.']
  fiscal_quarter_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the fiscal quarter. Used for quarterly financial period boundary identification.']
  fiscal_week varchar [note: 'The fiscal week number within the fiscal year (1-52 or 1-53). Used for weekly payroll cycles and operational reporting in staffing operations.']
  fiscal_week_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the fiscal week. Used for weekly payroll cutoff and operational reporting in staffing operations.']
  fiscal_week_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the fiscal week. Used for weekly payroll processing and operational reporting cycles in staffing operations.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this calendar period belongs. Represents the annual financial reporting cycle for the staffing enterprise.']
  fiscal_year_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the fiscal year to which this calendar record belongs. Defines the close of the annual financial reporting cycle.']
  fiscal_year_name varchar [note: 'The display name or label for the fiscal year (e.g., FY2024, 2024-2025). Used for user-friendly reporting and financial statement presentation.']
  fiscal_year_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the fiscal year to which this calendar record belongs. Defines the beginning of the annual financial reporting cycle.']
  holiday_name varchar [note: 'The name of the holiday if the date is a recognized holiday. Used for payroll processing and employee communication.']
  is_business_day boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the calendar date is a business day (True) or non-business day (False). Used for business day calculations in financial processing and service level agreements.']
  is_holiday boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the calendar date is a recognized company or statutory holiday. Used for payroll premium calculations and business day adjustments.']
  is_period_end boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the calendar date is the last day of a fiscal period. Used for financial close processes and period-end reporting triggers.']
  is_quarter_end boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the calendar date is the last day of a fiscal quarter. Used for quarterly financial close and regulatory reporting.']
  is_weekend boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the calendar date falls on a weekend (Saturday or Sunday). Used for operational scheduling and payroll processing.']
  is_year_end boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the calendar date is the last day of a fiscal year. Used for annual financial close and year-end reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fiscal calendar record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  period_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the fiscal period. Open periods accept transactions, closed periods are locked for reporting, future periods are not yet active.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for fiscal_calendar. Referenced by fiscal_calendar_id.'
}

Table finance.purchase_order {
  purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for purchase_order']
  amended_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on purchase_order (amended_purchase_order_id)']
  approved_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or manager who approved this purchase order.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center or department to which this purchase order expense will be allocated.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Purchase orders need GL account linkage for proper accounting classification. The schema currently has \'general_ledger_account_id\' which is a domain-level FK pattern (general_ledger is the domain name']
  requisition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating purchase requisition that initiated this purchase order.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or procurement specialist responsible for creating and managing this purchase order.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier from whom goods or services are being purchased.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date when goods were received or services were completed.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when the purchase order was formally approved by authorized personnel.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The first line of the billing address for invoice and payment purposes.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'The second line of the billing address, typically used for suite, building, or department information.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'The city for the billing address.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the billing address.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code for the billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'The state or province for the billing address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this purchase order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the purchase order is denominated.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total discount amount applied to this purchase order, including volume discounts and promotional offers.']
  is_blanket_release boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this purchase order is a release against a blanket purchase agreement.']
  is_urgent boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this purchase order requires expedited processing and delivery.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this purchase order record was last updated or modified.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The agreed-upon payment terms with the vendor, such as Net 30, Net 60, or 2/10 Net 30.']
  po_date date [note: 'The date when the purchase order was officially created and issued to the vendor.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable purchase order number used for vendor communication and document tracking.']
  po_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order in the procurement workflow.']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase order based on procurement method and usage pattern.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'The date the vendor has committed to deliver the goods or complete the services.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the buyer requests delivery of goods or completion of services.']
  shipping_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The first line of the delivery address where goods should be shipped.']
  shipping_address_line2 varchar [note: 'The second line of the delivery address, typically used for suite, building, or department information.']
  shipping_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The shipping and handling charges associated with this purchase order.']
  shipping_city varchar [note: 'The city where goods should be delivered.']
  shipping_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the delivery destination.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'The method of shipment or delivery specified for this purchase order, such as ground, air, or freight.']
  shipping_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code for the delivery address.']
  shipping_state_province varchar [note: 'The state or province where goods should be delivered.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Additional instructions, notes, or requirements communicated to the vendor regarding delivery, handling, or service execution.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount of all line items before taxes, fees, and discounts are applied.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount applied to this purchase order.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final total amount of the purchase order including all taxes, fees, shipping, and discounts.']
  vendor_contact_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the vendor contact person for communication regarding this purchase order.']
  vendor_contact_name varchar [note: 'The name of the primary contact person at the vendor organization for this purchase order.']
  vendor_contact_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number of the vendor contact person for inquiries and coordination.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for purchase_order. Referenced by purchase_order_id.'
}

Table finance.payment_batch {
  payment_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_batch']
  approved_by_user_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this payment batch for processing.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the company bank account from which payments in this batch will be disbursed.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department or cost center responsible for this payment batch.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Payment batches must be associated with a legal entity for proper financial reporting and compliance. The schema currently has \'company_id\' which is semantically equivalent to legal_entity_id in finan']
  reprocessed_payment_batch_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_batch (reprocessed_payment_batch_id)']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this payment batch in the system.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment batch requires explicit approval before processing, based on business rules or payment amount thresholds.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment batch was approved for processing by an authorized approver.']
  auto_generated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment batch was automatically generated by the system (e.g., recurring payroll) or manually created by a user.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the payment batch, typically used in external communications and reporting.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment batch based on the nature of payments included (e.g., payroll for employee wages, vendor for supplier payments, contractor for contingent worker payments).']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when all payments in this batch were successfully processed and the batch was marked as complete.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'The confirmation or reference number provided by the bank or payment processor upon successful receipt and processing of the payment batch.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment batch record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which payments in this batch are denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP).']
  description varchar [note: 'A free-text description providing additional context or details about the purpose or contents of this payment batch.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed error message or reason for failure if the payment batch processing encountered errors or was rejected.']
  failed_payment_count varchar [note: 'The number of payment transactions in this batch that failed to process due to errors or validation issues.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code to which the total batch amount will be posted for accounting purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment batch record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes or comments added by users regarding this payment batch, used for operational communication and documentation.']
  payment_file_name varchar [note: 'The name of the electronic payment file generated for this batch and transmitted to the bank or payment processor.']
  payment_file_path varchar [note: 'The storage location or file path where the payment file for this batch is stored in the system.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The financial instrument or mechanism used to execute payments in this batch (e.g., ACH for automated clearing house, wire for wire transfer, direct deposit for employee payroll).']
  payment_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period for which payments in this batch are being made (e.g., the end of a pay period for payroll batches).']
  payment_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period for which payments in this batch are being made (e.g., the beginning of a pay period for payroll batches).']
  priority varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned to this payment batch, determining the order in which it should be processed relative to other batches.']
  processing_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the payment batch was processed by the financial system.']
  reconciled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment batch was successfully reconciled against bank statements and accounting records.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the reconciliation process for this payment batch, indicating whether payments have been matched against bank statements and accounting records.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The date on which payments in this batch are scheduled to be processed and disbursed to recipients.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment batch indicating its position in the payment processing workflow.']
  successful_payment_count varchar [note: 'The number of payment transactions in this batch that were successfully processed and disbursed.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of all payments included in this batch, representing the gross amount to be disbursed.']
  total_payment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual payment transactions included in this batch.']
  transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the payment file for this batch was transmitted to the bank or payment processor.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_batch. Referenced by payment_batch_id.'
}

Table finance.business_unit {
  business_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for business_unit']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity under which this business unit operates for regulatory and tax purposes.']
  parent_business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent business unit in the organizational hierarchy. Null for top-level units.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who serves as the manager or director of this business unit.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the business unit headquarters or primary location.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, floor, or building information.']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the business unit for reporting and integration purposes.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the business unit is located.']
  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to consolidate this business unit into group financial statements.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'General ledger cost center code assigned to this business unit for expense tracking and allocation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code where the business unit is domiciled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this business unit record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the functional currency of the business unit.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the business unit purpose, scope, and operational focus.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the business unit ceased operations or was closed. Null for currently active units.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the business unit became operational and active in the organization.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for business unit correspondence and inquiries.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the business unit, if applicable.']
  gl_account_segment varchar [note: 'The segment or dimension value in the chart of accounts that represents this business unit for financial reporting.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'NAICS or SIC code representing the primary industry classification of the business unit.']
  is_intercompany_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this business unit participates in intercompany transactions and eliminations.']
  is_revenue_generating boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this business unit directly generates revenue or is a support/overhead unit.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this business unit record was last modified.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full legal or operational name of the business unit.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the business unit.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the business unit location.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'General ledger profit center code assigned to this business unit for revenue and profitability tracking.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or display name of the business unit used in reports and user interfaces.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the business unit is located.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the business unit in its lifecycle.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number or employer identification number assigned to the business unit by tax authorities.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the business unit location used for scheduling and reporting.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the business unit indicating its organizational role and hierarchy level.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for business_unit. Referenced by business_unit_id.'
}

Table finance.requisition {
  requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for requisition']
  amended_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on requisition (amended_requisition_id)']
  account_manager_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the account manager responsible for the client relationship associated with this requisition.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when the requisition was approved for processing.']
  approved_by_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who approved this requisition.']
  background_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a background check is required for candidates filling this requisition.']
  bill_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'The hourly or daily rate charged to the client for the candidate\'s services.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason provided when a requisition is cancelled, such as client budget constraints or position elimination.']
  candidate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate being placed through this requisition.']
  client_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client organization requesting the staffing requisition.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this requisition was formally closed, either due to successful fill or cancellation.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'The cost center code used for financial tracking and allocation of requisition expenses.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this requisition record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this requisition (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  department varchar [note: 'The client department or business unit where the candidate will be assigned.']
  drug_test_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a drug screening test is required for candidates filling this requisition.']
  end_date date [note: 'The scheduled or actual end date for the candidate\'s assignment or employment. Nullable for permanent placements.']
  estimated_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The estimated total hours for the assignment or project duration.']
  estimated_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'The projected total revenue expected from this requisition based on bill rate and estimated hours or duration.']
  fill_date date [note: 'The date when the requisition was successfully filled with a candidate placement.']
  general_ledger_account varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code to which revenue and expenses from this requisition are posted.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this requisition generates billable revenue to the client (true) or is non-billable (false).']
  is_overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for overtime compensation under applicable labor laws.']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the job responsibilities, requirements, and qualifications for the requisition.']
  job_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order that this requisition fulfills.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The job title or position name for the requisition.']
  markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage markup applied to the pay rate to determine the bill rate, representing the staffing agency\'s margin.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this requisition record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes or comments related to the requisition, including special instructions or client preferences.']
  number_of_positions varchar [note: 'The quantity of positions or candidates required to fulfill this requisition.']
  pay_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'The hourly or daily rate paid to the candidate for their services.']
  placement_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'The one-time fee charged to the client for permanent or contract-to-hire placements, typically a percentage of annual salary.']
  placement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement record associated with this requisition.']
  positions_filled varchar [note: 'The number of positions that have been successfully filled for this requisition.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The urgency or priority level assigned to filling this requisition (low, medium, high, urgent, critical).']
  recruiter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal recruiter responsible for filling this requisition.']
  requisition_date date [note: 'The date when the requisition was formally created or submitted by the client.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the requisition, typically formatted as REQ-XXXXXXXX.']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the requisition (draft, submitted, approved, in-progress, filled, cancelled, closed).']
  requisition_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the requisition based on employment arrangement (temporary, permanent, contract, contract-to-hire, temp-to-perm, project-based).']
  start_date date [note: 'The scheduled start date for the candidate\'s assignment or employment.']
  work_location varchar [note: 'The physical or remote work location where the candidate will perform their duties.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for requisition. Referenced by requisition_id.'
}

Table employee.staff_profile {
  staff_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the internal employee staff profile record. Primary key for the staff_profile product.']
  job_title_id varchar [note: 'FK to employee.job_title']
  manager_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on staff_profile (manager_staff_profile_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the employee\'s home residence.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number of the employee\'s home residence.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the employee\'s home residence.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the employee\'s home residence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this staff profile record was first created in the system.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Employee\'s date of birth used for benefits eligibility, age verification, and compliance reporting.']
  disability_status varchar [note: 'Employee\'s self-identified disability status for OFCCP Section 503 reporting and reasonable accommodation purposes.']
  eeo_job_category varchar [note: 'EEO-1 job category classification for federal compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Executive/Senior Level Officials and Managers|First/Mid-Level Officials and Managers|Professionals|Technicians|Sales Workers|Administrative Support Workers|Craft Workers|Operatives|Laborers and Helpers|Service Workers — promote to reference product]']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person to contact in case of employee emergency.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the emergency contact person.']
  emergency_contact_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the emergency contact to the employee.']
  employee_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier assigned to the employee by the organization. Used for payroll, HR systems, and operational identification.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee within the organization.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employee\'s work arrangement based on hours and commitment level.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Classification determining whether the employee is exempt or non-exempt from overtime pay requirements under FLSA.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Employee\'s self-identified gender for EEOC reporting and diversity analytics.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee was originally hired or most recently rehired by the organization.']
  i9_verification_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s I-9 employment eligibility verification was completed.']
  i9_work_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Employee\'s work authorization status as verified through I-9 employment eligibility verification process.']
  legal_first_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s legal first name as it appears on government-issued identification and tax documents.']
  legal_last_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s legal last name (surname) as it appears on government-issued identification and tax documents.']
  legal_middle_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s legal middle name or initial as it appears on government-issued identification.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Employee\'s mobile phone number for business and emergency contact purposes.']
  original_hire_date date [note: 'Date of the employee\'s very first hire with the organization, used for seniority and tenure calculations when rehire_flag is true.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the employee is paid, indicating the pay period structure.']
  pay_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Employee\'s current compensation rate, expressed as hourly wage or annual salary depending on employment type.']
  personal_email varchar [note: 'Employee\'s personal email address used for non-work communications and emergency contact purposes.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the employee\'s home residence.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'Name the employee prefers to be called in day-to-day business interactions, which may differ from legal name.']
  rehire_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is a rehire (previously employed and returned) or a new hire.']
  ssn_masked varchar [note: 'Masked Social Security Number showing only the last four digits for identification purposes while protecting full SSN.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the employee\'s home residence, used for tax withholding and compliance.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended or will end. Null for active employees.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason for the employee\'s termination or separation from the organization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this staff profile record was last modified.']
  veteran_status varchar [note: 'Employee\'s veteran status for OFCCP VETS-4212 reporting and affirmative action compliance.']
  work_email varchar [note: 'Corporate email address assigned to the employee for business communications and system access.']
  work_phone varchar [note: 'Business phone number assigned to the employee, including desk phone or direct line.']

  Note: 'SSOT master record for every internal employee at Staffing Hr — recruiters, account managers, sourcing specialists, operations staff, and corporate personnel. Captures core identity: legal name, preferred name, employee number, personal email, work email, work phone, mobile phone, date of birth, gender, SSN (masked), home address, emergency contact name, emergency contact phone, hire date, rehire flag, termination date, employment status (active, on leave, terminated), employment type (full-time, part-time, contract), FLSA classification, EEO job category, veteran status, disability status, and I-9 work authorization status. This is the authoritative identity record for all internal workforce — distinct from candidate.profile (external talent supply) and placement.assignment (worker deployments).'
}

Table employee.job_title {
  job_title_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job title record. Primary key for the job title reference master.']
  parent_job_title_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on job_title (parent_job_title_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job title record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for salary amounts. Supports multi-currency compensation for international operations.']
  eeo1_category varchar [note: 'Standard EEO-1 job category code used for federal compliance reporting to the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission. Required for annual EEO-1 Component 1 filings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Executive/Senior Level Officials and Managers|First/Mid-Level Officials and Managers|Professionals|Technicians|Sales Workers|Administrative Support Workers|Craft Workers|Operatives|Laborers and Helpers|Service Workers — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this job title became or will become active and available for use in the organization. Supports time-based job architecture changes and organizational restructuring.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when this job title was or will be retired from active use. Null for currently active titles. Used for historical reporting and compliance with record retention requirements.']
