  period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period covered by the survey (e.g., the end of the quarter or year being assessed).']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period covered by the survey (e.g., the beginning of the quarter or year being assessed).']
  recognition_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the employee\'s satisfaction with recognition, appreciation, and acknowledgment of their contributions. Typically on a scale of 0-100.']
  reminder_count varchar [note: 'The number of reminder notifications sent to the employee to encourage survey completion.']
  resources_tools_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the employee\'s satisfaction with the resources, tools, technology, and support provided to perform their job effectively. Typically on a scale of 0-100.']
  response_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or platform through which the employee submitted their survey response (web portal, mobile app, email link, kiosk, or paper form).']
  response_completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of survey questions answered by the employee, calculated as (answered questions / total questions) * 100. Used to assess response quality and completeness.']
  response_date date [note: 'The date on which the employee submitted their survey response. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the survey response indicating whether the employee completed the survey, partially completed it, has not started, or declined to participate.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the employee completed and submitted the survey response, including time zone information.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system from which this survey response record was extracted (e.g., SAP SuccessFactors, Qualtrics, Culture Amp).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this survey response record in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  survey_name varchar [note: 'The name or title of the survey instrument administered (e.g., \'Annual Engagement Survey 2024\', \'Q2 Pulse Check\', \'New Hire 90-Day Feedback\').']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Category of survey administered. Distinguishes between annual engagement surveys, pulse checks, onboarding satisfaction surveys, exit surveys, manager effectiveness assessments, culture surveys, and ad-hoc surveys. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual_engagement|pulse|onboarding_satisfaction|exit|manager_effectiveness|culture|ad_hoc — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tenure_months varchar [note: 'The employee\'s tenure with the organization in months at the time of the survey response. Used for tenure-based engagement analysis.']
  vendor varchar [note: 'The name of the third-party survey platform or vendor used to administer the survey (e.g., Qualtrics, Culture Amp, Glint, SurveyMonkey).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the survey instrument used, allowing tracking of changes to survey questions and methodology over time.']
  work_life_balance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the employee\'s satisfaction with work-life balance, flexibility, workload, and ability to manage personal and professional responsibilities. Typically on a scale of 0-100.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing internal employee engagement survey responses and pulse check results. Tracks survey name, survey type (annual engagement, pulse, onboarding satisfaction, exit), survey period, response date, overall engagement score, eNPS (Employee Net Promoter Score), category scores (manager effectiveness, career development, compensation satisfaction, culture, work-life balance), response anonymization flag, and whether the employee consented to attribution. Supports internal retention analytics, manager effectiveness programs, and culture initiatives at Staffing Hr.'
}

Table employee.office_location {
  office_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the office location. Primary key for the office location entity.']
  parent_location_office_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent office location in a hierarchical structure. For example, a satellite office may roll up to a regional hub, which rolls up to corporate HQ. Supports multi-level organizational reporting.']
  parent_office_location_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on office_location (parent_office_location_id)']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal employee who serves as the branch manager or location leader responsible for this office. Used for organizational hierarchy and accountability reporting.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the office location including street number and street name. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, floor, building, or unit information. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  ats_instance_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the ATS instance or database partition that this office location uses in Bullhorn or other applicant tracking systems. Some large staffing firms run multiple ATS instances by region or division.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the office location is situated.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) cost center code assigned to this office location for financial accounting and expense allocation. Used in payroll burden allocation and P&L reporting by location.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the office is located (e.g., \'USA\', \'CAN\', \'MEX\'). Critical for international tax compliance and regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this office location record was first created in the system. Part of standard audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for financial amounts associated with this location (e.g., \'USD\', \'CAD\', \'MXN\'). Required for multi-currency financial consolidation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this office location ceased operations or was closed. Null for currently active locations. Supports historical reporting and location lifecycle tracking.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this office location became operational and active in the system. Supports historical reporting and location lifecycle tracking.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'General business email address for the office location (e.g., chicago.branch@staffinghr.com). Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the office location if applicable. Still used for certain compliance and legal document transmission. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  is_client_facing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location regularly hosts client meetings and has client-facing facilities. True for branch offices with meeting rooms, false for back-office or remote locations.']
  is_remote_work_hub boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is designated as a remote work hub or virtual office with no physical branch presence. True for remote hubs, false for physical offices.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the office location in decimal degrees. Used for mapping, territory assignment, and proximity-based analytics.']
  lease_end_date date [note: 'Date when the current lease or occupancy agreement expires. Used for lease renewal planning and facility cost forecasting.']
  lease_start_date date [note: 'Date when the lease or occupancy agreement for this location began. Critical for lease accounting and renewal planning.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Type of occupancy arrangement for the office location. Owned properties are company-owned, leased properties are under long-term lease, subleased properties are rented from another tenant, coworking spaces are shared flexible workspaces, and virtual locations have no physical presence.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the office location used in operational systems and reporting. Typically a short alphanumeric code assigned by corporate for branch identification.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the office location (e.g., \'Chicago Downtown Branch\', \'Corporate Headquarters\', \'Dallas Regional Hub\').']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the office location by its operational function within the organization. Branch offices serve local markets, corporate HQ houses executive leadership, regional hubs coordinate multi-branch operations, remote hubs support distributed workforce, satellite offices provide limited local presence, and shared services centers handle back-office functions.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the office location in decimal degrees. Used for mapping, territory assignment, and proximity-based analytics.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market or sales region that this office serves (e.g., \'Midwest\', \'Northeast\', \'Southwest\'). Used for regional performance reporting and market segmentation analysis.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this office location record was last updated. Part of standard audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  monthly_rent_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monthly rent or lease payment amount for the office location in the local currency. Confidential business financial data used for cost center budgeting and P&L analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional information about the office location such as special access instructions, facility features, or operational notes.']
  office_location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the office location. Active locations are fully operational, inactive locations are not currently in use, pending opening locations are being set up, temporarily closed locations are expected to reopen, permanently closed locations will not reopen, and under renovation locations are being upgraded.']
  parking_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parking facilities are available at or near this office location. Relevant for employee commuting and client visit planning.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary business phone number for the office location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code or postal code for the office location. Supports payroll tax jurisdiction determination and geographic reporting. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  public_transit_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the office location is accessible via public transportation. Relevant for employee commuting and sustainability reporting.']
  seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of employees that can be seated at this location. Supports headcount planning and space utilization analysis.']
  square_footage varchar [note: 'Total usable square footage of the office space. Used for facility cost allocation, capacity planning, and lease management.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code where the office is located (e.g., \'CA\', \'TX\', \'NY\'). Used for payroll tax jurisdiction determination and compliance reporting.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the office location (e.g., \'America/Chicago\', \'America/New_York\'). Critical for scheduling, timesheet management, and workforce dispatch across multiple locations.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all physical Staffing Hr branch offices, corporate offices, and remote work hubs where internal employees are based. Captures location name, location code, address (street, city, state, zip, country), location type (branch office, corporate HQ, regional hub, remote), phone number, fax number, time zone, market region, branch manager employee reference, and active status. Supports employee location assignment, payroll tax jurisdiction determination, and branch-level performance reporting.'
}

Table employee.benefit_plan {
  benefit_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for benefit_plan']
  superseded_benefit_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on benefit_plan (superseded_benefit_plan_id)']
  benefit_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of salary or wages replaced by the benefit. Applicable to disability and retirement plans. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 60.00 for 60%).']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance carrier or provider administering the benefit plan.']
  carrier_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy or contract number assigned by the insurance carrier for this benefit plan.']
  cobra_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit plan is eligible for COBRA continuation coverage after employment termination.']
  coinsurance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of costs the employee pays after meeting the deductible. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 20.00 for 20%).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the benefit plan with applicable regulations such as ERISA, ACA, and HIPAA.']
  contribution_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which employee and employer contributions are made for this benefit plan.']
  copay_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed amount the employee pays for specific services such as doctor visits or prescriptions.']
  coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum benefit amount or coverage limit provided by the plan. Applicable to life insurance and disability plans.']
  coverage_level varchar [note: 'Scope of coverage provided by the benefit plan indicating who is eligible for benefits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this benefit plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this benefit plan.']
  deductible_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual deductible amount that must be met before the benefit plan begins paying claims. Applicable to health, dental, and vision plans.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the benefit plan including coverage details, terms, and conditions.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit plan is no longer available for new enrollments. Nullable for open-ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit plan becomes active and available for employee enrollment.']
  eligibility_waiting_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days an employee must wait after hire date before becoming eligible to enroll in this benefit plan.']
  employee_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount the employee contributes per pay period for this benefit plan.']
  employer_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount the employer contributes per pay period for this benefit plan.']
  employer_match_limit_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage of salary up to which the employer will match contributions in retirement plans. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 6.00 for 6%).']
  employer_match_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of employee contributions that the employer will match in retirement plans. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 50.00 for 50%).']
  enrollment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether enrollment in this benefit plan is voluntary, mandatory, or automatic for eligible employees.']
  fsa_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit plan allows employees to contribute to a Flexible Spending Account for eligible expenses.']
  hsa_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit plan qualifies as a high-deductible health plan eligible for HSA contributions.']
  last_compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit plan was last reviewed for regulatory compliance.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that last modified this benefit plan record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this benefit plan record was last modified in the system.']
  network_type varchar [note: 'Type of provider network associated with the benefit plan. Applicable to health, dental, and vision plans.']
  out_of_pocket_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount an employee will pay out-of-pocket in a plan year before the plan covers 100% of costs. Applicable to health plans.']
  plan_administrator_contact varchar [note: 'Contact information (phone or email) for the benefit plan administrator.']
  plan_administrator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or organization responsible for administering the benefit plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the benefit plan used in HR systems and employee communications.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the benefit plan as displayed to employees and in HR documentation.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the benefit plan indicating availability for enrollment and administration.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of benefit plan that segments the population of available benefits.']
  plan_year_end_date date [note: 'End date of the benefit plan year used for tracking deductibles, out-of-pocket maximums, and annual limits.']
  plan_year_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the benefit plan year used for tracking deductibles, out-of-pocket maximums, and annual limits.']
  summary_plan_description_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the Summary Plan Description document required by ERISA.']
  vesting_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months an employee must participate in the plan before employer contributions become fully owned. Applicable to retirement plans.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created this benefit plan record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for benefit_plan. Referenced by benefit_plan_id.'
}

Table employee.compensation_plan {
  compensation_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for compensation_plan']
  created_by_employee_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who created this compensation plan record.']
  last_modified_by_employee_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who last modified this compensation plan record.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or executive who approved this compensation plan. Null if not yet approved or approval not required.']
  superseded_compensation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on compensation_plan (superseded_compensation_plan_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this compensation plan was approved for use. Null if not yet approved or approval not required.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether management approval is required before assigning this plan to an employee.']
  base_salary_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed annual base salary amount for this compensation plan. Null if plan does not include base salary component.']
  bonus_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees on this plan are eligible for discretionary or performance bonuses.']
  clawback_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after placement during which commission can be clawed back if placement fails. Null if no clawback provision.']
  clawback_provision_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether commissions or bonuses can be reclaimed if placement fails or client cancels within a specified period.']
  commission_basis varchar [note: 'The revenue metric upon which commission is calculated (gross revenue, net revenue, placement fee, margin, billable hours).']
  commission_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission rate as a percentage of revenue or placement fees. Null if plan does not include commission component.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compensation plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this plan (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the compensation plan structure, eligibility criteria, and calculation methodology.']
  draw_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Guaranteed advance payment amount against future commissions. Null if plan does not include draw component.']
  draw_type varchar [note: 'Whether the draw must be repaid from future commissions (recoverable) or is guaranteed (non-recoverable). Null if no draw.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan expires or is no longer available for new assignments. Null indicates open-ended plan.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan becomes active and can be assigned to employees.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic market scope this plan is designed for (local, regional, national, international, global).']
  job_role_category varchar [note: 'Primary job role category this compensation plan is designed for (recruiter, account manager, sales, operations, management, administrative, executive).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compensation plan record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, special conditions, or exceptions related to this compensation plan.']
  overtime_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees on this plan are eligible for overtime pay under labor regulations.']
  overtime_rate_multiplier decimal(3,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base hourly rate for overtime hours (e.g., 1.5 for time-and-a-half). Null if not overtime eligible.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'How often compensation is paid to employees under this plan (weekly, biweekly, semimonthly, monthly, quarterly, annual).']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the compensation plan used in external communications and reporting.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the compensation plan (e.g., \'Base Salary + Commission\', \'Recruiter Tiered Bonus\').']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the compensation structure (salary only, commission only, salary plus commission, tiered bonus, draw against commission, profit sharing).']
  seniority_level varchar [note: 'Target seniority level for employees assigned to this plan (entry, junior, mid, senior, principal, executive).']
  split_commission_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether commissions under this plan can be split among multiple employees (e.g., recruiter and account manager).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compensation plan indicating whether it is available for assignment to employees.']
  target_bonus_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target annual bonus amount at 100% performance achievement. Null if no bonus component or bonus is discretionary.']
  tier_count varchar [note: 'Number of performance tiers in the compensation structure. Null if tier_structure_flag is false.']
  tier_structure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan uses tiered commission or bonus rates based on performance thresholds.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for compensation_plan. Referenced by plan_id.'
}

Table employee.accrual_policy {
  accrual_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for accrual_policy']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity to which this accrual policy applies.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the HR employee responsible for maintaining and administering this accrual policy.']
  superseded_accrual_policy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on accrual_policy (superseded_accrual_policy_id)']
  superseded_by_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the accrual policy that replaces this policy if it has been superseded. Null if current.']
  accrual_calculation_basis varchar [note: 'Basis on which accrual is calculated (e.g., hours worked, days employed, calendar time).']
  accrual_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which time off accrues to employees under this policy (e.g., monthly, per pay period).']
  accrual_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric rate at which time accrues per accrual period (e.g., 1.67 hours per pay period, 10 days per year).']
  accrual_start_event varchar [note: 'Event that triggers the start of accrual for an employee under this policy.']
  accrual_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for accrued time (hours or days).']
  advance_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required for employees to request time off under this policy.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the accrual policy was officially approved for use by HR leadership or management.']
  carryover_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unused accrued time can be carried over to the next accrual period or calendar year.']
  carryover_maximum decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of accrued time that can be carried over if carryover is allowed. Null if unlimited or carryover not allowed.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating the jurisdiction where this policy applies (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the accrual policy record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the accrual policy including purpose, rules, and any special conditions.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the accrual policy ceases to be active. Null for open-ended policies.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the accrual policy becomes active and begins applying to assigned employees.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Description of employee eligibility requirements for this accrual policy (e.g., full-time only, specific job levels, tenure requirements).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the accrual policy was last reviewed for compliance and accuracy.']
  maximum_accrual_balance decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of time that can be accrued under this policy. Null if no cap applies.']
  minimum_usage_increment decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum increment in which accrued time can be used (e.g., 0.25 hours, 0.5 days).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the accrual policy record was last modified or updated.']
  negative_balance_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees can use time off before it has fully accrued, resulting in a negative balance.']
  negative_balance_limit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum negative balance allowed if negative balances are permitted. Null if not applicable.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next policy review to ensure ongoing compliance and relevance.']
  payout_on_termination boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unused accrued time is paid out to the employee upon termination of employment.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the accrual policy, used for external reference and system integration.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the accrual policy (e.g., \'Standard PTO\', \'Executive Leave\', \'Contractor Time Off\').']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the accrual policy indicating the type of leave or time off it governs.']
  proration_method varchar [note: 'Method used to prorate accruals for employees who start mid-period or have partial eligibility.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether time off requests under this policy require manager or HR approval before use.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'State or province code for jurisdiction-specific policies (e.g., CA for California, ON for Ontario).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accrual policy indicating whether it is currently in use or available for assignment.']
  tier_based boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy uses tiered accrual rates based on tenure or other factors.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the policy to track changes and revisions over time.']
  waiting_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days an employee must wait after hire or assignment before accrual begins under this policy.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for accrual_policy. Referenced by accrual_policy_id.'
}

Table employee.review_period {
  review_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for review_period']
  modified_by_employee_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who last modified this review period record.']
  prior_review_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on review_period (prior_review_period_id)']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who created this review period record.']
  applies_to_all_employees boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this review period applies to all employees (True) or only specific groups/departments (False).']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which the review period primarily falls (e.g., 2024).']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date when the review period was officially closed and all reviews finalized.']
  compensation_review_linked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this review period is directly linked to compensation decisions such as salary increases or bonuses (True/False).']
  completed_review_count varchar [note: 'The number of performance reviews that have been completed for this review period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this review period record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the review period including its purpose, scope, and any special instructions or focus areas.']
  eligible_employee_count varchar [note: 'The total number of employees eligible for review during this period.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the review period officially ends. Performance and activities up to this date are included in the evaluation.']
  evaluation_criteria varchar [note: 'Description of the key performance criteria and competencies being evaluated during this review period.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter within the fiscal year (1, 2, 3, or 4). Null for non-quarterly review periods.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this review period belongs (e.g., 2024).']
  goal_setting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees and managers are required to set performance goals for the next period as part of this review cycle (True/False).']
  includes_360_feedback boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 360-degree feedback (manager, peer, subordinate, self) is collected during this review period (True/False).']
  includes_peer_review boolean [note: 'Indicates whether peer reviews are included as part of the evaluation process for this review period (True/False).']
  includes_self_assessment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees are required to complete a self-assessment as part of this review period (True/False).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this review period record is currently active (True) or has been archived/deactivated (False).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether participation in this review period is mandatory for all eligible employees (True) or optional (False).']
  launch_date date [note: 'The date when the review period was officially launched and review activities began.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this review period record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about the review period for internal reference.']
  notification_sent_date date [note: 'The date when employees and managers were notified about the upcoming review period.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Short code or identifier used to reference the review period in business operations and reporting (e.g., \'Q1-2024\', \'ANN-2023\').']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the review period (e.g., \'Q1 2024 Performance Review\', \'Annual Review 2023\', \'Mid-Year Check-In\').']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review period based on frequency and purpose (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly, probationary, project-based).']
  promotion_eligibility_linked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether performance in this review period affects promotion eligibility decisions (True/False).']
  rating_scale_type varchar [note: 'The type of rating scale used for performance evaluations in this review period.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which all performance reviews for this period must be completed and submitted.']
  review_template_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the performance review template or form used for this review period.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the review period officially begins. Performance and activities from this date onward are evaluated.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the review period indicating its operational state.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for review_period. Referenced by review_period_id.'
}

Table employee.development_plan {
  development_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for development_plan']
  approved_by_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or manager who approved this development plan.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department or business unit to which this development plan is aligned.']
  job_role_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target job role or position that this development plan is preparing the employee for.']
  linked_development_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on development_plan (linked_development_plan_id)']
  linked_performance_review_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the performance review that triggered or is associated with this development plan.']
  manager_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or supervisor responsible for overseeing and approving this development plan.']
  mentor_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mentor or coach assigned to guide the employee through this development plan.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal employee (recruiter, account manager, operations staff) for whom this development plan is created.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date when the development plan was completed. Null if not yet completed.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when the development plan was formally approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the development plan indicating whether it has been reviewed and approved by management.']
  budget_allocated decimal(15,2) [note: 'The financial budget allocated for executing this development plan, including training, certifications, and resources.']
  budget_spent decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actual amount of budget spent to date on this development plan.']
  competencies_targeted varchar [note: 'List or description of the specific competencies, skills, or knowledge areas that this development plan targets for improvement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this development plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for budget amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the development plan objectives, scope, and expected outcomes.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this development plan is mandatory (e.g., compliance training, onboarding requirements) or voluntary.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this development plan record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or observations related to the development plan execution and progress.']
  objectives varchar [note: 'Specific, measurable objectives or goals that the development plan aims to achieve.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier or code for the development plan, used for reporting and cross-system reference.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the development plan (e.g., \'Senior Recruiter Career Path\', \'Leadership Development Track\').']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the development plan by its primary purpose (career progression, skill development, leadership, performance improvement, onboarding, succession planning).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this development plan indicating its urgency and importance.']
  progress_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of completion for the development plan, calculated based on milestones or activities completed (0.00 to 100.00).']
  review_date date [note: 'The date when the development plan is scheduled for review or progress assessment.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'The scheduled frequency at which progress on this development plan is reviewed.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the development plan becomes effective and execution begins.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the development plan indicating its operational state.']
  success_criteria varchar [note: 'Defined criteria or metrics that will be used to determine successful completion of the development plan.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'The planned or target date by which the development plan should be completed.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the development plan, incremented with each revision or update to track plan evolution.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for development_plan. Referenced by linked_development_plan_id.'
}

Table employee.territory {
  territory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for territory']
  commission_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commission or compensation plan applicable to this territory.']
  created_by_user_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this territory record.']
  parent_territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent territory in a hierarchical territory structure, enabling multi-level territory organization (e.g., sub-region rolling up to region).']
  sales_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales or recruiting team assigned to this territory.']
  staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (recruiter, account manager, or sales leader) who owns or manages this territory.']
  updated_by_user_staff_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last updated this territory record.']
  account_segment varchar [note: 'Target account segment for this territory: enterprise (large corporations), mid_market (medium-sized businesses), small_business (SMB), strategic (key accounts), government (public sector), or non_profit (charitable organizations).']
  client_target_count varchar [note: 'Target number of active clients to be managed or acquired within this territory.']
  country_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes covered by this territory (e.g., \'USA,CAN,MEX\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this territory record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the territory, including coverage notes, strategic focus, and any special considerations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this territory definition expires or is superseded, nullable for open-ended territories.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this territory definition becomes active and operational.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic coverage (e.g., \'California, Nevada, Arizona\' or \'EMEA excluding UK\').']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the territory hierarchy (e.g., 1 for national, 2 for regional, 3 for local), enabling depth-based queries and reporting.']
  industry_verticals varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of industry verticals or sectors assigned to this territory (e.g., \'Healthcare,Finance,Technology\'), applicable for industry-based territory models.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this territory is exclusive (true) or shared among multiple employees (false).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes for internal use, capturing operational details, historical context, or special instructions.']
  overlap_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this territory can overlap with other territories (true) or must be mutually exclusive (false).']
  placement_target_count varchar [note: 'Target number of candidate placements expected from this territory within the performance period.']
  postal_code_ranges varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of postal code ranges or prefixes defining the territory (e.g., \'90000-92999,93000-93599\').']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking indicating the strategic priority of this territory relative to others (lower number = higher priority).']
  revenue_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual revenue target or quota assigned to this territory, used for performance measurement and planning.']
  state_province_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of state or province codes within the territory (e.g., \'CA,NV,AZ\' for US states).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the territory: active (operational), inactive (not in use), pending (awaiting activation), suspended (temporarily halted), archived (historical), or planned (future territory).']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the territory, used in external communications and reporting.']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the territory (e.g., Northeast Region, California Bay Area, EMEA Central).']
  territory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the territory structure: geographic (location-based), industry (vertical-based), account_based (specific client portfolios), hybrid (combination), strategic (key accounts), or named_account (individual major clients).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this territory record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for territory. Referenced by territory_id.'
}

Table employee.job_role {
  job_role_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for job_role']
  parent_job_role_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on job_role (parent_job_role_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status for this job role definition, indicating whether it has been reviewed and approved by HR leadership.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this job role definition for active use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this job role definition was approved.']
  compensation_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation band or grade assigned to this role within the organization\'s pay structure.']
  competencies varchar [note: 'Key skills, behaviors, and competencies required for successful performance in this role, often mapped to a competency framework.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this job role record was first created in the system.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary department or organizational unit where this role typically resides.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the role\'s purpose, scope, and key responsibilities.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this job role definition ceased or will cease to be effective. Null for currently active roles.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this job role definition became or will become effective and available for use.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Standard employment classification for this role indicating full-time, part-time, contract, or other arrangement.']
  exempt_status varchar [note: 'Classification under FLSA indicating whether the role is exempt from overtime pay requirements.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether time worked by employees in this role is typically billable to clients or customers.']
  is_client_facing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this role regularly interacts directly with external clients or customers.']
  job_posting_template varchar [note: 'Standard template or boilerplate text used when creating external job postings for this role.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that most recently modified this job role record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this job role record was most recently updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes or comments about this job role for internal reference.']
  performance_metrics varchar [note: 'Key performance indicators or metrics used to evaluate success and performance in this role.']
  physical_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of any physical demands or requirements necessary to perform this role, relevant for ADA compliance.']
  preferred_qualifications varchar [note: 'Desirable but not mandatory qualifications, skills, or experience that would enhance performance in this role.']
  remote_work_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this role is eligible for remote or work-from-home arrangements.']
  reports_to_role_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job role to which this role typically reports in the organizational hierarchy.']
  required_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant professional experience required for this role.']
  required_qualifications varchar [note: 'Mandatory educational credentials, certifications, or licenses required to perform this role.']
  responsibilities varchar [note: 'Comprehensive list of primary duties and responsibilities expected of individuals in this role.']
  role_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the job role used across systems and reporting. Typically a short alphanumeric code assigned by HR.']
  role_family varchar [note: 'Broad functional family or category to which this role belongs, used for workforce planning and career pathing.']
  role_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level or seniority tier of the role within the organization, used for compensation banding and career progression.']
  role_title varchar [note: 'Official title of the job role as used in job postings, employment contracts, and organizational charts.']
  salary_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for salary ranges associated with this role.']
  salary_range_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual salary for this role in the organization\'s base currency.']
  salary_range_minimum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum annual salary for this role in the organization\'s base currency.']
  standard_hours_per_week decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected number of working hours per week for this role under normal circumstances.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job role indicating whether it is actively used for hiring and assignments.']
  succession_planning_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this role is designated as critical for succession planning due to business impact or difficulty to fill.']
  travel_requirement_percentage varchar [note: 'Expected percentage of work time requiring business travel, expressed as an integer from 0 to 100.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this job role definition, incremented with each significant revision to track changes over time.']
  work_environment varchar [note: 'Description of the typical work environment and conditions for this role, such as office, field, or hybrid.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this job role record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for job_role. Referenced by job_role_id.'
}

Table shared.holiday_calendar {
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for holiday_calendar']
  parent_holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on holiday_calendar (parent_holiday_calendar_id)']
  advance_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in advance that employees and clients should be notified about this holiday observance for planning purposes.']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific business unit, division, or operating entity to which this holiday calendar entry applies. Null indicates organization-wide applicability.']
  client_specific_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this holiday is specific to a particular client engagement or contract, requiring special staffing considerations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating the country where this holiday is observed (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR, AUS).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this holiday calendar entry was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  day_of_week varchar [note: 'The day of the week on which the holiday falls, used for scheduling and observance rule application.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the holiday, including cultural or historical significance, observance traditions, and any special business considerations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this holiday calendar entry ceases to be effective. Null indicates ongoing applicability.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this holiday calendar entry becomes effective for business operations and scheduling.']
  holiday_date date [note: 'The specific date on which the holiday occurs. Core business event date for this reference entity.']
  holiday_name varchar [note: 'The official name of the holiday or non-working day (e.g., New Year\'s Day, Christmas, Independence Day, Company Shutdown).']
  holiday_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the holiday indicating its nature and scope (public, federal, state-specific, company-specific, religious observance, or floating holiday).']
  is_mandatory_closure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organization mandates office/facility closure on this holiday, affecting staffing availability and client service delivery.']
  is_observed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this holiday is actively observed by the organization for payroll, scheduling, and operational purposes.']
  is_paid boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees receive paid time off for this holiday. Critical for payroll processing and compensation calculations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this holiday calendar entry was last updated. Critical for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this holiday observance, including exceptions or special handling requirements.']
  observance_rule varchar [note: 'Defines how the holiday date is determined: fixed (same date annually), floating (varies by year based on rules), calculated (algorithmic determination), or substitute (observed on alternate date when falls on weekend).']
  observed_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the holiday is observed for business purposes, which may differ from the holiday_date when substitution rules apply (e.g., Monday observance for Sunday holiday).']
  premium_pay_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'The multiplier applied to standard pay rates for employees working on this holiday (e.g., 1.5 for time-and-a-half, 2.0 for double-time). Used in payroll calculations.']
  recurring_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this holiday recurs annually or is a one-time observance (e.g., special company anniversary, one-time government-declared holiday).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system or authority from which this holiday calendar entry was sourced (e.g., government calendar, HR policy system, client contract).']
  staffing_impact_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the impact this holiday has on staffing availability and workforce planning, ranging from none to critical.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'State or province code for holidays that are region-specific within a country (e.g., CA for California, ON for Ontario). Null for national holidays.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this holiday calendar entry indicating whether it is actively used for scheduling and payroll operations.']
  year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which this holiday occurs. Enables year-over-year holiday planning and historical analysis.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for holiday_calendar. Referenced by holiday_calendar_id.'
}
